issues.body:
## Status

Completed by squash-merged PR #7.

## Why

Establish the smallest buildable Rust foothold and baseline local guardrails for the complete rewrite so later UI, domain, image, and processing work starts from strict compiler defaults instead of the inherited C/CMake build.

## How

- Add a virtual workspace in `Cargo.toml` with resolver `3`, shared Rust 2024 package metadata, and exactly one initial member: `crates/rusttable-core`.
- Add `Cargo.lock`, `crates/rusttable-core/Cargo.toml`, and `crates/rusttable-core/src/lib.rs` with a dependency-free product-name smoke API and unit test.
- Define inherited workspace Rust lints that deny warnings and forbid `unsafe_code`, deny the Clippy `all` and `pedantic` groups, opt the crate into those lints, add `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, and make warnings fatal through `.cargo/config.toml`.
- Add `rust-toolchain.toml` with the minimal stable toolchain plus `clippy` and `rustfmt`; the exact numeric pin and completed-run hook budgets were deliberately deferred to #8.
- Add `scripts/check-source-policy.sh`, `scripts/precommit-fast.sh`, and `scripts/prepush-fast.sh`; wire the pre-commit entry point through `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and the pre-push entry point through `.githooks/pre-push`. Keep independent checks parallel where practical.
- Do not add `iced`, image I/O, metadata, processing, persistence, domain behavior beyond the smoke API, or RustTable GitHub Actions in this PR.
- Deliver this scope through exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Dependencies

- Depends on completed #2 for the repository execution contract.
- Completed prerequisite for completed #8 / PR #35.
- The remaining milestone-#1 sequence is #40 → #14 → #17.

## Validation

- `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `Cargo.toml` is a virtual resolver-3 workspace whose only initial member is `crates/rusttable-core`; `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` reports that member successfully.
- `Cargo.lock`, `crates/rusttable-core/Cargo.toml`, and `crates/rusttable-core/src/lib.rs` are present on `main`, and the focused `product_name()` unit test passes.
- Root workspace lint tables, `.cargo/config.toml`, crate lint inheritance, and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` make a deliberate Rust warning or unsafe block fail compilation without depending only on text search.
- `scripts/check-source-policy.sh` rejects an over-1,000-line handwritten Rust file and an unapproved `unsafe` token.
- `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` runs formatting and source-policy checks in parallel; `scripts/prepush-fast.sh` runs tests and Clippy in parallel; `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and `.githooks/pre-push` invoke those repository-owned entry points.
- No RustTable UI, image-processing, persistence, or GitHub Actions behavior is introduced.
- PR #7 closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: feat: bootstrap strict Rust workspace

issues.body:
## Why

Milestone #2 needed the smallest runnable proof that the Rust rewrite could launch `iced` independently of the inherited darktable C/C++ application. The shell establishes the desktop package and dependency boundary; interactive state flow, layout, and UI test coverage remain separate PRs.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **First runnable vertical slice: 1 of 4 — completed.**
- Depends on completed #4 / merged PR #7.
- Implemented by merged PR #25.
- Unblocks #9.
- `crates/rusttable-app` is the iced desktop package. The non-UI `rusttable-catalog` boundary tracked by #10 remains separate.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-app` to the root workspace.
- Add `crates/rusttable-app/Cargo.toml` with inherited package metadata and lints, `rusttable-core` as a path dependency, and `iced = "0.14.0"` with default features disabled and only `thread-pool` plus `tiny-skia` enabled.
- Use the package-default binary `rusttable-app` from `crates/rusttable-app/src/main.rs`; keep it as a thin call to `rusttable_app::run()`.
- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs` with the initial `iced::application` wiring, one centered native window titled `RustTable`, and a static view that renders `rusttable_core::product_name()`.
- Update `Cargo.lock` through Cargo.
- Do not add image I/O, metadata, processing, persistence, catalog behavior, configuration, network access, or filesystem access to the application crate.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `cargo run -p rusttable-app --locked`, followed by a normal window close

## Acceptance criteria

- `main` contains `crates/rusttable-app/Cargo.toml`, `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs`, and `crates/rusttable-app/src/main.rs`, and the root workspace includes `crates/rusttable-app`.
- `cargo run -p rusttable-app --locked` opens exactly one centered native window titled `RustTable` and visibly renders `RustTable`.
- Closing the window exits normally without a panic or lingering process.
- The application package uses iced 0.14 with the reviewed `thread-pool` and `tiny-skia` feature set and does not compile or link the inherited darktable targets.
- The shell contains no product interaction beyond the static product label; state/message/update behavior belongs to #9.
- No image, metadata, processing, catalog, database, network, configuration, or filesystem behavior is introduced.
- PR #25 closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: feat(ui): add the runnable iced application shell

issues.body:
## Status

Completed by squash-merged PR #35.

## Why

PR #7 established the strict Cargo workspace and parallel local checks, but `rust-toolchain.toml` still floated on `stable`, Cargo validation could resolve without proving the committed lockfile was usable, and the hook scripts did not report or fail when their completed run exceeded the 30-second pre-commit or 60-second pre-push contracts in `TASK.md`.

## How

- Pin `rust-toolchain.toml` to exact Rust `1.95.0` with the `minimal` profile and the `clippy` plus `rustfmt` components.
- Add `scripts/with-validation-budget.sh` with a reusable `run_with_budget LIMIT COMMAND...` function that preserves command failures, prints elapsed seconds, and returns `124` when an otherwise-successful completed command exceeds its budget.
- Add `scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh` with focused passing and over-budget regression cases.
- Update `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` to run these checks in parallel and report/enforce a 30-second completed-run budget:
  - `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
  - `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
  - `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
  - `bun run test:computer-use`
- Update `scripts/prepush-fast.sh` to run these checks in parallel and report/enforce a 60-second completed-run budget:
  - `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
  - `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
  - `bun run test:computer-use`
- Preserve the existing hook entry points and per-check failure logs.
- Do not add GitHub Actions in this PR.
- Deliver this scope through exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Dependencies

- Depends on completed #4 / PR #7.
- Completed prerequisite for #40, which hardens the elapsed-time guard into an interrupting deadline before #14 adds PR CI.
- #40 blocks #14, and #14 blocks #17.

## Validation

- `grep -Fx 'channel = "1.95.0"' rust-toolchain.toml`
- `rustc --version --verbose`
- `bash scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `rust-toolchain.toml` selects exact Rust `1.95.0`, not a floating channel, and installs Clippy plus rustfmt.
- `scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh` proves an in-budget fixture succeeds and an otherwise-successful fixture that completes after its limit fails.
- A clean pre-commit run executes formatting, locked metadata, source policy, and Computer Use tests in parallel, reports elapsed time against 30 seconds, and completes within the recorded budget.
- A clean pre-push run executes locked workspace tests, locked all-feature Clippy, and Computer Use tests in parallel, reports elapsed time against 60 seconds, and completes within the recorded budget.
- A failed child check remains a failure even when elapsed time is within budget, and the responsible captured log is printed.
- The completed-run budget helper does not claim to interrupt a hung process or reap a process tree; that narrower hard-deadline behavior is explicitly owned by #40 rather than retroactively attributed to PR #35.
- No GitHub workflow is added or modified.
- PR #35 closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: chore: pin Rust toolchain and enforce hook budgets

issues.body:
## Why

The initial shell from #5 could launch iced but had a zero-field state, an uninhabited message type, and no user-driven transition. This PR establishes one explicit shell-only state change before visual layout or product behavior is added.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **First runnable vertical slice: 2 of 4 — completed.**
- Depends on completed #5 / merged PR #25.
- Implemented by merged PR #39.
- Unblocks #12.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; no catalog, image, metadata, processing, persistence, or adapter behavior belongs in this issue.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/app.rs` with:
  - `pub(crate) struct Shell` containing a private `sidebar_visible: bool` field;
  - an explicit `Default` implementation that starts with the sidebar visible;
  - `pub(crate) enum Message { ToggleSidebar }`, deriving `Debug`, `Clone`, `Copy`, `PartialEq`, and `Eq`;
  - `pub(crate) fn update(&mut Shell, Message) -> iced::Task<Message>` that toggles the field and returns `Task::none()`;
  - `pub(crate) fn view(&Shell) -> iced::Element<'_, Message>` as a read-only projection of shell state.
- Retain the visible `RustTable` label, render a neutral `Sidebar` placeholder when visible, and render a button labelled `Hide sidebar` or `Show sidebar` that emits `Message::ToggleSidebar`.
- Update `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs` to wire `Shell::default`, `update`, and `view` into the existing `iced::application` entry point.
- Keep `crates/rusttable-app/src/main.rs` and the dependency graph unchanged.
- Add exactly three focused unit tests: default-visible state, first toggle hides, and a second toggle restores.
- Do not add subscriptions, asynchronous work, timers, menus, shortcuts, navigation, I/O, catalog services, or image-processing behavior.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-app --locked app::tests`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `cargo run -p rusttable-app --locked`, followed by a normal startup, two-way toggle, and clean close

## Acceptance criteria

- At startup, the native window visibly contains `RustTable`, `Sidebar`, and a control labelled `Hide sidebar`.
- Activating the control hides the sidebar and changes the label to `Show sidebar`; activating it again restores the initial state.
- `app::tests` proves the default state and both deterministic `ToggleSidebar` transitions without opening a native window.
- `Shell` fields remain private, state mutation occurs only in `update`, and `view` accepts `&Shell` without mutation or I/O.
- The update path returns only `Task::none()` and introduces no subscription, asynchronous operation, clock, filesystem, network, database, configuration, image, metadata, or processing behavior.
- The dependency graph and `Cargo.lock` are unchanged.
- PR #39 changes only `crates/rusttable-app/src/app.rs` and `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs`, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: feat(ui): establish explicit state, message, update, and view flow

issues.body:
## Status

Completed by squash-merged PR #44.

## Why

RustTable needed an explicit dependency direction before domain behavior grew so catalog state could not become coupled to Iced, image codecs, persistence, processing backends, or inherited darktable C/C++ runtime types.

## How

- Added `crates/rusttable-catalog` as a root workspace member.
- Added `crates/rusttable-catalog/Cargo.toml` with inherited workspace metadata/lints and exactly one dependency: the local `rusttable-core` package.
- Added `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/lib.rs` with `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, crate responsibility documentation, and the documented direction `rusttable-catalog -> rusttable-core`.
- Kept `rusttable-core` dependency-free and updated its crate documentation to state that catalog code may depend on core, never the reverse.
- Added `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/crate_boundaries.rs`, which invokes `cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1` and verifies that:
  - `rusttable-core` has no manifest dependencies;
  - `rusttable-catalog` has exactly one manifest dependency and it is `rusttable-core`;
  - catalog has no direct dependency named for Iced, GTK, bindgen, CMake, database, image/JPEG/PNG, processing, or FFI integration.
- Updated root workspace membership and `Cargo.lock` without changing `crates/rusttable-app` or adding domain, UI, image, metadata, processing, or persistence behavior.
- Added no C headers, bindgen output, CMake integration, object files, FFI declarations, copied darktable structs, or compatibility shim.

## Validation

- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`

## Acceptance criteria

- Root `Cargo.toml` contains `rusttable-app`, `rusttable-catalog`, and `rusttable-core` as workspace members.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` shows no normal dependency.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal` shows only `rusttable-core`.
- Both core and catalog crate roots forbid unsafe code and document their distinct responsibilities and one-way dependency direction.
- The metadata-driven architecture test passes for the intended graph and rejects an added direct catalog integration dependency.
- PR #44 changes only workspace/lockfile metadata, core documentation, and the new structural catalog crate/test; it adds no domain behavior and does not modify the Iced application crate.
- No handwritten Rust file exceeds 1,000 lines, no inherited C/C++ target is compiled or linked, and PR #44 closes only this issue through a squash merge.
issues.title: arch: establish core and catalog crate boundaries

issues.body:
## Why

Merged PR #39 establishes the shell state and toggle transition, but its widget tree is still a single unthemed column with an empty text node standing in for a hidden sidebar. The next PR must establish a stable visual and module boundary before the deterministic UI smoke test or any product-specific desktop work depends on widget structure.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **First runnable vertical slice: 3 of 4.**
- Depends on completed #9 / merged PR #39 and is now unblocked.
- **Best first open issue in milestone #2.**
- Blocks #15. Per `TASK.md`, do not begin #15 until this issue is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; it must not create or modify `crates/rusttable-catalog`, `rusttable-core`, image-processing code, or adapter behavior.

## How

- Add a crate-visible read-only `Shell::sidebar_visible()` accessor in `crates/rusttable-app/src/app.rs`; keep the field itself private and preserve the #9 update behavior and tests.
- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/theme.rs` containing:
  - `pub(crate) fn theme(_: &Shell) -> iced::Theme` returning exactly `iced::Theme::Dark`;
  - named `f32` constants `CONTENT_PADDING = 16.0`, `REGION_SPACING = 12.0`, `HEADER_HEIGHT = 48.0`, and `SIDEBAR_WIDTH = 240.0`.
- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/view.rs` with `pub(crate) fn view(shell: &Shell) -> iced::Element<'_, Message>`.
- Move all widget construction and `rusttable_core::product_name` use out of `app.rs`. After this PR, `app.rs` owns only `Shell`, `Message`, the read-only accessor, default state, update logic, and state-transition tests.
- Build exactly three neutral regions using standard iced widgets:
  1. a fixed-height header containing `RustTable` and the existing sidebar toggle;
  2. an optional fixed-width sidebar containing only `Sidebar`;
  3. a fill-width, fill-height workspace containing only `Workspace`.
- Use the named constants from `theme.rs` for repeated shell padding, spacing, header height, and sidebar width. Do not scatter duplicate numeric layout literals through `view.rs`.
- When the sidebar is hidden, omit the sidebar element from the body row entirely. Do not render `text("")` or leave an invisible fixed-width spacer.
- Update `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs` to declare `theme` and `view`, wire `view::view` into `iced::application`, and call `.theme(theme::theme)`.
- Keep `crates/rusttable-app/src/main.rs`, `Cargo.toml`, and `Cargo.lock` unchanged.
- Do not add a status bar, custom widget, custom palette, icons, image assets, repository-owned fonts, menus, dialogs, navigation destinations, shortcuts, tool panels, responsive breakpoints, persistence, or image/domain behavior.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-app --locked app::tests`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `cargo run -p rusttable-app --locked`; verify the default layout, toggle twice, and resize the content area to 800×600

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-app/src/theme.rs` is the single owner of the application theme and the four named shell layout constants.
- The application selects exactly `iced::Theme::Dark` through the builder theme callback; no custom theme or palette is introduced.
- `crates/rusttable-app/src/view.rs` is the single production widget-construction module, while `app.rs` contains no iced widget imports or widget construction after the move.
- When the window is resized to 800×600, it visibly contains `RustTable`, `Sidebar`, `Workspace`, and `Hide sidebar` with no overlap or clipping.
- The visible sidebar consumes exactly 240 logical pixels. Hiding it removes the sidebar region and allows the workspace to expand; showing it restores the original structure.
- `Shell::sidebar_visible()` is read-only and crate-visible; the underlying field remains private and `app::update` remains the only production mutation path.
- Header height, sidebar width, spacing, and padding use the named constants and are not duplicated as unexplained literals in `view.rs`.
- No status surface, custom visual asset, product navigation, image state, processing control, persistence, network access, configuration, or filesystem behavior is added.
- The PR changes only `crates/rusttable-app/src/app.rs`, `lib.rs`, and the new `theme.rs` and `view.rs`; it does not change dependencies or `Cargo.lock`.
- The implementation is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.
issues.title: feat(ui): define the dark theme and shell layout baseline

issues.body:
## Why

The domain model needs strongly typed identities and validated scalar values before photos, assets, operations, edits, or catalog state can be represented. Raw integers permit entity mix-ups and sentinel values; unconstrained `f64` permits NaN/infinity and nondeterministic equality; implicit ID generation would couple core behavior to clocks, randomness, persistence, or process state.

These primitives must be clean-room Rust values with stable textual forms and explicit failure modes. They must not inherit darktable integer IDs, database keys, C layouts, UUID allocation, or serialization policy.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by completed #10 / merged PR #44, which establishes the dependency-free core and one-way catalog boundary.
- **Milestone #3 sequence: 2 of 7 and the best first open issue in “Architecture & Core Domain”.**
- Blocks #16; #18, #38, #19, and #41 follow transitively.

## How

- Write the failing tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-core/tests/identifiers.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-core/tests/value_objects.rs`.
- Record the initial failing targeted commands/output in the implementation PR before adding the production modules.
- Add `crates/rusttable-core/src/id.rs` with four distinct opaque newtypes backed by `NonZeroU128`:
  - `PhotoId`;
  - `AssetId`;
  - `EditId`;
  - `OperationId`.
- Keep each field private. For every ID type provide:
  - `TryFrom<u128>` with shared typed `IdValueError::Zero`;
  - `get() -> u128`;
  - canonical `Display` as exactly 32 lowercase hexadecimal ASCII characters with no prefix;
  - strict `FromStr` returning typed `IdParseError` variants for wrong length, uppercase/noncanonical input, invalid ASCII hexadecimal input, and the all-zero value;
  - deterministic `Copy`, `Clone`, `Debug`, `Eq`, `Ord`, and `Hash` behavior.
- Do not trim input or accept `0x`, separators, uppercase text, short forms, decimal text, whitespace, or an all-zero ID. Leading zeroes are valid only as part of the exact 32-character nonzero canonical form.
- Add at least one `compile_fail` doctest in `id.rs` proving that a `PhotoId` cannot be passed where an `AssetId` is required. Also prove that callers cannot construct an ID by naming its private field.
- Add `crates/rusttable-core/src/value.rs` with:
  - opaque `Revision(u64)`, exposing `Revision::ZERO`, `const fn from_value(u64)`, `get() -> u64`, and `checked_next() -> Result<Revision, RevisionOverflow>`;
  - opaque `FiniteF64(f64)`, constructible only through `TryFrom<f64>`, with typed rejection of NaN and positive/negative infinity;
  - canonical normalization of every `-0.0` input to `+0.0` before storage;
  - deterministic `Eq`, `Ord`, and `Hash` for every constructible `FiniteF64`, using the canonical finite representation rather than approximate comparison.
- Implement `Display` and `std::error::Error` for every public error type. Preserve observable variants and relevant input details; do not use string-only error kinds or an opaque catch-all.
- Re-export only the intentional public types from `crates/rusttable-core/src/lib.rs`; keep representation fields and helper macros private.
- Use only the Rust standard library. Do not add `uuid`, `rand`, `serde`, a time crate, a database crate, global counters, environment reads, filesystem/network I/O, or process-local allocation behavior.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test identifiers --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test value_objects --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-core/src/id.rs` publicly defines four non-interchangeable opaque ID types; a compile-fail doctest rejects cross-type use and private-field construction.
- For every ID type, `TryFrom(0)` returns `IdValueError::Zero`, `1` and `u128::MAX` are accepted, and `get()` returns the exact original nonzero value.
- `Display` always emits exactly 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters. Parsing that output round-trips for boundary and representative values.
- Parsing rejects empty/short/long input, `0x` prefixes, whitespace, separators, uppercase characters, non-hex ASCII, non-ASCII text, and 32 zeroes with the documented observable `IdParseError` variant; no input is silently normalized.
- IDs derive no value from a random source, clock, environment variable, database, filesystem, global mutable state, or inherited darktable identifier.
- `Revision::ZERO.get()` is `0`; `Revision::from_value(n).checked_next()` returns `n + 1` for `n < u64::MAX`; advancing `u64::MAX` returns `RevisionOverflow` without wrapping or panicking.
- `FiniteF64` rejects NaN and both infinities, canonicalizes `-0.0` and `+0.0` to equal values with equal hashes, and provides a total deterministic order covering negative, zero, and positive finite values.
- Equal constructible values always have equal hashes, and ordering/hash tests do not depend on platform locale, wall clock, random input, filesystem, network, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` shows no dependency. No unsafe Rust, C/C++ bridge, serialization/persistence schema, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(core): add deterministic domain identifiers and scalar value objects

issues.body:
## Why

`main` still has no RustTable GitHub Actions result: the inherited `.github/workflows/ci.yml` targets `master` and gates its build to `darktable-org/darktable`, while the current `main` commit has no associated workflow run or status. The repository now contains an `iced` application and exact Bun tooling in addition to Rust, so PR validation must cover the complete current fast policy without compiling inherited C/CMake code. It must also avoid simply invoking both local hook wrappers, because each wrapper now runs the same Bun test and their concurrent Cargo invocations are optimized for warm developer worktrees rather than a cold hosted runner.

## How

- Add `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml` triggered by every `pull_request` whose base is `main`, plus `workflow_dispatch` for diagnostics. Do not use `pull_request_target`, path filters, or a `push` trigger.
- Set workflow permissions to read-only `contents: read`; do not expose repository secrets or grant write permissions.
- Add concurrency keyed by workflow plus pull-request number, with a branch/ref fallback for manual dispatch, and set `cancel-in-progress: true` so superseded PR commits are cancelled.
- Use one `ubuntu-latest` job with the stable check name `rust-pr / validate` and `timeout-minutes: 3`, the nearest GitHub integer timeout above the 150-second contract.
- Make the first executable step record an epoch timestamp before checkout. Add a final `if: always()` step that prints elapsed seconds and fails when execution from that first step exceeds 150 seconds. The measurement may exclude GitHub queue/runner-provisioning time, but it must include checkout, tool setup, cache restore, repository checks, and the final guard.
- Pin every reusable action to an immutable full commit SHA. Set up exact Rust `1.95.0` from `rust-toolchain.toml` and exact Bun `1.3.14` from `package.json`; do not install a floating channel or floating Bun release.
- Add a Cargo cache whose key includes runner OS, exact Rust version, and `hashFiles('Cargo.lock')`. Do not cache inherited C/CMake outputs. Cache use must not permit an unlocked dependency resolution.
- Add `scripts/pr-ci.sh` as the single repository-owned PR validation entry point. It must not call `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` or `scripts/prepush-fast.sh`, because that would execute `bun run test:computer-use` twice and couple CI to the 30/60-second local wrappers.
- Make `scripts/pr-ci.sh` run each of these checks exactly once:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
  - `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
  - `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
  - `bun run test:computer-use`
  - `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- Run the five cheap/noncompiling checks in one early phase, in parallel where useful, with captured logs and all failures reported. Run Clippy and tests in a deterministic cache-reusing order rather than launching two cold full-workspace compilations concurrently. Do not invoke CMake or any inherited darktable build target.
- Keep release builds, warning-denied documentation, cross-platform tests, performance, dependency/security policy, diagnostics, packaging, installation, and publication out of this PR workflow. #17 owns the merge-to-main validation foundation.
- Deliver this scope through exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Dependencies

- Depends on #40, which turns the local validation budgets into hard deadlines and stabilizes the exact command set.
- Blocks #17.
- This is the next milestone-#1 issue after #40.

## Validation

- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/rust-pr.yml`
- `git diff --check`
- Open one test PR targeting `main` and record:
  - a run with no matching Cargo cache;
  - an immediate rerun with a cache hit;
  - the elapsed seconds printed by the workflow guard for both runs.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every PR targeting `main`, including documentation-only PRs, receives the `rust-pr / validate` check; pushes to `main` do not trigger `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml`.
- The workflow uses `pull_request`, read-only permissions, immutable action SHAs, exact Rust `1.95.0`, exact Bun `1.3.14`, Cargo.lock-aware caching, concurrency cancellation, and no secret.
- `scripts/pr-ci.sh` executes each listed check exactly once, never calls either local hook wrapper, and never invokes CMake or an inherited darktable build.
- Bad formatting, lockfile drift, an unsafe token/block, a Rust warning, a failing Rust test, and a failing Bun test each make `rust-pr / validate` fail in separate temporary verification commits; those commits are reverted before merge.
- The early phase reports all simultaneous cheap-check failures with their labels and captured logs.
- Both the recorded no-cache run and cache-hit rerun complete at or below 150 seconds as measured from the first executable step; the final guard itself fails a temporary deterministic over-budget fixture.
- The job has exactly one repository validation job and a finite three-minute protective timeout. No merge-only or release-oriented validation is added.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: ci: add a 150-second RustTable pull-request gate

issues.body:
## Why

The state tests from #9 prove deterministic transitions, and #12 establishes the stable shell labels and presentation boundary. The vertical slice still needs one integration check that constructs the real iced widget tree, operates the real toggle control, and routes emitted messages through the real update function. It must run in ordinary workspace tests without an OS window or display server.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **First runnable vertical slice: 4 of 4 — final issue.**
- Blocked by #12.
- Tests only `crates/rusttable-app` and the shell established by #5, #9, and #12.
- It must not depend on `rusttable-catalog`, image fixtures, processing behavior, the macOS app installer, or later release tooling.

## How

- Add `iced_test = "0.14.0"` under `[dev-dependencies]` in `crates/rusttable-app/Cargo.toml`, matching the application’s iced version, and update `Cargo.lock` through Cargo.
- Keep the production `iced` dependency and its existing `default-features = false`, `thread-pool`, and `tiny-skia` settings unchanged. Do not enable `wgpu`; Cargo feature unification with the existing `tiny-skia` application dependency supplies the renderer used by `iced_test`.
- Add `#[cfg(test)] mod ui_smoke;` to `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs`.
- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/ui_smoke.rs` with exactly one test named `shell_renders_and_toggles_sidebar` returning `Result<(), iced_test::Error>`.
- Exercise the crate-visible production `Shell`, `Message`, `app::update`, and `view::view` items from #9 and #12; do not duplicate a test-only view or update implementation.
- In the test:
  1. construct `Shell::default()`;
  2. construct `iced_test::Simulator::with_size(iced_test::core::Settings::default(), iced_test::core::Size::new(800.0, 600.0), view::view(&shell))`;
  3. find `RustTable`, `Sidebar`, `Workspace`, and `Hide sidebar` by exact text selector;
  4. click `Hide sidebar`, collect `into_messages()` into a vector, assert that it contains exactly `Message::ToggleSidebar`, and call `let _ = app::update(&mut shell, message)` for that message;
  5. rebuild the simulator at the same 800×600 size and assert that exact selector `Sidebar` is absent while `RustTable`, `Workspace`, and `Show sidebar` remain present;
  6. click `Show sidebar`, again assert the exact emitted message, route it through `app::update`, rebuild once more, and assert that `Sidebar` and `Hide sidebar` are restored.
- Use text selectors only. Do not inspect pixels or use `Simulator::snapshot`.
- Do not add `.ice` recordings, screenshots, golden files, repository-owned fonts, filesystem fixtures, network access, environment-dependent assertions, wall-clock limits, sleeps, retries, native-window automation, or platform-specific code.
- Keep the standard workspace test command as the only CI entry point for this test; do not create a separate workflow or test script.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-app --locked ui_smoke::shell_renders_and_toggles_sidebar -- --exact`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-app/src/ui_smoke.rs` contains exactly one headless shell scenario at a fixed 800×600 logical viewport.
- The scenario constructs the production view, proves all four initial labels, operates the production `Hide sidebar` and `Show sidebar` controls, and routes each emitted message through the production update function.
- The test asserts exactly one `Message::ToggleSidebar` per click and proves the visible → hidden → visible round trip after rebuilding the real widget tree.
- The test opens no native window and requires no display server, screenshot backend, filesystem fixture, network, clock, sleep, retry, golden file, or platform-specific service.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked` discovers and runs the smoke scenario automatically within the repository’s pre-push and PR validation budgets.
- The production iced feature set remains exactly `thread-pool` plus `tiny-skia`; no `wgpu` or other production feature is added.
- Production behavior and public API remain unchanged; outside the dev dependency and lockfile, the only production-source edit is the `#[cfg(test)]` module declaration.
- The PR changes only `crates/rusttable-app/Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs`, and the new `crates/rusttable-app/src/ui_smoke.rs`.
- The implementation is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.
issues.title: test(ui): add deterministic headless iced shell smoke coverage

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable needs a clean distinction between a logical `Photo` and the immutable source `Asset` values that belong to it. The inherited darktable image structures mix identity, loader state, paths, metadata, processing flags, caches, UI/runtime state, database fields, and error codes. Copying that shape would carry C-era coupling into the rewrite.

The first aggregate must instead be deterministic, invariant-driven, and independent of I/O, persistence, metadata parsing, UI, and processing. It must also avoid redundant constructor inputs that can disagree—for example, a separately supplied “primary ID” and an asset role map.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #13, which supplies `PhotoId`, `AssetId`, and `Revision`.
- Uses the dependency-free core boundary completed by #10 / PR #44.
- **Milestone #3 sequence: 3 of 7.**
- Blocks #18; #38, #19, and #41 follow transitively.

## How

- Write the failing integration tests first in `crates/rusttable-core/tests/photo_asset_model.rs` and record the initial failing command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add:
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/model/mod.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/model/asset.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/model/photo.rs`.
- In `asset.rs`, define:
  - `AssetRole::{Primary, Sidecar, Auxiliary}`;
  - opaque `ByteLength(u64)` with `ByteLength::ZERO`, `const fn from_bytes(u64)`, and `get() -> u64`; zero is representable because the domain value records observed length and does not decide whether a codec can decode the content;
  - algorithm-tagged `ContentHash` with exactly one initial variant, `Sha256([u8; 32])`, plus read-only algorithm/byte access;
  - immutable `Asset` with private `AssetId`, `AssetRole`, `ContentHash`, and `ByteLength` fields, an explicit constructor, and read-only accessors.
- The core stores externally computed digest bytes. It must not open a file, compute SHA-256, infer a format, or select a source path.
- In `photo.rs`, define immutable `Photo` state with private:
  - `PhotoId`;
  - `Revision`;
  - derived primary `AssetId`;
  - `BTreeMap<AssetId, Asset>`.
- Provide:
  - `Photo::new(photo_id, assets)`, which sets `Revision::ZERO`;
  - `Photo::from_parts(photo_id, revision, assets)`, for deterministic replay/reconstruction without adding serialization or persistence policy;
  - read-only `id()`, `revision()`, `primary_asset_id()`, `primary_asset()`, `asset(id)`, and ascending `assets()` access.
- Do **not** accept a separate primary-asset ID. Derive it from the asset roles after validation so the aggregate has one source of truth.
- Validate in this deterministic order before constructing state:
  1. reject an empty input;
  2. reject the first repeated `AssetId` encountered while consuming the caller's sequence, even when the duplicate `Asset` values are equal;
  3. reject zero primary assets;
  4. reject multiple primary assets and report all conflicting IDs in ascending `AssetId` order.
- Define matchable `PhotoBuildError` variants for `NoAssets`, `DuplicateAssetId`, `MissingPrimaryAsset`, and `MultiplePrimaryAssets`. Implement `Display` and `std::error::Error` without an opaque catch-all.
- Preserve canonical aggregate equality and iteration regardless of input order by storing assets in `BTreeMap`; do not sort or mutate the caller's sequence for any other purpose.
- Re-export only the intentional types from `model/mod.rs` and `rusttable-core/src/lib.rs`. Keep fields private and expose no mutable map, interior mutability, or asset/photo mutation method in this PR.
- Use only the Rust standard library and retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Keep paths, path normalization, filesystem timestamps, EXIF/XMP/IPTC, ratings/tags, database IDs, UI selection, caches, thumbnails, pixel buffers, loader state, processing flags, codec types, random IDs, FFI, and inherited darktable structs/constants out of this PR.
- Keep every handwritten Rust file at or below 1,000 lines and deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test photo_asset_model --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `asset.rs` defines immutable `Asset`, the three documented `AssetRole` variants, opaque `ByteLength`, and algorithm-tagged `ContentHash::Sha256` without I/O or hashing behavior.
- `ByteLength::ZERO.get()` is `0`; representative values and `u64::MAX` round-trip exactly. The type does not infer validity from a codec or filesystem.
- `photo.rs` defines immutable `Photo` and typed `PhotoBuildError`; `Photo::new` always produces revision zero and `Photo::from_parts` preserves an explicitly supplied revision.
- A valid photo contains at least one asset and exactly one `Primary` role. `primary_asset_id()` is derived from and resolves to that asset; callers cannot supply a contradictory primary ID.
- Empty input, a repeated asset ID, no primary role, and multiple primary roles each return the documented distinct error variant without panic or partial aggregate construction.
- The multiple-primary error reports the same ascending set of IDs regardless of caller input order. Duplicate detection is deterministic and occurs before primary-role validation.
- Supplying the same unique assets in different orders produces equal `Photo` values and identical ascending `AssetId` iteration. `asset(id)` and `primary_asset()` return references into the canonical stored map.
- The model contains no path, clock, random generation, metadata parser, database, UI, image loader, pixel/processing state, FFI, C layout, or inherited darktable flag.
- Tests cover byte-length/hash access, valid construction, reconstruction, every validation branch, canonical equality/iteration, and deterministic error data with no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` shows no dependency. No unsafe Rust or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(core): model photos and immutable source assets deterministically

issues.body:
## Why

The 150-second PR gate in #14 must stay focused on fast, high-signal feedback, but every integrated `main` commit still needs a complete Rust validation result. The repository currently has no RustTable workflow for pushes to `main`; the inherited darktable workflow targets `master` and is not a substitute. Merge validation should re-run the fast policy on the integrated tree, add release-only and documentation coverage, and prove the Rust workspace remains portable across the supported desktop hosts without moving those costs into pull requests.

## How

- Add `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` triggered by every `push` to `main` plus `workflow_dispatch`. Do not add path filters, a schedule, or any pull-request trigger.
- Use read-only `contents: read` permission, no repository secret, and immutable full commit SHAs for every reusable action.
- Configure concurrency with the workflow name plus commit SHA and `cancel-in-progress: false`; separate `main` commits must never cancel or replace one another.
- Use exact Rust `1.95.0` from `rust-toolchain.toml` everywhere. Use exact Bun `1.3.14` only in the Linux full-validation job, where `scripts/pr-ci.sh` requires it. Make caches runner-OS-, toolchain-, and `Cargo.lock`-aware.
- Add a Linux `full` job on `ubuntu-latest` with a finite 20-minute timeout. Add `scripts/main-ci.sh` as its only repository-owned entry point.
- Make `scripts/main-ci.sh` run, in this order:
  1. `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh` to revalidate the exact fast gate against the integrated tree;
  2. `cargo test --workspace --doc --all-features --locked`;
  3. `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --release --locked`;
  4. `cargo build --workspace --all-targets --all-features --release --locked`;
  5. `RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --all-features --no-deps --locked`.
- Preserve useful command labels and stop with the failing command's nonzero status. Do not invoke inherited CMake or darktable build scripts.
- Add a `portable-tests` matrix with `macos-latest` and `windows-latest`, `fail-fast: false`, and a finite 20-minute timeout per job. Each matrix job must run `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked` using the repository toolchain and a platform-specific cache.
- Do not install Bun or run the macOS application installer in the portability matrix. The Bun unit tests are re-run by `scripts/pr-ci.sh` in the Linux full job.
- Keep performance budgets, dependency advisory/license/source policy, diagnostics, application-bundle CI, signing, notarization, installation, release publication, image fixtures, and end-to-end UI automation out of this issue. #29, #31, #24, and #43 extend this workflow or release-readiness path through separate one-issue PRs. Completed #30 is local Computer Use tooling and is not a blocker for this workflow foundation.
- Deliver this scope through exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Dependencies

- Depends on #14.
- Unblocks #29 and #24.
- #31 follows #29; final distribution issue #43 depends on both #31 and #24.
- This is the final issue in the milestone-#1 bootstrap sequence.

## Validation

- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `cargo test --workspace --doc --all-features --locked`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --release --locked`
- `cargo build --workspace --all-targets --all-features --release --locked`
- `RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --all-features --no-deps --locked`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/rust-main.yml`
- `git diff --check`
- Merge a test PR and inspect the workflow run for the resulting `main` SHA on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every push to `main` starts one `rust-main` workflow; pull-request synchronize/open/reopen events do not trigger it.
- Distinct `main` SHAs are never cancelled or collapsed, every job has a finite timeout, and the workflow uses only read-only repository permission and immutable action SHAs.
- The Linux full job re-runs `scripts/pr-ci.sh`, doctests, release-profile all-target/all-feature tests, a release build, and warning-denied documentation using exact Rust `1.95.0`, exact Bun `1.3.14`, and locked dependency resolution.
- macOS and Windows each run locked all-target/all-feature workspace tests with the exact Rust toolchain and do not install Bun, invoke CMake, or execute the macOS installer.
- A temporary warning-denied rustdoc failure, a doctest failure, a release-only compile/test failure, and a platform-specific test failure each fail the applicable job; the verification commits are reverted before merge.
- The workflow compiles no inherited C/C++ target, publishes no artifact or release, uses no signing/notarization secret, and adds no performance, security-policy, diagnostics, packaging, or end-to-end UI scope.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: ci: add heavyweight validation for every merge to main

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable needs a deterministic domain representation for one non-destructive edit operation before an `Edit` aggregate or processing engine exists. The inherited history stack combines module pointers, mutable parameter blobs, processing order, masks, UI names, and runtime state. Reproducing that shape would couple the clean Rust domain to plugins, C layouts, and execution policy.

This PR defines only validated operation vocabulary and immutable operation data. Authoring history belongs to #38; typed processing compilation/order belongs to #22/#23. Separating those concerns keeps each issue mapped to one reviewable PR.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #16; the photo/asset aggregate lands first so the domain sequence is linear and each PR starts from integrated core types.
- Uses `OperationId` and `FiniteF64` from #13.
- **Milestone #3 sequence: 4 of 7.**
- Blocks #38, which builds the immutable `Edit` aggregate.
- Independently blocks #22, which compiles a core `Operation` into a typed processing operation without changing this representation.

## How

- Write the failing integration tests first in `crates/rusttable-core/tests/operation_model.rs` and record the initial failing command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add:
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/edit/mod.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/edit/operation.rs`.
- In `operation.rs`, define opaque validated string values with private storage:
  - `OperationKey`;
  - `ParameterName`;
  - `ParameterText`.
- Define the exact `OperationKey` grammar:
  - ASCII only;
  - total UTF-8 byte length at most 128;
  - two through four `.`-separated nonempty segments;
  - each segment at most 32 bytes;
  - each segment begins with `[a-z]` and subsequent bytes are `[a-z0-9_]`.
- Define the exact `ParameterName` grammar:
  - ASCII only and 1 through 64 bytes;
  - begins with `[a-z]` and subsequent bytes are `[a-z0-9_]`;
  - no dots, whitespace, hyphens, uppercase, or implicit normalization.
- Define `ParameterText` as validated UTF-8 text that:
  - preserves the supplied text byte-for-byte;
  - permits the empty string;
  - rejects embedded NUL;
  - rejects values longer than 4,096 UTF-8 bytes.
- Provide explicit checked constructors/`TryFrom` implementations, `as_str()`, canonical `Display`, and deterministic `Clone`, `Debug`, `Eq`, `Ord`, and `Hash` where applicable.
- Define matchable validation errors with variants that distinguish length, segment-count/empty-segment, invalid initial byte, invalid subsequent byte, non-ASCII input, and embedded NUL as applicable. Include the relevant zero-based byte/segment position in observable error data.
- Define the closed enum:
  - `ParameterValue::Bool(bool)`;
  - `ParameterValue::Integer(i64)`;
  - `ParameterValue::Scalar(FiniteF64)`;
  - `ParameterValue::Text(ParameterText)`.
- Define immutable `Operation` with private:
  - `OperationId`;
  - `OperationKey`;
  - enabled state;
  - `BTreeMap<ParameterName, ParameterValue>`.
- Provide `Operation::new(id, key, enabled, parameters)`. Permit zero parameters. Detect a repeated `ParameterName` while consuming the caller's sequence and return `OperationBuildError::DuplicateParameterName { name }`; do not silently retain first/last values.
- Expose read-only `id()`, `key()`, `is_enabled()`, `parameter(name)`, and ascending `parameters()` access. Supplying the same unique parameters in different orders must produce equal operations and identical canonical iteration.
- Re-export only the intentional operation types from `edit/mod.rs` and `rusttable-core/src/lib.rs`.
- Do not add `edit.rs` or an `Edit` aggregate in this PR. Do not assign processing positions, interpret operation keys, enforce operation-specific parameter schemas, execute pixels, reorder operations, serialize/persist data, implement undo/redo, or include masks/UI state.
- Use only the Rust standard library; retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Add no function pointers, trait objects for plugins, dynamic loading, raw byte parameter blobs, FFI, C headers/layout assumptions, or copied darktable operation/module definitions.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test operation_model --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `operation.rs` defines validated `OperationKey`, `ParameterName`, `ParameterText`, closed `ParameterValue`, immutable `Operation`, and typed validation/build errors; no `Edit` aggregate is added.
- `rusttable.exposure` and `rusttable.linear_offset` are valid keys. Empty/single-segment/five-segment keys, empty segments, uppercase, non-ASCII, whitespace, hyphens, invalid leading digits/underscores, overlong segments, and overlong total keys return the documented observable errors.
- `stops` and `linear_offset_2` are valid parameter names. Empty, dotted, uppercase, non-ASCII, whitespace/hyphenated, digit/underscore-leading, and over-64-byte names are rejected without normalization.
- `ParameterText` preserves valid Unicode/empty text exactly, rejects an embedded NUL and 4,097-byte value, and accepts a 4,096-byte boundary value.
- `ParameterValue::Scalar` can contain only an already validated `FiniteF64`; raw NaN/infinity and untyped byte blobs are unrepresentable through the public API.
- `Operation::new` accepts an empty parameter list and preserves ID, key, and enabled state exactly. A repeated parameter name returns the exact duplicate and no partial operation.
- Equal unique parameter sets supplied in different orders produce equal `Operation` values and identical ascending parameter iteration. The enabled flag remains observable and does not remove the operation or its parameters.
- #22 can consume the exact public `Operation`/parameter API to recognize `rusttable.exposure` and `rusttable.linear_offset` without adding processing behavior to core.
- Tests cover every grammar boundary/error category, duplicate detection, canonical ordering/equality, accessors, and enabled state with no network, filesystem, clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` shows no dependency. No unsafe Rust, FFI/C/C++ bridge, plugin handle, serialization schema, processing code, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(core): define validated operation values and immutable operations

issues.body:
## Why

The pure core types need a deterministic non-UI boundary that registers photos and initial edits without leaking Iced, persistence, filesystem, codec, processing, or adapter errors into the domain. Initial state construction has its own invariant set: unique identities, valid photo references, current base-photo revisions, revision-zero edits, optimistic catalog revision checks, and atomic failure.

Replacement/lost-update behavior is deliberately split into #41. Keeping registration separate prevents one PR from mixing state construction, insert errors, edit replacement, and two levels of optimistic concurrency.

## Dependencies

- Uses the `rusttable-catalog -> rusttable-core` boundary completed by #10 / PR #44.
- Blocked by #38, which provides immutable edits with base-photo and edit revisions.
- Transitively uses identifiers/value objects from #13, photos/assets from #16, and operations from #18.
- **Milestone #3 sequence: 6 of 7.**
- Blocks #41, the final optimistic edit-replacement issue.

## How

- Write failing integration tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_registration.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_errors.rs`.
- Record the initial failing targeted commands/output in the implementation PR before adding production state behavior.
- Add:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/command.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/state.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/error.rs`.
- Expose the intentional surface from `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/lib.rs` without exposing internal maps or helper modules.
- Define `CatalogState` with private:
  - catalog `Revision`;
  - `BTreeMap<PhotoId, Photo>`;
  - `BTreeMap<EditId, Edit>`.
- Provide `CatalogState::new()`/`Default` at `Revision::ZERO` and read-only:
  - `revision()`;
  - `photo(id)` and ascending `photos()`;
  - `edit(id)` and ascending `edits()`.
- Define a closed `CatalogCommand` with exactly two variants in this PR:
  - `RegisterPhoto(Photo)`;
  - `CreateEdit(Edit)`.
- Implement one mutation entry point:
  - `CatalogState::apply(expected_catalog_revision, command) -> Result<Revision, CatalogError>`.
- Validate the caller's expected catalog revision before command-specific validation. A mismatch returns `CatalogRevisionConflict { expected, actual }` and performs no command work.
- Validate `RegisterPhoto` in this order:
  1. reject an existing `PhotoId` even when the supplied value is equal;
  2. ensure the catalog revision has a checked successor;
  3. insert exactly one photo and publish the successor revision.
- Validate `CreateEdit` in this order:
  1. reject an existing `EditId` even when the supplied value is equal;
  2. require the referenced `PhotoId` to exist;
  3. require the edit revision to equal `Revision::ZERO`;
  4. require `edit.base_photo_revision()` to equal the current stored `Photo::revision()`;
  5. ensure the catalog revision has a checked successor;
  6. insert exactly one edit and publish the successor revision.
- Define public, matchable `CatalogError` variants containing relevant IDs/revisions:
  - `CatalogRevisionConflict`;
  - `CatalogRevisionOverflow`;
  - `DuplicatePhoto`;
  - `UnknownPhoto`;
  - `DuplicateEdit`;
  - `InvalidInitialEditRevision`;
  - `EditBasePhotoRevisionConflict`.
- Implement stable `Display` and `std::error::Error` without `anyhow::Error`, `Box<dyn Error>`, string-only kinds, or an opaque catch-all.
- Validate the complete command and calculate the next catalog revision before mutating either map. On every error, every field and iterator result must remain equal to the pre-call state.
- Derive/implement deterministic `Clone`, `Debug`, and `Eq` for `CatalogState` so failure atomicity and replay can be asserted directly.
- Test catalog-revision overflow in a focused `#[cfg(test)]` module in `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/state.rs`; do not expose an invalid-state or test-only public constructor solely to reach the private max-revision fixture.
- Keep the state in-memory and single-threaded. Do not add replacement/update/delete commands, persistence/repository traits, database transactions, serialization, locks, atomics, threads, async runtimes, event buses, callbacks, Iced messages, file paths, codecs, processing, or global state.
- Retain `rusttable-core` as the only catalog normal dependency, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_registration --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog state::tests --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `command.rs` exposes exactly `RegisterPhoto` and `CreateEdit`; no replacement, delete, persistence, or UI command is added.
- `state.rs` contains deterministic `CatalogState::apply` behavior and read-only lookup/ascending iterators; the maps remain private and no second mutation entry point exists.
- A new state is empty at revision zero. Registering one photo at the matching expected revision inserts that exact photo, returns revision one, and leaves the edit map empty.
- Registering an existing `PhotoId` returns `DuplicatePhoto`; a stale expected catalog revision returns `CatalogRevisionConflict`; catalog overflow returns `CatalogRevisionOverflow`. Each leaves the complete state unchanged.
- Creating a valid revision-zero edit for an existing photo with a matching base-photo revision inserts that exact edit and advances the catalog revision exactly once.
- Creating an edit with a duplicate `EditId`, unknown `PhotoId`, nonzero initial edit revision, or stale/future base-photo revision returns the exact documented error variant and leaves the complete state unchanged.
- Validation precedence follows the documented order, including cases where a command violates multiple conditions; tests assert the selected variant rather than accepting any error.
- Equal command sequences applied to equal initial states produce equal final states, equal revisions, and identical ascending photo/edit iteration independent of registration input order.
- Every failed-command test clones the complete pre-call state and proves equality after the failure; no partial insert or revision advance is observable.
- Public error variants are matchable without parsing strings; `Display` is stable/useful and no adapter-specific or opaque error type crosses the crate boundary.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal` shows only `rusttable-core`. No UI, I/O, persistence, database, codec, processing, FFI, async/synchronization dependency, unsafe Rust, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- Tests use no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures. The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(catalog): add deterministic catalog registration state

issues.body:
## Status

Duplicate of #13, which already defines the milestone-#3 domain identifiers and scalar value-object PR.

No implementation PR should be opened for this issue. Continue with #13 after #10 is complete.
issues.title: duplicate: typed domain identifiers and deterministic value objects

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable needs one real, safe image-ingress slice before metadata and import behavior can be built. Codec types and defaults must not leak into `rusttable-core` or `rusttable-catalog`, and a dependency upgrade must not silently expand the product’s supported formats. The first slice should prove explicit JPEG/PNG probing and decoding with bounded source reads and checked decoded-buffer invariants, without inheriting darktable loaders, C structs, headers, fixtures, or control flow.

This issue deliberately introduces a codec-neutral image boundary plus one concrete adapter in the same PR so the abstraction is exercised by observable behavior rather than left speculative. Metadata interpretation, persistence, processing, and export remain separate issues.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #41, the final milestone-#3 catalog-state issue; this transitively includes #10, #13, #16, #18, #38, and #19.
- Uses the strict workspace and completed crate-boundary foundation from #4 / PR #7 and #10 / PR #44.
- Independent of the iced application sequence and must not modify `crates/rusttable-app`.
- **Milestone #4 sequence: 1 of 6 and the best first issue in this milestone.**
- Blocks #28 and #32. #33 reuses the checked image values introduced here.

## How

- Write the failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-image` as a root workspace member with no normal third-party dependency, inherited workspace lints, and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Add these codec-neutral modules:
  - `crates/rusttable-image/src/format.rs` with `InputFormat::{Jpeg, Png}` and a stable `SUPPORTED_INPUT_FORMATS` constant containing exactly those two variants;
  - `crates/rusttable-image/src/image.rs` with checked `ImageDimensions`, fixed `PixelLayout::Rgba8StraightAlpha`, explicit `ColorEncoding::Unspecified`, and immutable `DecodedImage`;
  - `crates/rusttable-image/src/input.rs` with checked `DecodeLimits`, immutable `ImageProbe`, object-safe `ImageInput`, and typed `ImageInputError`;
  - `crates/rusttable-image/src/lib.rs` with only deliberate public re-exports.
- Define `ImageDimensions` with nonzero `u32` width/height and checked `u64` pixel-count/decoded-byte calculations. `DecodedImage::new` must require exactly `width * height * 4` bytes and reject arithmetic overflow or length mismatch.
- Do not label decoded samples as sRGB. This slice does not interpret ICC profiles, PNG color chunks, JPEG color metadata, gamma, or transfer functions, so `ColorEncoding::Unspecified` must remain observable.
- Define `DecodeLimits` with explicit nonzero bounds for:
  - maximum source-file bytes;
  - maximum width;
  - maximum height;
  - maximum pixel count;
  - maximum decoded RGBA bytes.
  Construction must reject internally inconsistent or non-representable limits. The concrete adapter must require limits at construction; do not expose an unbounded default.
- Define `ImageProbe` to report only `InputFormat` and checked `ImageDimensions`. Define object-safe methods equivalent to `probe_path(&Path)` and `decode_path(&Path)` returning only RustTable/standard-library types.
- Define stable, matchable error categories for source-too-large, I/O, unsupported signature, malformed input, width/height limit, pixel limit, decoded-byte limit, arithmetic overflow, allocation failure, and decoded-buffer invariant failure. Do not expose `image::ImageError`, `std::io::Error`, or boxed/opaque errors in the public API.
- Add `crates/rusttable-image-io` as a root workspace adapter crate depending normally on `rusttable-image` and a reviewed `image` release. Use `default-features = false` and enable only the explicit JPEG and PNG decoder features; do not enable the default format bundle or Rayon.
- Implement `FileImageInput` in:
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/src/lib.rs` for the intentional concrete surface;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/src/input.rs` for bounded reads, signature dispatch, probing, decoding, and error translation.
- Read at most `max_source_bytes + 1` bytes through a checked bounded reader and return `SourceTooLarge` before codec dispatch when the limit is exceeded. Do not rely on filesystem metadata alone and do not let the codec perform an unbounded whole-file read.
- Detect format from an explicit JPEG/PNG signature allowlist, never from the path extension. Dispatch with an explicit `InputFormat`; never call a generic “guess any supported format” path that could widen behavior when dependencies change.
- Apply width/height limits to the decoder before decoded allocation where the codec API permits it, and independently enforce checked width, height, pixel-count, and decoded-byte limits before constructing `DecodedImage`. Treat any dependency allocation limit as defense in depth rather than the sole product limit.
- Normalize successful decoding to packed RGBA8 straight alpha:
  - PNG preserves decoded alpha samples;
  - JPEG produces alpha `255` for every pixel;
  - no orientation, premultiplication, ICC, gamma, tone, or color-space transform is performed.
- Add or update `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/crate_boundaries.rs` so the architecture contract recognizes the new graph without weakening existing rules:
  - `rusttable-core` remains free of normal third-party dependencies;
  - `rusttable-catalog` still depends normally only on `rusttable-core` in this PR;
  - `rusttable-image` has no normal third-party dependency and no dependency on app/catalog/core;
  - `rusttable-image-io -> rusttable-image`, with the codec dependency confined below the adapter;
  - no RustTable crate links inherited C/C++, CMake, bindgen, GTK, a database, or a processing backend.
- Add failing-first tests in:
  - `crates/rusttable-image/tests/input_contract.rs` for dimensions, limits, decoded-buffer invariants, and object-safe fake input behavior;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/jpeg_png_decode.rs` for the two supported formats, dimensions, layout, PNG samples, and JPEG opaque alpha;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/input_rejections.rs` for unsupported, unknown, truncated, malformed, and extension/signature disagreement;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/decode_limits.rs` for source-byte, width, height, pixel, and decoded-byte limits.
- Keep tiny clean-room fixtures under `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/fixtures/`: a reviewed 2x1 RGBA PNG, a reviewed 2x1 JPEG, a minimal unsupported GIF, and a truncated PNG. Generate or author them specifically for RustTable; do not copy darktable fixtures or tests.
- Do not add EXIF/XMP/IPTC interpretation, orientation application, ICC/gamma transforms, RAW/TIFF/WebP/GIF/BMP/HEIF/AVIF support, catalog writes, hashing, thumbnails, caching, async work, UI behavior, processing buffers, or output encoding in this PR.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --test input_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test jpeg_png_decode --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test input_rejections --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test decode_limits --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image-io --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `rusttable-image` exposes codec-neutral `InputFormat`, `ImageDimensions`, `PixelLayout`, `ColorEncoding`, `DecodedImage`, `DecodeLimits`, `ImageProbe`, `ImageInput`, and typed errors; it imports no `image` crate type and has no normal third-party dependency.
- `SUPPORTED_INPUT_FORMATS` contains exactly JPEG then PNG in a documented stable order. No additional format is accepted merely because the codec dependency supports it.
- The reviewed PNG decodes to 2x1, `Rgba8StraightAlpha`, `ColorEncoding::Unspecified`, and the exact reviewed RGBA bytes. The reviewed JPEG decodes to 2x1 and every output alpha byte is `255`; tests do not assert lossless or cross-version-identical JPEG RGB samples.
- A PNG payload under a `.jpg` path is probed/decoded as PNG, while GIF data under a `.png` path returns the exact unsupported-signature error. Extensions never select product behavior.
- A source one byte over `max_source_bytes` is rejected before codec decode. Width, height, pixel-count, and decoded-byte limits each have a focused fixture/fake that returns the exact typed limit error without panic or oversized allocation.
- `DecodedImage` cannot be constructed with zero dimensions, arithmetic overflow, or a byte length other than `width * height * 4`.
- Unknown, GIF, TIFF, WebP, BMP, RAW-like, HEIF/AVIF-like, truncated, and malformed payloads return stable typed errors and never fall through to a generic dependency format guesser.
- No API or test claims decoded JPEG/PNG samples are sRGB; profile, gamma, orientation, and transfer-function interpretation remain explicitly absent.
- `cargo tree` and the boundary test show codec dependencies only below `rusttable-image-io`; `rusttable-core` and `rusttable-catalog` retain their existing normal dependency contracts in this PR.
- No local unsafe Rust, FFI, C/C++ build target, inherited darktable loader/fixture/code, networked test, sleep, wall-clock dependency, randomness, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(image-io): add a bounded JPEG/PNG image-input slice

issues.body:
## Why

Issue #18 deliberately stores one non-destructive operation as generic, deterministic domain data: a validated operation key, enabled state, and canonical typed parameter map. That representation is appropriate for catalog/history state, but a renderer must not repeatedly interpret strings, inspect heterogeneous values, or narrow floating-point precision inside the pixel loop. Milestone #5 therefore needs one explicit compilation boundary that validates an operation schema once and produces small, closed, executable processing data.

This PR also establishes the finite `f32` scalar used by the first CPU processing path. Keeping domain parameters as `FiniteF64` while narrowing them explicitly at compilation preserves the core model without committing image buffers and hot-loop operation data to 64-bit samples and their doubled storage.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #18, which defines the immutable core `Operation`/parameter API.
- Uses `OperationId` and `FiniteF64` from completed #13. The crate boundary in #10 and photo/asset model in #16 are already integrated on `main` and are not remaining blockers.
- **Milestone #5 sequence: 1 of 4 and the best first issue in this milestone.**
- Blocks #23.
- Independent of catalog state in #19, edit aggregation in #38, image input in #21, and all iced/UI work. This issue must not modify `crates/rusttable-app` or require `rusttable-catalog`.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-processing` as a root workspace member with inherited workspace metadata/lints and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Give `rusttable-processing` exactly one dependency of any kind: the normal path dependency `rusttable-core`. Add no normal, build, or dev third-party dependency in this PR.
- Update the root `Cargo.lock` through Cargo. Do not hand-edit the lockfile.
- Add these production files:
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/src/lib.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/src/scalar.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/src/operation.rs`.
- In `scalar.rs`, define opaque `FiniteF32` with:
  - `FiniteF32::new(f32)` rejecting NaN and both infinities with typed `FiniteF32Error`;
  - normalization of negative zero to positive zero;
  - a read-only `get()` accessor;
  - deterministic equality, total ordering, and hashing for every constructible value;
  - `TryFrom<rusttable_core::FiniteF64>` using Rust's `as f32` conversion followed by validation. Return `ScalarNarrowingError::Overflow` when the converted value is not finite and `ScalarNarrowingError::Underflow` when a nonzero source converts to either signed zero; normalize an exact source zero to positive zero. No narrowing occurs implicitly elsewhere.
- In `operation.rs`, define immutable `ProcessingOperation { operation_id, enabled, kind }` and a closed `ProcessingOperationKind` with exactly:
  - `Exposure { stops: FiniteF32 }`, compiled from key `rusttable.exposure` and exactly one scalar parameter `stops`;
  - `LinearOffset { value: FiniteF32 }`, compiled from key `rusttable.linear_offset` and exactly one scalar parameter `value`.
- Add the explicit entry point `ProcessingOperation::compile(&rusttable_core::Operation) -> Result<Self, OperationCompileError>`. Preserve `OperationId` and enabled state exactly. Do not execute or reorder the operation.
- Define public, matchable `OperationCompileError` variants for:
  - unsupported operation key;
  - missing required parameter;
  - unexpected parameter;
  - wrong parameter type;
  - scalar narrowing overflow;
  - scalar narrowing underflow.
  Every variant retains the source `OperationId`; key errors retain the operation key; parameter-related variants retain the relevant parameter name. Implement `Display`, `std::error::Error`, deterministic equality, and no opaque catch-all.
- Make schema-error selection deterministic: after matching the operation key, report a missing required name first, then the lexicographically first unexpected name from the core operation's canonical parameter order, then type/narrowing failures for the required parameter. Never rely on hash-map iteration or whichever validation happens to be coded first.
- Write failing tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/processing_scalar.rs` for finite construction, non-finite rejection, negative-zero normalization, equality/order/hash behavior, exact narrowing, overflow, and underflow;
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/operation_compilation.rs` for both supported schemas, identity/enabled preservation, every error variant, deterministic error precedence, and equal-input/equal-output behavior;
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/crate_boundaries.rs`, invoking `cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1`, for the intended dependency graph. Reuse/adapt the standard-library metadata-object parsing pattern already on `main` in `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/crate_boundaries.rs`; do not add a JSON/dependency-analysis crate.
- Keep edit-wide ordering, pixel/image types, color transforms, evaluation, serialization, persistence, masks, dynamic plugins, UI, image decoding, filesystem access, async runtimes, GPU/SIMD code, FFI, and inherited darktable module structures out of this PR.

## Validation

- `cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test processing_scalar --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test operation_compilation --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, and `crates/rusttable-processing/Cargo.toml` add the processing crate through Cargo. `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` shows only `rusttable-core`, and Cargo metadata shows no build/dev third-party dependency. The manifests/source of `rusttable-core`, `rusttable-catalog`, and `rusttable-app` are not changed to depend on processing.
- `crates/rusttable-processing/src/scalar.rs` exposes no representable NaN, infinity, or negative zero. Tests named `rejects_non_finite_values`, `normalizes_negative_zero`, `orders_and_hashes_equal_values_consistently`, `narrows_exact_values`, `rejects_narrowing_overflow`, and `rejects_narrowing_underflow` pass.
- `TryFrom<FiniteF64>` is the only public domain-to-processing scalar conversion. `normalizes_narrowed_zero` proves exact source zero produces positive processing zero; nonzero underflow is never silently converted to zero.
- `crates/rusttable-processing/src/operation.rs` defines immutable `ProcessingOperation`, closed `ProcessingOperationKind`, and typed `OperationCompileError` values with no raw parameter map or `FiniteF64` in the compiled representation.
