- Tests named `compiles_exposure`, `compiles_linear_offset`, and `preserves_identity_and_enabled_state` prove the two exact schemas and source-field preservation against the public #18 API.
- Tests named `rejects_unsupported_key`, `rejects_missing_parameter`, `rejects_unexpected_parameter`, `rejects_wrong_parameter_type`, `rejects_scalar_overflow`, and `rejects_scalar_underflow` match the exact public error variants and retained context without string parsing.
- `equal_operations_compile_equally` proves equal core operations produce equal processing operations; `selects_schema_errors_deterministically` proves mixed-invalid input produces the documented stable error precedence.
- `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/crate_boundaries.rs` fails when a forbidden dependency on iced, catalog, a codec, filesystem helper, database, async runtime, GPU library, FFI/bindgen layer, or inherited C target is introduced.
- No operation is ordered or executed, no pixels are allocated, and no color-management or decoder behavior is introduced in this issue.
- No unsafe Rust is introduced; no handwritten Rust file exceeds 1,000 lines; tests use no network, filesystem fixtures, wall clock, randomness, or sleeps.
- The implementation PR records the initial failing focused test command/output, fits one focused worktree, and is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR containing `Closes #22` and closing no other issue.
issues.title: feat(processing): represent executable operations and compile edit intent

issues.body:
## Why

#38 makes operation authoring order and base-photo provenance explicit in immutable `Edit`, while #22 compiles one generic domain operation into closed processing data. The next boundary must compile the entire edit atomically while preserving authoritative order, source identities, the exact photo revision the edit was authored against, and the edit revision. Ordering must not depend on map iteration, operation key, operation ID, enabled filtering, or platform-specific sort behavior.

A pipeline index is an in-memory diagnostic value, not a persistence identifier. This issue therefore uses a typed zero-based `usize` index and tests exact sequence preservation rather than inventing an unreachable integer-overflow path.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by both #22 and #38: #22 supplies `ProcessingOperation`; #38 supplies immutable `Edit`, exact authoring order, base-photo revision, and edit revision.
- Uses `EditId`, `PhotoId`, `OperationId`, and `Revision` from `rusttable-core`.
- **Milestone #5 sequence: 2 of 4.**
- Blocks #26.
- Does not depend on catalog state, image I/O, UI, persistence, or a renderer.

## How

- Write failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/pipeline_order.rs` and record the initial red command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-processing/src/pipeline.rs` and re-export only the intended public types from `crates/rusttable-processing/src/lib.rs`.
- Define opaque `PipelineStepIndex(usize)` with a zero-based `get()` accessor. It is an in-memory diagnostic/indexing value only; do not serialize it or treat it as a stable database identifier.
- Define immutable `PipelineStep { index, operation }`, where `operation` is the `ProcessingOperation` from #22.
- Define immutable `CompiledPipeline` with private fields for:
  - source `EditId`;
  - source `PhotoId`;
  - source base-photo `Revision`;
  - source edit `Revision`;
  - `Vec<PipelineStep>` in executable order.
- Add `CompiledPipeline::compile(&rusttable_core::Edit) -> Result<Self, PipelineCompileError>`.
- Enumerate `Edit::operations()` exactly once and compile every operation with #22 in the same order. The operation at source index `N` must always become pipeline step index `N`.
- Retain disabled operations in `steps()` so provenance and indices remain stable. Provide `active_steps()` as a borrowed filtering iterator that omits disabled operations without allocating, renumbering, sorting, or changing the relative order of enabled steps.
- Preserve `EditId`, `PhotoId`, `base_photo_revision`, and edit `Revision` exactly; do not synthesize, increment, or reinterpret revisions during compilation.
- Treat construction as atomic. If any operation fails to compile, return no `CompiledPipeline` and expose no partially compiled steps.
- Define public, matchable `PipelineCompileError` containing source `EditId`, failing `PipelineStepIndex`, failing `OperationId`, and the exact `OperationCompileError` from #22. Implement `Display`, `std::error::Error`, and `source()` without reducing the nested error to text.
- Permit an edit with zero operations to compile successfully with preserved complete provenance and empty `steps()`/`active_steps()` iterators.
- Keep pixel/image types, color spaces/transforms, operation execution, render results, fingerprints/cache keys, serialization, persistence, undo/redo, masks, threads, async runtimes, GPU/SIMD work, UI, decoder behavior, and inherited darktable ordering tables out of this PR.
- Retain strict lints, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, the existing `rusttable-processing -> rusttable-core` dependency direction, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test pipeline_order --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-processing/src/pipeline.rs` defines opaque `PipelineStepIndex`, immutable `PipelineStep`, immutable `CompiledPipeline`, and typed `PipelineCompileError`; no public mutable step collection is exposed.
- `compiles_empty_edit_with_provenance` proves an empty edit produces empty all/active iterators while preserving `EditId`, `PhotoId`, base-photo revision, and edit revision.
- `preserves_authoring_order_and_indices` proves an edit `[A, B, C]` compiles to operation IDs `[A, B, C]` with indices `[0, 1, 2]` on repeated compilations.
- `different_authoring_order_produces_different_pipeline` proves `[A, B]` and `[B, A]` remain observably different; operation key, operation ID, parameter order, and enabled state never act as implicit sort keys.
- `retains_disabled_steps` proves disabled operations remain in `steps()` at their source indices.
- `active_steps_filter_without_renumbering` proves a source sequence with active indices `[0, 2, 4]` yields those exact indices and relative order without allocation-visible replacement data or renumbering.
- `reports_exact_failing_step_atomically` proves a failure at source index `N` reports the source edit ID, index `N`, operation ID, and exact nested #22 error, and the API returns no partial pipeline.
- `equal_edits_compile_equally` proves equal edits produce equal pipelines, including equal complete provenance and step data.
- Edits differing only in base-photo revision or edit revision compile to observably different pipelines while preserving identical operation order/data.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` shows only `rusttable-core`; this PR adds no new normal dependency.
- No pixels, color-management behavior, rendering, UI, decoder, filesystem, database, serialization, FFI, async runtime, GPU/SIMD path, or inherited darktable processing-order implementation is introduced.
- No unsafe Rust is introduced; no handwritten Rust file exceeds 1,000 lines; tests use no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(processing): compile deterministic pipelines from edit authoring order

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable has strict fast-feedback budgets but no product-performance contract. Ordinary unit tests can prove correctness while deterministic domain construction or CPU rendering regresses by an order of magnitude. Timing-sensitive checks are also inappropriate for pre-commit, pre-push, and pull-request CI because those paths must stay below 30, 60, and 150 seconds.

The first performance PR should therefore add a stable-Rust, single-process benchmark executable with reviewed budgets, fast non-timing tests for the budget machinery, and a merge-to-main gate. It must measure real RustTable APIs rather than inherited darktable code or synthetic placeholder functions, and it must be manifest-isolated so the repository’s existing `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` command never executes timed work.

## Dependencies

- Depends on #40 for the portable hard-deadline/process-tree cleanup contract in `scripts/with-validation-budget.sh`; #40 itself follows completed #8 / PR #35.
- Depends on #17 for `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` and the merge-only workflow foundation.
- Depends on #16 for immutable photo/asset construction.
- Depends on #27 for the first pure in-memory render evaluator; this transitively includes #22, #23, and #26.
- **Milestone #6 sequence: 3 of 4.** It is independently prerequisite-gated and does not block #29 or #31.
- Blocks final distribution issue #43; #43 must not declare the initial release-readiness slice complete until both #24 and #31 are closed.

## How

- Add an explicit stable benchmark target to `crates/rusttable-processing/Cargo.toml`:
  - `[[bench]]`
  - `name = "performance_budgets"`
  - `harness = false`
  - `bench = false`
  - `test = false`
- `bench = false` is intentional: generic/default benchmark selection, including the `--benches` component of `cargo test --all-targets`, must not select or execute this target. The merge-only command must name it explicitly with `cargo bench --bench performance_budgets`, which overrides default target selection. `test = false` is defense in depth for default test selection.
- Do not require nightly Rust, unstable `#[bench]`, Criterion, a statistics crate, an async runtime, or a network service.
- Add `crates/rusttable-processing/benches/performance_budgets.rs` as the executable entry point and split reusable code under `crates/rusttable-processing/benches/support/` before any handwritten file approaches 1,000 lines.
- Add root `performance/budgets.tsv` with a versioned, strict tab-separated schema containing exactly one row per case: case name, warm-up count, sample count, measured work units, calibration iterations, and maximum normalized p95 in integer parts-per-million. Reject unknown columns, duplicate cases, missing cases, zero counts, non-integer values, trailing fields, and unsupported schema versions.
- Add exactly two initial cases:
  1. `photo_build_and_iterate_128_assets`: prepare deterministic IDs/content hashes outside the timed region; in each measured iteration construct one valid #16 `Photo` containing exactly 128 assets and consume every asset ID/byte length in canonical order through `std::hint::black_box`.
  2. `render_256x256_two_step_pipeline`: prepare one deterministic 256×256 `WorkingRgbImage` and one compiled exposure-then-linear-offset pipeline outside the timed region; in each measured iteration call the #27 evaluator and consume all 65,536 output pixels and every channel through `black_box`.
- Use fixed fixtures and one OS thread. Do not use random input, filesystem/image fixtures, sleeps, network, GPU/SIMD dispatch, inherited C/C++ code, environment-dependent work sizes, or mutable global state.
- For every case:
  - execute the configured warm-ups without recording them;
  - collect the configured number of workload samples with `std::time::Instant`;
  - pair each workload sample with the same fixed integer-calibration loop measured immediately adjacent to it;
  - compute integer normalized ratios as workload nanoseconds per declared work unit divided by calibration nanoseconds per calibration iteration, scaled to parts-per-million with checked `u128` arithmetic;
  - compute raw median, raw nearest-rank p95, calibration p95, and normalized nearest-rank p95 without floating-point arithmetic;
  - fail on a zero-duration/calibration sample, arithmetic overflow, missing output consumption, or normalized p95 above the committed limit.
- Emit one stable machine-readable line per case in this shape:
  `RUSTTABLE_PERF_V1 case=<name> samples=<n> work_units=<n> raw_median_ns=<n> raw_p95_ns=<n> calibration_p95_ns=<n> normalized_p95_ppm=<n> limit_ppm=<n> status=<pass|fail>`.
  Emit a final summary line and return nonzero if any case or configuration check fails.
- The custom harness must accept exactly one Cargo-injected leading `--bench` argument, then support only `--check` (default), `--list`, and `--config <path>`. Reject a missing/duplicate/non-leading Cargo sentinel and unknown, repeated, or conflicting user arguments. `--list` must parse/validate the file and print the two case names without executing timed work.
- Add `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/performance_budget_contract.rs`, reusing the non-timing parser/statistics module from `benches/support/`. Fast deterministic tests must cover valid parsing, every malformed-config branch, exact nearest-rank median/p95 examples, normalization/overflow behavior, case-set equality, Cargo `--bench` sentinel parsing, user CLI parsing, stable line formatting, pass-at-limit, and fail-one-unit-over-limit. These tests must not call `Instant`.
- Select the initial committed limits from five independent release-mode runs on the same documented Linux host class after one discarded warm-cache run. For each case set the limit to the ceiling of 1.5× the slowest observed normalized p95, record all five observations and the calculation in the PR body, and never update a limit automatically during CI.
- Add `scripts/performance-budgets.sh` as the sole repository-owned execution path. It must create a clean `target/performance/` and invoke #40’s helper with label `performance-budgets`, a 300-second deadline, and a child shell equivalent to:
  `set -o pipefail; cargo bench -p rusttable-processing --bench performance_budgets --locked -- --check 2>&1 | tee target/performance/results.log`.
  Preserve the benchmark/Cargo exit status through `tee`; return exactly `124` when the hard deadline expires; and rely on #40’s helper to terminate/reap the benchmark process tree so no compiler, benchmark, or watchdog remains.
- Add a workflow-level finite timeout larger than 300 seconds as defense in depth for setup/action failures outside the repository helper. The measured benchmark command itself must complete at or below 300 seconds.
- Extend the existing `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` with one Linux `performance-budgets` job triggered only by `push` to `main` and `workflow_dispatch`. Use read-only contents permission, immutable full-SHA actions, Rust from `rust-toolchain.toml`, a cache keyed by toolchain/`Cargo.lock`, `timeout-minutes: 10`, and upload `target/performance/results.log` under `if: always()`.
- Do not add `cargo bench`, the timing harness, or release-profile measured work to `scripts/precommit-fast.sh`, `scripts/prepush-fast.sh`, `scripts/pr-ci.sh`, or `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml`. Only the non-timing contract tests run through normal workspace tests. `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked` must not print a `RUSTTABLE_PERF_V1` line or execute either measured case.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict compiler/Clippy policy, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit. This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test performance_budget_contract --locked`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `cargo bench -p rusttable-processing --bench performance_budgets --locked --no-run`
- `cargo bench -p rusttable-processing --bench performance_budgets --locked -- --list`
- `bash scripts/performance-budgets.sh`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/rust-main.yml`
- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- The manifest contains the exact named `harness = false`, `bench = false`, `test = false` benchmark target; an explicit named `cargo bench --bench performance_budgets` invocation still builds/runs it.
- From a clean dedicated target directory, `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked` executes no measured workload, prints no `RUSTTABLE_PERF_V1` record, and remains within the repository’s pre-push/PR budgets.
- `cargo bench ... -- --list` accepts Cargo’s single injected `--bench` sentinel, validates `performance/budgets.tsv`, prints exactly the two documented case names in stable order, executes no timed workload, and exits zero.
- The contract test exercises every configuration/CLI/statistics failure branch without wall-clock timing and runs automatically in the locked workspace test command.
- The photo case constructs and consumes exactly 128 deterministic assets per measured work unit; the render case evaluates and consumes exactly 65,536 pixels through the fixed two-step pipeline per measured work unit.
- A clean check prints exactly one `RUSTTABLE_PERF_V1` line per case plus the summary, with nonzero raw/calibration measurements and integer normalized p95 values.
- A temporary config with a limit equal to the computed normalized p95 passes; lowering that limit by one fails with the affected case named. Multiplying either measured workload by ten also fails; all verification changes are reverted before merge.
- The committed limit for each case is traceable to five PR-recorded observations and the exact 1.5× calculation; CI never rewrites `performance/budgets.tsv` or accepts a new baseline automatically.
- `scripts/performance-budgets.sh` preserves logs and child exit status, returns `124` at the 300-second deadline, kills/reaps every started process, and creates no delayed marker or success summary after timeout/failure.
- Every push to `main` runs the finite-timeout performance job and uploads its result log on success or failure; pull-request events never execute timed benchmarks.
- Measured clean pre-commit, pre-push, and PR validation remain at or below 30, 60, and 150 seconds respectively, while the benchmark command remains at or below 300 seconds.
- No inherited C/C++/CMake, decoder, image fixture, filesystem input, randomness, sleep, network, GPU, SIMD, telemetry, or unsafe Rust is introduced.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: perf: add a merge-only benchmark harness with enforced budgets

issues.body:
## Why
Issue #5 establishes the first runnable desktop slice for the RustTable rewrite.

## How
Adds `rusttable-app` as a workspace member with iced 0.14, a minimal tiny-skia/thread-pool feature set, a thin binary entry point, and a static `RustTable` shell view. It introduces no image, metadata, database, network, or filesystem behavior.

## Validation
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- Native `cargo run -p rusttable-app` startup and clean process exit verified.

Closes #5.
issues.title: feat: add iced application shell

issues.body:
## Why

A processing operation must never receive RGB samples whose encoding, transfer function, and numeric range are implicit. #22 establishes finite `f32` processing scalars, while the preceding #23 PR establishes the ordered executable-pipeline module in the same crate. Evaluation still needs a concrete boundary between encoded source RGB and linear-light working RGB.

The previous draft defined a transform trait but supplied no production transform, so the milestone could have passed while no source sample ever crossed the boundary. This issue instead implements the smallest real, specification-based conversion: normalized sRGB source samples to linear-light sRGB working samples. It does not claim that decoded sRGB is scene-referred, and it does not claim full ICC color management; it establishes the type and conversion seam that later camera/profile adapters must honor.

For validated channels in `0.0..=1.0`, both branches of the sRGB decoding function are finite. The conversion is therefore total over the public source-image domain and must not expose an artificial, untestable transform-error path.

## Dependencies

- **Technical dependency:** blocked by #22 because the color/sample types use `FiniteF32`.
- **Delivery order:** follows #23 so milestone-#5 changes to `crates/rusttable-processing/src/lib.rs` land serially under the repository's one-issue/one-worktree execution loop. This issue does not consume pipeline types from #23.
- **Milestone #5 sequence: 3 of 4.**
- Blocks #27.
- Independent of JPEG/PNG decoding in #21. Integration from `DecodedImage`/RGBA8 into this source type is a later explicit issue; this PR must not depend on `rusttable-catalog` or `rusttable-image-io`.

## How

- Write failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/color_boundary.rs` and record the initial red command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-processing/src/color.rs` and re-export only the intentional public surface from `crates/rusttable-processing/src/lib.rs`.
- Define checked `RasterDimensions` with nonzero `u32` width and height. `pixel_count()` must widen both operands before multiplication and return `u64`; never multiply dimensions in `u32` or `usize`.
- Define `RgbChannel::{Red, Green, Blue}` for stable channel attribution in the later evaluator.
- Define opaque `SrgbChannel` backed by #22 `FiniteF32`. Its checked constructor must reject NaN, both infinities, and values outside the inclusive normalized range `0.0..=1.0` with a public, matchable `SrgbChannelError`; preserve no invalid intermediate value.
- Define distinct immutable pixel types:
  - `SourceRgb { red: SrgbChannel, green: SrgbChannel, blue: SrgbChannel }` for normalized, transfer-encoded sRGB samples;
  - `LinearRgb { red: FiniteF32, green: FiniteF32, blue: FiniteF32 }` for linear-light working samples. `LinearRgb` deliberately permits finite negative values and values above one and performs no clipping.
- Define explicit product-space tags with exactly one initial variant each:
  - `SourceColorSpace::Srgb`;
  - `WorkingColorSpace::LinearSrgb`.
- Define immutable `SourceRgbImage` and `WorkingRgbImage` with private dimensions and row-major `Vec` storage. Their space accessors return the fixed tags above rather than accepting caller-supplied tags. Checked public constructors compare the supplied vector's `usize` length to the widened `u64` pixel count and return `ImageBuildError::PixelCountMismatch { expected: u64, actual: usize }`; they must not allocate from dimensions. Expose read-only dimensions, space, pixel-slice, iterator, and checked-index accessors. Mutation may be crate-private for #27 but must not be public.
- Add the total function `to_linear_srgb(source: &SourceRgbImage) -> WorkingRgbImage` using the standard sRGB decoding transfer function independently on each channel:
  - `c / 12.92` when `c <= 0.04045`;
  - `((c + 0.055) / 1.055).powf(2.4)` otherwise.
- Traverse pixels in ascending row-major index and channels in `Red`, `Green`, `Blue` order. Produce exactly one output pixel for each source pixel, preserve dimensions and order, and construct the private output image directly from that proven one-to-one mapping. Do not route the internally known-valid vector through a public caller-input error or use `unwrap`, `expect`, panic, or an unreachable transform-error variant.
- Build each calculated channel through a crate-private `FiniteF32` construction path whose safety condition is “the supplied value has already been proven finite by the normalized-sRGB formula.” Keep the ordinary public `FiniteF32::new` checked. Do not expose an unchecked public constructor.
- Do not clamp, quantize, apply a matrix, change primaries, process alpha, infer a camera/input profile, inspect an embedded profile, claim scene-referred semantics, or perform display/export conversion.
- Add a `compile_fail` doctest in the public API showing that `SourceRgbImage` cannot be passed where `WorkingRgbImage` is required.
- Use only synthetic data. Include one 256x1 fixture covering every normalized 8-bit sRGB code value; do not use image files.
- Keep operation execution, decoded RGBA bridging, ICC parsing, chromatic adaptation, LUTs, monitor profiles, metadata, filesystem access, UI, persistence, threading, async runtimes, GPU/SIMD code, and inherited darktable color implementation out of this PR.
- Retain strict lints, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, the existing `rusttable-processing -> rusttable-core` dependency direction, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test color_boundary --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-processing/src/color.rs` defines checked `RasterDimensions`, `RgbChannel`, `SrgbChannel`, distinct `SourceRgb`/`LinearRgb` samples, fixed source/working space tags, immutable source/working image types, and typed dimension/channel/image-build errors. No public `ColorTransformError` or fallible sRGB conversion is introduced.
- `rejects_zero_dimensions` matches distinct zero-width and zero-height errors. `pixel_count_uses_widened_multiplication` proves a large valid `u32` pair is multiplied as `u64` without wraparound.
- `rejects_non_finite_and_out_of_range_srgb_channels` proves NaN, infinities, values below zero, and values above one cannot enter `SourceRgb`.
- `rejects_pixel_count_mismatch` proves both too-short and too-long row-major vectors return exact expected/actual counts without panic, truncation, or allocation based on dimensions.
- `space_tags_are_fixed_by_image_type` proves callers cannot construct an sRGB-tagged working image or a linear-tagged source image.
- `linear_samples_preserve_extended_range` proves `LinearRgb` and `WorkingRgbImage` preserve finite negative and above-one values exactly and perform no clamp.
- `converts_srgb_reference_values` verifies, within a documented `f32` tolerance, at least `0.0 -> 0.0`, `1.0 -> 1.0`, the `0.04045` breakpoint, and `0.5 -> approximately 0.21404114`.
- `converts_every_u8_srgb_code_value` converts a row containing all 256 normalized 8-bit code values and proves every output channel is finite, within `0.0..=1.0`, monotonically nondecreasing, and in the same row-major position.
- `preserves_dimensions_space_and_row_major_order` converts a distinguishable 2x1 source and proves dimensions, `WorkingColorSpace::LinearSrgb`, and pixel order are unchanged.
- `equal_sources_convert_equally` proves repeated conversion of equal source images produces equal working images.
- Source inspection and tests prove the total conversion contains no `unwrap!`, `expect`, `panic!`, public unchecked scalar constructor, or artificial error branch.
- Public docs describe the output as linear-light sRGB and explicitly do not label it scene-referred. The compile-fail doctest rejects source/working image interchange at compile time; no blanket `From`/`Into` conversion bypasses `to_linear_srgb`.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` still shows only `rusttable-core`; no codec, ICC/color library, filesystem, UI, database, async runtime, GPU, FFI, or inherited C dependency is added.
- No unsafe Rust is introduced; no handwritten Rust file exceeds 1,000 lines; tests use no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(processing): define the sRGB-to-linear-light working-color boundary

issues.body:
## Why

#22 provides closed executable operations and finite `f32` processing values, #23 provides an atomic pipeline with authoritative order/provenance, and #26 provides a real sRGB-to-linear-light-sRGB boundary. The initial milestone now needs one complete in-memory render slice proving that immutable edit intent can be compiled and evaluated into a new working image without mutating the source, reading a file, invoking iced, or introducing a full engine.

The evaluator must make order observable, skip disabled steps without changing indices, preserve extended linear-light values without clipping, and fail with exact step/pixel/channel context when floating-point arithmetic would leave the finite working-image domain. This slice does not claim scene-referred color semantics.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #23 for `CompiledPipeline`, stable source indices, and edit provenance. This transitively requires the #38 immutable `Edit` aggregate.
- Blocked by #26 for `WorkingRgbImage`, `LinearRgb`, `RgbChannel`, dimensions, and the source-to-working conversion.
- Uses the #22 operation/scalar contracts through #23.
- **Milestone #5 sequence: 4 of 4 and the final issue in the initial non-destructive editing foundation.**
- Independent of catalog persistence, image decoding, metadata, UI, export, and the concrete image-I/O adapter.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-processing/src/evaluate.rs` and re-export a focused entry point from `crates/rusttable-processing/src/lib.rs`:
  - `evaluate(pipeline: &CompiledPipeline, input: &WorkingRgbImage) -> Result<WorkingRgbImage, EvaluationError>`.
- Clone the input pixel vector exactly once into a private output buffer, then mutate only that buffer. Do not allocate a full replacement image for each operation. Construct and return the final `WorkingRgbImage` only after all active steps succeed.
- Factor the operation loop into a crate-private helper that accepts `&mut [LinearRgb]`. Its slice-based signature must make full-buffer replacement/reallocation impossible inside the step loop; keep it out of the public API.
- Iterate `pipeline.active_steps()` exactly once in #23 order. Never reorder or group operations by kind, key, ID, parameter value, or enabled state.
- Within each active step, visit pixels in ascending zero-based row-major index and channels in `Red`, `Green`, `Blue` order. Return the first error in that deterministic traversal order. A non-finite exposure multiplier is detected before visiting any pixel.
- Implement exactly the two #22 operation kinds over every `LinearRgb` pixel:
  - `Exposure { stops }`: compute one multiplier per step with `stops.get().exp2()`, validate that multiplier through `FiniteF32`, then multiply all three channels;
  - `LinearOffset { value }`: add the same finite value to all three channels.
- Construct every changed channel through `FiniteF32`. Do not clamp, quantize, gamma-encode, replace non-finite results, assign scene-referred meaning, or silently continue after an arithmetic failure.
- Preserve `RasterDimensions`, `WorkingColorSpace::LinearSrgb`, and row-major order. Borrow the compiled pipeline and leave its edit/photo/revision provenance unchanged; evaluation does not copy that provenance into the image, increment revisions, or mutate the pipeline.
- Define public, matchable `EvaluationError` variants:
  - `NonFiniteExposureMultiplier { step_index, operation_id }`;
  - `NonFiniteChannelResult { step_index, operation_id, pixel_index, channel }`.
  Use `PipelineStepIndex`, `OperationId`, and `RgbChannel` directly. Implement deterministic equality, `Display`, and `std::error::Error` without an opaque string/catch-all variant.
- Treat evaluation as externally atomic. On any error, return no `WorkingRgbImage`; the borrowed input and pipeline remain equal to their pre-call values, and no partial output is observable.
- Add failing integration tests first in `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/render_slice.rs` for every externally observable scenario named below, using only synthetic 1x1 and 2x1 images/edits. Record the initial failing focused command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add a focused module unit test in `crates/rusttable-processing/src/evaluate.rs` named `reuses_single_output_slice_across_steps`; it must apply multiple steps to one `Vec<LinearRgb>` through the crate-private slice helper and prove the vector pointer/capacity are unchanged. This requires no custom/global allocator and no unsafe code.
- Include one end-to-end integration test that constructs a synthetic `SourceRgbImage`, calls #26 `to_linear_srgb`, constructs a core `Edit` through the public #38 API, compiles it through #23, and evaluates it. This proves the complete milestone boundary without a decoder or UI.
- Keep masks, blending, alpha, geometry, resampling, tiling, caches, threads, async runtimes, SIMD, GPU code, previews, display/export transforms, persistence, filesystem access, UI, and inherited darktable processing implementation out of this PR.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test render_slice --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing evaluate::tests::reuses_single_output_slice_across_steps --locked -- --exact`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-processing/src/evaluate.rs` exposes only the focused `evaluate` entry point plus typed `EvaluationError`; it does not expose a mutable image buffer, scheduler, cache, or backend abstraction.
- `empty_pipeline_returns_equal_new_image` proves an empty pipeline returns a `WorkingRgbImage` equal to the input with identical dimensions, space tag, and row-major samples while leaving the input untouched.
- `disabled_steps_have_no_effect` proves disabled operations remain in pipeline provenance but are not executed.
- `exposure_plus_one_stop_doubles_binary_fractions` and `exposure_minus_one_stop_halves_binary_fractions` use exactly representable fixtures and compare exact `FiniteF32` results.
- `linear_offset_preserves_extended_range_without_clipping` proves finite negative and above-one linear-light results remain present after evaluation.
- `pipeline_order_is_execution_order` proves `LinearOffset(0.25)` followed by `Exposure(1.0)` differs from the reverse sequence for the same input and matches the two manually calculated expected outputs.
- `reports_non_finite_exposure_multiplier` uses a finite extreme stop value whose `exp2()` is non-finite and matches the exact original step index/operation ID before any pixel is visited.
- `reports_first_non_finite_channel_deterministically` supplies multiple overflowing candidates and proves the evaluator reports the lowest row-major pixel index and `Red` before `Green` before `Blue`, together with the exact original step index and operation ID.
- `retains_original_step_index_after_disabled_predecessor` proves an error in an active step after one or more disabled steps reports its #23 source index rather than a renumbered active-step position.
- `success_does_not_mutate_inputs` and `failure_does_not_mutate_inputs` compare cloned input images and pipelines before/after calls and prove no caller-visible mutation or partial output.
- `equal_inputs_evaluate_equally` proves equal pipelines and equal working images produce equal results or equal typed errors.
- `synthetic_srgb_source_compiles_and_renders` crosses #26's source/working type boundary, builds a two-operation #38 edit, compiles it through #22/#23, evaluates it, and checks the documented final 1x1 linear-light result within an explicit `f32` tolerance.
- The module unit test `evaluate::tests::reuses_single_output_slice_across_steps` proves multiple operations mutate the same fixed slice without pointer/capacity change; code review confirms `evaluate` performs its single full-buffer clone before invoking that helper and has no full-image allocation inside the active-step loop.
- Public docs describe the working samples as linear-light sRGB and explicitly do not label them scene-referred.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` still shows only `rusttable-core`; no decoder, filesystem, UI, database, FFI, async runtime, SIMD/GPU library, cache, or inherited C target is introduced.
- No unsafe Rust is introduced; no handwritten Rust file exceeds 1,000 lines; tests use no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- The implementation PR records red-test evidence, fits one focused worktree, and is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR containing `Closes #27` and closing no other issue.
issues.title: feat(processing): evaluate an in-memory non-destructive render slice

issues.body:
## Why

The image-input slice in #21 intentionally stops at format, dimensions, and uninterpreted decoded samples. Before any EXIF, XMP, or IPTC parser is selected, RustTable needs a small metadata domain model with explicit supported fields, validated values, canonical ordering, and no parser tag numbers or raw byte bags. Otherwise incidental parser types and darktable-era schema assumptions will leak into the core model and durable import representation.

This PR is model-only. It does not read files or choose a metadata parser. An empty `ImageMetadata` value must be sufficient for the first import slice, while later parser adapters can populate the same checked entries without changing catalog or persistence contracts.

## Dependencies

- Sequenced after #21 so the milestone lands one public boundary at a time; this issue adds no dependency on `rusttable-image` or `rusttable-image-io`.
- Uses the strict, standard-library-only `rusttable-core` foundation and scalar conventions completed by #13.
- **Milestone #4 sequence: 2 of 6.**
- Blocks #32, whose `ImportRecord` persists these values.

## How

- Write failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-core/tests/metadata_values.rs` and `crates/rusttable-core/tests/metadata_model.rs`; record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add:
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/metadata/mod.rs` for deliberate re-exports;
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/metadata/value.rs` for checked scalar metadata values;
  - `crates/rusttable-core/src/metadata/model.rs` for supported fields, typed entries, and immutable metadata state.
- Define `MetadataText` as exact validated UTF-8 text. Reject empty text, embedded NUL, and values over 4,096 UTF-8 bytes. Do not trim, normalize Unicode, change case, localize, or interpret formatting.
- Define `PositiveRational` with a positive nonzero `u64` numerator and denominator. Normalize each value by greatest common divisor so equivalent fractions compare and hash equally. Return typed errors for zero numerator or denominator; never use floating-point storage.
- Define `Orientation` as a closed representation of the eight standard stored-orientation codes. Provide checked conversion from `u8` for exactly `1..=8`, a stable numeric accessor, and typed rejection of every other value. Do not rotate pixels in this issue.
- Define `MetadataField` with exactly these variants and explicit stable ranks:
  0. `CameraMake`;
  1. `CameraModel`;
  2. `LensModel`;
  3. `CaptureDateTimeOriginal`;
  4. `Orientation`;
  5. `ExposureTime`;
  6. `FNumber`;
  7. `IsoSpeed`;
  8. `FocalLength`.
- Expose `MetadataField::rank()` and a stable `ALL_FIELDS` array in that order. Implement `Ord`/`PartialOrd` manually from `rank()` rather than deriving order from Rust declaration position or enum discriminants. Reordering source declarations must not change observable metadata order.
- Define a typed `MetadataEntry` enum rather than a generic field/value pair:
  - `CameraMake(MetadataText)`;
  - `CameraModel(MetadataText)`;
  - `LensModel(MetadataText)`;
  - `CaptureDateTimeOriginal(MetadataText)`;
  - `Orientation(Orientation)`;
  - `ExposureTime(PositiveRational)`;
  - `FNumber(PositiveRational)`;
  - `IsoSpeed(NonZeroU32)`;
  - `FocalLength(PositiveRational)`.
  Each entry must expose its corresponding `MetadataField`. Invalid field/value combinations must be unrepresentable through the public API.
- Treat `CaptureDateTimeOriginal` as source text only. Do not parse calendar components, infer a timezone, convert to UTC/local time, consult a clock, or add a date/time dependency.
- Define immutable `ImageMetadata` backed by `BTreeMap<MetadataField, MetadataEntry>`. `ImageMetadata::empty()` is valid. Checked construction from an iterator must detect duplicate fields before collection and return a typed error containing the exact duplicate field.
- Expose read-only field lookup, length/empty checks, and iteration in explicit rank order. Do not expose a mutable map or unknown-field extension bag.
- Implement deterministic `Debug`, `Clone`, `Eq`, and `Hash` behavior where the contained values permit it. Hash metadata by canonical ranked entry iteration rather than Rust enum memory/discriminant representation. Equivalent reduced rationals and equal entry sets must compare/hash equally regardless of input order.
- Re-export only the intentional metadata types from `crates/rusttable-core/src/lib.rs`. Keep constructors/invariants inside their defining modules.
- Use only the Rust standard library. Do not add `serde`, a date/time crate, EXIF/XMP/IPTC libraries, raw tag-number enums, parser-specific values, arbitrary byte values, filesystem access, database schemas, metadata writing, UI behavior, or a parser/input trait in this PR.
- Do not copy darktable metadata structs, tag tables, SQL columns, parser logic, constants, fixtures, or tests.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test metadata_values --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test metadata_model --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-core/src/metadata/value.rs` exposes checked `MetadataText`, reduced `PositiveRational`, and the eight-value `Orientation`; no parser, serialization, filesystem, locale, or clock type appears in their APIs.
- Empty, NUL-containing, and over-4,096-byte text return distinct observable errors. Valid UTF-8 text round-trips byte-for-byte without trimming, case folding, Unicode normalization, or locale conversion.
- `PositiveRational` rejects zero numerator and zero denominator. Values such as `1/2`, `2/4`, and `50/100` normalize to equal values with equal hashes and the same stored numerator/denominator.
- Orientation codes `1` through `8` round-trip exactly; `0`, `9`, and every other `u8` return the typed invalid-orientation error. No pixel transformation is introduced.
- `MetadataField` exposes exactly the nine listed fields, ranks `0..=8`, and `ALL_FIELDS` in the documented order; there is no unknown/raw-tag variant.
- Tests prove field comparison/iteration follows explicit rank. A test-only declaration/reconstruction path or equivalent focused assertion demonstrates canonical order is not obtained by serializing or inspecting Rust discriminants.
- The typed `MetadataEntry` variants make camera text, date text, orientation, exposure/f-number/focal-length rationals, and nonzero ISO assignments explicit; a caller cannot pair a field with the wrong value type.
- `ImageMetadata::empty()` succeeds. Duplicate fields return the exact duplicate `MetadataField`; supplying identical unique entries in different orders produces equal metadata, equal canonical hashes, and identical ranked iteration.
- `CaptureDateTimeOriginal` preserves source text exactly and performs no timezone, locale, calendar, UTC, or wall-clock conversion.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` still shows no normal third-party dependency. No image codec, metadata parser, serde, database, UI, processing, or FFI dependency is introduced.
- No local unsafe Rust, copied darktable implementation/schema/fixture, networked test, filesystem test, sleep, wall-clock dependency, randomness, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(metadata): define canonical metadata values and model boundaries

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable now has a committed Cargo lockfile, an exact Rust toolchain from completed #8, the substantial iced dependency graph from completed #5, additional core/catalog crates, and Bun-based macOS developer tooling from completed #30. The project still has no executable supply-chain policy: a new RustSec advisory, disallowed license, wildcard requirement, alternate registry, git dependency, unexplained duplicate, or JavaScript package can enter without a durable report.

The real advisory database and full dependency graph checks are networked or comparatively expensive. They must run after merges to `main`, not in the 30-second pre-commit, 60-second pre-push, or 150-second pull-request path. Only deterministic fake-tool/manifest regression coverage belongs in fast validation.

## Dependencies

- Depends on #17, which creates `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` and the merge-only validation foundation.
- Transitively follows #40 → #14 → #17, so fast-check process cleanup and the 150-second PR contract are already stable.
- Uses the exact Rust toolchain completed by #8 / PR #35.
- Audits the current locked Rust graph and verifies that completed #30’s Bun project remains dependency-free under exact `bun@1.3.14` until a future issue deliberately revises that policy.
- **Milestone #6 sequence: 1 of 4.**
- Blocks #31 so diagnostics dependencies are added only after policy exists.
- #24 is independently blocked by processing prerequisites; final distribution issue #43 waits for both the #29 → #31 path and #24.

## How

- Add root `deny.toml` using the configuration schema supported by one exact reviewed `cargo-deny` version.
- Define explicit policy for:
  - **licenses:** enumerate the accepted SPDX expressions needed by the locked graph; do not use blanket “OSI”, “copyleft”, “unlicensed”, or wildcard allowances. Any exception must name the exact crate and version, detected expression, distribution rationale, owner, and review-by date in an adjacent comment;
  - **sources:** allow only the canonical crates.io registry source by default; deny unknown registries and unknown git sources. Any future source exception must identify the exact source URL, crate/version, rationale, owner, and review-by date;
  - **bans:** deny wildcard dependency requirements and explicitly banned crates. Set multiple versions to **warn/report**, not fail, because GUI/platform graphs can contain intentional semver-incompatible versions. An optional skip/skip-tree entry must be exact and documented; do not hide the complete duplicate report.
- Use `cargo-audit` as the authoritative RustSec advisory gate. Add `audit.toml` only when the current lockfile requires an exact advisory ignore; each ignore must include the RustSec ID, exact affected crate/version, risk rationale, owner, and review-by date. No blanket or unbounded ignore is permitted.
- Add `scripts/security-tool-versions.env` containing exact reviewed versions of `cargo-audit` and `cargo-deny`. Bun’s expected version remains sourced from the exact `packageManager` field in `package.json`; do not duplicate it in this file.
- Add `scripts/dependency-security.sh` as the sole real-check entry point. It must:
  1. resolve and verify the repository root;
  2. require tracked root `Cargo.lock`, `Cargo.toml`, `deny.toml`, and `package.json`;
  3. verify the exact audit/deny versions from `security-tool-versions.env`;
  4. parse `package.json` with the installed Bun executable, require `packageManager` to be exactly `bun@1.3.14`, and verify `bun --version` reports `1.3.14`;
  5. reject `dependencies`, `devDependencies`, `optionalDependencies`, or `peerDependencies` entries and reject `bun.lock`/`bun.lockb` while the project declares no external JavaScript package;
  6. run `cargo metadata --locked --format-version 1` so a stale lockfile fails before policy evaluation;
  7. run `cargo-audit` against the root lockfile with machine-readable output;
  8. run `cargo-deny` bans, licenses, and sources checks against the complete workspace graph;
  9. run `cargo tree --workspace --all-features --duplicates --locked` as a non-suppressible duplicate inventory;
  10. write text/JSON reports and `target/security/summary.txt` containing Rust/Bun/audit/deny versions, Cargo.lock SHA-256, checked workspace packages, duplicate inventory status, exception IDs, JavaScript dependency count, and final pass/fail.
- Permit `CARGO_BIN`, `CARGO_AUDIT_BIN`, `CARGO_DENY_BIN`, and `BUN_BIN` overrides only for the regression test; production defaults must resolve the real tools from `PATH`.
- Add `scripts/test-dependency-security.sh` using temporary fake executables and manifests. It must cover success plus distinct failures for missing/untracked lockfile, stale-lock metadata failure, missing/malformed packageManager, Bun version mismatch, every prohibited package section, unexpected Bun lockfile, audit version mismatch, deny version mismatch, audit violation, deny violation, and duplicate-report command failure. It must assert exact child arguments/stage order and prove no later success marker is written after a failing stage.
- Run only `scripts/test-dependency-security.sh` as one parallel task in `scripts/precommit-fast.sh`, after measuring it. Do not install or invoke the real networked audit/deny tools from pre-commit, pre-push, `scripts/pr-ci.sh`, or `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml`; preserve the 30/60/150-second budgets.
- Extend the existing `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` with one finite-timeout `dependency-security` job. Use read-only contents permission, immutable full-SHA action pins, Rust from `rust-toolchain.toml`, exact Bun `1.3.14`, exact audit/deny versions, a cache keyed by runner OS/tool versions/`Cargo.lock`, and upload `target/security/` under `if: always()`.
- Do not use repository write permission, secrets, automatic dependency updates, or inherited C/C++/CMake jobs.
- Retain strict compiler/lint policy, unsafe denial, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit. This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Validation

- `bash scripts/test-dependency-security.sh`
- `bash scripts/dependency-security.sh`
- `bun --version`
- `cargo metadata --locked --format-version 1`
- `cargo audit --file Cargo.lock`
- `cargo deny check bans licenses sources`
- `cargo tree --workspace --all-features --duplicates --locked`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/rust-main.yml`
- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `deny.toml` explicitly governs licenses, wildcard/banned dependencies, duplicate reporting, registries, and git sources; it contains no broad allowance and every exception is exact, owned, rationalized, and review-dated.
- `scripts/dependency-security.sh` fails before auditing when root dependency metadata is absent, untracked, or inconsistent with `Cargo.lock`, and it fails when any installed Cargo security tool version differs from `scripts/security-tool-versions.env`.
- The real locked workspace passes `cargo-audit` plus cargo-deny bans/licenses/sources checks, or the repository contains only exact documented exceptions satisfying the issue’s exception schema.
- The complete duplicate inventory is always emitted and uploaded. Intentional duplicate versions are visible warnings rather than a blanket failure; no skip entry suppresses the inventory.
- The Bun check parses rather than greps JSON, proves the running binary is exactly 1.3.14, reports zero JavaScript packages, and fails on any prohibited package section or Bun lockfile until a future issue intentionally adds and audits JavaScript dependencies.
- `scripts/test-dependency-security.sh` deterministically proves every child command/argument and each fail-closed branch without network access or installing the real tools.
- Every push to `main` runs the finite-timeout security job with exact Rust/Bun/audit/deny tools and uploads metadata, advisory, deny, duplicate, JavaScript-manifest, and summary reports on success or failure; pull-request events never run the real networked checks.
- The fake-tool regression runs in the pre-commit parallel set while measured clean pre-commit, pre-push, and PR validation remain at or below 30, 60, and 150 seconds respectively.
- No repository write permission, secret, automatic updater, or inherited darktable build is introduced.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: ci(security): enforce locked dependency, advisory, license, and source policy

issues.body:
## Status

Completed by squash-merged PR #34.

This issue owns the local macOS Computer Use installation path that is now present on `main`. It does not own release artifact version provenance, archive/checksum generation, merge-only distribution validation, signing, notarization, or publication; the remaining distribution-smoke work is tracked by #43.

## Why

Computer Use needs one stable, registered `RustTable.app` instead of an unbundled Cargo executable or whichever worktree bundle LaunchServices finds first. The developer path must build the real `rusttable-app` binary, install without `sudo`, replace an existing RustTable bundle transactionally, and remove only stale bundles that can be positively identified as this repository’s application.

## Dependencies

- Depends on completed #5 / merged PR #25, which introduced the `crates/rusttable-app` iced binary.
- This is developer tooling associated with milestone #2 “Desktop Experience & Iced UI”, not the future release artifact gate.
- #43 builds on this completed installer for the merge-only distribution smoke artifact.

## How

- Add root `package.json` with exact `bun@1.3.14` package-manager metadata and the `install:computer-use` / `test:computer-use` scripts.
- Add `scripts/dev/computer-use-app-install.ts` for option parsing, bundle-identifier parsing, worktree discovery, LaunchServices parsing, transactional replacement, identity-checked cleanup, and stale-registration filtering.
- Add `scripts/dev/install-computer-use-app.ts` as the executable orchestration path.
- Support `--app-path`, `--compact`, `--no-build`, `--no-install`, `--no-launch`, and `-h`/`--help`; reject unknown options and a missing `--app-path` value.
- Build `rusttable-app` in release mode and assemble `target/release/bundle/macos/RustTable.app` with executable `Contents/MacOS/RustTable` and bundle identifier `com.cgasgarth.rusttable`.
- Install by default to `~/Applications/RustTable.app` without `sudo`. Validate the source, staged, and existing destination bundle identity before mutating them; stage and back up transactionally so a failed replacement restores the prior bundle.
- Register the canonical installation with LaunchServices and optionally launch it.
- Discover generated bundles only below this repository’s Git worktrees and remove/unregister only bundles whose identifier is exactly `com.cgasgarth.rusttable`; never delete by filename alone.
- Add focused Bun tests in `scripts/dev/computer-use-app-install.test.ts` and run them in the existing parallel pre-commit and pre-push scripts without exceeding their budgets.
- Deliver the implementation through exactly one squash-merged PR.

## Validation

Recorded by PR #34:

- `bun run test:computer-use`
- `bun run install:computer-use -- --help`
- Existing-bundle `--no-build` smoke
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` — 0.06 seconds in the PR validation record
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh` — 14.31 seconds in the PR validation record
- Native macOS build, install, LaunchServices registration, launch, and clean-exit smoke

## Acceptance criteria

- `bun run install:computer-use -- --help` documents every supported option, and unsupported or incomplete options exit nonzero.
- A normal macOS run builds a real release `rusttable-app`, creates a bundle with identifier `com.cgasgarth.rusttable`, installs it to `~/Applications/RustTable.app` without `sudo`, registers that exact path, and can launch it.
- Existing canonical installation replacement is transactional: an unexpected source/staged/destination identifier is rejected, and failure after backup restores the prior bundle.
- Worktree discovery and stale-registration cleanup affect only exact RustTable identifiers beneath repository worktrees; same-named or unrelated bundles are preserved.
- The six focused parser/filter tests run from both local validation entry points while pre-commit remains below 30 seconds and pre-push remains below 60 seconds.
- PR #34 closes only this issue and was squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: tooling: add install:computer-use for the RustTable macOS app

issues.body:
## Why

The runnable `rusttable-app` from completed #5 has no durable local evidence for startup, normal shutdown, iced startup failure, or an unexpected panic. Release-oriented builds need enough information to reproduce failures without attaching a debugger, but a photo application must not dump process state, command-line arguments, environment variables, catalog paths, image paths, or metadata into diagnostics and must never transmit data automatically.

The implementation must remain a small application-boundary facility: synchronous, bounded, safe Rust; no telemetry service, generic logging facade, subscriber stack, uploader, background runtime, or domain/UI coupling.

## Dependencies

- Depends on #29 so the single new path-resolution dependency is evaluated by the locked advisory/license/source policy before this PR merges.
- Builds on completed #5 / PR #25 and its `crates/rusttable-app` binary entry point.
- Uses the exact Rust toolchain and validation budgets completed by #8 / PR #35 and hardened by #40.
- **Milestone #6 sequence: 2 of 4.**
- Blocks final distribution issue #43 so the staged application contains the production diagnostics bootstrap.
- #24 is the independently prerequisite-gated performance issue; #43 waits for both #31 and #24.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-diagnostics` as a workspace member with inherited package metadata/lints and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Give it exactly one normal third-party dependency: a reviewed release line of `directories` for platform-appropriate application-data paths. Use the Rust standard library for synchronization, clocks, process IDs, backtraces, files, bounded rotation/retention, JSON escaping, and report formatting.
- Do not add `tracing`, `log`, `tracing-subscriber`, `tracing-appender`, `serde`, `serde_json`, an async runtime, network client, crash SDK, uploader, iced, `rusttable-core`, catalog, image-I/O, or processing dependencies.
- Expose only RustTable-owned types from `crates/rusttable-diagnostics/src/lib.rs`:
  - `install() -> Result<DiagnosticsGuard, DiagnosticsError>`;
  - a closed `DiagnosticEvent::{Startup, Shutdown, ApplicationFailure(ApplicationFailureCode)}` enum;
  - a closed initial `ApplicationFailureCode::IcedRun` enum;
  - `DiagnosticsGuard::record(&self, DiagnosticEvent) -> Result<(), DiagnosticsError>`.
  Do not accept arbitrary messages, key/value maps, paths, metadata, or `Debug` values and do not expose `std::io::Error` directly.
- Split implementation before any file approaches 1,000 lines:
  - `storage.rs` owns directory creation, current/backup log rotation, synchronized writes, flush, and crash-report retention;
  - `json.rs` owns deterministic JSON string escaping and the fixed event/report envelopes;
  - `crash.rs` owns panic-hook installation, bounded backtrace formatting, payload classification/redaction, and report creation;
  - `event.rs` owns the closed event/error-code schema and stable string codes.
- Resolve the default directory with `directories::ProjectDirs::from("com", "cgasgarth", "RustTable")`. Read only `RUSTTABLE_DIAGNOSTICS_DIR` as an explicit support/test override; do not inspect arbitrary configuration files or any other environment variable.
- `DiagnosticsGuard` must own a `std::sync::Mutex<std::io::BufWriter<std::fs::File>>`; `record` acquires the mutex, writes one complete UTF-8 line, appends `\n`, and flushes before returning. A poisoned mutex or short/write/flush failure returns a matchable RustTable error and never panics.
- Write newline-delimited JSON to `rusttable.log`. Each line must contain exactly these ordered top-level fields: `schema_version`, `timestamp_unix_ms`, `package_version`, `event`, and `failure_code`. Use `null` for `failure_code` unless the event is `application_failure`. No target/module/thread/span or arbitrary field may appear.
- Implement JSON escaping directly and test every JSON control escape plus quotes, backslashes, valid non-ASCII UTF-8, and rejection/replacement policy for disallowed control data. Do not construct event JSON through unchecked string interpolation.
- Before opening the current writer, rotate `rusttable.log` when its size is at least 5 MiB: remove an existing regular-file `rusttable.log.1`, rename the current regular file to that single backup, and open a fresh current file. Refuse to follow a symlink for the current or backup path. Never retain more than current plus one backup.
- Install one panic hook that first attempts to write a new regular file named `crash-<unix-millis>-<pid>.json` with create-new semantics, then invokes the previously installed hook exactly once.
- A crash report must contain a fixed ordered JSON envelope with schema version, package version, timestamp, PID, target OS/architecture, panic file/line/column or null, payload kind, payload text/redaction marker, backtrace status, and backtrace text.
- Bound each crash report to 256 KiB including the final newline. Preserve a static `&'static str` payload after JSON/control escaping, but replace a dynamic `String` or unknown payload with an explicit redacted marker; do not serialize arbitrary panic payloads.
- Retain only the five newest valid RustTable crash-report regular files by parsed `(unix_millis, pid)` filename order. Ignore and preserve unrelated files, malformed names, directories, and symlinks. A retention failure is reported best-effort to stderr and must not recursively panic or suppress the original panic.
- Diagnostics output must never enumerate or automatically record the process environment, full command line, current/home directories, open files, hostname/user name, image/catalog paths, metadata values, pixel data, or stable telemetry/device identifiers. No upload endpoint, URL, socket, HTTP client, or network call may exist.
- Update `crates/rusttable-app/Cargo.toml` with only a path dependency on `rusttable-diagnostics`.
- Refactor `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs` so the existing public `run() -> iced::Result` performs a private bootstrap sequence:
  1. attempt diagnostics installation;
  2. on success record `Startup` and retain the guard for the entire iced run;
  3. on initialization or startup-record failure print exactly one concise warning to stderr and still invoke the iced runner;
  4. record `Shutdown` after success or `ApplicationFailure(IcedRun)` before returning the original iced error;
  5. a later record/flush failure may emit one concise warning but must not replace the iced result.
- Put the injectable bootstrap state machine in `crates/rusttable-app/src/bootstrap.rs`. Unit tests must inject fake installer/guard/application/stderr closures so no native window is opened and prove the application closure is invoked exactly once on diagnostics success, install failure, startup-record failure, shutdown-record failure, and iced failure.
- Add `crates/rusttable-diagnostics/tests/diagnostics_process.rs`. Re-execute the integration-test binary in explicit child modes so global panic-hook state cannot leak between scenarios. Use only deterministic temporary paths derived from test name, PID, and an atomic counter; no sleeps, network, user directories, or external fixtures.
- Child-process coverage must include normal event flush, concurrent event-line integrity, exact 5-MiB rotation, replacement of the single backup, symlink refusal, five-report retention, static-message panic capture, dynamic-message redaction, previous-hook invocation, invalid/unwritable directory degradation, required/ordered JSON fields, 256-KiB bound, and privacy sentinels supplied through environment and arguments.
- Run these tests through the existing locked workspace test command. Do not create a dedicated heavy workflow; measured pre-push and PR validation must stay within 60 and 150 seconds.
- Run `scripts/dependency-security.sh` during implementation/PR validation and record its result, but do not add the real networked security command to fast hooks or PR Actions.
- Retain strict compiler/Clippy policy and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit. This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-diagnostics --test diagnostics_process --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-app --locked`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-diagnostics --edges normal --locked`
- `bash scripts/dependency-security.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo tree -p rusttable-diagnostics --edges normal --locked` shows only `directories` as a normal third-party dependency and no logging/subscriber, serialization, async, network, UI, domain, catalog, image, or processing crate.
- A normal child run using an isolated directory creates `rusttable.log`, writes parseable single-line `Startup` and `Shutdown` records with exactly the ordered fixed fields, flushes each record, and exits zero.
- Eight threads recording fixed events concurrently produce the exact expected number of complete newline-terminated JSON records with no interleaving, truncation, duplicate fragments, or poisoned-lock panic.
- Starting with a regular current log of exactly 5 MiB produces one `rusttable.log.1` containing the prior bytes and a fresh log; a second rotation replaces rather than accumulates backups. A current/backup symlink is rejected without touching its target.
- A static-message panic exits nonzero and creates exactly one bounded crash report containing the required platform/location/backtrace fields; a dynamic-message panic records the redacted marker and not the supplied dynamic text.
- Creating seven valid crash reports plus unrelated/malformed files, directories, and symlinks leaves exactly the five newest valid regular reports and preserves every unrelated entry.
- Sentinels present only in an environment variable, command-line argument, current-directory name, and unrelated file are absent from the application log and crash report.
- The previous panic hook is invoked exactly once after the RustTable write attempt, and a report-directory/write/retention failure does not cause a second panic or suppress the original nonzero exit.
- App bootstrap unit tests prove that every diagnostics failure emits at most one warning for that stage, never replaces the iced result, and the iced application closure is called exactly once in every path.
- No public API accepts arbitrary diagnostic text/data; the crate contains no unsafe block, async/background writer, network request, upload endpoint, environment dump, telemetry identifier, or handwritten file over 1,000 lines.
- The complete dependency graph passes #29 policy, and measured clean pre-commit, pre-push, and PR validation remain at or below 30, 60, and 150 seconds respectively.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: feat(diagnostics): add bounded local events and privacy-preserving crash reports
feat(catalog): define deterministic import records and idempotency rules

issues.body:
## Why

After #21, RustTable has checked decoded-image values but no application-owned output contract. A safe first export boundary must make supported output formats, JPEG quality, encoded-size limits, alpha handling, metadata omission, destination collision semantics, and stable failures explicit before a concrete encoder or filesystem strategy is selected.

The earlier issue combined catalog lookup, source selection, decoding, JPEG/PNG encoding, temporary-file publication, metadata-marker inspection, and receipts in one PR. This issue now owns only codec-neutral output values and the create-new port in `rusttable-image`. The concrete adapter is split into #45; catalog-to-render-to-export orchestration remains later work.

## Dependencies

- Code-level dependency on #21, which defines `DecodedImage`, dimensions, pixel layout, and explicit unspecified color encoding.
- Sequenced after #42 so milestone-#4 public contracts and adapters land one at a time; this issue does not depend normally on `rusttable-catalog` or `rusttable-catalog-store`.
- Independent of milestone #5 processing and must not evaluate `Edit` operations or depend on `rusttable-processing`.
- **Milestone #4 sequence: 5 of 6.**
- Blocks #45, which implements this exact boundary with JPEG/PNG encoders and create-new filesystem behavior.

## How

- Write failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-image/tests/output_contract.rs` and `crates/rusttable-image/tests/output_errors.rs`; record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-image/src/output.rs` and re-export only the intentional public surface from `crates/rusttable-image/src/lib.rs`.
- Define `OutputFormat::{Png, Jpeg}` and a stable `SUPPORTED_OUTPUT_FORMATS` constant containing exactly PNG then JPEG. This list is independent of filename extensions and dependency capabilities.
- Define checked `JpegQuality` accepting exactly integer values `1..=100`, with a stable `get()` accessor and a typed range error. Do not accept codec defaults, floating-point quality, `0`, or values above `100`.
- Define checked `OutputLimits` with a nonzero maximum encoded-byte count. The concrete adapter must be configured with this value and return a typed size-limit error rather than growing an output buffer without a product bound.
- Define `OutputOptions` as a closed enum:
  - `Png`;
  - `Jpeg { quality: JpegQuality }`.
  Provide an explicit `format()` accessor. This shape must make “JPEG without quality” and “PNG with JPEG quality” unrepresentable.
- Define immutable checked `OutputReceipt` containing:
  - destination `PathBuf`;
  - explicit `OutputFormat`;
  - `ImageDimensions`;
  - nonzero encoded byte length.
  The receipt represents completed create-new publication by an adapter; it does not claim directory fsync, crash durability, catalog mutation, or source provenance.
- Define an object-safe `ImageOutput` trait with one operation equivalent to:
  `write_new(&self, image: &DecodedImage, destination: &Path, options: OutputOptions) -> Result<OutputReceipt, ImageOutputError>`.
- Document the create-new contract:
  - output format comes only from `OutputOptions`, never the extension;
  - an existing destination is never read, truncated, replaced, or deleted;
  - a receipt is returned only after the adapter’s publication step succeeds;
  - adapter-owned temporary state must not remain after a normally returned error;
  - stronger crash-atomicity or directory-durability guarantees are not part of this first portable contract.
- Define public, matchable `ImageOutputError` variants with relevant stable data for:
  - invalid destination;
  - missing destination parent;
  - destination already exists;
  - non-opaque JPEG input, including the first offending pixel index;
  - encoded output exceeding `OutputLimits`;
  - allocation failure;
  - encode failure with explicit format;
  - temporary-file creation failure;
  - write failure;
  - sync failure;
  - publish failure.
  Implement `Display` and `std::error::Error` without exposing `std::io::Error`, codec errors, tempfile errors, boxed errors, or string-only categories.
- Make metadata behavior explicit by omission: `DecodedImage`, `OutputOptions`, and `ImageOutput::write_new` carry no EXIF, XMP, IPTC, ICC, comment/text, timestamp, or permission payload. Implementations must not synthesize or copy user metadata. Codec-required headers and technical encoder chunks are outside the domain model and may be emitted by #45.
- Preserve the #21 color contract. The output boundary performs no orientation, ICC, gamma, transfer-function, working-space, premultiplication, or compositing behavior.
- Define JPEG alpha policy at the boundary: every input alpha byte must be `255`; implementations return `NonOpaqueJpegInput` rather than silently discard alpha or composite against an implicit color. PNG accepts the checked RGBA8 straight-alpha samples unchanged.
- Add a deterministic recording fake inside `output_contract.rs` to prove object safety, explicit format dispatch, borrowed image input, destination forwarding, and receipt/error propagation without filesystem or codec behavior.
- Add unit/integration coverage for quality bounds, output-limit construction, option format selection, receipt invariants, stable error matching/display, and create-new contract documentation/doctests.
- Keep JPEG/PNG encoders, the `image` dependency, tempfile facilities, filesystem calls, catalog/source lookup, import records, metadata writing, processing, UI, and darktable exporter behavior out of this PR.
- `rusttable-image` must retain no normal third-party dependency, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --test output_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --test output_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-image/src/output.rs` defines `OutputFormat`, `SUPPORTED_OUTPUT_FORMATS`, checked `JpegQuality`, checked `OutputLimits`, `OutputOptions`, checked `OutputReceipt`, object-safe `ImageOutput`, and matchable `ImageOutputError` without importing a codec, tempfile, database, catalog, or processing type.
- `SUPPORTED_OUTPUT_FORMATS` contains exactly PNG then JPEG in the documented stable order.
- JPEG quality `1` and `100` succeed and round-trip through `get()`; `0` and `101` return the exact typed range error. `OutputOptions` cannot represent JPEG without quality or PNG with quality.
- `OutputLimits` rejects zero and preserves its exact nonzero encoded-byte bound.
- A recording fake proves the explicit `OutputOptions` format is forwarded unchanged even when the destination extension is absent or contradictory; extensions are never consulted by the boundary.
- `OutputReceipt` rejects zero encoded bytes and preserves exact destination, explicit format, dimensions, and byte length. It contains no photo/source/catalog ID and makes no crash-durability claim.
- Callers can match invalid destination, missing parent, existing destination, non-opaque JPEG pixel, encoded-size limit, allocation, encode, temporary-file, write, sync, and publish failures without parsing strings or downcasting third-party errors.
- The trait contract explicitly forbids overwrite and requires cleanup after normally returned adapter errors; the real race/collision/cleanup behavior is observable in #45.
- No metadata input or metadata-preservation option exists. The boundary documents omission of EXIF/XMP/IPTC/ICC/comment/timestamp/permission payloads and permits only codec-required technical output from adapters.
- JPEG non-opaque input is an explicit error contract; PNG retains RGBA8 straight alpha. No implicit alpha compositing, orientation, ICC, gamma, transfer-function, or working-space conversion is introduced.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal` shows no normal third-party dependency. No filesystem, codec, catalog, database, serialization, UI, processing, async-runtime, or FFI dependency is introduced.
- No local unsafe Rust, inherited darktable exporter/preset/code, networked test, filesystem test, sleep, wall-clock dependency, randomness, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(image): define an explicit create-new PNG/JPEG output boundary
