issues.body:
## Why

The operation vocabulary from #18 is only one element of non-destructive history. RustTable also needs a small immutable `Edit` aggregate that binds an ordered operation sequence to one photo **and to the exact photo revision it was authored against**.

Without a base-photo revision, catalog code could not distinguish an edit for the current asset set from an edit built against stale photo state. Keeping this aggregate separate from operation-key/parameter validation makes the PR reviewable and prevents the catalog or processing layers from inventing edit invariants or mutating operation vectors in place.

This is clean-room Rust domain work. It must not reproduce darktable history-stack structs, module pointers, mutable parameter blobs, database row layouts, or processing-order rules.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #18, which defines immutable `Operation` values.
- Uses `PhotoId`, `EditId`, `OperationId`, and `Revision` from completed #13 and the revisioned `Photo` identity established by completed #16.
- **Milestone #3 sequence: 5 of 7.**
- Blocks #19, which registers edits and checks their photo/base-revision relationship.
- Also blocks #23, whose compiled pipeline consumes an `Edit` in authoritative authoring order.

## How

- Write the failing integration tests first in `crates/rusttable-core/tests/edit_model.rs` and record the initial failing command/output in the implementation PR before adding production code.
- Add `crates/rusttable-core/src/edit/edit.rs` and extend `crates/rusttable-core/src/edit/mod.rs` plus `crates/rusttable-core/src/lib.rs` only for deliberate public re-exports.
- Define immutable `Edit` state with private fields:
  - `EditId`;
  - `PhotoId`;
  - base photo `Revision`;
  - edit `Revision`;
  - `Vec<Operation>` in caller-supplied authoring order.
- Provide these checked construction paths:
  - `Edit::new(edit_id, photo_id, base_photo_revision, operations)`, which always starts the edit at `Revision::ZERO`;
  - `Edit::from_parts(edit_id, photo_id, base_photo_revision, revision, operations)`, for deterministic replay/reconstruction without I/O or serialization assumptions;
  - `Edit::revised(&self, operations)`, which retains `EditId`, `PhotoId`, and base-photo revision, advances the edit revision exactly once, and returns a new value without mutating `self`.
- Permit an empty operation vector. An empty edit is valid domain/history data and later compiles to an empty processing pipeline.
- Detect repeated `OperationId` values while consuming the caller's sequence, preserve the original vector order, and return `EditBuildError::DuplicateOperationId { operation_id }`; do not silently keep one value or sort the vector.
- Define `EditRevisionError` with distinct variants for edit-revision overflow and invalid replacement operations. Preserve an underlying `EditBuildError` as a typed `std::error::Error::source` rather than formatting it into a string.
- `Edit::revised` must call the existing `Revision::checked_increment()` before consuming/validating the replacement sequence. A max-revision edit therefore reports overflow deterministically and remains unchanged.
- Expose read-only `id()`, `photo_id()`, `base_photo_revision()`, `revision()`, and exact-order `operations()` access. Do not expose mutable collection access, indexing mutation, interior mutability, or global registries.
- Implement deterministic `Clone`, `Debug`, `Eq`, and ordered-vector equality. Equality must include both revisions and operation order. Do not require a hash implementation for the aggregate.
- Implement `Display` and `std::error::Error` for public errors without opaque catch-all variants.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, use only the Rust standard library in `rusttable-core`, and keep every handwritten Rust file at or below 1,000 lines.
- Keep persistence, serialization, undo/redo stacks, catalog commands, UI labels/focus, processing positions, automatic operation sorting, masks, pixel evaluation, filesystem/database behavior, FFI, and inherited C/C++ types out of this PR.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test edit_model --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --doc --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `crates/rusttable-core/src/edit/edit.rs` defines immutable `Edit`, `EditBuildError`, and `EditRevisionError`; only the intentional types are re-exported from the module and crate roots.
- `Edit::new` produces edit revision zero and preserves the exact supplied operation order, including disabled operations; it preserves the caller's `PhotoId` and base-photo revision exactly.
- `Edit::from_parts` preserves caller-supplied base-photo and edit revisions while enforcing the same duplicate-operation invariant as `Edit::new`.
- `Edit::revised` returns a distinct edit with the same `EditId`, `PhotoId`, and base-photo revision, an edit revision exactly one greater, and the exact replacement operation order; the original edit remains equal to its pre-call value.
- Two edits containing the same operations in different orders are observably unequal and iterate in their respective supplied orders. Edits differing only in base-photo revision or edit revision are also unequal.
- Empty edits are accepted. Any repeated `OperationId` is rejected with the exact duplicate ID and without panic, sorting, mutation, or partial construction.
- Revising an edit reconstructed with the existing `Revision::from_u64(u64::MAX)` API returns the typed overflow variant before consuming replacement operations and leaves the original edit unchanged.
- A replacement operation sequence containing a duplicate returns the typed nested build error and leaves the original edit unchanged; the nested source is available through `std::error::Error::source`.
- Tests cover construction, reconstruction, empty edits, base-photo revision preservation, order significance, duplicate IDs, successful revision, overflow, equality, accessors, and failure immutability with no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` shows no dependency. No unsafe Rust, C/C++ bridge, serialization/persistence behavior, processing policy, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(core): model immutable edits with ordered operations and revisions

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #8 / PR #35 added an exact Rust toolchain and a useful completed-run budget check, but the current `scripts/with-validation-budget.sh` waits indefinitely for its command and only returns `124` after that command eventually succeeds beyond the limit. The hook scripts also start parallel children without an `INT`/`TERM` cleanup trap, so a hung compiler, test, or Bun process can outlive the documented 30-second and 60-second contracts. Before PR CI depends on these checks, the existing soft measurement must become a real portable deadline rather than a duplicate budget implementation.

## How

- Update the existing `scripts/with-validation-budget.sh`; do not add a second budget helper.
- Change its callable contract to `run_with_budget <seconds> <label> <command> [args...]` while retaining a directly executable form for regression tests.
- Preserve an in-budget command's exact exit status, return `124` when the deadline expires, and print the supplied label plus elapsed/budget seconds.
- On deadline, `INT`, or `TERM`, terminate and reap the command and every validation child that the helper/script started before returning. Do not leave a compiler, test process, Bun process, watchdog, or delayed fixture running.
- Keep the implementation compatible with the default macOS Bash 3.2 and Ubuntu Bash. Do not require GNU `timeout`, GNU `date`, `gdate`, `wait -n`, associative arrays, or another language runtime.
- Expand `scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh` with deterministic fixtures for:
  - in-budget success;
  - exact nonzero-status propagation, using a status such as `7`;
  - argument and whitespace preservation;
  - deadline exit `124`;
  - `INT` and `TERM` cleanup;
  - prevention of a late marker-file side effect after the helper exits.
- Update `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` to pass a `pre-commit` label, retain its existing four checks exactly once, retain parallel execution, and clean up all outstanding PIDs through traps.
- Update `scripts/prepush-fast.sh` to pass a `pre-push` label, retain its existing three checks exactly once, add `--all-features` to the locked workspace test command, retain parallel execution, and clean up all outstanding PIDs through traps.
- Preserve per-check log capture and print every failed check's log even when multiple parallel checks fail.
- Preserve `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.githooks/pre-push`, and the explicit `RUSTTABLE_SKIP_PREPUSH=1` hook escape hatch. `scripts/prepush-fast.sh` itself must never silently skip.
- Do not add or edit GitHub Actions; #14 owns PR CI.
- Deliver this scope through exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Dependencies

- Depends on completed #8 / squash-merged PR #35.
- Blocks #14, which will use the stabilized commands in the 150-second pull-request workflow.
- **Best first open issue in milestone #1.**

## Validation

- `bash scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bun run test:computer-use`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Run the helper regression suite and both hook scripts on macOS and Ubuntu; record the clean durations and the timeout-cleanup evidence in the PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- The existing helper returns `0` for an in-budget successful fixture, preserves a fixture exit status of `7`, and returns exactly `124` at the deadline rather than waiting for the over-budget command to finish naturally.
- Arguments containing spaces and shell metacharacters reach the fixture unchanged; the helper prints the validation label and elapsed/budget seconds.
- After deadline, `INT`, or `TERM`, no validation child or watchdog remains and a delayed marker file is never created.
- A clean pre-commit run executes formatting, locked metadata, source policy, and Computer Use tests exactly once, reports `pre-commit` against `30s`, and completes at or below 30 seconds on the recorded macOS and Ubuntu runs.
- A clean pre-push run executes locked all-feature tests, locked all-feature Clippy, and Computer Use tests exactly once, reports `pre-push` against `60s`, and completes at or below 60 seconds on the recorded macOS and Ubuntu runs.
- Deliberately breaking two parallel checks in the same run makes the script fail and prints both responsible logs.
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and `.githooks/pre-push` still invoke the repository-owned scripts, and no GitHub workflow changes in this PR.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: chore: turn local validation budgets into hard deadlines

issues.body:
## Why

After #19 can register photos and initial edits, RustTable needs one explicit transition for replacing an edit without lost updates, identity drift, or accidental rebasing onto a different photo revision. This boundary must distinguish catalog-level staleness from edit-level staleness, reject attempts to move or rebase an edit implicitly, and prove that every failure is atomic.

Keeping replacement in its own PR prevents the registration-state issue from mixing construction, insertion, optimistic concurrency, and replacement semantics. This remains deterministic in-memory Rust use-case behavior—not persistence, undo/redo, UI state, or an incremental port of darktable history/database code.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #19, which establishes `CatalogState::apply`, registration commands, canonical state maps, and the initial `CatalogError` surface.
- Uses the immutable, revisioned `Edit` aggregate from #38, including its base-photo revision.
- **Milestone #3 sequence: 7 of 7 and the final issue in the initial “Architecture & Core Domain” foundation.**
- Blocks #21 and the later image-I/O/import sequence, which may rely on the completed catalog state/error contract.

## How

- Write the failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/edit_replacement.rs` and extend `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_errors.rs`. Record the initial failing commands/output in the implementation PR before production changes.
- Extend `CatalogCommand` in `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/command.rs` with exactly one variant:
  - `ReplaceEdit { edit_id: EditId, expected_edit_revision: Revision, replacement: Edit }`.
- Extend the existing `CatalogState::apply(expected_catalog_revision, command)` implementation in `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/state.rs`; do not add a second mutation entry point.
- Validate replacement in this deterministic order before mutating state:
  1. the caller's expected catalog revision matches the current catalog revision, using the #19 behavior;
  2. `edit_id` identifies an existing edit;
  3. `replacement.id()` equals the explicit target `edit_id`;
  4. `replacement.photo_id()` equals the existing edit's `PhotoId`;
  5. `replacement.base_photo_revision()` equals the existing edit's base-photo revision;
  6. `expected_edit_revision` equals the existing edit revision;
  7. the existing edit revision has a checked successor;
  8. `replacement.revision()` equals that exact successor;
  9. the catalog revision has a checked successor.
- Only after every validation succeeds, replace the one `BTreeMap` entry and advance the catalog revision exactly once. Preserve all unrelated entries and canonical map ordering.
- Extend the public typed `CatalogError` in `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/error.rs` with distinct, matchable variants carrying the relevant IDs/revisions:
  - `UnknownEdit`;
  - `EditIdMismatch`;
  - `EditPhotoMismatch`;
  - `EditBasePhotoRevisionMismatch`;
  - `EditRevisionConflict`;
  - `InvalidEditRevisionAdvance`;
  - `EditRevisionOverflow`.
- Retain all #19 variants for catalog revision conflict/overflow and registration failures. Implement stable `Display` and `std::error::Error` without an opaque catch-all or string-erased error data.
- Add deterministic replay and failure-atomicity fixtures. For every publicly reachable failure variant, clone the complete pre-call `CatalogState`, apply the command, and assert the post-call state is equal to the clone.
- Add multi-violation tests that prove the documented validation precedence—for example, an unknown explicit target whose replacement also has a mismatched identity must report `UnknownEdit`, while a known target with both photo and revision mismatches reports `EditPhotoMismatch` first.
- `EditRevisionOverflow` is intentionally unreachable through normal public registration because #19 accepts only revision-zero edits and this transition advances one revision at a time. Test that defensive branch in the existing private `#[cfg(test)]` module in `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/state.rs` by constructing an internally consistent max-revision fixture with `Edit::from_parts`. Do not add a public unchecked/test constructor or attempt `u64::MAX` public transitions.
- The private overflow test must clone the complete state, assert `EditRevisionOverflow`, and prove no map/revision mutation. Retain the private catalog-revision-overflow coverage established by #19.
- Keep the public state surface read-only except for `apply`. Do not expose mutable maps, locks, atomics, threads, async tasks, event buses, callbacks, persistence traits, database transactions, filesystem paths, Iced messages, processing execution, or global state.
- Retain `rusttable-catalog -> rusttable-core` as the only normal dependency, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test edit_replacement --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog state::tests --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `CatalogCommand` adds only the documented `ReplaceEdit` variant; registration command behavior from #19 remains unchanged.
- Replacing an existing edit with matching catalog/edit expectations, identical `EditId`/`PhotoId`/base-photo revision, and edit revision exactly `current + 1` succeeds, changes only that edit entry, and advances the catalog revision exactly once.
- The replacement operation vector is retained in its exact authoring order, and canonical iteration of the surrounding `BTreeMap` is unchanged.
- An unknown target, replacement-ID mismatch, replacement-photo mismatch, base-photo-revision mismatch, stale edit expectation, skipped/reused replacement revision, edit-revision overflow, catalog-revision conflict, and catalog-revision overflow each return their exact observable `CatalogError` variant.
- A replacement cannot silently rebase an edit: changing only `base_photo_revision` is rejected even when ID, photo ID, operations, and expected revisions otherwise match.
- Validation precedence follows the documented order in commands that violate multiple rules; tests assert the exact selected variant.
- Every failed replacement leaves the catalog revision, every photo, every edit, and their canonical iteration order equal to the complete pre-call state; no partial replacement is observable.
- The max-edit-revision branch is covered only through a private module test; the public API remains unable to inject arbitrary catalog maps or bypass #19's revision-zero creation invariant.
- Replaying the same valid registration/replacement command sequence from equal initial states yields equal final states, equal revisions, and identical ascending photo/edit iteration.
- Error `Display` output is stable and useful, while callers can match all error data without parsing strings or downcasting opaque errors.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal` shows only `rusttable-core`; no UI, I/O, persistence, database, codec, processing, FFI, async-runtime, synchronization, or opaque-error dependency is introduced.
- Tests use no network, filesystem, wall clock, randomness, sleeps, or external fixtures. No unsafe Rust, C/C++ bridge, public unchecked constructor, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(catalog): add optimistic edit replacement and conflict boundaries

issues.body:
## Why

#32 defines `SourcePath`, `ImportRecord`, idempotency rules, and an object-safe repository port, but deliberately uses deterministic fakes and chooses no storage technology. RustTable needs one durable adapter that proves imported photo records can be committed atomically, closed, reopened, listed canonically, and replayed into equal catalog state without moving database or serialization concerns into `rusttable-core`, `rusttable-image`, or `rusttable-catalog`.

This PR is persistence-only. It must not inspect files, decode images, compute hashes, allocate IDs, parse metadata, orchestrate imports, persist edits, or add output behavior. Splitting the adapter from #32 keeps the application contract reviewable independently from the embedded store, schema, and byte encoding.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #32 and implements its exact `ImportRepository`, `ImportRecord`, `SourcePath`, lookup, conflict, commit, and canonical-listing contracts.
- Transitively encodes checked domain/image/metadata values from #13, #16, #21, and #28.
- **Milestone #4 sequence: 4 of 6.**
- Blocks the sequencing gate for #33; #33 itself remains independent of the store at the Rust dependency level.

## How

- Write failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-catalog-store` as a root workspace member with inherited workspace lints and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Give the adapter direct path dependencies only on the RustTable crates whose public values it must reconstruct: `rusttable-catalog`, `rusttable-core`, and `rusttable-image`. It must not depend on `rusttable-app`, `rusttable-image-io`, or `rusttable-processing`.
- Use one reviewed, pinned, toolchain-compatible `redb` release as the embedded transactional store. Keep `redb` and any encoding/serialization dependencies private to `rusttable-catalog-store`; no such type may appear in a public RustTable API.
- Implement:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/src/lib.rs` for the intentional `RedbImportRepository` surface;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/src/repository.rs` for the exact #32 repository behavior and error translation;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/src/schema.rs` for table definitions, database schema version, and open-time compatibility checks;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/src/codec.rs` for explicit versioned adapter DTOs and checked encode/decode.
- Define one database schema version and one import-record format version. Creating a new database must write the schema version in the same transaction that initializes all required tables. Opening an existing database must validate the schema before exposing a repository handle or performing any write.
- Use separate tables for:
  - schema metadata;
  - import records keyed by canonical `SourcePath` UTF-8 bytes;
  - the unique `PhotoId -> SourcePath` index;
  - the unique `AssetId -> SourcePath` index.
- Implement `ImportRepository::commit` as one redb write transaction. The record and both unique indexes must become visible together or not at all. Recheck all three uniqueness constraints inside that transaction rather than trusting #32’s preflight lookups.
- Implement source/photo/asset lookup and canonical listing with read transactions that return owned RustTable values. Never expose redb transactions, guards, table handles, borrowed pages, or database lifetimes.
- Encode adapter DTOs explicitly and version them independently from Rust memory layout:
  - `PhotoId`/`AssetId` use fixed-width canonical 16-byte big-endian values or the exact canonical 32-character lowercase form from #13;
  - SHA-256 uses exactly 32 digest bytes with an explicit algorithm tag;
  - revisions and byte lengths use fixed-width documented integer encodings;
  - input formats, metadata fields, metadata-entry kinds, orientation, and asset roles use stable documented names/codes rather than Rust enum discriminants;
  - `PositiveRational` stores explicit numerator and denominator;
  - text stores exact UTF-8 bytes with checked lengths;
  - `SourcePath` stores its canonical `/`-separated UTF-8 bytes.
  Do not persist `Debug`/`Display` output except where #13 explicitly defines that representation as canonical, native-endian integers, pointer-sized values, Rust discriminants, or platform-native path separators.
- Decode only through checked RustTable constructors. Non-canonical IDs, zero/invalid integers, unknown tags, invalid UTF-8, invalid orientation, invalid rational, duplicate metadata field, unsupported record version, or a record that violates `Asset`, `Photo`, `ImportRecord`, or `SourcePath` invariants must return `ImportRepositoryError::CorruptPersistedData` without yielding a partial value.
- Define deterministic persistence as:
  - equal logical records encode to equal adapter value bytes;
  - canonical keys and index values are stable across insertion order and supported platforms;
  - `list()` returns ascending canonical `SourcePath` order;
  - replaying listed records’ photos through `CatalogState::apply(RegisterPhoto)` produces equal catalog state and revisions for equal stored record sets.
  Do not require complete redb database files to be byte-for-byte identical.
- Add/update the architecture contract so:
  - `rusttable-core` and `rusttable-image` remain free of normal third-party dependencies;
  - `rusttable-catalog` remains free of database/serialization dependencies;
  - redb and encoding dependencies exist only below `rusttable-catalog-store`;
  - `rusttable-catalog-store` has no app, codec, UI, processing, FFI, CMake, or inherited C/C++ dependency.
- Add failing-first tests in:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/tests/repository_round_trip.rs` for commit, all three lookup paths, close/reopen, and canonical listing;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/tests/repository_conflicts.rs` for source/photo/asset conflicts detected inside the transaction and complete rollback;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/tests/schema_versions.rs` for new, current, missing/malformed, older-unsupported, and unknown-newer schemas;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/tests/canonical_encoding.rs` for exact DTO key/value fixtures, order-independent insertion, and cross-value round trips;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog-store/tests/catalog_replay.rs` for deterministic reconstruction of catalog photo state from canonical stored records;
  - corruption tests in `codec.rs` or a focused integration test covering each checked decode boundary without relying on arbitrary fuzz timing.
- Use isolated temporary directories. Test data must use explicit fixed IDs/hashes/metadata; do not use random IDs, current timestamps, sleeps, network access, or the user’s real catalog.
- Do not persist decoded pixel buffers, edits/history, thumbnails, caches, absolute import roots, filesystem timestamps, UI state, output settings, third-party error strings, or darktable compatibility rows.
- Do not copy darktable SQL, table/column names, migration code, image records, C structs, serialization layouts, constants, fixtures, or tests.
- Retain strict workspace lints and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit; split codec/schema/repository modules before approaching the limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store --test repository_round_trip --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store --test repository_conflicts --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store --test schema_versions --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store --test canonical_encoding --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store --test catalog_replay --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store codec::tests --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog-store --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `RedbImportRepository` implements the exact #32 `ImportRepository` surface without widening the trait or exposing a redb/encoding/serde type.
- Committing one valid record makes the record plus its photo and asset indexes visible in one transaction; closing and reopening returns an `ImportRecord` equal to the committed value through source, photo-ID, and asset-ID lookup.
- Source, `PhotoId`, and `AssetId` conflicts detected at commit time return their exact #32 conflict categories and leave record/index table contents logically equal to the complete pre-transaction state.
- `list()` returns ascending canonical `SourcePath` order regardless of insertion order. Equal record sets inserted in different orders produce equal canonical key/value fixtures and equal lookup/listing results; complete database-file bytes are not asserted.
- Replaying listed records’ photos in canonical order through the completed catalog registration API yields equal catalog state, equal revisions, and identical photo iteration for equal stored record sets.
- A new database writes the current schema version. Missing/malformed, unsupported older, and unknown newer schema versions fail closed before writes and do not perform an implicit migration.
- The record format has an explicit version. Unknown record versions, non-canonical IDs/hashes/paths, unknown tags, invalid UTF-8/rational/orientation, duplicate metadata fields, and values that fail RustTable aggregate constructors return the corrupt-data category without panic or partial value.
- Exact codec fixture tests demonstrate stable documented key/value bytes for representative IDs, source path, hash, image probe, all metadata entry kinds, and record structure; Rust enum order or memory layout is not part of the format.
- `rusttable-core`, `rusttable-image`, and `rusttable-catalog` gain no redb, encoding, database, or filesystem dependency. Those concerns remain confined to `rusttable-catalog-store`, which gains no app, codec, UI, or processing dependency.
- No decoded pixels, edit history, thumbnails, caches, absolute roots, timestamps, output settings, inherited darktable schema/code, or third-party error strings are persisted.
- No local unsafe Rust, C/C++ build target, networked test, sleep, wall-clock dependency, randomness, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.
issues.title: feat(catalog-store): persist import records transactionally with a versioned schema

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #30 / merged PR #34 provides a safe local Computer Use installer and creates `target/release/bundle/macos/RustTable.app`, but it does not define a release/distribution smoke contract. On current `main`, the bundle builder hardcodes `0.1.0`, runs the release build without `--locked`, validates only `CFBundleIdentifier`, and omits root `LICENSE`. The application binary has no side-effect-free version probe, and no merge-to-main job executes, archives, checksums, verifies, and uploads the staged application.

Those gaps must be closed without moving release compilation, bundle assembly, archive work, GUI startup, or LaunchServices mutation into the 30-second pre-commit, 60-second pre-push, or 150-second pull-request path.

## Dependencies

- Builds on completed #30 / PR #34. Extend the existing `install:computer-use` builder and bundle location; do not create a competing package script or second macOS bundle layout.
- Depends on #31 so the distributed binary contains the bounded production diagnostics bootstrap and the CLI test can prove `--version` bypasses it.
- Depends on #24 so the initial distribution artifact is not declared ready before merge-only performance budgets exist and pass.
- Through #31, depends on #29; through #24 and #29, depends on #17 and #40.
- Uses the completed `crates/rusttable-app` binary from #5.
- **Milestone #6 sequence: 4 of 4 and the final issue in the initial quality/performance/release-readiness sequence.**
- Signing, notarization, update feeds, public GitHub Releases, and non-macOS installers remain separate future issues.

## How

- Extend the existing Bun tooling without adding npm/Bun runtime dependencies or another installer command.
- Add `scripts/dev/rusttable-app-bundle.ts` and `scripts/dev/rusttable-app-bundle.test.ts` for package-version resolution, plist construction, strict manifest parsing, bundle construction, and identity/version validation. Keep transactional installation and LaunchServices behavior in `scripts/dev/computer-use-app-install.ts`; keep executable orchestration in `scripts/dev/install-computer-use-app.ts`.
- Update `package.json` so `bun run test:computer-use` runs both the existing installer tests and new bundle tests while retaining exact `bun@1.3.14` metadata and no third-party package sections/lockfile.
- Resolve the version by running `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`, selecting exactly one workspace package whose name is `rusttable-app` and whose manifest path is `crates/rusttable-app/Cargo.toml`. Reject missing/duplicate packages, malformed JSON, non-string versions, or a version that cannot be represented by the required macOS plist fields. Do not parse Cargo TOML with a regular expression or duplicate the product version in TypeScript.
- Change the existing release build to exactly:
  `cargo build --release --package rusttable-app --bin rusttable-app --locked`.
- Assemble a clean `target/release/bundle/macos/RustTable.app` containing only:
  - `Contents/MacOS/RustTable`, copied from `target/release/rusttable-app` and executable;
  - `Contents/Info.plist`, containing `CFBundleDisplayName=RustTable`, `CFBundleName=RustTable`, `CFBundleExecutable=RustTable`, `CFBundleIdentifier=com.cgasgarth.rusttable`, `CFBundlePackageType=APPL`, and both version fields derived from the locked `rusttable-app` package version;
  - `Contents/Resources/LICENSE`, copied byte-for-byte from root `LICENSE`.
- Replace identifier-only inspection with a RustTable-owned `BundleManifest` value. The parser must detect a missing or duplicate required plist key and reject unexpected display/name/executable/identifier/package-type/version values. Validate the source bundle and staged transactional copy before installation, registration, launch, deletion, or archive creation.
- Preserve #30’s exact installed identifier `com.cgasgarth.rusttable`; do not rename the product identifier in this PR.
- Add CLI dispatch before `rusttable_app::run()` in `crates/rusttable-app/src/main.rs` and focused integration coverage in `crates/rusttable-app/tests/cli.rs` using `CARGO_BIN_EXE_rusttable-app`.
- With the sole argument `--version`, print exactly `RustTable <CARGO_PKG_VERSION>\n` and exit zero before iced initialization, #31 diagnostics installation, directory/file creation, or any installer/LaunchServices code. Preserve the zero-argument desktop launch path. Reject `--version` combined with any other argument with a nonzero status and concise stderr.
- Add non-timing Bun tests for:
  - Cargo metadata package/version selection and every rejection branch;
  - exact plist generation and round-trip manifest values;
  - missing, duplicate, and mismatched required fields one field at a time;
  - byte-identical license copying;
  - refusal to mutate a source, staged, or installed bundle whose complete manifest is invalid;
  - preservation of the #30 transactional rollback and worktree identity filters.
- Add `scripts/macos-distribution-smoke.sh` as the repository-owned smoke implementation. It must require macOS, resolve repository root, and start from clean `target/distribution/` and `target/release/bundle/macos/RustTable.app` paths. It must:
  1. run `bun run install:computer-use -- --compact --no-install --no-launch`;
  2. validate `Info.plist` with `plutil -lint` and compare every required field to locked Cargo metadata;
  3. verify the bundle contains only the documented payload and that `Contents/MacOS/RustTable` is executable;
  4. execute the bundled binary with `--version` using an isolated `RUSTTABLE_DIAGNOSTICS_DIR`, compare exact stdout/stderr/status, and prove the directory remains absent;
  5. compare bundled and root licenses byte-for-byte;
  6. create `target/distribution/RustTable-<version>-macos-unsigned.zip` with `COPYFILE_DISABLE=1 ditto -c -k --keepParent` so resource forks, extended-attribute sidecars, and `__MACOSX` entries do not expand the declared payload;
  7. write `RustTable-<version>-macos-unsigned.zip.sha256` with `shasum -a 256` and immediately verify it;
  8. extract the archive into a clean verification directory, reject any top-level entry other than `RustTable.app`, revalidate its manifest/license/payload, and rerun the archived executable’s exact `--version` probe;
  9. write `target/distribution/smoke.log` containing schema version, Cargo package version, Git commit SHA, bundle identifier, archive basename/size, checksum, and one named pass record per assertion.
- The workflow must invoke #40’s helper as:
  `bash scripts/with-validation-budget.sh 600 macos-distribution-smoke bash scripts/macos-distribution-smoke.sh`.
  A timeout must return `124`, terminate/reap the complete build/smoke process tree, and leave no delayed archive or success marker.
- Extend the existing `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` with one `macos-distribution-smoke` job on `macos-latest`, triggered only by the workflow’s existing `push` to `main` and `workflow_dispatch` events. Use read-only contents permission, immutable full-SHA action pins, Rust from `rust-toolchain.toml`, exact Bun `1.3.14`, `timeout-minutes: 15`, lockfile-aware caches, and upload `target/distribution/` under `if: always()`.
- Standard GitHub checkout/tool setup, locked Cargo dependency retrieval, cache operations, and artifact upload are permitted workflow infrastructure. The built RustTable executable and smoke assertions must perform no application telemetry, update check, crash upload, product API call, or other product-level network request.
- Do not install to `~/Applications`, mutate LaunchServices, open a GUI window, invoke inherited C/C++/CMake targets, publish a release, use repository write permission, consume signing secrets, codesign, or notarize in the merge job.
- Do not add release compilation, bundle assembly, archive extraction, or checksum work to `scripts/precommit-fast.sh`, `scripts/prepush-fast.sh`, `scripts/pr-ci.sh`, or `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml`. Only Rust CLI tests and non-timing Bun contract tests flow through normal PR validation.
- Retain strict compiler/Clippy policy, `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit. This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-app --test cli --locked`
- `cargo run --release -p rusttable-app --bin rusttable-app --locked -- --version`
- `bun run test:computer-use`
- `bun run install:computer-use -- --help`
- `bun run install:computer-use -- --compact --no-install --no-launch`
- `bash scripts/with-validation-budget.sh 600 macos-distribution-smoke bash scripts/macos-distribution-smoke.sh`
- `plutil -lint target/release/bundle/macos/RustTable.app/Contents/Info.plist`
- `cmp LICENSE target/release/bundle/macos/RustTable.app/Contents/Resources/LICENSE`
- `shasum -a 256 -c target/distribution/RustTable-*-macos-unsigned.zip.sha256`
- `bash scripts/dependency-security.sh`
- `bash scripts/performance-budgets.sh`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/rust-main.yml`
- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- Locked Cargo metadata is the sole version source: changing `[workspace.package].version` in a temporary verification commit changes the binary output, both plist version fields, archive filename, and smoke log without editing TypeScript constants; reverting restores the original values.
- The CLI integration test proves exact `--version` stdout/newline/status, rejects combined arguments, and proves the version path creates no native window, diagnostics directory/file, application directory, LaunchServices side effect, or product-level network request.
- The staged and extracted applications contain only the documented executable/plist/license payload; `plutil` accepts both plists, the executables are runnable, both licenses are byte-identical to root `LICENSE`, and the zip contains no `__MACOSX`/AppleDouble/resource-fork sidecar.
- Bundle tests reject each missing, duplicate, and mismatched required field before any install, registration, launch, deletion, or archive operation; the existing #30 rollback and same-named-foreign-bundle protections remain covered.
- A clean smoke run exits zero within 600 seconds, verifies both staged and archived executables, produces the unsigned zip/checksum/log, and verifies the checksum immediately.
- Corrupting the executable mode/content, any manifest field, license, archive content, or checksum fails at the corresponding named assertion and produces no final success record.
- A deterministic timeout fixture returns `124`, leaves no compiler/Bun/smoke child, and cannot create a delayed archive or success marker.
- Every push to `main` runs the finite-timeout macOS distribution-smoke job and uploads the log plus any produced archive/checksum on success or failure; pull-request events never execute release packaging.
- The merge job performs no installation, LaunchServices mutation, GUI launch, signing, notarization, release publication, inherited darktable build, secret access, repository write, or product-level telemetry/update/crash-upload request.
- Clean pre-commit, pre-push, and PR validation remain at or below 30, 60, and 150 seconds respectively.
- One PR closes only this issue and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: build(release): add a merge-only macOS distribution smoke artifact

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable needs an explicit dependency direction before domain behavior grows. This prevents catalog state from becoming coupled to iced, image codecs, persistence, processing backends, or inherited C/C++ runtime types.

## How

- adds `crates/rusttable-catalog` with the one-way normal dependency `rusttable-catalog -> rusttable-core`
- keeps `rusttable-core` dependency-free and documents the allowed direction in both crate roots
- forbids unsafe Rust in the catalog crate
- adds a metadata-driven architecture contract test for dependency count and forbidden integration dependencies
- updates workspace membership and lockfile only; no domain, UI, image, metadata, processing, or persistence behavior

Validation: cargo metadata, core/catalog dependency trees, boundary tests, locked workspace Clippy/tests, rustfmt, source policy, and local pre-commit/pre-push budgets.

Closes #10.
issues.title: arch: establish core and catalog crate boundaries

issues.body:
## Why

#33 defines the codec-neutral create-new output contract: explicit PNG/JPEG selection, required JPEG quality, encoded-byte limits, opaque-only JPEG input, metadata omission, typed failures, and no-overwrite semantics. RustTable now needs one concrete adapter PR proving that checked `DecodedImage` values can be encoded and published without extension-based dispatch, silent alpha loss, unbounded encoded data, implicit overwrite, copied user metadata, or leaked partial destinations.

Keeping this adapter separate from #33 prevents codec/filesystem implementation details from widening the public boundary. Catalog lookup, import records, processing, color management, and UI orchestration remain outside the first output slice.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #33 and implements its exact `OutputFormat`, `JpegQuality`, `OutputLimits`, `OutputOptions`, `OutputReceipt`, `ImageOutput`, and `ImageOutputError` surface.
- Uses the checked `DecodedImage`, dimensions, RGBA8 straight-alpha layout, and explicit unspecified color encoding from #21.
- **Milestone #4 sequence: 6 of 6 and the final issue in the first image-I/O, metadata, import-persistence, and output-boundary sequence.**
- Independent of milestone #5 processing. This PR must not depend on `rusttable-processing` or evaluate an `Edit`.

## How

- Write failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-image-io/src/output.rs` and re-export only `FileImageOutput` from `crates/rusttable-image-io/src/lib.rs`.
- Implement `FileImageOutput` for the exact `ImageOutput` trait from #33. Its checked constructor must require `OutputLimits`; do not add a second output trait, an unbounded constructor, or a public codec/tempfile/filesystem implementation type.
- Reuse the reviewed `image` dependency introduced by #21 with `default-features = false` and only the explicit JPEG/PNG feature set. Do not enable the default format bundle, Rayon, AVIF, TIFF, WebP, GIF, BMP, HEIF, or other encoders.
- Select the encoder exclusively from `OutputOptions`:
  - `OutputOptions::Png` always writes PNG;
  - `OutputOptions::Jpeg { quality }` always writes JPEG with the exact validated quality.
  Destination extensions are never read as format policy and never override the explicit option.
- PNG behavior:
  - accept the checked RGBA8 straight-alpha buffer from #21;
  - preserve dimensions and every RGBA sample through an encode/decode round trip;
  - perform no orientation, premultiplication, gamma, ICC, transfer-function, or working-space transform.
- JPEG behavior:
  - scan alpha before any encoded allocation or filesystem mutation;
  - return `ImageOutputError::NonOpaqueJpegInput` with the first non-`255` pixel index;
  - convert checked opaque RGBA8 samples to RGB8 with checked length arithmetic and fallible allocation;
  - pass the exact `JpegQuality` to the JPEG encoder;
  - never discard non-opaque alpha or composite against an implicit color.
- Enforce `OutputLimits` while bytes are produced, not only after encoding completes. Add an adapter-private limited writer that:
  - checks `current_len + incoming_len` with overflow-safe arithmetic before every append;
  - refuses an append whose resulting logical encoded length would exceed the configured bound;
  - never requests a reserve for more logical bytes than the remaining bound;
  - uses `try_reserve`/fallible allocation and maps size exhaustion separately from allocator failure;
  - never exposes its type from the crate.
  The contract bounds logical retained bytes and requested growth; it does not make a false promise about allocator capacity rounding.
- Implement create-new publication in this order:
  1. validate that the destination has a file name and an existing parent directory;
  2. validate image/options compatibility, including JPEG alpha;
  3. encode through the limited writer completely before touching the destination directory;
  4. create a uniquely named temporary file in the destination directory using an established adapter-private temporary-file facility;
  5. write all encoded bytes, flush, and `sync_all` the temporary file;
  6. publish with a no-clobber operation so a destination that existed initially or appears during publication is never overwritten;
  7. return `OutputReceipt` only after publication succeeds, using the final encoded byte length.
- Do not claim stronger cross-platform crash atomicity or directory durability than the primitive provides. The observable first-slice contract is: no overwrite, no partial final destination before successful publication, a synced temporary file before publication, and cleanup of adapter-owned temporary files after normally returned errors.
- On a no-clobber collision, preserve the existing destination byte-for-byte. Drop/close the returned temporary-file handle so its path is removed before returning the typed collision error.
- Map invalid destination, missing parent, destination exists, non-opaque JPEG, encoded-size limit, allocation, encode, temporary-file creation, write, sync, and publish failures to the exact #33 variants. Preserve relevant destination/format/pixel/limit data without exposing `image`, `tempfile`, or `std::io` errors.
- Metadata is absent from the #33 payload. The adapter must not synthesize or copy EXIF, XMP, IPTC, ICC, user comments/text, source timestamps, or source permissions. Codec-required headers and technical encoder-generated chunks/markers are allowed.
- Keep deterministic failure and publication-race injection behind `#[cfg(test)]` adapter-private seams in `output.rs`; do not widen the public API and do not use sleep-based thread races.
- Add failing-first tests in:
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/output_png.rs` for exact RGBA round trip, dimensions, absent/contradictory extension, encoded limit, and create-new success;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/output_jpeg.rs` for exact quality forwarding, dimensions, opaque success, pre-mutation non-opaque rejection, and encoded limit;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/output_collisions.rs` for a destination that already exists before the call;
  - `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/output_metadata.rs` for absence of user metadata marker/chunk classes in generated fixtures;
  - unit tests beside `crates/rusttable-image-io/src/output.rs` for exact limited-writer boundaries, a destination inserted deterministically immediately before publish, and injected encode/allocation/temp/write/sync/publish failures plus cleanup.
- Use tiny purpose-built pixel buffers and temporary directories. Tests must not use the network, sleeps, wall-clock-generated assertions, randomness-dependent expectations, or the user’s real files.
- Do not copy darktable exporters, format plugins, parameter structs, presets, metadata tables, C headers, fixtures, constants, or tests.
- Do not create parent directories, overwrite destinations, read catalog state, select imported sources, apply edits, add UI, or add RAW/TIFF/WebP/GIF/HEIF/AVIF output in this PR.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.
- Deliver exactly one focused PR that closes only this issue.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_png --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_jpeg --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_collisions --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_metadata --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io output::tests --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image-io --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `FileImageOutput` implements the exact object-safe `ImageOutput` trait from #33, requires checked `OutputLimits` at construction, and exposes no codec, limited-writer, tempfile, or filesystem type.
- `OutputOptions::Png` produces a PNG and `OutputOptions::Jpeg` produces a JPEG even when the destination has no extension or a contradictory extension.
- A PNG round trip of the reviewed RGBA8 fixture preserves exact dimensions and byte-for-byte RGBA samples without color/orientation transformation.
- JPEG output preserves dimensions, forwards the exact validated quality, and rejects a non-opaque input with the first offending pixel index before encoded allocation, temporary-file creation, or destination mutation.
- The limited writer accepts an append that brings logical length exactly to the configured bound, rejects the next byte with the exact encoded-size error, uses checked arithmetic, and never requests/retains more logical bytes than the bound. Tests do not assert allocator capacity because capacity rounding is not controlled by the adapter.
- Both PNG and JPEG have focused too-small-limit tests that return the encoded-size error and create no filesystem output.
- An existing destination remains byte-for-byte unchanged. A destination inserted through the private pre-publish seam is also never overwritten and returns the exact collision error without timing or sleeps.
- Injected encode, allocation, temporary-file, write, sync, and publish failures return the documented typed category, create no final destination, and leave no adapter temporary file after the method returns `Err`.
- The adapter writes and syncs the temporary file before no-clobber publication. The receipt is returned only after publication and reports the exact explicit format, dimensions, destination, and final nonzero byte length; it makes no directory-fsync or universal crash-atomicity claim.
- Generated files contain no EXIF, XMP, IPTC, ICC, user comment/text, source timestamp, or source-permission payload copied or synthesized by RustTable. Tests permit codec-required and technical encoder-generated chunks/markers.
- The adapter performs no orientation, ICC, gamma, transfer-function, premultiplication, or working-space conversion; `ColorEncoding::Unspecified` remains unchanged at the boundary.
- `rusttable-image` retains no normal third-party dependency. Codec and temporary-file dependencies remain confined to `rusttable-image-io`, which gains no catalog, store, UI, or processing dependency.
- No local unsafe Rust, C/C++ build step, inherited darktable implementation/fixture, networked test, sleep, wall-clock-dependent assertion, implicit overwrite/compositing, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR contains recorded red-test evidence, passes every listed command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged.

## Why

Typed, deterministic domain primitives are required before RustTable can represent photos, assets, edits, operations, or catalog state without mixing entities or admitting invalid values.

## Why

RustTable needs a deterministic distinction between logical photos and immutable source assets before catalog, metadata, or processing behavior is added. This model keeps identity, revision, roles, digest bytes, and observed length independent of I/O and runtime state.

## How

- adds immutable `Asset`, `AssetRole`, `ByteLength`, `ContentHash::Sha256`, and read-only accessors
- adds immutable `Photo` with derived primary asset ID and canonical `BTreeMap` ordering

- supports revision-preserving reconstruction without persistence or serialization policy
- adds focused integration coverage for values, construction, equality/order independence, and all validation branches

## Why

RustTable needs an immutable, validated representation of one non-destructive operation before edit history or processing compilation is introduced. This keeps operation data independent of plugins, masks, processing positions, serialization, persistence, and legacy C layouts.

## Why

An operation is only one element of non-destructive history. RustTable needs an immutable edit aggregate that binds ordered operations to a photo and the exact base-photo revision they were authored against.

## How

- adds immutable `Edit` in `crates/rusttable-core/src/edit/edit.rs`

## Why

RustTable needs an in-memory, deterministic boundary that registers photos and initial edits without leaking UI, persistence, filesystem, codec, processing, or adapter behavior into the catalog.

## Why

RustTable now has an explicit import-record contract, and this PR gives it one durable transactional adapter without moving storage or encoding concerns into the core, image, or catalog crates. The adapter validates schema compatibility before writes, stores records plus unique indexes atomically, and reconstructs equal RustTable values after close/reopen.

## How

- Adds `rusttable-catalog-store` with pinned `redb`, `postcard`, and private `serde` DTO dependencies.
- Adds a versioned schema metadata table plus records, photo-ID, and asset-ID index tables.
- Implements `RedbImportRepository` with owned RustTable values only, canonical source ordering, typed corruption translation, and commit-time uniqueness checks inside one write transaction.
- Encodes IDs and revisions as fixed-width big-endian bytes, uses explicit stable tags for roles/formats/metadata, and validates all reconstructed values through RustTable constructors.
- Adds round-trip, reopen, conflict rollback, schema-version, canonical-order, exact-byte, metadata-kind, malformed-data, and catalog-replay coverage.

## Why

The core edit model intentionally stores validated operation intent as generic deterministic data. This PR adds the explicit processing boundary that compiles supported operation schemas once into small, closed data suitable for a future pixel path, while preserving operation identity and enabled state. It also establishes the finite `f32` scalar contract without coupling processing to images, codecs, persistence, UI, or execution.

## How

- Return public typed errors with operation, key, and parameter context for unsupported keys, missing/unexpected parameters, wrong types, overflow, and underflow.
- Define deterministic schema error precedence using canonical `BTreeMap` parameter order.
- Add scalar, compilation, and dependency-boundary tests; do not execute operations or allocate pixels.

## Why

## Why

The processing path needs an explicit boundary between normalized transfer-encoded source RGB and linear-light working RGB. This PR supplies the first real conversion seam with distinct types and fixed color-space tags, so later evaluation and profile work cannot receive samples with an implicit encoding or silently clamp extended-range working values.

- Implement total normalized sRGB-to-linear-light sRGB conversion using the standard breakpoint formula, preserving dimensions and row-major order without clipping.
- Add a crate-private proven-finite scalar path used only after the normalized input formula; public finite scalar construction remains checked.

## Why

The initial processing foundation now needs one complete in-memory render slice proving that immutable edit intent can become a new working image. This evaluator makes operation order and disabled-step provenance observable, preserves extended linear-light values, and fails atomically with exact step/pixel/channel context.
