issues.body:
## Why

#15 completes deterministic coverage for the shell established by #5, #9, and #12, but the application state still has no typed concept of which workspace is active. Adding photo widgets directly to the existing `Message` match would make routing, data lookup, and future processing effects easy to mix together.

The next UI foundation should introduce one pure navigation boundary first. It must identify photos only by the existing `rusttable_core::PhotoId`; it must not fetch catalog records, open files, decode images, persist state, or invoke processing.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **Desktop Experience & Iced UI follow-on sequence: 1 of 4.**
- Blocked by #15 and therefore transitively by #12. Do not begin this issue until #15 is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; it may use the app's existing `rusttable-core` dependency but must not add dependencies on `rusttable-catalog`, `rusttable-catalog-store`, `rusttable-image`, `rusttable-image-io`, or `rusttable-processing`.
- Blocks #64, which defines the deterministic photo-presentation contract after route ownership is settled.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/navigation.rs` with:
  - `pub(crate) enum WorkspaceRoute { Library, PhotoDetail(PhotoId) }`;
  - `pub(crate) enum NavigationIntent { ShowLibrary, ShowPhoto(PhotoId) }`;
  - a small `NavigationState` with a private current route, `Default` set to `Library`, a read-only route accessor, and one pure `apply` method that reports whether the route changed.
- Add a private `NavigationState` field to `Shell`; preserve the existing private sidebar state and `ToggleSidebar` behavior.
- Extend the existing app message boundary with exactly one navigation variant carrying `NavigationIntent`. `app::update` delegates route changes to `NavigationState` and continues to return `Task::none()` for every path.
- Keep `Workspace` as the stable shell-region label required by #15. Inside that region, render:
  - `Library` for the library route;
  - `Photo detail`, the canonical `PhotoId` text, and a text button labelled `Back to library` for a detail route.
- Add a text button labelled `Library` to the existing sidebar. It emits `NavigationIntent::ShowLibrary`; no other destination is added in this PR.
- Add focused reducer tests for default route, library-to-detail, detail-to-library, and idempotent repeated navigation.
- Add one headless iced test that places the state on a detail route through the production update function, clicks the real `Back to library` control, asserts exactly one navigation message, routes it through the production update function, and verifies the library projection after rebuilding the real view.
- Do not add a navigation stack, history, tabs, deep links, URL handling, commands, subscriptions, async work, timers, dialogs, photo collections, edit controls, or adapter traits.
- Do not change dependencies or `Cargo.lock`.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- focused navigation reducer tests
- focused headless navigation test at an 800×600 logical viewport
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`

## Acceptance criteria

- A default `Shell` projects `Workspace` and `Library`, with the sidebar and its existing toggle behavior unchanged.
- Applying `ShowPhoto(photo_id)` changes the route to exactly `PhotoDetail(photo_id)` without reading or copying any photo, asset, metadata, edit, image, or processing value.
- Applying `ShowLibrary` from a detail route returns to `Library`; applying either intent when already at its target is observably idempotent.
- The real `Back to library` button emits exactly one typed navigation message and the headless test proves the detail → library transition through production `view` and `update` code.
- The #15 shell smoke assertions for `RustTable`, `Sidebar`, `Workspace`, and the sidebar toggle continue to pass unchanged.
- No persistence handle, repository/store type, filesystem path, decoded image, processing pipeline, async task, or external service enters `Shell`, `NavigationState`, `WorkspaceRoute`, or `NavigationIntent`.
- The dependency graph and `Cargo.lock` are unchanged.
- The implementation is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.
issues.title: feat(ui): establish typed workspace navigation boundaries

issues.body:
Closes #40

## Why

The local validation wrappers previously measured elapsed time only after their commands completed, so hung checks could outlive the documented 30-second and 60-second contracts. This PR turns those soft measurements into real portable deadlines and makes the PR gate dependency #14 safe to build on.

## How

- Update the existing `run_with_budget <seconds> <label> <command> [args...]` helper to poll a background command, enforce deadline exit `124`, preserve in-budget statuses, and print labeled duration output.
- Add recursive `pgrep -P` process-tree termination and direct-helper INT/TERM cleanup without GNU `timeout`, `date`, `wait -n`, associative arrays, or another runtime.
- Keep precommit’s four checks exactly once and parallel; move their child creation inside the bounded function so waits/reaping occur in the owning shell.
- Keep prepush’s three checks exactly once and parallel, add `--all-features` to locked tests, and use the `pre-push` label.
- Add deterministic fixtures for success, status 7 propagation, argument preservation, deadline cleanup, INT/TERM cleanup, and late marker prevention.

## Validation

- `bash -n scripts/with-validation-budget.sh scripts/precommit-fast.sh scripts/prepush-fast.sh scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh`
- `bash scripts/test-validation-budgets.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Observed clean runs: precommit 1s / 30s budget; prepush 21s / 60s budget.
issues.title: chore(validation): enforce local deadline cleanup

issues.body:
## Why

#62 establishes typed workspace routing, but the iced view still has no application-owned contract for the display-ready data a library grid and photo-detail screen need. Passing `CatalogState`, repository handles, source paths, decoded images, metadata parser values, `Edit`, or processing types directly into widgets would couple the desktop layer to persistence and processing internals and make headless state tests expensive.

Define a small immutable presentation boundary before rendering photo collections. The boundary should carry stable identity plus bounded display text only; adapters that produce these values remain future work.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **Desktop Experience & Iced UI follow-on sequence: 2 of 4.**
- Blocked by #62. Per `TASK.md`, begin only after #62 is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Uses only the existing `rusttable_core::PhotoId` identity type and standard-library collections.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; it must not add dependencies on or modify catalog, catalog-store, image, image-I/O, processing, or persistence crates.
- Blocks #65, which consumes these exact contracts for the photo grid/detail interaction slice.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/presentation.rs` containing immutable, crate-visible presentation types:
  - `PresentationText`, a checked owned UTF-8 value that rejects empty or whitespace-only input, ASCII control characters, and values longer than 256 UTF-8 bytes;
  - `PhotoCardViewModel { id: PhotoId, title: PresentationText, secondary: Option<PresentationText> }`;
  - `PhotoFactViewModel { label: PresentationText, value: PresentationText }`;
  - `PhotoDetailViewModel { id: PhotoId, title: PresentationText, facts: Vec<PhotoFactViewModel> }`;
  - `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel`, owning an ordered card collection and deterministic detail lookup by `PhotoId`.
- Give `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel` one checked constructor that:
  1. validates cards in supplied display order;
  2. rejects the first duplicate card ID;
  3. rejects duplicate detail IDs;
  4. rejects a card without exactly one matching detail;
  5. rejects an orphan detail with no card;
  6. preserves card order exactly and stores detail lookup in canonical `PhotoId` order.
- Define typed public-within-the-crate error enums with stable variants and offending IDs/byte lengths; do not use string-only errors or an opaque catch-all.
- Expose read-only accessors and iterators only. Do not expose mutable collections or representation fields.
- Extend `Shell` with a private `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel` and add one crate-visible constructor for injecting a checked model. `Shell::default()` uses an empty model so the production application still starts without I/O or fixtures.
- Keep navigation state independent: the presentation model does not select routes, issue commands, or perform lookups outside its owned values.
- Add deterministic unit tests for text validation, empty model, order preservation, successful lookup, duplicate-card precedence, duplicate-detail rejection, missing detail, and orphan detail.
- Do not add widgets, photo-grid layout, thumbnail bytes, paths, metadata tags, edit controls, adapter traits, async tasks, filesystem/network access, serialization, or fixture files in this PR.
- Do not change dependencies or `Cargo.lock`.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- focused `presentation` unit tests
- existing #15 and #62 headless UI tests
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`

## Acceptance criteria

- Constructing the same checked inputs produces equal `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel` values, identical card iteration order, and identical detail lookup results.
- Card order is exactly caller-supplied display order; detail lookup is deterministic and independent of hash iteration order.
- Empty, whitespace-only, control-containing, and over-256-byte presentation text is rejected with an observable typed error.
- Duplicate cards, duplicate details, missing details, and orphan details are each rejected by distinct typed variants containing the relevant `PhotoId`; tests prove the documented validation precedence.
- `Shell::default()` remains fully deterministic and contains an empty photo workspace without reading environment variables, files, databases, clocks, randomness, or services.
- The UI contract contains no `CatalogState`, repository/store object, source path, raw metadata tag, decoded pixel buffer, `Edit`, processing operation/pipeline, serializer, future, channel, or callback.
- Production widgets and the existing shell/navigation behavior remain unchanged in this PR.
- The dependency graph and `Cargo.lock` are unchanged.
- The implementation is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.
issues.title: feat(ui): define deterministic photo workspace view-model contracts

issues.body:
## Why

#62 provides typed library/detail routing and #64 provides checked display-ready photo data, but no real product interaction connects them. The next PR should prove that the iced workspace can project an injected photo collection, open one photo by stable identity, render its detail facts, and return to the library without introducing image loading, persistence, processing, or test-only widget implementations.

This is the first photo-workspace interaction slice. It deliberately uses neutral preview placeholders so grid/detail behavior is reviewable before thumbnail adapters or edit controls exist.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **Desktop Experience & Iced UI follow-on sequence: 3 of 4.**
- Blocked by #64 and transitively by #62 and #15. Begin only after #64 is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Consumes the exact `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel`, card, detail, fact, and presentation-text contracts from #64; do not widen those contracts in this PR except for a defect required to implement their documented behavior.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; it must not add dependencies on catalog, catalog-store, image, image-I/O, processing, filesystem, or async runtimes.
- Blocks #76, which adds keyboard-operable action controls and deterministic focus behavior over these completed controls.

## How

- Extend `theme.rs` with named photo-workspace layout constants, including exactly `PHOTO_GRID_COLUMNS: usize = 3`, plus named card width, card height, and grid spacing values. Do not scatter duplicate grid literals through the view.
- Extend the production `view` projection while preserving the stable `RustTable`, `Sidebar`, `Workspace`, and sidebar-toggle labels required by #15.
- For `WorkspaceRoute::Library`:
  - render `No photos` when the injected model is empty;
  - otherwise render a fill-width scrollable grid by chunking the ordered card slice into rows of exactly three standard iced buttons;
  - render every card's title, optional secondary text, and a neutral `Preview unavailable` placeholder;
  - make the entire card button emit the existing typed `NavigationIntent::ShowPhoto(card.id())` through the production message boundary.
- For `WorkspaceRoute::PhotoDetail(photo_id)`:
  - resolve the detail only through `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel`;
  - render `Photo unavailable` plus the existing `Back to library` control when the ID is absent;
  - otherwise render the detail title followed by its fact label/value pairs in supplied order and the existing back control.
- Keep `app::update` as the only production state mutation path. Opening a card changes only the navigation route; it does not clone or mutate the presentation model.
- Add focused pure-view tests for empty library and missing-detail projections.
- Add one headless iced scenario at 800×600 using an in-memory four-photo checked model that:
  1. proves the four titles and `Preview unavailable` placeholders are present;
  2. uses selector bounds—not screenshots—to prove cards 1–3 share the first row and card 4 begins the second row;
  3. clicks card 2 by exact title and asserts exactly one `ShowPhoto` message for card 2's `PhotoId`;
  4. routes that message through production `app::update`, rebuilds the production view, and proves the selected detail title/facts are present while unrelated detail content is absent;
  5. clicks `Back to library`, routes the exact message through production update, rebuilds, and proves all four cards are restored in the same order.
- Keep all test data in Rust constructors. Do not add image files, filesystem fixtures, `.ice` recordings, screenshots, golden files, sleeps, retries, clocks, or native-window automation.
- Do not add thumbnail decoding, zoom/pan, selection sets, range selection, drag-and-drop, import, sorting/filtering controls, edit controls, context menus, dialogs, or persistence.
- Do not change dependencies or `Cargo.lock`.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- focused empty/missing projection tests
- focused headless `photo_grid_opens_detail_and_returns` scenario at 800×600
- existing #15 and #62 headless scenarios
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`

## Acceptance criteria

- An empty default application visibly renders `Workspace`, `Library`, and `No photos` without performing I/O.
- A checked four-card model is rendered in exact model order as a three-column first row plus a one-card second row at 800×600; headless bounds assertions prove the layout without pixel snapshots.
- Clicking a real card emits exactly one typed navigation intent containing that card's `PhotoId`; no catalog lookup, file access, image decode, or processing call occurs.
- After routing the message through production update, the real detail view renders only the matching title and fact rows in their checked order and retains a functional `Back to library` text button.
- Returning to the library restores the same cards in the same order; the presentation model remains immutable across the round trip.
- A detail route for an unknown `PhotoId` deterministically renders `Photo unavailable` and can return to the library without panic.
- The existing shell smoke labels and sidebar visible → hidden → visible behavior remain valid.
- No decoded image, thumbnail cache, source path, repository/store, metadata parser object, `Edit`, processing pipeline, async task, timer, network request, or filesystem fixture is introduced.
- The dependency graph and `Cargo.lock` are unchanged.
- The implementation is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.
issues.title: feat(ui): add deterministic photo grid and detail interactions

issues.body:
## Why

`CatalogState` currently owns photos and the latest immutable `Edit` values, while `ImportRepository` owns the durable `ImportRecord` values containing `SourcePath`, `ImageProbe`, and `ImageMetadata`. A caller that needs one catalog item must manually join those two boundaries, choose its own ordering, and decide what to do when persisted provenance disagrees with in-memory state.

That is not an appropriate responsibility for iced views, processing orchestration, or storage adapters. RustTable needs one deterministic, owned, read-only catalog projection that joins current state with import provenance and metadata, validates the cross-boundary relationship once, and exposes zero or more edits without inventing a premature “current edit” policy.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Depends on completed #41 for the final current in-memory edit replacement contract.
- Depends on completed #32 and #42 for `ImportRecord`, `ImportRepository`, canonical listing, and durable import provenance.
- Uses the metadata model completed by #28 and the existing `rusttable-catalog -> rusttable-core + rusttable-image` dependency boundary.
- **Milestone #3 follow-on sequence: 1 of 3 and the best first new issue in “Architecture & Core Domain”.**
- Blocks #72 and #77. Per `TASK.md`, do not begin #72 until #66 is integrated or explicitly blocked; #77 follows #72.
- Independent of the UI sequences #12/#15/#62/#64/#65/#76, image-I/O #68, processing #70/#73, and quality/release #14/#17/#24/#29/#31/#43/#67/#71/#74/#75. This issue must not modify those scopes or make them dependencies.

## How

- Write the failing integration tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_snapshot.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_snapshot_errors.rs`.
- Record the initial failing targeted commands/output in the implementation PR before adding production code.
- Add `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/snapshot.rs` and re-export only the intentional public snapshot types from `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/lib.rs`.
- Define an opaque, owned `CatalogEntry` containing exactly:
  - one validated `ImportRecord`;
  - every current `Edit` in `CatalogState` whose `photo_id()` matches that record’s photo, stored in ascending `EditId` order.
- Expose read-only accessors for the entry’s source, photo, primary asset, image probe, metadata, and edits. Delegate source/photo/probe/metadata values to the owned `ImportRecord`; do not duplicate independently supplied values that can disagree.
- Define an opaque, owned `CatalogSnapshot` containing:
  - the exact `CatalogState` revision observed during construction;
  - entries in ascending canonical `SourcePath` order;
  - private lookup indexes sufficient for exact lookup by `SourcePath` and `PhotoId` without exposing mutable maps.
- Add `CatalogSnapshot::load(state: &CatalogState, repository: &dyn ImportRepository) -> Result<CatalogSnapshot, CatalogSnapshotError>`.
- `load` must call `ImportRepository::list()` exactly once, then build the complete snapshot from that returned owned record set and the immutable `CatalogState`. It must not perform per-entry repository lookups or retain a repository borrow/transaction.
- Canonicalize the returned records by `SourcePath`, then validate in this exact observable order:
  1. repository/list failure;
  2. duplicate `SourcePath`;
  3. duplicate `PhotoId` across records;
  4. duplicate primary `AssetId` across records;
  5. persisted record whose photo is absent from `CatalogState`;
  6. persisted record whose `Photo` is not exactly equal to the state photo with the same ID;
  7. state photo that has no import record.
- Define typed, matchable `CatalogSnapshotError` variants preserving the relevant source/photo/asset IDs and nesting `RepositoryError` as an error source. Do not collapse failures into strings or an opaque catch-all.
- Associate edits only after all import/photo consistency checks pass. Preserve each `Edit` unchanged; do not sort operations, select one edit, synthesize an empty edit, compile a pipeline, or infer UI state.
- The result must own all projected values so it remains valid after the source state and repository handle are dropped. Construction failures must return no partial snapshot and must not mutate either input.
- Add deterministic fake-repository coverage for unsorted listings, each duplicate/cross-boundary failure, repository failure, zero edits, multiple edits for one photo, edits for multiple photos, exact source ordering, exact edit ordering, and lookup behavior.
- Keep `rusttable-catalog` free of database, serialization, iced, codec, filesystem, processing, async-runtime, and synchronization dependencies. Add no normal dependency and no new workspace crate.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_snapshot --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_snapshot_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- One `CatalogSnapshot::load` call performs exactly one repository listing and produces an owned snapshot whose revision equals the input state revision.
- Entries iterate in ascending canonical `SourcePath` order regardless of repository return order; edits within each entry iterate in ascending `EditId` order regardless of catalog insertion order.
- `by_source` and `by_photo_id` return the same entry instance logically, return `None` for unknown keys, and expose the exact imported source/probe/metadata plus the exact equal state `Photo`.
- A photo with no edit yields an empty edit iterator. A photo with multiple edits exposes all of them and does not label any edit as current, preferred, selected, or default.
- Duplicate source/photo/asset identities, a persisted-only photo, a persisted/state photo mismatch, and a state-only photo each return their documented typed error with relevant identity data and no partial result.
- A repository failure is preserved as the nested source of `CatalogSnapshotError`; no third-party storage error or adapter type enters the public API.
- Dropping the input `CatalogState`, fake repository, and their temporary fixtures after construction does not invalidate or alter the snapshot.
- No source file is opened, decoded, rendered, compiled, written, or inspected beyond the already-owned domain values. No iced, processing, catalog-store, codec, database, serialization, filesystem, network, clock, randomness, unsafe Rust, or inherited darktable type/code is introduced.

## One-PR boundary

This issue delivers only the joined read projection and its consistency errors/tests in `rusttable-catalog`. It does not persist edits, change catalog commands, resolve a processing request, add a concrete store adapter, modify iced, or orchestrate image I/O/processing. One focused PR must close only this issue and be squash-merged before #72 begins.
issues.title: feat(catalog): define immutable joined catalog snapshots

issues.body:
Superseded by numbered execution issue #169 (`[0013] Enforce the complete workspace dependency DAG in fast validation`).

This issue is closed to keep one executable issue per pull request and one ordered source of truth under parent #158. No implementation should target this issue.
issues.title: [SUPERSEDED] Workspace dependency DAG validation

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #21 / merged PR #52 deliberately limits `InputFormat` and `FileImageInput` to JPEG and PNG. TIFF is the next useful non-RAW interchange codec for a photo application, but enabling a dependency feature without a product-owned subset would silently admit multi-image containers, high-bit-depth sample conversion, or future decoder behavior that RustTable has not reviewed.

This issue adds exactly one bounded codec to the existing input contract. It must preserve signature-first dispatch, explicit product limits, typed failures, and the current codec-neutral crate boundary. Metadata extraction, orientation, ICC interpretation, RAW/DNG support, output encoding, catalog writes, and processing remain separate work.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #21 / PR #52 and extends its exact `rusttable-image` / `rusttable-image-io` split; do not introduce a second image-input trait or adapter family.
- Uses the current `ImageDimensions`, `DecodedImage`, `DecodeLimits`, `ImageProbe`, `ImageInput`, and `ImageInputError` contracts on `main`.
- **Milestone #4 follow-on sequence: 1 of 4 and the best next issue in this milestone.**
- Blocks #79, whose bounded canonical EXIF reader targets the completed JPEG/PNG/TIFF input set. Per `TASK.md`, do not begin #79 until #68 is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Independent of `rusttable-catalog`, `rusttable-catalog-store`, `rusttable-processing`, and `rusttable-app`.

## How

- Write the focused failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Extend `InputFormat` with exactly `Tiff` and change `SUPPORTED_INPUT_FORMATS` to the stable order JPEG, PNG, TIFF. No other format may become accepted through dependency features or generic guessing.
- Extend the public typed input-error surface only as needed to distinguish an unsupported-but-recognized TIFF feature from malformed input. Use a closed RustTable-owned reason enum; do not expose `image`, `tiff`, `std::io`, or boxed parser errors.
- In `rusttable-image-io`, enable only the reviewed TIFF decoder feature in addition to the existing JPEG and PNG features. Keep `default-features = false`; do not enable the default codec bundle, Rayon, encoders, RAW/DNG, WebP, GIF, BMP, OpenEXR, HDR, DDS, ICO, QOI, AVIF, or HEIF.
- Detect classic TIFF only from the byte signature:
  - little-endian `II 2A 00`;
  - big-endian `MM 00 2A`.
  A `.tif` or `.tiff` extension must never select behavior. Recognize BigTIFF signatures sufficiently to return the typed unsupported-feature error rather than treating them as an unknown format.
- Preserve the existing bounded-read rule: read at most `max_source_bytes + 1`, reject over-limit input before decoder dispatch, and do not rely on filesystem metadata alone.
- Probe dimensions before decoded allocation and independently enforce width, height, pixel-count, and decoded-RGBA-byte limits using the existing product values. Dependency limits remain defense in depth, not the public contract.
- Accept exactly one primary TIFF image with 8-bit grayscale, grayscale-plus-alpha, RGB, or RGBA samples. Normalize successful results to packed RGBA8 straight alpha:
  - grayscale replicates the sample into R/G/B;
  - missing alpha becomes `255`;
  - present straight alpha is preserved byte-for-byte.
- Reject, with observable typed unsupported-feature reasons, at least:
  - BigTIFF;
  - multiple image directories/pages;
  - 1/2/4-bit packed samples;
  - 16-bit or floating-point samples;
  - CMYK, Lab, YCbCr, palette/indexed, or other unreviewed color models.
  Do not silently select the first page or down-convert high-bit-depth/channel models through `DynamicImage::to_rgba8`.
- Treat malformed offsets, strip/tile tables, compression streams, dimensions, and truncated data as `MalformedInput { format: Tiff, ... }` without panic or partial output.
- Do not apply TIFF orientation, read EXIF/GPS/XMP/IPTC, interpret ICC/gamma/chromaticity, premultiply alpha, convert color spaces, generate thumbnails, or expose decoder-specific values.
- Keep the existing JPEG and PNG behavior byte-for-byte compatible. Add regression coverage proving their signatures, limits, and unsupported-format behavior did not widen.
- Add purpose-built tiny fixtures under `crates/rusttable-image-io/tests/fixtures/` for:
  - little-endian 2x1 RGB8 TIFF;
  - big-endian 1x2 RGBA8 TIFF;
  - grayscale8 TIFF;
  - BigTIFF;
  - two-page TIFF;
  - 16-bit TIFF;
  - malformed/truncated TIFF.
  Generate or author the fixtures specifically for RustTable; do not copy darktable fixtures or loader tests.
- Update the crate-boundary regression test so codec dependencies remain below `rusttable-image-io`; `rusttable-image` must retain no normal third-party dependency.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, deterministic tests, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --test input_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test jpeg_png_decode --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test input_rejections --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test decode_limits --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test tiff_decode --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test tiff_rejections --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image-io --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `SUPPORTED_INPUT_FORMATS` contains exactly JPEG, PNG, TIFF in that stable order; no other dependency-supported format is accepted.
- A valid TIFF payload under a `.jpg` path probes/decodes as TIFF, while a JPEG or PNG payload under `.tif` continues to dispatch from its signature. Extensions are never consulted.
- Reviewed little-endian RGB8, big-endian RGBA8, and grayscale8 TIFF fixtures produce the exact expected dimensions and RGBA8 straight-alpha bytes.
- BigTIFF, multi-page TIFF, 16-bit TIFF, and each unreviewed sample/color model return matchable unsupported-feature errors rather than silently selecting, truncating, or down-converting data.
- A TIFF one byte over `max_source_bytes` is rejected before codec work. Width, height, pixel-count, and decoded-byte limits each return the existing exact typed limit category without oversized allocation.
- Malformed offsets, truncated strips/tiles, corrupt compression data, and invalid dimensions return `MalformedInput` for TIFF without panic, partial `DecodedImage`, or leaked third-party errors.
- JPEG and PNG success/rejection tests remain unchanged except for the intentional new TIFF variant; GIF/WebP/BMP/RAW/DNG/HEIF/AVIF remain unsupported.
- `cargo tree` and boundary tests show `rusttable-image` still has no normal third-party dependency and TIFF implementation dependencies remain confined to `rusttable-image-io`.
- No local unsafe Rust, FFI, C/C++ build step, inherited darktable implementation/fixture, networked test, sleep, wall-clock assertion, random behavior, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.

## One-PR scope

Deliver exactly one focused PR that adds bounded TIFF **input only**, records failing-first evidence, passes every listed validation command, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged. Do not add TIFF output, metadata parsing, orientation application, color management, processing integration, catalog import orchestration, or UI behavior in this PR.
issues.title: feat(image-io): add bounded TIFF input support

issues.body:
Closes #14

## Why

RustTable’s PRs currently receive no repository-specific Rust validation because the inherited workflows target darktable’s `master` branch and C/CMake build. This adds the bounded, read-only PR gate required for the Rust rewrite while keeping heavyweight validation and release work out of the 150-second path.

## How

- Add `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml` for every PR targeting `main` plus manual dispatch, with one `rust-pr / validate` job, read-only contents permission, cancellation concurrency, a three-minute protective timeout, and a measured 150-second budget.
- Pin checkout, Bun setup, and Cargo cache actions to immutable full commit SHAs; install exact Rust `1.95.0` and Bun `1.3.14`.
- Cache only Cargo registry/git/target artifacts with OS, exact Rust, and `Cargo.lock` in the key.
- Add `scripts/pr-ci.sh` as the single PR validation entry point: run the five cheap checks in parallel with captured logs, then run strict locked Clippy and all-feature workspace tests once each.
- Keep inherited CMake/darktable workflows untouched and do not call local precommit/prepush wrappers from PR CI.

## Validation

- `bash -n scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`
- Verified action references resolve to full commit SHAs.
- Verified workflow contains no push trigger, `pull_request_target`, path filters, or local-hook invocation.
issues.title: ci: add bounded Rust pull request validation

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #27 proves that an immutable compiled pipeline can evaluate exposure and linear-offset operations into a new linear-light image, but operation strength is still binary: enabled operations run at full effect and disabled operations do not run. Production editing needs an explicit partial-strength boundary before masks are introduced. Encoding opacity as an operation-specific parameter would duplicate common blend semantics across operation schemas and make authoring, compilation, and evaluation disagree.

This PR adds only operation-level opacity and normal blending. It preserves the existing ordered pipeline, finite-scalar rules, non-destructive evaluator, and exact diagnostic context while creating the seam that a later per-pixel mask can multiply into the same blend weight.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #18 for immutable core `Operation` values.
- Builds on completed #22, #23, and #27 for typed compilation, stable source indices, and deterministic in-memory evaluation.
- **Milestone #5 sequence: 5 of 8 and the first follow-up after the initial processing foundation.**
- The next milestone-#5 issue starts only after this issue is integrated so changes to the core operation and processing evaluator remain serial and reviewable.
- Independent of image decoding, output encoding, ICC profiles, filesystem adapters, catalog persistence, iced, and UI state.
- This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.

## How

- Write failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-core/tests/operation_opacity.rs` and `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/operation_opacity.rs`; record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add a public `OperationOpacity` value object in `rusttable-core`:
  - `OperationOpacity::new(f64) -> Result<Self, OperationOpacityError>`;
  - inclusive range `0.0..=1.0`;
  - distinct `NonFinite`, `BelowZero`, and `AboveOne` errors;
  - exact `ZERO` and `ONE` constants plus a read-only `get()` accessor;
  - deterministic `Copy`, `Eq`, ordering, hashing, `Display`, and `std::error::Error` behavior consistent with the existing finite scalar conventions.
- Add an `opacity` field to core `Operation`. Preserve the existing `Operation::new(...)` signature and make it delegate to opaque `OperationOpacity::ONE`; add `Operation::new_with_opacity(...)` for explicit opacity and expose a read-only `opacity()` accessor. Operation equality must include opacity.
- Re-export only the new public core value/error types through the existing `edit` and crate-root modules. Do not add serialization or persistence policy.
- Carry opacity into `ProcessingOperation` as a private finite `f32` value and expose a read-only accessor. Narrow the checked core opacity exactly once during compilation. Add a typed `OperationCompileError::OpacityNarrowingUnderflow { operation_id }`; overflow and out-of-range opacity are impossible after core construction and must not be represented as artificial public errors.
- Keep operation-specific schemas unchanged: `rusttable.exposure(stops)` and `rusttable.linear_offset(value)` gain no opacity parameter.
- Refactor the evaluator so each active step computes an operation candidate and then applies normal blending with the current channel value:
  - opacity `0.0`: skip all operation arithmetic and leave every pixel bit-for-bit unchanged;
  - opacity `1.0`: use the existing direct operation result path so current full-strength bit patterns and error behavior remain unchanged;
  - intermediate opacity: evaluate `delta = candidate - current`, `weighted_delta = delta * opacity`, and `output = current + weighted_delta` in that exact order, validating each intermediate through `FiniteF32`.
- Add public, matchable `BlendArithmeticStage::{Delta, WeightedDelta, Output}` and `EvaluationError::NonFiniteBlendResult { step_index, operation_id, pixel_index, channel, stage }`. Preserve the existing `NonFiniteExposureMultiplier` and `NonFiniteChannelResult` variants for operation arithmetic.
- Preserve deterministic traversal: pipeline source order, ascending row-major pixel index, then `Red`, `Green`, `Blue`. Return the first error in that order and retain the original #23 step index after disabled predecessors.
- Keep evaluation externally atomic and non-destructive. Continue cloning the input pixel vector exactly once and do not allocate a second full-image buffer for blending or one buffer per operation.
- Keep masks, mask storage, additional blend modes, alpha compositing, premultiplication, geometry, resampling, tiling, caches, threads, async runtimes, SIMD/GPU code, persistence, UI, and inherited darktable processing code out of this PR.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test operation_opacity --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test operation_opacity --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test render_slice --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `Operation::new` remains source-compatible and produces exactly `OperationOpacity::ONE`; `Operation::new_with_opacity` preserves an explicit checked opacity.
- Focused tests cover both inclusive endpoints, representative interior values, non-finite input, below-zero input, above-one input, equality/ordering/hashing, and opacity-sensitive `Operation` equality.
- Compilation preserves opacity and reports the exact operation ID when a positive nonzero core opacity underflows to `f32`; no opaque string or catch-all error is added.
- `zero_opacity_skips_operation_arithmetic` proves an otherwise overflowing exposure succeeds and leaves the image bit-for-bit unchanged at opacity zero.
- `opaque_matches_existing_full_strength_results` proves opacity one produces the same exact exposure and linear-offset bits as the pre-issue evaluator.
- `half_opacity_blends_exact_binary_fractions` proves normal blending against exactly representable fixtures with no clipping or quantization.
- `opacity_participates_in_pipeline_order` proves two partially blended operations execute in #23 authoring order and match a manually calculated result.
- `reports_first_non_finite_blend_stage_deterministically` matches the exact step, operation, pixel, channel, and `BlendArithmeticStage` for competing failures.
- Disabled operations remain present in pipeline provenance and are not evaluated; opacity-zero operations remain enabled steps with their original source indices but have no pixel effect.
- Success and failure leave the borrowed core edit, compiled pipeline, and input image unchanged; no partial output is observable.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal` remains empty and `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` still shows only `rusttable-core`.
- No mask model, alpha behavior, extra blend mode, decoder, filesystem, database, UI dependency, unsafe Rust, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR records red-test evidence, contains one coherent opacity/blending change, includes `Closes #<this issue>`, closes no other issue, and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: feat(processing): add operation opacity and deterministic normal blending

issues.body:
## Why

`CatalogState` can create and optimistically replace immutable `Edit` values, but those values currently exist only in memory. The durable catalog slice from #42 intentionally persists import records only, so a restart can reconstruct photos and import provenance while losing every edit. Adding a concrete database table now would prematurely couple domain semantics to redb and the current schema.

RustTable first needs a storage-neutral edit repository contract and one failure-atomic application service that coordinates the existing catalog commands with durable writes. The contract must make create, replacement, retry, conflict, and consistency behavior explicit before any adapter, UI authoring flow, or startup replay is implemented.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Depends on completed #41, which defines `CatalogCommand::CreateEdit`, `CatalogCommand::ReplaceEdit`, catalog revision checks, edit revision checks, and failure-atomic in-memory transitions.
- Sequenced after #66 so the joined read model lands before the write-side persistence port; this is a project-order dependency, not a requirement for the repository trait to consume snapshot types.
- Uses the immutable `Edit` aggregate from completed #38 and the existing `rusttable-catalog -> rusttable-core + rusttable-image` boundary.
- #42 remains the completed import-record adapter and must not be widened or rewritten by this issue.
- **Milestone #3 follow-on sequence: 2 of 3.**
- Blocks #77. Per `TASK.md`, do not begin #77 until #72 is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Independent of the UI sequences #12/#15/#62/#64/#65/#76, image-I/O #68, processing #70/#73, and quality/release #14/#17/#24/#29/#31/#43/#67/#71/#74/#75. This issue must not modify those scopes or make them dependencies.

## How

- Write the failing integration tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/edit_repository_contract.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/durable_edit_service.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/durable_edit_errors.rs`.
- Record the initial failing targeted commands/output in the implementation PR before adding production code.
- Add:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/edit_repository.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/durable_edit.rs`.
- Define an object-safe `EditRepository` port whose public surface uses only owned/borrowed RustTable values:
  - `find_by_edit_id(EditId) -> Result<Option<Edit>, EditRepositoryError>`;
  - `list() -> Result<Vec<Edit>, EditRepositoryError>`;
  - `commit_new(&Edit) -> Result<(), EditRepositoryError>`;
  - `commit_replacement(expected_edit_revision: Revision, &Edit) -> Result<(), EditRepositoryError>`.
- `list()` must return current edits in ascending `EditId` order. The contract stores exactly one current immutable value per `EditId`; it does not define historical revision retention, undo/redo, branches, or garbage collection.
- Define typed, matchable `EditRepositoryError` variants for:
  - repository unavailable;
  - corrupt persisted data;
  - new-edit ID conflict;
  - replacement of an unknown edit;
  - persisted edit revision conflict with expected/actual revisions;
  - persisted photo identity mismatch;
  - persisted base-photo revision mismatch;
  - commit failure.
  Preserve relevant `EditId`, `PhotoId`, and `Revision` values. Do not expose database, transaction, serialization, or third-party error types.
- Specify that `commit_new` rechecks absence of the ID inside its durable transaction. Specify that `commit_replacement` rechecks existence, exact edit ID, photo ID, base-photo revision, expected current revision, and exact one-step successor revision inside its durable transaction. Every failure leaves durable state logically unchanged.
- Add `DurableEditService` with exactly two public operations:
  - create one edit using an expected catalog revision;
  - replace one edit using an expected catalog revision and expected edit revision.
- Define owned `DurableEditOutcome` variants for `Created`, `Replaced`, and `AlreadyPresent`, each carrying the authoritative `Edit` and resulting/current catalog revision.
- For both service operations, validate the expected catalog revision before any repository call. Then handle persisted retries/consistency before preflighting the existing `CatalogState` command on a clone.
- Create semantics:
  - no persisted edit: preflight `CreateEdit`, call `commit_new`, then replace the caller’s state with the preflight state;
  - persisted edit exactly equal to the requested edit and an exactly equal edit already exists in state: return `AlreadyPresent` without mutation;
  - persisted edit present but unequal, persisted-only edit, or state-only edit: return a typed service consistency/conflict error without mutation.
- Replacement semantics:
  - load the persisted current edit by ID and require it to equal the state’s current edit before preflight;
  - require both persisted and state revisions to equal the caller’s expected edit revision;
  - preflight the existing `ReplaceEdit` command on a clone, call `commit_replacement`, then replace the caller’s state with the preflight state;
  - if the replacement is already the exact persisted and state value, return `AlreadyPresent` only when its revision is the exact successor of the caller’s expected edit revision;
  - reject divergent identity, base-photo revision, revision, operations, missing-side, and repository failures with typed errors and no mutation.
- Define a typed `DurableEditError` that nests `CatalogError` and `EditRepositoryError` as error sources and has explicit variants for persisted-only, state-only, and persisted/state mismatch conditions. Preserve all relevant IDs/revisions; do not use string-only categories.
- Document and test exact observable validation precedence for create and replacement. Repository commit is the final fallible step before assigning the preflight clone to the caller’s state.
- Use deterministic fake repositories to test object safety, canonical listing, successful create/replace, exact retry, every conflict/consistency branch, stale catalog/edit revisions, repository lookup/commit failures, and complete state/repository atomicity.
- Keep `rusttable-catalog` free of database, serialization, iced, codec, filesystem, processing, async-runtime, and synchronization dependencies. Add no normal dependency and no new workspace crate.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test edit_repository_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test durable_edit_service --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test durable_edit_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `EditRepository` is object-safe, exposes no adapter/serialization type, and its fake contract proves ascending `EditId` listing plus transaction-time create/replacement checks.
- A successful create advances the catalog exactly once, writes exactly one equal edit, and returns `Created`; a successful replacement advances the edit and catalog exactly once, writes the exact replacement, and returns `Replaced`.
- Repeating an already-completed create or replacement with the documented revisions returns `AlreadyPresent`, performs no additional durable write, and leaves state byte-for-byte/logically equal to its pre-call value.
- A stale catalog revision fails before any repository method is invoked. A stale edit revision, identity mismatch, base-photo mismatch, invalid successor, duplicate, or unknown edit returns the documented typed category.
- Persisted-only, state-only, and unequal persisted/state edits are distinguishable errors; no call silently repairs, deletes, overwrites, rebases, or selects one side as authoritative.
- Lookup failure, commit failure, and every validation failure leave both the caller’s `CatalogState` and the fake repository logically equal to their complete pre-call states.
- `Error::source` exposes nested `CatalogError`/`EditRepositoryError` where applicable, while no redb, serde, postcard, filesystem, or third-party error enters the public surface.
- The contract persists only the current complete `Edit` value per ID. It adds no history log, undo/redo, branch graph, operation delta format, startup replay, schema migration, concrete adapter, source-file access, pipeline compilation, rendering, UI, network, clock, randomness, unsafe Rust, or inherited darktable code.

## One-PR boundary

This issue delivers only the `EditRepository` port, durable create/replace coordination, typed errors/outcomes, and deterministic fake tests in `rusttable-catalog`. It does not modify `rusttable-catalog-store`, add a concrete database schema/adapter, load edits at startup, change `CatalogState` command semantics, add UI, or invoke image/processing code. One focused PR must close only this issue and be squash-merged before #77 begins.
issues.title: feat(catalog): define failure-atomic durable edit writes

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #26 establishes normalized transfer-encoded sRGB input and a linear-light sRGB working image, while completed #27 deliberately preserves finite negative and above-one working values without clipping. That is correct for editing, but the pipeline still has no explicit boundary back to bounded transfer-encoded RGB for display or file-output integration.

Clipping at an arbitrary operation, decoder, encoder, or UI call site would make results depend on integration order and hide lost highlight/shadow information. The next color step must therefore define one deterministic linear-sRGB-to-encoded-sRGB boundary and make every out-of-gamut channel observable without adding quantization or a display-profile claim.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #26 for checked sRGB/linear-light sample and raster types.
- Builds on completed #27 for finite extended-range `WorkingRgbImage` output.
- Follows #70 so the follow-up milestone sequence remains serial; this issue does not otherwise depend on opacity implementation details.
- **Milestone #5 sequence: 6 of 8.**
- Blocks the decoded-image render integration later in this milestone follow-up.
- Independent of file encoders, ICC parsing, display profiles, catalog state, persistence, iced, and UI behavior.
- This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.

## How

- Write failing tests first in `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/output_srgb.rs`; record the initial red command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add `crates/rusttable-processing/src/output.rs` and re-export only the intentional public output-color types and function from `lib.rs`; do not turn `color.rs` into an orchestration module.
- Define immutable output-specific types distinct from source and working images:
  - `EncodedSrgb` containing three normalized `SrgbChannel` values;
  - `EncodedSrgbImage` containing `RasterDimensions` and a private row-major `Vec<EncodedSrgb>` with read-only dimensions, pixel-slice, iterator, and checked-index accessors;
  - `ChannelCounts { red: u64, green: u64, blue: u64 }` with read-only accessors;
  - `GamutClipReport { below_zero: ChannelCounts, above_one: ChannelCounts }`;
  - `EncodedSrgbOutput { image: EncodedSrgbImage, clipping: GamutClipReport }`.
- Add total `encode_linear_srgb(input: &WorkingRgbImage) -> EncodedSrgbOutput`.
- For each channel in ascending row-major pixel order and `Red`, `Green`, `Blue` order:
  1. inspect the original finite linear value;
  2. increment the matching below-zero or above-one per-channel count when the value is outside `0.0..=1.0`;
  3. clip only at this output boundary to `0.0..=1.0`;
  4. apply the standard sRGB encoding transfer function:
     - `12.92 * c` when `c <= 0.0031308`;
     - `1.055 * c.powf(1.0 / 2.4) - 0.055` otherwise;
  5. construct the encoded channel through the existing normalized `SrgbChannel` invariant.
- Keep the conversion total over `WorkingRgbImage`: finite input plus clipping makes both transfer branches finite and normalized. Do not add an artificial transform error or opaque catch-all.
- Make clipping counts deterministic and non-overflowing by storing one `u64` count per channel/direction; each individual count can never exceed `RasterDimensions::pixel_count()`.
- Preserve dimensions and row-major order exactly. Borrow and do not mutate the working image.
- Document that the result is transfer-encoded sRGB suitable for a later bounded output bridge; it is not an ICC display transform, proofing transform, tone mapper, scene-referred representation, or encoded file.
- Keep integer quantization, dithering, alpha, premultiplication, rendering orchestration, image-I/O dependencies, metadata, masks, additional gamut mapping, local contrast/tone mapping, display calibration, filesystem access, UI, SIMD/GPU, and inherited darktable code out of this PR.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test output_srgb --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test render_slice --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `EncodedSrgbImage` is a distinct public type; callers cannot pass a `WorkingRgbImage` or `SourceRgbImage` where encoded output is required without an explicit conversion.
- `black_and_white_encode_exactly` proves linear `0.0` and `1.0` become exact normalized encoded endpoints with an empty clipping report.
- `uses_both_standard_transfer_branches` covers values at and immediately around `0.0031308` with an explicit `f32` tolerance and the documented branch selection.
- `source_to_linear_to_output_round_trip` crosses #26 in both directions for representative normalized sRGB channels and matches the originals within a committed tolerance without quantization.
- `reports_every_clipped_channel_by_direction` supplies multiple negative and above-one candidates and matches exact red/green/blue counts independently for below-zero and above-one values.
- Values exactly `0.0` and `1.0` are not counted as clipped. Finite negative zero follows the existing `FiniteF32` normalization and is not reported as below zero.
- `clipping_is_only_at_the_output_boundary` proves the borrowed `WorkingRgbImage` retains its original extended values after conversion.
- Equal working images produce equal encoded images and equal reports on every run; output order never depends on maps, threads, platform sorting, or operation kind.
- The public output types expose no mutable pixel buffer and no constructor that can attach an arbitrary color-space tag.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` still shows only `rusttable-core`; no image codec, ICC library, UI framework, filesystem, async runtime, GPU/SIMD library, or other dependency is added.
- No quantization, alpha policy, profile conversion, tone mapping, hidden clipping, unsafe Rust, network/clock/random test, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.
- The implementation PR records red-test evidence, contains one coherent output-color boundary, includes `Closes #<this issue>`, closes no other issue, and is squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: feat(processing): encode linear-sRGB working images with observable gamut clipping

issues.body:
## Why

#65 adds real library-card and detail interactions, but those controls are still pointer-oriented and the application has no explicit focus state or keyboard contract. Relying on incidental widget-tree behavior would make focus order unstable as the shell evolves, while global ad hoc shortcuts would be difficult to test and could conflict with future edit controls.

Add one application-owned keyboard/focus boundary that preserves pointer behavior, makes every existing action reachable and activatable without a mouse, and remains independent of catalog, persistence, image, and processing internals. This issue establishes keyboard parity and a visible focus foundation; it does not claim platform screen-reader or accessibility-tree support.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **Desktop Experience & Iced UI follow-on sequence: 4 of 4 — final issue.**
- Blocked by #65 and therefore transitively by #64, #62, #15, and #12. Begin only after #65 is integrated or explicitly blocked.
- Reuses the `iced_test = "0.14.0"` dev dependency introduced by #15 and the exact route, presentation, grid, and detail controls completed by #62/#64/#65.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; it must not add dependencies on catalog, catalog-store, image, image-I/O, processing, persistence, or an async runtime.

## How

- Add `crates/rusttable-app/src/input.rs` with:
  - `pub(crate) enum FocusTarget { SidebarToggle, Library, PhotoCard(PhotoId), BackToLibrary }`;
  - `pub(crate) enum InputIntent { FocusNext, FocusPrevious, Activate, Escape }`;
  - a small `InputState` with a private focused target and pure transition helpers.
- Define the authoritative focus chain from current shell state:
  1. the sidebar toggle, which is always present;
  2. `Library` only while the sidebar is visible;
  3. on the library route, photo cards in exact `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel` order;
  4. on a detail route, `BackToLibrary`.
- Default focus is `SidebarToggle`. `FocusNext` and `FocusPrevious` wrap at the ends of the current chain. Hiding the sidebar while `Library` is focused repairs focus to `SidebarToggle`; no transition may leave focus on a control absent from the projected view.
- When a focused photo card is activated, reuse the existing typed `ShowPhoto(photo_id)` navigation path and set detail focus to `BackToLibrary`. Returning through `BackToLibrary` or `Escape` restores focus to the originating photo card when that card still exists; otherwise it falls back to the first valid target in the library chain.
- Add exactly one reusable application-owned `ActionButton` widget in `crates/rusttable-app/src/action_button.rs`. It may use iced's `advanced` API, so add only the `advanced` feature to the existing production iced dependency; keep `default-features = false`, `thread-pool`, and `tiny-skia` unchanged and do not add a crate.
- `ActionButton` must:
  - wrap standard iced button content and preserve the existing pointer/touch message path;
  - receive logical focus as an explicit read-only input rather than owning application state;
  - emit `FocusNext` for unmodified Tab, `FocusPrevious` for Shift+Tab, its existing action for Enter or Space, and `Escape` for unmodified Escape;
  - ignore key releases, repeated key presses, and Tab/Enter/Space/Escape carrying Control, Alt, Command, or Logo modifiers;
  - capture a handled keyboard event so one physical key press emits exactly one message;
  - render a visible focus outline using named constants/style helpers owned by `theme.rs` and never use hover or color alone to communicate focus.
- Replace only actionable shell/navigation/photo buttons with `ActionButton`: sidebar toggle, sidebar `Library`, each photo card, and `Back to library`. Static labels, facts, placeholders, and `Preview unavailable` remain non-focusable.
- Keep `app::update` as the sole production mutation path. Input transitions may return only `Task::none()` because logical focus is explicit state; do not add subscriptions, background work, timers, or native key hooks.
- Add deterministic unit tests for focus-chain construction, forward/backward wrapping, hidden-sidebar repair, detail entry, escape restoration, missing-origin fallback, modifier filtering, repeat filtering, and exact activation mapping.
- Add one 800×600 headless iced scenario using the production four-photo view from #65. Drive real Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, and Escape events with `iced_test::Simulator`, assert exactly one emitted message per handled key, route every message through production `app::update`, and verify the focused target plus library/detail projection after each rebuild.
- Keep all data in Rust constructors. Do not add screenshots, golden files, `.ice` recordings, sleeps, retries, native-window automation, repository-owned fonts, or platform-specific code.
- Do not add arrow-key grid navigation, type-ahead search, accelerators for import/edit/export, editable controls, screen-reader claims, accessibility-tree integration, custom menus, or persistence.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- focused `input` and `action_button` unit tests
- focused headless `keyboard_operates_photo_workspace` scenario at 800×600
- existing #15, #62, and #65 headless scenarios
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `cargo run -p rusttable-app --locked`; verify the named focus order and visible focus outline without using a pointer

## Acceptance criteria

- A default shell has exactly `FocusTarget::SidebarToggle`; the focused target is always present in the current production view.
- Tab and Shift+Tab traverse and wrap through the documented chain. Hiding the sidebar removes `Library` from that chain and repairs focus deterministically when necessary.
- Enter and Space on a focused action emit the same typed message and produce the same state transition as clicking that control; each handled key press emits exactly one message.
- Activating a photo card opens that exact `PhotoId`, focuses `BackToLibrary`, and Escape returns to the library with focus restored to the originating card or the documented fallback.
- Repeated presses, key releases, and conflicting modifier combinations produce no application message and no state change.
- Every actionable control uses `ActionButton`, retains distinct visible text, is keyboard reachable, and has a visible focus outline; static content never enters the focus chain.
- The headless scenario proves keyboard-only library traversal, detail entry, and return through the real production widget tree and update path without screenshots, native windows, a display server, filesystem fixtures, clocks, sleeps, or retries.
- Existing pointer-based shell and photo-grid smoke coverage continues to pass unchanged.
- No catalog/store handle, path, decoded image, metadata parser object, `Edit`, processing pipeline, network access, timer, async task, or platform-specific input API enters the input or widget boundary.
- The only dependency change is enabling iced's existing `advanced` feature; no new crate is added and `Cargo.lock` remains unchanged.
- The implementation is delivered by exactly one squash-merged PR that closes only this issue.
issues.title: feat(ui): add keyboard-operable action controls and deterministic focus state

issues.body:
## Why

#66 will provide a coherent owned view of imported source provenance, metadata, photos, and their current edits. That is sufficient for inspection, but a future editor or processing coordinator still needs a deliberate handoff for one exact photo/edit pair. Fetching the photo, source record, metadata, and edit independently would permit stale or cross-photo combinations, while choosing “the latest edit” implicitly would hide product policy inside a low-level lookup.

RustTable needs one revision-pinned selection contract that resolves an explicit `PhotoId` and `EditId` from an immutable catalog snapshot and returns an owned, internally consistent input. It must preserve the source/content/metadata provenance needed by future decode and processing orchestration without opening a file, compiling a pipeline, rendering pixels, or depending on iced or `rusttable-processing`.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Blocked by #66, which supplies the owned joined `CatalogSnapshot` and `CatalogEntry` values this issue resolves.
- Sequenced after #72 so read projection and durable edit-write semantics are settled before a processing-facing selection contract is declared; this is a project-order dependency and this issue does not consume `EditRepository`.
- Uses immutable `Photo`, `Asset`, `Edit`, `ImageMetadata`, `ImportRecord`, `SourcePath`, and revision/identity values already present on `main`.
- Completed #23 and #27 are downstream evidence that an exact `Edit` plus source provenance can later be compiled and evaluated; this issue adds no normal dependency on `rusttable-processing` and does not invoke those APIs.
- The UI contracts in #62/#64/#65/#76 remain presentation/interaction-only. A future adapter may translate an explicit UI choice into this selection, but this issue must not modify `rusttable-app` or those issues.
- **Milestone #3 follow-on sequence: 3 of 3 and the final issue in this consultation’s Architecture & Core Domain extension.**
- Independent of image-I/O #68, processing #70/#73, and quality/release #14/#17/#24/#29/#31/#43/#67/#71/#74/#75.

## How

- Write the failing integration tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/develop_input.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/develop_input_errors.rs`.
- Record the initial failing targeted commands/output in the implementation PR before adding production code.
- Add `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/develop.rs` and re-export only the intentional public selection/input/error types from `crates/rusttable-catalog/src/lib.rs`.
- Define an opaque, immutable `DevelopSelection` containing exactly:
  - expected catalog revision;
  - selected `PhotoId`;
  - expected photo revision;
  - selected `EditId`;
  - expected edit revision.
- Give `DevelopSelection` one explicit constructor plus read-only accessors. Derive deterministic `Copy`, `Clone`, `Debug`, `Eq`, and `Hash`; do not read clocks, allocate IDs, infer a default edit, or carry repository/UI/processing handles.
- Define an opaque, owned `DevelopInput` containing exactly:
  - the observed catalog revision;
  - one cloned `ImportRecord` for the selected photo;
  - one cloned `Edit` for the selected edit.
- Expose read-only accessors for catalog revision, source, photo, primary asset, image probe, metadata, and edit. Delegate source/photo/probe/metadata to the owned `ImportRecord`; do not duplicate independently supplied values that can disagree.
- Add `CatalogSnapshot::resolve_develop(selection: DevelopSelection) -> Result<DevelopInput, DevelopInputError>`.
- Add a private `EditId` lookup index to `CatalogSnapshot` if needed for deterministic unknown-edit versus wrong-photo diagnostics. Do not expose mutable maps or change #66’s public ordering/entry semantics.
- Resolve and validate in this exact observable order:
  1. selected expected catalog revision versus snapshot revision;
  2. selected `PhotoId` exists;
  3. selected `EditId` exists anywhere in the snapshot;
  4. the selected edit belongs to the selected photo;
  5. selected expected photo revision versus the photo revision;
  6. selected expected edit revision versus the edit revision.
- Define a typed, matchable `DevelopInputError` variant for each failure above, preserving all relevant IDs and expected/actual revisions. Implement stable `Display` and `std::error::Error`; do not use string-only errors or an opaque catch-all.
- Preserve the existing `CatalogState` invariant that the selected edit’s `base_photo_revision()` equals the selected photo revision. Do not add a redundant error variant for a state combination the public catalog API cannot construct.
- Return owned values only after every check passes. Resolution must perform no repository call and retain no borrow into the snapshot, repository, database, transaction, or catalog state.
- Keep edit selection explicit:
  - zero edits for a photo is not an implicit empty edit;
  - multiple edits do not create a preferred/current edit;
  - disabled operations remain part of the selected edit;
  - operation order and every provenance/revision field remain unchanged.
- Add deterministic fixtures covering a source path, content hash/length, image probe, representative metadata, one photo with multiple edits, and a second photo. Test success, all six validation branches, exact error precedence, explicit selection among multiple edits, and ownership after the snapshot/fixture repository is dropped.
- Add no file-root resolution, absolute/native path, filesystem access, decoding, pipeline compilation, rendering, cache, thumbnail, export, UI message, async task, serialization, database call, network access, or new workspace dependency.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test develop_input --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test develop_input_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_snapshot --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- Resolving a matching selection returns an owned `DevelopInput` with the exact snapshot catalog revision, equal imported `Photo`, source, primary asset/content identity, image probe, metadata, and equal selected `Edit`; the edit’s base-photo revision equals the returned photo revision.
- Dropping the `CatalogSnapshot`, fake repository, and all construction fixtures after success does not invalidate or alter the returned input.
- A stale catalog revision fails before photo/edit lookup. Unknown photo, unknown edit, edit-for-another-photo, stale photo revision, and stale edit revision each return their distinct documented variant with exact IDs/revisions.
- Tests containing multiple simultaneous faults prove the documented six-step error precedence.
- A photo with two edits requires the caller to name one exact `EditId`; either valid selection returns that edit, while no API returns a latest, first, default, empty, or preferred edit.
- Disabled operations and authoring order are preserved byte-for-byte/logically in the selected edit; this boundary does not filter or compile operations.
- Resolution makes zero repository, filesystem, image-I/O, processing, UI, clock, random, or network calls and mutates no snapshot/domain value.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal` remains limited to the existing `rusttable-core` and `rusttable-image` dependencies. No `rusttable-processing`, iced, catalog-store, codec, database, serializer, unsafe Rust, or inherited darktable type/code is introduced.

## One-PR boundary

This issue delivers only explicit revision-pinned selection and the owned develop-input/error contract in `rusttable-catalog`, with focused tests and any private snapshot edit index required to support it. It does not implement a UI adapter, current-edit policy, source-root resolver, file opening, decode, pipeline compilation/evaluation, export, durable adapter, startup replay, cache, or background task. One focused PR must close only this issue and be squash-merged after #72.
issues.title: feat(catalog): resolve revision-pinned develop inputs
