- Construct a row window against explicit `RasterDimensions`; validate with widened checked arithmetic and do not accept an empty or partially out-of-bounds range.
- Add:
  - `evaluate_graph(graph: &CompiledOperationGraph, input: &WorkingRgbImage) -> Result<WorkingRgbImage, EvaluationError>`;
  - `evaluate_graph_window(graph: &CompiledOperationGraph, input: &WorkingRgbImage, window: RasterRowWindow) -> Result<EvaluatedRowWindow, GraphWindowEvaluationError>`.
- Define typed `GraphWindowEvaluationError::{Window { source }, Evaluation { source }}` and preserve both nested errors through `std::error::Error::source`. Window validation fails before allocating/copying pixels or applying an operation.
- Refactor the existing private evaluator into one deterministic slice engine shared by pipeline/full-graph/window entry points. Do not duplicate operation dispatch, opacity blending, or finite-arithmetic checks.
- Preserve exact traversal within a window: graph node/topological order, ascending global row, ascending column, then `Red`, `Green`, `Blue`. Convert local window offsets to the original full-image `pixel_index = row * width + column` with checked arithmetic before constructing any `EvaluationError`.
- For a valid window, copy only that contiguous row range into one output vector, reuse its allocation across all graph nodes, and return no output after any failure. Do not clone the full input, allocate one full buffer per node, or retain a borrow into the input/graph.
- Treat the canonical graph’s disabled nodes as identity and enabled zero-opacity nodes as arithmetic-free identity exactly as #27/#70 do. An empty graph returns an owned copy of only the requested source rows.
- Keep `evaluate(&CompiledPipeline, ...)` source-compatible and bit-for-bit stable. `evaluate_graph` over the full frame must produce the same image or same typed arithmetic error as `evaluate` for an equivalent edit.
- Add no stitching helper with hidden ordering policy. `EvaluatedRowWindow` exposes its source dimensions, start/count, read-only slice/iterator, and checked local pixel access so callers can place rows explicitly.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and `rusttable-processing -> rusttable-core` as the only normal dependency.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test graph_window_evaluation --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test graph_window_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test operation_graph --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test render_slice --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test operation_opacity --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test rgb_gain --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --all-targets --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- Window construction accepts the first row, last row, and complete frame, and returns distinct typed errors for zero count, start at/after height, an end beyond height, and checked range arithmetic overflow.
- `evaluate_graph` and existing `evaluate` produce equal `WorkingRgbImage` values for empty, disabled, zero-opacity, opaque, and intermediate-opacity exposure/offset/RGB-gain edits.
- Concatenating independently evaluated one-row windows in ascending row order reproduces the exact full-frame pixel bits. The same holds for uneven multi-row partitions whose final window is shorter.
- Requesting valid windows in reverse or arbitrary order does not change any returned window; graph/input values remain unchanged and no call shares mutable state with another.
- `EvaluatedRowWindow` owns exactly `width * row_count` pixels, retains the original full-frame dimensions/window origin, and remains valid after dropping the source image and graph.
- A failure inside a nonzero-start window reports the original graph step/operation plus the global full-frame pixel index and channel, not a window-local index.
- Competing arithmetic failures within a window return the first failure in graph-node, global-row-major, `Red`/`Green`/`Blue` order. A window-range error always precedes operation evaluation.
- Success and failure leave the borrowed graph and full input bit-for-bit unchanged; no partially evaluated window is observable.
- Small-window evaluation copies only the requested contiguous rows and reuses one mutable window buffer across nodes; it does not clone or allocate a second full-frame pixel vector.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal` remains limited to `rusttable-core`; no image/render/catalog, thread-pool, async, serializer, database, unsafe, or inherited darktable dependency/code is introduced.
- All handwritten Rust files remain at or below 1,000 lines. Measured pre-commit, pre-push, and pull-request validation remain within the hard 30-second, 60-second, and 150-second gates.

## One-PR scope

Deliver only checked row-window values, graph/full-window evaluation entry points, one shared deterministic slice engine, global diagnostics, typed errors, and focused tests. Do not add arbitrary rectangles, branch/fan-out execution, threads, work stealing, caches, preview resizing, color-space changes, source provenance, file I/O, UI, SIMD, or GPU code. One PR must close only this issue.
issues.title: feat(processing): evaluate graph row windows deterministically

issues.body:
## Why

Current `main` pins the development and CI compiler in `rust-toolchain.toml`, but `[workspace.package]` has no Cargo `rust-version` and none of the eleven workspace packages inherits one. Consequently, locked Cargo metadata does not advertise the minimum supported Rust release for any RustTable crate, downstream tooling cannot reject an older compiler before compilation, and a newly added package can silently omit the compatibility declaration.

This is a package-metadata compatibility gap, not a hosted-toolchain propagation gap. Open #111 owns deriving workflow Rust/Bun setup and cache keys from canonical pins. #29 owns dependency versions, advisories, licenses, registries, and sources. #67 owns the workspace package dependency DAG. This issue must not duplicate any of those scopes.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Independent of #111: either issue may land first. This issue must preserve #111 when present and must not edit workflow setup, Bun setup, or cache keys.
- Independent of #67: discover the current workspace package set from locked Cargo metadata rather than maintaining another package/DAG allowlist.
- Independent of #71, #74, #75, #78, and #112. If #112 has landed, register the new fast check in its validation-surface contract in the same PR; otherwise do not create a competing validation inventory.
- No product behavior, package format, release-evidence schema, dependency edge, or toolchain upgrade depends on this issue.
- This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree and one squash-merged PR.

## How

- Add `rust-version = "1.95"` to `[workspace.package]` in the root `Cargo.toml`.
- Add `rust-version.workspace = true` to every current workspace package manifest. Do not place a literal Rust version in an individual crate.
- Add dependency-free `scripts/workspace-rust-version.ts` as the sole production checker and `scripts/workspace-rust-version.test.ts` for deterministic fixtures. Use only Bun/Node standard modules under the existing exact Bun pin; add no package dependency or lockfile.
- The production checker must:
  1. resolve the repository root and reject a root/workspace manifest path that is a symlink or escapes the repository;
  2. parse only the root `[workspace.package] rust-version` field and require one exact stable `major.minor` release with no pre-release, build metadata, wildcard, range, or duplicate declaration;
  3. run `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1` and require a nonempty workspace package set;
  4. require every workspace package to report exactly the root `rust_version` value and to reside at `crates/<package>/Cargo.toml`;
  5. inspect each discovered package manifest and require exactly `rust-version.workspace = true`, rejecting a package-local literal, omitted field, duplicate field, or false/non-boolean inheritance value;
  6. report every independently detectable package/manifest mismatch in canonical package-name order and return nonzero.
- Do not encode package names, local dependency edges, third-party integration owners, dependency versions, workflow pins, cache keys, compiler-install commands, or Bun versions in this checker.
- Add exact package scripts `test:workspace-rust-version` and `check:workspace-rust-version`.
- Run the deterministic fixture tests plus the real checker as one parallel task in `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` and in the cheap phase of `scripts/pr-ci.sh`. Run the real checker once in `scripts/prepush-fast.sh`; `scripts/main-ci.sh` receives it transitively through `scripts/pr-ci.sh` and must not duplicate it.
- Fixture tests must not invoke Cargo, rustup, GitHub, or the network. Cover valid metadata plus malformed/duplicate root fields, unsupported version syntax, empty workspace, malformed Cargo JSON, missing/duplicate package entries, package outside `crates/`, missing inheritance, package-local literal, false inheritance, metadata/manfiest disagreement, Cargo failure, and multiple simultaneous package violations.
- Add one temporary verification fixture showing a newly discovered workspace package fails until its manifest inherits the workspace Rust version; revert the fixture before merge.
- Preserve strict compiler/Clippy checks, `unsafe_code = "forbid"`, the 1,000-line handwritten Rust-file limit, locked Cargo resolution, no-network fast checks, and the 30/60/150-second budgets.

## Validation

- `bun test scripts/workspace-rust-version.test.ts`
- `bun run test:workspace-rust-version`
- `bun run check:workspace-rust-version`
- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1`
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Record clean pre-commit, pre-push, and hosted PR durations after the new fast policy task is wired.

## Acceptance criteria

- Root workspace metadata declares `rust-version = "1.95"`, every current workspace crate inherits it, and locked Cargo metadata reports `rust_version` `1.95` for the complete discovered package set.
- Temporarily removing inheritance, replacing it with a package-local literal, changing one package’s metadata value, duplicating the root field, using a version range/pre-release, or adding a new crate without inheritance makes the checker fail with the responsible manifest/package named; all verification edits are reverted before merge.
- The checker discovers packages from locked Cargo metadata and contains no second workspace package list, dependency DAG, third-party owner policy, workflow version, or Bun version.
- Deterministic fixture tests exercise every parser/comparison/failure branch without spawning Cargo/rustup or using the network.
- Existing exact toolchain selection, workflow setup, cache keys, immutable action pins, permissions, strict warnings/Clippy, unsafe prohibition, and source-size policy remain unchanged except for any already-integrated #111/#112 contract entry required to register this checker.
- Clean pre-commit, pre-push, and PR validation remain at or below 30, 60, and 150 seconds respectively.
- No new third-party dependency, Bun lockfile, workflow file/job, compiler installation, toolchain upgrade, product behavior, packaging change, inherited darktable build, or release claim is introduced.

## Exactly one-PR scope

Deliver only workspace Cargo `rust-version` inheritance, one metadata/manfiest checker with deterministic fixtures, package scripts, and minimal fast-check registration. The PR must close only this issue and be squash-merged to `main`.
issues.title: build(compat): declare and verify the workspace minimum Rust version

issues.body:
## Why

The root `README.md` is still the inherited darktable document. It identifies the repository as darktable, links darktable’s `master` workflow and releases, describes CMake/GTK/native dependencies, advertises legacy download/install commands, and gives no usable path for the RustTable workspace, hooks, validation budgets, worktrees, or iced application.

That landing page directly contradicts `AGENTS.md` and `TASK.md` and can cause a contributor—or automation following repository documentation—to build or modify the retained C implementation instead of the Rust rewrite. The complete migration needs one accurate entry point, plus a deterministic way to diagnose local setup before a developer spends the validation budget on compilation.

This issue replaces the existing README rather than adding another process document. It does not rewrite user-facing product documentation, delete legacy sources, or change product behavior.

## How

- Write failing README/doctor fixture tests first and record the initial red command/output in the implementation PR.
- Replace the inherited root `README.md` with a concise RustTable-specific document containing:
  - project identity and the complete darktable-to-Rust/iced rewrite goal;
  - current development status and an explicit statement that inherited darktable sources are retained for history/reference and are not the RustTable build path;
  - canonical prerequisites whose versions are read from `rust-toolchain.toml` and `package.json`, without duplicating numeric pins in prose;
  - clone/worktree setup consistent with `AGENTS.md`, including the required repository remotes and a warning never to push to `darktable-org/darktable`;
  - tracked hook activation (`core.hooksPath=.githooks`) and `pre-commit` availability;
  - the repository doctor command;
  - exact local commands for pre-commit, pre-push, PR-equivalent, and main-only validation, with the 30/60/150-second budgets and heavyweight-after-merge rule;
  - the minimal locked commands to build/run the iced application and the existing Computer Use installation path;
  - the one-issue/one-PR/squash workflow, referring to `TASK.md`/`AGENTS.md` rather than duplicating their full policy;
  - links only to current repository-owned files/workflows or clearly labeled historical darktable context.
- Do not add a second contributing/process markdown file. `README.md`, `TASK.md`, and `AGENTS.md` remain the complete repository-owned entry-point set.
- Add `scripts/dev/doctor.sh`, compatible with macOS Bash 3.2 and Ubuntu Bash, as a read-only, network-free local diagnostic. It must report all independently detectable problems in one run and verify:
  - execution from a RustTable Git worktree;
  - the current branch is neither `main` nor `master` for implementation work;
  - `core.hooksPath` resolves to `.githooks`;
  - tracked pre-commit/pre-push hook entry points exist and are executable;
  - `git`, `rustup`, `cargo`, `rustc`, `rustfmt`, `cargo-clippy`, `bun`, `pre-commit`, and `rg` are available;
  - the active Rust toolchain/components match `rust-toolchain.toml` and Bun matches `package.json#packageManager` without hard-coded versions;
  - `origin`/`upstream` resolve to `cgasgarth/RustTable` as required by `AGENTS.md`, a `darktable` reference remote may exist, and no push URL targets `darktable-org/darktable`;
  - tracked `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `rust-toolchain.toml`, `package.json`, `TASK.md`, and `AGENTS.md` are present.
- The doctor must not install tools, edit Git configuration, fetch remotes, invoke the network, compile the workspace, run the application, or expose credentials. It may print exact remediation commands after collecting all failures.
- Add deterministic shell fixtures with fake executables and temporary Git repositories covering a fully compliant worktree plus each missing tool, wrong version/component, protected branch, wrong/missing hooks path, non-executable hook, wrong remote, forbidden darktable push URL, missing tracked file, malformed canonical version file, multi-failure aggregation, and credential/token redaction.
- Add a README contract test that rejects inherited darktable badges/release-download sections, `master` build instructions, CMake/GTK build commands, remote `curl | sh`/PowerShell install snippets, and links to inherited executable workflows. Permit only the deliberate migration/reference mentions documented above.
- Run only the deterministic doctor/README fixtures exactly once in pre-commit and once in the PR cheap phase. The real doctor remains an explicit developer command and must not run inside hosted CI.
- Preserve all existing product crates, workflows, repository settings, and release behavior.

## Dependencies and sequencing

- Depends on completed #1 / PR #1, #2 / PR #3, #8 / PR #35, #40 / PR #63, #14 / PR #69, #17 / PR #82, and #84 / PR #109 for the workflow and repository contracts it documents.
- Independent of #74 and #87. If either lands first, document its final repository-owned behavior without expanding this issue into workflow or PR-policy implementation.
- Independent of #67, #71, #75, and #78; README may describe their eventual high-level role only after they are implemented, but this issue creates no architecture, coverage, offline, or release-evidence behavior.
- Independent of product milestones and product code.

## Validation

- `bash scripts/dev/test-doctor.sh`
- `bash scripts/dev/test-readme-contract.sh`
- `bash scripts/dev/doctor.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Record clean pre-commit, pre-push, and hosted PR durations after fixture wiring.

## Acceptance criteria

- The repository landing page identifies RustTable, the Rust/iced rewrite goal, and the legacy darktable tree’s reference-only role; it no longer presents darktable’s badges, releases, CMake/GTK dependency list, `master` workflow, build helper, or download/install instructions as the active project path.
- A contributor can follow the README from clone/worktree creation through hook activation, doctor, local validation, application build/run, and one-issue/one-PR submission without consulting inherited build documentation.
- Numeric Rust/Bun versions are not duplicated in README prose; the document points to canonical repository files.
- On a compliant worktree the doctor exits zero and prints a stable summary. Each missing/mismatched tool, component, branch, hook, remote, push URL, or tracked file fails with an actionable diagnostic; simultaneous failures are all reported.
- The doctor is read-only and network-free, never installs or edits anything, never compiles/runs RustTable, and redacts credentials/tokens from remote or command diagnostics.
- Fixture tests use fake tools/temp repositories and do not depend on the developer machine, network, or real compilation.
- The README contract test fails if any prohibited inherited badge, release/build section, CMake/GTK command, `master` workflow instruction, or remote pipe-to-shell installer is reintroduced.
- Deterministic fixtures run exactly once in pre-commit and once in PR validation; the real doctor does not run in hosted workflows.
- Clean validation remains at or below 30 seconds pre-commit, 60 seconds pre-push, and 150 seconds in the hosted PR workflow.
- No product code, dependency, workflow job, repository setting, legacy source, release artifact, or new process document is added or changed outside this exact scope.

## One-PR scope

Deliver all and only the RustTable replacement README, read-only local doctor, deterministic doctor/README fixtures, and fast-check wiring in one dedicated issue worktree and one squash-merged pull request. That PR must close only this issue.
issues.title: docs(dev): replace the inherited README with a verified RustTable bootstrap

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #68 / merged PR #104 gives RustTable a deliberately bounded classic-TIFF input slice, while completed #33/#45 and current `OutputFormat` still publish exactly PNG or JPEG. The asymmetry prevents a lossless TIFF interchange workflow even though `rusttable-image-io` already carries the reviewed TIFF codec feature. #68 and #85 both explicitly excluded TIFF output, so this is new work rather than an extension hidden inside either completed issue.

Enabling a generic encoder is not sufficient. RustTable needs one product-owned output subset that preserves the checked RGBA8 buffer, remains single-image/classic-TIFF only, obeys the existing encoded-byte and create-new publication contracts, and does not silently introduce high-bit-depth conversion, BigTIFF, metadata, color management, or extension-selected behavior.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #33 / PR #56 and #45 / PR #57 for `OutputFormat`, `OutputOptions`, `OutputLimits`, `OutputReceipt`, `ImageOutput`, `ImageOutputError`, bounded encoding, and no-clobber publication.
- Builds on completed #68 / PR #104 for the reviewed classic-TIFF input subset and round-trip decoder evidence.
- The existing durable capability from completed #85 / PR #107 must continue to work for every `OutputOptions` variant; this issue extends the same `FileImageOutput` rather than introducing another publisher.
- **Milestone #4 replenishment sequence: 1 of 5 and the best first new issue in “Image I/O & Metadata”.**
- Independent of metadata writing, sidecars, catalog/import state, processing, render orchestration, and iced. Later metadata-output work must not be folded into this PR.

## How

- Write focused failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Extend `OutputFormat` with exactly `Tiff` and preserve the stable supported order `Png`, `Jpeg`, `Tiff`. Extend `OutputOptions` with exactly one parameter-free `Tiff` variant; do not expose an unreviewed compression/options bag.
- Extend the existing `FileImageOutput` encoding path. Do not add a second image-output trait, output adapter, temporary-file strategy, or publication algorithm.
- Emit exactly one classic TIFF image with:
  - 8-bit unsigned samples;
  - packed RGBA channels in `R, G, B, A` order;
  - straight alpha preserved byte-for-byte;
  - contiguous planar configuration;
  - one reviewed deterministic byte order and compression mode, fixed by RustTable tests and documentation.
- The chosen encoder path must fail closed if it cannot prove classic TIFF rather than BigTIFF, one image rather than multiple directories/pages, and exact RGBA8 representation rather than implicit channel/bit-depth conversion.
- Keep `image` default features disabled. Use only the already-reviewed TIFF feature or one narrowly reviewed pure-Rust encoder dependency confined to `rusttable-image-io`; do not enable a default codec bundle, Rayon, FFI, C/C++, bindgen, RAW/DNG, HEIF/AVIF, OpenEXR, or unrelated formats.
- Reuse the existing `LimitedWriter` so the encoded-byte limit is enforced while the encoder writes, not after an unbounded buffer has been constructed. Return the existing exact `EncodedOutputTooLarge` category with observed length evidence.
- Complete all option validation and bounded encoding before temporary-file creation or destination mutation. Ordinary and durable publication must retain their completed pre-/post-publication state guarantees, collision behavior, cleanup semantics, and receipt construction.
- Output selection must remain explicit: `OutputOptions::Tiff` writes TIFF bytes under any destination extension, while PNG/JPEG options continue to ignore a `.tif` or `.tiff` extension.
- Do not write EXIF, XMP, IPTC, ICC, comments, thumbnails, orientation tags, timestamps, permissions, or application history. Do not rotate/flip pixels, premultiply alpha, convert color spaces, quantize to another bit depth, or composite transparency.
- Add purpose-built tiny tests/fixtures for opaque and non-opaque RGBA8, exact encoded-bound rejection, extension mismatch, collision, durable publication, and malformed/unsupported encoder outcomes. Decode successful TIFF output through the completed bounded `FileImageInput` and assert exact dimensions and RGBA8 bytes.
- Preserve existing PNG/JPEG output bytes and all current ordinary/durable success and failure behavior except for the intentional new enum variant.
- Update dependency-boundary coverage only as required for the selected TIFF encoder. Note #67 as the future consolidated DAG policy; do not implement or duplicate it here.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, deterministic/no-network tests, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --test output_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image --test durable_output_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_tiff --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_tiff_limits --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_collisions --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test durable_output --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test durable_output_collisions --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test tiff_decode --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_png --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_jpeg --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image-io --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Record clean runs proving pre-commit remains within 30 seconds, pre-push within 60 seconds, and the pull-request workflow within 150 seconds. Move any genuinely exhaustive codec corpus work to merge-to-`main`; do not weaken these budgets.

## Acceptance criteria

- `SUPPORTED_OUTPUT_FORMATS` contains exactly PNG, JPEG, TIFF in that stable order, and `OutputOptions::format()` reports the matching explicit format.
- A successful TIFF write produces one nonempty classic-TIFF image whose bounded RustTable input adapter decodes to the exact original dimensions and RGBA8 straight-alpha bytes, including non-opaque alpha.
- The output is one reviewed 8-bit contiguous RGBA image. Tests fail if the encoder emits BigTIFF, multiple directories/pages, a different bit depth/sample format, planar-separated channels, or an implicit color conversion.
- `OutputOptions::Tiff` produces TIFF regardless of the destination extension; PNG/JPEG options remain selected only by their explicit variants.
- Exact-limit output succeeds, the first byte beyond the limit returns `EncodedOutputTooLarge`, and no over-limit attempt creates a final destination or leaves adapter-owned temporary state after an ordinary returned error.
- Initial and deterministic publication collisions preserve the pre-existing destination byte-for-byte. Durable TIFF output returns a receipt only after the same file-sync, publication, temporary cleanup, and parent-directory-sync sequence completed by #85.
- TIFF output contains no user metadata/history payload and performs no orientation, color-space, alpha-premultiplication, compositing, or pixel-dimension change.
- Existing PNG/JPEG output, metadata-omission, collision, cleanup, and durability regression suites remain behaviorally unchanged.
- Public APIs and errors expose no encoder, `image`, TIFF dependency, `std::io`, raw OS, or boxed error type.
- No local unsafe Rust, FFI, C/C++ build step, inherited darktable implementation/fixture, networked test, sleep, wall-clock assertion, randomness-dependent behavior, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.

## One-PR scope

Deliver exactly one focused PR that adds bounded classic-TIFF **output only** to the existing ordinary and durable `FileImageOutput` paths, records failing-first evidence, passes every listed validation command and budget, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged. Do not add metadata embedding, XMP/IPTC/ICC, TIFF input changes, sidecars, catalog/import behavior, orientation application, processing, UI, or additional output formats.
issues.title: feat(image-io): add bounded classic TIFF output

issues.body:
## Why

`crates/rusttable-app/src/app.rs` currently stores a `PhotoWorkspaceViewModel` directly, and `view.rs` infers all non-ready conditions from that one value: an empty model renders `No photos`, while an unresolved detail ID renders `Photo unavailable`. That is sufficient for injected tests but cannot distinguish a catalog that has not finished loading from a successfully loaded empty catalog or a load that failed.

Before production startup performs I/O, the application needs one pure, closed lifecycle contract that widgets, navigation, focus, and headless tests can observe deterministically. Establishing the states separately from the loader keeps error wording privacy-safe, prevents async concerns from leaking into `view.rs`, and gives the later boot/retry PR a stable reducer target rather than combining state design, I/O, and task wiring in one change.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **Desktop Experience & Iced UI replenishment sequence: 2 of 5.**
- Blocked by #115, which supplies the pure persisted-record-to-presentation adapter and remains the only catalog-facing formatting boundary.
- Builds on completed #65 and #76 for the current library/detail projections and authoritative focus-chain behavior.
- Blocks the startup catalog loading/retry increment that follows in this milestone sequence.
- Confined to `crates/rusttable-app`; it must not add a repository/store dependency, choose a catalog path, open files, create tasks, or perform background work.

## How

- Write focused failing state/view tests first and include the initial red command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add a small module such as `crates/rusttable-app/src/library.rs` with a closed application-owned state model:
  - `Loading`;
  - `Empty`;
  - `Ready(PhotoWorkspaceViewModel)`;
  - `Failed(LibraryFailureKind)`.
- Define a closed, matchable `LibraryFailureKind` suitable for the later loader, with distinct variants for at least:
  - catalog location unavailable;
  - repository unavailable;
  - corrupt persisted catalog;
  - catalog record could not be converted into the checked presentation model.
- Keep user-visible failure copy application-owned and deterministic. Each failure kind must project a fixed title/detail pair; never render a raw filesystem path, OS error, redb diagnostic, source record, debug representation, or nested error chain.
- Replace the direct `Shell::photo_workspace` field in `app.rs` with the lifecycle state while retaining small read-only helpers. `Shell::default()` remains deterministic and starts in `Empty` for this issue so production behavior changes only in the explicit state projection, not through I/O.
- Preserve `Shell::with_photo_workspace` as a focused test/construction seam that creates `Ready(model)`, or replace it with an equivalently narrow constructor and update all existing tests in the same PR.
- Update `view.rs` on the library route to render exactly one mutually exclusive primary surface:
  - `Loading`: `Loading library` and no photo cards;
  - `Empty`: `No photos in this catalog` and no photo cards;
  - `Ready`: the existing ordered grid/detail behavior without layout or label regressions;
  - `Failed`: `Library unavailable` plus the fixed safe reason for the failure kind and no photo cards.
- Do not add an animated spinner, timer, progress percentage, retry control, refresh control, modal dialog, toast queue, or native notification in this issue.
- Adapt `input.rs` so the focus chain receives only the cards that exist in `Ready`. Loading, empty, and failed library surfaces must never retain or create `FocusTarget::PhotoCard`; the existing sidebar toggle and visible sidebar `Library` control remain reachable in their documented order.
- Preserve the completed unknown-detail contract from #65: a `PhotoDetail` route without a matching ready detail still renders the existing deterministic `Photo unavailable` surface and `Back to library` control rather than panicking.
- Keep navigation mutations in `app::update`, and keep every update in this issue synchronous with `Task::none()`.
- Add deterministic unit/headless tests for all four state projections, exact mutual exclusion of labels/cards, safe error wording, focus-chain membership, ready-grid compatibility, and the existing unknown-detail return path.
- Keep tests fully in memory. Do not use a redb file, environment variables, temporary directories, image fixtures, native windows, screenshots, clocks, sleeps, retries, or network access.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- focused `library` state and view tests, including the recorded initial red test
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- existing shell, grid/detail, missing-detail, and keyboard-only headless scenarios
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` with a recorded clean duration no greater than 30 seconds
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh` with a recorded clean duration no greater than 60 seconds
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh` with a recorded clean duration no greater than 150 seconds

## Acceptance criteria

- `Loading`, `Empty`, `Ready`, and each `Failed` kind are distinct, equal-comparable application states with no repository handle, future, callback, or mutable presentation collection.
- At 800×600, the production widget tree renders exactly the documented primary label for the active state. Loading/empty/failure surfaces contain zero photo-card actions; ready state preserves the current three-column ordered grid and detail behavior.
- Failure copy is stable and privacy-safe: no path, OS/redb message, debug formatting, source record, or nested error text appears in the widget tree.
- The authoritative focus chain contains photo-card targets only for cards in a ready model. Rebuilding loading, empty, or failure views cannot leave logical focus on an absent photo card.
- `Shell::default()` performs no I/O and deterministically renders the explicit empty state. Injecting the same checked model produces equal ready state and unchanged pointer/keyboard interaction.
- An unknown detail ID remains an observable `Photo unavailable` state with a keyboard-operable `Back to library` action.
- `app::update` still returns only `Task::none()` for every message in this PR; no subscription, task, thread, timer, filesystem, database, or network behavior is introduced.
- No dependency or `Cargo.lock` change is required.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace warning/Clippy denial, and rustfmt remain intact without broad lint suppression.
- Every handwritten Rust source file remains at or below 1,000 lines; split `library.rs`, `view.rs`, or tests before approaching the limit.
- All fast gates pass within the 30/60/150-second budgets.

## Exactly-one-PR scope

One focused squash-merged PR must close only this issue. It may change the app lifecycle model, `Shell` storage/accessors, `view.rs`, `input.rs`, and focused in-memory tests. It must not add catalog/store I/O, change dependencies, choose an application-data directory, schedule an iced task, add retry/refresh/import controls, decode images, or modify any non-UI crate.
issues.title: feat(ui): model explicit library loading, empty, ready, and failure states

issues.body:
## Why

Current `main` has an explicit but single-space color contract. `rusttable-image::ColorEncoding` can distinguish only `Unspecified` and `Srgb`; `rusttable-processing` accepts only normalized transfer-encoded sRGB and converts it to `WorkingColorSpace::LinearSrgb`; `rusttable-render` can require declared sRGB or explicitly assume sRGB for unspecified bytes. That is materially safer than an implicit transform, but every declared non-sRGB raster is still unrepresentable.

Display P3 is the smallest useful second declared RGB encoding for this boundary: it is D65-referred, uses the same transfer curve as sRGB, and requires a real primary-conversion matrix before current operations run in linear sRGB. Adding it proves that source declaration, transfer decoding, gamut conversion, clipping evidence, and render policy are separate decisions instead of aliases for “RGBA8”.

This issue implements one fixed, specification-based Display P3-to-linear-sRGB path. It does not claim ICC color management, camera/raw input transforms, display proofing, or decoder profile discovery.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #26 for normalized encoded channel validation, distinct source/working image types, and the existing sRGB transfer boundary.
- Builds on completed #73 for deterministic linear-sRGB output encoding and observable below-zero/above-one clipping.
- Builds on completed #83 for `ColorEncoding`, explicit source-color policy/decision, decoded RGBA8 coordination, quantization, alpha preservation, and render error precedence.
- Follows #116 in milestone delivery order so processing/color changes remain serial and reviewable; it is technically independent of row-window evaluation.
- **Milestone #5 replenishment sequence: 3 of 5.**
- The preview/full-resolution orchestration issue follows this one so it handles both declared source encodings through one settled render path.
- Existing JPEG/PNG/TIFF decoders remain `ColorEncoding::Unspecified`; this issue adds no format inference and no hidden default.
- Independent of open catalog issues #66/#72/#77, metadata parsing, image publication, iced, persistence, filesystem roots, and GPU work.
- This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree, one pull request containing `Closes #<this issue>`, and one squash merge.

## How

- Write failing tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-processing/tests/display_p3_boundary.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/display_p3_render.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/display_p3_policy_errors.rs`.
  Record the initial red targeted commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Extend `rusttable-image::ColorEncoding` with exactly `DisplayP3`. Preserve `Unspecified` and `Srgb`; keep the enum RustTable-owned, closed, `Copy`, and free of profile bytes or decoder-library types.
- Add focused processing types without weakening existing type separation:
  - checked normalized `DisplayP3Channel` with the same finite `0.0..=1.0` range/error categories as `SrgbChannel`;
  - immutable `DisplayP3Rgb` and row-major `DisplayP3RgbImage` with exact dimension/pixel-count validation and read-only accessors;
  - `SourceColorSpace::DisplayP3` while retaining `SourceColorSpace::Srgb`;
  - `to_linear_srgb_from_display_p3(&DisplayP3RgbImage) -> WorkingRgbImage`.
- Share private normalized-channel and transfer-decoding mechanics where appropriate, but keep public sRGB and Display P3 source values nominally distinct so a P3 raster cannot be passed to `to_linear_srgb` accidentally.
- Convert each Display P3 pixel in this exact conceptual order:
  1. decode each normalized channel with the existing sRGB transfer branches and breakpoint;
  2. multiply the resulting linear Display P3 triplet by one documented fixed D65 Display P3-to-linear-sRGB 3×3 matrix;
  3. validate each result through `FiniteF32` and preserve finite negative/above-one values without clipping.
- Commit the matrix coefficients and arithmetic order in RustTable-owned code with standards/source documentation in comments. Do not call platform color APIs, use an ICC engine, perform chromatic adaptation (both spaces are D65 here), or round/clamp intermediate working values.
- Extend render source conversion so packed RGBA8 tagged `ColorEncoding::DisplayP3` is normalized into the new P3 source types and transformed once before operation evaluation. Preserve straight alpha exactly as #83 does.
- Preserve existing policy behavior and add only the minimum explicit capability:
  - `SourceColorPolicy::RequireDeclaredSrgb` continues to accept only declared sRGB and rejects declared Display P3 with the actual encoding;
  - add `SourceColorPolicy::RequireDeclaredSupported`, accepting declared sRGB or Display P3 and rejecting `Unspecified`;
  - `AssumeSrgbWhenUnspecified` continues to make an explicit sRGB assumption only for `Unspecified`, while accepting either declared supported encoding without relabeling it;
  - add `SourceColorDecision::DeclaredDisplayP3`; retain `DeclaredSrgb` and `AssumedSrgb`.
- Keep output policy fixed to transfer-encoded sRGB. Display P3 colors outside the sRGB gamut flow through existing extended-range working values and #73 clipping counts; do not add a Display P3 output encoder in this PR.
- Preserve render error precedence: source policy before pipeline compilation, then evaluation, then final image construction. No source-space error becomes a string-only catch-all.
- Split color/render modules as needed so every handwritten Rust file remains at or below 1,000 lines.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, `rusttable-processing -> rusttable-core` only, and `rusttable-render -> rusttable-core + rusttable-image + rusttable-processing` only.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test display_p3_boundary --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test color_boundary --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test output_srgb --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test display_p3_render --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test display_p3_policy_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_decoded --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-processing --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-render --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `DecodedImage::new` remains source-compatible and returns `ColorEncoding::Unspecified`; `new_with_color_encoding` preserves exact `Unspecified`, `Srgb`, or `DisplayP3` tags without changing dimensions/layout/buffer checks.
- Display P3 channel construction covers both inclusive endpoints, representative interior values, non-finite input, below-zero input, above-one input, equality, ordering, and hashing with the same closed error distinctions as sRGB.
- Compile-fail coverage proves a `DisplayP3RgbImage` cannot be passed to the sRGB-only conversion function and a `SourceRgbImage` cannot be passed to the Display P3 conversion function.
- Documented reference fixtures for neutral values and representative red/green/blue/mixed P3 values produce the committed linear-sRGB results in the exact matrix/arithmetic order; repeated conversions are equal and preserve row-major order/dimensions.
- Display P3 primary fixtures may produce finite negative or above-one linear-sRGB channels; those values remain unclipped until #73, where exact per-direction/per-channel clipping counts are observable.
- `RequireDeclaredSrgb` rejects Display P3 before an invalid operation schema can be compiled. `RequireDeclaredSupported` accepts declared sRGB/P3 and rejects unspecified input. `AssumeSrgbWhenUnspecified` records `AssumedSrgb` only for unspecified bytes and records `DeclaredDisplayP3` for P3.
- A real Display P3 render containing exposure, offset, opacity, and RGB gain returns deterministic sRGB bytes, exact edit provenance, `DeclaredDisplayP3`, straight-alpha preservation, and the expected gamut-clipping report.
- Existing all-256-code sRGB round-trip, quantization, source-color, operation, and render-error tests remain unchanged and passing; no P3 behavior silently changes sRGB bits.
- Existing image decoders still return `Unspecified`; no file signature, extension, EXIF field, or library default is treated as proof of sRGB/P3 in this PR.
- Dependency-tree and crate-boundary tests show no new normal dependency. No ICC/profile parser, LittleCMS/platform API, serializer, catalog, UI, unsafe Rust, or inherited darktable color code is introduced.
- All handwritten Rust files remain at or below 1,000 lines. Measured pre-commit, pre-push, and pull-request validation remain within the hard 30-second, 60-second, and 150-second gates.

## One-PR scope

Deliver only the declared Display P3 tag/types, fixed transfer-plus-matrix conversion into linear sRGB, minimal render policy/decision extension, typed tests/errors, and existing sRGB-output integration. Do not add ICC profiles, camera matrices, raw demosaic, white-balance policy, chromatic adaptation, display/proof transforms, P3 output encoding, decoder inference, preview scaling, source receipts, filesystem work, UI, SIMD, or GPU code. One PR must close only this issue.
issues.title: feat(color): transform declared Display P3 sources into linear sRGB

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #28 / PR #53 defines an immutable canonical nine-field `ImageMetadata`, and completed #79 / PR #105 reads that model from bounded JPEG, PNG, and classic-TIFF EXIF. The current `rusttable-metadata` crate is read-only: it has no parser-neutral output contract and no way to serialize the exact canonical values for a later image exporter. As a result, RustTable can inspect metadata but cannot establish a controlled metadata round trip.

A metadata writer must not become an arbitrary EXIF copier. RustTable needs one deterministic, bounded encoder for only the reviewed canonical fields, with exact representability checks and a closed error surface. Unsupported text/rational values must fail closed rather than being truncated, transliterated, converted through floating point, or silently omitted.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #28 / PR #53 for the exact `ImageMetadata`, `MetadataField`, `MetadataEntry`, `MetadataText`, `PositiveRational`, and `Orientation` invariants.
- Builds on completed #79 / PR #105 for the authoritative IFD locations, canonical EXIF tags, bounded reader, and byte-for-byte round-trip oracle.
- **Milestone #4 replenishment sequence: 2 of 5.** Delivery-sequenced after #119 so milestone work remains serial; there is no TIFF-output code dependency.
- Blocks the later explicit metadata-bearing JPEG/PNG publication issue. That issue must consume this encoder rather than define another EXIF serializer.
- Independent of image encoding/publication, TIFF output, XMP/IPTC/GPS/ICC, sidecars, catalog/import state, processing, and iced.

## How

- Write the focused failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add an object-safe, parser-neutral `MetadataOutput: Send + Sync` operation equivalent to:
  `encode_exif(&self, metadata: &ImageMetadata) -> Result<Option<EncodedExif>, MetadataOutputError>`.
  Empty canonical metadata returns `Ok(None)`; it must not synthesize an empty EXIF packet.
- Add a checked `MetadataOutputLimits` requiring explicit nonzero limits for:
  - maximum encoded EXIF payload bytes;
  - maximum total IFD entries emitted;
  - maximum bytes for one encoded text/value;
  - maximum aggregate temporary allocation used by the encoder.
  Reject zero, internally inconsistent, non-representable, and arithmetic-overflowing configurations. Do not provide an unbounded or permissive default.
- Define opaque immutable `EncodedExif` bytes representing exactly one classic-TIFF EXIF payload (without a JPEG `Exif\0\0` prefix or PNG chunk framing). Expose borrowed bytes and checked nonzero length only; do not expose mutable storage, parser/encoder types, offsets, or tag maps.
- Implement `CanonicalExifOutput` in `rusttable-metadata` using safe Rust. Prefer the standard library; any new dependency must be pure Rust, narrowly featured, reviewed under #29, and private to this crate.
- Emit one deterministic little-endian classic-TIFF payload with:
  - one primary image IFD containing camera make, camera model, stored orientation, and at most one EXIF-sub-IFD pointer;
  - one EXIF sub-IFD only when at least one EXIF-sub-IFD field is present;
  - capture date/time original, exposure time, f-number, ISO speed, focal length, and lens model in that EXIF sub-IFD;
  - canonical ascending numeric tag order within each IFD;
  - zero next-IFD offsets and no thumbnail/secondary IFD.
- Map exactly the nine existing fields and no others. Do not emit unknown tags, user comments, software/artist/copyright, XMP, IPTC, GPS, MakerNotes, ICC, thumbnails, previews, ratings, keywords, edit history, or arbitrary extension data.
- Preserve representable values exactly:
  - ASCII metadata text is emitted with exactly one required trailing NUL and no other trimming, normalization, case change, locale conversion, or padding-dependent semantic change;
  - non-ASCII text fails with a field-attributed `UnrepresentableText` error rather than being transliterated or replaced;
  - `PositiveRational` values are emitted as exact unsigned EXIF rationals only when numerator and denominator each fit `u32`; otherwise fail with a field-attributed `UnrepresentableRational` error;
  - orientation emits its exact `1..=8` code;
  - ISO emits one deterministic unsigned integer tag/type capable of preserving the canonical nonzero `u32` value exactly.
- Calculate every IFD count, offset, payload length, and allocation with checked arithmetic before writing beyond the current bounded buffer. Enforce the entry, value, payload, and allocation limits independently.
- Define stable, matchable `MetadataOutputError` categories carrying the relevant field/limit data for invalid limits, unrepresentable text, unrepresentable rational, entry/value/payload/allocation limits, arithmetic overflow, allocation failure, and internal invariant failure. Do not expose EXIF dependency errors, raw tags, `std::io::Error`, boxed errors, or string-only catch-alls.
- Add tiny purpose-built fixtures covering each individual field, all nine fields, empty metadata, each orientation, exact rational reduction, maximum representable values, non-ASCII text, oversized text/payload, rational overflow, entry-limit failure, and allocation/offset overflow seams.
- Round-trip every successful encoded payload through the completed `ExifMetadataInput` as `InputFormat::Tiff` and require equal canonical `ImageMetadata`. Also pin one all-fields payload to exact bytes so byte order, tag order, offsets, types, NUL handling, and output determinism are reviewable.
- Keep the current bounded reader behavior unchanged. The writer must not relax #79’s duplicate, malformed, nesting, entry, or value checks.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, deterministic/no-network tests, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test metadata_output_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test exif_encode --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test exif_encode_limits --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test exif_round_trip --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test metadata_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test jpeg_exif --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test png_exif --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test tiff_exif --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test metadata_values --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core --test metadata_model --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-metadata --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-core --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Record clean runs proving pre-commit remains within 30 seconds, pre-push within 60 seconds, and the pull-request workflow within 150 seconds. Keep exhaustive fuzz/corpus validation, if added later, in merge-to-`main` rather than weakening the fast budgets.

## Acceptance criteria

- `MetadataOutput` is object-safe and accepts only an immutable canonical `ImageMetadata`; `CanonicalExifOutput` has no unbounded/default construction path.
- Empty metadata returns `None`. Every nonempty representable metadata set returns one nonempty bounded classic-TIFF EXIF payload with no container-specific JPEG/PNG framing.
- An all-nine-field payload has deterministic exact bytes across repeated calls and clean processes. It uses little-endian classic TIFF, canonical numeric tag ordering, one primary IFD, at most one EXIF sub-IFD, zero next-IFD offsets, and no thumbnail IFD.
- Every successful single-field and all-fields payload round-trips through completed #79 to equal `ImageMetadata`, preserving exact ASCII text, orientation code, nonzero ISO, and reduced rational values.
- Non-ASCII text, a rational component above `u32::MAX`, and any other non-representable canonical value return the exact field-attributed typed error; no value is truncated, approximated, transliterated, converted through floating point, or silently dropped.
- Entry, per-value, payload-byte, and aggregate-allocation limits each have a deterministic exact-bound success and first-byte/entry-over failure without oversized allocation or partial `EncodedExif` output.
- The encoder emits exactly the nine canonical field tags and no XMP/IPTC/GPS/MakerNote/ICC/comment/rating/keyword/history/thumbnail data.
- Existing JPEG/PNG/TIFF metadata-read tests and malformed/duplicate/limit behavior remain unchanged.
- Public values and errors expose no parser/encoder crate, raw numeric tag, mutable byte buffer, `std::io`, raw OS, or boxed error type.
- No local unsafe Rust, FFI, C/C++ build step, inherited darktable serializer/fixture, networked test, sleep, wall-clock assertion, randomness-dependent behavior, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.

## One-PR scope

Deliver exactly one focused PR that adds the bounded parser-neutral canonical EXIF payload encoder and round-trip tests, records failing-first evidence, passes every listed validation command and budget, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged. Do not add JPEG/PNG/TIFF container insertion, filesystem publication, sidecars, XMP/IPTC/GPS/ICC, metadata model fields, orientation application, catalog/import mutation, processing, or UI.
issues.title: feat(metadata): encode the canonical EXIF subset deterministically

issues.body:
## Why

After #115 and #120, RustTable will have a deterministic adapter from persisted import records to the checked photo workspace and explicit loading/empty/ready/failure UI states. The production entry point in `crates/rusttable-app/src/lib.rs` will still start from a static default state, so users will never see records already stored by `RedbImportRepository`.

The next vertical slice should make the real desktop application open one local catalog at boot, list its records once, project them through the single adapter, and surface a recoverable failure without blocking window creation. This is the smallest real library workflow that connects the existing persistence boundary to iced. It must remain distinct from photo import, edit persistence, image decoding, previews, and the joined edit-aware snapshot in #66.

## Sequence and dependencies

- **Desktop Experience & Iced UI replenishment sequence: 3 of 5.**
- Blocked by #115 for catalog-record presentation and #120 for the closed loading/empty/ready/failure state contract.
- Uses the existing `rusttable_catalog_store::RedbImportRepository::open` and `rusttable_catalog::ImportRepository::list` APIs on `main`; it must not modify either crate's schema or repository behavior.
- Independent of open #66, #72, and #77. Startup loads persisted import records only; it does not replay edits, choose a current edit, or resolve a develop input.
- Blocks the explicit refresh/reconciliation increment that follows in this milestone sequence.
- If #67 lands first, the implementation PR must update its one central workspace-DAG policy for the deliberate `rusttable-app -> rusttable-catalog-store` edge and retain the #115 `rusttable-app -> rusttable-catalog` edge.

## How

- Write a focused failing boot/reducer test first and include the initial red command/output in the implementation PR.
- Add only the dependencies required for the existing adapter and store:
  - `rusttable-catalog-store` as an exact workspace path dependency;
  - `directories = "=6.0.0"`, matching the already locked version used by `rusttable-diagnostics`, for the platform application-data location.
- Add a small module such as `crates/rusttable-app/src/library_loader.rs` with three separated responsibilities:
  1. pure catalog-path selection;
  2. synchronous load logic that opens/lists/projects one catalog;
  3. iced task construction/message mapping owned by `app.rs` or a narrow coordinator.
- Resolve the catalog file deterministically:
  - a nonempty `RUSTTABLE_CATALOG_PATH` value is an explicit file-path override;
  - otherwise use `ProjectDirs::from("com", "cgasgarth", "RustTable")` and the local data directory with the exact filename `catalog.redb`;
  - never fall back to the current working directory, executable directory, home-directory string concatenation, or a random filename;
  - create the parent directory before opening the database and map location/directory failures to the closed failure kind from #120.
- Keep environment access testable without mutating process-global state: make path choice a pure helper receiving an optional override and optional default data directory, while production gathers `var_os`/`ProjectDirs` once at boot.
- Define a typed, monotonic `LibraryLoadRequestId` and a closed load result that distinguishes `Empty`, `Ready(PhotoWorkspaceViewModel)`, and the #120 failure kinds. Increment IDs with checked arithmetic; do not use time, randomness, pointer addresses, or hashes for request identity.
- Replace `Shell::default` as the iced boot function with a small `boot` function returning a loading shell plus exactly one `Task` for the first request. Continue using `iced::application`; do not add a subscription, custom executor, channel, detached thread, or external async runtime.
- Add messages equivalent to:
  - `LibraryLoaded { request_id, result }`;
  - `RetryLibrary`.
- The load operation must perform exactly one parent-directory preparation, one `RedbImportRepository::open`, one `ImportRepository::list`, and one #115 presentation conversion per request. It must not poll, watch the filesystem, decode a source image, or retain an open repository handle in `Shell`.
- Apply completions only when their request ID equals the currently active load. Ignore stale or duplicate completions without changing state, route, focus, or launching another task.
- Add a `Retry library` `ActionButton` only on the failed startup surface and a corresponding `FocusTarget::RetryLibrary`. Place it after the existing shell/sidebar actions in the authoritative focus chain. Activating it starts exactly one new checked request, changes the primary surface to `Loading library`, and removes the retry target until that request fails again.
- A retry message received while loading or after success must be an idempotent no-op returning `Task::none()`.
- Map all lower-layer errors to closed, safe UI failure kinds. Preserve detailed typed errors internally for tests/diagnostics, but never display the catalog path, OS error, redb error text, debug output, or record contents.
- Add deterministic coverage for:
  - override/default/no-project-directory path selection through pure inputs;
  - a newly created real redb file loading as `Empty`;
  - a real redb file containing ordered import records loading as the exact #115 `Ready` model;
  - repository-unavailable, corrupt-data, and presentation failures through controlled fakes or direct result injection;
  - boot yielding loading state and one active request;
  - success/failure completion, retry, duplicate retry suppression, stale completion rejection, and request-ID overflow;
  - a headless failed → retry loading → ready scenario using the production view/update boundary.
- Real-store tests may use a deterministic standard-library temporary-directory helper based on process ID plus an atomic counter, with explicit cleanup. Do not use clocks, random values, sleeps, retries, network access, or native-window automation.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- focused `library_loader` path/store/reducer tests, including the recorded initial red test
- focused headless boot/failure/retry/ready scenario at 800×600
- `cargo check -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked`
- existing app lifecycle, grid/detail, and keyboard scenarios
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- the repository-owned dependency-DAG check if #67 has landed
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` with a recorded clean duration no greater than 30 seconds
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh` with a recorded clean duration no greater than 60 seconds
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh` with a recorded clean duration no greater than 150 seconds
- `cargo run -p rusttable-app --locked` with an isolated `RUSTTABLE_CATALOG_PATH`; verify loading resolves to empty, and an invalid/unavailable path resolves to the safe retry surface without preventing window startup

## Acceptance criteria

- Production boot immediately projects `Loading library` and owns exactly one active nonzero request ID plus one iced task; no catalog I/O occurs in `view.rs` or widget construction.
- With no override, the database path is exactly the platform-local RustTable data directory plus `catalog.redb`. A nonempty override is used exactly; no working-directory fallback or random path exists.
- A new valid store resolves to `Empty`; a populated valid store resolves to the exact deterministic #115 ready model; unavailable, corrupt, or unpresentable data resolves to the corresponding safe #120 failure surface.
- Each request performs one open, one list, and one presentation conversion, then drops all repository/store handles before its completion message is applied.
- The failed surface contains one keyboard- and pointer-operable `Retry library` action. One activation starts one newer request and renders loading; duplicate retry messages while loading/successful do not start work.
- Only the active request may replace library state. Stale and duplicate completion messages are observable no-ops in reducer tests.
- User-visible errors contain no filesystem path, environment value, OS/redb/debug text, record contents, panic, or nested error chain.
- No photo is imported, no source image is opened/decoded, no edit is selected or replayed, and no preview/export/processing work is performed.
- No subscription, timer, polling loop, file watcher, channel, detached thread, custom executor, or third-party async runtime is introduced.
- Dependency changes are limited to the existing workspace store edge and exact already-used `directories` version; source and DAG policies are updated in the same PR when applicable.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict warning/Clippy denial, and rustfmt remain intact without broad lint suppression.
- Every handwritten Rust source file remains at or below 1,000 lines; split loader, path, reducer, and test modules before approaching the limit.
- All fast gates pass within the 30/60/150-second budgets.

## Exactly-one-PR scope

One focused squash-merged PR must close only this issue. It may change `rusttable-app` dependencies/lockfile, boot wiring, the loader/path module, lifecycle messages/reducer, retry focus/control, and focused tests; it may update the central #67 DAG policy if present. It must not change redb schema/repository semantics, add refresh/polling/import, decode or render images, persist edits, modify processing, or implement a general service container.
issues.title: feat(ui): load the local catalog at boot with recoverable retry

issues.body:
## Why

`CatalogState` on `main` can only start empty and grow through `CatalogState::apply`. That is sufficient for a live process, but it is not sufficient for reopening durable current state after #72: `CreateEdit` intentionally accepts only revision-zero edits, so a persisted edit at revision 3 cannot be replayed through the public command sequence without either failing or discarding its observed revision.

The current command boundary also permits `RegisterPhoto(Photo::from_parts(...))` with a nonzero photo revision and does not enforce catalog-wide `AssetId` ownership. Those states cannot have been produced by the documented one-step catalog command history and split canonical identity invariants between `CatalogState` and `ImportRepository`.

RustTable needs one pure, storage-neutral restoration boundary that reconstructs the exact current maps, validates cross-aggregate identities and revisions, and derives the catalog revision rather than accepting a second independently supplied value that can disagree. Under the current command model, every photo/edit creation advances the catalog once and every aggregate revision advance also advances the catalog once, so the exact revision is:

`sum(1 + photo.revision()) + sum(1 + edit.revision())`

with checked `u64` arithmetic.

## Sequencing and dependencies

- Depends on completed #19 and #41 for the current `CatalogState`, command, and optimistic edit-replacement semantics.
- Depends on #72 for the final storage-neutral current-edit contract and its retry/conflict semantics.
- Sequenced after #77 so the existing `#66 -> #72 -> #77` milestone-3 chain lands unchanged before restoration extends the state boundary.
- **Milestone #3 replenishment sequence: 1 of 4 and the best first issue after #77.**
- Blocks the later atomic checkpoint/bootstrap issue in this replenishment.
- Independent of #67, #71, #74, #75, #78, #87, iced/UI work, image codecs, processing, and release tooling.

## How

- Use TDD. Write the failing tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_restore.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_restore_errors.rs`;
  - focused additions to `crates/rusttable-catalog/tests/catalog_registration.rs` for the tightened registration invariants.
- Record the initial failing targeted commands/output in the implementation PR before adding production code.
- Add a focused restoration module, or equivalently split `state.rs`, before any handwritten Rust file approaches 1,000 lines. Re-export only the intentional restoration error/API from `rusttable-catalog`.
- Add `CatalogState::restore(photos, edits) -> Result<CatalogState, CatalogRestoreError>` over owned iterables of current `Photo` and current `Edit` values.
- Do not accept a caller-supplied catalog revision. Derive it with checked arithmetic as the sum of one creation step plus the stored revision for every photo and edit.
- Canonicalize restored values into the existing ascending `BTreeMap` order and build all private indexes before publishing a complete state.
- Enforce catalog-wide `AssetId` ownership across every asset of every photo, not only primary assets imported through `ImportRepository`. Add a read-only `asset_owner(AssetId) -> Option<PhotoId>` lookup backed by private state or an equivalent deterministic implementation; expose no mutable map.
- Validate restoration in this exact observable order:
  1. duplicate `PhotoId`;
  2. one `AssetId` owned by two different photos;
  3. duplicate `EditId`;
  4. edit whose `PhotoId` is absent;
  5. edit whose `base_photo_revision()` differs from the current photo revision;
  6. overflow while reconstructing the exact catalog revision.
- Define typed, matchable `CatalogRestoreError` variants preserving the relevant `PhotoId`, `AssetId`, `EditId`, and expected/actual `Revision` values. Do not collapse errors into strings or an opaque catch-all.
- Tighten `CatalogCommand::RegisterPhoto` so a new photo must have `Revision::ZERO`, matching the existing revision-zero rule for `CreateEdit`.
- Tighten `RegisterPhoto` so no asset in the new photo reuses an `AssetId` already owned by another catalog photo. Add typed `CatalogError` variants for nonzero initial photo revision and cross-photo asset identity conflict, with deterministic validation precedence after duplicate `PhotoId` and before catalog revision overflow.
- Preserve existing successful command behavior and failure atomicity. Every failed registration or restoration must leave the original state unchanged and return no partial state.
- Add history-equivalence fixtures that:
  - build a state through valid registration/create/replace commands;
  - collect only its current photos and edits;
  - restore from those values in deliberately shuffled order;
  - prove complete `CatalogState` equality, including exact catalog revision, canonical iteration, and asset ownership lookup.
- Cover empty restoration, multiple photos/assets, multiple edits per photo, nonzero current edit revisions, duplicate identities, unknown photos, base-revision mismatch, aggregate revision `u64::MAX`, and cumulative-sum overflow.
- Add a regression fixture proving that a nonzero `Photo` can be reconstructed by `restore` but cannot be inserted as a new photo through `RegisterPhoto`; restoration and creation are intentionally different operations.
- Document the revision-accounting invariant beside `CatalogState`: every future catalog command that creates or advances an aggregate must advance the catalog exactly once, and any command that changes that accounting must update restoration tests in the same PR.
- Keep `rusttable-catalog` free of database, serialization, filesystem, iced, codec, processing, async-runtime, synchronization, clock, randomness, and inherited darktable dependencies.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, and the 1,000-line handwritten Rust file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_restore --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_restore_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_registration --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- Restoring the current values from any valid command-built state produces a completely equal `CatalogState`, including the exact derived catalog revision, ascending photo/edit iteration, and catalog-wide asset ownership lookup.
- Empty restoration produces the same state as `CatalogState::new()` at `Revision::ZERO`.
- A photo contributes exactly `1 + photo.revision().get()` and an edit contributes exactly `1 + edit.revision().get()` to the reconstructed catalog revision; boundary and cumulative overflows return the documented typed error without a partial value.
- Duplicate photo, cross-photo asset, duplicate edit, unknown edit photo, and edit/base-photo revision mismatch each return their distinct documented variant with exact identity/revision data and documented precedence.
- `RegisterPhoto` rejects a nonzero initial photo revision and any asset identity already owned by another photo, while preserving duplicate-photo precedence and complete failure atomicity.
- A valid nonzero-revision `Photo` remains reconstructable through `CatalogState::restore`; the new registration rule does not weaken `Photo::from_parts` or persistence decoding.
- Restoration performs no repository, database, serialization, filesystem, image-I/O, processing, UI, clock, random, network, or async work and retains no external borrow.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal` remains limited to the existing `rusttable-core` and `rusttable-image` dependencies.
- No unsafe Rust, inherited darktable type/code, handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines, or nondeterministic test fixture is introduced.

## Exactly one-PR scope

This issue delivers only catalog-wide asset identity enforcement, revision-zero photo registration, exact current-state restoration, derived catalog revision accounting, typed errors, and focused tests in `rusttable-catalog`. It does not read repositories, persist edits, add a concrete store adapter, create a startup session, change snapshot/develop-input behavior, retain edit history, add delete/rebase/source-refresh commands, or modify iced/image/processing code. One focused PR must close only this issue and be squash-merged before the checkpoint/bootstrap issue begins.
issues.title: feat(catalog): restore validated catalog state with exact derived revisions

issues.body:
## Why

Completed #33/#45 deliberately define ordinary PNG/JPEG output as metadata-free, and completed #85 adds a separate stronger durability capability without changing that omission contract. #122 will provide a deterministic bounded serializer for the exact canonical `ImageMetadata` fields, but serialized EXIF bytes alone do not produce an interoperable image file.

RustTable needs one explicit opt-in output capability that embeds the canonical EXIF payload in JPEG APP1 or PNG `eXIf`, enforces the final encoded-file limit including metadata, and preserves the existing no-clobber/failure-atomic publication behavior. The ordinary `ImageOutput::write_new` API must remain metadata-free and byte-compatible so callers cannot begin leaking metadata merely because the serializer exists.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Blocked by #122, which supplies the only allowed canonical EXIF serializer and its representability/size policy. Do not implement a second EXIF writer in this issue.
- Builds on completed #33 / PR #56 and #45 / PR #57 for explicit PNG/JPEG selection, bounded encoding, receipts, typed failures, sibling temporary files, file sync, and no-clobber publication.
- Uses completed #79 / PR #105 as the round-trip reader oracle.
- **Milestone #4 replenishment sequence: 3 of 5.** Implement only after #122 is squash-merged or explicitly blocked. It is independent of #119 because this first metadata-bearing publication slice supports JPEG and PNG only.
- Completed #85’s `DurableImageOutput` remains unchanged and metadata-free in this PR; a durable metadata-bearing capability is separate future work rather than an implicit guarantee here.
- Independent of TIFF metadata insertion, XMP/IPTC/GPS/ICC, sidecars, catalog/import state, render orchestration, and iced.

## How

- Write focused failing tests first and record the initial red commands/output in the implementation PR.
- In `rusttable-metadata`, add a separate object-safe `MetadataImageOutput: Send + Sync` operation equivalent to:
  `write_new_with_metadata(&self, image, metadata, destination, options, metadata_limits) -> Result<OutputReceipt, MetadataImageOutputError>`.
  The trait may use `DecodedImage`, `ImageMetadata`, `OutputOptions`, `OutputReceipt`, and #122’s checked limits, but no codec/filesystem/parser type.
- Implement `MetadataImageOutput` for the existing `rusttable_image_io::FileImageOutput`. Do not add a second encoder/publisher type, alternate temporary-file naming scheme, overwrite mode, or generic container-rewriter API.
- Keep `ImageOutput::write_new` and `DurableImageOutput::write_new_durable` source- and behavior-compatible with completed #33/#45/#85. They must continue to omit all user metadata and must not call the new path internally.
- For nonempty metadata, call #122’s `CanonicalExifOutput` exactly once before any temporary-file creation or destination mutation. Serializer failure returns a nested typed metadata error and produces no output artifact.
- Support exactly these explicit output formats:
  - JPEG: insert one APP1 segment immediately after SOI containing `Exif\0\0` plus the exact #122 payload; enforce the JPEG 16-bit segment-length limit before allocation and reject any unexpected pre-existing EXIF APP1 in the adapter-owned encoded stream;
  - PNG: insert one `eXIf` chunk containing the exact #122 payload immediately after the single IHDR chunk, compute the exact chunk CRC, and reject malformed adapter-owned chunk structure, duplicate IHDR/IEND, or an unexpected pre-existing `eXIf` chunk.
- `OutputOptions::Tiff` from #119, if present, must return a stable typed `UnsupportedMetadataOutputFormat { format: Tiff }` before pixel encoding or filesystem mutation. Do not silently omit metadata or add TIFF insertion here.
- Empty `ImageMetadata` must produce exactly the same bytes and `OutputReceipt` as `ImageOutput::write_new` for equal image/options/destination conditions; do not emit empty APP1/`eXIf` structures.
- Treat the existing `OutputLimits::max_encoded_bytes` as the final complete-file limit, including APP1/chunk framing, EXIF bytes, and CRC. Use checked arithmetic and bounded writers/reservations so metadata insertion cannot first build an over-limit file in memory.
- Reuse/refactor the existing bounded encoder and publication helpers once. All pixel encoding, EXIF serialization, container validation/insertion, final-size validation, and receipt construction data must be complete before the sibling temporary file is created.
- Preserve explicit format selection regardless of extension. A metadata-bearing JPEG under `.png` is JPEG, and a metadata-bearing PNG under `.jpg` is PNG.
- Define stable, matchable `MetadataImageOutputError` variants with relevant format/limit data for:
  - unsupported metadata-bearing output format;
  - nested #122 metadata serialization failure;
  - EXIF segment/chunk framing limit;
  - malformed or unexpected adapter-owned encoded-container structure;
  - nested existing `ImageOutputError` for encode, destination, limit, write, sync, collision, cleanup, and publication behavior.
  Preserve nested sources through `std::error::Error::source`; expose no `image`, EXIF, CRC, `std::io`, raw OS, or boxed error type.
- Preserve canonical metadata only. Do not copy unknown source EXIF, XMP, IPTC, GPS, ICC, comments, timestamps, permissions, thumbnails, ratings, keywords, MakerNotes, or edit history.
