- Do not rotate/flip pixels, rewrite orientation, convert timezones, convert color spaces, premultiply alpha, composite JPEG transparency, or alter the existing PNG/JPEG quality/alpha policy.
- Add purpose-built tiny fixtures for all-nine-field and empty metadata, wrong extensions, exact final-byte bounds, APP1 segment overflow, PNG chunk/CRC validation, serializer failure, destination collision, and deterministic injected pre-publication failures.
- Read successful outputs through `ExifMetadataInput` and require equal canonical metadata. Decode the PNG and require exact pixels; retain the existing JPEG pixel/quality assertions without pretending lossy samples round-trip exactly.
- Update crate-boundary tests for the deliberate `rusttable-image-io -> rusttable-metadata` integration edge and any narrowly reviewed pure-Rust CRC dependency. Note #67 as the future consolidated DAG policy; do not implement it here.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, strict workspace lints, deterministic/no-network tests, and the 1,000-line handwritten-file limit.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test metadata_image_output_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test exif_encode --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata --test exif_round_trip --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_exif_jpeg --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_exif_png --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_exif_limits --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_metadata --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_jpeg --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_png --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_collisions --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test durable_output --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-metadata --edges normal`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-image-io --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `git diff --check`

Record clean runs proving pre-commit remains within 30 seconds, pre-push within 60 seconds, and the pull-request workflow within 150 seconds. Keep exhaustive malformed-container corpora out of the fast path and in merge-to-`main` if later justified.

## Acceptance criteria

- `MetadataImageOutput` is object-safe, implemented by the existing `FileImageOutput`, and is the only opt-in API that accepts canonical metadata. Existing ordinary and durable output APIs remain metadata-free and behavior-compatible.
- A nonempty metadata-bearing JPEG contains exactly one APP1 `Exif\0\0` payload immediately after SOI; a nonempty metadata-bearing PNG contains exactly one valid-CRC `eXIf` chunk immediately after IHDR.
- Reading either output through completed #79 returns `ImageMetadata` equal to the representable input. The PNG also decodes to exact original RGBA8 bytes; JPEG retains completed quality, dimension, and opaque-alpha behavior.
- Empty metadata produces byte-for-byte the same PNG/JPEG file as the ordinary `ImageOutput::write_new` path and adds no APP1/`eXIf` payload.
- The final complete file, not merely the pixel stream, is bounded by `OutputLimits`. Exact final size succeeds; the first byte beyond the limit fails before temporary creation and leaves no destination or adapter-owned temporary state.
- An EXIF payload too large for one JPEG APP1 segment, a malformed adapter-owned JPEG/PNG stream, a duplicate/unexpected EXIF container, or PNG length/CRC arithmetic failure returns its exact typed fail-closed category without publishing a metadata-free fallback.
- Initial and deterministic no-clobber collisions preserve the existing destination byte-for-byte. Every normally returned pre-publication error leaves no final file and no adapter-owned temporary name.
- Explicit options, never extensions, select JPEG versus PNG. TIFF and every other format are rejected before mutation rather than silently dropping metadata.
- Outputs contain only the exact canonical EXIF subset. No unknown source metadata, XMP/IPTC/GPS/ICC/MakerNote/comment/rating/keyword/history/thumbnail data is copied or synthesized.
- Existing PNG/JPEG metadata-omission, quality, alpha, collision, cleanup, and durable-output tests remain unchanged except for intentional shared-helper refactoring.
- Public APIs/errors expose no codec, EXIF, CRC, parser, `std::io`, raw OS, or boxed implementation type.
- No local unsafe Rust, FFI, C/C++ build step, inherited darktable exporter/fixture, networked test, sleep, wall-clock assertion, randomness-dependent behavior, or handwritten Rust file over 1,000 lines is introduced.

## One-PR scope

Deliver exactly one focused PR that adds explicit bounded canonical-EXIF publication for ordinary create-new JPEG and PNG outputs, records failing-first evidence, passes every listed validation command and budget, closes only this issue, and is squash-merged. Do not alter ordinary/durable metadata omission, add durable metadata-bearing output, insert TIFF metadata, add XMP/IPTC/GPS/ICC, preserve unknown source metadata, handle sidecars, mutate catalog/import state, apply orientation, process pixels, or add UI.
issues.title: feat(image-io): publish bounded canonical EXIF in JPEG and PNG outputs

issues.body:
## Why

Current `rusttable-render::render_edit` always processes the complete decoded raster at its original dimensions. It provides a correct full-resolution bridge, but it has no request model that distinguishes a darkroom preview from an export-quality render, no bounded preview dimensions, and no observable sampling decision. A future UI or application coordinator would therefore have to resize pixels ad hoc, potentially choose different aspect-ratio rounding, process a full image unnecessarily, or hide whether preview bytes were sampled before or after the processing boundary.

RustTable needs one pure render-target contract before caches, background scheduling, or UI integration arrive. The first preview policy should be deliberately narrow: fit inside explicit nonzero bounds without upscaling, select source pixels by one documented center-point rule, then run the same source-color, operation, output-encoding, quantization, alpha, clipping, and provenance path as a full-resolution render.

This issue adds deterministic preview/full-resolution orchestration only. It does not add a decoder scaling API, interpolation filters, crop/orientation policy, cache behavior, threads, async tasks, or UI state.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #83 for the pure decoded-image render bridge, explicit source-color policy, deterministic quantization, straight-alpha preservation, clipping evidence, provenance, and typed error precedence.
- Follows #121 so both declared sRGB and declared Display P3 sources use the same settled preview/full-resolution orchestration. This is the only technical dependency in the replenishment sequence.
- The graph and row-window issues #113/#116 remain complementary processing work; this PR does not consume graph windows or introduce a second evaluator.
- **Milestone #5 replenishment sequence: 4 of 5.**
- Blocks the source-bound render receipt/error issue that follows, which will record the exact plan and sampling decision rather than inventing another target model.
- Independent of open catalog issues #66/#72/#77. A later application adapter may combine #77, `ImageInput`, and this API, but this issue accepts an already-decoded image and an already-selected immutable `Edit`.
- Independent of filesystem paths, image output/publication, iced, persistence, metadata/orientation, caches, async runtimes, SIMD, and GPU work.
- This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree, one pull request containing `Closes #<this issue>`, and one squash merge.

## How

- Write failing tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/render_plan.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/preview_render.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/preview_errors.rs`.
  Record the initial red targeted commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Split `rusttable-render` into focused modules such as `plan.rs`, `sample.rs`, and the existing coordinator implementation; keep re-exports intentional and every handwritten Rust file at or below 1,000 lines.
- Define checked, immutable public values with read-only accessors:
  - `PreviewBounds`, containing nonzero maximum width and height, with distinct `ZeroWidth` and `ZeroHeight` construction errors;
  - `RenderTarget::{FullResolution, PreviewFit(PreviewBounds)}`;
  - `RenderSampling::{Identity, CenterPoint}`;
  - `RenderPlan`, containing the source dimensions, target, exact output dimensions, and sampling decision.
- Add `RenderPlan::for_source(source_dimensions: ImageDimensions, target: RenderTarget) -> Self` as a pure deterministic planner. It reads no pixels, clocks, randomness, runtime state, or device capabilities.
- Plan `PreviewFit` with widened integer arithmetic only:
  - never upscale either axis;
  - if the source already fits, preserve exact source dimensions and select `Identity`;
  - otherwise choose the limiting axis by cross multiplication, set that output axis to its bound, and floor the other axis from the source ratio, clamped to at least one pixel;
  - the output must fit both bounds and preserve the largest deterministic integer fit under that rule.
  Do not use floating-point scale factors or platform image APIs.
- Add `render_edit_with_plan(edit: &Edit, input: &DecodedImage, policy: SourceColorPolicy, plan: RenderPlan) -> Result<RenderOutput, RenderError>`.
- Validate `plan.source_dimensions()` against `input.dimensions()` before source-color policy or pipeline compilation. Add a typed, matchable `RenderError::PlanSourceDimensions { planned, actual }`; preserve the existing source-color, pipeline, evaluation, and image errors and their nested `std::error::Error::source` behavior.
- Preserve `render_edit(edit, input, policy)` as a source-compatible public API. Make it construct a full-resolution plan and delegate to `render_edit_with_plan`; full-resolution output bytes, clipping, provenance, source-color decisions, and error behavior must remain unchanged.
- Extend `RenderOutput` with the immutable `RenderPlan` used for the result and expose a read-only accessor. Existing image, color-decision, clipping, and provenance accessors remain unchanged.
- For `RenderSampling::Identity`, pass the original decoded image through the existing render path without constructing a replacement raster.
- For `RenderSampling::CenterPoint`, create one owned sampled RGBA8 raster before source-color conversion and processing:
  - traverse output rows and columns in ascending row-major order;
  - map each output pixel center to a source index with one documented widened-integer formula equivalent to `floor(((2 * output_index + 1) * source_extent) / (2 * output_extent))`, clamped to the final valid source index;
  - copy all four bytes from that source pixel, preserving straight alpha and transparent-pixel RGB exactly;
  - preserve the input `ColorEncoding` tag on the sampled raster;
  - allocate exactly one output-sized RGBA8 buffer and no full-resolution working/output buffer for a downscaled preview.
- Run the sampled or identity raster through the single existing source-color conversion, pipeline compilation/evaluation, linear-sRGB output encoding, quantization, clipping, and provenance implementation. Do not duplicate transfer functions, operation dispatch, blending, or output encoding.
- Preserve observable error precedence: plan/source-dimension mismatch, then source-color policy, then pipeline compilation, then evaluation, then final checked image construction. Return no partial preview after any error and leave the borrowed edit/input unchanged.
- Do not add bilinear/bicubic/area/Lanczos filtering, sharpening, dithering, mipmaps, arbitrary crop rectangles, orientation transforms, masks, geometry-operation scale semantics, decoder-native scaling, cache keys, scheduling, cancellation, threads, async, filesystem access, or UI messages.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, workspace warning/Clippy denial, rustfmt, and the existing exact normal dependency boundary: `rusttable-render -> rusttable-core + rusttable-image + rusttable-processing`.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_plan --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test preview_render --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test preview_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_decoded --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test quantization_contract --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test display_p3_render --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-render --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `PreviewBounds` accepts nonzero axes and returns distinct typed errors for zero width and zero height. Equal bounds compare equal and produce equal plans for the same source dimensions.
- Full-resolution plans preserve source dimensions and select `RenderSampling::Identity` for landscape, portrait, square, one-pixel-wide, and one-pixel-high sources.
- Preview plans never upscale. Sources already within both bounds select identity; larger landscape, portrait, square, highly asymmetric, and odd-sized fixtures fit both bounds and match the documented cross-multiplication/flooring results exactly.
- Planning the same source/target repeatedly yields an equal plan on every run and uses no pixel reads, floating-point scale factor, clock, randomness, thread count, or host/device property.
- `render_edit` and `render_edit_with_plan` with a full-resolution plan return equal outputs or equal typed errors for existing sRGB and Display P3 fixtures, including all-256-code round trips, opacity/RGB-gain pipelines, clipping, quantization, provenance, and alpha tests.
- A hand-authored coordinate fixture proves every downscaled output pixel uses the documented center-point source row/column and copies its exact RGBA bytes before processing. Alpha zero does not suppress or alter the selected RGB bytes.
- Downscaled sRGB and Display P3 previews run through their existing declared color paths and return `ColorEncoding::Srgb` output, the exact source-color decision, exact edit provenance, output-sized dimensions, and deterministic clipping counts.
- A plan created for different source dimensions fails before source-color policy and before an invalid operation schema can be compiled, preserving exact planned/actual dimensions and returning no output.
- Success and every error leave the borrowed `Edit` and original `DecodedImage` unchanged. Equal edit/image/policy/plan inputs produce equal output or equal typed error.
- A downscaled preview allocates one output-sized sampled RGBA8 buffer and processing buffers sized to the preview, not to the original full-resolution raster; no full-frame clone is created by the preview path.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-render --edges normal` and the crate-boundary regression still show exactly the three intended local dependencies with no reverse edge. No codec, resize library, catalog, persistence, UI, async/thread-pool, unsafe Rust, or inherited darktable implementation is introduced.
- All handwritten Rust files remain at or below 1,000 lines. Measured pre-commit, pre-push, and pull-request validation remain within the hard 30-second, 60-second, and 150-second gates.

## One-PR scope

Deliver only preview bounds/target/plan/sampling values, deterministic center-point downsampling, the planned render entry point, compatibility delegation from `render_edit`, plan-aware output/errors, and focused tests. Do not add interpolating filters, arbitrary crops, orientation, geometry-operation scaling, decoder-native previews, caches, scheduling/cancellation, source-file provenance, output publication, UI, persistence, SIMD, or GPU work. One PR must close only this issue.
issues.title: feat(render): orchestrate deterministic preview and full-resolution targets

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Make the existing unsigned macOS distribution artifact unambiguous and mechanically verifiable. This PR must continue producing one native-architecture application archive, but the target triple and Mach-O slice must be encoded in both the artifact name and the smoke evidence.

## Fixed decisions

- Build exactly `aarch64-apple-darwin` on the current Apple Silicon merge runner; do not produce an x86_64 or universal binary in this issue.
- Rename the archive to `RustTable-<version>-aarch64-apple-darwin-macos-unsigned.zip`.
- Keep `RustTable.app` as the bundle root and `Contents/MacOS/rusttable-app` as the executable path.
- Extend the existing merge-only workflow; do not add another workflow or trigger.
- Treat `file` and `lipo -archs` output as diagnostic input only. The smoke script must parse and compare the expected target, reported Mach-O architecture, extracted executable, and staged executable.

## Required implementation

- Update `scripts/macos-distribution-smoke.sh` to accept the expected target triple and fail on a missing, foreign, fat/universal, or mismatched executable.
- Emit a stable `smoke.log` schema containing: schema version, source SHA, Cargo package version, target triple, Mach-O architectures, bundle identifier, staged executable SHA-256, extracted executable SHA-256, archive SHA-256, and `--version` output.
- Use deterministic zip entry ordering and timestamps already established by the current packaging path.
- Update the existing merge workflow artifact upload name and every fixture/parser test that consumes the old basename or log schema.
- Add shell fixture tests for arm64 success, x86_64 mismatch, universal-binary rejection, malformed tool output, missing executable, and staged/extracted hash mismatch.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The merge job produces exactly one `aarch64-apple-darwin` unsigned zip with the new basename.
- [ ] Both staged and extracted executables are proven arm64 and byte-identical.
- [ ] The smoke script exits non-zero for every mismatch fixture and leaves no partial success log.
- [ ] `rusttable-app --version` runs from the extracted bundle without creating catalog/config state.
- [ ] Existing pre-commit, pre-push, PR, and merge time budgets remain within their enforced limits.

## Dependencies

Builds on the current macOS packaging implementation already on `main`. It is independent of later signing, notarization, universal packaging, release aggregation, and non-macOS packaging.

## One-PR boundary

Only macOS target identity, artifact naming, deterministic smoke evidence, tests, and existing workflow wiring belong in this PR. Do not add signing, notarization, publication, update feeds, or another platform.
issues.title: Make the macOS artifact architecture-explicit

issues.body:
## Why

Current `RenderProvenance` records the selected edit ID, photo ID, base-photo revision, and edit revision only after a render succeeds. `RenderOutput` separately exposes the source-color decision and clipping report, while `RenderError` preserves stage-specific causes but carries no source asset identity, content identity, probe, requested policy, or render plan. A caller cannot therefore prove which imported bytes and dimensions a successful preview came from, or correlate a pipeline/evaluation failure with the exact source-and-plan request without maintaining a parallel ad hoc record.

Open #77 will eventually return an owned revision-pinned develop input containing the selected `Edit`, primary `Asset`, `ImageProbe`, metadata, and source provenance. The render layer should remain independent of catalog policy, but it needs a small RustTable-owned value that an application adapter can construct from that input. The value must exclude filesystem paths and runtime handles, validate the relationships that are observable at the decoded/render boundary, and survive both success and failure.

This issue adds source-bound render request context, deterministic success receipts, and stage-attributed typed failures. It does not select a catalog item, open or hash a file, decode bytes, publish output, serialize an attestation, or add logging/diagnostics infrastructure.

## Dependencies and sequence

- Builds on completed #83 for existing edit provenance, source-color decision, clipping report, typed render errors, and the pure decoded-image coordinator.
- Blocked by #129 for the authoritative `RenderPlan`, target, output dimensions, and sampling decision that must be captured rather than redefined here.
- Builds on existing core `AssetId`, `ContentHash`, `ByteLength`, `PhotoId`, and revision types plus `rusttable-image::ImageProbe`; all are already reachable through `rusttable-render`'s three intended local dependencies.
- Designed to compose with open #77 after that catalog issue lands, but does not depend normally on `rusttable-catalog`, `DevelopInput`, `ImportRecord`, or `SourcePath`. Tests construct the source context directly from lower-layer values.
- **Milestone #5 replenishment sequence: 5 of 5 and the final issue in this consultation.**
- Independent of durable edit writes #72, image output/publication, metadata encoding, iced, persistence, filesystem roots, caches, async runtimes, SIMD, and GPU work.
- This issue maps to exactly one focused worktree, one pull request containing `Closes #<this issue>`, and one squash merge.

## How

- Write failing tests first in:
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/render_receipt.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/render_provenance_errors.rs`;
  - `crates/rusttable-render/tests/render_failure_context.rs`.
  Record the initial red targeted commands/output in the implementation PR.
- Add a focused `crates/rusttable-render/src/provenance.rs` module and split existing coordination/error code as needed so every handwritten Rust file remains at or below 1,000 lines.
- Define an immutable public `RenderSourceProvenance` containing exactly:
  - selected `PhotoId`;
  - selected primary `AssetId`;
  - selected primary asset `ContentHash`;
  - selected primary asset `ByteLength`;
  - the `ImageProbe` produced for that source snapshot.
  Provide one explicit constructor and read-only accessors. Do not carry a path, repository handle, metadata object, file descriptor, byte buffer, clock value, random token, or decoder implementation.
- Define immutable `RenderRequestContext` containing:
  - `RenderSourceProvenance`;
  - the requested edit identity/revisions represented by the existing `RenderProvenance` value constructed from the borrowed `Edit` before compilation;
  - the explicit `SourceColorPolicy`;
  - the #129 `RenderPlan`.
  Equal inputs must create equal contexts without reading pixels or performing I/O.
- Define closed `RenderFailureStage::{SourcePhoto, SourceDimensions, Plan, SourceColor, Pipeline, Evaluation, Image}`.
- Define a public, matchable `ProvenancedRenderError` with an owned `RenderRequestContext` and a closed error kind:
  - source photo differs from `Edit::photo_id()`, preserving both IDs;
  - probed dimensions differ from `DecodedImage::dimensions()`, preserving both dimensions;
  - nested planned render failure, preserving the complete existing `RenderError`.
  Expose `context()`, `stage()`, and exact typed-kind accessors or matching. Implement stable concise `Display` and `std::error::Error`; `Error::source()` returns the nested `RenderError` only for planned-render failures and never flattens it to text.
- Map each existing `RenderError` variant to exactly one failure stage: #129 plan mismatch, source color, pipeline, evaluation, or image. Do not infer the stage from a display string.
- Define immutable `RenderReceipt` containing the successful request context plus the actual:
  - `SourceColorDecision`;
  - existing edit `RenderProvenance` returned by rendering;
  - `GamutClipReport`;
  - output `ImageDimensions`;
  - output `ColorEncoding`.
  Construction is private to the successful coordinator so request/edit/plan/output fields cannot be supplied independently and disagree.
- Define `ProvenancedRenderOutput`, owning the existing `RenderOutput` and its source provenance/requested policy, with read-only `output()` and `receipt()` access. `receipt()` may return an owned copy derived from the output; do not expose mutable duplicate state.
- Add one focused entry point:
  - `render_edit_with_provenance(edit: &Edit, input: &DecodedImage, policy: SourceColorPolicy, plan: RenderPlan, source: RenderSourceProvenance) -> Result<ProvenancedRenderOutput, ProvenancedRenderError>`.
- Construct the complete request context before validation, then apply this exact observable precedence:
  1. source `PhotoId` versus edit `PhotoId`;
  2. probed dimensions versus decoded dimensions;
  3. delegate to #129 `render_edit_with_plan`, preserving its plan/source-color/pipeline/evaluation/image precedence.
- Do not validate facts unavailable at this boundary: do not recompute the content hash, compare byte length to decoded bytes, reopen the source, infer primary-asset role, compare metadata, or claim the decoded buffer was produced from the named hash. The receipt records caller-supplied imported provenance and the relationships this layer actually checked.
- On success, verify internally that the delegated output's edit provenance, plan, dimensions, and output encoding populate one coherent receipt. On failure, return no image/receipt but retain the complete immutable request context and nested cause.
- Preserve all existing `render_edit` and `render_edit_with_plan` APIs and results. Callers that do not possess source provenance are not forced to fabricate it; the new entry point is an explicit stronger capability.
- Keep error `Display` messages concise and deterministic; do not print filesystem paths, full content-hash bytes, raw image bytes, nested debug dumps, or host-specific I/O text. Matchable values remain available through accessors/variants for programmatic diagnostics.
- Add no serializer, output digest, timestamp, signature, standards-attestation claim, tracing dependency, global logger, catalog/store dependency, file access, network, thread, async task, UI message, or inherited darktable code.
- Retain `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, workspace warning/Clippy denial, rustfmt, and the exact normal dependency boundary: `rusttable-render -> rusttable-core + rusttable-image + rusttable-processing`.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_receipt --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_provenance_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_failure_context --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_plan --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test preview_render --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test preview_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_decoded --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test render_errors --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-render --test display_p3_render --locked`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test crate_boundaries --locked`
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-render --edges normal`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh`
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Acceptance criteria

- `RenderSourceProvenance` preserves the exact photo ID, primary asset ID, SHA-256 content identity, byte length, input format, and probed dimensions supplied to its constructor; equal source facts compare equal and no path/runtime value is representable.
- Constructing a request context reads no source path/bytes/pixels, performs no repository/decoder call, and deterministically preserves source facts, edit ID/photo/base-photo/edit revisions, requested color policy, and exact render plan.
- A source photo/edit photo mismatch fails first with `RenderFailureStage::SourcePhoto`, exact source/edit IDs, and the complete request context, even when probe dimensions, plan dimensions, source color, and operation schema are also invalid.
- Matching photo IDs with mismatched probe/decoded dimensions fail next with `RenderFailureStage::SourceDimensions` and exact probed/decoded dimensions before plan or color validation.
- After provenance preflight passes, plan mismatch, source-color rejection, pipeline compilation, evaluation, and final image errors retain #129/#83 precedence, map to their exact `RenderFailureStage`, preserve the nested typed `RenderError` through `Error::source`, and retain an equal request context.
- Successful full-resolution and preview renders return the unchanged delegated `RenderOutput` plus a receipt whose source, requested policy, plan/sampling, edit provenance, color decision, clipping, output dimensions, and output encoding exactly equal the corresponding input/output accessors.
- Receipt tests cover declared sRGB, declared Display P3, explicitly assumed sRGB, identity/full-resolution, and center-point preview plans. Equal requests produce equal outputs and equal receipts on every run.
- Dropping all construction fixtures, the borrowed `Edit`, original `DecodedImage`, and source-provenance value after success does not invalidate or alter the owned output/receipt. Errors likewise own their context after inputs are dropped.
- Success and every failure leave the borrowed edit and decoded image unchanged; no partial rendered image or success receipt is observable on error.
- The content hash and byte length are recorded, not revalidated. Instrumented fixtures prove this entry point performs no source read, hash computation, repository call, decode, output write, clock/random lookup, network request, or background work.
- Existing `render_edit` and `render_edit_with_plan` tests and public behavior remain unchanged; source provenance is an opt-in stronger contract rather than a hidden required global.
- `Display` output is stable and concise and does not include source paths, complete hash bytes, raw pixels, debug dumps, or host-specific text. Programmatic matching retains exact typed IDs/dimensions/stages/causes.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-render --edges normal` and the crate-boundary regression still show exactly core, image, and processing with no catalog/store/codec/serializer/tracing dependency or reverse edge. No unsafe Rust or inherited darktable implementation is introduced.
- All handwritten Rust files remain at or below 1,000 lines. Measured pre-commit, pre-push, and pull-request validation remain within the hard 30-second, 60-second, and 150-second gates.

## One-PR scope

Deliver only source/request provenance values, deterministic failure-stage/context wrapping, success receipts, the provenance-aware render entry point, compatibility preservation, and focused tests. Do not add catalog selection, source paths, file opening/rehashing/decoding, metadata validation, output hashes/signatures/serialization, publication, logging infrastructure, caches, retries, UI, persistence, threads/async, SIMD, or GPU work. One PR must close only this issue.
issues.title: feat(render): bind render receipts and failures to source provenance

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Produce the first installable-shaped Linux artifact and prove that the extracted release binary is the expected x86_64 GNU/Linux executable with all runtime dependencies resolvable on the build runner.

## Fixed decisions

- Build exactly `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`; AppImage, Flatpak, deb, rpm, musl, ARM64, signing, and publication are separate later issues.
- Stage `RustTable-<version>-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/` containing `bin/rusttable-app` and a machine-readable `build.json` manifest. Do not stage repository legal/notices files in this issue.
- Archive with deterministic GNU tar metadata and gzip settings as `RustTable-<version>-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu-unsigned.tar.gz`.
- Run only in the existing merge-to-main workflow and retain read-only repository permissions.

## Required implementation

- Add `scripts/linux-distribution-smoke.sh` with strict error handling, temporary-directory cleanup, and explicit expected target input.
- Verify the staged and extracted binaries using ELF header parsing (`readelf -h`) and reject non-ELF, non-64-bit, non-x86_64, PIE/interpreter inconsistencies, or changed bytes after extraction.
- Run `ldd` and fail on `not found`, unexpected resolution outside documented system locations, or a static/non-dynamic result that contradicts the manifest.
- Execute `rusttable-app --version` from both staging and extraction with isolated temporary config/cache/catalog directories and verify no persistent state is created.
- Emit stable `smoke.log`, `build.json`, and `SHA256SUMS` records containing source SHA, package version, target triple, ELF class/machine/interpreter, dynamic libraries, binary hash, archive hash, and version output.
- Add deterministic fixture tests for valid ELF metadata, wrong machine, malformed `readelf`, unresolved library, missing interpreter, modified extraction, and side-effectful version execution.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A merge build uploads exactly one deterministic unsigned GNU/Linux archive and its evidence files.
- [ ] Staged and extracted binaries are byte-identical x86_64 ELF executables.
- [ ] All dynamic dependencies resolve and are listed in canonical order.
- [ ] `--version` succeeds without creating user state.
- [ ] Negative fixtures fail before a success receipt is emitted.

## Dependencies

Uses the existing release build and merge workflow. It is independent of [0001] except for shared evidence conventions.

## One-PR boundary

Only Linux staging, deterministic tar creation, ELF/dependency smoke checks, evidence schema, tests, and existing workflow wiring belong in this PR.

issues.body:
Closes #116

## Why

RustTable needs bounded, independently requestable processing work for previews, zoomed regions, and future scheduling while preserving one arithmetic path and globally stable diagnostics. This slice makes contiguous row windows a deterministic, owned evaluation unit without introducing threads, tiles, caches, or a second evaluator.

## How

- Add checked immutable `RasterRowWindow` values with distinct zero, start, end, and overflow errors.
- Add owned `EvaluatedRowWindow` values with full-frame dimensions, origin, row count, iteration, and checked local pixel access.
- Add `evaluate_graph` and `evaluate_graph_window` over the canonical immutable operation graph.
- Refactor pipeline and graph evaluation through one shared slice engine, preserving disabled/zero-opacity identity and operation arithmetic.
- Preserve full-frame row-major pixel indices in nonzero-window failures and nested error sources.
- Add focused tests for exact stitching, arbitrary request order, ownership, identity operations, validation ordering, and global diagnostics.
- Keep `rusttable-processing` dependent only on `rusttable-core`.

## TDD evidence

The initial focused command `cargo test -p rusttable-processing --test graph_window_evaluation --locked` was intentionally run before implementation and failed because the row-window API did not exist. The focused suites then passed after the shared engine and public API were implemented.

Closes #121

## Why

RustTable must distinguish declared source encodings from transfer decoding, gamut conversion, render policy, and output clipping. Display P3 is the first additional declared RGB source needed to prove that boundary without ICC/profile inference or platform color APIs.

## How

- Add closed `ColorEncoding::DisplayP3` support while preserving unspecified and sRGB tags.
- Add nominally distinct checked `DisplayP3Channel`, `DisplayP3Rgb`, and `DisplayP3RgbImage` types.
- Decode the shared sRGB/Display P3 transfer curve, then apply a fixed documented D65 Display P3-to-linear-sRGB matrix without clipping intermediate values.
- Extend render source conversion for tagged RGBA8 Display P3 while preserving straight alpha and sRGB output.
- Add `RequireDeclaredSupported` and `DeclaredDisplayP3`; keep strict sRGB policy rejection and unspecified-input assumptions explicit.
- Preserve render error precedence and the existing sRGB bit-level behavior.
- Add compile-fail documentation proving P3/sRGB source types cannot be mixed accidentally.

## TDD evidence

## Why

RustTable needs one pure render-target contract before preview caches, scheduling, and UI integration. issues.body:
Closes #119

## Why

RustTable already has bounded classic-TIFF input, but output was limited to PNG/JPEG. This adds an explicit TIFF output path so a checked RGBA8 image can round-trip losslessly without extension-driven dispatch or metadata leakage.

## How

- Adds `OutputFormat::Tiff` and parameter-free `OutputOptions::Tiff`, preserving stable PNG/JPEG/TIFF order.
- Extends the existing ordinary and durable `FileImageOutput` paths; no second publisher or temporary-file algorithm.
- Uses the already-enabled pure-Rust `image` TIFF feature and standard classic-TIFF encoder.
- Adds a seek-aware bounded writer so the encoded-byte limit applies during TIFF encoding.
- Preserves RGBA8 straight-alpha samples, emits one uncompressed classic TIFF, and keeps metadata omitted.
- Keeps PNG/JPEG behavior and extension-independent option dispatch unchanged.
- Adds round-trip, exact-limit, collision, durable-publication, metadata, and contract coverage.

## TDD evidence

Initial focused red command:

```
cargo test -p rusttable-image-io --test output_tiff --locked
```

issues.body:
## Why

RustTable is a complete rewrite of darktable. The original darktable C/C++ implementation and its native build surface must not remain in the RustTable working repository once the Rust replacement has been established; otherwise contributors can accidentally maintain or execute two implementations. The local darktable reference clone remains the authoritative source for historical comparison and porting reference.

## Scope

- Add the deletion rule to AGENTS.md: when a darktable capability has been ported, delete its RustTable-side C/C++ implementation and obsolete native build/configuration/packaging surface; consult the separate local darktable clone when historical reference is needed.
- Remove the inherited C/C++ source tree and native build entry points from RustTable where they are not required by the Rust workspace.
- Remove obsolete C/C++-specific CMake modules, compiler configuration, native packaging/build helpers, generated native build metadata, and RustTable workflow/script references that invoke them.
- Preserve RustTable-owned Rust crates, iced UI, Rust validation, policy files, tests, and the separate darktable reference clone.
- Do not delete or modify the separate local darktable reference clone.
- Do not add FFI, a C compiler step, a build.rs bridge, or native dependencies as a replacement.
- Add a fail-closed inventory/check so future RustTable changes cannot reintroduce governed C/C++ source or native build entry points without an explicit reviewed exception.
- Keep the existing Rust-only boundary issue #114 coherent and avoid duplicating its direct native-linkage checker; this issue owns removal and the post-removal inventory, while #114 owns detailed forbidden-pattern enforcement.

## Sequencing

This is one issue and one squash-merged PR. Port-specific deletion must only remove functionality already represented by RustTable code or obsolete inherited surface; any still-needed reference material must stay in the separate darktable clone. The PR must close only this issue.

## Acceptance criteria

- RustTable's tracked tree contains no inherited C/C++ implementation or obsolete CMake/native build entry point in the governed removal scope.
- cargo metadata --locked --no-deps --format-version 1, Rust tests, strict Clippy, formatting, source policy, and fast gates remain green.
- No RustTable workflow or script invokes CMake, a C/C++ compiler, native binding generation, or inherited darktable build paths.
- The separate local darktable clone remains unchanged and available for reference.
- A deterministic, dependency-free inventory test/check fails if governed legacy C/C++ paths or native build references are reintroduced.
- No unsafe Rust, FFI, new native dependency, product regression, or extra project markdown document is introduced.
- Pre-commit remains <=30 seconds, pre-push <=60 seconds, and hosted PR validation <=150 seconds.

## Validation

Run focused deletion/inventory tests first, then the complete relevant Rust and policy gates. Record timings and inspect the final diff for accidental RustTable-owned deletion.
issues.title: arch(migration): remove legacy C and native build surface from RustTable

issues.body:
## Why

## What changed

- Added object-safe `MetadataOutput`, checked `MetadataOutputLimits`, immutable `EncodedExif`, and closed typed output errors.
- Added `CanonicalExifOutput` with deterministic little-endian classic-TIFF EXIF encoding: canonical tag order, one primary IFD, at most one EXIF sub-IFD, zero next-IFD offsets, and no thumbnail/secondary data.
- Added exact ASCII/rational/orientation/ISO representability checks and independent entry, value, payload, allocation, arithmetic, and allocation-failure handling.

issues.body:
## Why

`CatalogState` could only be rebuilt by replaying creation commands, which rejected persisted nonzero revisions and did not enforce catalog-wide asset ownership. This PR adds the storage-neutral restoration boundary required to reopen exact current state.

## How

- Add `CatalogState::restore(photos, edits)` with canonical `BTreeMap` state and private catalog-wide `AssetId -> PhotoId` ownership index.
- Derive the catalog revision with checked `u64` arithmetic as `sum(1 + photo.revision()) + sum(1 + edit.revision())`.
- Add typed restoration errors with the required validation precedence and exact identity/revision context.
- Add `asset_owner`, reject nonzero initial photo revisions, and reject cross-photo asset reuse during `RegisterPhoto`.
- Add history-equivalence, shuffled-input, nonzero-current-revision, overflow, precedence, and failure-atomicity coverage.
- Keep all changes inside `rusttable-catalog`; no storage, filesystem, UI, codec, processing, async, or core changes.

## Initial failing evidence

Before production edits:

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_restore --locked` failed to compile because `CatalogState::restore` was missing (exit 101, 0.17s).
- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog --test catalog_restore_errors --locked` failed to compile because `CatalogRestoreError` and `CatalogState::restore` were missing (exit 101, 0.15s).

## Validation

- Focused restoration, error, registration, catalog-error, and crate-boundary tests: passed.
- `cargo tree -p rusttable-catalog --edges normal`: only `rusttable-core` and `rusttable-image`.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, source policy, and `git diff --check`: passed.

issues.body:
Closes #125

## Why

Adds the explicit opt-in metadata-bearing publication capability requested by #125 while keeping ordinary and durable output metadata-free and byte-compatible.

## How

- Adds object-safe `MetadataImageOutput` and stable nested `MetadataImageOutputError` APIs.
- Implements the capability on the existing `FileImageOutput` using the canonical bounded EXIF serializer.
- Publishes exactly one bounded EXIF APP1 immediately after JPEG SOI or one CRC-valid `eXIf` chunk immediately after PNG IHDR.
- Enforces final encoded-file limits including metadata framing, rejects malformed/duplicate adapter-owned containers, preserves explicit format selection, no-clobber publication, and empty-metadata equivalence.
- Adds focused JPEG/PNG round-trip, limit, collision, serializer-failure, contract, and crate-boundary coverage.

## Validation

- All focused issue commands pass.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked`

- `git diff --check`

## What changed

- Resolve the catalog path from `RUSTTABLE_CATALOG_PATH` or the platform-local RustTable data directory as `catalog.redb`.
- Boot iced into an explicit loading state and load one catalog asynchronously through `RedbImportRepository`.
- Project empty, ready, corrupt/unavailable, and presentation failures into safe library states.
- Add monotonic request IDs, stale/duplicate completion rejection, checked retry transitions, and keyboard/pointer-operable `Retry library`.
- Add deterministic real-store, reducer, focus, and 800×600 headless UI coverage.

## Why

The desktop entry point previously started from a static default library and never surfaced persisted import records. This connects the existing catalog persistence and presentation boundaries at startup while keeping failures recoverable and user-safe.

The initial focused red command was `cargo test -p rusttable-app --lib --locked`; it failed before implementation completion on the missing loader wiring, enriched-message ownership, and source/presentation issues. The final focused suite passes.

issues.body:
## Why

Linux main validation currently proves compilation but does not produce an installable-shaped Linux distribution subject. This adds a narrowly scoped, unsigned x86_64 GNU/Linux smoke archive with authoritative ELF/dependency identity, deterministic archive/checksum evidence, and a side-effect-free version probe.

## How

- Add dependency-free Bun identity parsing and focused rejection coverage for Rust host, ELF header, interpreter, NEEDED libraries, ldd resolution, and archive naming.
- Add a Linux-only merge workflow job that builds the release binary with the locked Cargo command, stages only `bin/RustTable` and `LICENSE`, validates staged and extracted payloads, builds two byte-identical GNU tar/gzip archives, and uploads evidence under `if: always()`.
- Add deterministic fake-tool orchestration coverage for exact build arguments, staging, archive/checksum promotion, extraction safety, failure cleanup, and validation-budget timeout behavior.
- Keep PR validation deterministic and fast: identity and fake smoke tests run in pre-commit and the PR cheap phase; real packaging runs only after merge to `main`.

## Validation

- `bun test scripts/linux-artifact-identity.test.ts` — pass.
- `bash scripts/test-linux-distribution-smoke.sh` — pass.
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` — 16s / 30s budget.
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh` — 2s / 60s budget.
- `bash scripts/pr-ci.sh` — 13s / 150s budget.
- `bash scripts/main-ci.sh`, source policy, native-removal policy, Bun workflow policy, actionlint, and `git diff --check` — pass.
- The real Linux packaging command is merge-only and was not runnable on this Darwin arm64 worker; the Linux job provides the hosted execution subject.

Closes #137

issues.body:
## Why

Closes #130. The existing #43 macOS smoke artifact used an architecture-ambiguous basename and log, so a runner-image architecture change could silently relabel the executable. This PR makes the unsigned artifact identity mechanically auditable before #78 consumes it.

## How

- Add dependency-free Bun identity parsing for stable `rustc -vV` host triples and exact single-slice `lipo -archs` output.
- Derive `RustTable-<version>-<rust-host-target>-unsigned.zip` and checksum names from validated Cargo metadata and Rust host identity.
- Extend the existing merge-only smoke path with v2 canonical evidence, staged/extracted architecture checks, and executable byte/SHA-256/size/mode identity checks.
- Add deterministic fixtures and wire the identity suite into pre-commit and PR cheap validation. Existing macOS workflow policy, native-removal enforcement, exact Bun pin, budgets, and #137 Linux packaging surface are preserved; no workflow file is changed.

## Validation

- `bun run test:macos-artifact-identity` (22 tests)
- `bun run test:computer-use`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` (13s / 30s)

The real release smoke remains merge-only per issue scope and was not run locally.

issues.body:
# RustTable master execution plan

RustTable is a source-anchored, feature-complete Rust migration of `darktable-org/darktable`, not a greenfield photo editor that merely resembles darktable. Every child issue maps one coherent upstream responsibility to one Rust pull request. Priority is controlled by labels and readiness by explicit GitHub dependencies; numeric title prefixes are prohibited.

## Canonical upstream baseline

- Repository: `darktable-org/darktable`.
- Accepted source commit: `cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.
- The generated commit `0eba994d638a4ae0c4e5fccd185600cf6f2118db` is stale and cannot satisfy migration evidence.
- #449/#494 own the executable/data reference identity; #495 owns operation semantic/layout evidence; #555 owns the complete source-to-issue map.
- #496 is the completed initial foundation-gate framework. #556 is the corrective current foundation acceptance that consumes #555 and the real #181 catalog-preview evidence and rejects the historical #496 receipt schema for current readiness.
- An upstream-baseline change is one reviewed change that regenerates capability, operation, source, fixture, issue, and foundation mappings and reports semantic drift by owner issue.

## Product target

RustTable will replace the darktable application with Rust-owned equivalents of:

- native catalog/library management and darktable catalog/XMP migration;
- raster and RAW discovery, import, metadata, thumbnails, decoding, processing, and export;
- deterministic, versioned pixelpipe execution with canonical CPU output and WGPU acceleration;
- scene/display color management, masks, blending, scopes, history, styles, and presets;
- lighttable, darkroom, import/export, map, tethering, print, slideshow, preferences, accessibility, localization, and input-device workflows;
- bounded Lua compatibility and WASM Component Model extensions;
- Linux x86-64, Apple Silicon macOS, and Windows x86-64 packages with complete CPU fallback.

## Shift-in-place migration contract

“Shift in place” means responsibility parity and traceability, not line-for-line transliteration or unsafe C-shaped Rust.

1. Every open implementation issue contains an `## Upstream darktable source anchors` section naming the canonical commit, exact paths, symbols/types/tables/config keys/kernels/actions, and source role.
2. Each issue states **Preserve**, **Redesign**, and **Excluded** behavior. Silence is not permission to omit behavior.
3. Each upstream responsibility has exactly one implementation owner or an explicit split by disjoint symbol/responsibility. No discovered responsibility may disappear between issues.
4. Rust crate boundaries may differ from the C directory layout, but the issue names the Rust files/crates that assume each upstream responsibility.
5. Completion evidence compares against the pinned source/executable or exact persisted/schema fixtures where numerical execution is not applicable.
6. Closed work is not accepted merely because it merged. Current source-map, receipt, schema, and behavioral evidence remain authoritative; uncovered responsibility creates a corrective issue.
7. `src/external/*` code is treated as an integration/reference surface. It is not copied wholesale into product crates unless a dedicated issue explicitly owns a clean Rust replacement.

### Required source coverage by issue class

- **Foundation/runtime:** identify the `src/main.c`, `src/common/darktable.c`, `src/control/*`, `src/gui/*`, and subsystem-init/shutdown responsibilities replaced.
- **Catalog/compatibility:** identify exact `src/common/database.*` schema/migration code, tables/columns, image/history/mask/style/preset/tag/collection/config readers and writers, and XMP paths.
- **Image I/O/metadata:** identify central imageio probing/dispatch plus each `src/imageio/format/*`, `src/imageio/storage/*`, EXIF/XMP/IPTC/profile, thumbnail, and RAW loader source.
- **Pixelpipe/color/masks/GPU:** identify `src/develop/*`, `src/common/iop_profile.*`, `src/common/colorspaces.*`, `src/common/opencl.*`, and every owned `data/kernels/*` program/kernel.
- **Image operations:** identify the exact `src/iop/<operation>.c|cc`, introspection version/type, `legacy_params`, flags/group/colorspace, parameter commit, ROI/tiling, CPU/OpenCL entry points, presets, GUI callbacks, shared helpers, and kernels.
- **Desktop UI:** identify corresponding `src/views/*`, `src/libs/*`, `src/bauhaus/*`, `src/dtgtk/*`, `src/gui/*`, action/accelerator, keyboard/pointer/tablet, and state transitions.
- **Camera/map/print/platform:** identify camera-control/gphoto, map/geolocation, print/CUPS, display-profile, credential, localization, and OS integration sources.
- **Scripting/extensions:** identify Lua registration/modules/events/widgets/storage APIs and the host responsibilities being preserved or deliberately redesigned.

#555 defines and verifies the machine-readable source map. Completed #518 provides the semantic issue-spec foundation that #555 extends; it does not depend on #555. Completed #496 provides the initial foundation-gate framework. #556 upgrades that gate to require current #555 source coverage and real #181 preview evidence.

## Fixed technical decisions

1. **Rust ownership:** Product behavior is implemented in Rust. The darktable C/C++ tree is reference evidence and is not linked or shipped.
2. **Compiler:** Use exact dated beta `beta-2026-07-17` until a dedicated baseline issue changes it. Beta-only stable-on-that-channel APIs are permitted when recorded and tested; nightly feature gates are not product dependencies.
3. **UI:** Iced owns application state, widgets, accessibility, events, and windows. No product WebView UI.
4. **GPU:** WGPU owns compute/presentation. Canonical CPU behavior remains available; GPU qualification or device loss never removes CPU/library functionality.
5. **Concurrency:** Tokio owns application and asynchronous I/O orchestration. Bounded Rayon pools own CPU image work. Domain crates remain runtime-agnostic.
6. **Persistence:** Native state uses versioned redb schemas and transactional migrations. darktable SQLite/XMP/config are read-only migration inputs. Rust memory layouts are never durable formats.
7. **RAW:** Use the pinned Rust RAW backend behind RustTable-owned contracts. There is no hidden LibRaw/RawSpeed product fallback.
8. **Color:** Every image boundary carries explicit profile/encoding identity. Any native ICC adapter is narrow and cannot leak handles or implicit global state.
9. **Image data:** Buffers carry sample type, channel layout, alpha, color, dimensions, stride, orientation, and alignment. Untyped bytes/pointers are not cross-crate image APIs.
10. **Safety:** Warnings and Clippy findings are errors. Unsafe Rust is forbidden by default and isolated only in approved SIMD/native/platform adapters with documented invariants.
11. **Errors/privacy:** Public failures use stable typed codes and bounded diagnostics. Receipts do not expose private absolute paths, secrets, personal metadata values, or unbounded native output.
12. **Compatibility:** Unknown operations, parameter versions, masks, metadata, extensions, and records are preserved opaquely and reported. They are never guessed, dropped, or silently defaulted.
13. **Determinism:** Canonical CPU output defines expected behavior. GPU, tiled, cached, SIMD, and parallel paths use the current comparison schema and real fixtures.
14. **One issue, one PR:** One PR closes exactly one issue. Adjacent work is not absorbed for convenience.
15. **Documentation:** Document public contracts, migrations, safety invariants, non-obvious algorithms, and changed user behavior in the implementing PR. Standalone planning and licensing-only backlog is excluded.

## Priority semantics

Every open child has exactly one priority label.

- **`priority: P0`** — current usable-product delivery or an active correctness/data/source-mapping blocker on that delivery.
- **`priority: P1`** — next native engine lane: color, operations, pixelpipe, CPU, WGPU.
- **`priority: P2`** — required broad parity: compatibility, RAW depth, catalog, remaining operations, masks, scopes, color, workflows.
- **`priority: P3`** — integrations and product completion consuming established services: advanced export, credentials, AI, external editors, broad UI, benchmarks.
- **`priority: P4`** — deferred policy, release, optional-platform work, validation optimization, documentation consolidation, and polish.

Priority changes only by label. Milestones classify subsystem/delivery domain, not urgency.

## Readiness and selection

An issue is ready only when:

1. every `Depends on #…` dependency is closed completed or has an explicit completed replacement;
2. it has one priority label and one milestone;
3. completed #518 reports a semantically complete body for the exact current body hash;
4. #555 reports valid canonical source anchors for the exact current body hash;
5. no other open PR already claims it;
6. any foundation-gate dependency required by its class has a current passing #556-schema merge receipt.

Select the ready issue in the highest priority; within a priority use lowest issue number unless dependencies leave a different sole ready issue. CPU/reference behavior lands before SIMD/WGPU optimization. UI issues consume established services and cannot move processing/persistence logic into widgets.

## Current P0 usable-raster lane

The current objective is a usable raster photo workflow while source-map corrections are applied:

1. #177 — supported platform and GPU capability registry. Completed; source-map audit annotation recorded.
2. #178 — structured product diagnostics.
3. #179 — typed configuration and atomic migration.
4. #180 — application lifecycle/service registry.
5. #181 — persisted catalog-to-preview vertical slice.
6. #222 — typed image/decode contracts.
7. #223 — immutable source snapshots.
8. #224 — static signature-first decoder registry.
9. #535 — real catalog photos/previews in Iced.
10. #256 — raster import from Iced.
11. #536 — persisted exposure/RGB-gain editing.
12. #542 — save selected persisted edit as PNG.

## Current P0 source/foundation correction lane

These correctness issues may proceed alongside dependency-ready usable-raster work without pretending the historical closed gate is current evidence:

1. #555 — canonical darktable source inventory/map, exact issue source blocks, source drift, and readiness integration.
2. #556 — after #181 and #555, upgrade the foundation receipt to require current source-map and real catalog-preview evidence and reject the historical #496 schema.

#555 blocks broad P2/P3 acceptance, but does not invalidate continuing a P0 item after its own body has been directly source-anchored. #556 blocks any policy that claims current foundation acceptance from the old #496 receipt.

## Current P1 native-engine lane

After ready P0 delivery:

1. #257 color identities/transform contracts.
2. #263 operation descriptor and parameter schema.
3. #264 instances, workflow stages, and ordering.
4. #265 deterministic first-party operation registry.
5. #266 immutable pixelpipe snapshots.
6. #267 forward/reverse ROI propagation.
7. #268 tiling requirements and memory estimates.
8. #269 aligned host-buffer pools/leases.
9. #270 bounded cache and complete keys.
10. #271 pipeline mode policies.
11. #272 generation cancellation/publication gates.
12. #273 bounded CPU scheduler.
13. #274 failure/retry/CPU-fallback policy.
14. #539 production CPU pixelpipe for existing basic operations.
15. #290 WGPU device/capability service.
16. #292 WGSL source registry/reflection and basic point shaders.
17. #293 GPU resource pools/accounting.
18. #294 host↔GPU transfers.
19. #295 checked compute dispatch.
20. #296 asynchronous submission/resource retirement.
21. #297 full/tiled GPU execution and OOM recovery.
22. #301 production basic WGPU pixelpipe with CPU parity/fallback.

## Repository structure

```text
crates/
  rusttable-app/                # composition root, lifecycle, Iced application
  rusttable-ui/                 # design system, widgets, accessibility, view models
  rusttable-core/               # stable IDs, typed values, limits, shared errors/events
  rusttable-catalog/            # photos, edits, collections, metadata ports
  rusttable-catalog-store/      # redb schemas, transactions, migrations, recovery
  rusttable-compat/             # darktable DB/XMP/parameter inspection/conversion
  rusttable-metadata/           # EXIF/IPTC/XMP domain
  rusttable-import/             # discovery, source registration, duplicate handling
  rusttable-image/              # typed/aligned buffers, pixels, ROIs
  rusttable-image-io/           # probes, decoders, encoders, thumbnails
  rusttable-color/              # profile/color identities and transforms
  rusttable-processing/         # descriptors, registry, canonical CPU operations
  rusttable-pixelpipe/          # snapshots, ROI, tiling, cache, scheduling, execution
  rusttable-masks/              # mask and blend domain/execution
  rusttable-simd/               # isolated architecture acceleration
  rusttable-gpu/                # WGPU devices, resources, shaders, compute
  rusttable-render/             # preview/full/export orchestration/presentation
  rusttable-export/             # requests, recipes, queue, encoders, destinations
  rusttable-camera/             # discovery, tethering, capture, live view
  rusttable-scripting/          # Lua compatibility host/APIs
  rusttable-plugin-api/         # WASM Component Model protocol
  rusttable-diagnostics/        # diagnostics, health, support evidence
  rusttable-testkit/            # fixtures/reference/differential helpers
  rusttable-*-native/           # approved narrow native/platform adapters only
  xtask/                        # repository generation/validation/evidence
shaders/                        # reviewed WGSL and reflection manifests
fixtures/                       # registered valid/malformed inputs/results
architecture/                   # capabilities, operations, source map, DAG/platform data
quality/                        # compiler/dependency/validation/file/evidence contracts
```

Dependency direction is mechanically enforced. Lower domain crates cannot depend on Iced, Tokio, WGPU, redb, SQLite, scripting runtimes, native adapters, tooling, or the composition root unless the assigned role explicitly permits it.

## Required child-issue format

Every child implementation issue contains:

1. **Outcome** — concrete capability delivered.
2. **Fixed decisions** — selected crates/APIs/algorithms/formats/defaults/limits/fallback.
3. **Upstream darktable source anchors** — canonical commit, exact path/symbol/data/kernel/action ownership, Preserve/Redesign/Excluded behavior, Rust target owners, and oracle fixtures.
4. **Implementation** — exact components and integration points.
5. **Failure and edge behavior** — invalid input, exhaustion, cancellation, rollback, unsupported variants, privacy.
6. **Test matrix** — positive, negative, boundary, concurrency/restart/platform/parity/UI cases as applicable.
7. **Acceptance evidence** — executable commands, receipts, artifacts, tolerances, or end-to-end behavior.
8. **Dependencies** — direct GitHub issue references or `None`.
9. **One-PR boundary** — explicit exclusions.

Issues that only say “investigate,” “decide later,” “implement support,” “add tests,” cite only a directory/capability ID, or repeat generic source sections are not ready.

## Definition of done

- Fixed decisions are implemented without reopening architecture in the PR.
- Every named upstream responsibility is implemented, deliberately redesigned with evidence, or explicitly excluded by another owner.
- Public APIs, durable formats, safety invariants, and user behavior are documented with code.
- Errors and partial results are typed, bounded, cancellation-safe, privacy-safe, and transactionally consistent.
- Relevant unit/property/corpus/differential/UI/accessibility/shader/backend/restart evidence is included.
- `cargo fmt`, strict Clippy, targeted tests, architecture/file/dependency/source-map checks, and applicable workflows pass.
- Placeholder data, synthetic compatibility claims, ignored contract fields, inherited native implementation, web UI, global mutable state, unstable Rust ABI plug-ins, unbounded allocation, silent compatibility loss, unexplained upstream omissions, and unrelated refactors are prohibited.
- The PR closes only its issue and leaves dependent work independently implementable.

## Existing issue handling

- Revise unstarted issues in place when decisions, source anchors, dependencies, priority, or scope are wrong.
- If completed work is materially incomplete, add a post-completion audit and create one focused corrective issue. #518→#555 and #496→#556 are the current examples.
- Close weak duplicates as duplicate/not planned and point to the canonical owner.
- Split an issue when its upstream file/symbol set cannot coherently fit one PR.
- Consolidate overlapping infrastructure instead of implementing competing models.
- Licensing/compliance-only work is outside this plan.

## Index maintenance

GitHub issue bodies, dependencies, milestones, states, replacement links, priorities, `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml`, validated generated manifests, and the current #556-schema foundation receipt are the executable index. There is no numeric work order and no unvalidated title-derived source mapping.
issues.title: Master execution plan: feature-complete Rust rewrite of darktable

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Create the final top-level Rust package boundaries now, before more implementation accumulates in the current broad crates. This PR adds compileable crate skeletons and a machine-readable ownership map; it does not move existing production code.

## Fixed decisions

Add these workspace members: `rusttable-ui`, `rusttable-compat`, `rusttable-color`, `rusttable-pixelpipe`, `rusttable-masks`, `rusttable-gpu`, `rusttable-export`, `rusttable-camera`, `rusttable-scripting`, `rusttable-plugin-api`, `rusttable-simd`, `rusttable-testkit`, and root `xtask`.

The dependency direction is fixed:

`core` → no internal crates; `image`/`metadata` → `core`; `catalog` → `core,image,metadata`; `catalog-store` → `catalog,core`; `image-io` → `core,image,metadata`; `import` → `catalog,image-io,metadata`; `color` → `core,image`; `processing` → `core,image,color`; `masks` → `core,image`; `pixelpipe` → `core,image,color,processing,masks`; `gpu` → `core,image,processing,pixelpipe`; `render` → `catalog,color,pixelpipe,gpu`; `export` → `catalog,render,image-io,metadata`; `ui` → `catalog,render,export`; `camera` → `image-io,metadata,import`; `scripting` → `catalog,render,export`; `plugin-api` → `core,image,processing`; `app` is the only composition root allowed to depend on every product subsystem. `testkit` and `xtask` are tooling-only.

## Required implementation

- Add each crate with package metadata, workspace lints, a minimal public module, and a compile-time purpose statement.
- Add `architecture/workspace-dag.toml` containing every package, allowed internal dependency, integration owner, and tooling-only edge.
- Add a deterministic parser test that rejects duplicate packages, unknown edges, cycles, and product crates depending on `app`, `ui`, `testkit`, or `xtask` contrary to the map.
- Keep all new crates free of placeholder dependencies and production behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo metadata --locked` contains exactly the declared workspace members.
- [ ] `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passes with no new warnings.
- [ ] The map is complete, deterministic, and tested against invalid fixtures.
- [ ] No existing module is moved and no public behavior changes.

## One-PR boundary

Only crate skeletons, the ownership map, and its parser tests belong here. Dependency enforcement against real Cargo metadata is [0013].

## Dependencies

Depends on #446.
issues.title: Create the target workspace crate skeleton and dependency ownership map

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Create an executable inventory proving that every user-visible darktable subsystem is represented by a RustTable issue. The inventory must be generated from the pinned reference tree plus a small reviewed override file; it must not be a hand-maintained prose checklist.

## Fixed decisions

- Store the normalized inventory at `architecture/darktable-capabilities.toml`.
- Add `xtask parity scan-darktable --source <path>` as the only generator once [0011] exists; until then expose the same implementation through a library entry point with a thin temporary binary.
- Classify each capability as `required`, `redesigned`, or `unsupported`. `deferred` and `unmapped` are invalid for the final manifest.
- Required 1.0 domains are: catalog/library, darkroom, every registered image operation, masks/blending, color management, RAW/raster I/O, metadata, import, export/storage, camera/tethering, map/geotagging, printing, scripting, input devices, accessibility, localization, and packaging.

## Required implementation

- Parse the reference `src/iop`, `src/libs`, `src/views`, `src/imageio/format`, `src/imageio/storage`, Lua API registration, build options, and OpenCL program registry.
- Record stable capability ID, reference path/symbol, category, status, replacement issue sequence, test evidence required, and optional redesign note.
- Add a narrow `architecture/capability-overrides.toml` for capabilities that cannot be discovered mechanically; every override requires a source path and reason.
- Fail on duplicate IDs, missing source paths, unknown statuses, stale issue sequences, unregistered discovered modules, or an override that masks a discoverable entry.
- Produce a deterministic summary grouped by phase and milestone.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Two scans of the same reference commit are byte-identical.
- [ ] Fixtures prove discovery of IOP, view, library, format, storage, Lua, and build-option entries.
- [ ] Removing or adding a reference module changes the generated inventory and fails validation until mapped.
- [ ] Every inventory row points to one or more numbered issues under #158.

## Dependencies

Depends on [0003] for target crate ownership. The reference source remains external and read-only.

## One-PR boundary

Only scanner, manifest, overrides, validation, and tests. Do not implement any product capability.
issues.title: Generate the darktable feature-parity inventory from the reference tree

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Generate the authoritative compatibility manifest for every darktable image operation and every persisted version that RustTable must read. This manifest drives decoders, operation descriptors, migration tests, UI schemas, and final parity accounting.

## Fixed decisions

- Store the reviewed output at `architecture/darktable-operations.toml`.
- Derive operation registration/order from the reference build files and module API; derive parameter structures and versions from module source/introspection declarations; use explicit overrides only where C macros prevent reliable extraction.
- Preserve darktable operation names as immutable compatibility identifiers. RustTable display names and internal Rust types may differ.
- Unknown parameter versions are retained as opaque bytes and block execution; they are never guessed, truncated, or silently defaulted.

## Required implementation

For each operation record:

- operation name, module version, parameter byte size/layout hash, default-enabled state, default order, group/tags, multi-instance support, blend/mask support, input/output color spaces, ROI behavior, tiling requirements, CPU implementation, legacy OpenCL program/kernel mapping, parameter migration edges, preset sources, and owning numbered issue;
- history/XMP/database field names and rules for enabled state, instance name/priority, blend version, and operation ordering;
- deterministic fixture IDs for every persisted version and migration path.

Add generator fixtures covering plain C parameters, C++ modules, introspection macros, manually versioned modules, no-parameter modules, deprecated modules, shared OpenCL programs, and operations with custom legacy migration. Validation must fail on missing versions, disconnected migration graphs, duplicate operation IDs, unknown kernel references, or an issue mapping outside the operation range.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every operation discovered by [0004] has exactly one manifest entry.
- [ ] Every known persisted version has a byte-layout identity and either an executable decoder/migration target or explicit opaque-blocking status.
- [ ] Generation is deterministic and changes when reference declarations change.
- [ ] The manifest can be deserialized without loading product crates or executing reference code.

## Dependencies

Depends on [0004].

## One-PR boundary

Only extraction, manifest schema, overrides, validation, and tests. No operation implementation or data migration belongs here.
issues.title: Generate the operation, parameter-version, and history compatibility manifest

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Make every test fixture reproducible, bounded, privacy-safe, and mechanically accounted for. This issue deliberately excludes licensing policy and attribution work.

## Fixed decisions

- Store small committed fixtures under `fixtures/`; store large corpora outside normal clones and address them by SHA-256 through `fixtures/external.toml`.
- Track fixture identity, byte length, media type, compression, privacy classification, expected consumers, and checksum. Do not track or enforce license fields.
- Synthetic fixtures are preferred for malformed inputs, metadata edge cases, masks, ICC profiles, catalogs, and parameter blobs.
- Real camera files must be scrubbed of GPS, serial numbers, owner names, free-text comments, and unrelated thumbnails before registration unless the test explicitly validates one of those fields.

## Required implementation

- Add `rusttable-testkit::fixtures` with typed manifest parsing, canonical path resolution, SHA-256 verification, size limits, and duplicate-content detection.
- Add `xtask fixtures verify`, `xtask fixtures list`, and `xtask fixtures scrub-report` entry points after [0011]; expose the library API immediately.
- Define `fixtures/manifest.toml` with stable IDs and relative paths; reject unregistered files beneath governed fixture roots.
- Add a deterministic metadata privacy scanner for EXIF, XMP, IPTC, JPEG comments, PNG text chunks, TIFF strings, and filename/path leaks. The scanner reports exact fields and never rewrites files automatically.
- Verify decompression-bomb limits before parsing expected outputs or archives.
- Add invalid-manifest, checksum-drift, path-traversal, oversized-file, duplicate-content, hidden-file, and privacy-leak fixtures.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every governed fixture is registered and checksum-valid.
- [ ] Large fixture references can be verified from an already-populated local cache without network access.
- [ ] Privacy leaks fail validation with the fixture ID and field path, never raw sensitive values.
- [ ] The testkit API is deterministic on Linux, macOS, and Windows paths.
- [ ] No licensing or attribution gate is introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on [0003].

## One-PR boundary

Only fixture schema, integrity/privacy tooling, tests, and initial registration of existing RustTable fixtures. The representative corpus itself is [0009].
issues.title: Create deterministic fixture manifests, privacy scrubbing, and integrity checks

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Provide one test-only process boundary that runs a pinned external darktable executable as a behavioral oracle. Product crates must never link, load, or invoke reference code.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement the runner in `rusttable-testkit::reference`; only `rusttable-testkit`, `xtask`, integration tests, and merge-only jobs may depend on it.
- Pin the accepted reference identity by exact semantic version plus Git commit SHA in `fixtures/reference/darktable.toml`.
- Invoke `darktable-cli` as a child process with a completely isolated temporary HOME, XDG config/cache/data, OpenCL cache, locale, timezone, and output directory.
- Default to CPU reference runs (`--disable-opencl`) for canonical parity. GPU reference runs are an explicit test mode and never define the golden output.

## Required implementation

- Add capability probing that validates executable path, version/commit identity, supported CLI flags, database independence, and required data directory.
- Define typed `ReferenceRequest` and `ReferenceReceipt` values containing source fixture ID, optional XMP/config, output format/profile, dimensions, timeout, exit status, stdout/stderr hashes, output hash, and reference identity.
- Enforce process-tree termination, wall-clock timeout, stdout/stderr byte limits, output-size limit, and temporary-directory cleanup on success, error, panic, or cancellation.
- Normalize only nondeterministic log fields explicitly listed in a versioned rule set; retain raw logs as merge artifacts.
- Add fake-executable fixtures for success, timeout, child-spawn, malformed version, excessive output, missing image, nonzero exit, partial output, and ignored cancellation.
- Add an architecture test that rejects `std::process` reference invocation outside the approved testkit module.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A known fixture renders twice with identical receipt fields and output hash.
- [ ] Wrong reference versions fail before processing.
- [ ] Timeout/cancellation kills descendants and removes temporary state.
- [ ] Product binaries and libraries contain no reference-runner dependency.
- [ ] Tests do not require the reference executable unless explicitly marked and skipped with a structured reason.

## Dependencies

Depends on [0006].

## One-PR boundary

Only the process runner, receipt schema, isolation, fake fixtures, and boundary enforcement. No image comparison or corpus expansion.
issues.title: Build the isolated darktable reference CLI runner for differential tests

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement the single comparison engine used for RustTable-versus-darktable, CPU-versus-WGPU, full-frame-versus-tiled, and serialization round-trip image validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-testkit::image_diff`; do not use external image-diff commands.
- Compare decoded linear-light `f32` RGBA buffers after explicit conversion into the comparison profile. Dimensions, channel count, alpha mode, and non-finite locations are always exact checks.
