- Every supported schema boundary and randomized physical row order.
- Source-map mutation changing tags/assignments/color-label/image flag/group schema or helper semantics without owner.
- Architecture tests for no target catalog command/persistence/UI dependency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-compat -p rusttable-sqlite-native darktable_organization` passes tags/flags/labels/groups/graphs/source suites.
- `cargo xtask compatibility decode-organization --all-schema-fixtures --graph-failures --verify-raw-values` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 189` proves every listed darktable organization responsibility is accounted for.
- Every organization row is represented or keyed blocking; literal names/raw bits/unknown integers round-trip; validation is bounded; no target commands are introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on #183, #184, #450, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements tag/rating/reject/color-label/group compatibility records, explicit queries, graph validation, source accounting, findings, fixtures, and reports. Do not implement target tag/group/rating commands, persistence, collection filters, or UI.
issues.title: Decode darktable tags, ratings, color labels, and image groups

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Decode current, recent, and saved darktable collection/filter rules into a bounded evaluation-neutral compatibility AST that can be inspected and migrated without executing source SQL, extension predicates, regex, Lua, or plug-in code. Preserve original serialized bytes/text, order, conjunction mode, sort/group hints, and unresolved entity references.

## Fixed decisions

- Supported AST nodes: boolean group/negation; equality/inequality; numeric/date range; contains/prefix/suffix/glob; membership; missing/present; rating/reject; color label; tag hierarchy; folder/film; camera/lens; image type; geolocation presence/range; history/edit state; sort keys/directions; grouping hints that are explicitly evidenced.
- Source SQL fragments, regex/custom operators, plug-in/Lua predicates, and unrecognized properties remain exact opaque predicates and block executable parity for that collection. They are never passed to SQLite or another evaluator.
- Preserve source token/order/conjunction/negation/property/operator/literal bytes and escaping before normalization.
- Date/time values retain literal text, parsed components, and source local/naive interpretation. Target timezone semantics are later.
- Resolve source IDs to compatibility records without replacing IDs with display labels in AST identity.
- Parser limits: tokens, nesting, children, literal bytes, pattern complexity, numeric magnitude, entity references, findings, and total source bytes.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/collection.h` and `src/common/collection.c`: collection property/filter enums, serialized rule representation, rule parsing/serialization, SQL query construction, conjunction/negation, sort/order/grouping, current/recent collection state, update/signals, and supported property semantics.
- `src/libs/collect.c`: saved/current rule UI, rule rows, property/operator/value serialization, add/remove/clear, tag/folder/camera/date/rating/label/geolocation property controls, and collection update behavior.
- `src/libs/filters.c` and `src/libs/tools/filter.c` or current filter modules registered in the pinned tree: quick filters, text/rating/color/group/image-type/history constraints, serialization, and sort controls. Exact present paths must be enumerated by #555 rather than assumed from title.
- `src/libs/filtering.c`/filter helper sources if present at the pinned commit: filter proxy/state and current collection integration.
- `src/common/database.c`: data/library tables holding collection presets/recent collections/config-derived rule strings where applicable; exact table/config ownership is recorded per schema.
- `src/control/conf.h/.c` and `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`: configuration keys storing current/recent collection rules, sorting/grouping, and UI filter state; #191 owns selected configuration conversion.
- `src/common/tags.h/.c`, `src/common/film.h/.c`, `src/common/image.h/.c`, `src/common/metadata.h/.c`, and collection property SQL helpers: entity/property values referenced by rules.
- `src/common/selection.h/.c`, `src/views/lighttable.c`, and `src/libs/filmstrip.c`: collection result refresh/selection/grouping consumers; execution/UI remain later.
- Lua collection modules under `src/lua/*`: script-facing collection rule/query API and custom predicates; bounded Lua compatibility remains separate and opaque here.

**Preserve:** every source collection/saved-search rule and original representation, exact node/order/conjunction/negation/property/operator/literal/escaping, supported property semantics, sort/group hints, recent/current state evidence, unresolved IDs, and unknown custom/SQL predicates.

**Redesign:** replace direct rule-string-to-SQL construction, GTK row state, config/global proxies, translated display labels, and evaluator-coupled parsing with immutable compatibility AST, explicit source spans/raw literals, closed typed properties/operators, bounded parser, entity refs, canonical normalized projection, and opaque unsupported nodes.

**Excluded:** executing the AST, generating RustTable/redb queries, collection persistence/UI, timezone policy, filesystem discovery, and Lua/custom predicate execution. Later issues own them.

Rust ownership: compatibility AST/parser in `rusttable-compat`; source query/config adapters supply serialized rules; final collection domain/evaluator/UI later.

## Implementation

- Define rule source/version, node/property/operator/literal/span/entity ref, boolean/conjunction/negation, sort/group, opaque predicate, findings, limits, canonical encoding, and normalized display projection.
- Enumerate every supported darktable collection serialization/schema/config version from pinned source and #450 fixtures.
- Implement parser with explicit tokenizer/escaping/version grammar and no recursion beyond bounded iterative stack.
- Resolve tag/film/folder/camera/lens/label/group IDs against #184/#189 while retaining source IDs and unresolved status.
- Validate malformed stacks/parentheses, missing operands, invalid ranges/dates/numbers, unknown properties/operators, ambiguous escaping, duplicate rules, absent entities, excessive nesting/size/pattern complexity.
- Preserve original source bytes/text externally by hash/length; canonical AST does not overwrite it.
- Add source-accounting matrix for every property/operator/serialization/config/table/UI/Lua source and later evaluator owner.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Unsupported rule version/property/operator/SQL/regex/plugin syntax becomes opaque with exact source span and blocking execution status, not parse loss.
- Malformed syntax yields bounded findings and retains original source; no partial AST marked executable.
- Entity-resolution failure preserves source ID/literal and never substitutes a similar display label.
- Adversarial depth/size/patterns fail before stack/memory exhaustion and emit retained/omitted counts.
- Equal source representation/entity snapshot yields byte-identical AST independent of map/query/localization order.

## Test matrix

- Nested boolean/negation/conjunction/order and every supported property/operator/literal/sort/group hint.
- Tag hierarchy/folder/film/camera/lens/rating/reject/labels/type/GPS/history/date/numeric/string/glob/missing/present.
- Unicode, escaping, wildcard literals, timezone-free dates, min/max/nonfinite/overflow numeric values.
- Current/recent/saved representations across versions/config sources.
- Malformed stacks/operands/ranges/escaping, unknown properties/operators, SQL/plugin/Lua/regex opaque nodes, deep/oversized adversarial input.
- Missing/duplicate entity refs and randomized entity/map ordering.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing collection property/operator/serialization/config/UI path without owner.
- Architecture tests proving no SQL/evaluator/UI dependency and no source expression execution.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-compat collection_compat` passes grammar/property/entity/opaque/bounds/source suites.
- `cargo xtask compatibility decode-collections --all-serializations --all-properties --adversarial --verify-no-execution` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 190` proves every listed darktable collection-rule responsibility is accounted for.
- Supported rules parse deterministically and preserve original bytes; unknown predicates remain opaque/non-executable; no source rule/fragment is executed.

## Dependencies

Depends on #183, #184, #189, #191, #450, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements collection compatibility models/parser, entity resolution, canonical/normalized projections, source accounting, findings, fixtures, and tests. Do not implement target collection evaluation/storage/UI, timezone policy, or custom predicate execution.
issues.title: Decode darktable collections and saved search rules into a safe AST

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Decode the bounded subset of darktable configuration that materially affects migrated workflows: export/print defaults, module groups/visibility/order preferences, keyboard/MIDI/device mappings, display/proof/export profile choices, OpenCL/resource preferences, Lua enablement, and map/camera toggles. Preserve unknown nonsecret keys opaquely while excluding credentials and machine-local state. Never write or apply RustTable configuration.

## Fixed decisions

- Darktable configuration is compatibility input only; RustTable runtime configuration remains #179.
- `architecture/darktable-config.toml` is the reviewed semantic key map. Each entry records exact source key/pattern, type/encoding/default/version, upstream source/caller, target issue/capability, migration class, privacy, range, and disposition.
- Unmapped nonsecret keys are retained as opaque line/key/value records with source spans and counts; they are never copied automatically into RustTable config.
- Credentials, passwords/tokens, credential-store blobs, recent/open file lists, window geometry, cache/temp paths, printer queue IDs, device serials, and other machine-local/private values are excluded/redacted before snapshot/report creation.
- Parse `darktablerc` locale-independently with exact key/value escaping and duplicate-line order. Do not use current process locale or execute values.
- Normalize shortcut/device mappings into compatibility actions/inputs but do not bind/apply them until #512/#513.
- Preserve unsupported profile IDs/paths and operation/module-group action refs as opaque source records with findings; never silently default to sRGB/current action.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/control/conf.h` and `src/control/conf.c`: darktablerc initialization/read/write, key/value storage, typed get/set/default/delete/list behavior, escaping, duplicate/update semantics, config directory/file lifecycle, and caller contract.
- `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`: complete preference key/type/default/min/max/enum/category/restart/user description inventory. This issue semantically maps only reviewed migration keys and classifies all others.
- `tools/generate_prefs.xsl` and generated preferences/config code: source key metadata/default/range generation and GUI binding evidence.
- `src/gui/preferences.c`, `src/gui/gtk.c`, and preference pages/modules: user-visible preference grouping/application/restart behavior; target UI remains later.
- `src/libs/export.c`, `src/imageio/imageio.c`, `src/imageio/format/*`, and `src/imageio/storage/*`: export format/storage/profile/intent/size/metadata defaults and last-used values. Actual recipes/destinations remain #396–#418.
- `src/libs/print_settings.c`/`src/libs/print.c` and `src/common/print.c/.h` where present at the pinned commit: print dimensions/margins/profile/intent/printer/page settings. Exact paths are enumerated by #555; print service remains #467.
- `src/libs/modulegroups.c`, `src/libs/ioporder.c`, `src/common/iop_group.c/.h`, and `src/common/iop_order.c/.h`: module group definitions/visibility/order/workflow preferences.
- `src/gui/accelerators.c/.h`, action definitions/registrations across views/libs/iops, and shortcut export/import code: keyboard/mouse/action mappings.
- `src/libs/tools/midi.c`, `cmake/modules/FindPortMidi.cmake`, and MIDI config/action callbacks: MIDI mappings/settings. RustTable replaces PortMidi with #512.
- Gamepad/tablet/input paths in GTK/GDK/action code and any SDL2-conditioned source/build options: device mappings/settings owned by #512/#513.
- `src/common/colorspaces.c/.h`, `src/common/iop_profile.c/.h`, display-profile callbacks, proofing/gamut UI, and export/print profile settings: profile IDs/paths/intents/BPC choices. Parsing/execution remains color issues.
- `src/common/opencl.c/.h`, `src/common/resource_limits.c/.h`, and OpenCL/resource keys in `darktableconfig.xml.in`: GPU enable/device priorities/memory/async/pinned settings and host memory/thread policies mapped to #177/#179/#290 or explicitly excluded.
- `src/lua/init.c/.h`, `src/lua/lua.c`, `src/lua/preferences.c`, and Lua-related config keys: enablement/script paths/preferences; bounded Lua host remains #422 and private paths are not migrated.
- `src/common/camera_control.c/.h`, `src/views/map.c`, `src/libs/location.c`, map/camera modules and config keys: feature toggles/defaults later owned by camera/map issues.
- `src/common/file_location.c/.h`: config/data/cache/search paths used to locate darktablerc and classify machine-local paths.

**Preserve:** mapped key values/types/default-version evidence, duplicate/order/source lines, workflow/export/print/module/input/profile/resource/Lua/map/camera intent, unknown nonsecret keys, unsupported refs, and exact reason each key is converted/preserved/excluded.

**Redesign:** replace global `dt_conf_*`, machine-local path/string keys, generated GTK preferences, action pointer bindings, OpenCL-specific tuning, and implicit defaults with a read-only locale-independent parser, reviewed semantic key registry, typed compatibility records, normalized action/device/profile refs, privacy classification, and explicit later owner.

**Excluded:** credentials/secrets, recent paths/window/cache/device machine state, applying settings, writing RustTable TOML, executing profiles/export/print/input/Lua/map/camera behavior, and preferences UI.

Rust ownership: config compatibility parser/map in `rusttable-compat`; #179 remains runtime config owner.

## Implementation

- Define source file/line/key/value/raw text, mapped typed values, normalized action/input/profile/module/export/print/resource refs, opaque/excluded records, migration class, privacy, findings, limits, canonical receipts.
- Implement exact bounded darktablerc parser: binary/UTF-8 handling, line endings/comments, first separator/key/value escaping, duplicates, booleans/integers/floats/lists/path bytes, locale-independent numbers, source spans.
- Generate/review `architecture/darktable-config.toml` from `darktableconfig.xml.in` plus actual pinned call sites, then require explicit disposition for every discovered key/pattern.
- Map export/storage/print/module groups/visibility/order/shortcuts/MIDI/input/profiles/OpenCL/resources/Lua/map/camera values into typed compatibility records.
- Detect/redact secrets and machine-local values before durable model; preserve only key/source/disposition/hash where needed.
- Validate enums/ranges/refs/duplicates/conflicts/oversize/malformed values against source metadata and decoded presets/actions/profiles.
- Add source-accounting report joining each mapped/excluded key to source declaration, callers, and target issue.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Malformed/oversized file/line/key/value/escape, duplicate conflicting keys, locale decimal traps, unknown enum/range, missing referenced preset/action/profile/module, unsupported version, private/secret classification failure, and cancellation produce stable findings.
- Unknown nonsecret keys remain opaque with original key/value bytes externally; secrets never enter model/report/debug output.
- Duplicate keys retain every occurrence/order and a separate effective-source interpretation only when darktable semantics are proven.
- Unsupported profile/path/action remains unresolved; never substitute current machine resources.
- Equal source bytes/map version yield byte-identical output independent of locale/platform.

## Test matrix

- Supported darktable versions/config defaults; LF/CRLF; comments/escaping/Unicode/invalid UTF-8; duplicates; locale comma/dot traps; bool/int/float/list/path.
- Every mapped workflow/export/print/module/input/profile/resource/Lua/map/camera category and missing/invalid refs.
- Credentials/tokens/private paths/window/cache/recent/device values leak tests.
- Old module groups/shortcut/MIDI formats; unknown keys/enums/values; malformed/oversized files.
- Randomized map insertion/locales/canonical output.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing config key/caller/default/range without disposition owner.
- Architecture tests for no #179 write/application and no current locale use.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-compat darktable_config` passes parser/mapping/privacy/reference/source suites.
- `cargo xtask compatibility decode-config --all-fixtures --all-mapped-keys --locale-matrix --verify-secrets-absent` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 191` proves every reviewed darktable config responsibility/key has a source and target disposition.
- Every mapped key yields typed value/finding; unknown nonsecret keys round-trip opaquely; secrets are absent; no RustTable config is written/applied.

## Dependencies

Depends on #178, #179, #182, #188, #190, #492, #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements darktablerc parser, reviewed key map, typed compatibility records, privacy/exclusions, reference validation, source accounting, findings, fixtures, and tests. Do not apply/write RustTable config, implement preferences UI, credentials, export/print/input/color/Lua/map/camera behavior.
issues.title: Decode darktable workflow, export, print, color, and input configuration

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Compose the read-only darktable database/configuration/compatibility decoders into one cancellation-aware scanner that consumes one coherent library/data snapshot plus optional darktablerc/XMP/source-location inputs and returns a complete owned `CompatibilitySnapshot` and bounded report without creating RustTable state or mutating any source.

## Fixed decisions

- One #183 source snapshot/transaction feeds all database sections. No decoder reopens or re-queries the live source after materialization.
- Scan order is reference/schema → images/sources → histories/order → blend → masks → styles/presets → organization → collections → configuration → cross-section validation. Final canonical ordering is independent of worker completion.
- Concurrent decoding is allowed only over already-owned rows/blobs from the same snapshot, with #179/#273 bounded workers/memory; no SQLite connection/statement crosses workers.
- Optional XMP/source/config inputs are independently fingerprinted and joined by explicit image/source keys. Missing optional input is reported, not guessed.
- Every visited row/blob/config line/XMP record has exactly one disposition from #182. A complete snapshot requires zero unaccounted records and no truncation.
- Source mutation/fingerprint drift/cancellation/fatal decoder failure prevents `Complete`; partial diagnostics may be returned but cannot feed migration commit.
- CLI exit codes remain 0 compatible, 2 warnings, 3 blocking, 4 scanner/infrastructure failure.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

This scanner owns composition/accounting across the upstream read surfaces already mapped by #183–#191:

- `src/common/database.c/.h`: coherent library/data open/attach/version/schema/snapshot/transaction and all source tables.
- `src/common/image.c/.h`, `src/common/film.c/.h`, `src/common/history.c/.h`, `src/common/iop_order.c/.h`, `src/common/styles.c/.h`, `src/common/tags.c/.h`, `src/common/collection.c/.h`, `src/common/metadata.c/.h`: cross-table record and relationship ownership.
- `src/develop/blend.c/.h`, `src/develop/masks.h`, `src/develop/masks/*`, `src/develop/imageop.c/.h`: history→blend→mask→instance linkage.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: optional XMP sidecar/history/metadata/style/tag/mask records and sidecar-source association.
- `src/control/conf.c/.h`, `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`, `src/common/file_location.c/.h`: optional darktablerc/key-map and source-location discovery.
- `src/control/crawler.c`, `src/common/mipmap_cache.c`, and image source/path helpers: source availability/change evidence; scanner does not load pixels.
- `src/main.c`/`src/common/darktable.c`: darktable startup order/database/config initialization that determines coherent source relationships but is not executed by the scanner.
- CLI/database maintenance/import/export tools in `src/cli/*` and `tools/*` that inspect or modify source state: scanner preserves read-only inspect behavior and explicitly excludes their mutation paths.

**Preserve:** one coherent source generation, exact schema/record relationships, deterministic section/accounting order, optional input provenance, source-change detection, bounded progress/findings, and the ability to report every source record once.

**Redesign:** replace process-global DB/config/XMP access, independent module queries, GTK progress/dialogs, raw paths, and mutable source caches with one immutable scan request, source snapshot, owned section inputs, explicit dependency graph, cancellation/progress events, canonical report, and source-mutation sentinel.

**Excluded:** target ID allocation, migration plan, redb writes, image decode, XMP write, config apply, and UI. Named later issues consume only complete snapshots.

Rust ownership: scanner orchestration in `rusttable-compat`/`xtask`; native SQLite/XMP/config adapters remain isolated.

## Implementation

- Define `ScanRequest`, source aliases, section set, limits, progress/event/cancellation, section receipt, complete/partial status, `CompatibilitySnapshot`, and stable CLI outcomes.
- Open/fingerprint/snapshot through #183; materialize bounded section rows/blobs under one transaction; release SQLite before parallel semantic decoding where possible.
- Invoke #184–#191 decoders with exact source/schema/decoder identities and merge canonical records/findings.
- Validate all cross-section references: image↔film/history/mask/style/tag/collection/config/action/profile/source/XMP; duplicate/unresolved/conflicting relationships remain keyed findings.
- Maintain visited/disposition ledger proving each materialized record/blob/config/XMP item accounted exactly once.
- Track counts/bytes/timing/peak owned memory/truncation/source/decoder/schema identities without private values or raw paths.
- Implement `cargo xtask compatibility inspect` JSON/human output and bounded artifact references for opaque payloads.
- Add source-accounting matrix proving all upstream scanner inputs have section owners and no later decoder bypasses the scanner snapshot.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle missing/mismatched DB pair, corrupt/unsupported schema, unstable WAL/source, optional input missing/corrupt, section decoder failure, unresolved refs, row/blob/string/report/memory/time limits, cancellation, and source replacement after materialization.
- Decoder failure in one section retains bounded failure evidence but produces no complete snapshot.
- Source change at any pre-publication sentinel invalidates complete result and removes owned temporary snapshot files.
- Partial reports clearly state sections/records retained and omitted; migration commit must reject them.
- Scanner never opens source images or writes target/source files.

## Test matrix

- Full supported corpus across schema versions with/without XMP/config/source-location input.
- Cancellation/failure/limit/source change before, between, during, and after every section.
- Missing/corrupt optional sections, duplicate/conflicting cross refs, deterministic concurrency/worker count/row order.
- Complete accounting proof and deliberate unaccounted-record mutation.
- Privacy/path alias/report size/opaque artifact behavior.
- Source-map mutation adding a DB/XMP/config record family with no scanner section owner.
- Architecture tests for no target redb/catalog/image decode/write dependency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-compat -p rusttable-sqlite-native compatibility_scanner` passes composition/accounting/cancel/source-change/limits/source suites.
- `cargo xtask compatibility inspect --all-corpus --verify-accounting --repeat 2` emits byte-identical complete reports or exact blocking status.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 192` proves every listed upstream scanner input is owned.
- Every enabled source record is accounted once; cancellation/source change yields no complete snapshot and releases all handles/temp files; no source/target write occurs.

## Dependencies

Depends on #183, #184, #185, #186, #187, #188, #189, #190, #191, #450, #515, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements scanner composition, owned row materialization, progress/cancellation, cross-section/accounting validation, CLI inspect, source accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement target IDs, migration planning/writes, image decode, or UI.
issues.title: Implement the bounded read-only darktable compatibility scanner

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Convert one complete #192 compatibility snapshot into a deterministic, fully accounted, executable darktable-to-RustTable migration plan before any target catalog is opened for writing. The plan states exactly which photos/assets/virtual copies/groups/edits/masks/blends/metadata/tags/styles/presets/collections/config/opaque payloads are created, transformed, preserved, skipped, or blocking and records every source-to-target identity and dependency.

## Fixed decisions

- Target IDs are deterministic UUIDv5 values under a fixed RustTable migration namespace using source snapshot identity, source record family/table/key, image/virtual-copy version, and target entity kind. Reordered rows/paths/display labels do not change IDs.
- Every source ledger item from #192 has exactly one disposition: `Exact`, `Transformed`, `OpaquePreserved`, `SkippedByFixedDecision`, or `Blocking`. Scanner failures/truncation cannot produce a ready plan.
- Unsupported executable history/mask/blend is `OpaquePreserved` plus an explicit execution blocker; data is not erased to make import proceed.
- Source images remain referenced in place by default. Copy/move/hash/decode is not performed here.
- Initial plan targets one absent target path/catalog identity. Existing-target merge/reconciliation remains separate; target existence is a planning blocker.
- Plan policy is versioned and explicit: source availability requirements, missing-file handling, opaque preservation, privacy exclusions, duplicate-source behavior, configuration mapping, and target schema versions.
- Plan is pure over immutable snapshot + target schema/policy metadata. It does not open sources, SQLite, redb, XMP, or filesystem paths except optional target-existence probe through a narrow port.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

This issue owns the transformation/accounting plan across upstream responsibilities, not their parsing:

- `src/common/database.c/.h`: all library/data schema record families and migration relationships represented by #183–#192; plan must account for every supported/opaque source row rather than rerun darktable SQL migrations.
- `src/common/image.c/.h`, `src/common/film.c/.h`: images, film rolls, sources, virtual copies, groups, metadata/cache/source state mapped to RustTable assets/photos/source aliases.
- `src/common/history.c/.h`, `src/common/iop_order.c/.h`, `src/develop/imageop.c/.h`: histories, module instances/order/versions/payloads mapped to RustTable edits/stacks or opaque execution-blocking records.
- `src/develop/blend.c/.h`, `src/develop/masks.h`, `src/develop/masks/*`: blend/mask/raster graphs mapped to RustTable mask/blend DTOs or exact opaque payloads.
- `src/common/styles.c/.h`, `src/gui/presets.c/.h`: styles/presets and auto-apply constraints mapped to target reusable assets or preserved opaque records.
- `src/common/tags.c/.h`, `src/common/metadata.c/.h`, `src/common/collection.c/.h`, ratings/color/group helpers: organization and collection records mapped to target domain items/rules.
- `src/control/conf.c/.h`, `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`: selected configuration mappings and explicit exclusions.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: XMP/sidecar overlap and source provenance; precedence/conversion follows scanner records, not reparsing.
- `src/common/database.c` `_migrate_schema`, `_upgrade_*`, `_create_*`: source evidence only. RustTable does not translate or execute darktable migration SQL; the plan maps semantic records into independent redb schemas.
- darktable import/copy history/style/tag UI and CLI paths: user-visible migration outcomes later presented by RustTable UI/CLI, without invoking source behavior.

**Preserve:** every source ledger item, exact source/target key mapping, relationship/dependency order, virtual-copy/group/history/mask/style/tag/collection/config semantics where supported, exact opaque payloads, blocking chains, source availability state, and deterministic rerun identity.

**Redesign:** replace in-place SQLite migration/repair, mutable global state, sequential UI-driven import, auto-assigned row IDs, and silent default/skip behavior with a pure immutable migration graph, deterministic UUIDs, explicit dispositions/dependencies, target-schema projections, byte/count estimates, privacy-safe reports, and no-write readiness proof.

**Excluded:** target catalog creation/write/rollback, source file copying, image decode/hash, UI, and actual edit/mask execution. Named issues consume the ready plan.

Rust ownership: migration plan domain in `rusttable-compat` or dedicated migration module; target schema codecs are read-only metadata inputs.

## Implementation

- Define plan policy/version/status, source-to-target identity map, plan item families, dependencies, dispositions, blockers, target schema references, size/count estimates, opaque artifact refs, privacy exclusions, reports, and canonical receipt.
- Consume the complete #192 ledger and require one plan item/disposition per source item; detect omissions/duplicates.
- Build deterministic IDs and full graph for assets/photos/versions/groups/edits/instances/masks/blends/metadata/tags/labels/ratings/styles/presets/collections/config/opaque records.
- Validate all target references, cycles, source blockers, target schema availability, operation/mask conversion support, duplicate logical sources/names, missing files, and privacy exclusions.
- Estimate exact or upper-bound encoded bytes using versioned target codecs/schemas; unknown codecs create pending-schema blockers rather than guessed sizes.
- Produce canonical JSON plus bounded human/TSV summaries with aliases, counts/dispositions, target IDs, dependency/blocking chains, and required producer/schema/manifest versions.
- Add optional absent-target probe with no create/write.
- Add source-accounting matrix proving each upstream record family has a target family/disposition rule.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject incomplete/partial scanner snapshots, unknown ledger items/dispositions, ID collisions, duplicate plan ownership, missing/cyclic references, target schema mismatch, existing target, overflow/size/report limit, cancellation, and policy/schema drift.
- Blocking items prevent `Ready` but remain in plan and do not erase unrelated exact/transformed/opaque items.
- Missing files follow explicit policy and never trigger content reads or path-based ID changes.
- Opaque artifact hash/length/source mismatch blocks readiness.
- Equal snapshot/policy/target schema produces byte-identical IDs/plan/report independent of row/map/thread order.

## Test matrix

- Complete empty/minimal/full corpus plans, all record families/dispositions, virtual copies/groups/histories/masks/styles/tags/collections/config.
- Reordered source rows/maps, duplicate logical sources, path case/Unicode aliases, missing files, opaque histories/masks, broken dependencies/cycles, privacy exclusions.
- Target schema absent/incompatible, existing target, ID collision injection, unaccounted/duplicate source item, size/overflow/report limits, cancellation.
- Deterministic UUIDv5 known-answer and repeated process/root runs.
- Source-map mutation adding a compatibility record family without target mapping/disposition.
- Architecture tests for no SQLite/redb/source image/file-write dependencies.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-compat migration_plan` passes identity/accounting/dependency/disposition/estimate/privacy/source suites.
- `cargo xtask compatibility plan --all-corpus --target-schema current --verify-accounting --repeat 2` emits byte-identical plans or exact blockers.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 193` proves every listed darktable semantic record family has a target disposition.
- Every source item appears exactly once; ready plans have valid target graph/schema/IDs; planning opens no image content and writes no source/target state.

## Dependencies

Depends on #192, target schema/codec issues #194–#197 as they become available, #495, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements migration-plan domain, deterministic IDs, complete accounting/classification/dependency graph, target schema estimates, source accounting, reports, and tests. Do not create/write redb, copy/read image content, execute edits, or add UI.
issues.title: Implement a deterministic dry-run darktable-to-RustTable migration plan

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Define the canonical durable RustTable byte format for non-destructive edit stacks and operation instances, including exact preservation of imported darktable operation payloads that are not yet executable. The format is independent of Rust layout, declaration order, pointer width, host endianness, live registry, and darktable C structs while retaining every source responsibility needed for lossless migration.

## Fixed decisions

- Use versioned postcard over dedicated persistence DTOs in `rusttable-catalog-store`; domain structs are never serialized directly.
- Envelope v1: fixed magic `RTEDIT\0\x01`, envelope version, payload schema version, fixed-width payload length, SHA-256 payload digest, and payload bytes. SHA-256 matches the repository’s established identity primitive; no second checksum dependency is introduced.
- All semantic integers use explicit fixed widths/little-endian postcard representation; enum/field/value tags are permanent numeric values documented in a registry and never derived from Rust discriminant/declaration order.
- Floating values are stored as exact IEEE-754 `f32`/`f64` bit patterns according to parameter schema. NaN payload/signed zero survive; domain validation decides executability.
- One edit stores stable edit/photo/base/revision IDs, stack/template/order schema, ordered instances, and bounded extension records.
- Each instance stores stable instance ID, RustTable operation ID, optional exact darktable compatibility operation name, parameter schema/current source version, implementation-independent parameter map, enabled, opacity, mask/blend refs, presentation name, and optional opaque imported payload references.
- Unknown future fields/value kinds/operations remain bounded opaque extension records and make only the affected entity non-executable. Decode→re-encode preserves extension payload bytes/ordering exactly.
- Opaque imported darktable `op_params`/blend/mask payloads are stored as separately content-addressed artifacts with source commit/schema/ABI/version/table/row identity, not embedded repeatedly in JSON/logs.
- Canonical encoding requires exact #264 operation order and canonical parameter ID order; maps are represented as sorted vectors, not hash maps.
- Hard limits apply before allocation: envelope/payload/edit/operation/parameter counts, string/blob/list/map/curve/matrix sizes, depth, extension records, and total referenced opaque bytes.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

The Rust format replaces the persisted state represented by these darktable sources:

- `src/common/database.c` schema/upgrade SQL for `main.history`, `main.images.history_end`/history hash, `main.module_order`, `main.mask`, `main.styles`, `main.style_items`, and `data.presets`: operation/version/params/enabled/multi-instance/blend/mask/order payload fields.
- `src/common/history.h` and `src/common/history.c`: `dt_dev_history_item_t`, history load/write/copy/compress/truncate/hash/end, operation/blend/mask payload ownership, and instance/order state.
- `src/develop/imageop.h`, `src/develop/imageop.c`, and `src/iop/iop_api.h`: operation compatibility identity/version, params/defaults, instance ID/priority/name, enable/opacity/blend/mask state, and `legacy_params` migration interface.
- `src/common/iop_order.h/.c`: persisted/default/custom operation order versions/lists and mandatory operation placement.
- `src/develop/blend.h/.c` and `src/develop/masks.h`/`src/develop/masks/*`: blend/mask/raster references and versioned payloads referenced by an edit.
- `src/common/styles.h/.c` and `src/gui/presets.h/.c`: reusable operation lists/payloads sharing the same operation-instance serialization semantics; their target envelopes remain separate issues.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: XMP history operation/version/params/blend/mask/order encoding and opaque source preservation.
- `src/iop/*` introspection/`legacy_params` definitions plus #495: exact parameter field/tag/version/ABI evidence translated into typed values or preserved source artifacts.

**Preserve:** edit/photo/base/revision identity; authored instance order; operation compatibility name/version; instance ID/name/priority; enabled/opacity; exact parameter values/bits; mask/blend/raster refs; operation-order/template evidence; unknown future fields; exact imported darktable blobs/source provenance; and non-executable status.

**Redesign:** replace SQLite rows, XMP base64/blob representations, native C struct layouts, operation pointers, implicit enum widths, database-specific ordering, and direct serializer use of mutable domain structs with one explicit versioned DTO envelope, permanent tags, canonical vectors, checked limits, content-addressed opaque artifacts, and safe conversion errors.

**Excluded:** mask/blend payload codecs (#195), style/preset codec (#196), target redb schema/transactions (#197 onward), operation migration/execution, and XMP writing.

Rust ownership: DTO/codec in `rusttable-catalog-store`; domain values in core/processing remain independent and convert through checked adapters.

## Implementation

- Define envelope, `StoredEditV1`, `StoredOperationV1`, stack/order provenance, typed parameter values (bool, signed/unsigned fixed integers, f32/f64 bits, bounded text/bytes, enum, color, point/vector/matrix/curve/list/map/content/profile refs), extension fields, opaque artifact refs, and conversion errors.
- Maintain a checked permanent wire-tag registry with duplicate/reuse/schema-diff tests.
- Implement domain↔DTO conversion, canonical sorting/order validation, postcard encode/decode, length/digest validation, schema dispatch, and allocation-limited readers without `unwrap`/`expect` on stored bytes.
- Encode unknown extension records with exact tag/order/payload and preserve through decode/re-encode.
- Validate IDs/revisions/schema versions, operation order, parameter uniqueness/type/schema, bounds/depth, mask/blend refs, opaque artifact metadata, and source provenance before domain publication.
- Add golden bytes for identity/basic stack/all value kinds/multiple instances/opaque darktable operation/unknown fields/nonfinite values/max limits and malformed/truncated/digest-corrupt inputs.
- Add property/fuzz tests for canonicality, cross-platform deterministic bytes, order sensitivity, extension preservation, bounded decoder allocation, and no partial state.
- Add source-accounting table mapping every darktable persisted edit/history/instance/order field to a DTO field, opaque artifact, or named separate codec.
- Emit bounded encoding receipt with schema, counts, payload/envelope sizes, SHA-256, extension/opaque counts, and no parameter/private values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject bad magic/version/length/digest, unsupported schema, duplicate/reused tags/IDs/parameters, invalid order/refs/types, oversized/deep values, malformed postcard, unknown required field, artifact mismatch, arithmetic overflow, and domain conversion failure before returning partial state.
- Unknown optional extensions survive exactly; unknown required semantics mark affected edit/operation non-executable without discarding bytes.
- Corrupt one operation cannot cause neighboring operation bytes/order to be misinterpreted; envelope/length framing remains explicit.
- Equal logical edit encodes identically on Linux/macOS/Windows; different order/bit pattern/provenance changes bytes.

## Test matrix

- Empty/basic/multiple operation/instance stacks; all value kinds/boundaries; exact f32/f64 bits including NaN/signed zero; names/Unicode; mask/blend refs.
- Exact opaque `op_params`/unknown operation/version/source provenance and missing/tampered artifact.
- Unknown optional/required extension fields/value kinds and byte-preserving re-encode.
- Malformed/truncated/oversized/digest/length/tag/version/order/ref inputs and allocation/depth attacks.
- Golden files on all supported targets and randomized valid round trips/order insertion.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable history/instance/order/payload field without DTO/disposition owner.
- Architecture tests proving no Rust layout/serde domain derive/native struct/SQLite/XMP dependency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store edit_codec` passes golden/property/fuzz/limit/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog-codec edit --goldens --property 10000 --malformed-corpus --verify-cross-platform` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 194` proves every listed darktable persisted edit responsibility maps to this or a named separate codec.
- Equal edits encode identically; goldens detect wire drift; unknown extensions/opaque artifacts survive; malformed input yields no partial domain state.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #193, #263, #264, #495, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements edit/operation DTOs, permanent tags, canonical postcard envelope/codec, conversions, opaque refs, source accounting, golden/property/fuzz tests, and receipts. Do not implement mask/blend/style/preset codecs, redb schema/transactions, operation migration/execution, or XMP writing.
issues.title: Implement canonical RustTable edit-operation serialization

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Define the canonical durable RustTable byte formats for mask graphs and per-operation blend state, including typed drawn/parametric/group/source/raster records and exact preservation of imported darktable mask/blend payloads that are not yet executable. The format is independent of Rust/C layout and retains all upstream identifiers, graph order, versions, coordinate evidence, and opaque bytes needed for shift-in-place migration.

## Fixed decisions

- Reuse #194’s versioned postcard envelope, SHA-256 digest, permanent wire tags, extension mechanism, canonical vectors, and allocation limits with distinct mask/blend magic and schema identifiers.
- Mask geometry stores source-oriented normalized coordinates plus explicit coordinate-space/version/source-dimensions/orientation evidence. No interpretation from current rendered dimensions is implicit.
- Mask graph is an ordered directed graph. Executable conversion rejects cycles, duplicate child entries, self-reference, missing masks, or invalid operation/raster refs; opaque storage may retain invalid source graphs with findings.
- Blend state stores enabled, mode, blend color space, opacity, inversion, drawn/group root, raster source instance/output, parametric channels/thresholds, feather/blur/contrast/brightness, and extensions.
- Exact float bits and source enum/flag integers are retained; validated semantic tags are stored separately where supported.
- Imported darktable mask/blend bytes remain content-addressed artifacts with source commit/schema/ABI/version/table/row identity until typed conversion is proven. Unknown data blocks only affected execution.
- Canonical order: masks by permanent ID; shape point/sample order as authored; group children by explicit order then child ID tie; parametric channels by permanent channel tag.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for `main.mask`, `main.history.blendop_params`/`blendop_version`, `main.style_items` blend fields, and any mask/history relationship columns.
- `src/develop/masks.h` and `src/develop/masks/masks.c`: mask IDs/types/versions/names/source coordinates, point blobs/counts, form load/write/copy/delete, graph ownership, transform/raster dispatch, and history/database association.
- `src/develop/masks/circle.c`, `ellipse.c`, `gradient.c`, `path.c`, `brush.c`, `group.c`, and `object.c`: exact shape/control/sample/source/group field/version semantics and authored order.
- `src/develop/blend.h`, `src/develop/blend.c`, `src/develop/blend_gui.c`, and `src/develop/blends/*`: `dt_develop_blend_params_t`, version/mode/colorspace/opacity/mask/raster/parametric/feather/blur fields, defaults, flags, group/raster lookup, and history/preset/style use.
- `src/develop/imageop.h/.c`: operation instance IDs, blend params/version, raster-mask output/source IDs, and history synchronization.
- `src/common/history.c/.h`: mask/blend payload load/write/copy/hash and operation association.
- `src/common/styles.c/.h` and `src/gui/presets.c/.h`: reusable operation blend payload/version storage/application.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: XMP mask/history/blend serialization and source versions.
- `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c`: runtime mask/blend/raster dependency resolution; execution remains #277–#284.

**Preserve:** every mask/form/group ID/kind/version/name/point/source, shape/control/sample bit pattern/order, clone/heal source, group child/operator/opacity order, coordinate system/evidence, blend version/mode/colorspace/opacity/inversion, parametric thresholds, drawn/raster refs, exact source enum/flags/reserved fields, and opaque original payload provenance.

**Redesign:** replace SQLite/XMP blobs, native C structs/enum widths, mutable recursive form lists, operation pointers, implicit coordinate interpretation, and raw blend blobs with explicit versioned DTOs, permanent tags, exact bit fields, bounded canonical graph encoding, source-local→target IDs, content-addressed opaque artifacts, and checked domain conversion.

**Excluded:** mask rasterization/transforms/editing, blend execution, styles/preset envelopes, operation execution, UI, and XMP writing.

Rust ownership: mask/blend persistence DTOs/codecs in `rusttable-catalog-store`; executable mask/blend domain remains later crates/issues.

## Implementation

- Define separate mask graph/envelope and blend envelope DTOs for every typed shape, point/control/brush sample, source coordinate, group child/operator, parametric channel, raster ref, blend field, extension, and opaque artifact ref.
- Assign/check permanent tags and implement domain↔DTO plus #186/#187 compatibility→typed/opaque conversion.
- Encode/decode exact f32/f64 bits, canonical IDs/order, graph dependencies, source coordinate evidence, and extension bytes.
- Validate IDs/versions/types, finite/ranges, point/sample/depth/edge counts, path closure, group DAG, mask/operation/raster refs, thresholds/order, blend modes/colorspaces, total bytes/depth, and artifact metadata before executable domain publication.
- Add golden bytes for every shape/blend kind/nested group/source/raster/parametric mode/unknown extension/opaque imported version.
- Add property/fuzz/corruption tests for canonicality, cross-platform identity, invalid graphs, missing refs, malformed/oversized payloads, digest/length/tag/schema errors, and exact opaque re-encode.
- Add source-accounting table mapping every darktable mask/blend persisted field/version to a DTO field, opaque artifact, or named executable/UI owner.
- Emit bounded receipts with counts/graph depth/bytes/hashes/opaque/extension status and no geometry/private payload values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Bad envelope/digest/schema/tag, invalid/duplicate IDs, missing/cyclic refs, malformed graph/order/shape/path/sample/threshold, nonfinite/out-of-range values, unsupported source version, oversized/deep input, artifact mismatch, or conversion error returns no partial executable graph.
- Opaque invalid source graphs may be stored exactly with `NonExecutable` status and findings; they are never normalized/repaired silently.
- Unknown optional extensions survive byte-for-byte. Unknown required semantics block only affected mask/blend/edit.
- Corruption in one framed mask/blend record cannot shift neighboring records.

## Test matrix

- All circle/ellipse/gradient/path/brush/group/source/raster forms and known versions; all blend modes/colorspaces/mask/parametric fields.
- Nested ordered groups, duplicate/missing/self/cyclic refs, operation/raster links, multiple image-local ID sources.
- NaN/Inf/signed zero, extreme coordinates/counts, path closure, huge brush/depth/edge limits.
- Opaque imported payloads and unknown optional/required extensions with exact re-encode.
- Malformed/truncated/oversized/digest/tag/schema inputs and allocation attacks.
- Cross-platform golden/property tests and source-map mutation for new darktable field/version.
- Architecture tests for no rasterization/execution/UI/native layout dependency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store mask_blend_codec` passes goldens/property/fuzz/graph/opaque/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog-codec mask-blend --goldens --property 10000 --malformed-corpus --verify-cross-platform` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 195` proves every listed darktable persisted mask/blend responsibility maps to this or a named later owner.
- Equal valid graphs encode identically; valid group/raster links round-trip; invalid graphs yield no executable state; opaque imported payloads remain exact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #186, #187, #194, #277, #279, #495, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements mask/blend DTOs, envelopes, permanent tags, canonical codecs/conversions, opaque refs, source accounting, golden/property/fuzz tests, and receipts. Do not rasterize/evaluate/edit masks, implement UI, styles/preset serialization, or XMP writing.
issues.title: Implement canonical RustTable mask and blend serialization

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Define canonical durable RustTable formats for reusable styles and operation presets, including mixed typed/opaque content imported from darktable. The formats reuse #194/#195 envelopes and preserve every upstream item/order/operation/version/instance/blend/mask/constraint/source field required to migrate darktable styles/presets without identity collisions or silent loss.

## Fixed decisions

- A style is a stable-ID named ordered edit fragment with explicit application policy and optional referenced mask/blend bundle. A preset is stable-ID and scoped to one operation compatibility identity + parameter schema version + optional blend payload.
- User-visible name/description are metadata, never identity. Duplicate names are valid and disambiguated by stable IDs/source provenance.
- Reuse versioned postcard envelopes, SHA-256 payload digest, permanent tags, canonical vectors/maps, extension fields, and opaque artifact references from #194/#195 with distinct style/preset magic/schema IDs.
- Auto-apply rules use a bounded typed target predicate DTO derived from #188/#190 compatibility AST; never store executable SQL/regex/Lua/script.
- Imported styles/presets may contain typed and exact opaque items. Unsupported items remain visible/source-preserved and make exact application unavailable only for affected item/style/preset according to policy.
- Canonical style item order is explicit authored order; masks/blends are referenced by stable IDs and included once in a canonical bundle.
- Preserve exact source database/key/name/description/item order/operation/version/params/enabled/multi-instance/blend/writeprotect/visibility/autoapply/filter/default/provenance evidence.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for `main.styles`, `main.style_items`, and `data.presets`, including all operation/version/params/enabled/blend/multi-instance/name/description/writeprotect/autoapply/filter/format/camera/lens/exposure fields.
- `src/common/styles.h` and `src/common/styles.c`: style IDs/names/descriptions/items/order, create/delete/duplicate/import/export, history↔style conversion, operation/blend/mask payload ownership, and unsupported/missing operation behavior.
- `src/gui/presets.h` and `src/gui/presets.c`: preset storage/apply/update/delete, operation/version matching, blend/default payloads, duplicate names, built-in/write-protected/visible states, auto-apply/filter predicate behavior, and preset lifecycle.
- `src/develop/imageop.h/.c` and per-operation `init_presets`/preset registration in `src/iop/*.c|cc`: operation identity/version/defaults, built-in preset payloads, format/camera/lens constraints, and instance eligibility.
- `src/common/history.h/.c` and `src/common/iop_order.h/.c`: ordered operation-instance fragments and history/order semantics reused by styles.
- `src/develop/blend.h/.c` and `src/develop/masks/*`: referenced blend/mask payloads and dependencies encoded through #195.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: XMP style/preset/history payload representation and import/export provenance where supported.
- `src/libs/styles.c`, style actions in lighttable/darkroom, `src/libs/modulegroups.c`, and preset UI/actions in image-operation GUI code: user-visible list/application/visibility behavior later owned by target UI/application issues.

**Preserve:** every source style/preset and key, duplicate names/descriptions, ordered items, operation/version/params/enabled/multi-instance/blend/mask refs, built-in/write-protected/visible/default state, auto-apply predicates/constraints, exact opaque payloads, source provenance/classification, and unknown future fields.

**Redesign:** replace SQLite rows, mutable global style/preset APIs, translated-name identity, module pointers, direct SQL/glob application, and native payload structs with stable UUID IDs, versioned DTO envelopes, explicit ordered item vectors, content-addressed opaque artifacts, bounded typed predicate DTOs, canonical metadata/tags, and checked executable status.

**Excluded:** target style/preset tables/repositories, applying styles/presets, predicate evaluation, UI, operation execution, and XMP writing.

Rust ownership: DTO/codecs in `rusttable-catalog-store`; target domain/repository/application later; compatibility conversion consumes #188 records.

## Implementation

- Define `StoredStyleV1`, ordered item refs/inline operation fragments, mask/blend bundle refs, application policy, metadata/tags/revisions/source provenance/extensions; define `StoredPresetV1`, operation/version/params/blend/default/visibility/writeprotect/autoapply/predicate/provenance/extensions.
- Assign/check permanent tags and implement domain↔DTO plus #188 compatibility→typed/opaque conversion.
- Reuse #194 operation values and #195 mask/blend envelopes/artifact refs without duplicating schemas.
- Implement bounded predicate DTO conversion from supported #190 AST nodes; unsupported predicates stored opaquely/non-executable.
- Validate duplicate/missing IDs/items/refs, operation/version/schema/payload mismatches, invalid item order, mask/blend dependency cycles, predicate bounds, metadata/tag sizes, revisions, source artifact hashes, and executable status.
- Add cross-platform golden bytes for empty/multi/multi-instance/duplicate-name/built-in/user/writeprotected/autoapply/opaque/unknown extension/max-bound cases.
- Add property/fuzz/corruption tests for canonicality, order sensitivity, reference integrity, predicate preservation, digest/schema/tag errors, and allocation bounds.
- Add source-accounting table mapping every darktable style/preset schema/API/built-in field to DTO/disposition or later application/UI owner.
- Emit privacy-safe receipts with IDs/counts/bytes/hashes/opaque/executable status, not names/predicates/payload values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Bad envelope/digest/schema/tag, duplicate IDs/items/tags, missing refs, invalid order/revision/operation/version, malformed/oversized predicate/metadata, artifact mismatch, dependency cycle, or conversion error yields no executable domain object.
- Mixed typed/opaque style persists as source-preserved with exact affected-item status; policy determines whether partial application is prohibited (default exact migration: prohibit).
- Duplicate names round-trip and never merge/collide.
- Unknown optional extensions survive exactly; unknown required semantics block affected object.

## Test matrix

- Empty/single/multiple/multi-instance styles; masks/blends; duplicate names; order; revisions/tags/metadata.
- Presets with/without blend/default, built-in/user/writeprotected/hidden/visible/autoapply and all supported constraints.
- Supported/opaque predicates, unsupported operations/versions, mixed typed/opaque items, missing refs/cycles.
- Unknown extensions and malformed/truncated/oversized/digest/tag/schema inputs.
- Cross-platform golden/property/randomized construction order.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable style/preset field/API/built-in registration without owner.
- Architecture tests for no application/evaluation/SQLite/XMP/native layout dependency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store style_preset_codec` passes goldens/property/fuzz/reference/predicate/opaque/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog-codec style-preset --goldens --property 10000 --malformed-corpus --verify-cross-platform` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 196` proves every listed darktable persisted style/preset responsibility maps to this or a named later owner.
- Equal objects encode identically; duplicate names retain distinct IDs; mixed opaque evidence survives; invalid refs/predicates produce no executable object.

## Dependencies

Depends on #188, #190, #194, #195, #495, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements style/preset DTOs, envelopes/tags/codecs/conversions, predicate DTO, opaque refs, source accounting, golden/property/fuzz tests, and receipts. Do not implement target tables/repositories/application/evaluation/UI or XMP writing.
issues.title: Implement canonical RustTable style and preset serialization

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the complete versioned native redb catalog-schema and maintenance runner that replaces darktable’s writable SQLite schema creation/upgrade/repair/maintenance path. RustTable catalogs migrate monotonically and atomically through reviewed table/index/codec versions with verified backups, crash recovery, explicit repair/compaction, and read-only newer-schema inspection; no SQLite write path remains.

## Fixed decisions

- `redb` 4.1.x remains the only native writable persistence engine. Postcard DTO schemas (#194–#196 and later records) and redb table/index key formats are independently versioned.
- Catalog header stores magic, catalog UUID, schema version, minimum reader/writer, creation/update revisions, migration/maintenance state, feature flags, clean-shutdown generation, last verified backup, codec registry identity, and source-migration provenance.
- Migrations are contiguous immutable functions `Vn -> Vn+1`; no skipped versions, downgrade writes, implicit current-table creation, or “best effort” partial upgrade.
- One migration step uses one redb write transaction. Large transforms build validated shadow tables/indexes in that transaction and switch header/table aliases only at final commit.
- Persistent savepoints are short-lived operator-visible rollback markers using Immediate durability, created before tables open, bounded/count-expiring, and explicitly removed after success because pages remain pinned.
- External verified backup from the backup issue remains authoritative cross-process rollback before user-visible schema migration. Savepoints do not replace it.
- Repair callback/progress is explicit. Startup enters maintenance mode for repair/interrupted migration; no silent automatic repair.
- Newer schemas open via read-only inspection when possible and never acquire a writer/downgrade.
- Compaction is explicit with free-space/resource preflight, cancellation boundaries, close/reopen verification, and receipt; never unpredictable during editing.
- Extension namespaces are preserved unless colliding with reserved names; unknown reserved tables/codec tags block writes.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

This issue replaces, rather than translates, darktable’s writable database lifecycle:

- `src/common/database.h` and `src/common/database.c`:
  - `dt_database_t`, current/full schema constants, database create/open/lock/attach/close lifecycle;
  - `_create_library_schema`, `_create_data_schema`, `_upgrade_library_schema_step`, `_upgrade_data_schema_step`, `_migrate_schema`, version metadata, and all SQL migration/repair/maintenance branches;
  - transaction boundaries, backup/lock/error state, quick/integrity checks, maintenance/optimize/vacuum, corruption/upgrade prompts, and unclean shutdown handling.
- `src/common/darktable.c/.h` and `src/main.c`: startup database initialization/maintenance mode, error handling, clean shutdown, and application service ordering.
- `src/common/file_location.c/.h` and config keys: default database/backup paths and maintenance settings; RustTable uses #179 platform paths.
- `src/common/cache.h/.c`, `src/common/image_cache.h/.c`, `src/common/mipmap_cache.h/.c`: persistence-dependent cache invalidation/cleanup around DB schema/open/close; cache implementations remain separate.
- Table consumers/writers in `src/common/image.c`, `film.c`, `history.c`, `styles.c`, `tags.c`, `metadata.c`, `collection.c`, `selection.c`, `colorlabels`/ratings/group helpers, `src/develop/masks/*`, and `src/gui/presets.c`: semantic record families that define RustTable reserved tables/indexes/codecs.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: sidecar/database synchronization and history/metadata persistence that influences migration provenance but remains separate from redb schema mechanics.
- darktable maintenance/backup UI and CLI call sites in preferences/dialogs/`src/cli/*`: user-visible operation/progress/recovery expectations later exposed by RustTable maintenance UI/CLI.
- SQLite ICU/extension/pragma behavior in `src/common/sqliteicu.c/.h` and `database.c`: intentionally not reproduced in redb; equivalent query/sort semantics belong target domain/index issues.

**Preserve:** explicit schema versions and contiguous migrations, transactional all-or-nothing upgrades, complete record/index invariants, backup/recovery before destructive change, lock/single-writer safety, corruption/repair visibility, unclean/interrupted state detection, read-only newer-schema inspection, explicit maintenance progress/cancellation, and no partially current header.

**Redesign:** replace two attached SQLite files, SQL create/ALTER/copy migrations, pragmas/vacuum/ICU extensions, writable source repair, database-global handles, and dialog-driven recovery with independent versioned redb tables/indexes/codecs, monotonic Rust migration registry, transactional shadow structures, durable header/markers, redb savepoints/repair APIs, verified external backups, typed maintenance plans, and canonical receipts.

**Excluded:** reading/mutating darktable source DB (read-only #183), domain repository commands, migration plan execution (#198 onward), backup UI, automatic background compaction, and cross-database replication.

Rust ownership: `rusttable-catalog-store` owns redb schema/migration/maintenance; domain/catalog APIs never expose redb types.

## Implementation

- Define catalog/table/index/codec descriptors, reserved/extension namespaces, migration registry/step context, header/state/feature flags, maintenance plan, savepoint/repair/compaction policy, statistics/invariant checks, receipts, and typed errors.
- Create current schema containing existing tables plus versioned reserved descriptors for assets/sources/photos/edits/revisions/operation envelopes/masks/blends/styles/presets/metadata/tags/groups/collections/journal/opaque/source-provenance/indexes.
- Implement create, read-only inspect, older detection, sequential migration, per-step/final invariant validation, header transition, crash-marker detection, recovery plan, and idempotent reopen.
- Integrate redb durability/savepoints/repair callback/statistics/compaction behind adapter-owned values.
- Require verified backup identity before schema-changing migration; record it in header/receipt.
- Add golden catalog files for every schema version/state and exact expected tables/keys/values/indexes/header/receipts.
- Add fault injection at every durable boundary: allocation/codec/index conflict/disk/commit/shadow/swap/header/savepoint/repair/compaction/close/reopen.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping each darktable table/record family/migration responsibility to a redb table/codec/migration or explicit target exclusion.
- Add architecture checks for no writable SQLite/sqlx, raw redb guards/transactions in public APIs, ad hoc table creation/version changes outside registry.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject bad magic/header/schema/codec/table/index, missing/duplicate migration, newer writer, interrupted/ambiguous state, reserved collision, backup mismatch, savepoint ordering/pin limits, repair/compaction preflight, corruption/invariant failure, disk/commit/close/reopen/cancellation.
- Migration failure commits neither step/header and leaves original current old schema usable plus verified backup. Crash recovery deterministically distinguishes precommit/committed/postheader states.
- Repair/compaction cancellation never claims success/current maintenance generation; progress remains bounded.
- Newer schema may expose safe header/statistics read-only but cannot be written/downgraded.
- Unknown extension tables survive migrations unless explicitly rejected by namespace conflict.

## Test matrix

- Create/current open, every supported old→current chain, repeated reopen/idempotence, newer read-only, downgrade rejection.
- Exact table/index/codec invariants and all record families from #194–#196/target catalog.
- Persistent/ephemeral savepoint create/list/restore/delete/order/pinning/durability.
- Crash/fault before/after every transaction/header/shadow/swap/backup/repair/compaction boundary.
- Corrupt/missing/extra reserved/extension tables, duplicate index keys, schema/codec drift, allocation/disk/full/permission/lock.
- Repair progress/cancel/fail/success; compaction space/cancel/interruption/verify.
- Source-map mutation adding darktable record family/migration without redb disposition owner.
- Architecture mutation adding SQLite write/ad hoc redb table/raw guard leak.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store schema_migrations` passes golden/chain/savepoint/repair/compaction/crash/fault/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog migrate-matrix --all-schema-goldens --fault-every-boundary --repair --compact --verify-backup-rollback` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 197` proves each darktable writable schema/migration/maintenance responsibility has an explicit redb replacement/exclusion.
- Every supported catalog reaches current through exact chain; failures leave old catalog+backup recoverable/no partially current header; newer opens read-only; no writable SQLite/public redb guard exists.

## Dependencies

Depends on #194, #195, #196, #453, #515, the verified-backup issue, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements redb schema descriptors, migration/maintenance runner, savepoint/repair/compaction policy, initial version chain, source accounting, golden catalogs, rollback/fault tests, receipts, and architecture checks. Do not implement migration UI, general domain commands, background auto-compaction, or replication.
issues.title: Implement versioned redb catalog-schema migrations with atomic rollback

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Extend the native catalog so every imported photo owns exactly one primary source asset and zero or one explicit sidecar asset, with independent immutable provenance, source availability, content evidence, and transactional indexes. This replaces darktable’s implicit filename/film/image/XMP sidecar association with explicit RustTable asset records while preserving source relationships needed for migration and later sidecar reconciliation.

## Fixed decisions

- Roles are `Primary` and `Sidecar`; one import/photo cannot contain duplicate roles. Additional auxiliaries require a later schema issue.
- Sidecar pairing is explicit input to this issue. Automatic discovery/pairing belongs to the later sidecar discovery issue and may not be hidden in persistence.
- Each asset stores stable asset ID, logical source key, originating platform/path policy, exact normalized native path bytes, SHA-256, byte length, modification/file-identity evidence, media type, role, snapshot status, source availability, and source-provenance version. File bytes are never stored in redb.
- Primary and sidecar have independent content identities and availability. A missing sidecar does not make the primary unavailable.
- Source-key/asset-ID/content/import-role indexes are updated in one redb transaction with authoritative records. Same content at different logical paths is allowed and indexed as multiple assets.
- Existing single-primary catalogs migrate without changing primary asset/photo IDs or source/content evidence.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` image/film/history schema and migrations that store source filename/film ID/image identity while sidecars remain filesystem-adjacent rather than first-class DB assets.
- `src/common/image.h` and `src/common/image.c`: primary image source identity/path resolution, image availability/change state, sidecar filename construction/calls, virtual-copy sharing of one source, and image row lifecycle.
- `src/common/film.h` and `src/common/film.c`: source-folder/filename relationship used to reconstruct the primary asset logical path.
- `src/common/exif.h` and `src/common/exif.cc`: XMP sidecar path construction/discovery/read/write, sidecar version/history/metadata association, and source-image relation.
- `src/control/jobs/sidecar_jobs.c/.h`: sidecar read/write/resync jobs, selected image/source identity, change/error behavior, and lifecycle.
- `src/common/history.c/.h`: history/image relationship whose sidecar representation belongs to the same photo/source but is persisted separately.
- `src/control/crawler.c`: detection of missing/moved source and sidecar state; continuous reconciliation remains later.
- `src/libs/import.c`, `src/common/import_session.c/.h`, and film jobs: source registration and optional sidecar/import metadata association later supplied to this persistence boundary.
- lighttable/darkroom/image-info/sidecar actions in `src/libs/*` and views: user-visible source/sidecar status later projected by UI.

**Preserve:** exact primary source identity, optional adjacent XMP sidecar identity, independent file/content/change/availability evidence, virtual copies referencing the same primary/sidecar relationship, source path bytes/platform semantics, and failure-atomic relationship updates.

**Redesign:** replace implicit `film_rolls.folder + images.filename`, convention-only `.xmp` adjacency, repeated path reconstruction, and non-first-class sidecar state with explicit immutable primary/sidecar asset records, typed roles, platform-aware logical keys, independent content hashes, redb indexes, and transactional role uniqueness.

**Excluded:** sidecar discovery, XMP parsing/writing/precedence, source byte copy/delete/read, edit/history import, relocation/crawler, and UI.

Rust ownership: domain assets in catalog/core; DTO/tables/indexes/migration in catalog-store; source snapshot evidence originates from #223/import services.

## Implementation

- Extend asset/import/photo domain constructors/commands to accept one primary and optional sidecar while preserving single-primary callers.
- Define versioned asset provenance DTO/key formats and migrate existing schema through #197.
- Add authoritative asset records and indexes: asset by ID; logical source key→asset; content hash→ordered asset IDs; import/photo→role→asset; availability/source-state indexes needed by later reconciliation.
- Enforce role/source/asset uniqueness and validate path bytes/platform policy/content evidence/source status before commit.
- Implement create/read/delete/reopen atomically; rejected duplicate/index/codec/commit failure leaves no records or index residue.
- Preserve non-UTF-8 path bytes and compare logical keys using stored originating platform policy rather than lossy strings/current host.
- Add invariant checker rebuilding expected indexes/role counts from authoritative records.
- Add source-accounting table mapping darktable image/film/sidecar relationship responsibilities to target fields or later sidecar services.
- Emit privacy-safe receipts with IDs/roles/hashes/sizes/availability/index counts, not raw paths.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing/duplicate primary, duplicate sidecar role, reused asset ID/source key, inconsistent photo/import ownership, invalid path/platform/content/hash/length/media/status, index conflict, codec/schema/transaction/cancellation failure.
- Same content/different path remains valid; same logical key/different content is a conflict requiring later reconciliation, not silent replacement.
- Missing/inaccessible source is storable with explicit availability and original evidence; no file open occurs here.
- Migration failure leaves old catalog/current IDs unchanged under #197 rollback.
- Delete removes all indexes atomically and refuses deletion while referenced by edits/opaque provenance unless caller supplies a later explicit policy.

## Test matrix

- Primary only; primary+sidecar; missing/inaccessible assets; non-UTF-8/Linux/macOS/Windows logical paths.
- Duplicate roles/IDs/source keys; same content/different path; same key/different content; virtual-copy/shared source relationships.
- Existing schema migration with unchanged IDs/evidence; reopen/canonical role order.
- Fault injection at record/index/codec/commit/delete/migration boundaries and cancellation before commit.
- Index rebuild/invariant checks and randomized insertion order.
- Source-map mutation changing darktable image/sidecar relationship behavior without target owner.
- Architecture tests for no file content read/XMP parser/redb DTO leakage.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store asset_provenance` passes migration/index/fault/path/source-accounting suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog verify-assets --migration-matrix --faults all --verify-indexes` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 198` proves every listed darktable primary/sidecar relationship responsibility is accounted for.
- Reopened imports contain exactly one primary/at most one sidecar in deterministic order; indexes reconcile after success/rejection/failure; no file bytes are read/copied/deleted/interpreted.

## Dependencies

Depends on #197, #223, the existing import-record model, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements domain extension, redb schema/indexes/migration, repository behavior, source accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement sidecar discovery, XMP parsing/writing, relocation, content reading/copying, edit import, or UI.
issues.title: Persist primary and optional sidecar asset provenance in redb

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Persist exactly one authoritative current non-destructive edit per RustTable photo with optimistic revision checks, canonical #194/#195 payloads, execution status, source provenance, and transactional indexes. This replaces darktable’s mutable `history` rows plus `images.history_end`/history-hash current-state model with one explicit redb current-edit record while preserving imported history provenance and opaque blockers.

## Fixed decisions

- `EditRevision` is monotonic `u64` scoped to one photo; first persisted edit revision is 1.
- Create requires no current edit. Compare-and-replace requires exact current edit revision and exact base photo revision, then writes revision+1. Blind overwrite is forbidden.
- Current edit record, canonical operation envelope, mask/blend bundle, source/import provenance, executable status/blockers, content identity, and all indexes commit in one redb transaction.
- Unsupported/opaque operations, masks, or blends may persist exactly; they are never marked executable without registered conversion/execution support.
- Delete/reset is an explicit optimistic command and cannot erase immutable history once #204 exists; before #204 it records a reset tombstone/revision rather than silently removing provenance.
- Domain APIs expose owned edit values/errors, never redb/postcard guards/DTOs.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.
