- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for `main.history`, `main.images.history_end`, history/current hash columns, `main.module_order`, `main.mask`, and operation/blend/multi-instance fields.
- `src/common/history.h` and `src/common/history.c`: history add/write/delete/truncate/compress, current history endpoint, history hash, operation/blend/mask payload ownership, current-state updates, and transaction behavior.
- `src/develop/develop.c/.h`: current image/history synchronization, change detection, invalidation, and selected/current edit generation.
- `src/develop/imageop.h/.c` and `src/common/iop_order.h/.c`: current operation instances/order/params/enabled/multi-instance identity applied to persisted history.
- `src/develop/blend.h/.c` and `src/develop/masks/*`: current blend/mask state associated with operation history.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: current history/XMP synchronization and history hash/provenance interactions.
- `src/libs/history.c`, `src/libs/snapshots.c`, darkroom module actions, and undo/redo call sites: user-visible current history state and replacement conflicts later projected by UI/history issues.

**Preserve:** one authoritative current endpoint per photo, exact operation/order/instance/params/blend/mask payload, base image revision, optimistic conflict semantics, current/history hash identity, unsupported opaque content, atomic update/invalidation, and restart-equivalent current edit.

**Redesign:** replace mutable numbered SQLite history rows and `history_end`, implicit global develop state, direct row rewrites/truncation, and pointer-based current modules with an immutable canonical edit envelope, photo-scoped revision, compare-and-replace transaction, explicit execution blockers, content/source provenance, and reconstructable indexes.

**Excluded:** immutable revision history/undo graph (#204/#285), migration import execution, operation rendering, XMP writing, and edit UI.

Rust ownership: domain commands/repository port in `rusttable-catalog`; redb records/indexes/transactions in `rusttable-catalog-store`.

## Implementation

- Define `CurrentEditRepository` and checked create/read/compare-and-replace/reset/delete commands/results/errors.
- Add versioned current-edit DTO/table keyed by photo ID plus edit-ID, revision, content-hash, executable-status, opaque-blocker, and source-provenance indexes.
- Validate photo existence, base photo revision, edit/photo identity, operation/mask/blend refs, #194/#195 envelope length/digest/schema, expected current revision, execution status, and index uniqueness before commit.
- Store canonical envelope bytes/content identity once and reconstruct equal domain values on reopen.
- Implement fault-atomic record/index updates, concurrent writer conflict, schema migration from existing edit storage, and invariant rebuild/verification.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping darktable current-history fields/APIs to target current-edit fields or later immutable history owner.
- Emit privacy-safe receipts with IDs/revisions/hashes/counts/status/blocker codes, not parameters/private values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Return typed `AlreadyExists`, `NotFound`, `RevisionConflict`, `PhotoRevisionConflict`, `InvalidEdit`, `UnsupportedSchema`, `Codec`, `IndexConflict`, `Storage`, `Cancellation`, and corruption failures with no partial write.
- Exactly one concurrent replacement with same expected revision commits; loser sees authoritative revision/content unchanged.
- Opaque content persists and remains explicitly non-executable.
- Reset/delete cannot orphan mask/blend/opaque artifacts or remove immutable revision history; reference/invariant failure blocks transaction.
- Migration/codec/index/commit failure leaves prior current record and every index unchanged.

## Test matrix

- Create/read/replace/no-op/reset/delete/reopen; revision/base-photo conflicts; two/many concurrent writers.
- Canonical known/opaque operations, mask/blend bundles, max-size/invalid envelope/digest/schema/refs/executable status.
- Inject failure after each record/index write, codec, commit, cancellation; invariant rebuild.
- Existing schema migration with unchanged IDs/content.
- Deterministic list/index ordering and randomized map construction.
- Source-map mutation changing darktable current-history endpoint/hash/payload field without target owner.
- Architecture tests for no redb/postcard dependency in domain.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store current_edit` passes conflict/reopen/opaque/fault/migration/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog verify-current-edits --concurrent-writers --faults all --verify-indexes` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 199` proves every listed darktable current-edit responsibility is accounted for.
- Exactly one concurrent writer commits; conflicts/failures leave current record/indexes intact; reopen returns byte-equivalent edit/execution status; opaque data never becomes executable.

## Dependencies

Depends on #194, #195, #197, existing photo persistence, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements current-edit domain port/commands, redb records/indexes/migration, optimistic transaction behavior, source accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement immutable revision history, UI, rendering, or XMP writing.
issues.title: Persist current edits with optimistic revision checks in redb

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Extend #199 so every successful current-edit change creates one immutable, auditable revision record suitable for undo/redo, named snapshots, migration provenance, crash recovery, and reproducible rendering without duplicating unchanged canonical payload bytes. This replaces darktable’s mutable/truncated SQLite history endpoint with append-only RustTable revisions while preserving source history provenance.

## Fixed decisions

- Revision numbers are monotonically increasing per photo and never reused, reordered, mutated, or deleted by normal editing.
- Store canonical #194 edit envelopes and #195 mask/blend bundles in content-addressed blob tables keyed by SHA-256 + schema + length. SHA-256 is the established repository identity primitive; no BLAKE3 dependency is introduced.
- A revision stores photo/edit/revision IDs, parent revision, content blob refs, command kind, monotonic commit sequence, wall-clock timestamp as metadata only, optional bounded user label, execution status, source/migration provenance, and extension fields.
- Every #199 create/replace/reset/undo/redo/commit transaction atomically writes current state, exactly one immutable revision, blob refs/refcounts, indexes, and journal entry.
- Undo/redo creates a new revision whose payload references selected historical content; it never moves a mutable pointer backward or rewrites/deletes old rows. This preserves a linear auditable commit sequence while recording `restored_from_revision`.
- Same canonical payload shares blob bytes but never merges revision identity.
- Revision graph is a linear parent chain per photo in v1; labels/snapshots are metadata on revisions. Branch visualization may infer restore/edit sequences later.
- Automatic pruning is prohibited. Explicit blob compaction may remove only unreachable blobs after transactional full reachability verification; revision rows remain immutable.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for `main.history`, `main.images.history_end`, history/current hashes, module order, masks, and any snapshot/history-state columns.
- `src/common/history.h` and `src/common/history.c`: append/replace/truncate/compress history, current endpoint, redo tail, history hash, copy/paste, snapshot reconstruction, database load/write, and current-state mutation.
- `src/libs/history.c`: history stack display, current item/end selection, compression, delete/truncate, and user undo/redo/history actions later redesigned against append-only revisions.
- `src/libs/snapshots.c`: named/temporary darkroom snapshots, source history state, comparison, and lifecycle; v1 stores named revision labels while visual compare UI remains later.
- `src/libs/copy_history.c` and `src/libs/log_history.c`: history copy/paste/log/audit behavior and source item order/provenance.
- `src/develop/develop.c/.h`: current history generation, undo/redo/develop invalidation, pipeline rebuild, and selected current edit.
- `src/develop/imageop.h/.c`, `src/common/iop_order.c/.h`, `src/develop/blend.c/.h`, `src/develop/masks/*`: exact operation/order/blend/mask state contained in each revision payload.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: sidecar history snapshots/current history hash and migration provenance.
- darktable undo framework under `src/common/undo.c/.h` and callers: undo stack/action grouping semantics; RustTable append-only revision commands replace edit-history mutation while general UI undo remains #285.

**Preserve:** every committed edit state, exact operation/mask/blend payload, parent/current relationship, history order, source/migration provenance, current state after restart, undo/redo restore intent, named snapshot label, history hash/content identity, and failure-atomic current+history updates.

**Redesign:** replace mutable numbered history rows, `history_end` pointer, truncation/redo-tail mutation, in-memory undo actions, duplicate payload rows, and snapshot-only UI state with append-only immutable revisions, content-addressed canonical blobs, monotonic commit sequence, restore-as-new-revision, explicit current pointer/indexes, transactional journal, and reconstructable reachability.

**Excluded:** user-facing history/undo/snapshot UI (#285), pruning policy, visual snapshot compare, XMP write/export, edit merging, and distributed replication.

Rust ownership: revision/blob domain/ports in catalog; redb tables/indexes/transactions in catalog-store.

## Implementation

- Define `EditRevisionRecord`, command/source/provenance enums, parent/restore/label relations, blob identity/record/refcount, current pointer, paging/query ports, journal event, receipts, and typed errors.
- Modify #199 transactions so current state + one immutable revision + blob refs/refcounts + indexes + journal commit atomically.
- Validate exact parent/current/photo/edit/base revisions, monotonic next revision, content envelope digest/length/schema, execution status, no cycles, restore target, label limits, and blob identity before commit.
- Add redb tables/indexes for photo+revision, current revision, blob hash/schema/length, blob refcounts, provenance, labels, restore origin, commit sequence, and journal.
- Implement bounded ascending/descending paging, get/current, reconstruction, reachability verification, and explicit unreferenced-blob compaction transaction.
- Add migration from current-edit-only schema creating initial revision 1 without changing current edit/photo IDs/content.
- Add source-accounting mapping darktable history/current/snapshot/undo semantics to revision fields or later UI behavior.
- Emit privacy-safe receipts with IDs/revisions/hashes/counts/command/provenance codes, not labels/parameters/private metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject parent/current/photo/base conflict, nonmonotonic/overflow revision, duplicate record, invalid restore target, blob digest/schema/length mismatch, hash collision with different bytes, refcount/index/journal conflict, label bounds, codec/storage/cancellation failure.
- Exactly one concurrent writer commits through #199; revision/current/blob/index/journal remain unchanged on loser/failure.
- Identical payload uses existing blob after byte/digest/schema verification.
- Compaction refuses when reachability/refcount/invariant mismatch and never removes referenced blobs.
- Corrupt/missing blob prevents reconstruction and reports exact revisions; no neighboring state is silently substituted.

## Test matrix

- Initial/create/replace/reset/undo/redo/edit-after-restore/named snapshot; identical payload reuse; opaque content.
- Concurrent conflicts, parent cycles/invalid restore, revision overflow, blob collision fixture, digest/length/schema mismatch.
- Fault injection at every current/revision/blob/refcount/index/journal/commit step and reopen/invariant repair.
- Migration from current-edit-only with unchanged IDs/content; paging order/bounds/maximum history.
- Reachability/compaction success and all corruption/mismatch cases.
- Source-map mutation changing darktable history/current/snapshot/undo behavior without target owner.
- Architecture tests for no pruning/UI/redb leakage/domain serialization.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store edit_revisions` passes atomicity/concurrency/blob/paging/migration/compaction/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog verify-edit-history --concurrent-writers --faults all --reachability --migration` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 200` proves every listed darktable immutable-history/current/snapshot responsibility is accounted for.
- Every committed current change has exactly one revision; identical payloads share bytes; restore creates new revision; reopen reconciles current/revisions/blobs/indexes/journal; no pruning/UI is introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on #199, #285 for later UI only, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements immutable revision/blob domain/ports, atomic redb persistence/indexes/journal, migration/paging/reachability/compaction, source accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement user-facing undo/redo/history/snapshot UI, pruning policy, XMP writing, or distributed replication.
issues.title: Persist immutable edit revision history with content-addressed payloads

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Provide one storage-neutral checkpoint port and redb implementation that reconstructs every startup-relevant catalog record from one coherent read transaction: catalog header/revision, imports/sources/assets/photos/virtual copies/groups, current edits/revision metadata, source availability, content-addressed payloads, and opaque execution blockers. This replaces darktable’s startup-time mixture of long-lived SQLite handle, image cache, history loads, and per-module queries with one immutable validated application snapshot.

## Fixed decisions

- `CatalogCheckpointV1` is an immutable owned value; no redb transaction/guard/borrow/iterator/DTO escapes.
- One redb read transaction observes state before or after a concurrent commit, never a mix.
- Canonical order is stable IDs + explicit sequence/order fields, never redb iteration order.
- Every startup reference is resolved/validated before return. Any codec/index/relationship/limit/cancellation failure returns no partial checkpoint.
- Later table families extend via versioned optional sections with required/optional semantics and producer/schema identities; unknown required section blocks startup, unknown optional section is preserved/reportable.
- Checkpoint logical identity excludes timing/path/local cache state but includes catalog UUID/schema/revision, table/codec versions, authoritative record/content identities, and execution blockers.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.h/.c`: one attached library/data connection/transaction, schema/header/version, startup queries, table consistency, lock/open/close, and coherent state assumptions.
- `src/common/darktable.c/.h`: startup order creating database, image/mipmap caches, collection/selection, develop state, module registries, and global subsystem access to the same catalog generation.
- `src/common/image_cache.h/.c`: image-record materialization, read/write ownership, missing/corrupt row behavior, cache lifetime, and source/image fields required by consumers.
- `src/common/image.h/.c` and `src/common/film.h/.c`: image/film/source/virtual-copy/group records and relationships used at startup/library browsing.
- `src/common/history.h/.c` and `src/common/iop_order.h/.c`: current history endpoint, operation instances/order, history hash/payloads, and per-image reconstruction.
- `src/develop/masks/*` and `src/develop/blend.h/.c`: mask/blend/raster relationships required for current-edit execution status.
- `src/common/collection.h/.c`, `src/common/selection.h/.c`, `src/common/tags.h/.c`, `src/common/metadata.h/.c`, and ratings/color/group helpers: library/selection organization state eventually added as checkpoint sections.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h/.c`: startup/runtime image result cache is explicitly excluded from the durable checkpoint; only source/photo/edit identity needed to request previews belongs here.
- `src/develop/develop.c/.h`, `src/views/lighttable.c`, `src/views/darkroom.c`, `src/libs/filmstrip.c`: consumers requiring a coherent image/current-edit/catalog revision and rejecting stale/mixed state.

**Preserve:** one coherent catalog generation, complete image/source/virtual-copy/current-history relationships, current revision/payload identity, startup blockers, deterministic ordering, corruption/missing-reference detection, and no partially initialized library/develop state.

**Redesign:** replace on-demand per-module SQLite queries, global handle/cache borrows, mutable image/history structs, redb guard leakage, and accidental iteration order with one immutable versioned checkpoint, owned records, canonical relationship indexes, section descriptors, complete integrity validation, bounded receipt, and explicit optional extensions.

**Excluded:** in-memory domain restoration (#202), thumbnail/pixel caches, live query services, migration writes, UI, and processing execution.

Rust ownership: checkpoint domain/port in catalog; redb snapshot adapter in catalog-store; app/lifecycle receives one owned checkpoint.

## Implementation

- Define `CatalogCheckpointV1`, section descriptor/status, limits, catalog/checkpoint identity/hash, blockers, and `CatalogCheckpointRepository` port.
- In one read transaction, read authoritative header/tables, decode canonical DTOs, verify blob digest/schema/length, resolve imports/assets/photos/versions/groups/current edits/revisions/mask-blend refs/opaque blockers, and compare all startup-relevant secondary indexes against primaries.
- Bound records/relationships/aggregate encoded+decoded bytes/string/blob sizes/depth/wall time and check cancellation between bounded batches.
- Canonically sort records/relationships and compute checkpoint hash from schema/codec identities plus authoritative values.
- Emit privacy-safe receipt with UUID/schema/revision/section/table/record/index/blob/blocker counts, hash, and elapsed section times; no paths/metadata/payload values.
- Add source-accounting table mapping darktable startup DB/cache/image/history consumers to checkpoint fields or named later live/cache services.
- Add architecture checks preventing redb/postcard dependencies or adapter guards in domain/app consumers.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject bad header/schema/codec, missing/corrupt record/blob/index, digest/length mismatch, dangling/duplicate/cyclic refs, inconsistent current pointer/revision, unknown required section, limits/deadline/cancellation, and read transaction/storage failure.
- Concurrent writer before transaction start is fully observed; after start is fully excluded.
- Unknown optional sections are retained as bounded descriptors/artifact refs, not silently discarded.
- Failure publishes no checkpoint or partial section; all read guards/transactions close deterministically.

## Test matrix

- Empty/minimal/full catalogs, primary+sidecar, virtual copies/groups, current/opaque edits, revisions/blobs, optional/unknown sections.
- Concurrent commits immediately before/after/while snapshot transaction begins.
- Missing/corrupt primary/index/blob/header/codec, dangling refs/cycles, digest mismatch, oversized counts/bytes, timeout/cancel.
- Randomized physical table/index order and cross-process reopen yielding identical checkpoint/hash.
- Source-map mutation adding a darktable startup record dependency without checkpoint/later owner.
- Architecture tests for no redb/postcard/borrow leak.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store catalog_checkpoint` passes coherence/concurrency/integrity/limits/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog checkpoint-matrix --all-catalog-fixtures --concurrent-writer --corruptions all --verify-determinism` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 201` proves every listed darktable startup-catalog responsibility is accounted for.
- Checkpoint observes before/after commit only, resolves every startup reference, orders/hashes equal state identically, and returns no partial data on failure.

## Dependencies

Depends on #198, #199, #200, #197, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements checkpoint domain/port, redb read adapter, section/relationship/index validation, limits/cancellation, source accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement in-memory restoration, live queries, caches, processing, migration writes, or UI.
issues.title: Implement atomic catalog checkpoints spanning imports, assets, and edits

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Restore the complete owned in-memory RustTable catalog state and one immutable reader snapshot exclusively from a single #201 checkpoint. Startup, library browsing, edit resolution, and processing selection observe one catalog generation and perform no secondary redb, filesystem, configuration, decoder, or compatibility reads. This replaces darktable’s startup-time reconstruction through global SQLite handles, image/history caches, selection/collection state, and per-module queries with one validated immutable Rust state.

## Fixed decisions

- Restoration is a pure deterministic transformation from `CatalogCheckpointV1` to `CatalogState` plus `Arc<CatalogSnapshot>`; it performs zero I/O and spawns no work.
- Authoritative mutable state remains private behind catalog command services. Readers receive immutable snapshots carrying catalog UUID/schema/revision/checkpoint identity and blocker summary.
- Reconstruct all primary records and canonical reverse indexes before publishing: imports, source assets, photos/virtual copies/groups, current edits, immutable revisions, content blobs, source/content lookups, operation/mask/blend blockers, and optional checkpoint sections.
- Invalid relationships are typed errors. Opaque/non-executable edits remain visible with exact blocker/provenance; they are not dropped or defaulted.
- Canonical order is explicit: imports/photos by persisted order then stable ID; assets by role/ID; versions/group members by source order/ID; edits/revisions/operations by explicit sequence; no hash-map/redb/memory order.
- Restoration may use structural sharing internally but no returned snapshot exposes mutable containers or references checkpoint backing bytes.
- Equal checkpoint bytes/limits yield equal state/snapshot/hash independent of process, allocator, thread count, or insertion order.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/darktable.h` and `src/common/darktable.c`: global catalog/database/cache/collection/selection/develop ownership initialized at startup and shared throughout the process.
- `src/common/database.c/.h`: attached library/data connection and coherent startup generation from which images/history/tags/collections are loaded.
- `src/common/image_cache.h/.c`: materialization of `dt_image_t` records, read/write ownership, source/image field validation, and cache-based startup/runtime lookup.
- `src/common/image.h/.c` and `src/common/film.h/.c`: image/film/source/virtual-copy/group records and relationship reconstruction.
- `src/common/history.h/.c`, `src/common/iop_order.h/.c`, `src/develop/imageop.h/.c`, `src/develop/blend.h/.c`, and `src/develop/masks/*`: current history, operation instances/order, blend/mask relations, and executable/unavailable state reconstructed per image.
- `src/common/collection.h/.c`, `src/common/selection.h/.c`, `src/common/tags.h/.c`, `src/common/metadata.h/.c`, ratings/color/group helpers, and lighttable consumers: immutable reader projections and reverse indexes later extended as checkpoint sections.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h/.c`: result-cache state explicitly excluded; only identities required to request previews belong in the restored catalog snapshot.
- `src/develop/develop.c/.h`, `src/views/lighttable.c`, `src/views/darkroom.c`, and `src/libs/filmstrip.c`: consumers requiring one coherent image/current-edit/catalog generation and deterministic lookup/order.

**Preserve:** one coherent catalog generation, complete source/photo/version/group/current-edit/revision relationships, exact source ordering, blocker/opaque visibility, fast canonical lookups, deterministic startup state, and no partially initialized global state.

**Redesign:** replace global mutable structs, SQLite/cache borrows, lazy per-module joins, pointer indexes, and hidden iteration order with one pure checkpoint restoration, owned nominal maps/indexes, immutable `Arc` snapshot, complete invariant validation, canonical ordering/hash, and explicit blocker metadata.

**Excluded:** repository reads/writes, source probing, preview caches, live queries, migration execution, background jobs, UI wiring, and processing execution.

Rust ownership: restoration/domain in `rusttable-catalog`; application receives only the owned state/service and immutable snapshot.

## Implementation

- Define restoration request/limits/result, canonical indexes, blocker projection, memory estimate, snapshot identity, and typed failure/receipt.
- Rebuild every primary map and reverse index from checkpoint records; verify index cardinality/uniqueness against authoritative records.
- Validate catalog UUID/schema/revision, duplicate IDs/source keys/content identities, photo/import/asset ownership, role constraints, virtual-copy/group relationships, current-edit/base-photo/revision pointers, blob hashes/schema/length, mask/blend/operation refs, execution blockers, and optional section requirements.
- Build one immutable joined `CatalogSnapshot` with stable iterators/lookups and presentation/processing projections that retain no mutable/backing/checkpoint references.
- Implement structural-sharing strategy only after canonical correctness; report exact owned/estimated bytes and blocker counts.
- Add property tests comparing state built through public commands with state checkpointed then restored.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping each darktable startup/cache/collection/develop lookup responsibility to a restored field/index or named later live/cache service.
- Add architecture checks proving restoration performs no I/O and snapshot types expose no mutable/redb/postcard internals.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject duplicate/missing IDs/keys, dangling/cyclic group/version/edit/mask refs, role violations, current/revision mismatch, blob digest/schema/length mismatch, unknown required section, checkpoint hash mismatch, limits/overflow/cancellation token already cancelled, and memory-estimate overflow.
- Any failure returns no state/snapshot and leaves the previous application state unpublished.
- Unknown optional sections remain bounded descriptors/blockers as defined by #201; they are not silently discarded.
- Opaque edits/masks remain queryable but their executable projection is unavailable with exact source blocker codes.
- Equal checkpoints produce identical canonical snapshot even when checkpoint sections/records are permuted in test input.

## Test matrix

- Empty/minimal/full catalogs; primary+sidecar; virtual copies/groups; current/opaque edits; revision/blob chains; optional/unknown sections.
- Incremental public-command state versus checkpoint→restore equivalence.
- Reordered sections/records/maps; duplicate IDs/source/content; missing parents/assets/blobs; group cycles; role/virtual-copy/edit/revision mismatches; corrupt blocker refs.
- Maximum counts/bytes/depth/overflow and no partial publication.
- Immutability/ownership tests after checkpoint/backing values are dropped.
- Source-map mutations adding a darktable startup relationship/lookup without restored index or later owner.
- Architecture mutation adding repository/filesystem/config/decoder/background access or mutable snapshot exposure.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog catalog_restore` passes equivalence/invariant/order/ownership/limits/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog restore-matrix --all-checkpoints --permute-records --corruptions all --verify-no-io` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 202` proves every listed darktable startup-state responsibility is accounted for.
- Incremental and restored states have identical canonical snapshots/hashes; restoration performs zero external calls; failure publishes no partial state; readers cannot mutate authoritative collections.

## Dependencies

Depends on #201 and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements pure checkpoint restoration, indexes, immutable snapshot, invariant validation, source accounting, receipts, property/corruption tests, and architecture checks. Do not implement repository I/O, source probing, live queries, caches, processing, application startup wiring, or UI.
issues.title: Restore immutable in-memory catalog state from one checkpoint

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement verified user/CLI-invoked backup, verify, restore, and pre-migration snapshot services for RustTable redb catalogs. A backup captures one coherent catalog generation, reopens to the same #201 checkpoint identity, excludes source images/private machine state, and can replace a closed target atomically with a retained rollback copy. This replaces darktable’s SQLite backup-before-upgrade, recovery, lock, and database-copy workflow with an explicit redb-native archive contract.

## Fixed decisions

- A backup is either an uncompressed deterministic directory or `.rtbackup` ZIP containing exactly the catalog file, `manifest.json`, and `SHA256SUMS`. Source images, sidecars, thumbnails, caches, configuration, logs, credentials, WGPU caches, and temporary files are excluded.
- Consistency comes from a catalog maintenance/read lease and redb-compatible snapshot/copy primitive. Copying an actively changing catalog without coordination is prohibited.
- Manifest v1 records backup schema, catalog UUID/schema/revision/checkpoint hash, redb/codec versions, file size/SHA-256, creation time, source build/toolchain identity, compression mode, and optional pre-migration source/target schema intent. Paths are aliases only.
- ZIP entry order, names, permissions, compression parameters, and timestamps are deterministic. Duplicate, symlink, hard-link, device, absolute, parent-traversal, alternate-separator, and case-colliding entries are prohibited.
- Restore never overwrites an open catalog. It verifies/extracts into a temporary sibling, opens and validates the restored catalog read-only, computes #201 checkpoint identity, preserves an existing closed target as a rollback sibling, atomically promotes the verified replacement, fsyncs file/parent where supported, and removes rollback only after final reopen success.
- Newer unsupported writer schemas may be inspected/verified but not restored writable by an older build.
- #197 schema migration must call `create_pre_migration_backup` and verify its identity before any schema-changing write transaction. Savepoints do not replace this archive.
- No scheduled/automatic cloud backup, source asset backup, or background retention policy in this issue.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.h` and `src/common/database.c`: database filenames/locks, startup backup checks, backup-before-schema-upgrade behavior, upgrade failure/recovery paths, corruption/maintenance handling, database copy/rename/delete helpers, rollback state, and close/reopen verification.
- `_migrate_schema`, `_upgrade_library_schema_step`, `_upgrade_data_schema_step`, and the backup/upgrade call sites in `database.c`: source evidence that destructive/version-changing operations require a recoverable pre-change copy.
- `src/common/darktable.c/.h` and `src/main.c`: startup/open/lock/maintenance/upgrade prompts, application lifecycle ordering, and no-open-database replacement behavior.
- `src/common/file_location.c/.h`: database, backup, configuration, cache, and data path derivation; RustTable backup includes only the selected catalog.
- `src/control/conf.c/.h` and database backup/maintenance keys in `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`: backup naming/location/frequency/retention preferences. This issue fixes manual/pre-migration service behavior; scheduled policy remains later.
- Database maintenance/preferences/dialog call sites under `src/gui/*`, `src/libs/*`, and CLI helpers in `src/cli/*`: user-visible backup/restore/upgrade/error/rollback expectations later projected by RustTable UI/CLI.
- SQLite WAL/journal/companion handling in `database.c`: darktable backup consistency responsibility; RustTable redb backup uses one coordinated catalog file/snapshot instead of copying live companions.
- `src/common/image.c`, `history.c`, `styles.c`, `tags.c`, and other table writers: source mutations that make an uncoordinated file copy invalid.

**Preserve:** coherent backup generation, pre-upgrade recovery copy, exact catalog/schema/revision identity, verification before restore, lock/open-state safety, failure rollback, corruption detection, deterministic cleanup, explicit newer-schema rejection, and user-visible recovery evidence.

**Redesign:** replace SQLite file/WAL copying, backup suffix conventions, dialog-driven upgrade recovery, and loosely verified rename/delete flows with a versioned manifest/checksum archive, maintenance lease, redb checkpoint verification, hardened ZIP extraction, sibling staging, atomic promotion, retained rollback file, and privacy-safe receipts.

**Excluded:** source/image/sidecar backup, scheduled retention, cloud synchronization, automatic background backup UI, and target catalog schema migration logic beyond the required hook.

Rust ownership: backup/restore service in `rusttable-catalog-store` or a dedicated catalog-maintenance module; CLI wiring in `xtask`/application; domain/catalog APIs receive typed requests/results without filesystem internals.

## Implementation

- Define backup/restore/verify requests, archive mode, manifest/schema, catalog/checkpoint identity, maintenance lease, progress/cancellation, rollback state, receipts, and typed errors.
- Implement coordinated catalog snapshot/copy, streamed SHA-256, deterministic directory/ZIP generation, atomic archive publication, and verify-only reopening/checkpoint validation.
- Implement hardened archive reader with total/entry/ratio/path/name/count limits, duplicate/case-collision detection, regular-file-only policy, checksum/manifest/catalog-header validation, and no extraction outside the staging root.
- Implement restore preflight for target closed state, parent permissions, free space, schema/writer compatibility, rollback capacity, and conflicting temp/rollback files.
- Implement staged extraction/copy, read-only #197/#201 verification, target→rollback rename, staged→target promotion, fsync, final reopen/checkpoint comparison, rollback-on-any-failure, and idempotent cleanup/recovery after interruption.
- Add `create_pre_migration_backup` that requires expected current catalog/checkpoint/schema and returns a verified immutable backup identity consumed by #197.
- Add CLI commands `catalog backup`, `catalog backup verify`, and `catalog restore` with explicit paths and confirmation for replacement; no default destructive action.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable backup/upgrade/recovery responsibility to this service or a later scheduled-policy/UI owner.
- Add architecture checks preventing arbitrary catalog-file copy/rename/restore outside this service.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle open/locked target, concurrent writer/change during snapshot, catalog/checkpoint mismatch, bad manifest/schema/checksum, truncated/corrupt ZIP, zip bomb, duplicate/traversal/symlink/case-collision entries, newer/older incompatible schema, insufficient space, permission/read-only path, short write, fsync/rename failure, existing temp/rollback ambiguity, cancellation, crash at every replacement stage, and final reopen failure.
- Any verification failure occurs before target replacement.
- Restore failure leaves the original target usable or automatically restores the retained rollback. If rollback itself fails, both verified/rollback artifacts remain and the receipt gives exact manual recovery aliases; no file is silently deleted.
- Cancellation before promotion removes staging; cancellation after promotion completes verification/rollback decision rather than claiming rollback occurred.
- Backup/restore never follows attacker-replaced symlinks and never includes private source/config/log data.
- Equal catalog generation/archive mode produces byte-identical deterministic archive except the explicitly fixed/recorded creation-time policy; for strict reproducibility tests, creation time is injected/fixed.

## Test matrix

- Empty/minimal/large catalogs and every supported schema; uncompressed/ZIP; repeated deterministic backup; reopen/checkpoint equality.
- Concurrent writer, changed source during copy, cancellation at every stage, interrupted/crashed replacement recovery.
- Corrupt hash/manifest/header/catalog/blob/index, truncated/oversized/zip-bomb/duplicate/traversal/symlink/hard-link/device/case-collision archive entries.
- Newer read-only inspect/restore rejection; older supported restore then #197 migration.
- Insufficient space, read-only/permission/full disk, short write, sync/rename/replace failures, Windows rename semantics, rollback success/failure.
- Privacy inventory proving only catalog/manifest/checksums enter archive.
- Source-map mutation changing darktable backup/upgrade/recovery behavior without target owner.
- Architecture mutation adding direct catalog file copy/replace elsewhere.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store catalog_backup_restore` passes deterministic/archive/security/concurrency/crash/rollback/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog backup-matrix --all-catalog-fixtures --formats directory,zip --fault-every-boundary --verify-privacy` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 203` proves every listed darktable backup/upgrade/recovery responsibility is accounted for.
- Every backup reopens to the source checkpoint hash/revision; any byte/manifest/catalog mismatch is detected before replacement; restore failure preserves/recovers the original target; no source assets/private logs/config are included.

## Dependencies

Depends on #197, #201, #515, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements catalog snapshot/archive/verify/restore, pre-migration hook, hardened extraction, atomic rollback/recovery, CLI commands, source accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement scheduled retention UI, cloud backup, source-asset backup, or migration business logic.
issues.title: Implement verified catalog backup, restore, and migration snapshots

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Guarantee that at most one RustTable process owns writable access to one catalog while other processes receive explicit read-only/conflict status, with safe stale-record recovery after abrupt termination, PID reuse, path replacement, and unreliable filesystems. This replaces darktable’s database lockfile/open conflict behavior with cross-platform OS-lock authority and a versioned redb catalog lease.

## Fixed decisions

- Use adjacent `<catalog>.lock` held by an exclusive OS file lock for writer lifetime. The OS lock is authoritative; JSON PID/time text is diagnostics only.
- Lease v1 records catalog UUID/path fingerprint, process ID/start identity where available, boot/session nonce, executable/build/schema identity, acquired time, lock adapter/filesystem capability, and prior/next clean-shutdown generation. It excludes hostname/user/raw path.
- Read-only opens do not acquire/rewrite writer lease. Writable open requires exclusive lease, path/file identity revalidation, #197 header/integrity preflight, and no unresolved restore/migration state.
- Recovery only when the OS lock is obtainable. A stale text record is archived as bounded diagnostics and atomically replaced; an unavailable active lock is never deleted/overridden by age/PID.
- Network/unreliable filesystems are writable only after an explicit lock/exclusion capability probe passes; otherwise read-only with a typed finding.
- Acquisition/release integrate with #180 startup/shutdown and #203 restore so no process replaces/restores an open catalog.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.h` and `src/common/database.c`: database lockfile naming/creation, lock acquisition/conflict status, PID/lock metadata, stale-lock detection/recovery, library/data database relationship, startup open failure, cleanup/unlink, and read-only/maintenance behavior.
- `src/common/darktable.c/.h` and `src/main.c`: database initialization before service/UI startup, existing-process/locked-catalog diagnostics, clean shutdown, and no second writable process.
- `src/common/file_location.c/.h`: canonical database/lock path derivation and platform path semantics.
- platform-specific lock/file/process helpers referenced by `database.c` under Unix/Windows: advisory/exclusive lock and process existence behavior. RustTable uses dedicated safe platform adapters rather than copying PID checks.
- `src/gui/gtk.c`, database error/lock dialogs, and CLI startup paths under `src/cli/*`: user-visible active-owner/read-only/conflict/recovery behavior later projected through RustTable UI/CLI.
- database backup/restore/migration/maintenance call sites in `database.c`: operations prohibited while another writer owns the catalog and coordinated by #197/#203.
- `src/control/conf.c/.h` database path keys: resolved before lease acquisition; configuration does not become lock authority.

**Preserve:** one writer per catalog, conflict before state mutation, active lock never overridden by PID/age alone, stale recovery only after OS ownership disappears, safe database/lock path relationship, cleanup on normal/abrupt termination, and read-only inspection without changing lock state.

**Redesign:** replace darktable lockfile/PID/proc heuristics, unlink-based recovery, SQLite connection ownership, global lock state, and platform conditionals with OS-level advisory/exclusive file locks, versioned diagnostic lease record, process-start/session nonce, catalog/header identity revalidation, capability-tested filesystem policy, RAII ownership, and typed conflict/read-only statuses.

**Excluded:** general application single-instance IPC, remote/network catalog synchronization, catalog backup/restore implementation, schema migration, and user-facing conflict UI.

Rust ownership: lease domain/service in `rusttable-catalog-store`; small Unix/Windows adapters isolated behind safe interfaces; #180 owns lifetime.

## Implementation

- Define lease request/status/record/owner evidence/filesystem capability, read-only/writer lease handles, clean-shutdown marker, receipts, and typed errors.
- Implement Unix advisory lock and Windows file-lock/share-mode adapters without exposing handles; use close-on-exec/inheritance-safe flags and RAII release.
- Resolve/open lock/catalog parents without following unsafe symlinks; acquire lock; revalidate catalog path/file ID/UUID/header after acquisition; archive/replace stale record atomically.
- Implement read-only health inspection that does not write lease bytes/mtime and reports active/stale/unreadable/unsupported status safely.
- Probe filesystem lock exclusivity using two independent processes/handles plus rename/replace behavior; cache only for exact filesystem/device/session identity.
- Integrate with #180 startup rollback/shutdown marker, #197 migration maintenance lease, #203 backup/restore preflight, and #201 checkpoint open.
- Add multiprocess test helper for concurrent owners/abrupt exit/PID reuse/path replacement/unreliable lock behavior on all supported targets.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable database lock/open/recovery branch to RustTable status/action.
- Add architecture checks preventing writer catalog open/restore/migration outside the lease service.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle active writer, unavailable/unsupported lock, malformed/stale record, PID reuse, missing process-start identity, catalog/lock symlink or path replacement, catalog UUID/header change, permission/read-only filesystem, network filesystem, lock API failure, cancellation, startup rollback, abnormal exit, record write/sync/rename failure, and shutdown release failure.
- If the OS lock is held, no stale/PID/age evidence permits recovery.
- If record update fails after lock acquisition, release lock and return no writer lease unless policy explicitly allows an in-memory-only diagnostic record; initial policy requires durable record.
- Cancellation/drop releases the OS lock and cannot write a clean-shutdown marker unless full catalog shutdown completed.
- A renamed/replaced catalog after lock acquisition is detected before writer open and no state is mutated.
- Read-only status inspection never opens catalog writable or changes lock record.

## Test matrix

- Two/ten writer processes; writer+many readers; abrupt SIGKILL/termination; PID reuse simulation; stale/malformed/missing records; same PID/different start identity.
- Catalog/lock rename/replace/symlink/hard-link races, missing/changed UUID/header, same catalog through path aliases.
- Unix advisory lock and Windows sharing/file lock; process-handle inheritance/child exec; unreliable/network filesystem probe pass/fail.
- Permission/read-only/full disk/short write/sync/rename failure; cancellation/startup failure/normal shutdown/restore/migration conflicts.
- Read-only inspection byte/mtime invariance and privacy-safe receipts.
- Source-map mutation changing darktable lock/open/recovery behavior without target owner.
- Architecture mutation opening redb writable/restoring/migrating without lease.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store catalog_lease` passes multiprocess/platform/race/recovery/privacy/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog lease-matrix --multi-process --abrupt-owner --pid-reuse --path-races --filesystem-probe` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 204` proves every listed darktable lock/open/recovery responsibility is accounted for.
- Two processes never both receive writer ownership; active locks are never overridden by text; abrupt owner is recoverable only after OS release; read-only inspection leaves lease bytes/mtime unchanged.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #197, #201, #203, #515, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements lease/status domain, Unix/Windows adapters, stale recovery, filesystem probe, lifecycle/backup/migration integration, source accounting, multiprocess tests, receipts, and architecture checks. Do not implement general single-instance IPC, remote catalogs, or conflict UI.
issues.title: Implement single-writer catalog leases and stale-owner recovery

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Detect RustTable catalog corruption and derived-index drift before writable use, classify every finding as repairable or authoritative/blocking, rebuild only information provably derivable from verified primary records, and place damaged catalogs in explicit read-only quarantine until restore or a successful verified repair. This replaces darktable’s SQLite integrity/foreign-key/maintenance/repair and cache/index-rebuild behavior with a redb-native invariant registry and atomic shadow-index repair.

## Fixed decisions

- Authoritative records and immutable revision/blob payloads are never guessed, synthesized, truncated, or rewritten by automatic repair.
- Secondary indexes, cached counts, denormalized facets, search keys, refcounts that are exactly derivable, and maintenance metadata may be rebuilt from verified authoritative records.
- Verification modes:
  - `Quick` at every writable open: header/schema/migration state, table/codec registry, critical current-edit/blob/index relationships, lease/clean-shutdown state;
  - `Full` by explicit command and before restore promotion, migration, or repair;
  - `ForensicReadOnly` streams recoverable authoritative rows/findings without claiming normal catalog validity.
- A blocking finding prevents writable open and marks the catalog `QuarantinedReadOnly`. Quarantine is a typed health state plus sidecar report; no automatic rename/delete/move.
- Repair requires a verified #203 backup, exact catalog UUID/revision/checkpoint hash, immutable repair-plan hash, exclusive #204 writer lease, and no concurrent catalog change.
- Rebuild selected indexes/derived tables into shadow namespaces, verify complete equality/cardinality against authoritative records, switch atomically in one redb transaction, update maintenance generation, reopen, and rerun Full verification.
- Repair cannot alter payload checksums, missing/corrupt authoritative records, ambiguous relationships, unknown required codec/schema, or migration-state corruption except through #197’s dedicated recovery path.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.h` and `src/common/database.c`: SQLite `quick_check`/integrity/foreign-key checks, schema/version/object validation, corruption/error state, maintenance/optimize/vacuum/analyze, migration interruption, lock/open restrictions, backup-before-repair, and user-visible recovery decisions.
- database maintenance helpers and preference/config keys in `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`: automatic/manual maintenance frequency, optimize/vacuum, corruption/upgrade prompts, and startup gating. RustTable uses explicit verification/repair policy rather than copied frequency settings.
- `src/common/image_cache.h/.c`, `src/common/mipmap_cache.h/.c`, and `src/common/cache.h/.c`: derived cache/index invalidation/rebuild and distinction between authoritative database records and disposable cache data. Runtime pixel caches remain separate, but the repair principle is preserved.
- `src/common/image.c/.h`, `film.c/.h`, `history.c/.h`, `styles.c/.h`, `tags.c/.h`, `metadata.c/.h`, `collection.c/.h`, selection/rating/color/group helpers, `src/develop/masks/*`, and `src/gui/presets.c/.h`: table/index/refcount/relationship invariants that darktable expects when reading/writing catalog state.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: sidecar/database state divergence and history/metadata consistency; source sidecars are not repaired here but inconsistencies are reportable through source provenance.
- `src/common/darktable.c/.h` and `src/main.c`: database verification/maintenance before normal writable services and degraded/read-only startup behavior.
- database maintenance/preferences dialogs and CLI utilities under `src/gui/*`, `src/libs/*`, and `src/cli/*`: progress/cancellation/findings/repair-plan presentation later consumed by RustTable UI/CLI.
- `src/common/sqliteicu.c/.h` and SQLite index/query extensions: intentionally not repaired/recreated; equivalent RustTable derived indexes must be declared in the redb invariant registry.

**Preserve:** corruption detection before writable use, schema/object/relationship/index verification, distinction between authoritative data and rebuildable derivatives, backup before repair, explicit maintenance progress/cancellation, no silent data loss, atomic repair, reopen/reverify, and read-only recovery when authoritative corruption remains.

**Redesign:** replace SQLite pragma checks/vacuum/analyze, SQL index recreation, cache flushes, dialog-driven repair, and mutable database-global error state with a versioned redb invariant registry, streaming typed findings, repairability proof, content-addressed repair plan, shadow tables/indexes, one-transaction switch, health sidecar, and deterministic receipts.

**Excluded:** repairing darktable source databases, guessing payload content, schema migration recovery (#197), backup/restore implementation (#203), runtime pixel-cache repair, source image/sidecar modification, and user-facing maintenance UI.

Rust ownership: invariant/repair domain and redb implementation in `rusttable-catalog-store`; application/lifecycle consumes health state; CLI wiring in `xtask`/app.

## Implementation

- Define invariant IDs/versions/scopes/severity/repairability, finding/provenance, verification mode/progress/status, quarantine report, repair action/plan, shadow target, maintenance generation, receipt, and typed errors.
- Register invariants for:
  - header/schema/migration/lease/clean-shutdown state;
  - table/key/value codec identity and canonical key↔record IDs;
  - assets/sources/photos/imports/virtual copies/groups/current edits/revisions/blobs/masks/blends/styles/presets/metadata/tags/collections/opaque records;
  - parent/current/revision chains and blob digest/refcount/reachability;
  - every secondary index and denormalized count/facet/search structure;
  - source-provenance/backup/migration journal consistency.
- Implement bounded streaming Quick/Full/Forensic scans with deterministic record order, cancellation, progress, memory/time/finding limits, and no writable transaction.
- Produce repair plan only when every proposed target is exactly derivable and every blocker is absent; include expected primary/index counts, hashes, catalog identity, invariant versions, and required backup identity.
- Implement shadow rebuild, complete comparison, atomic swap/header update, rollback on failure, final reopen and Full verify.
- Add `catalog verify` and `catalog repair --plan <receipt>` commands with explicit confirmation, exact plan/catalog/backup validation, and no implicit repair.
- Add corruption/fault fixtures for every table/index/relationship/blob/header state and every repair boundary.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable integrity/maintenance/table/index expectation to a RustTable invariant or explicit nonapplicable disposition.
- Add architecture checks preventing direct index/table repair or quarantine bypass outside this service.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Detect malformed header/schema/codec/table/key/value, missing/extra/wrong index entries, duplicate IDs, dangling/cyclic refs, current/revision/blob/refcount/reachability mismatch, payload digest failure, partial migration, stale lease/unclean marker, unknown required extension, invariant version drift, limits/cancellation, read/storage failure.
- Repairable and blocking findings may coexist; any blocker prevents repair commit and writable open.
- Catalog revision/checkpoint change after plan creation invalidates the plan before any write.
- Backup missing/mismatched/unverified, shadow build failure, comparison mismatch, disk/full/commit/swap/header/reopen/final-verify/cancellation failure leaves original authoritative tables/index aliases and quarantine state consistent.
- A failed repair never deletes the backup or hides findings.
- Forensic mode may return bounded recoverable records/findings but cannot clear quarantine or feed normal writable startup.

## Test matrix

- Clean empty/minimal/full catalogs in all schema versions; Quick versus Full coverage.
- Corruption/inconsistency for every authoritative table, blob, current/revision relation, source/group/edit/mask/style/tag/collection ref, header, codec, journal, lease, and index.
- Missing/extra/wrong/duplicate index entries and rebuild of each repairable target.
- Mixed repairable+blocking findings; plan identity/revision/backup drift; concurrent writer/change; cancellation/limits.
- Fault injection at every shadow write/verify/swap/header/commit/reopen/final-verify boundary and rollback.
- Forensic read-only behavior, quarantine persistence/reopen, explicit restore recovery.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable table/index/integrity/maintenance responsibility without invariant/disposition owner.
- Architecture mutation writing/rebuilding indexes or bypassing quarantine elsewhere.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog-store catalog_integrity` passes invariant/corruption/repair/quarantine/fault/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog integrity-matrix --all-catalog-fixtures --corrupt-every-record-index --repairable-and-blocking --fault-every-boundary` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 205` proves every listed darktable integrity/maintenance responsibility has a RustTable invariant or explicit exclusion.
- Clean catalogs pass; every injected corruption is detected/classified; only derived data is repaired after verified backup; failed/cancelled repair preserves authoritative state and quarantine; writable open remains blocked until Full verification succeeds.

## Dependencies

Depends on #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements invariant registry, Quick/Full/Forensic verification, quarantine state/report, repair planning, atomic shadow-index/derived-table rebuild, CLI, source accounting, receipts, corruption/fault tests, and architecture checks. Do not repair authoritative payloads, implement restore/schema recovery, runtime caches, or maintenance UI.
issues.title: Implement catalog integrity verification, index repair, and corruption quarantine

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Define the canonical immutable photo-summary projection shared by catalog queries, collections, search, import results, lighttable/filmstrip/map candidates, scripting, and tests. Every summary is derived from one catalog checkpoint/snapshot generation and preserves enough darktable-equivalent identity and presentation state to avoid each caller rebuilding ad hoc joins.

## Fixed decisions

- `PhotoSummary` is an owned immutable domain value in `rusttable-catalog`; no redb/SQLite/path/parser/UI/decoder types.
- Stable fields:
  - photo ID and catalog revision;
  - primary asset/source ID and exact source-availability state;
  - current edit ID/revision/execution status and current-history blocker summary;
  - virtual-copy source/version/sibling count;
  - group ID/representative/member count;
  - safe display filename alias and optional film/source-root alias;
  - dimensions/orientation;
  - capture timestamp/source precision;
  - rating/rejected/color labels;
  - bounded camera/lens/focal/exposure/aperture/ISO facts;
  - bounded tag IDs/display paths/count;
  - geolocation-present flag, not coordinates by default;
  - metadata/history/style/source-changed/preview-availability summary flags;
  - thumbnail/preview content identities where available, never pixel bytes.
- Private source paths, GPS coordinates, free-form metadata, notes, tag private values, and operation parameters are absent from the summary.
- Summary schema/version and field-presence bits are explicit. Future optional fields do not change existing equality/ordering unless declared.
- Canonical ordering is not embedded in the summary; query result order is a separate typed sort key. Summary equality represents one photo projection at one catalog revision.
- Presentation text is validated/bounded and localization-ready but remains source/user data, not translated at storage/query time.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/image.h` and `src/common/image.c`: `dt_image_t` fields used throughout the UI—image/film/group IDs, filename, dimensions/orientation, capture/camera/lens/exposure data, flags/rating/reject/color, geolocation, virtual-copy version, source/missing state, and current history/hash.
- `src/common/film.h/.c`: film/source-root folder identity and display grouping.
- `src/common/collection.h/.c`: query projections, sort/filter fields, image IDs, grouped/ungrouped visibility, ratings/labels/tags/camera/date/history/source state, and canonical result ordering inputs.
- `src/common/selection.h/.c` and group/virtual-copy helpers in image/collection/lighttable code: selected/group representative/member/version relationships.
- `src/common/tags.h/.c`, `src/common/metadata.h/.c`, ratings/color-label helpers, and `src/common/geotagging.h`/location helpers: tag/metadata/label/GPS summary facts and privacy-sensitive fields.
- `src/common/history.h/.c`, `src/common/iop_order.h/.c`, and `src/develop/imageop.h/.c`: current history/edit identity, history state/hash, executable/unavailable operation state, and blocker facts.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h/.c`: thumbnail/preview availability/content identity and asynchronous cache state; pixel storage/cache policy remains separate.
- `src/views/lighttable.c`, `src/libs/filmstrip.c`, `src/libs/image.c`, `src/libs/tools/image_infos.c`, `src/libs/ratings.c`, `src/libs/colorlabels.c`, `src/libs/tagging.c`, and `src/views/map.c`: user-visible photo-card/filmstrip/info/rating/label/tag/map candidate fields consumed by later Iced views.
- Lua image/collection APIs under `src/lua/*`: bounded photo summary fields exposed to scripts; full metadata/source paths require separate permissions later.

**Preserve:** the stable photo/source/current-edit/version/group identity and all lighttable/filmstrip/filter presentation facts darktable users rely on, including missing-source/history/preview states, while keeping exact ordering and privacy boundaries explicit.

**Redesign:** replace repeated SQLite joins, mutable `dt_image_t` cache pointers, module-specific presentation structs, direct path/GPS/free-text exposure, and UI-side flag decoding with one immutable checked summary projection, safe aliases, nominal IDs, typed flags, field-presence/schema identity, and checkpoint-bound provenance.

**Excluded:** detailed metadata/EXIF, exact GPS coordinates, source paths, operation parameters, preview pixels, edit controls, collection filtering/sorting execution, and UI widgets. Later services request those through explicit ports/permissions.

Rust ownership: summary domain/projection in `rusttable-catalog`; query services return summaries; UI/scripts consume them without database joins.

## Implementation

- Define `PhotoSummaryV1`, source/edit/group/version/capture/camera/tag/preview/status subvalues, field-presence flags, presentation-text bounds, canonical codec/hash, and typed errors.
- Implement projection from #202 `CatalogSnapshot` records and optional organization/metadata/preview sections with one deterministic validation order.
- Validate IDs/revisions/ownership, source role/availability, dimensions/orientation, current edit/revision, virtual-copy/group relations, rating/reject/labels, numeric capture fields, tag ordering/count, safe aliases, and preview identities.
- Provide read-only accessors and query-sort key extractors without embedding locale collation.
- Add schema-versioned bounded JSON/test projection that redacts private values and uses stable aliases.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable image/card/filmstrip/info/collection field to a summary field, detailed-record owner, or explicit exclusion.
- Add architecture checks preventing query/UI/script crates from reconstructing alternative photo-card joins or importing database/image-cache types.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing primary asset/photo/edit, revision mismatch, invalid group/version relation, invalid dimensions/orientation/rating/label/capture values, unsafe/oversized display text, duplicate tags, malformed preview identity, unknown required field/schema, and aggregate limit overflow.
- Opaque/non-executable current edit remains a valid summary with explicit blocker status.
- Missing source/preview/metadata is represented as typed absence/status, not default success.
- Projection failure returns no partial summary for that record and is surfaced by the owning query with exact photo ID/finding.
- Equal snapshot/photo yields byte-identical summary independent of map/index iteration.

## Test matrix

- Minimal/full photo; missing/inaccessible/changed source; no/current/opaque edit; virtual copies/groups; ratings/reject/all labels; tags; capture fields; GPS-present; preview identities.
- Invalid/missing relationships, malformed text/values, duplicates, limits, unknown optional/required fields.
- Projection from incremental and restored snapshots; randomized insertion/index order.
- Privacy tests proving no raw path/GPS/free text/parameters/blob values in summary/Debug/JSON.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable photo-card/filter/info field without summary/detailed/exclusion owner.
- Architecture mutation rebuilding summaries in UI/query consumers or importing persistence types.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog photo_summary` passes projection/relationships/privacy/order/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog photo-summary-matrix --all-catalog-fixtures --verify-privacy --repeat 2` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 206` proves every listed darktable summary responsibility is accounted for.
- The same photo projects identically from incremental/restored catalog; all required lighttable/filter facts are available without I/O; private paths/GPS/text/parameters are absent.

## Dependencies

Depends on #202 and organization/metadata/preview section providers as optional inputs, plus #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the photo-summary domain, projection/validation, source accounting, privacy-safe codec/test output, architecture checks, and tests. Do not implement catalog query filtering/sorting, metadata detail APIs, preview generation, UI, or scripting permissions.
issues.title: Implement persistent photo groups and representative selection

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Define the storage-neutral metadata-domain model that represents the darktable/RustTable photographic and descriptive metadata needed by import, catalog queries, migration, editing, export policy, scripting, and UI without leaking EXIF/IPTC/XMP parser tags, SQLite/redb rows, source paths, native handles, or arbitrary unbounded bags.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-metadata`; `rusttable-core::ImageMetadata` compatibility surface is migrated/aliased rather than duplicated.
- Stable field identifiers cover:
  - camera make/model/unique model, lens, software;
  - capture datetime literal + parsed components + offset precision, orientation;
  - exposure time, aperture, ISO, focal length, focal-length-35mm, focus distance, flash, metering/exposure mode, white balance;
  - copyright, creator, publisher, title, description, rights/usage terms, rating/rejected/color labels where metadata-owned;
  - location name hierarchy plus latitude/longitude/elevation when permission/policy allows;
  - keyword/tag values with source namespace;
  - image unique ID, source metadata digest, embedded profile ID, and bounded extension records.
- Every value carries provenance: source kind (container EXIF/IPTC/XMP, sidecar, darktable DB, user edit, generated), source field/tag identity, source priority/conflict state, and revision.
- Text is UTF-8 checked, NFC-normalized for comparison, preserves original bytes/text identity where available, rejects controls/NUL, and has field-specific byte limits. Source literal and normalized display value are distinct.
- Numeric values use checked reduced rationals/fixed-width integers/finite coordinates. Exact source rational numerator/denominator and raw enum/integer are retained when needed.
- Metadata is a canonical ordered map from permanent `MetadataFieldId` to one value or a bounded ordered multivalue set. Duplicate/conflicting source values are represented explicitly; no last-wins in the domain.
- Privacy classes are fixed: Public, Personal, Location, DeviceIdentifier, Rights, Secret, Payload. Export/UI/script access is policy-mediated later.
- Unknown fields are preserved as bounded namespaced extension records with content hash/length and privacy class; arbitrary raw parser bags are not public API.
- The model defines no source precedence/merge/write policy beyond representing provenance/conflict. #242/#413 and migration issues choose policy.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/metadata.h` and `src/common/metadata.c`: darktable metadata key registry, type/flags/visibility/private/export/display/category semantics, image metadata get/set/clear/copy/list, database storage, import/export behavior, and user-facing names/descriptions.
- `src/common/exif.h` and `src/common/exif.cc`: EXIF/IPTC/XMP tag extraction and writing, capture/camera/lens/exposure/orientation/date/GPS/rights/title/description/keywords/profile/source fields, raw tag values, duplicate/conflict handling, sidecar/container precedence, and privacy-sensitive values.
- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for metadata definition/value tables (`data.meta_data`, `main.meta_data`/historical equivalents and indexes), image capture columns, and metadata-related schema changes.
- `src/common/image.h` and `src/common/image.c`: camera/lens/exposure/orientation/GPS/rating/label/source metadata cached on `dt_image_t`, and synchronization with metadata tables/sidecars.
- `src/common/tags.h/.c`: keyword/tag metadata overlap, export/private/internal tag semantics, synonyms, and XMP keyword mapping.
- `src/common/geotagging.h` and geolocation/location modules: coordinate/elevation/location-name metadata and privacy boundaries.
- `src/common/colorspaces.h/.c` and `src/common/iop_profile.h/.c`: embedded/input profile identity carried as metadata evidence; actual color transforms remain color issues.
- `src/gui/import_metadata.c/.h`, `src/libs/metadata.c`, `src/libs/metadata_view.c`, `src/libs/tagging.c`, `src/libs/location.c`, `src/libs/ratings.c`, and `src/libs/colorlabels.c`: import/edit/display field sets, multiple values, validation, privacy, and user-facing behavior later consumed by Iced issues.
- `src/imageio/imageio.c`, `src/common/metadata_export.c/.h`, and format/storage modules: metadata selection/redaction/export packets; policy/writing remains #413/#242–#246.
- Lua metadata/image APIs under `src/lua/*`: script-visible field/value/provenance responsibilities later permissioned by #422-related API issues.

**Preserve:** every photographic/descriptive/location/rights/tag/profile/source metadata field used by darktable, exact source value/provenance/conflict/multivalue state, raw rational/enum identity, user edits, private/export/internal semantics, unknown namespaced fields, and deterministic canonical ordering.

**Redesign:** replace integer metadata-key IDs tied to database rows, parser-native tag/value bags, mutable `dt_image_t` fields, implicit sidecar/container/database precedence, translated names as identity, and unclassified arbitrary strings with permanent Rust field IDs, checked nominal values, source provenance/revision, explicit conflicts/multivalues, privacy classes, extension records, and parser-neutral immutable values.

**Excluded:** container/XMP parsing, source precedence/merge, persistence repositories, metadata editing commands/UI, geolocation lookup, export redaction/serialization, and scripting permissions. Named issues consume this model.

Rust ownership: `crates/rusttable-metadata`; catalog/photo summaries reference bounded projections/IDs only.

## Implementation

- Define permanent metadata field/namespace/source/provenance/revision/privacy/value/conflict/multivalue/extension/schema types, checked text/rational/date/time/coordinate/location/tag/profile/source-ID values, canonical ordering/hash/DTO, and typed errors.
- Create a reviewed field registry generated from darktable metadata definitions/tag mappings plus RustTable-specific stable IDs; include source tag/key mappings and target privacy/default capabilities.
- Implement checked constructors, NFC/source literal handling, rational reduction without precision loss, date/time offset/precision representation, coordinate/elevation validation, duplicate/conflict aggregation, canonical multivalue ordering, and extension bounds.
- Add projections for safe `PhotoSummary` fields and full metadata detail without policy decisions.
- Implement canonical versioned DTO/encoding independent of Rust layout for catalog-store use later.
- Add schema diff/tag-reuse validation and architecture checks preventing parser/database/UI/native types or competing metadata domains in catalog/image crates.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable metadata definition/tag/image field/database column/UI consumer to a domain field, extension namespace, or explicit exclusion.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject duplicate/reused field IDs/tags, invalid text/control/NUL/UTF-8/length, zero denominator/overflow, invalid date/time/offset/precision, nonfinite/out-of-range coordinates/elevation, invalid enums/profile refs/source provenance, privacy downgrade, oversized multivalue/extension/schema, and canonical encoding overflow.
- Conflicting source values are valid domain state with explicit conflict; no constructor silently selects one.
- Unknown namespaced values remain extensions but cannot be treated as known export/script-safe fields.
- Equal logical metadata/provenance yields identical canonical bytes/hash independent of insertion/localization/process.

## Test matrix

- Every reviewed field/value/source/privacy class and boundaries.
- Camera/lens/exposure rational vectors, dates with/without timezone/subsecond, all orientations, GPS/elevation/location hierarchy, rights/text/tag/profile/source IDs.
- Duplicate equal values, conflicting container/sidecar/DB/user values, multivalue ordering, unknown extensions.
- Unicode/NFC/invalid UTF-8/control/oversized text, rational/date/coordinate/enum errors, privacy downgrade.
- Canonical encoding/hash/property/randomized insertion/cross-platform goldens.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable metadata key/tag/field/privacy/export/UI use without domain/disposition owner.
- Architecture mutation importing Exif/XMP/SQLite/redb/Iced/native types or adding duplicate metadata model.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata metadata_domain` passes field/value/provenance/conflict/privacy/canonical/source/architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask metadata domain-matrix --all-fields --all-sources --conflicts --verify-privacy --verify-canonical` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 207` proves every listed darktable metadata responsibility is accounted for.
- The model represents the complete reviewed darktable field set/provenance/conflicts without parser/storage types; equal metadata encodes identically; private/location/device values remain explicitly classified.

## Dependencies

Depends on #159, #178, #182, #206, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements metadata domain/field registry/value/provenance/conflict/privacy/canonical DTOs, source accounting, projections, architecture checks, and tests. Do not implement EXIF/IPTC/XMP parsing, persistence, merge policy, editing UI, geolocation lookup, export serialization, or scripting access.
issues.title: Implement virtual copies with shared assets and independent edit histories

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Persist canonical #207 metadata, source provenance, conflicts, multivalues, privacy, and user revisions transactionally in the native redb catalog. Metadata values and secondary indexes remain reconstructable and failure-atomic; unknown extension records survive without becoming known/exportable fields. This replaces darktable’s metadata-definition/value SQLite tables and mutable image-column synchronization with explicit typed records and indexes.

## Fixed decisions

- `rusttable-catalog` exposes storage-neutral metadata repository/query/command ports; `rusttable-catalog-store` owns redb DTOs/tables/indexes/transactions.
- Store metadata definitions by permanent #207 field ID/schema and values keyed by `(photo_id, field_id, provenance_id, value_id)` with exact canonical value bytes, source/provenance/revision/privacy/conflict/status.
- One authoritative record table plus reconstructable indexes:
  - photo→field→value IDs;
  - field→canonical value hash→photo IDs for approved queryable fields;
  - provenance/source→records;
  - conflict/privacy/status indexes;
  - normalized text/tag/date/rating facets only for fields whose registry declares index semantics.
- Private/Location/DeviceIdentifier fields are not placed in broad text/search indexes by default. Index policy is registry-versioned and explicit.
- Metadata writes use optimistic catalog/photo/metadata revision checks and atomically update records, indexes, photo summary revision, journal, and conflict state.
- Import/migration may write multiple source values. User edit creates a new user provenance/revision and does not erase source values unless an explicit command records suppression/tombstone.
- Unknown extension fields are stored with namespace/tag/content hash/bytes/privacy/source but cannot enter known-field indexes or be returned as known values.
- Canonical values are deduplicated only as storage blobs where byte/schema identity matches; record/provenance identity remains distinct.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for metadata definition/value tables, image capture/metadata columns, tag/metadata relationships, indexes, and data/library schema version changes.
- `src/common/metadata.h` and `src/common/metadata.c`: metadata key registry, database get/set/clear/copy/list, visible/private/export flags, value ordering, duplicate handling, selection-wide changes, and cache/image synchronization.
- `src/common/image.h` and `src/common/image.c`: capture/camera/lens/GPS/rating/label fields duplicated/cached in image rows and synchronized with metadata/table/XMP state.
- `src/common/tags.h/.c`: keyword/tag metadata storage and image assignment indexes; target tag persistence remains separate but metadata provenance must retain overlap.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: container/XMP metadata read/write and database precedence/synchronization; parsing/merge remains later.
