- `src/gui/import_metadata.c/.h`, `src/libs/metadata.c`, `src/libs/metadata_view.c`, `src/libs/tagging.c`, `src/libs/location.c`, and ratings/color-label modules: user-edit/import values, multiple selected images, validation, reset/delete, and privacy/export semantics later consumed by command/UI issues.
- `src/common/collection.h/.c`: metadata fields/facets used in collection queries and indexes; target query/index policy must be explicit.
- `src/common/metadata_export.c/.h` and `src/imageio/imageio.c`: exportable/private metadata selection; records preserve flags/provenance while export policy remains #413.
- Lua metadata APIs under `src/lua/*`: stored metadata access/update responsibilities later permissioned through scripting ports.

**Preserve:** every metadata definition/value/provenance/source/revision, duplicate/conflicting/multivalue state, user edits and source values, privacy/export/queryability semantics, image-summary synchronization, keyword/tag overlap, deterministic per-photo ordering, and atomic database/index updates.

**Redesign:** replace mutable SQLite key/value rows, integer DB-local key IDs, direct SQL callers, duplicated image columns, last-wins updates, broad metadata indexes, and parser/UI types with permanent field IDs, canonical value DTOs, explicit provenance/conflict/tombstones, privacy-aware index descriptors, optimistic transactional commands, append-like user revisions, and rebuildable redb indexes.

**Excluded:** EXIF/IPTC/XMP parsing/merge, metadata editor UI, target tag domain, geolocation lookup, export packet creation, and scripting permissions.

Rust ownership: metadata domain #207; commands/ports in catalog; redb schema/indexes in catalog-store.

## Implementation

- Define repository/query/command APIs: list photo metadata, get field/source/provenance, import source values, set/suppress/restore user value, clear user layer, compare-and-replace metadata revision, and page indexed queries.
- Define versioned metadata definition/value/provenance/conflict/tombstone/blob/index/journal DTOs and schema migration through #197.
- Validate field registry/schema, photo existence/revision, value/provenance/privacy/source/revision, canonical codec/hash, multivalue cardinality, conflict/suppression references, index eligibility, and limits before commit.
- Implement failure-atomic record/blob/index/photo-summary/journal updates and exactly one metadata revision increment per successful command.
- Implement canonical reconstruction from authoritative records, index verifier/rebuilder integration with #205, and checkpoint/photo-summary inclusion in #201/#202/#206.
- Add read APIs returning owned #207 values and privacy-filtered projections; no redb guards/DTOs.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable metadata table/index/write/synchronization responsibility to target records/indexes or named later tag/export/parser owners.
- Emit privacy-safe receipts with IDs/field IDs/counts/hashes/privacy/status/revisions, not values/paths/GPS/text.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown/reused field schema, missing photo, photo/metadata revision conflict, invalid value/provenance/privacy, duplicate record ID, conflicting tombstone/suppression, disallowed index, codec/hash/length mismatch, index conflict, limits, cancellation, storage/commit failure.
- One concurrent writer commits; others receive authoritative revision and no partial records/indexes.
- Source import never erases user/source values. User suppression references exact values and can be reversed.
- Corrupt/missing canonical blob/record/index causes typed quarantine/integrity finding; no neighboring value substitution.
- Unknown extension fields remain queryable by namespace/source only under explicit permission, not broad text/facet indexes.
- Failed migration/write leaves prior metadata/photo-summary/index/journal unchanged.

## Test matrix

- Every field/value/source/privacy class; single/multivalue/conflicts; import then user set/suppress/restore/clear; unknown extensions.
- Optimistic concurrent edits, photo revision conflicts, duplicate records, invalid source/provenance/privacy/index eligibility.
- Reopen/checkpoint/restore/photo summary/query ordering and identical canonical values with distinct provenance.
- Fault injection at record/blob/index/summary/journal/commit/migration boundaries; index rebuild/corruption.
- Privacy tests proving sensitive values absent from receipts/broad indexes.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable metadata table/index/write behavior without target owner.
- Architecture tests for no redb/DTO/parser/UI types in domain.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store metadata_persistence` passes conflict/provenance/index/fault/migration/privacy/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog metadata-matrix --all-fields --conflicts --concurrent-writers --faults all --verify-indexes --verify-privacy` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 208` proves every listed darktable metadata persistence responsibility is accounted for.
- Reopen reconstructs byte-equivalent #207 values/provenance/conflicts; one concurrent writer commits; failed writes leave records/indexes/summary/journal intact; private/location/device values are not broadly indexed/logged.

## Dependencies

Depends on #197, #201, #202, #206, #207, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements metadata repository/commands, redb tables/indexes/migration, optimistic transactions, checkpoint/summary integration, source accounting, receipts, fault/index/privacy tests. Do not parse/merge XMP/EXIF/IPTC, implement tag persistence, metadata UI, geolocation lookup, export serialization, or scripting access.
issues.title: Implement source, content, and probable-photo duplicate detection

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the native hierarchical tag domain and redb persistence used by catalog queries, metadata, import/migration, export, scripting, and later Iced tagging UI. Tags have stable IDs, explicit parent/category/synonym/privacy/export semantics, deterministic sibling order, atomic assignments, and rebuildable indexes. This replaces darktable’s SQLite tag tree, pipe-delimited name identity, mutable global tag APIs, and selection-driven assignment updates.

## Fixed decisions

- Tag identity is a stable UUID independent of display name/path. Duplicate display names are allowed under different parents and may coexist under one parent only when their canonical source identity differs and the command explicitly permits an import conflict record; normal user create rejects sibling canonical duplicates.
- A tag stores ID, optional parent, display name, canonical comparison key, category flag, internal/system/private/export flags, optional description, ordered synonyms, source/provenance, revision, and extension records.
- Hierarchy is an explicit acyclic parent graph. Canonical display path is derived from ancestor names; source darktable literal path components remain migration provenance, not identity.
- Assignments are exact `(photo_id, tag_id)` records with source/provenance/revision and optional source order. Duplicate assignments are idempotent only when all semantic fields match; conflicting duplicates remain findings during migration.
- Tag and assignment commands use optimistic catalog/tag/photo revisions and update authoritative records, parent/child/name/synonym/assignment/photo/tag-count/query indexes, journal, and photo summary atomically.
- Internal/system/private tags are excluded from public/export/script projections unless explicit policy grants them. Privacy classification is inherited by assignment projections.
- Synonyms are aliases for search/display only; they do not create hidden duplicate tag IDs or implicit assignments.
- Delete requires explicit policy: reject when children/assignments exist by default; optional `ReparentChildrenAndRemoveAssignments` is one atomic audited command. No cascade by accident.
- Move/rename revalidates descendant paths/cycles/name collisions atomically and preserves ID/assignments.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for `data.tags`, `main.tagged_images`, tag/name/count/assignment indexes, tag flags/synonyms/categories/internal/private fields, and historical tag representation changes.
- `src/common/tags.h` and `src/common/tags.c`: tag IDs/names/hierarchy separator/path tree, category/internal/private/export flags, synonyms, create/delete/rename/attach/detach, image assignment, counts, import/export, suggestion/search, and database transaction behavior.
- `src/common/metadata.h/.c` and XMP keyword/tag integration in `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: metadata keyword overlap, private/internal/export filtering, XMP hierarchical subjects, and source provenance. Packet parsing/writing remains separate.
- `src/libs/tagging.c`: hierarchical tag tree/search/suggestions, attach/detach/create/delete/rename/copy/export/private/category/synonym actions, selected-image batch behavior, and UI state later migrated to Iced.
- `src/common/collection.h/.c` and `src/libs/collect.c`: tag hierarchy/assignment filtering and collection query semantics; target query service remains #212–#215.
- `src/common/image.h/.c`, `src/common/selection.h/.c`, and lighttable/filmstrip callers: assignment effects on selected photos, photo summaries, grouping/virtual copies, and collection refresh.
- `src/common/metadata_export.c/.h` and `src/imageio/imageio.c`: public/private/internal tag selection and hierarchy export policy; target export remains #413.
- Lua tag/image APIs under `src/lua/*`: tag listing/creation/assignment and permission surface later routed through scripting APIs.
- darktable tag import/export data/files and built-in/system tag creation call sites: source records migrated through #189/#193.

**Preserve:** stable source tag/assignment relationships, hierarchy/category/internal/private/export flags, synonyms, duplicate/conflicting source rows, deterministic child/assignment order, atomic attach/detach/tree changes, tag counts/search/filter/export visibility, selected-image effects, and unknown extension provenance.

**Redesign:** replace database-local integer IDs, pipe-delimited full-name identity, mutable global tag tree/cache, direct SQL callers, translated/display names as identity, implicit cascade, and selection-coupled updates with stable UUIDs, explicit parent edges, checked names/canonical keys, typed flags/privacy, transactional commands, rebuildable indexes, journal events, and permission-filtered projections.

**Excluded:** tagging UI, metadata merge/XMP parsing/writing, collection evaluation, export packet policy, scripting permission implementation, and automated AI tagging.

Rust ownership: tag domain/commands/ports in `rusttable-catalog`; redb records/indexes in `rusttable-catalog-store`; UI/export/scripts consume ports/projections.

## Implementation

- Define `TagId`, checked tag name/description/synonym, flags/privacy/source/provenance/revision, tag/assignment records, tree/query projections, commands/results/errors, canonical DTOs, and receipts.
- Add redb authoritative tables/indexes:
  - tag ID→record;
  - parent→ordered child IDs;
  - parent+canonical name→tag IDs;
  - synonym canonical key→tag IDs;
  - photo→ordered assigned tag IDs;
  - tag→ordered photo IDs;
  - category/private/export/internal facets and derived counts.
- Implement create/rename/move/set flags/set synonyms/delete/attach/detach/batch attach/detach through optimistic atomic transactions with journal/photo-summary revision updates.
- Validate parent existence/cycles/depth, name/synonym bounds/canonical collisions, reserved system/internal namespaces, privacy/export conflicts, assignment photo/tag existence, revisions, batch limits, and index uniqueness.
- Implement canonical tree/list/search/assignment queries returning owned values with privacy filter and pagination; no redb guards.
- Integrate #189 compatibility→target mapping using deterministic #193 IDs and preservation of duplicate/invalid source records as migration findings/opaque records rather than corrupting active tree.
- Add #205 invariant/index rebuild and #201/#202/#206 checkpoint/summary integration.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable tag table/field/API/action/query/export/script responsibility to this issue or a named later UI/export/script owner.
- Add architecture checks preventing alternative tag domains/direct redb access/pipe-delimited identity logic in consumers.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing/duplicate IDs, parent cycles/self/depth overflow, empty/control/oversized names/synonyms/descriptions, canonical sibling/synonym collisions, reserved namespace/flag/privacy conflicts, missing photo/tag, revision conflict, duplicate/conflicting assignment, unsafe delete/move, index/codec/storage/cancellation failure.
- One concurrent command commits; losers receive current revision/state and no partial record/index changes.
- Batch commands are all-or-nothing and bounded; one invalid photo/tag rejects entire requested transaction unless a later explicit best-effort API is introduced.
- Unknown imported flags/extensions remain provenance/opaque and cannot silently become public/exportable/system behavior.
- Failed migration/write leaves tags, assignments, indexes, counts, journal, and photo summaries unchanged.

## Test matrix

- Root/nested/category/internal/private/export/system tags; Unicode/NFC/case comparison; same leaf under different parents; synonyms/search/path derivation.
- Create/rename/move/cycle/collision/delete policies and descendant path updates.
- Single/batch/idempotent/conflicting attach/detach; multiple photos/virtual copies/groups; concurrent writers/revisions.
- Migration of duplicates/orphans/cycles/unknown flags from #189 with deterministic IDs/findings.
- Reopen/checkpoint/photo-summary/collection index and #205 rebuild/invariant verification.
- Fault injection at every record/index/count/journal/summary/commit/migration boundary and cancellation.
- Privacy/export/internal projection tests and no secret/private tag leakage in receipts.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing darktable tag field/action/query/export/script behavior without owner.
- Architecture mutation adding pipe-name identity/direct redb/parallel tag model.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store tags` passes hierarchy/commands/concurrency/index/migration/privacy/fault/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog tag-matrix --tree-operations --batch-assignments --migration-conflicts --faults all --verify-indexes` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 209` proves every listed darktable tag responsibility is accounted for.
- Reopen reconstructs the same tree/assignments; one concurrent writer commits; cycles/collisions/cascade accidents cannot occur; private/internal flags govern projections; failed commands leave every index/summary/journal unchanged.

## Dependencies

Depends on #189, #193, #197, #201, #202, #205, #206, #208, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements tag domain, redb tables/indexes/migration, transactional commands/queries, compatibility projection, checkpoint/summary/invariant integration, source accounting, receipts, fault/concurrency tests, and architecture checks. Do not implement tagging UI, XMP/metadata merge, collection evaluation, export packets, or scripting permissions.
issues.title: Implement ratings, rejection, and color-label commands and indexes

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the native RustTable photo-group and virtual-copy relationship domain, persistence, commands, and query projections. Groups have stable IDs, one explicit representative, deterministic member order, transactional membership changes, and clear behavior for virtual copies and source sharing. This replaces darktable’s `images.group_id`/representative conventions and global grouping helpers with an explicit auditable model.

## Fixed decisions

- A photo may belong to at most one group. A group contains at least one photo and exactly one representative that is a current member.
- Group identity is a stable UUID independent of representative/member/source IDs.
- Membership order is explicit and persisted. Default order on creation is caller order; migrated darktable groups preserve source order evidence, then stable photo ID tie-break.
- Virtual copies may be grouped with their original/siblings or independently, but their shared source relationship remains separate from group membership.
- Creating a virtual copy does not automatically group it unless the command/policy explicitly requests `JoinSourceGroup`.
- Removing the representative requires an explicit replacement in the same transaction; deleting the last member deletes the group.
- Moving a photo between groups is one atomic command that validates source/virtual-copy and representative effects before commit.
- Grouping is presentation/selection/collection state. It does not change edit/source/content identity and is excluded from pixel cache keys.
- Commands use optimistic catalog/photo/group revisions and atomically update authoritative group/member records, photo membership fields, representative/member indexes, collection facets, journal, and #206 summaries.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/database.c` schema/migrations for `main.images.group_id`, group/version-related indexes, virtual-copy version/max-version columns, and grouping-state migrations.
- `src/common/image.h` and `src/common/image.c`: group ID/representative/member helpers, group add/remove/change representative, virtual-copy/original relationships, image flag/state updates, and database writes.
- group-related helper source under `src/common/grouping.*` if present in the pinned tree, plus grouping calls in `image.c`, collection, selection, and views. #555 must enumerate the exact present file/symbol set rather than relying on a guessed filename.
- `src/common/collection.h/.c`: grouped-versus-ungrouped query behavior, representative filtering, group expansion/collapse, sort/filter interaction, and collection refresh.
- `src/common/selection.h/.c` and `src/libs/select.c`: selection of group representatives/members, group expansion effects, batch actions, and current selection consistency.
- `src/views/lighttable.c`, `src/libs/filmstrip.c`, `src/libs/image.c`, and grouping actions in lighttable/darkroom: representative card/filmstrip behavior, expand/collapse, membership/ungroup/change-representative actions, and virtual-copy interaction later migrated to Iced.
- `src/libs/duplicate.c`: virtual-copy creation and optional group/source sibling behavior.
- `src/common/history.h/.c` and `src/common/image.c`: virtual copies with independent history/current edit while sharing original source identity.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: group/virtual-copy sidecar interactions where source metadata/history representation overlaps; sidecar behavior remains separate.
- Lua image/group APIs under `src/lua/*`: group membership/representative exposure later permissioned by scripting issues.

**Preserve:** explicit group membership and representative, grouped/ungrouped collection behavior, deterministic member visibility/order, virtual-copy/source independence, atomic group actions, selection/filmstrip/lighttable refresh, migration of malformed/ambiguous source groups as findings, and no pixel-impacting semantics.

**Redesign:** replace overloading one `group_id` image column and representative conventions, mutable global collection/selection state, implicit virtual-copy grouping, direct SQL updates, and UI-side group logic with stable group UUIDs, explicit representative/member records/order, optimistic transactional commands, reconstructable indexes, typed collection projection, and semantic Iced actions.

**Excluded:** group UI, collection filter execution, virtual-copy creation itself, source sharing, edit history, XMP writing, and scripting permissions. Named issues consume this domain.

Rust ownership: group domain/commands/ports in `rusttable-catalog`; redb records/indexes in `rusttable-catalog-store`; UI/query/scripts consume owned projections.

## Implementation

- Define `PhotoGroupId`, group/member/order/representative/revision/provenance records, create/add/remove/move/reorder/set-representative/delete commands, results/errors, query projections, DTOs, and receipts.
- Add redb authoritative tables/indexes:
  - group ID→record;
  - photo ID→group ID;
  - group ID→ordered member IDs;
  - representative photo ID→group ID;
  - source/original/virtual-copy→group relation facets for migration/query validation;
  - grouped/ungrouped/expanded collection facets as derived target indexes where appropriate.
- Implement optimistic atomic commands with photo/group revision validation, membership/representative/order invariants, journal events, #206 summary updates, and collection invalidation tokens.
- Integrate #184/#189/#193 compatibility migration: deterministic target group IDs, preserved source representative/member/order evidence, and blocking findings for cycles/multiple representatives/missing members/cross-group conflicts.
- Implement owned paginated group/member queries and summary projection without redb guards.
- Add #201/#202 checkpoint, #205 invariant/index rebuild, and catalog journal integration.
- Add source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable group/representative/virtual-copy action/query/UI/script responsibility to this issue or a named later owner.
- Add architecture checks preventing direct group-field mutation or alternate grouping models in UI/query consumers.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing/duplicate group/photo IDs, photo already in another group, empty group, representative not member, remove representative without replacement, duplicate member/order, invalid move/reorder, revision conflict, source/virtual-copy inconsistency, index/codec/storage/cancellation failure.
- One concurrent group command commits; losers receive current group/photo revisions and no partial membership/index changes.
- Deleting/ungrouping never deletes photos/edits/sources.
- Migrated malformed source groups remain compatibility findings and are not silently normalized into valid active groups without an explicit transformation in #193.
- Failed commands/migrations leave all group/photo/index/summary/journal state unchanged.

## Test matrix

- Create/single/multi groups; add/remove/move/reorder/set representative/delete; original/virtual copies; shared source across groups.
- Remove last member, representative replacement, duplicate/missing members, photo in two groups, invalid order, revision conflicts, many concurrent writers.
- Migration of valid/malformed darktable groups: missing/multiple representative, duplicate/cyclic/cross-group membership, randomized source order.
- Reopen/checkpoint/restore/summary/collection projections and #205 index rebuild.
- Fault injection at every record/member/index/photo/summary/journal/commit/migration boundary and cancellation.
- Source-map mutation changing darktable group/selection/collection/virtual-copy behavior without target owner.
- Architecture mutation directly changing photo group fields or defining grouping in UI.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-catalog -p rusttable-catalog-store photo_groups` passes commands/concurrency/migration/index/fault/source suites.
- `cargo xtask catalog group-matrix --all-commands --virtual-copies --concurrent-writers --migration-conflicts --faults all --verify-indexes` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 210` proves every listed darktable group/representative responsibility is accounted for.
- Every active group has exactly one representative/member relation after reopen; one concurrent writer commits; group changes never alter edit/source/pixel identity; failed commands leave all state intact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #184, #189, #193, #197, #201, #202, #205, #206, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements group domain, redb tables/indexes/migration, transactional commands/queries, checkpoint/summary/invariant integration, source accounting, receipts, concurrency/fault tests, and architecture checks. Do not implement group UI, collection evaluation, virtual-copy creation, XMP writing, or scripting permissions.
issues.title: Implement hierarchical tags, aliases, and atomic photo assignments

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement persistent, typed, revisioned photo metadata for fields users organize and export, while preserving embedded/sidecar/import provenance and separating catalog edits from later file writeback.

## Fixed decisions

- Supported editable fields are title, description, creator, publisher, rights, license text/URL, location name, city, state/province, country, country code, and up to five user-defined text fields.
- Capture/device values such as maker, model, lens, exposure, aperture, ISO, focal length, dimensions, orientation, and timestamps are read-only probe metadata in this domain.
- Each value stores origin (`Embedded`, `Sidecar`, `ImportedCatalog`, `User`), source revision/hash, and last catalog revision. User values override imported values for presentation/export but do not delete lower-priority provenance.
- Empty, missing, and explicitly cleared are distinct states. Strings use UTF-8, preserve line breaks where allowed, reject NUL/control characters, and have per-field byte limits.

## Required implementation

- Define field IDs/descriptors, typed values, provenance stack, effective-value resolution, metadata revision, repository/query ports, and commands for set/clear/restore-origin/batch-apply.
- Persist authoritative values/provenance plus field/effective-value search indexes transactionally in redb; extend checkpoint, integrity, migration, deletion, and event schemas.
- Validate supported field/type, text/URL/country-code limits, photo/revision existence, bounded batch IDs, and no-op policy.
- Import relevant [0028]/[0033] catalog values and later image metadata without conflating identical text from distinct origins.
- Provide canonical export projection that returns effective values and provenance, but does not write files.
- Add property/concurrency/reopen tests for precedence, explicit clear, duplicate origins, multiline/Unicode, invalid URL/code/control characters, batch rollback, virtual copies, imported conflicts, index failure, and deterministic projection.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Effective metadata follows the fixed precedence while retaining every origin record.
- [ ] Batch edits and indexes commit atomically.
- [ ] Missing, empty, and cleared survive exact persistence/reopen.
- [ ] Read-only technical metadata cannot be edited through these commands.
- [ ] XMP/file writeback and metadata UI are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #184, #189, #205, #207, #209, and #210.

## One-PR boundary

Only metadata domain/descriptors/commands, redb persistence/indexes/migration, projections, hooks, and tests.
issues.title: Implement typed catalog metadata fields, provenance, and batch editing

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement RustTable’s authoritative typed collection/filter language and pure evaluator over immutable catalog snapshots, replacing ad hoc filters and providing the target for migrated darktable collection rules.

## Fixed decisions

- The AST supports boolean `All/Any/Not`, equality/ordering/range, string contains/prefix/suffix/glob, missing/present, set membership, tag ancestor/descendant, date intervals, geospatial box/radius, source availability, edit executability, group/copy state, and duplicate class.
- Supported fields are declared in a registry with value type, allowed operators, index owner, null semantics, and stable ID. Arbitrary SQL, regex, scripts, and UI callbacks are forbidden.
- Evaluation uses three-valued internal logic (`True`, `False`, `Missing`) with operator-specific missing behavior; top-level `Missing` is false unless the rule explicitly tests missing.
- Sort keys are explicit, stable, locale-independent domain values with `PhotoId` as final tie-breaker. Pagination uses an opaque cursor containing query hash, catalog revision, and last sort tuple.

## Required implementation

- Define AST/literal/sort/page types, field registry, validation/normalization, canonical hash/serialization, complexity limits, and explanatory validation errors.
- Implement a pure snapshot evaluator and query planner interface that can select direct scan or registered indexes without changing semantics.
- Add field adapters for current catalog data: IDs/source/folder, capture/import values, camera/lens/exposure, rating/reject/labels, tags, metadata, groups/copies, missing assets, edit status, and duplicate evidence.
- Prove indexed results equal direct evaluation with property-generated catalogs/rules.
- Add fixtures for every field/operator, nested logic, missing values, Unicode/globs, dates, geospatial boundaries, stable pagination, changed catalog revision, invalid cursors, excessive complexity, and migrated [0034] AST conversion.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Valid rules have deterministic canonical bytes/hash and evaluation.
- [ ] Indexed and direct results/order/pagination are identical.
- [ ] Invalid field/operator/type combinations fail before evaluation.
- [ ] Cursor reuse against a different query or catalog revision is rejected.
- [ ] No SQL/string-expression execution is introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on #190 and #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211.

## One-PR boundary

Only collection AST/registry/validation/evaluator/planner interface, current field adapters, conversion, property tests, and serialization. Saved collections/UI are later.
issues.title: Implement the typed collection query AST and deterministic evaluator

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Persist named saved collections plus bounded recent/active collection state using the canonical [0056] AST, sort, grouping, and pagination-independent view definition.

## Fixed decisions

- A saved collection has UUID identity, name, optional description, canonical query AST, sort keys, group-collapse policy, revision, and provenance. Duplicate names are allowed.
- Recent queries are content-addressed by canonical query+sort hash, ordered by last use, and capped at 50. Reusing an identical query updates its timestamp/revision rather than adding a duplicate.
- Active view stores only collection ID or inline query/sort/group definition plus selection anchor; it never stores a page cursor tied to an old catalog revision.
- Invalid imported darktable collection rules may be stored as opaque migration records but cannot become active executable queries.

## Required implementation

- Define saved/recent/active domain records, repository ports, and commands for create, update with expected revision, rename, delete, duplicate, mark recent, and set active.
- Persist authoritative records plus normalized-name, recent-order, query-hash, and active-state indexes transactionally in redb; extend schema/checkpoints/integrity/events.
- Validate query through [0056], name/text limits, duplicate IDs, stale revisions, missing collection references, opaque status, recent cap/eviction, and no-op behavior.
- Convert supported [0034] collections through [0056] with deterministic migration IDs/provenance.
- Add concurrency/property/reopen tests for duplicate names, revisions, recent dedup/eviction, active saved/inline rules, deleted active collection fallback, opaque imports, index failure, migration, and stable ordering.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Saved/recent/active definitions reopen with exact canonical query hashes.
- [ ] Recent history never exceeds 50 and has deterministic eviction.
- [ ] Stale updates and invalid/opaque active rules cannot partially commit.
- [ ] Deleting the active saved collection switches to the canonical `AllPhotos` inline view atomically.
- [ ] Collection-builder UI is excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #190, #205, and #212.

## One-PR boundary

Only saved/recent/active collection domain, redb persistence/indexes/migration, checkpoint/integrity/events integration, and tests.
issues.title: Persist saved collections, recent queries, and active library view state

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement a rebuildable redb-backed full-text index for filenames, folders, editable metadata, tags/aliases, camera, and lens fields, with deterministic parsing, ranking, paging, and collection integration.

## Fixed decisions

- Do not add SQLite FTS or an external search service. Store a derived inverted index in redb using composite token/photo keys, per-field term frequencies, document lengths, and field-presence records.
- Tokenization uses Unicode word boundaries, NFC normalization, case folding, diacritic-preserving terms plus a secondary diacritic-folded term. Stop words are not removed.
- Query syntax supports terms, quoted phrases, field qualifiers, required/excluded terms, and final-token prefix (`foo*`). Regex, fuzzy edit-distance, arbitrary wildcards, and user-supplied scoring scripts are unsupported.
- Rank with versioned BM25 using fixed `k1=1.2`, `b=0.75`, field boosts: title 4, filename 3, tags 3, description 2, creator/location 2, folder/camera/lens 1. `PhotoId` is the final tie-breaker.

## Required implementation

- Define text-field registry, tokenizer/query AST, index revision/version, search result/explanation, stable cursor, and query limits.
- Build/update index entries transactionally from authoritative photo/tag/metadata snapshots; delete stale postings on field changes/deletion.
- Implement phrase-position verification, field filtering, exclusions, prefix range scan, BM25 statistics/ranking, and deterministic paging tied to catalog/index revision.
- Expose a [0056] `TextSearch` predicate whose candidate IDs and order match direct search results.
- Add full index rebuild into [0049] repair and verify posting/document/statistics consistency.
- Add fixtures/property tests for Unicode, combining marks, diacritics, punctuation, paths, phrases, prefixes, exclusions, duplicate terms, missing fields, ranking ties, 100k synthetic photos, updates/deletes, stale cursors, corruption/rebuild, and direct-vs-index equivalence.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Search results/ranking are deterministic across platforms and reopen.
- [ ] Incremental updates equal a clean rebuilt index.
- [ ] Invalid/over-complex queries fail before index traversal.
- [ ] Search indexes are fully derivable and never authoritative.
- [ ] No SQLite/FTS/external-daemon dependency is introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on #205, #210, #211, #212, #213.

## One-PR boundary

Only tokenizer/query model, redb derived index, ranking/paging, collection adapter, rebuild/integrity hooks, and tests. Search UI is later.
issues.title: Implement deterministic full-text search over catalog text and tags

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Provide exact, deterministic facet values and counts for collection/search results without re-evaluating the full catalog separately for every value.

## Fixed decisions

- Facet fields are capture/import year-month-day, folder components, camera maker/model, lens, ISO, aperture, shutter speed, focal length, rating, rejection, each color label, tags, source availability, image/RAW type, edit executability, group/copy state, and duplicate class.
- Missing values use a distinct `Missing` bucket; numeric zero remains a valid value where the field permits it.
- Capture date uses the stored capture-local value and explicit offset when available; calendar buckets do not apply the current machine timezone.
- Numeric bins are fixed: ISO exact standard values plus `Other`; aperture in one-third-stop buckets; shutter in one-third-stop EV buckets; focal length rounded to the nearest integer millimeter.
- Counts are computed against a validated [0056] base query and optional text-search candidate set, excluding the facet’s own predicate only when the request explicitly asks for drill-sideways behavior.

## Required implementation

- Define facet descriptors/value types, request mode, exact count/provenance, truncation, ordering, and stable cursor.
- Add/reuse redb composite indexes for each facet value-to-photo mapping and authoritative statistics needed for efficient intersections.
- Implement candidate-set intersection, direct-evaluation fallback, stable display ordering, hierarchy-aware tag/folder facets, and cardinality caps with explicit `Other/Truncated` metadata.
- Extend [0049] verification/rebuild for every derived facet index.
- Prove indexed counts equal direct [0056] evaluation using generated catalogs and random queries.
- Add fixtures for missing/zero, dates with/without offset, Unicode/case folders, camera/lens aliases, numeric boundaries, tags, multi-label photos, rejected+rating, virtual copies/groups, stale index revision, high-cardinality values, truncation, and cancellation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every returned count equals direct evaluation for the same catalog revision and mode.
- [ ] Equal data/query/request yield stable values, order, and provenance.
- [ ] Missing never collapses into empty/zero.
- [ ] Derived indexes rebuild exactly and are not authoritative.
- [ ] UI facet controls are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #205 and #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214.

## One-PR boundary

Only facet registry/query service, derived redb indexes/intersections, rebuild/integrity hooks, property tests, and benchmarks.
issues.title: Implement date, folder, camera, lens, exposure, and organization facets

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement persistent per-photo geolocation with provenance and deterministic GPX track matching, providing catalog/query behavior without map UI or network geocoding.

## Fixed decisions

- Store WGS84 latitude/longitude, optional altitude above mean sea level, optional image direction, uncertainty meters, origin, source timestamp, and metadata revision.
- Valid ranges are latitude [-90,90], longitude normalized to [-180,180), altitude [-500,20000] m, direction [0,360), uncertainty [0,1_000_000] m. Missing is distinct from zero.
- User edits override embedded/sidecar/GPX values but retain lower-priority provenance per [0055].
- GPX matching parses GPX 1.0/1.1 track points, converts photo capture-local time using an explicit user-supplied timezone/offset, applies a user-supplied camera clock offset, and linearly interpolates only between points in the same segment when both are within 15 minutes. No timezone is guessed.
- Reverse geocoding/network lookup is excluded.

## Required implementation

- Define geolocation/provenance types, repository/query ports, set/clear/restore-origin/batch commands, GPX parser/track index, and match request/receipt.
- Persist authoritative location/provenance and latitude/longitude bucket indexes transactionally in redb; add [0056] box/radius predicates and [0059] presence/location facets.
- Implement antimeridian-safe bounding boxes and haversine radius filtering with exact final calculation after index candidate selection.
- Import valid darktable coordinates and retain invalid source values as compatibility findings.
- Add fixtures/tests for poles, antimeridian, zero coordinates, altitude/direction boundaries, provenance precedence, batch rollback, GPX namespaces/segments/gaps, timezone/clock offsets, interpolation, duplicate times, malformed/oversized GPX, virtual copies, index rebuild, and reopen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Valid coordinates/provenance persist exactly and invalid values never partially commit.
- [ ] GPX results are deterministic for explicit time parameters and never guess timezone.
- [ ] Box/radius indexed results equal direct geometry evaluation.
- [ ] Missing, (0,0), and cleared locations remain distinct.
- [ ] Map display, geocoding, and source-file writeback are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #184, #205, #211, #212, and #215.

## One-PR boundary

Only geolocation domain/commands, GPX parsing/matching, redb indexes, query/facet integration, migration, and tests.
issues.title: Implement geolocation, altitude, direction, and GPX track matching

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Track whether catalog assets still resolve to their recorded bytes and provide explicit, recoverable commands to relink missing sources or move managed source files without losing catalog identity.

## Fixed decisions

- Availability states are `AvailableExact`, `AvailableChanged`, `Missing`, `Inaccessible`, and `Unchecked`, with last-check evidence. Metadata-only timestamp changes do not imply content change when hash verification succeeds.
- `Relink` points an asset to an existing file only after size and SHA-256 match the recorded content. A mismatching candidate requires a separate reimport and cannot replace provenance.
- `Move` preserves content and asset ID. Same-filesystem moves use an atomic rename where supported. Cross-filesystem moves copy to a temporary target, flush/sync, verify hash/length, atomically publish, commit catalog path, then remove the old file.
- Because filesystem and redb cannot share a transaction, every move uses a durable relocation intent/state record with recovery states. Startup completes or rolls back interrupted moves deterministically.
- Sidecar and primary may be moved together only when both are selected explicitly; partial pairs remain valid and their paths update independently.

## Required implementation

- Define availability evidence, relocation request/plan/state/receipt, conflict policies, repository ports, and commands for check, batch check, relink, move, and recover.
- Persist last-check state, logical/source path updates, path indexes, and relocation intents transactionally; extend checkpoints/integrity/events.
- Defend against symlink races, target traversal, case-only renames, existing targets, source replacement during copy, permissions, insufficient space, cancellation, and non-UTF-8 paths.
- Use streamed hashing/copy limits; never load whole files into memory. Preserve source permissions/timestamps only after content publish and report unsupported attributes.
- Add process/filesystem tests for same/cross filesystem adapters, missing/changed/inaccessible, relink exact/mismatch, duplicate target, case-only move, interruption at every phase, DB commit failure, old-file removal failure, pair moves, Windows rename semantics, and recovery on reopen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Relink cannot accept content differing from recorded hash/length.
- [ ] Successful moves retain asset/photo/edit identity and exact bytes.
- [ ] Every interrupted state is recoverable without losing both source and target.
- [ ] Catalog path/index changes never point to an unverified unpublished target.
- [ ] UI dialogs and automatic background scans are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #198, #204, #205, and #208.

## One-PR boundary

Only availability/relocation domain, filesystem adapter, durable intent/recovery, redb updates, integrity/events hooks, CLI commands, and tests.
issues.title: Implement source availability scanning, relinking, and managed file moves

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement safe removal of photos from the catalog and an explicit, recoverable workflow for sending unreferenced primary/sidecar source files to the operating-system trash.

## Fixed decisions

- Default removal is catalog-only and never changes source files.
- Removing a photo deletes its independent edits/history/organization state and updates groups/copies/indexes/events; shared assets remain while any photo references them.
- `RemoveAndTrashSources` is permitted only when every selected asset will have zero remaining photo references, every source is `AvailableExact`, and the user explicitly selected primary and/or sidecar roles.
- Permanent unlink/delete is not supported in 1.0. Files go through the platform trash/recycle-bin adapter; unsupported platforms return an error before catalog mutation.
- Catalog removal and external trash cannot be one transaction, so use a durable removal intent. Move files to trash first, record returned restore tokens/locations where available, then commit catalog removal; on catalog failure, attempt restore and leave a blocking recovery record if restoration is incomplete.

## Required implementation

- Define removal plan, role/refcount checks, intent states, receipts, recovery commands, repository ports, and catalog-only/source-trash command paths.
- Implement complete cascade rules for current edits/revisions/blobs, ratings/labels/tags/metadata/location, groups, virtual-copy promotion, collections’ active selection, duplicate/search/facet indexes, and orphan content blobs.
- Add platform trash adapter behind safe Rust APIs and deterministic fake adapter tests; validate symlink/path replacement, source content hash, missing/inaccessible files, shared assets, target role selection, cancellation, and catalog revision.
- Extend checkpoint/integrity and startup recovery for incomplete removal intents.
- Add integration/process tests for catalog-only, virtual copies/shared assets, group representative removal, primary+sidecar selection, unsupported trash, partial external success, catalog failure/restore, abrupt termination at each state, missing/changed source, and reopen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Catalog-only removal never accesses or changes source files.
- [ ] A shared asset is never sent to trash while any photo references it.
- [ ] External side effects and catalog state are either reconciled or represented by a recoverable blocking intent.
- [ ] All derived indexes and blob reachability reconcile after successful removal.
- [ ] Permanent deletion and UI confirmation dialogs are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217.

## One-PR boundary

Only removal domain/cascades, trash adapter, durable intent/recovery, redb/index/event updates, CLI command, and tests.
issues.title: Implement catalog removal and explicit source-to-trash workflows

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement a typed edit patch that copies selected operation instances, order, parameters, blend state, and masks from one photo revision and applies it atomically to one or many target photos.

## Fixed decisions

- Copy always reads an immutable [0044] revision and produces a detached, canonical `EditPatch` with source photo/revision, selected instance IDs, required masks/blends, and compatibility manifest versions.
- Apply modes are `OverwriteEdit`, `AppendInstances`, and `ReplaceMatchingInstances`. There is no field-by-field parameter merge.
- Matching uses compatibility operation name plus explicit instance name/ordinal selected by the patch; IDs are remapped to fresh target instance/mask IDs.
- `OverwriteEdit` replaces all target operations but preserves target-specific mandatory defaults inserted later by the processing registry. `AppendInstances` appends in canonical operation-order constraints. `ReplaceMatchingInstances` replaces only matched instances and fails if any requested match is ambiguous.
- Normalized drawn masks may be transferred across dimensions. Pixel/raster/source-dependent masks require identical source geometry/CFA and otherwise block that target; no silent mask omission.
- Multi-photo apply is one catalog transaction and all-or-nothing.

## Required implementation

- Define patch DTO/hash/limits, selection model, apply plan, ID-remap table, geometry compatibility result, per-target preview, and receipts.
- Implement source revision read, dependency closure for masks/raster links, canonical patch encoding, and validation against current operation/mask manifests.
- Plan each target from its current revision, enforce expected revisions, operation ordering, unique instances, mask DAG, geometry rules, opaque execution status, and encoded limits.
- Commit new current edits and immutable revision records for all targets atomically, sharing content blobs where identical.
- Add tests for all modes, selective/multi-instance operations, mask dependency closure, raster links, geometry mismatch, opaque operations, target revision conflict, ambiguous matches, 100-photo batch, cancellation, injected failure, undo history, and reopen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Patch bytes/hash are deterministic and independent of target IDs.
- [ ] Every applied target receives valid remapped instance/mask references and one new revision.
- [ ] Any target conflict/incompatibility prevents all target commits.
- [ ] Source and existing target revisions remain immutable.
- [ ] UI clipboard/selection controls are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #194, #195, #200, #205, and #207.

## One-PR boundary

Only edit-patch domain/codec, planning/remapping, atomic commands/persistence, events, and tests.
issues.title: Implement selective edit-history copy and atomic multi-photo paste

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Implement user-managed RustTable styles and operation presets using [0040], including exact migration of supported darktable records, deterministic auto-apply selection, and atomic application to photos.

## Fixed decisions

- Styles and presets use UUID identity and optimistic revisions; duplicate names are allowed.
- Style application compiles the style into an [0063] `EditPatch` and supports `AppendInstances` or `OverwriteEdit`. IDs/masks are remapped exactly as history copy/paste.
- A preset applies to one operation compatibility name and exact supported parameter schema. Applying it replaces that instance’s complete parameter/blend state; there is no partial-field merge.
- Auto-apply is evaluated only when creating a new operation/default edit or when explicitly requested. It never changes existing edits in the background.
- Matching presets sort by explicit priority descending, then specificity (number of constrained fields) descending, then UUID. At most one auto preset applies per operation instance; multi-instance presets must explicitly request a new instance.
- Opaque imported items remain persistable/visible but cannot be applied until all required operation/mask versions are executable.

## Required implementation

- Define style/preset repositories and commands for create, update, duplicate, delete, list/query, export/import canonical files, evaluate auto-apply, and apply to bounded photo sets.
- Persist records, canonical payload blobs, normalized-name, operation, priority, and auto-apply indexes transactionally in redb; extend checkpoint/integrity/events.
- Convert supported [0032] records through [0040] with deterministic migration IDs/provenance and explicit blockers for unsupported items.
- Reuse [0063] planning/atomic revision creation for style application; validate expected revisions, geometry/masks, operation availability, and per-target failures before commit.
- Add tests for duplicate names, optimistic conflicts, imported mixed opaque styles, every auto-apply predicate, priority/specificity ties, new-instance presets, style modes, multi-photo rollback, canonical file round-trip, index failure, migration, and reopen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Stored payload bytes/hashes match [0040] canonical encoding.
- [ ] Auto-apply selection is stable and never mutates an existing edit implicitly.
- [ ] Style application creates one valid immutable revision per target or commits none.
- [ ] Unsupported/opaque content is retained and reported, never silently omitted.
- [ ] Style/preset UI is excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #188, #196, #205, #212, and #219.

## One-PR boundary

Only style/preset domain/commands, redb persistence/indexes/migration, deterministic matching/application, canonical file I/O, events, and tests.
issues.title: Implement persistent styles, operation presets, and deterministic application

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Publish every successful catalog-domain mutation as an immutable, globally ordered event committed in the same redb transaction as the state change, then expose deterministic catch-up and live subscription for UI, jobs, scripts, plugins, and diagnostics.

## Fixed decisions

- Event sequence is a catalog-global monotonically increasing `u64`; sequence 1 is the first mutation after catalog creation. Sequence values are never reused.
- Events are a durable transactional outbox in redb. A command that changes state must append all of its events before the write transaction commits; failed/no-op commands append none.
- Event payloads contain stable type/schema, catalog revision, command/correlation ID, affected entity IDs and before/after revisions, and bounded typed summary fields. They do not contain source bytes, edit blobs, private metadata values, raw paths, or credentials.
- Batch commands produce one batch envelope plus deterministically ordered per-entity events when consumers require entity invalidation.
- The 1.0 journal is not automatically pruned. A future explicit compaction format must preserve sequence gaps and audit receipts; no retention ambiguity remains in this issue.

## Required implementation

- Define event envelope, stable event type registry covering all catalog commands through [0064], payload schemas, encoding/checksum, sequence allocator, page cursor, and subscriber errors.
- Integrate event append into each redb mutation adapter atomically; add integrity invariants tying catalog revision/event sequence/affected revisions.
- Implement `read_after(sequence, limit)`, bounded replay to a catalog revision, and a live in-process broadcaster that always replays durable events before delivering newer live events.
- Handle slow subscribers with a bounded queue and explicit `Lagged { last_delivered, durable_head }` requiring replay; never block catalog commits.
- Add restart/catch-up, concurrent writers, batch ordering, no-op/failure, subscriber race, lag, cancellation, corrupt event, unknown future event, maximum payload, migration, backup/restore, and deterministic encoding tests.
- Emit diagnostics correlation without duplicating sensitive event data.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] State and corresponding events are committed or rolled back together.
- [ ] Sequence/catalog revision ordering is strict and deterministic across reopen.
- [ ] A subscriber starting during a commit receives every event exactly once through replay+live handoff semantics.
- [ ] Slow subscribers cannot block writers and receive an actionable lag result.
- [ ] Unknown future events can be preserved/paged without crashing old readers.

## Dependencies

Depends on #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220.

## One-PR boundary

Only event domain/codec/registry, transactional integration, replay/live stream, integrity/checkpoint/backup hooks, and tests. Script/plugin consumers are later.
issues.title: Implement the transaction-ordered catalog event journal and subscriber stream

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Establish the singular storage-neutral image contracts that every RustTable decoder, metadata extractor, RAW pipeline, color transform, thumbnail job, pixelpipe node, GPU upload, renderer, and exporter uses. These contracts replace darktable’s dispersed image, ROI, buffer, CFA, orientation, and imageio structures with checked typed Rust values without dropping any representable source responsibility.

## Fixed decisions

- Pixel samples are represented by owned/borrowed typed planes with explicit width, height, row stride, channel layout, sample type, alpha mode, byte order, orientation, and color encoding. Raw byte slices without descriptors cannot cross crate APIs.
- Canonical processing buffers are interleaved linear-light RGBA `f32`, 16-byte pixel aligned and at least 64-byte allocation aligned. RAW mosaics use one `u16` plane plus black/white levels and CFA descriptor.
- Supported sample types are `U8`, `U16`, `F16`, and `F32`; supported layouts include Gray, GrayA, RGB, RGBA, Bayer, X-Trans, and explicit planar variants required by registered codecs. Conversion to canonical processing format is explicit.
- Orientation is stored as the eight EXIF transforms and applied logically through a transform descriptor; decoders do not rotate/copy pixels unless requested.
- Dimensions, strides, multiplication, allocation size, ROI, crop, orientation, CFA phase, and plane offset arithmetic use checked operations and configured pixel/byte limits.
- `ImageDescriptor`, `ImageView`, `ImageViewMut`, `OwnedImage`, `RawMosaic`, `Roi`, and decode request/result types are nominally distinct; image/color/pixelpipe/GPU crates do not define parallel equivalents.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/image.h` — type `dt_image_t` carries image dimensions, orientation, flags, and source metadata.
- `src/develop/imageop.h` — type `dt_iop_roi_t` defines operation ROI geometry.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h` — type `dt_mipmap_buffer_t` defines cached pixel-buffer ownership and layout.
- `src/imageio/imageio_common.h` — enum `dt_imageio_retval_t` defines typed loader outcomes.
- `src/common/colorspaces.h` — enum `dt_colorspaces_color_profile_type_t` identifies attached color profiles.
- `src/common/exif.h` — function `dt_exif_read` is the EXIF-to-image metadata interpretation action.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Add domain types for dimensions, rect/ROI, orientation transform, sample/channel/alpha layout, byte order, color-encoding/profile reference, CFA pattern/phase, black/white levels, image planes/views, decode request/result/receipt, and typed errors.
- Implement validated constructors, checked byte-size/offset calculations, row/plane iteration, crop/view creation, orientation coordinate transforms, CFA phase after crop/orientation, and canonical buffer allocation through the shared buffer-pool interface.
- Define decoder capability descriptors and output guarantees without adding a registry yet.
- Encode darktable-to-RustTable source mappings for `dt_image_t`, `dt_iop_roi_t`, mipmap buffers, loader outputs, and CFA/orientation fields; every used source field is preserved, redesigned, or explicitly delegated.
- Add architecture checks preventing decoders, pixelpipe, GPU, render, or export crates from defining parallel pixel/ROI/orientation/sample descriptors.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject zero/overflowing dimensions where the type requires an image, short/overflowing strides, inconsistent plane lengths, unsupported sample/layout combinations, misalignment, invalid alpha/profile/CFA values, out-of-bounds ROI/crop, aliasing mutable views, and allocation beyond configured limits.
- Empty ROIs may be represented where operation propagation requires them, but cannot construct a nonempty image view.
- Orientation and CFA transforms fail rather than wrap when dimensions/coordinates overflow.
- Views never outlive backing storage; writable views require exclusive leases.
- Unknown future source format facts are carried as bounded opaque descriptor extensions only when they do not make safe access ambiguous.

## Test matrix

- All eight orientation transforms and inverse coordinate/image round trips; width/height swaps and one-pixel edges.
- Extreme dimensions/strides, zero/one-pixel images, overflow, padded rows, planar/interleaved views, alignment, alias safety, crop and nested view bounds.
- Bayer/X-Trans phase after every crop/orientation combination and black/white-level validation.
- U8/U16/F16/F32, endian behavior, Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA/RAW layouts, alpha modes, and profile references.
- Property tests for byte counts, offsets, row iteration, ROI intersections, and no out-of-bounds slice construction.
- Source-accounting goldens for `dt_image_t`, `dt_iop_roi_t`, mipmap/imageio descriptors, orientation, and RAW metadata; upstream field additions fail #555 until assigned.
- Architecture mutation fixtures adding a duplicate descriptor in a consumer crate.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image image_contracts` passes value, view, orientation, CFA, alignment, overflow, and property suites.
- `cargo xtask image-contracts qualify --all-layouts --all-orientations --all-cfa --verify-alignment` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 222` proves every listed darktable image/ROI/buffer responsibility is accounted for.
- Invalid descriptors cannot construct usable views, all transforms round-trip, buffer arithmetic cannot overflow, and canonical allocations meet declared alignment.

## Dependencies

Depends on #159, #162, #269 for the production pool implementation interface, and #555’s accepted source-map schema. Existing `rusttable-image` primitives are migrated rather than duplicated.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements shared image/decode contracts, validation/math/views/allocation interface, source-accounting, tests, and architecture enforcement. Do not implement codecs, file I/O, color transforms, cache policy, GPU resources, or processing algorithms.
issues.title: Define typed image, pixel-format, orientation, and decode contracts

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Provide the single safe immutable file-input primitive used by probing, decoders, metadata, hashing, sidecars, import, relocation, compatibility inspection, and exports that must reread sources without observing path replacement or combining bytes from different file versions.

## Fixed decisions

- A `SourceSnapshot` opens the path once, records file identity/length/timestamps, and performs all reads through that handle. Later path lookups are diagnostic only.
- Regular files are supported. Directories, devices, sockets, pipes, and symlinks outside caller policy are rejected.
- Random access is bounded `read_exact_at(offset, len)` plus sequential reader creation; decoders never receive a bare filesystem path.
- Snapshot validity is rechecked after significant reads and before publishing results. Changed identity/length/mtime or hash mismatch returns `SourceChanged` and invalidates the result.
- SHA-256 is computed incrementally on demand and cached only after an unchanged full-file read. Memory mapping is not used for the initial product contract.
- Optional stable-copy mode streams bytes to an owned cache file, computes identity during copy, syncs, atomically publishes, and then exposes a normal immutable snapshot.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/image.h` — field `dt_image_t::filename` is the legacy source-path identity carried by an image.
- `src/common/film.h` — type `dt_film_t` groups source paths into catalog film records.
- `src/imageio/imageio_common.h` — function family `dt_imageio_open*` is the path-reopening decoder boundary replaced by snapshots.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h` — function `dt_mipmap_cache_get` triggers source-backed preview/full acquisition.
- `src/common/exif.h` — function `dt_exif_read` is a second path-consuming metadata reader that must share snapshot evidence.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Define source identity, logical path bytes, snapshot policy/limits, random/sequential reader traits, content evidence, and typed open/read/change errors.
- Implement platform adapters using positioned reads, checked offset/length arithmetic, cancellation, byte-read budgets, and no whole-file buffering.
- Implement stable-copy mode with streamed copy+hash+sync+atomic publish and source revalidation before accepting the copy.
- Defend against path/symlink replacement, sparse/oversized files, short reads, truncation/growth, permission changes, non-UTF-8 paths, and platform file-ID differences.
- Emit privacy-safe receipts with path alias, length, identity class, hash status, bytes read, copy identity where used, and elapsed time.
- Add architecture checks rejecting direct filesystem-path input in decoder/prober/metadata/compatibility traits after migration.
- Add a source-accounting table mapping each upstream filename-reopen call site to the snapshot API or a named later migration.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported file kinds, invalid/symlink-escaped paths, size/offset overflow, short reads, byte-budget exhaustion, source replacement/growth/truncation, unstable metadata, permission loss, hash mismatch, cancellation, copy/write/sync/publish failure, and receipt/path-alias violations.
- A result can never combine bytes from two handles or two file versions. Changed source invalidates all unpublished downstream work.
- Sparse files count logical bytes against limits; the implementation must not allocate by file length.
- Handle and temporary-copy cleanup is deterministic on every error/cancellation. Cleanup never follows attacker-replaced symlinks.
- Windows sharing/rename semantics and Unix positioned-read semantics are explicit adapter behavior and covered on hosted platforms.

## Test matrix

- Stable sequential/random reads; interleaved readers; hash caching; repeated evidence.
- Concurrent in-place modification, truncate, grow, rename, replace, hard-link change, symlink swap, permission change, deletion after open, and timestamp-only drift.
- Huge/sparse files, boundary offsets, short reads, invalid UTF-8 and long paths, device/pipe/directory rejection, and read budgets.
- Stable-copy success, source change during copy, disk-full/short-write/sync/rename failure, collision, cancellation, cleanup, and reopen verification.
- Linux/macOS file identity/positioned reads and Windows sharing/file-ID behavior.
- Architecture/source-map mutation proving every upstream path-based decoder/metadata/import caller is assigned and no later decoder needs a bare path.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image -p rusttable-image-io source_snapshot` passes filesystem, identity, modification, stable-copy, cancellation, and platform suites.
- `cargo xtask source-snapshot qualify --all-platforms --mutations --stable-copy --verify-cleanup` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 223` proves the listed darktable path/reopen responsibilities are accounted for.
- A downstream result can never combine two source versions; large reads remain bounded; every handle/copy is released on failure.

## Dependencies

Depends on #173, #178, #179, #222, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements source snapshot contracts/adapters, hashing, stable-copy mode, source-call-site accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement format probing, codec parsing, sidecars, discovery, import registration, relocation, or managed-library policy.
issues.title: Implement immutable source snapshots with bounded random access

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the single deterministic built-in decoder registry used by import, preview, thumbnails, metadata probing, and later export verification. The registry probes immutable #223 bytes by signature/container structure, selects exactly one decoder, dispatches bounded #222 header/full requests, and registers/qualifies real PNG, JPEG, and TIFF adapters required by the P0 raster workflow. It replaces darktable’s path-based loader cascade and format-specific loose output contracts rather than adding a parallel Rust-only abstraction with no upstream accounting.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement registry/domain in `rusttable-image-io`; outputs/requests use #222 and reads use #223. Callers never receive codec-specific public types or pass bare paths.
- First-party registration is static and compile-time through one declarative `builtin_decoders!` list. No dynamic modules, linker-section ordering, runtime directories, or application extension switches.
- Permanent initial IDs: `rusttable.decoder.png.v1`, `rusttable.decoder.jpeg.v1`, `rusttable.decoder.tiff.v1`.
- Detection is signature and bounded structural evidence first:
  - PNG exact signature and bounded IHDR;
  - JPEG SOI plus bounded marker traversal to a supported frame/header;
  - TIFF endian magic plus bounded first-IFD validation.
  Extension/MIME are diagnostics only.
- Probe outcomes are `NoMatch`, `Match`, or `MalformedRecognized`; malformed high-confidence data is not offered to unrelated decoders.
- Total shared probe budget is 64 KiB/source, default deadline 250 ms, with cancellation before/after each read.
- Registry construction rejects duplicate IDs/versions, unresolved overlapping signatures, ambiguous equal-priority matches, unsupported #222 contracts, and nondeterministic ordering.
- Request modes are `Header` and `Full`; thumbnail/region/animation/APNG/multi-page workflows are explicitly unsupported here.
- Header output includes dimensions, sample/layout/alpha/endian, orientation evidence, profile/color evidence, page/frame evidence, and bounded metadata capabilities without full allocation.
- Full output contracts:
  - PNG Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA U8/U16, straight alpha;
  - JPEG Gray/RGB U8, opaque;
  - TIFF one supported image, Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA U8/U16 variants declared by the pinned backend; unsupported sample/compression/planar/page variants fail.
- Decoders do not apply orientation or color conversion. Embedded ICC becomes content-addressed evidence; PNG sRGB becomes `SrgbD65`; otherwise RGB is `UnspecifiedRgb`.
- Hard limits: nonzero dimensions, 250 MP, 2 GiB logical decoded bytes, checked arithmetic, max 64 metadata/profile chunks, bounded backend diagnostics.
- Source stability is checked after probe/header and before full publication. Adapter panic is contained and does not trigger unrelated-decoder fallback.
- No RAW, HEIF/AVIF/JXL/WebP/EXR/JP2, SVG, PDF, video, network, clipboard, or external-command decoder.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/imageio/imageio_common.h` — function family `dt_imageio_open*` is the common typed loader contract.
- `src/imageio/imageio_module.h` — type `dt_imageio_module_format_t` describes a registered decoder module.
- `src/imageio/imageio_module.c` — function `dt_imageio_load_module_format` performs dynamic format-module loading.
- `src/imageio/imageio_png.c` — function `dt_imageio_open_png` is the PNG decoder action.
- `src/imageio/imageio_jpeg.c` — function `dt_imageio_open_jpeg` is the JPEG decoder action.
- `src/imageio/imageio_tiff.c` — function `dt_imageio_open_tiff` is the TIFF decoder action.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Define decoder ID/version/implementation identity, modes, capabilities, probe rules/evidence, registry snapshot/builder, selected result, header/full requests/results, limits, receipts, and typed errors.
- Implement deterministic static registration, startup validation, shared bounded probe cache, priority/disambiguation, cancellation/deadlines, source revalidation, and canonical registry identity.
- Adapt existing PNG/JPEG/TIFF Rust decoding behind one safe trait. Validate backend dimensions/layout/length/sample/alpha/color evidence before #222 construction.
- Implement bounded PNG IHDR, JPEG marker, and TIFF header/IFD probes independently of extension/full decode.
- Record decoder/backend/container/source/request/output evidence in privacy-safe receipts.
- Add fake decoders only for ambiguity/budget/panic/unsupported/source-change tests; product acceptance uses real containers.
- Add `cargo xtask decoders verify --registry --formats png,jpeg,tiff --probe-matrix --decode-corpus`.
- Add source/DAG checks preventing parallel registries, extension dispatch, bare-path decoder APIs, codec-specific public types, or generic fallback activation.
- Add a source-accounting registry that maps every upstream image loader source to this issue, a later format issue, or an explicit exclusion.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle no-match, malformed recognized data, registry ambiguity, unsupported request/sample/layout/compression/page/animation, overflow, limits/deadline/cancellation, short read, source change, backend error/panic, descriptor/length mismatch, profile evidence error, and nonfinite samples where applicable.
- Extension/MIME cannot override signature. Misleading `.jpg` PNG selects PNG; text named `.png` does not decode.
- Page/frame policy is explicit; unsupported multi-page content cannot be silently flattened.
- Failed/cancelled decode drops all output and publishes no success receipt.
- Equal source/registry/request identity yields the same decoder and descriptor independent of collection order.

## Test matrix

- Valid PNG U8/U16 Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA/alpha; JPEG Gray/RGB; supported TIFF endian/sample/channel/compression variants.
- Exact #450 PNG/JPEG/TIFF fixtures with independent dimensions/pixel hashes.
- Misleading/missing/mixed-case extension/MIME; strong/weak/overlapping matches; ambiguity; duplicate IDs; deterministic order.
- Malformed/truncated/oversized PNG chunks, JPEG markers, TIFF offsets/IFDs; unsupported animation/pages/sample/compression/planar variants.
- Shared-read budget/deadline/cancellation; excessive fake reads; source change during probe/header/full.
- Backend panic/error, malformed output, allocation/overflow, ICC/sRGB/untagged evidence, orientation pass-through, privacy.
- Source-map tests enumerating all darktable image loaders and proving PNG/JPEG/TIFF are owned here while every other loader has another owner/exclusion.
- Architecture mutations adding extension dispatch, bare paths, second registry, generic/native command fallback, or codec types in public APIs.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io decoder_registry` passes registry, probe, real decode, source-change, limit, panic, source-accounting, and architecture suites.
- `cargo xtask decoders verify --registry --formats png,jpeg,tiff --probe-matrix --decode-corpus --verify-source-stability` emits complete receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 224` proves the exact upstream loader/dispatch responsibilities are accounted for.
- Every P0 corpus fixture selects correctly independent of extension and produces expected #222 descriptor/pixel identity.
- #181/#256 use only this registry.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #450, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements static registry/probe/dispatch, production PNG/JPEG/TIFF adapters, source-loader accounting, receipts, fixtures, architecture checks, and tests. Do not add other formats, RAW, animation/multi-page workflows, region/thumbnail modes, color conversion, metadata editing, import registration, or external decoders.
issues.title: Implement the static image decoder registry and signature-first format probing

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add complete bounded JPEG still-image decoding to the [0068] registry using a pure-Rust implementation and [0066] output contracts.

## Fixed decisions

- Use `jpeg-decoder` as the backend and extend/wrap it in RustTable where required; do not link libjpeg/libjpeg-turbo.
- Support 8-bit baseline and progressive DCT, grayscale, YCbCr, RGB, CMYK, and YCCK; support 12-bit sequential JPEG by decoding to `U16` with original precision. Arithmetic coding, lossless JPEG, JPEG-LS, motion JPEG, and multi-image MPO are unsupported and reported explicitly.
- Apply Adobe APP14 inversion/color-transform rules for CMYK/YCCK. Do not perform ICC conversion; return embedded profile/EXIF/XMP segment locations to metadata issues.
- Header mode returns dimensions/precision/components/orientation metadata without full coefficient decode. Region mode is unsupported because JPEG entropy dependencies prevent exact bounded ROI decode.

## Required implementation

- Implement signature/probe and decoder capability registration for SOI/marker structure and `.jpg/.jpeg/.jpe` hints.
- Parse markers with checked segment lengths/limits; reject duplicate/conflicting SOF, invalid sampling, excessive dimensions/components/scans, malformed restart intervals, truncated entropy data, and decompression limits.
- Decode into Gray/RGB/CMYK `U8` or `U16` typed images with explicit color-encoding tags and no hidden orientation/profile transform.
- Emit receipt fields for SOF type, precision, components, sampling, scans, restart interval, source bytes read, output bytes, and metadata segment inventory.
- Add corpus/differential tests for baseline/progressive, subsampling modes, grayscale, CMYK/YCCK, 12-bit, odd dimensions, restart markers, malformed/truncated/oversized markers, misleading extension, cancellation, and source mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures match reference pixels within the `Transfer` tolerance class after explicit common color conversion.
- [ ] Header/full requests have deterministic receipts and bounded allocation.
- [ ] Unsupported coding modes return stable capability errors rather than generic corruption.
- [ ] Embedded metadata/profile bytes remain accessible but unmodified.
- [ ] No native JPEG library or encoder is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224 and #165.

## One-PR boundary
