Only JPEG probe/decode, typed outputs, metadata segment inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. JPEG export is later.
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust JPEG decoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add bounded PNG still-image decoding to the [0068] registry using the pure-Rust `png` crate and [0066] image contracts.

## Fixed decisions

- Support color types grayscale, grayscale+alpha, indexed, RGB, and RGBA at legal PNG bit depths 1/2/4/8/16, including Adam7 interlace and `tRNS` transparency.
- Expand sub-byte and palette samples to `U8`; preserve 16-bit samples as `U16` in network-to-host decoded values. Do not reduce precision implicitly.
- Preserve alpha as straight alpha. Do not apply gAMA/cHRM/sRGB/iCCP conversion during decode; return normalized color-description/profile chunks for [0084].
- APNG animation is unsupported. If an APNG contains a valid default image, decode only that default image and report animation metadata; otherwise return `UnsupportedAnimation`.
- Region decode is unsupported; header, thumbnail, and full decode are supported.

## Required implementation

- Register PNG signature/probe and parse IHDR/chunk structure with CRC verification, ordering rules, duplicate constraints, and configured chunk/decompressed-size limits.
- Decode filters/interlace through the backend into validated Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA typed buffers, expanding palette/transparency deterministically.
- Inventory EXIF, XMP text, ICC, sRGB/gAMA/cHRM, physical resolution, and textual chunks without interpreting private text values in logs.
- Reject invalid dimensions, color/bit-depth combinations, unknown critical chunks, corrupt CRC, decompression bombs, invalid palette indexes, malformed interlace, trailing critical data, and source changes.
- Emit receipt fields for color type, bit depth, interlace, palette/transparency, chunk inventory, decompressed/output bytes, and default-animation status.
- Add corpus/differential/fuzz tests for every legal color/depth class, Adam7, alpha/palette/tRNS, 16-bit endianness, profiles/chunks, default APNG, malformed CRC/order/chunks, bombs, cancellation, and source mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures decode to exact sample values before optional color conversion.
- [ ] 16-bit precision and straight alpha survive without truncation/premultiplication.
- [ ] Unknown critical chunks and APNG policy return precise errors.
- [ ] Allocation/decompression limits reject adversarial files before output publication.
- [ ] No native PNG library or encoder is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224 and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only PNG probe/decode, chunk inventory, typed outputs, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. PNG export is later.
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust PNG decoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add bounded photographic TIFF/BigTIFF decoding, page/subimage discovery, and tile/strip region reads to the [0068] registry without libtiff.

## Fixed decisions

- Use the pure-Rust `tiff` crate plus RustTable extensions; do not link libtiff.
- Support classic TIFF and BigTIFF, little/big endian, strips and tiles, chunky/planar samples, orientation, predictor 1/2/3, and multiple IFD/SubIFD pages.
- Support unsigned 1/2/4/8/16/32-bit, signed 8/16/32-bit, and IEEE 16/32-bit samples where valid. Output native typed planes without implicit precision reduction.
- Support photometric WhiteIsZero, BlackIsZero, Palette, RGB/RGBA, CMYK, YCbCr, and CIELab. Preserve alpha extrasample association and convert associated alpha to explicit premultiplied status rather than unpremultiplying silently.
- Support compression None, PackBits, LZW, Deflate/Adobe Deflate, Zstd, and new-style JPEG via [0069]. CCITT fax, old-style JPEG, JBIG, and proprietary codecs return precise unsupported errors.
- Default full decode selects the largest reduced-image-type=0 page, ties by lowest IFD offset; callers may request an explicit page. Region decode is required for tiled/stripped supported pages.

## Required implementation

- Register TIFF/BigTIFF probing and parse IFD graphs with checked offsets/counts/types, cycle detection, and hard limits on pages/tags/tiles/strips/metadata bytes.
- Normalize page descriptors, sample/photometric/compression/predictor/layout, tile/strip geometry, orientation, profile/EXIF/XMP/IPTC locations, and embedded thumbnails.
- Decode selected page/ROI with bounded temporary buffers, decompressed-byte accounting, predictor reversal, sample unpacking/sign/float handling, palette/YCbCr/Lab descriptors, and planar assembly.
- Reject overlapping/out-of-range offsets, recursive IFDs, duplicate contradictory tags, decompression bombs, invalid predictors/sampling, missing strips/tiles, short data, and source changes.
- Add corpus/differential/fuzz tests for endian/classic/BigTIFF, every supported sample/layout/photometric/compression, multi-page/SubIFD selection, tiled/stripped ROI edges, alpha, orientation, profiles/metadata, corrupt graphs/offsets/counts, cancellation, and mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported pages decode to exact stored samples before explicit color conversion.
- [ ] Region decode returns the same pixels as cropping full decode.
- [ ] IFD/offset/decompression limits prevent cycles and unbounded allocation.
- [ ] Page selection is stable and explicit in receipts.
- [ ] No libtiff/native decoder or TIFF encoder is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224, #225, #226 and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only TIFF/BigTIFF probe/page model/decode/ROI, metadata inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. RAW-DNG semantics and TIFF export are later.
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust TIFF and BigTIFF decoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add bounded OpenEXR scanline/tiled still-image decoding with multipart/channel discovery and half/float preservation using the pure-Rust `exr` crate.

## Fixed decisions

- Support OpenEXR 2/3 single-part and multipart files, scanline and tiled levels, `F16` and `F32` channels, data/display windows, chromaticities, adopted neutral, white luminance, ICC/XMP attributes, and named views/layers.
- Deep scanline/tile data is unsupported and returns `UnsupportedDeepData` before sample allocation.
- Support compression None, RLE, ZIP/ZIPS, PIZ, PXR24, B44/B44A, DWAA, and DWAB; extend/wrap the backend when corpus coverage exposes missing common photographic modes.
- Default part selection: first non-deep part containing RGB or Y channels, ordered by header index. Default layer/view prefers unprefixed `R/G/B[/A]`, then `beauty`, then lexicographically first complete RGB/Y layer. Callers may request explicit part/layer/view/channel mapping.
- Output preserves source `F16` or promotes mixed channels to `F32`; alpha association and missing-channel fill are explicit in the receipt. Do not apply chromaticity/ICC transforms.

## Required implementation

- Register EXR magic/version probe and parse bounded headers/attributes/parts/channel lists/tile levels/windows with duplicate/cycle/size checks.
- Define normalized part/layer/view descriptors and deterministic channel-selection rules.
- Decode full image or tiled ROI/level into Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA typed planes, respecting non-zero/negative data-window origins and fill values outside data window within display output.
- Reject unsupported flags, deep parts, excessive parts/channels/attributes/levels, invalid windows, decompression bombs, missing chunks, overlapping chunk tables, corrupt compression, and source changes.
- Emit receipt fields for version/flags, part/layer/view/channels, sample type, compression, windows, tile/level, metadata attributes, output origin/size, and bytes.
- Add differential/fuzz/corpus tests for each compression, half/float/mixed channels, scanline/tiled/mipmap, multipart/layers/views, negative origins, data-vs-display windows, alpha, metadata/chromaticities, malformed headers/chunks, cancellation, and mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures preserve finite sample values and half/float precision exactly within codec guarantees.
- [ ] Explicit/default part/layer selection is deterministic and receipt-visible.
- [ ] ROI/level decode equals the corresponding full-image crop/level.
- [ ] Deep/unsupported files return precise capability errors without partial output.
- [ ] No native OpenEXR library or encoder is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224 and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only EXR probe/header/selection/decode/ROI, metadata inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. EXR export is later.
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust OpenEXR decoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add bounded JPEG XL still-image decoding for both bare codestreams and ISOBMFF containers using the pure-Rust `jxl-oxide` backend.

## Fixed decisions

- Support VarDCT and modular still images, grayscale/RGB, alpha, spot/extra channels inventory, lossless/lossy coding, embedded ICC or structured color encoding, HDR transfer functions, orientation, and preview frames.
- Animation with more than one displayed frame is unsupported and returns `UnsupportedAnimation`; a single-frame animated header is treated as a still and reported.
- Decode the main frame to normalized `F32` Gray/GrayA/RGB/RGBA in the image’s declared color encoding. Do not convert to sRGB or apply display tone mapping.
- JPEG reconstruction data is inventoried but not used; reconstructed JPEG export is outside this issue.
- Header/full/thumbnail requests are supported. Region decode is supported only when the backend can decode the requested group set without full-frame fallback; otherwise the receipt explicitly records full decode plus crop and enforces the full-memory limit.

## Required implementation

- Register bare/container signatures and parse boxes/codestream headers with checked sizes, nesting, duplicate rules, and source bounds.
- Normalize dimensions, bit-depth declaration, orientation, frame/preview/extra-channel descriptors, color encoding/ICC, alpha association, animation status, and decoder feature requirements.
- Decode through bounded callbacks/buffers, validate output dimensions/channels/finiteness, and expose extra channels as metadata descriptors while selecting only alpha into the canonical output.
- Reject unknown essential boxes, unsupported animation, excessive dimensions/frames/channels/previews, corrupt group tables, decompression/resource bombs, invalid profiles, and source changes.
- Emit receipt fields for container/codestream, coding mode, frame count, color/ICC, orientation, alpha/extra channels, group/ROI behavior, bytes read, and output bytes.
- Add corpus/differential/fuzz tests for lossless/lossy VarDCT/modular, grayscale/RGB/alpha, HDR/ICC, orientation, bare/container, preview, single/multi-frame animation, extra channels, malformed boxes/codestreams, bombs, cancellation, and mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures match the reference decoder within the codec-appropriate `Transfer` tolerance.
- [ ] HDR/color encoding is preserved and no implicit sRGB/tone mapping occurs.
- [ ] Animation/ROI fallback policy is deterministic and receipt-visible.
- [ ] Resource limits reject adversarial streams before partial output publication.
- [ ] No native libjxl or encoder is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224 and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only JXL probe/container/header/decode/ROI policy, metadata inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. JXL export is later.
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust JPEG XL decoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add bounded lossy/lossless WebP still-image decoding using the pure-Rust `image-webp` backend.

## Fixed decisions

- Support simple lossy VP8, lossless VP8L, and extended VP8X still images with alpha, ICC, EXIF, and XMP chunks.
- Animated WebP (`ANIM`/`ANMF`) is unsupported and rejected before frame decode; RustTable never silently imports the first animation frame.
- Decode to `U8` RGB/RGBA with straight alpha. Preserve embedded color/profile metadata without applying ICC conversion.
- Header, thumbnail, and full decode are supported. Region decode is unsupported and may only be implemented as explicit full-decode-and-crop by a higher layer under full-frame limits.

## Required implementation

- Register RIFF/WEBP signature probing and validate RIFF size, chunk order/alignment, singleton constraints, canvas dimensions, and VP8/VP8L/VP8X feature consistency.
- Decode lossy/lossless data through bounded buffers and verify backend-reported dimensions/channels against container headers.
- Inventory ICC/EXIF/XMP and alpha/animation features, retaining chunk offsets/lengths for metadata extraction.
- Reject unsupported animation, conflicting simple/extended chunks, malformed sizes/padding, duplicate image data, invalid alpha dimensions, decompression bombs, truncated/corrupt streams, trailing critical inconsistencies, and source changes.
- Emit receipt fields for RIFF/canvas, coding mode, alpha, feature flags, metadata chunks, compressed/output bytes, and decoder version.
- Add corpus/differential/fuzz tests for lossy/lossless, RGB/RGBA, odd dimensions, ICC/EXIF/XMP, simple/extended containers, animation rejection, malformed chunk graphs/sizes/padding, bombs, cancellation, and mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures match reference pixels within the `Transfer` tolerance class.
- [ ] Alpha remains straight and color metadata remains unmodified.
- [ ] Animated files return a precise unsupported result before output allocation.
- [ ] RIFF/decompression limits prevent unbounded reads/allocation.
- [ ] No native libwebp or encoder is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224 and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only WebP probe/container/decode, metadata inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. WebP export is later.
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust WebP decoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Add bounded AVIF and HEIF/HEIC still-image decoding, primary-item selection, alpha, HDR color descriptors, and metadata inventory for ISOBMFF image containers.

## Fixed decisions

- AVIF uses a RustTable ISOBMFF item parser plus the pure-Rust `rav1d` AV1 decoder. HEIF/HEIC uses the approved `rusttable-libheif-native` adapter because no production-complete pure-Rust HEVC image decoder is adopted.
- Support primary/explicit item selection, grid items, monochrome/RGB-derived YUV, 8/10/12-bit, 4:2:0/4:2:2/4:4:4, full/limited range, auxiliary alpha, ICC/nclx CICP, EXIF/XMP items, clean aperture, pixel aspect, rotation, and mirroring.
- Image sequences/animations and video tracks are unsupported. Multiple still items are inventoried; default decode selects the container primary item and fails if it is absent/ambiguous.
- Decode YUV to normalized `F32` RGB using the declared range and CICP matrix coefficients; retain primaries/transfer/ICC as the output color encoding for later color management. Alpha is straight.
- Gain/depth/thumbnail auxiliary items are inventoried but not applied; callers may request a declared thumbnail item explicitly.

## Required implementation

- Register `ftyp` brand/box probing and parse bounded ISOBMFF boxes/item properties/references/extents with checked 32/64-bit sizes, nesting, cycle detection, and limits.
- Implement AVIF item/grid/alpha decode through rav1d, deterministic chroma upsampling, range/matrix conversion, crop/aspect/orientation descriptors, and metadata references.
- Implement safe HEIF adapter callbacks over [0067], error/status mapping, item/property inventory, decode into bounded owned planes, and no native handle/pointer escape.
- Reject unknown essential properties/brands, sequences/video, recursive grids/items, overlapping/out-of-range extents, unsupported chroma/bit depth/matrix, invalid ICC/CICP, decompression bombs, native allocation-limit violation, and source changes.
- Emit receipts for brands, items/primary/grid, codec/backend/version, bit depth/chroma/range, color/ICC, alpha/auxiliary items, transforms, bytes, and native-adapter use.
- Add differential/fuzz/corpus tests for AVIF/HEIC 8/10/12-bit, chroma/range variants, alpha/grid, HDR/ICC/CICP, transforms, metadata/thumbnail, multiple items, sequence rejection, malformed boxes/references/extents, adapter errors, cancellation, and mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures match trusted decoders within `Transfer` tolerance after identical color interpretation.
- [ ] Primary/item selection and YUV conversion are deterministic and receipt-visible.
- [ ] HDR/ICC/alpha/geometry metadata is preserved without display tone mapping.
- [ ] The native HEIF boundary exposes only safe owned Rust values and honors limits.
- [ ] No AV1/HEVC encoder or animation support is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #168, #222, #223, #224, and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only ISOBMFF probing/item parsing, AVIF decode, safe HEIF adapter/decode, metadata inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. Export is later.
issues.title: Implement AVIF and HEIF/HEIC still-image decoding

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Complete the initial non-RAW raster import surface with bounded decoders for Netpbm/PAM, PFM floating-point images, Radiance RGBE, QOI, and JPEG 2000 codestream/JP2.

## Fixed decisions

- PNM/PAM, PFM, RGBE, and QOI are implemented in pure Rust within `rusttable-image-io`; use small audited parsers where crate dependencies would add no value.
- JPEG 2000 uses an approved `rusttable-openjpeg-native` adapter around OpenJPEG, exposing only safe owned Rust values and [0066] contracts. The native-boundary manifest is updated in this PR.
- PNM supports PBM/PGM/PPM/PAM ASCII/binary variants, comments, maxval 1–65535, grayscale/RGB and alpha tuples. Expand to `U8` or `U16` without hidden gamma conversion.
- PFM supports grayscale/RGB `F32`, scale magnitude, sign-selected endianness, bottom-to-top row order, and non-finite sample preservation with findings.
- RGBE supports Radiance headers, old/new scanline RLE, XY/YX resolution/orientation, and converts RGBE/XYZE to `F32` RGB/XYZ-tagged output without tone mapping.
- QOI supports the v1 colorspace/alpha flags and exact `U8` RGB/RGBA output.
- JPEG 2000 supports raw J2K and JP2, grayscale/RGB/RGBA, 1–16-bit signed/unsigned components, ICC/enum color, alpha, tiles/resolution levels, full and region decode. Animation/MJ2 and unsupported component transforms return precise errors.

## Required implementation

- Register non-overlapping signatures/probes and capability descriptors for every format.
- Implement checked token/header/size parsing, sample conversion, orientation/row rules, decompression limits, cancellation, and source stability for pure-Rust formats.
- Implement safe OpenJPEG stream callbacks over [0067], error collection, allocation/component/tile limits, item/profile inventory, ROI/resolution decode, and no native pointer/handle escape.
- Emit format-specific receipts including precision/layout/color/orientation/compression/tile/level and bytes read/output.
- Add differential/fuzz/corpus tests for all variants, max-value/endianness/RLE/orientation/alpha/profile cases, malformed tokens/headers/runs/boxes/codestreams, bombs, adapter errors, cancellation, and mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported simple formats preserve exact integer/float samples after specified row/scale handling.
- [ ] JPEG 2000 full/ROI/level outputs match trusted OpenJPEG results within `Transfer` tolerance.
- [ ] Every parser/adapter enforces byte/dimension/allocation limits and publishes no partial output.
- [ ] The OpenJPEG boundary exposes safe values only and is the sole native code in these decoders.
- [ ] No encoders are added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #168, #222, #223, #224, and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only these five decoder families, safe OpenJPEG adapter, metadata/profile inventory, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, registry/native-policy wiring. Export is later.
issues.title: Implement PNM, PFM, RGBE, QOI, and JPEG 2000 decoders

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Establish RustTable’s RAW input architecture using the pure-Rust `rawler` ecosystem, without linking RawSpeed, LibRaw, dcraw, or inherited darktable code.

## Fixed decisions

- `rawler` is the only initial RAW parser/decoder backend and is pinned through [0018]. RustTable owns the stable adapter/domain API so backend types never leave `rusttable-image-io`.
- RAW detection is signature/container/camera-metadata based through [0068], never extension-only.
- Define `RawFrame` as one or more `U16` mosaic/linear planes plus active/crop areas, CFA pattern/phase or linear channel mapping, per-channel black/white levels, masked areas, orientation, camera identity, color matrices/illuminants, white-balance samples, compression/bit-depth evidence, DNG opcode descriptors, and embedded preview descriptors.
- RAW decode output is sensor/linear data only. Demosaicing, highlight handling, denoising, lens correction, color transforms, and display conversion are processing operations later.
- Unsupported camera/compression/layout returns a precise capability result with safe maker/model/container evidence; no native fallback is attempted.

## Required implementation

- Add stable RAW container/camera/capability descriptors, `RawDecodeRequest`, `RawFrame`, plane/layout/CFA/black-level/matrix/opcode/preview types, limits, receipts, and errors.
- Build a `rawler` adapter that accepts [0067] random access (or a bounded stable copy only if backend API requires seekable file ownership), maps all errors, honors cancellation/limits, and exposes owned RustTable values.
- Register the RAW prober/capability resolver in [0068] with deterministic precedence over generic TIFF/ISOBMFF decoders when RAW-specific structure is proven.
- Generate a capability manifest from the pinned backend camera/format data, keyed by maker/model/container/compression/CFA and linked to corpus fixtures.
- Validate dimensions/areas/CFA phase/levels/matrices/offsets/plane sizes/non-finite metadata and backend-declared allocations before constructing a frame.
- Add fake/backend corpus tests for camera match, ambiguous aliases, generic TIFF non-RAW, unsupported camera/compression, corrupted metadata, excessive dimensions, cancellation, stable-copy cleanup, source mutation, and capability-manifest drift.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] No RawSpeed/LibRaw/dcraw/native RAW dependency enters the graph.
- [ ] All backend outputs are converted into validated RustTable-owned contracts.
- [ ] RAW-vs-generic-container selection is deterministic and evidence-backed.
- [ ] Unsupported cases identify the exact missing capability without partial output.
- [ ] No pixel decode beyond adapter smoke fixtures or image processing is implemented here.

## Dependencies

Depends on #168, #174, #222, #223, #224, and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only RAW contracts, pure-Rust backend adapter, probing/capability manifest, validation, receipts, and tests. DNG/vendor pixel decode is [0078]/[0079].
issues.title: Implement the pure-Rust RAW container registry and RawFrame contract

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Decode standards-based DNG/TIFF RAW files into validated [0077] `RawFrame` values while preserving sensor precision, CFA/linear layout, calibration metadata, embedded previews, and DNG opcode evidence.

## Fixed decisions

- Use the pinned pure-Rust `rawler` backend plus RustTable’s checked TIFF/DNG adapters; do not use Adobe DNG SDK, RawSpeed, LibRaw, dcraw, or inherited darktable code.
- Support classic TIFF and BigTIFF DNG 1.1–1.7, single-frame still images, Bayer mosaics, non-Bayer CFA descriptors supported by the manifest, and linear RAW with 1/3/4 channels.
- Support 8–16-bit unsigned samples in strips or tiles with compression None, lossless JPEG (JPEG-92), Deflate, and JPEG XL where the backend supports the declared DNG profile. Unsupported proprietary compression returns a precise capability error.
- Preserve original mosaic/linear samples as `U16`; unpacking may left-align only when explicitly recorded in the receipt. No black subtraction, white normalization, demosaic, opcode execution, color conversion, denoise, or sharpening occurs here.
- Parse and validate active area, default crop, masked areas, CFA repeat/pattern/plane color/layout, black-level repeat/deltas, white levels, linearization table, analog balance, AsShotNeutral, color/calibration/forward matrices, illuminants, baseline exposure/noise/sharpness, noise profile, row/column scale, opcode lists 1–3, and embedded previews.
- DNG opcode payloads are represented as typed descriptors when supported and exact bounded opaque bytes otherwise. They are never executed in the decoder.

## Required implementation

- Register DNG-specific probing ahead of generic TIFF only when DNGVersion plus required RAW tags are present.
- Select the RAW IFD deterministically: non-reduced image with supported photometric/layout and largest active pixel area; ties use lowest IFD offset. Inventory all other IFD/SubIFD previews.
- Decode strips/tiles with checked offsets/counts, predictor handling, lossless-JPEG/JXL integration, sample unpacking, and exact CFA phase relative to the stored full sensor and active crop.
- Validate every dimension/area relationship, tag count/type, repeat pattern, matrix size/finiteness, level range, channel count, opcode length, compression parameters, and decompressed-byte budget before constructing `RawFrame`.
- Emit receipts containing DNG versions, selected IFD, compression/precision/layout, sensor/active/crop areas, CFA, levels, matrices/illuminants, opcode inventory, previews, source/output bytes, and backend version.
- Add corpus/differential/fuzz tests for uncompressed/JPEG-92/Deflate/JXL, strips/tiles, classic/BigTIFF, Bayer/linear, black deltas/linearization, crops/masked pixels, matrices/WB, previews, every opcode-list form, malformed IFD graphs/tags/offsets, bombs, cancellation, and source mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported fixtures produce exact stored sensor samples and validated metadata descriptors.
- [ ] CFA phase, active area, and crop remain correct for tiled/stripped and oriented sources.
- [ ] Unsupported compression/layout/opcodes are identified without partial `RawFrame` publication.
- [ ] No RAW development operation is applied during decode.
- [ ] No native RAW dependency is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #227, #229, #233, and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only DNG probing/IFD selection/pixel decode, metadata/opcode/preview extraction, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. DNG opcode execution and demosaicing are processing issues.
issues.title: Implement DNG and standardized TIFF RAW decoding

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Decode the supported non-DNG vendor RAW families into validated [0077] `RawFrame` values using the pinned pure-Rust `rawler` backend and an explicit camera/codec coverage manifest.

## Fixed decisions

- Initial required families are Canon CR2/CR3, Nikon NEF/NRW, Sony ARW/SR2/SRF, Fujifilm RAF including X-Trans, Olympus ORF, Panasonic/Leica RW2/RWL, Pentax PEF, Samsung SRW, Hasselblad 3FR/FFF, Phase One IIQ, and Epson ERF where the pinned backend declares production support.
- No LibRaw, RawSpeed, dcraw process, camera-vendor SDK, or native fallback is permitted. A file outside the reviewed manifest is `UnsupportedCameraOrCodec`, not an extension-based best effort.
- Support compressed and uncompressed layouts exposed by the backend, including lossless JPEG variants, packed samples, delta-coded rows, tiled/strip storage, and CR3 ISOBMFF item selection. Proprietary lossy modes are supported only when covered by corpus/reference tests.
- Preserve original `U16` mosaic or linear samples, active/crop/masked areas, Bayer or 6×6 X-Trans CFA and phase, black/white levels, camera matrices/illuminants, white balance, orientation, and preview descriptors. Do not perform black subtraction, demosaic, denoise, highlight recovery, lens correction, or color conversion.
- Camera quirks and aliases are data entries with stable IDs and exact source evidence. Format logic may not hard-code model-name string branches outside the generated profile data.

## Required implementation

- Generate `architecture/raw-camera-capabilities.toml` from the pinned backend plus reviewed RustTable overrides, recording maker/model aliases, container, decoder path, compression modes, precision, CFA/layout, fixture IDs, and support status.
- Implement vendor/container selection, backend decode, and conversion to `RawFrame`, including CR3 item/grid handling, RAF X-Trans phase, maker-note-derived black/white/WB values, masked pixels, and embedded JPEG previews.
- Validate backend result against the matched capability entry: dimensions, precision, CFA, plane count, crop/active area, levels, matrices, compression, and expected quirks. A mismatch is a decoder error and manifest-drift finding.
- Normalize model matching only through [0080]/[0087] aliases; retain raw maker/model bytes and backend match evidence.
- Emit receipts with family/container/camera-profile ID, compression/precision/layout/CFA, sensor/active/crop areas, levels/WB/matrices, previews, bytes, backend version, and any reviewed quirk IDs.
- Add corpus/differential/fuzz tests for each required family, compressed/uncompressed variants, Bayer/X-Trans, high-resolution/odd dimensions, multi-shot or unsupported modes, incorrect maker-note data, alias ambiguity, corrupt offsets/packed rows, resource bombs, cancellation, and source mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every manifest `supported` camera/codec entry has at least one registered positive fixture and a reference sample/metadata check.
- [ ] Decoded sensor samples and CFA/active-area metadata match the trusted reference for supported fixtures.
- [ ] Backend/manifest drift fails validation rather than silently broadening support.
- [ ] Unsupported cameras/codecs return precise evidence and no partial frame.
- [ ] No native or inherited RAW implementation enters the build.

## Dependencies

Depends on #233, #234, #165, and #174.

## One-PR boundary

Only vendor RAW capability data, selection/decode conversion, quirk validation, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and registry wiring. Metadata normalization and RAW development remain separate.
issues.title: Implement vendor RAW decoding and camera-profile coverage

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Convert heterogeneous DNG/vendor RAW metadata from [0078]/[0079] into one deterministic, provenance-rich camera/sensor record used by catalog metadata, default processing, white balance, noise profiles, and lens matching.

## Fixed decisions

- Preserve raw maker/model/unique-model strings and backend profile ID. Add normalized maker, model, camera key, sensor key, and variant only through reviewed alias tables; never overwrite source text.
- A `CameraKey` is `(normalized maker, normalized model, optional variant)`. Serial number is private provenance and never participates in public camera identity or logs.
- Sensor geometry records full mosaic dimensions, active area, default crop, masked areas, orientation, pixel aspect, CFA pattern/phase, channel meaning, precision, black/white levels, and optional dual-gain/multi-plane evidence.
- Calibration records illuminant-tagged camera-to-XYZ matrices, calibration/forward matrices, analog balance, AsShotNeutral/WB multipliers, baseline exposure, noise profile, and source precedence. Invalid or singular matrices remain findings and are not silently replaced.
- DNG standardized values outrank maker-note duplicates; reviewed camera-profile overrides outrank demonstrably invalid source values and retain both original and override provenance.

## Required implementation

- Define normalized camera/sensor/calibration domain types, provenance entries, quality/status, stable hashes, and conversion errors.
- Implement field reconciliation from `RawFrame`, DNG tags, maker notes, backend profile data, and override tables with exact precedence and conflict findings.
- Validate crop/masked-area containment, CFA phase after crop/orientation, level/channel counts, white>black, finite WB/matrices, matrix rank/conditioning, illuminant IDs, and reasonable sensor dimensions/pixel aspect.
- Produce a canonical `RawMetadataReceipt` containing source profile IDs, normalized keys, geometry/calibration hashes, selected values, conflicts, and fallback decisions without private serial/path values.
- Add fixtures for alias variants, contradictory maker-note/DNG values, invalid crops/levels/matrices/WB, X-Trans, monochrome/linear RAW, dual illuminants, missing calibration, reviewed overrides, and deterministic round-trip.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Equal decoded metadata yields identical normalized keys, values, provenance ordering, and hashes.
- [ ] Source strings/values remain available even when an override is selected.
- [ ] Invalid calibration never produces an executable profile silently.
- [ ] CFA/crop/orientation geometry remains internally consistent.
- [ ] No file parsing, demosaic, or catalog write is introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on #234, #235, and #165.

## One-PR boundary

Only RAW metadata normalization/reconciliation, override data, receipts, validation, and tests. General camera aliases are extended in [0087]; color transforms are later.
issues.title: Normalize RAW camera identity, sensor geometry, and calibration metadata

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Goal

Extract standards-based embedded photographic metadata from decoded containers into typed, provenance-rich records without executing maker-note code, mutating source files, or depending on Exiv2.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement a RustTable-owned bounded TIFF/EXIF parser shared with [0071]/[0078]. Do not link Exiv2, libexif, or inherited darktable metadata code.
- Read metadata only from [0067] snapshots and decoder-provided segment/item/IFD descriptors. The extractor never reopens a path or scans arbitrary container bytes independently.
- Support EXIF 2.x TIFF IFD0, ExifIFD, GPSIFD, InteroperabilityIFD, thumbnail IFD, embedded TIFF/DNG IFD metadata, JPEG APP1 EXIF, PNG `eXIf`, WebP EXIF, JPEG XL EXIF boxes, and AVIF/HEIF EXIF items.
- Support IPTC-IIM datasets from JPEG APP13 Photoshop resources and TIFF IPTC tags. XMP is inventoried but parsed only in [0082].
- Decode standard fields for camera/lens identity, capture/digitization timestamps and offsets/subseconds, exposure, aperture, ISO/sensitivity, focal length, focus distance, flash, metering/exposure/program modes, orientation, dimensions, resolution, copyright/artist/description, ratings where standardized, GPS coordinates/altitude/direction/datum/time, lens serial/camera serial, and embedded thumbnail descriptors.
- Maker notes and unknown/private tags are retained as bounded opaque descriptors with hash/offset/length and privacy classification. They are not interpreted in this issue.
- Raw serial numbers, owner names, GPS values, and free text are classified private and excluded from diagnostics by default.

## Required implementation

- Define `EmbeddedMetadata`, typed value/unit/rational/date/time/GPS types, tag provenance, raw-value descriptor, confidence/status, limits, and extraction receipt.
- Parse little/big-endian classic TIFF field types with checked offsets/counts, IFD cycle/depth limits, inline/out-of-line values, rational zero-denominator handling, encoded strings, and duplicate tag retention.
- Parse IPTC record/dataset lengths including extended lengths, character-set declaration, repeatable fields, and Photoshop resource block padding.
- Normalize timestamps into original text, parsed local components, optional UTC offset, subsecond precision, and ambiguity status; never guess a timezone.
- Convert GPS rationals and references only after complete validation; preserve original components and reject contradictory refs/ranges.
- Reconcile duplicate standard values within one embedded source using fixed precedence (ExifIFD over IFD0 for EXIF-owned fields; explicit offset/subsecond companions attached to their timestamp) while retaining all candidates/findings.
- Add corpus/fuzz tests for every container path, endian/type/count variants, nested/cyclic IFDs, thumbnails, duplicate/conflicting tags, zero/overflow rationals, invalid dates/GPS, encodings, IPTC extended lengths/resources, malformed offsets/segments, privacy redaction, cancellation, and source mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supported standard fields produce deterministic typed values with exact source locations and original raw evidence.
- [ ] Unknown/private/maker-note data is bounded and preserved as opaque descriptors, not executed or logged.
- [ ] Malformed metadata cannot escape container/source limits or invalidate an otherwise decodable image unless required structural bytes overlap the image.
- [ ] Timestamp and GPS normalization never guesses missing context.
- [ ] No native metadata library, XMP parser, catalog write, or source mutation is added.

## Dependencies

Depends on #223, #225, #226, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #235, and #178.

## One-PR boundary

Only embedded EXIF/GPS/IPTC parsing, typed records, opaque descriptors, privacy classification, receipts, fuzz/corpus tests, and decoder integration. XMP/maker-note normalization is later.
issues.title: Implement bounded embedded EXIF, GPS, and IPTC metadata extraction

## Outcome

Merge container, EXIF, maker-note, IPTC, embedded XMP, sidecar XMP, import defaults, catalog values, and user overrides into one canonical metadata view under explicit per-field precedence and conflict rules.

## Required work

- Define source classes, trust/priority, field-specific merge strategies, list deduplication, language selection, date/time precision, tag hierarchy, rating/label mapping, and immutable source provenance.
- Return both effective values and a deterministic conflict/decision receipt; never discard lower-priority values invisibly.
- Keep extracted source values distinct from catalog user overrides and export-only overrides.
- Add property/fixture tests for equal/conflicting values, null/clear markers, precision differences, duplicate tags, sidecar changes, invalid source values, and stable ordering.

## Acceptance criteria

- Equal input sets produce equal effective metadata regardless of enumeration order.
- Every conflict names sources, values or privacy-safe hashes, and the applied rule.
- Clearing an override reveals the next valid source rather than deleting extracted data.
- Persistence, file write-back, and UI are excluded.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Write approved canonical metadata updates to supported raster files or sidecars through staged, verified, atomic replacement while preserving image pixels and unmodified metadata structures.

## Required work

- Define format capability, writable field, embedded-versus-sidecar, loss-risk, backup, and conflict policies.
- Re-read source identity immediately before writing; reject stale/changed files and unsupported structural rewrites.
- Stage in the same filesystem, preserve documented permissions/timestamps, fsync where applicable, verify pixels/content and requested metadata, then atomically replace with recovery backup.
- Add fault injection for permission, disk-full, interrupted write, verification mismatch, rename failure, concurrent modification, unknown metadata, and retry.

## Acceptance criteria

- Failure before final replacement leaves the source byte-identical; failure after replacement retains a documented recovery path.
- Pixel data and non-targeted preservable metadata remain unchanged under format-specific verification.
- RAW originals are not modified unless a later explicit policy permits it; sidecars are preferred where required.
- UI and automatic background write-back are excluded.

## Dependencies

Depends on #240, #243, #198, #204, and supported raster decoders #234, #235, #236, #237, #238, #239.

Discover and snapshot explicitly supported XMP sidecars for primary assets without race-prone repeated reads, ambiguous filename guessing, or unbounded XML ingestion.

## Required work

- Define exact pairing rules for same-basename, virtual-copy/versioned, case-sensitive/case-insensitive filesystems, explicit user selection, and imported darktable conventions.
- Enumerate candidates deterministically and return absent, unique, ambiguous, inaccessible, changed-during-read, oversized, and unsupported-name outcomes.
- Read a selected sidecar once into an owned bounded snapshot with source key, byte length, content hash, filesystem identity, and privacy-safe diagnostics.
- Add fixtures for Unicode, multiple dots, case collisions, symlinks, hard links, concurrent replacement, size changes, permissions, and primary/sidecar same-file attacks.

## Acceptance criteria

- Discovery never selects an ambiguous sidecar automatically.
- The owned snapshot alone drives parsing and catalog registration; later stages do not reopen it.
- Changed-during-read and over-limit inputs produce no partial registration data.
- XMP parsing, history mapping, and sidecar writing are excluded.

Discover importable files from one or more roots with deterministic traversal, explicit include/exclude rules, supported-format probing, and safe handling of symlinks, hard links, permissions, filesystem boundaries, and cycles.

- Enumerate stable logical source keys while preserving raw filesystem paths only inside privacy-controlled adapters.
- Probe signatures cheaply before expensive decoding and distinguish unsupported, inaccessible, changed-during-scan, duplicate inode/content candidate, and sidecar files.
- Add temporary-filesystem tests for Unicode, case collisions, symlink loops, hard links, permission errors, disappearing files, device files, deep trees, and deterministic ordering.

## Acceptance criteria

- Equal directory state and policy yield equal ordered discoveries.
- Traversal is bounded and cannot follow a symlink cycle indefinitely or import special files.
- Discovery mutates no files and does not create catalog records.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Represent a multi-file import as a durable, resumable session with stable item identities, discovery order, policy snapshot, progress, per-item state, cancellation, and final summary.

## Required work

- Define session/item IDs, source discovery evidence, requested ingest policy, pending/running/succeeded/skipped/failed/cancelled states, attempts, and bounded diagnostics.
- Persist session creation and state transitions transactionally without treating partially imported items as complete.
- Resume only after revalidating source identity and policy compatibility; make stale/changed files explicit decisions.
- Add deterministic scheduling order, progress aggregation, cancellation tokens, crash/reopen recovery, retention, and cleanup.

## Acceptance criteria

- Restarting after any persisted transition cannot duplicate a successfully registered item or lose a failed item’s evidence.
- Equal discovery and policy produce equal session/item ordering and IDs under the documented generator.
- Cancellation stops new work and records in-flight outcomes without corrupting catalog state.
- Copy/move, decode, metadata, dedupe, and UI are delegated to later items.

## Dependencies

Depends on #221, #247, #204, and #179.

## Outcome

Copy or move discovered primary/sidecar assets into managed destinations using deterministic rename templates and recoverable staged filesystem operations.

## Required work

- Define destination roots, directory/filename template variables, sanitization, Unicode/case policy, sequence allocation, extension preservation, and maximum path/component limits.
- Produce a complete dry-run manifest with source identity, target path, sidecar relationship, collision decision, required space, and operation type.
- Stage copies with content verification and atomic promotion; implement moves as verified copy/promote followed by explicit source cleanup where cross-device atomic rename is unavailable.
- Handle existing identical/different targets, case-only collisions, concurrent creation, permissions, disk-full, cancellation, retries, and source change.

## Acceptance criteria

- No target is overwritten without an explicit policy and exact precondition.
- A failed ingest leaves source data intact and all staged artifacts discoverable/cleanable.
- Final target hashes equal the snapshotted sources before catalog registration.
- Backup destination and session orchestration are separate issues.

## Outcome

Prevent accidental duplicate registration during import by reconciling discovered sources against catalog source keys, primary/sidecar asset hashes, current session items, and exact duplicate sets before decode or commit.

## Required work

- Define exact-source, exact-content, same-primary/different-sidecar, same-sidecar/different-primary, virtual-copy candidate, changed-source, and ambiguous outcomes.
- Query catalog/session indexes in one coherent preflight and repeat required checks inside the registration transaction.
- Support explicit policies to skip, link to existing asset, create a virtual copy, update missing-source location, or import as a separate duplicate with evidence.
- Test concurrent sessions, retries, case aliases, hard links, source relocation, content changes after preflight, sidecar conflicts, and stale catalog revisions.

## Acceptance criteria

- Automatic decisions rely only on exact verified identity; probable similarity is never treated as equality.
- Two concurrent imports cannot both register the same unique source or asset contrary to policy.
- Every skip/link decision is retained in the session report.
- Perceptual duplicate detection and UI review are excluded.

## Outcome

Extend managed ingest so each source can be verified to an independent primary destination and optional backup destination before catalog registration, with an explicit policy for required versus best-effort backup.

## Required work

- Define destination identities, free-space checks, path templates, collision policies, required/optional status, and per-destination receipts.
- Stage and verify both copies from the same owned source snapshot; avoid rereading mutable source files separately.
- Promote destinations under a documented failure matrix and retain recoverable staged data when one destination fails.
- Register the primary catalog source only after the required destination set is complete; record backup location/evidence without making it an alternative active source.
- Test same-filesystem/different-filesystem, target aliasing, disk-full, permission, checksum mismatch, cancellation, retries, and concurrent collisions.

## Acceptance criteria

- Required-backup policy cannot report import success with only the primary copy.
- Primary and backup targets are proven byte-identical to the snapshot and cannot resolve to the same file accidentally.
- Failure leaves the original source intact and produces a resumable session state.
- Backup browsing/restoration UI is excluded.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Coordinate discovery, snapshots, ingest copies, metadata/decode work, sidecars, deduplication, and catalog registration through a recoverable import saga with deterministic rollback and resume instructions.

## Required work

- Define staged resource ownership and compensating actions for temporary files, promoted targets, catalog rows, thumbnail artifacts, session transitions, and optional source cleanup.
- Persist a recovery journal before irreversible steps and reconcile it at startup.
- Classify safe automatic retry, manual decision, cleanup-only, and unrecoverable states with exact affected paths represented through privacy-safe aliases.
- Add fault injection at every step, process termination between steps, duplicate recovery runs, changed sources/targets, and insufficient permissions.

## Acceptance criteria

- No injected failure leaves an item reported successful without all required durable results.
- Recovery is idempotent and cannot delete a file whose ownership/precondition no longer matches.
- The report names completed, rolled-back, retained, and manual actions for every item.
- Import UI and notification presentation are excluded.

Depends on #248, #249, #250, #251, #204, and #221.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Generate a deterministic hierarchy of color-managed thumbnails and preview mipmaps for catalog/lighttable use, with explicit source/edit/profile identity and bounded durable cache entries.

## Required work

- Define mip levels, requested-size selection, aspect/orientation handling, resampling quality, output pixel format, thumbnail color encoding, and embedded-preview versus pipeline-render provenance.
- Key entries by asset content, edit revision, decoder/renderer/profile versions, level, and relevant configuration.
- Store entries through atomic staged writes with headers, checksums, dimensions, format, creation provenance, and configured total/per-entry limits.
- Add fixtures for embedded previews, RAW fallback, transparent images, extreme aspect ratios, missing/corrupt entries, stale keys, and interrupted writes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Equal inputs/configuration produce equal cache keys and pixels within the selected deterministic path.
- Corrupt or mismatched entries are rejected and regenerable, never treated as authoritative.
- Generation observes cancellation and memory bounds and publishes no partial entry.
- Eviction, background prefetch, UI, and full pixelpipe expansion are separate issues.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224, #231, #234, #235, #236, #237, #238, #239, #200, and existing render contracts.

## PR contract

One PR for mipmap domain/generator, cache-file format/store, integration fixtures, and tests only.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Manage [0097] cache lifecycle under bounded disk/memory budgets, invalidating exact affected entries and prefetching useful levels without starving interactive work.

## Required work

- Consume catalog/edit/config/profile/source change events from [0065] and derive deterministic key invalidations.
- Implement LRU/size/age policies, pin/lease semantics, low-disk handling, startup reconciliation, orphan/temp cleanup, and cache-schema upgrades.
- Add bounded priority queues for visible, near-visible, explicit, and maintenance prefetch; support cancellation and stale-result rejection.
- Persist minimal cache index state or rebuild it safely from entry headers, with corruption and clock-independent tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- Changing one photo edit/profile invalidates only entries whose keys depend on it.
- Eviction never removes leased entries or exceeds configured work/memory/disk bounds.
- Prefetch cannot delay higher-priority user renders and publishes no stale result.
- UI viewport prediction is excluded; callers provide requested IDs/priorities.

## Dependencies

Depends on #221, #253, #179, and #178.

## PR contract

One PR for lifecycle manager, invalidation/event mapping, eviction/prefetch scheduler, reconciliation, and tests only.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add the first real user-driven import workflow to the Iced application. A user can choose or drag supported raster files, RustTable snapshots and signature-probes the actual bytes, validates PNG/JPEG/TIFF, registers sources/photos/default edits atomically in redb, generates bounded current-edit previews, and immediately sees the imported photos in #535. This is the direct Rust replacement for darktable’s lighttable import module/job/session path for the intentionally bounded raster subset.

## Fixed decisions

- Entry points are `Import files…` in #535, a multi-file platform picker, regular-file drag/drop, and a deterministic service test API.
- Accepted formats are production-qualified PNG/JPEG/TIFF through #224. Signature/container evidence is authoritative; extension/MIME are hints.
- Every selected file is opened through #223. UI/app code never hands a bare path to a decoder after snapshot creation.
- Import mode is `ReferenceInPlace`: source files are never copied/moved/deleted. Managed libraries, watched folders/volumes, sidecars, camera import, and backups remain separate issues.
- Source identity is SHA-256 + exact bytes + decoder/probe identity. Exact-content duplicates reuse/select one existing photo; same path with changed content creates a new source/photo and records path reuse; duplicate selections produce one photo plus duplicate results.
- One source transaction atomically commits source evidence, photo, default edit, import metadata summary, and receipt. Failure creates no partial photo.
- Default edit is exposure 0 and RGB gains 1 until #264’s stack migration; it must remain visually neutral.
- Metadata scope is bounded to validated dimensions, orientation, color declaration, safe capture timestamp/camera make/model when available, and presentation-safe filename alias.
- Initial concurrency is four items, additionally constrained by #180 resources. Results remain in original selection order.
- Progress states are `Queued`, `Opening`, `Hashing`, `Probing`, `DecodingHeader`, `Registering`, `GeneratingPreview`, `Completed`, `AlreadyImported`, `Failed`, and `Cancelled`.
- Cancellation before commit leaves no record; after commit it may cancel preview and returns `ImportedPreviewPending` without rolling back valid catalog state.
- Preview uses the same current-edit CPU service as #181/#535: thumbnails max 256×256, selected preview max 1536×1536.
- Success refreshes #535 and selects the first newly imported/existing duplicate. Failed items remain in a bounded result panel with retry/remove.
- Directories, out-of-policy symlinks, devices, sockets, URLs, clipboard bytes, RAW, XMP/sidecars, recursive folders, metadata editing, and advanced duplicate UI are unsupported here.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/import_session.h` — type `dt_import_session_t` records import-session identity and selected source state.
- `src/control/jobs/film_jobs.c` — function `dt_film_import` performs queued film/file import work.
- `src/common/film.c` — function `dt_film_new` creates the source-root/film record.
- `src/common/exif.h` — function `dt_exif_read` is the import-time metadata extraction action.
- `src/imageio/imageio_common.h` — function family `dt_imageio_open*` is the signature/container validation handoff.
- `src/control/crawler.c` — function `dt_control_crawler_run` is the imported-source reconciliation action.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Define `RasterImportRequest`, ordered item IDs/options/progress/results/retry, and versioned privacy-safe receipts.
- Add `RasterImportService` coordinating #223, #224, catalog/import ports, and #181 preview through #180 task groups.
- Implement per-item snapshot/hash/probe/header validation, duplicate lookup, neutral default edit, atomic catalog transaction, preview request, and ordered aggregation.
- Add platform picker port, drop routing, import panel/progress rows, retry/cancel/remove/close, selection refresh, keyboard focus, and accessible names/states.
- Revalidate source immediately before catalog commit and preview decode.
- Emit source/photo/content/decoder/catalog/edit/preview identities, stages/timing/status without raw paths/metadata.
- Add architecture checks preventing UI filesystem/decoder/redb access and a second import model.
- Add source-accounting fixtures mapping each relevant `libs/import`, import-session, film-job, film/image, metadata, lighttable selection, action, and drop responsibility to this issue or a named later owner.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle picker cancel/unavailable, missing/permission/nonregular/symlink-policy file, source change, read/hash/probe/decode error, misleading extension, unsupported/malformed/oversized image, overflow, duplicate, catalog conflict/failure, preview failure, cancellation, and shutdown.
- One item failure does not roll back other committed items. One item’s transaction is atomic.
- Preview failure after commit reports imported-with-preview-error and retries without duplication.
- Retry repeats source validation and duplicate lookup.
- Closing panel does not cancel committed items; active work requires explicit cancel or shutdown.
- No path modifies/deletes source files or silently accepts changed bytes.

## Test matrix

- Single/multiple real PNG/JPEG/TIFF, mixed formats, ordered results, picker/drop, duplicate in request/catalog, path reuse.
- Misleading extension/MIME, malformed/truncated/oversized, source rename/replace/grow/truncate during each stage, permission/nonregular paths.
- Catalog transaction failure/conflict, preview failure/retry, cancellation before/after commit, shutdown, resource limits, progress order.
- Restart shows imported photo/default edit/preview.
- Iced keyboard/focus/screen-reader/progress/error/high-DPI behavior.
- Source-map mutations removing import action/session/job/film/image/selection responsibility or incorrectly claiming camera/folder support.
- Architecture/privacy checks for no UI direct I/O/decoder/redb and no raw paths/metadata in receipts.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-app -p rusttable-import -p rusttable-ui raster_import` passes real-file, duplicate, transaction, cancellation, UI, restart, and source-accounting suites.
- `cargo xtask product raster-import --formats png,jpeg,tiff --multi --duplicates --failures all --verify-ui-refresh` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 256` proves the bounded darktable import/lighttable responsibility is covered and exclusions delegated.
- Launch/import/select displays the real persisted current-edit preview through #535; sources remain byte-identical and failures leave no partial photo.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #223, #224, #450, #535, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements file selection/drop, bounded raster import, duplicate handling, atomic registration/default edit, preview/result UI, source-accounting, accessibility, receipts, and tests. Do not implement RAW, sidecars, folders, managed copies/backups, watchers, camera import, advanced metadata, duplicate comparison, or export.
issues.title: Import supported raster files from the Iced workspace

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Create `rusttable-color` as the sole owner of color-space identities and transform-plan contracts used by decoding, processing, preview presentation, scopes, proofing, and export. The crate replaces darktable’s distributed profile/color enums, matrices, transfer helpers, transform descriptors, and pipeline profile state with explicit safe Rust data while preserving all source responsibilities and deferring execution/parsing to named issues.

## Fixed decisions

- Safe product-domain crate below pixelpipe/render/UI; no Iced/WGPU/redb/SQLite/Tokio/catalog-store/native adapters.
- Every image API carries explicit `ColorEncoding`; no contextual sRGB/scene/display/profile assumptions.
- Built-ins: Linear/SRGB D65, Linear/DisplayP3 D65, Linear/Rec2020 D65, ACEScg D60, XYZ D50/D65, Lab/LCh D50 with fixed primaries/white points/matrices/transfers/ranges/roles and independent vectors.
- External/embedded `ProfileId` is content-addressed SHA-256 + size + profile class/model/PCS/parser version. No bytes/native handles in equality/cache/receipt/domain structs.
- Roles: Input, Working, Display, Export, Proof, Analysis. Intent: Perceptual, Relative, Saturation, Absolute; BPC/adaptation explicit.
- Bradford default; same-white identity explicit.
- Immutable plan primitives: identity, transfer, matrix, adaptation, bounded per-channel 1D LUT, bounded tetrahedral 3D LUT, ordered composite. Plan is data; execution later.
- LUT bounds: 1D 2..=65536; 3D edge 2..=65; finite f32; explicit packing/interpolation.
- Request identity includes endpoint profiles/encodings, role, intent, BPC, adaptation, precision, alpha, extended range, and planner version.
- Alpha is linear coverage, unchanged unless explicit pre/unpremultiply. Negative/HDR linear values remain representable.
- Canonical versioned DTOs with explicit tags; never Rust layout/hash-map persistence.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/common/colorspaces.h` — enum `dt_colorspaces_color_profile_type_t` defines color-profile role/type identity.
- `src/common/colorspaces.c` — function `dt_colorspaces_get_profile` resolves profile material for a declared role.
- `src/common/colorspaces_inline_conversions.h` — function `dt_XYZ_to_Lab` is a scalar color-conversion primitive.
- `src/common/iop_profile.c` — function `dt_ioppr_get_profile_info` constructs pipeline profile information.
- `src/common/custom_primaries.h` — type `dt_colorspaces_primaries_t` represents custom primary/white-point inputs.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Add white point/primaries/matrix/transfer/built-in/profile ID/role/intent/adaptation/precision/alpha/range/LUT/request/plan/warning/failure types.
- Implement checked constructors, canonical hashing/serialization, built-in constants/evaluation helpers, matrix invertibility, LUT/composite validation, and resource estimates.
- Define `ColorTransformPlanner` and deterministic built-in planner for exact identity/transfer/matrix/adaptation plans among built-ins.
- Add bounded receipt projections using aliases/hashes only.
- Add DAG/source checks preventing parallel color/profile/intent types in decoders, processing, render, export, UI.
- Add a source-accounting matrix mapping every darktable profile enum/type/plan/cache responsibility above to this contract or a named parser/executor/policy issue.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown schema, nonfinite/singular matrices, invalid primaries/white points/transfers, oversized/malformed LUTs, endpoint mismatch, unsupported intent/profile class, resource overflow.
- Equal logical requests produce byte-identical identity independent of load/map/path/process order.
- Unknown external profiles retain hash/class evidence and return PlannerUnavailable; never silent sRGB.
- Missing display/profile provider cannot select a default in this crate.

## Test matrix

- Published matrix/transfer vectors for every built-in; D50/D65 Bradford; identity/roundtrip/negative/HDR boundaries.
- Canonical request/plan serialization/hash/mutation/schema.
- Singular/nonfinite matrix, invalid gamma, LUT boundaries/overflow/sample count, composite endpoint mismatch.
- Alpha pass-through/pre-unpremultiply semantics.
- Property roundtrips and deterministic planning.
- Source-map goldens for colorspaces/iop_profile/pixelpipe/IOP/export/OpenCL consumers and explicit deferred owners.
- Architecture mutations adding duplicate color domain or forbidden dependencies.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-color` passes known-answer/property/schema/failure/source-accounting tests.
- `cargo xtask color contracts --all-builtins --verify-roundtrip --verify-identities` emits deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 257` proves every listed darktable color-contract responsibility is accounted for.
- #181/#535 express decode→linear working→sRGB output solely through these contracts; no native pointer/WGPU/UI/catalog/raw-profile bytes appear in public values.

## Dependencies

Depends on #177, #180, #222, #223, #495 for exact source/operation color semantics, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR creates color crate, built-ins, transform-plan/request contracts, deterministic built-in planner, canonical identities/codecs, source accounting, validation, DAG checks, and tests. Do not implement ICC parsing, LCMS, monitor discovery, profile selection, bulk execution, WGPU transforms, proof/gamut algorithms, or UI.

## Outcome

Implement deterministic foundational color math used by profile parsing and operation code: standard transfer functions, RGB↔XYZ matrices, chromatic adaptation, Lab/LCh/JzCzhz conversions, luminance helpers, and gamut tests.

## Required work

- Implement linear, sRGB, Rec.709/2020, gamma, PQ, and HLG transfer functions with explicit domain/clamp/non-finite policy.
- Build/invert validated 3×3 matrices and Bradford/CAT adaptation between supported white points.
- Implement XYZ/Lab/LCh and selected HDR perceptual conversions using reviewed constants and precision rules.
- Add reference-vector, round-trip, edge, monotonicity, matrix-singularity, NaN/Inf, and property tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- Reference vectors match standards or cited source tolerances.
- Valid round trips remain within the [0008] numerical class and invalid domains fail explicitly.
- CPU primitives allocate no per-pixel heap objects and expose batch-friendly APIs.
- ICC parsing, caching, SIMD, WGPU, and display policy are excluded.

## Outcome

Compile validated [0103] profiles and [0104] role decisions into reusable CPU transform plans and execute them over bounded image/tile views.

## Outcome

Execute supported [0105] color transform plans on WGPU for preview and export while preserving CPU fallback and explicit numerical parity.
