issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Execute #268 tile plans on WGPU with exact overlap/alignment/edge semantics, bounded device residency, and deterministic OOM recovery. Initial production target is the basic point pixelpipe; tiled/full WGPU must match #539 CPU/full-GPU under #516 without seams/quality change. This is the Rust replacement for darktable’s OpenCL tiled pixelpipe, device-memory-aware tile selection, resident intermediate handling, and bounded CPU fallback.

## Fixed decisions

- Orchestration split between pixelpipe planning and GPU adapters; operations receive one prepared tile and cannot own global loops/retries.
- Input one #266 snapshot, #271 plan, #267 ROI, #268 legal candidates. Execution selects validated candidates only.
- Initial kernels zero overlap, but framework supports declared overlap/origin alignment/clipped edges/nonoverlap commit for contract fixtures.
- Build complete resource/residency/submission plan before allocation: tile resources/bytes; transfers; node residency; #293 lifetimes; #296 dependencies/limits; assembly/readback.
- Keep intermediates resident across adjacent compatible GPU nodes when budget/format/color/ROI/failure policy allow; CPU-only transition is explicit #294 host boundary.
- Residency local to device generation.
- Full-GPU only if range/allocation fits; otherwise preferred legal tile candidate.
- OOM recovery via #274: discard/retire failed attempt; next strictly smaller candidate; max two retries; then same snapshot #539 CPU. No endless halving/partial reuse.
- Tiling never changes params/quality/precision/color/masks/export semantics.
- Completion may vary; assembly/receipts canonical row-major/node order.
- Readback commits nonoverlap output once into fresh host assembly lease; bounded coverage validation.
- Backend/device/implementation/tile plan enters cache only where numerical output may differ; qualification requires #516.
- Post-submit cancel retires; obsolete never assembles/caches/publishes.
- No multi-adapter, persistent quarantine, masks/blends, histogram, surface, or complex production kernel.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/develop/tiling.h` — type `dt_develop_tiling_t` declares overlap, alignment, and memory constraints.
- `src/develop/tiling.c` — helper `_maximum_number_tiles` bounds tile-grid selection and retry.
- `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c` — identifier `dt_opencl` marks device-resident pipeline execution.
- `src/common/opencl.c` — function `dt_opencl_alloc_device` is the tile resource allocation action.
- `src/develop/imageop.c` — functions `_iop_modify_roi_in` and `_iop_modify_roi_out` define per-operation tile ROI transformation.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h` — function `dt_mipmap_cache_get` is the host input/final-output cache boundary.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Define tile/residency/attempt/assembly plans, lifetime intervals, host boundaries, coverage, metrics/receipts/errors.
- Materialize #268 candidates, #293 admission/allocation, #294 transfers, #295 dispatch, #296 submissions, #272 cancellation, #274 retry, #269 assembly.
- Execute initial point full/tiled and fake overlap/geometry pipelines.
- Independently compare to #539 via #516 before accepting qualification.
- Emit bounded candidates/attempts/tile/node/memory/residency/transfer/submission/cache/failure/fallback/output receipts.
- Add architecture checks rejecting operation tile loops/ad hoc OOM/overlap writes.
- Add source-accounting generator mapping every darktable tiling/OpenCL-residency/OOM/host-boundary path to this issue or operation owner.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject changed snapshot/plan, impossible candidate, budget mismatch, generation, coverage gap/overlap, transfer/dispatch/submission/readback/OOM/loss/cancel, malformed tile/final descriptor/hash.
- Failed attempt discards partial assembly and cannot cache/promote.
- Cleanup uncertainty stops retry as invariant.
- Export CPU fallback exact; no quality/operation drop.
- CpuOnly is unavailable GPU, not failed CPU.

## Test matrix

- Full/many grids, odd/tiny/large, edges, overlap/alignment, constrained budgets, resident/host boundaries.
- CPU/full-GPU/tiled-GPU parity, alpha/extended/tails/completion order.
- OOM first/second/all, finite retries/CPU fallback, loss/transfer/dispatch/submission/readback/cancel/stale.
- Coverage/malformed/generation/cleanup failures.
- Backend smokes/CpuOnly.
- Model/property candidate monotonicity/finite attempts/lifetime/coverage.
- Source-map mutation changing tiling callback/residency/OOM path without owner.
- Architecture mutation operation loop/retry/overlap write.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-gpu -p rusttable-pixelpipe gpu_tiling` passes planning/residency/parity/OOM/coverage/cancel/backend/source suites.
- `cargo xtask pixelpipe gpu-run --fixture corpus.raster.png.16-alpha --stack basic --full-and-all-tiles --memory-pressure --faults all --cpu-parity` emits receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 297` proves all listed darktable GPU tiling/residency/fallback responsibilities are accounted for.
- Full/tiled meet #516 with no seams; OOM terminates within two grids+optional CPU; failed/obsolete publishes nothing/releases all.

## Dependencies

Depends on #268, #272, #274, #293, #294, #295, #296, #495, #516, #539, #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements GPU tile/residency/full/tiled execution, finite OOM retry, host boundaries, assembly/coverage, initial parity, source accounting, faults/backend tests, receipts, architecture checks. Do not implement multi-adapter, persistent quarantine, complex kernels, masks/blending, scopes, or surfaces.
issues.title: Implement GPU tiling and out-of-memory recovery

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Support hosts with multiple qualified WGPU adapters by assigning complete pixelpipe requests to one device under an explicit, deterministic workload policy. The policy must protect foreground preview work, keep resources/caches device-local, and fall back to the canonical CPU executor without splitting one tile/pipeline across incompatible devices.

## Fixed decisions

- Multi-adapter policy lives in `rusttable-gpu::assignment`; #180 owns service lifecycle and #301 remains the single product pixelpipe execution service.
- A request is assigned wholly to one adapter/device generation for one execution attempt. Cross-device node or tile splitting is prohibited for the initial policy because transfer and numerical costs outweigh unproven benefit.
- Candidate devices are only #290 behaviorally qualified, healthy for the session, and capable of every required kernel/format/limit in the exact #266 snapshot range. CPU is always an explicit candidate.
- Workload classes are `InteractivePreview`, `VisibleFullView`, `UserExport`, `VisibleThumbnail`, `PrefetchThumbnail`, and `BackgroundAnalysis` matching #273.
- Ranking is deterministic from:
  1. request-required feature/kernel/format capability;
  2. session health/device generation;
  3. user policy (`Automatic`, `PreferLowPower`, `PreferHighPerformance`, or exact adapter alias);
  4. display association when explicitly known for presentation work;
  5. current admitted/in-flight work and reserved foreground capacity;
  6. conservative available #293 budget;
  7. transfer cost from current data residency;
  8. stable adapter identity tie-break.
- `Automatic` defaults:
  - interactive/full-view prefers the display-associated or lowest-latency capable hardware adapter;
  - user export prefers the highest-throughput capable hardware adapter that does not consume foreground reservation;
  - thumbnails/analysis use remaining capable capacity and may choose CPU;
  - software/fallback WGPU adapters rank below canonical CPU unless separately qualified as beneficial for that workload.
- At least one capable adapter or CPU lane remains reserved for interactive/full-view work. Background/export admission cannot consume all device/memory slots when foreground work is queued.
- Device-local resources, pipelines, caches, and intermediate identities never cross adapter/device generations. Reassignment starts from immutable host/source/snapshot evidence and fresh target-device resources.
- Already submitted GPU work is not migrated/preempted. A newer interactive request can choose another capable idle device or CPU; obsolete results remain unpublished through #272.
- Busy-device fallback does not silently change export quality. Export may wait within its deadline, choose another fully capable device, or use #539 CPU with exact semantics.
- User exact-adapter preference is best effort for preview and strict only when the request explicitly marks it required. An unavailable/unsupported exact adapter returns a typed finding and CPU/automatic fallback according to request policy.
- Persistent fault quarantine is #299. This issue consumes #290 session health and exposes assignment hooks; it does not persist health/blacklists.
- No cross-device peer transfer, shared textures, linked-adapter APIs, tile striping, or multi-GPU shader specialization is implemented.

## Implementation

- Define assignment request/candidate/policy/user preference/device load/residency/decision/rejection/receipt types and checked configuration.
- Implement deterministic candidate filtering/ranking, foreground reservations, per-device admission counters, assignment/release, reassignment after pre-submit failure, and integration with #301 backend selection.
- Integrate #271 mode/purpose, #290 capability/session health, #293 budget state, #296 in-flight submissions, #297 tile estimates, and #179 configuration snapshots.
- Add a model scheduler with synthetic integrated/discrete/eGPU/software/equal/failing devices and a real adapter-enumeration smoke where multiple adapters exist.
- Emit bounded assignment receipts containing request/workload, candidate aliases, exact rejection/rank reasons, selected device/CPU, load/budget summaries, and no raw device/driver strings or paths.
- Add architecture checks preventing product callers from selecting adapters directly or moving resources across device generations.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown policy/preference, duplicate adapter identities, stale capability/load snapshots, cross-device resource residency, impossible strict preference, admission/accounting overflow, and assignment after shutdown.
- Device loss before submit reassigns once through the normal policy when the request is live; loss after submit follows #296/#274 cleanup and then may start a fresh attempt.
- A request that cannot fit any capable device may use CPU if allowed; otherwise it fails with every bounded candidate reason.
- Configuration changes affect new attempts only and never mutate an in-flight assignment.
- Equal candidate state produces byte-identical decisions independent of adapter enumeration order.

## Test matrix

- Integrated/discrete pairs, multiple discrete adapters, eGPU, software adapter, equal identities/metrics, one/no capable GPU, CPU-only, and device-generation changes.
- Every workload class and user preference; foreground reservation under export/thumbnail load; waiting/deadline; load/resource release.
- Data residency cost, prohibited cross-device reuse, reassignment before/after submit, device loss, cancellation/stale request, and shutdown.
- Determinism under randomized enumeration/completion order and model/property stress.
- Architecture mutation fixtures for direct adapter choice, cross-device cache/resource reuse, or unbounded per-device queues.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-gpu multi_adapter` passes ranking, reservation, residency, fault, determinism, and model suites.
- `cargo xtask gpu assignment-matrix --synthetic-all --foreground-under-load --faults --verify-device-locality` emits deterministic receipts.
- Foreground work cannot be starved by background/export admission, and no resource/cache entry is reused across incompatible devices.
- CPU remains available when every GPU is unavailable, busy beyond policy, or fails.

## Dependencies

Depends on #179, #271, #290, #293, #296, #297, and #301.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements multi-adapter candidate/ranking/admission policy, foreground reservations, device-locality enforcement, configuration integration, model/real smokes, receipts, and tests. Do not implement persistent quarantine, cross-device transfers, tile/pipeline splitting, presentation surfaces, or new kernels.
issues.title: Implement multi-adapter selection and workload priority policy

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Persist privacy-safe, versioned GPU fault health so repeatedly failing adapter/driver/kernel combinations stop being selected across sessions while unrelated GPU capabilities and canonical CPU processing remain available. Quarantine decisions must be narrow, evidence-backed, reversible, and invalidated when the relevant software, driver, device, or kernel identity changes.

## Fixed decisions

- GPU health/quarantine lives in `rusttable-gpu::health`; #301 remains the product execution service and #274 owns per-request retry/fallback.
- A `GpuFaultKey` is exact and scoped by adapter stable identity, backend, driver identity hash, WGPU version, device-generation lineage, shader/entry/implementation identity, operation/kernel identity, numerical tier, and failure class. Incomplete keys cannot create persistent quarantine.
- Fault classes are `Validation`, `DeviceLoss`, `OutOfMemory`, `Timeout`, `Submission`, `Mapping`, `OutputValidation`, `ParityMismatch`, and `InternalInvariant`. User cancellation, source/edit errors, unsupported capability, and ordinary CPU fallback are not GPU faults.
- Severity/scope are explicit:
  - adapter-wide only for repeated device loss or device-wide validation corruption;
  - kernel-specific for shader/pipeline/dispatch/output/parity faults;
  - workload-size/budget-specific OOM evidence adjusts candidate policy but does not permanently blacklist the adapter;
  - transient timeout/submission faults start session cooldown before persistent action.
- Session policy is fixed:
  - first qualifying fault records evidence and disables the exact path for 30 seconds;
  - second identical fault in 24 hours disables that exact path for the session;
  - third identical fault across at least two sessions within 14 days creates persistent quarantine;
  - `ParityMismatch` or proven corrupted output quarantines the exact kernel/device identity immediately for the session and requires a successful explicit recovery probe before reuse.
- Persistent records expire after 30 days without recurrence and are invalidated immediately by driver, WGPU, shader/implementation, backend, or adapter identity change. Expiry/invalidation never deletes audit evidence until bounded compaction.
- Persistent state is stored through #179 configuration/state services as a bounded versioned record, maximum 256 active records and 1,024 compacted audit summaries. It contains hashes, classes, counters, times, and stable codes only—no raw device names, paths, driver logs, source/edit data, or pixels.
- Selection/query returns `Allowed`, `Cooldown`, `SessionQuarantined`, `PersistentlyQuarantined`, or `RecoveryProbeRequired`, with exact scope and expiry.
- CPU fallback is always selected through #274/#301 for a live request after GPU failure when semantically allowed. Quarantine never changes edit, quality, color, export settings, or cache identity.
- A user can clear one exact record or all records from a later preferences UI; this PR exposes service APIs and test commands only. Clearing does not mark the path healthy—it permits normal qualification/attempt again.
- Recovery probes are bounded, use registered #292 probe/basic kernels and real output validation, do not access user images/catalogs, and run only on explicit service/startup maintenance policy when idle. One successful probe clears cooldown/session quarantine; persistent quarantine requires two successful probes in separate process sessions.
- Multi-adapter assignment #298 consumes health states but is not required for single-device quarantine correctness.

## Implementation

- Define fault key/evidence/class/scope/severity/record/state/policy/recovery/compaction/receipt types and canonical serialization.
- Implement event ingestion from #290/#295/#296/#297/#301, exact-key aggregation, thresholds, cooldown/session/persistent transitions, expiry/invalidation, bounded storage/compaction, query, clear, and recovery-probe results.
- Integrate #274 fallback and #301 backend selection so quarantined paths are filtered before allocation/submission and a newly qualifying fault can stop future attempts without affecting the current cleanup path.
- Integrate optional #298 candidate filtering through the same immutable health snapshot.
- Add deterministic fake clock/session IDs and synthetic device/kernel identities for model tests, plus real probe smoke on available backends.
- Emit bounded health receipts with aliases/hashes, transition reason, expiry, recovery results, and no raw driver/native output.
- Add architecture checks preventing independent blacklists/counters or direct persistence of raw GPU strings elsewhere.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/incomplete keys, unknown classes, future schema, counter/time overflow, duplicate/conflicting records, storage corruption, and transition without qualifying evidence.
- Corrupt persistent state is quarantined as configuration data, ignored for selection, and replaced only after an explicit validated rewrite; it cannot disable all GPU silently.
- Clock rollback uses monotonic session ordering plus bounded wall-clock evidence and cannot extend quarantine indefinitely.
- OOM is never promoted to permanent adapter failure solely because large requests exceed budget.
- Device/kernel identity change invalidates only matching records; unrelated adapters/kernels remain unaffected.
- Health-service failure degrades to conservative session-only behavior and CPU fallback, not application failure.

## Test matrix

- Every fault class/scope, first/second/third thresholds, session boundaries, expiry, identity/version/driver invalidation, parity-corruption immediate session action, and recovery probes.
- OOM size-specific handling, transient timeout cooldown, repeated loss adapter scope, kernel-only validation, multiple adapters/kernels, and unrelated-path preservation.
- Persistent storage migration/corruption/limits/compaction, clock rollback, concurrent ingestion/query/clear, cancellation/shutdown, and restart.
- Integration with #301 exact CPU fallback and #298 filtering.
- Privacy scans and architecture mutations adding broad/incomplete blacklists or raw strings.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-gpu gpu_health` passes policy/model/persistence/recovery/privacy/integration suites.
- `cargo xtask gpu health-matrix --all-faults --sessions 4 --expiry --identity-changes --recovery --verify-narrow-scope` emits deterministic receipts.
- Repeated identical faults stop selecting only the exact unsafe path; unrelated devices/kernels and #539 CPU remain usable.
- Driver/WGPU/shader upgrades invalidate stale quarantine and require fresh evidence rather than carrying an obsolete blacklist forward.

## Dependencies

Depends on #178, #179, #274, #290, #295, #296, #297, and #301. Integrates with #298 when multi-adapter policy is available but does not depend on it.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements GPU fault-health domain, threshold/cooldown/session/persistent policy, bounded persistence/compaction, recovery probes, selection/fallback integration, model/backend tests, privacy receipts, and architecture checks. Do not implement preferences UI, general diagnostics dashboards, multi-adapter policy, new GPU kernels, or performance tuning.
issues.title: Implement GPU fault quarantine and CPU retry behavior

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the color-managed WGPU presentation path that turns a completed immutable pixelpipe image into a display-ready texture for the Iced viewport without unnecessary CPU readback. Presentation must be monitor/profile/surface/HDR/proof/gamut aware, generation safe, and independent of export pixels. Unsupported presentation capabilities fall back explicitly to the existing sRGB CPU/raster presentation path without changing the source edit or pipeline result.

## Fixed decisions

- Presentation domain lives in `rusttable-render::presentation`; WGPU resource/shader/submission implementation lives in `rusttable-gpu`. Iced widgets consume an opaque presentation handle and view model, not raw device/resources/color transforms.
- Input is one immutable completed #266/#301 image result or device-resident result with explicit descriptor, color encoding/profile identity, alpha, ROI/dimensions, device generation, and content identity.
- A `DisplayPresentationRequest` contains:
  - source result identity;
  - target monitor/display identity and resolved profile from #509/#260;
  - surface format/color-space capability and WGPU device generation;
  - SDR/HDR mode and target/reference luminance;
  - rendering intent/BPC/adaptation;
  - optional proof profile and gamut-warning mode from #262;
  - background/checker color for alpha presentation;
  - viewport output dimensions/scale and interpolation;
  - dither policy;
  - generation/publication target.
- Presentation never mutates or replaces the underlying source/pipeline/cache value. Display/proof/gamut/background/dither choices are presentation identities and are excluded from export/edit identity.
- SDR default is sRGB display output with the exact monitor transform when available. Missing/unusable monitor profile produces an explicit `SrgbFallback` presentation state and diagnostic; it does not silently label the monitor profile as sRGB in persistent state.
- HDR is enabled only when operating-system/surface/device capability, monitor identity, format, and luminance evidence are all qualified. Initial HDR output supports scRGB linear float or platform-supported HDR surface modes exposed safely by WGPU/Iced. Unsupported/failed HDR falls back to SDR with visible state; no tone-mapping mode is guessed.
- Color transforms use #257/#260/#261 validated plans. Built-in matrix/transfer paths and validated LUT plans may execute on WGPU through registered #292 presentation shaders. Complex/native-only profile transforms use the CPU transform result and upload it; no approximate matrix replacement is permitted.
- Proof/gamut behavior uses #262 plan identities. Gamut warnings are an overlay/presentation result and never modify cached working/export pixels.
- Alpha is composited only for presentation against the selected checker/background in linear display space. The immutable source alpha remains unchanged.
- Dithering is deterministic and keyed by content/presentation identity plus pixel coordinates, not frame/time/random state. It is applied only when quantizing to a lower-precision surface.
- Texture/resource/cache identity includes source content, source color, transform/proof/gamut plan, monitor/profile, surface format/color space, HDR/luminance, background, dither, output dimensions/scale, shader/implementation, device generation, and policy schema.
- Monitor/profile/surface/device changes increment a presentation generation and invalidate only matching presentation cache entries. A late texture for a previous monitor/window/generation cannot publish.
- Device-resident zero-copy reuse is permitted only when source and presentation use the same device generation and exact resource ownership/format/access can be represented through #293. Otherwise use explicit GPU copy/transform/upload; cross-device resources are never reused.
- Iced integration uses the established custom shader/primitive boundary from #420. Zoom/pan/overlay interaction remains a separate UI issue; this PR supplies the texture/primitive presentation service and minimal selected-preview integration.
- CPU-only or failed GPU hosts retain the existing #535 sRGB image-handle presentation. The application remains usable.

## Implementation

- Define presentation request/plan/result/handle/cache/progress/failure/receipt types and checked constructors.
- Implement monitor/surface resolution, color/proof/gamut transform planning, HDR/SDR selection, alpha/background/dither plan, and exact cache identity.
- Add reviewed WGSL shaders/reflection for matrix/transfer/LUT presentation, alpha compositing, gamut overlay, and deterministic dither through #292; use #293–#296 resources/transfers/dispatch/submission.
- Integrate #272 scopes/publication permits, #290 device generation/capabilities, #299 health filtering, and #301 device-resident output when available.
- Implement CPU-transform/upload fallback for plans that cannot execute exactly on WGPU and CPU-only raster presentation fallback for unavailable presentation GPU paths.
- Integrate one selected-photo preview in #535 with opaque texture handles, generation-safe replacement, resize/monitor-change/device-loss handling, and explicit fallback/HDR/proof status.
- Emit bounded receipts containing source/presentation/monitor-profile aliases, plan/shader/device/surface identities, HDR/fallback state, texture/cache identity, timings, and no raw profile bytes, paths, EDID/serial, pixels, or unbounded driver text.
- Add architecture checks rejecting color transforms in Iced widgets, raw WGPU texture ownership in view models, stale monitor-independent cache keys, or export reuse of presentation pixels.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle missing/changed monitor/profile, invalid transform/proof plan, unsupported HDR/surface/format, resource/upload/shader/dispatch/submission failure, device loss/quarantine, cross-device source, cache corruption, resize/monitor movement, cancellation, stale generation, and shutdown.
- A presentation failure preserves the last valid texture only when it belongs to the same source photo/edit and target window; otherwise show the explicit CPU/failure placeholder.
- A failed HDR transition re-plans SDR from immutable source pixels; it never reuses partially transformed HDR output.
- Profile or monitor changes cannot publish a texture created for the old identity even when dimensions/content are equal.
- Publication/cache failure never invalidates the underlying processed image.
- CPU fallback remains bounded and cancellable and cannot block export/background work indefinitely.

## Test matrix

- Built-in sRGB/P3/Rec2020 display plans, matrix/transfer and LUT plans, CPU-only complex plan, identity and monitor-profile change.
- SDR, qualified scRGB/HDR, unsupported HDR fallback, luminance/format/surface capability changes.
- Alpha/checker/background, deterministic dither, proof/gamut warning, transparent/opaque, negative/HDR source values.
- Same-device resident reuse, required copy, CPU upload, cross-device rejection, cache hit/miss/invalidation, device generation/loss/quarantine.
- Rapid resize/monitor movement/profile update/window close with out-of-order completions and stale publication rejection.
- CPU/WGPU color-patch parity against #516 and independently known color vectors.
- Iced high-DPI/multi-window/minimal selected-preview integration, accessibility status, and architecture/privacy mutations.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-render -p rusttable-gpu -p rusttable-ui display_presentation` passes planning, color, HDR, cache, generation, fallback, backend, and UI suites.
- `cargo xtask presentation qualify --all-builtins --proof-gamut --sdr-hdr --monitor-changes --device-loss --cpu-parity` emits deterministic receipts.
- Moving a selected preview between monitors/profile identities cannot reuse stale transformed pixels, and unsupported HDR/GPU paths visibly fall back to correct SDR/CPU presentation.
- Export/current-edit pixel identities remain independent of display/proof/presentation choices.

## Dependencies

Depends on #257, #260, #261, #262, #272, #290, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #299, #301, #509, and #535.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements presentation request/plan/cache domain, exact GPU/CPU transform paths, HDR/SDR selection, alpha/background/dither/proof/gamut presentation, device-resident reuse, minimal Iced selected-preview integration, receipts, and tests. Do not implement general zoom/pan interaction, crop/mask overlays, scopes, monitor-profile discovery itself, full darkroom UI, multi-adapter assignment, export transforms, or new processing operations.
issues.title: Implement color-managed viewport texture presentation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Integrate the completed WGPU stack into the real RustTable processing service and accelerate the same basic raster pixelpipe already delivered by #539 and used by #535/#536. On a qualified device, transfer decode/encode, exposure, linear offset, and RGB gain execute as one full or tiled WGPU pipeline; on unavailable or failed GPU, the exact immutable snapshot executes on CPU with complete provenance and no user-data or quality change.

This is the first production GPU milestone and the bounded source-accounted replacement for darktable’s real CPU/OpenCL pixelpipe selection, execution, tiling, transfer, caching, fallback, and preview/export publication path. It deliberately does not wait for masks, histograms, external ICC profiles, SIMD, complex darktable operations, multi-adapter scheduling, or performance baselines.

## Fixed decisions

- Add one `PixelpipeExecutionService` implementation owned by application composition. It selects `WgpuBasic` only when the exact #266 snapshot/mode/node range has qualified #265 GPU bindings and the current #290 capability snapshot satisfies every kernel; otherwise it selects `CpuCanonical` from #539.
- Backend selection occurs once per execution attempt and is recorded. Operation/UI code cannot force raw WGPU features or bypass CPU availability.
- The production WGPU-supported range is exactly:
  - built-in sRGB transfer decode;
  - `rusttable.exposure`;
  - `rusttable.linear_offset`;
  - `rusttable.rgb_gain`;
  - built-in sRGB transfer encode.
  Disabled nodes are omitted through normal snapshot semantics. Any unsupported enabled node creates an explicit CPU host range or, for this initial integration, selects the full CPU pipeline rather than claiming partial-GPU acceleration.
- Use full-image WGPU execution when #293 budget permits; otherwise use #297 tiles. Full/tiled GPU and CPU execute the same #266 snapshot/#271 mode/output request.
- Preview, Full, Thumbnail, and Export are supported. Export allows GPU only after the exact backend/device/kernel combination passes #516 qualification; otherwise it uses CPU without quality degradation.
- GPU output is read back into a typed #269 immutable image for the existing renderer/export path. Zero-copy viewport presentation remains separate; this PR prioritizes correctness and product integration over avoiding readback.
- Cache integration uses #270 identities including actual implementation/backend/device generation where required. CPU and GPU entries are not assumed interchangeable unless the registered tolerance/cache policy explicitly permits it; final current-result selection is independent.
- #274 fallback actions are fixed:
  - preparation/feature unavailable → CPU without submitting GPU work;
  - allocation/OOM → #297 smaller grids, at most two, then CPU;
  - validation/dispatch/submission/device loss/timeout/output mismatch → discard all GPU output and execute CPU once when request is still live;
  - cancellation/stale generation → no fallback and no publication.
- A GPU result is independently validated for descriptor, dimensions, finite policy, and content hash, then optionally compared against CPU in qualification/test mode. Normal product preview does not execute CPU redundantly after qualification.
- #535/#536 preview UI keeps the last successful image while GPU/CPU work runs and displays a bounded backend/fallback status. No new user GPU settings UI is included.
- Startup with no adapter/device remains fully functional and does not show an error modal; health/diagnostics state reports CPU-only.
- Numerical acceptance uses schema-3 #516 per kernel/range. No broad #286/architecture-SIMD dependency is required for this basic WGPU slice.
- Profiling counters are recorded—latency, transfers, submissions, cache, memory, tile attempts—but #455 owns benchmark baselines and thresholds later.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/develop/pixelpipe.h` — type `dt_dev_pixelpipe_t` defines pipeline purpose/state and typed ROI/profile buffers.
- `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c` — function `dt_dev_pixelpipe_process` executes ordered CPU/OpenCL pipeline work and result handoff.
- `src/develop/develop.h` — type `dt_develop_t` owns image/history and full/preview pipeline generations.
- `src/develop/imageop.c` — callback action `commit_params` prepares operation state for the selected backend.
- `src/develop/tiling.h` — type `dt_develop_tiling_t` provides full-versus-tiled resource constraints.
- `src/common/opencl.c` — function `dt_opencl_events_flush` is queued-device completion/error evidence.
- `data/kernels/programs.conf` — program entry `basic` selects the initial exposure-kernel program.
- `data/kernels/basic.cl` — kernel `exposure` is the only direct initial operation-kernel identity.
- `src/iop/exposure.c` — field `dt_iop_exposure_global_data_t::kernel_exposure` and callback `process_cl` bind direct exposure GPU execution.
- `src/common/mipmap_cache.h` — function `dt_mipmap_cache_get` supplies source/preview/full buffer acquisition.
- `src/imageio/imageio.c` — function `dt_imageio_export` is exact export pipeline construction/publication.
- `src/control/jobs.c` — function `dt_control_job_cancel` provides job cancellation around interactive/export work.
- `src/common/profiling.c` — function `dt_timer_start_with_name` is timing provenance, not a correctness threshold.


**Preserve:** the listed upstream responsibilities, their observable ordering/default/failure semantics, and the explicitly named acceptance oracles; any behavior not yet represented by a typed anchor remains blocked migration debt.

**Redesign:** implement the responsibility in safe Rust at this issue’s Rust-owned boundaries, without copying, compiling, linking, or invoking darktable C/C++/OpenCL code.

**Excluded:** unanchored upstream behavior, source-text translation, and any product scope outside this issue’s one-PR boundary.

Rust ownership: the Rust crates/modules and tests named by this issue’s implementation and acceptance contracts own the replacement behavior.
## Implementation

- Define execution backend/attempt/range/selection/result/progress/metrics/receipt types and integrate them into #180 services.
- Compose #290 capability/device, #292 shaders, #293 resources, #294 transfers, #295 dispatch, #296 submission, #297 full/tiled execution, #274 fallback, #272 publication, and #270 cache behind one application-facing service.
- Register the initial GPU bindings through #265 and make #539 CPU implementations their explicit references.
- Replace any test-only GPU smoke invocation with the product execution path; no parallel product GPU coordinator is allowed.
- Integrate the real #181 fixture and #535/#536 selected-photo/current-edit workflows. Add a test/diagnostic backend badge/status view model only; user configuration is later.
- Run qualification per supported backend/device/kernel identity and persist only bounded evidence/health state allowed by #290 policy. An unqualified current device remains CPU-only.
- Emit `PixelpipeExecutionReceipt` containing snapshot/mode/backend/device alias/kernel/tile/transfer/submission/cache/fallback/output identities, timings, and no paths/pixels/driver dumps.
- Add architecture checks proving all WGPU product execution routes through this service, every unsupported enabled node forces an explicit CPU decision, and all failures retain CPU access.
- Add source-accounting generation joining the full darktable CPU/OpenCL pixelpipe path, initial source-equivalent exposure binding, no-direct-analogue operations, UI/export callers, cache/job/profiling behavior, and excluded stages to their exact owners.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle CPU-only, feature/kernel unavailable, shader/pipeline/bind mismatch, resource/transfer/dispatch/submission/readback failure, OOM, device loss, timeout, cancellation, stale generation, output validation failure, and CPU fallback failure as typed outcomes.
- Any GPU failure discards/poisons partial output and releases/retires every resource before CPU publication.
- CPU fallback reuses the immutable source/snapshot but acquires fresh host output/scratch leases. It never consumes possibly corrupted GPU readback.
- If the request becomes stale before fallback starts/completes, no result publishes.
- A device generation change invalidates GPU prepared/cache/resource state but not CPU caches/results.
- The UI/catalog/source/edit is never mutated to “remember” a fallback backend.
- An unsupported or newly discovered darktable stage cannot be skipped to keep the GPU path active; it must have a qualified owner/binding or force CPU.

## Test matrix

- Real fixture/basic edit on CPU-only, qualified full GPU, constrained tiled GPU, and forced fallback.
- Every combination of enabled/disabled exposure/offset/gain, parameters, opacity, alpha, extended range, Preview/Full/Thumbnail/Export.
- CPU/full-GPU/tiled-GPU schema-3 parity across qualified backends, odd/tail dimensions, many tile grids, worker/submission order, and cache states.
- Feature unavailable, shader/resource/transfer/dispatch/submission/readback/OOM/device-loss/timeout/output-corruption failures and exact fallback/cleanup.
- Rapid #536 live edits/selection changes, cancellation/stale results, last-good-preview behavior, restart, and no adapter startup.
- Cache key/backend/device generation mutation and no invalid cross-device reuse.
- Exact direct exposure source/kernel/host-binding tests and explicit no-analogue assertions for transfer/offset/gain.
- Source-map mutation adding/changing CPU/OpenCL stage, operation, host binding, kernel, cache/job/profiling/publication branch without owner.
- Architecture mutation fixtures introducing direct product WGPU execution, GPU-only application startup, silent unsupported-node skip, or duplicate coordinator.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-app -p rusttable-pixelpipe -p rusttable-gpu basic_wgpu_pipeline` passes product integration, parity, fallback, cache, cancellation, source-accounting, and backend suites.
- `cargo xtask pixelpipe qualify-basic-wgpu --fixture corpus.raster.png.16-alpha --all-modes --full-and-all-tiles --faults all --verify-cpu-fallback` emits complete deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 301` proves the complete bounded darktable CPU/OpenCL product path, initial exposure kernel, callers, and explicit exclusions are owned.
- On a qualified device, the real selected-photo/basic-edit workflow uses WGPU and matches #539 under #516; on no/faulted device it produces the canonical CPU result.
- No masks, scopes, full ICC selection, SIMD, complex operation, multi-adapter, or profiling-gate dependency blocks this milestone.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #257, #265, #266, #270, #271, #272, #274, #290, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #495, #516, #535, #536, #539, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR composes the existing basic WGPU stack into the product pixelpipe, adds source-accounted backend selection/qualification/fallback, integrates the real UI/edit fixture, records metrics/receipts, and supplies parity/fault/backend tests. Do not implement new operations, masks/blending, scopes, external profile execution, multi-adapter policy, persistent quarantine, zero-copy viewport presentation, SIMD, or performance thresholds.
issues.title: Accelerate the basic pixelpipe on WGPU with CPU parity

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `rawprepare` image operation as a registered, versioned RustTable pixelpipe node. After this PR, supported Bayer and X-Trans `RawFrame` inputs can be cropped to the active sensor area, normalized from camera black/white levels into the pipeline’s canonical floating RAW representation, and passed to demosaic with the exact CFA phase, dimensions, metadata, cache identity, and detail-mask inputs required by downstream operations.

This issue implements one operation. It does not alter RAW container decoding, demosaic algorithms, white-balance policy, highlight reconstruction, or the Iced darkroom UI.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement the operation in `rusttable-processing::operations::rawprepare`; registration is through the descriptor/registry contracts from [0107] and [0109]. No operation-specific registry branch is allowed.
- Compatibility identity is exactly `rawprepare`. Rust type/module names may differ, but persisted and imported operation identity does not.
- The canonical output is a typed planar/interleaved `f32` RAW buffer defined by `rusttable-image`, carrying dimensions, row stride, CFA pattern and phase, channel count, normalized black/white evidence, and source orientation. Normalized valid sensor samples use `(sample - black) / (white - black)` without implicit display clipping.
- Per-channel/per-row/per-column black levels, black-level repeat dimensions, white levels, masked areas, active area, and default crop are resolved from `RawFrame` evidence. Parameter overrides are applied only where the imported darktable parameter version explicitly permits them.
- Crop coordinates are integer sensor coordinates. Cropping must update CFA phase mathematically; it must never copy the original phase unchanged after an odd offset.
- CPU scalar execution is the canonical reference. WGPU is implemented in the same PR only for layouts that can be represented without changing semantics; unsupported layouts execute on CPU and record the fallback reason.
- Tiling is allowed only when a tile can be normalized independently with identical results. Active-area/crop planning happens once before tile dispatch. Tiles do not reinterpret local coordinates as sensor coordinates.
- No inherited C/C++ source, OpenCL kernel, LibRaw/RawSpeed type, or opaque native pointer is used.

## Implementation

- Define the complete versioned parameter DTO set and migration functions for every `rawprepare` version listed by [0005]. Each migration must map field-by-field into the canonical parameter model and preserve an opaque source payload until differential parity is accepted.
- Add the operation descriptor: stable identity, schema version, display/localization keys, pipeline order constraints, accepted input/output formats, RAW-only capability, ROI rules, tiling capability, CPU/WGPU support, history behavior, detail-mask publication, and generated control hints.
- Add a checked `RawPreparePlan` builder that resolves active area, crop, black-level grids/deltas, white levels, channel mapping, scaling coefficients, output dimensions, CFA phase, required alignment, and memory estimates before allocating output.
- Reject zero/negative normalization denominators, out-of-bounds crops, inconsistent black-level grid dimensions, unsupported channel layouts, arithmetic overflow, and output sizes above configured limits before pipeline mutation or allocation.
- Implement the scalar CPU kernel with deterministic `f32` arithmetic, explicit handling for samples below black and above white, no accidental integer truncation, cancellation checks at bounded row intervals, and exact alpha/channel initialization where the canonical RAW buffer carries spare lanes.
- Implement ROI mapping from output coordinates back to source sensor coordinates. Full-frame and tiled runs must use the same plan and produce the same output dimensions and CFA phase.
- Publish the detail/luminance source required by downstream mask processing using the established raster/detail-mask contract; include its parameters and source geometry in cache identity.
- Add a WGSL shader and reflected binding entry for supported Bayer/X-Trans normalization layouts. Validate storage-buffer strides and dispatch dimensions through the shader registry; use checked upload/download sizes and device-memory budgets.
- Integrate operation parameters with edit serialization, history revisioning, cache keys, processing receipts, generated settings schema, default workflow selection, and imported darktable histories.
- Emit typed findings for unsupported source evidence or parameter versions. Preserve the operation and source bytes in the catalog even when execution is blocked.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Cancellation returns no partially published image, cache entry, mask, or history result. Temporary CPU/GPU buffers return to their pools.
- Out-of-range sensor samples are retained as finite values outside `0..=1` unless the source format or compatibility version explicitly requires clipping; the choice is recorded in the execution receipt.
- NaN or infinity in synthetic/decoded input is handled according to the shared non-finite policy and must not trigger undefined indexing or shader divergence.
- Missing optional metadata uses the exact documented `RawFrame` fallback. Missing required black/white/CFA evidence is a blocking capability error, not a guessed default.
- WGPU adapter/device loss, validation errors, unsupported storage limits, or allocation failure trigger the established CPU fallback once. Repeated fallback loops are prohibited.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for crop validation, black/white coefficient construction, CFA phase changes for every crop parity, black-level repeat grids, per-channel white levels, integer overflow, and parameter migrations.
- Property tests generating bounded dimensions/crops/CFA phases and proving ROI mapping stays in bounds and full-frame output equals arbitrary legal tile partitions.
- Differential tests against [0007] over the [0009] Bayer and X-Trans corpus, including masked pixels, black-level deltas, unusual active areas, 12/14/16-bit sources, below-black samples, clipped highlights, and legacy parameter versions.
- CPU scalar versus WGPU comparisons using the `Transfer` or stricter class from [0008], including odd widths, padded strides, tiny images, edge tiles, and constrained GPU memory.
- Cancellation tests before allocation, during CPU rows, after GPU submission, and before cache publication.
- Persistence tests proving canonical parameters round-trip, unknown versions remain opaque/blocking, and failed execution does not mutate the current edit revision.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing rawprepare`
- focused `rusttable-pixelpipe` integration tests for full/tiled/cancel/cache behavior;
- `xtask parity operation rawprepare --cpu --wgpu --tiled` receipt covering all registered fixtures and parameter versions;
- no changed pixel outside the tolerance selected by [0008], no tile seam, and exact agreement for output dimensions, CFA phase, cache identity inputs, and compatibility status;
- benchmark receipt records megapixels/second, peak host/GPU memory, transfer bytes, and fallback status without introducing a release threshold in this PR.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, and #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `rawprepare` only: descriptor, parameter versions/migrations, planning, CPU/WGPU execution, detail-mask publication, persistence/history integration, focused tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify RAW decoding, demosaic, temperature/white-balance algorithms, highlight recovery, unrelated operations, broad UI surfaces, or repository policy.
issues.title: Implement the rawprepare operation for sensor normalization

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `demosaic` operation as a versioned RustTable pixelpipe node for supported Bayer, X-Trans, and monochrome RAW inputs. The PR must convert the normalized mosaic from [0146] into the canonical scene-linear RGB buffer used by downstream color and tone operations, while preserving dimensions, crop/CFA geometry, image metadata, detail-mask output, deterministic preview/export behavior, and exact compatibility status for imported darktable histories.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::demosaic`; compatibility identity remains exactly `demosaic`.
- Implement every algorithm represented by the reviewed [0005] manifest for current darktable histories: passthrough/monochrome, PPG, VNG4, RCD, LMMSE, AMaZE, Markesteijn 1-pass and 3-pass, and FDC where the reference manifest registers it. Removed historical identifiers are decoded and migrated only when an exact mapping is proven; otherwise the operation remains opaque and execution-blocking.
- Scalar CPU implementations define canonical output. Algorithm implementations may share checked neighborhood, CFA, interpolation, and scratch-buffer primitives, but must not be collapsed into one approximate algorithm.
- Preview quality is explicit and persisted in the render request, not silently inferred from image size. Export and full-quality renders never substitute a preview approximation.
- Dual demosaic is implemented as the compatibility-defined combination of a selected base algorithm and its detail/high-frequency refinement, with the blend/mask calculation represented in cache identity and execution receipts.
- Output is scene-linear `f32` RGB with a defined alpha/spare-channel policy from `rusttable-image`. No display transfer, working-profile conversion, white-balance policy, sharpening, denoising, or highlight reconstruction is hidden in this operation.
- Algorithm-specific internal tile sizes, overlaps, borders, and full-frame requirements are fixed constants derived from parity tests. They are not user preferences and are validated before allocation.
- WGPU kernels are required for PPG, RCD, VNG4, Markesteijn, and the shared color-smoothing/detail steps when the adapter satisfies declared limits. AMaZE, LMMSE, or FDC may use CPU fallback only when the issue records a reproducible unsupported GPU constraint and the fallback receipt is tested.
- No C/C++, OpenCL, RawSpeed, LibRaw, or source-translated generated Rust is permitted.

## Implementation

- Add versioned parameter DTOs and field-by-field migrations for method, quality, green-equilibration, color-smoothing passes, median thresholds, dual-demosaic controls, edge thresholds, and all manifest-listed defaults/presets.
- Register the complete descriptor: RAW mosaic input, scene-linear RGB output, operation order, colorspace/format transition, ROI transforms, full-frame flags, per-algorithm overlap, tiling and WGPU capabilities, detail-mask publication, generated control schema, and hidden/deprecated values.
- Build a checked `DemosaicPlan` from image/CFA evidence and canonical parameters. The plan resolves algorithm, CFA layout/phase, channels, source/output extents, border radius, overlap, scratch buffers, internal tile dimensions, color-smoothing passes, memory estimate, preview policy, and CPU/GPU executor.
- Implement shared checked primitives for Bayer/X-Trans sample access, mirrored/clamped borders as specified by each algorithm, green matching, chroma interpolation, median/color smoothing, gradients, directional weights, and detail-mask extraction.
- Implement each CPU algorithm in a dedicated module below the repository file-size limit. Preserve operation order and floating-point expression order where required by the selected [0008] tolerance; use explicit fused/non-fused operations rather than relying on target-dependent reassociation.
- Implement ROI planning that expands requested output by the exact algorithm radius, aligns input to CFA phase, trims the result to requested output, and produces identical results under every legal tile grid. Algorithms that cannot be independently tiled must request full-frame execution through the pixelpipe contract rather than approximating edges.
- Add bounded scratch-buffer leases from the host pool and release them on completion, cancellation, or failure. Memory estimates must include per-thread/internal tiles and prevent oversubscription when Rayon executes multiple tiles.
- Add WGSL shaders, generated reflection manifests, validated binding layouts, per-algorithm pipeline keys, and bounded GPU scratch allocations. Dispatch dimensions and local workgroup sizes are selected by the registered adapter capability, not hard-coded to one vendor.
- Publish the canonical detail/luminance mask with exact geometry and source hash for downstream masks. Do not expose algorithm-private scratch buffers as raster masks.
- Integrate history serialization, legacy migration findings, presets/default workflow, cache keys, operation receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated parameter UI metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing/invalid CFA evidence, unsupported plane layouts, impossible crop/phase combinations, invalid method identifiers, arithmetic overflow, or memory requirements above configured limits fail before output allocation.
- Tiny images smaller than an algorithm’s neighborhood either use the exact documented compatibility fallback or return an explicit unsupported-size error; no out-of-bounds padding guess is allowed.
- Cancellation is checked between internal tiles/passes and prevents publication of image, mask, cache, or history results.
- Non-finite source values follow the shared non-finite policy and cannot participate in unchecked index/weight calculations.
- WGPU validation/device/allocation errors fall back once to the same planned CPU algorithm. A different algorithm is not an acceptable fallback.
- Unsupported imported methods remain preserved and visible in history with a blocking finding. They are not rewritten to the nearest available method.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every parameter migration, method ID, CFA phase, Bayer/X-Trans neighbor lookup, border rule, internal tile calculation, memory estimate, and preview/full-quality selection.
- Algorithm fixtures for flat fields, impulses, horizontal/vertical/diagonal edges, color gratings, Siemens stars, zipper/maze patterns, saturated highlights, clipped channels, high-frequency foliage, false-color targets, and monochrome sensors.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported method and parameter version across the [0009] camera corpus, including odd active-area offsets and non-standard strides.
- Property tests proving output bounds, ROI expansion, deterministic plan identity, full-frame versus randomized legal tile grids, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU comparisons for all advertised GPU methods under [0008], across multiple workgroup sizes and constrained-memory paths.
- Dual-demosaic, color-smoothing, green-equilibration, detail-mask, preview/full/export, cache hit/miss, and forced GPU-fallback integration tests.
- Benchmark receipts by method for megapixels/second, peak host/GPU memory, scratch reuse, transfers, dispatch time, and preview p95 latency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing demosaic` and focused pixelpipe/render integration suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation demosaic --all-versions --all-methods --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces no blocking mismatch for advertised methods.
- Every supported method meets its reviewed [0008] tolerance, has no tile seams/CFA-phase drift, and returns identical dimensions, profile/format metadata, and detail-mask geometry across CPU, WGPU, full, tiled, and cached execution.
- Export receipts prove no preview method was substituted.
- Unsupported historical identifiers round-trip byte-for-byte and remain explicitly blocking.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #302, and #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `demosaic` only: all declared algorithms, migrations, planning, CPU/WGPU paths, detail mask, presets, persistence/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and algorithm contract documentation. Do not change RAW decoding, raw preparation, white balance, highlight reconstruction, denoising, sharpening, Iced UI widgets, or unrelated operations.
issues.title: Implement the demosaic operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver `temperature` as the production white-balance operation for imported darktable histories and new RustTable edits. The operation must resolve camera/reference/as-shot/custom white balance into canonical channel multipliers, apply those multipliers in the correct pre- or post-demosaic representation, and persist enough evidence to reproduce the same result without re-querying mutable camera databases.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::temperature`; compatibility identity is exactly `temperature`.
- Canonical persisted parameters store normalized channel multipliers plus the selected source (`CameraReference`, `AsShot`, `DaylightReference`, `Preset`, `TemperatureTint`, `Spot`, or `Custom`). Temperature/tint values are UI/input representations; execution always consumes resolved multipliers.
- Camera preset lookup uses the immutable camera-data snapshot from [0076]. The resolved maker/model alias, preset identifier, tuning value, source-table revision, and multipliers are copied into the edit receipt so later database updates do not change existing edits.
- Bayer/X-Trans RAW processing occurs before demosaic and respects CFA plane mapping. Post-demosaic compatibility modes multiply scene-linear RGB channels only when the imported version requires that behavior.
- Green normalization follows the exact compatibility version. Channel order is derived from typed CFA/color metadata, never array position assumptions.
- Multiplier-to-temperature/tint and temperature/tint-to-multiplier conversion use one documented chromaticity/CAT implementation in `rusttable-color`; round-trip error limits are tested. Conversion failure does not replace valid stored multipliers.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for supported buffer layouts because this is a point operation; adapter failure falls back once to CPU.
- Spot-white-balance sampling is not performed inside the operation kernel. This issue defines and consumes a resolved sample result; interactive picker behavior belongs to the darkroom UI range.

## Implementation

- Add every manifest-listed parameter DTO/version and field-by-field migration, including legacy mode identifiers, coefficients, temperature/tint, preset references, and default workflow behavior.
- Register descriptor metadata: accepted RAW/RGB formats, operation ordering, color semantics, ROI identity, tile independence, masks/blend capability where valid, CPU/WGPU support, generated controls, and history behavior.
- Implement a checked `WhiteBalancePlan` resolving source evidence, channel mapping, multiplier normalization, preset provenance, execution stage, output metadata, and cache key fields.
- Validate positive finite multipliers, supported channel counts, CFA/color-plane mapping, preset identity/tuning bounds, and temperature/tint range before allocation or history mutation.
- Implement deterministic scalar and WGSL point kernels with explicit `f32` behavior, alpha/spare-channel preservation, cancellation checks, row strides, odd dimensions, and no implicit clipping of scene-linear highlights.
- Update output white-balance/color evidence so downstream input-profile/color-calibration operations can distinguish applied camera balance from source metadata.
- Integrate edit serialization, imported opaque payload retention, history revisions, defaults/presets, cache identity, execution receipts, generated parameter schema, and typed compatibility findings.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing required camera data uses the exact fallback encoded by the selected mode: `AsShot` may use embedded multipliers; `Custom` uses persisted values; unresolved named presets are blocking rather than silently changed to daylight.
- Invalid or ambiguous maker/model aliases produce a finding containing normalized camera identity but no private file path.
- Cancellation or GPU failure cannot publish partial pixels, cache entries, or revised history.
- Non-finite inputs follow the common policy; finite values above 1.0 remain unclipped.
- Unknown legacy modes/versions round-trip opaquely and block execution.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, channel/CFA mapping, multiplier normalization, source precedence, preset resolution, temperature/tint conversion, validation limits, and cache-key construction.
- Differential tests over the [0009] camera corpus for as-shot, camera reference, daylight, named presets/tuning, custom multipliers, and historical versions.
- Bayer/X-Trans phase and channel-order fixtures, neutral/color-chart targets, mixed illuminants, extreme but valid multipliers, clipped highlights, negative/HDR values, padded strides, and tiny images.
- CPU/WGPU, full/tiled, cached/uncached, preview/export, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].
- Persistence tests proving resolved preset evidence is stable after camera-data updates and unknown modes remain byte-preserved/blocking.
- Spot-result integration tests using deterministic sampled RGB values; no pointer/UI tests in this issue.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing temperature` and focused color/pixelpipe integration tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation temperature --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` covers every registered fixture/mode.
- Advertised modes meet the assigned [0008] tolerance; channel mapping, resolved multipliers, output metadata, and cache identity are exact across execution paths.
- A camera-data revision change does not alter an existing edit’s result.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `temperature` only: parameter versions/migrations, preset resolution, conversion math, CPU/WGPU application, metadata/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement picker interaction, demosaic, color calibration, input profiles, camera-data ingestion, or unrelated UI.
issues.title: Implement the temperature operation for white balance

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the `cacorrect` and `cacorrectrgb` compatibility operations as one production chromatic-aberration correction subsystem. The PR must correct lateral color displacement for normalized mosaics and scene-linear RGB images, preserve the selected reference channel and image geometry, and reproduce imported darktable histories without folding lens distortion, crop, demosaic, or color-profile behavior into the operation.

The two compatibility identities remain distinct because they execute at different pipeline stages and persist different parameter versions. They share checked interpolation, radial sampling, ROI, tiling, and test infrastructure in this PR.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement `cacorrect` and `cacorrectrgb` under `rusttable-processing::operations::chromatic_aberration`; register two descriptors with exact compatibility identities and independent schema versions.
- `cacorrect` consumes normalized Bayer/X-Trans mosaic data before demosaic. `cacorrectrgb` consumes scene-linear RGB after demosaic. The registry enforces those formats and order constraints.
- Implement every mode and parameter version present in the approved [0005] operation manifest. Legacy versions are migrated field-by-field; an unknown method or layout is preserved opaquely and blocks execution rather than being mapped to the nearest mode.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU execution is required for both descriptors and must use the same radial model, neighborhood radius, interpolation rule, border rule, and reference-channel choice as CPU.
- Use inverse sampling from each output pixel into the source. Interpolation is the shared bicubic/linear primitive specified by the imported method version; forward splatting is prohibited.
- The output dimensions are unchanged. ROI planning expands input by the exact maximum channel displacement plus interpolation radius. Tile-local coordinates are converted to full-image coordinates before evaluating radial displacement.
- Scene-linear values are not clipped. Alpha/spare channels are copied unchanged. Mosaic correction never changes CFA phase or invents missing color samples.
- Lensfun and the `lens` operation are not consulted. This operation corrects only its persisted chromatic-aberration model.

## Implementation

- Add canonical parameter models for channel scales/offsets, center, reference channel, automatic/manual mode, interpolation mode, strength, and every manifest-listed legacy field. Generate exact migrations and compatibility findings.
- Add descriptors declaring accepted formats, pipeline stages, ROI behavior, overlap, tile independence, CPU/WGPU support, history visibility, blend/mask capability, generated controls, and cache-key fields.
- Build a checked `ChromaticAberrationPlan` that resolves image center, normalized radial coordinates, per-channel transforms, maximum source displacement, interpolation radius, source/output strides, memory estimate, and selected executor before allocation.
- Implement the mosaic path with CFA-aware same-color sampling. Any compatibility mode that estimates displacement from the image must run its deterministic analysis pass over the required full-resolution evidence and store the resolved coefficients in the execution receipt.
- Implement the RGB path with reference-channel-preserving inverse resampling. Channel transforms use full-image coordinates so full-frame, tiled, and cached runs are identical.
- Add CPU kernels with bounded cancellation checks and pooled scratch storage. Add WGSL kernels and generated reflection/binding metadata with validated dimensions, strides, and adapter limits.
- Integrate parameters, resolved automatic coefficients, operation order, cache identity, edit history, presets/defaults, execution receipts, GPU fallback, and opaque preservation into catalog and pixelpipe services.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite coefficients, invalid centers, displacements that overflow source-coordinate arithmetic, unsupported channel/CFA layouts, impossible interpolation neighborhoods, and memory estimates above configured limits before output publication.
- Automatic estimation that lacks sufficient image evidence returns a typed blocking finding. It does not silently switch to zero correction or manual defaults.
- Border behavior is deterministic and versioned. No uninitialized pixels, tile seams, or channel-dependent dimension changes are permitted.
- Cancellation or GPU/device failure releases all temporary resources and publishes no image, cache entry, mask, or history result. GPU failure falls back once to the equivalent CPU plan.
- Unknown imported versions and fields remain byte-preserved and visible in compatibility reports.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every parameter migration, radial transform, maximum-displacement calculation, CFA same-color lookup, interpolation/border rule, ROI expansion, cache key, and validation failure.
- Synthetic fixtures containing colored radial spokes, high-contrast corners, impulse grids, checkerboards, off-center optical axes, subpixel displacements, negative/HDR values, odd dimensions, padded strides, and tiny images.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported legacy/RGB version and automatic/manual mode represented by [0009].
- Property tests proving source samples remain in planned ROI, full-frame output equals randomized legal tile grids, unchanged channels are bit-identical where required, and cancellation does not publish partial state.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, and forced-fallback comparisons under the assigned [0008] tolerance.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing chromatic_aberration` and focused pixelpipe integration tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation cacorrect --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` and the equivalent `cacorrectrgb` command produce registered receipts with no unexplained mismatch.
- Output dimensions, CFA phase, reference channel, resolved coefficients, cache identity, and compatibility status are exact across execution paths; image deltas remain within [0008].
- No fixture exhibits a tile seam, out-of-bounds sample, unintended clipping, or implicit lens correction.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the two compatibility descriptors and their shared chromatic-aberration engine, migrations, CPU/WGPU paths, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement lens distortion, crop/perspective correction, demosaic, color profiles, or interactive UI overlays.
issues.title: Implement legacy and RGB chromatic-aberration correction

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `hotpixels` operation for Bayer and X-Trans mosaics. The operation must detect isolated hot or dead photosites using same-color neighborhood evidence, replace only qualifying samples, preserve CFA geometry and active-area dimensions, expose an optional corrected-pixel visualization mask, and reproduce all supported imported darktable parameter versions.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::hotpixels`; compatibility identity remains exactly `hotpixels`.
- The operation executes on normalized mosaic data after `rawprepare` and before demosaic. RGB images are rejected by the descriptor.
- Detection uses the exact threshold/strength/marking semantics for each manifest-listed version. Same-color neighbors are selected from the typed CFA pattern; adjacent different-color sites are never used as substitutes.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU execution is required for Bayer and X-Trans layouts supported by the RAW contract.
- Replacement is computed from immutable source samples. A corrected pixel cannot influence detection or replacement of another pixel in the same pass.
- Detection and replacement are one logical operation with a deterministic two-stage implementation: classify against the source, then write output. The corrected-pixel mask is generated from the same classification result.
- Output dimensions, CFA pattern, CFA phase, metadata, and finite values outside `0..=1` are preserved. Only detected photosites change.
- ROI overlap is the maximum same-color neighborhood radius for the selected CFA/method. Full-image coordinates determine CFA color and border behavior.

## Implementation

- Add canonical versioned parameter DTOs for threshold, strength, permissiveness, mark-fixed-pixels behavior, and all manifest-listed legacy fields; implement exact field migrations and opaque preservation.
- Register RAW input/output format, operation order, ROI overlap, tile independence, CPU/WGPU support, corrected-pixel raster-mask capability, generated controls, history behavior, and cache-key inputs.
- Build a checked `HotPixelPlan` resolving CFA layout/phase, normalized threshold coefficients, neighborhood offsets, border rule, mask request, dimensions/strides, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement source-only classification for high and low outliers using the compatibility-defined robust same-color statistic and threshold. Keep a compact classification buffer or bitset from the bounded pool; do not mutate input in place.
- Implement replacement using the compatibility-defined same-color estimate and strength interpolation. Preserve samples not classified by copying them exactly.
- Add deterministic handling for borders, masked sensor regions, repeated hot-pixel clusters, tiny images, padded strides, and row/tile boundaries.
- Add CPU kernels with cancellation checks between bounded row blocks. Add WGSL classification/replacement passes, reflected bindings, checked dispatch sizes, and pooled GPU buffers.
- Publish the optional corrected-pixel mask through the raster-mask contract, with full geometry and source/parameter hashes included in cache identity.
- Integrate parameters, presets/defaults, edit history, imported compatibility data, cache keys, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated parameter metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported CFA layouts, invalid/non-finite thresholds or strengths, dimensions too small for the selected neighborhood, arithmetic overflow, and memory requirements above configured limits before publication.
- A tiny image with no valid same-color neighborhood returns an explicit unsupported-size result; it is not silently passed through as successful correction.
- Non-finite source values follow the shared non-finite policy and cannot poison neighborhood statistics.
- Cancellation and GPU/device failure release classification/output/mask buffers and publish no partial result. GPU fallback runs the identical CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions or method flags remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for all migrations, CFA neighbor tables/phases, threshold normalization, classifier/replacement math, border behavior, ROI overlap, mask encoding, and cache identity.
- Synthetic Bayer/X-Trans fixtures with single hot/dead sites, two-site clusters, dense defects, defects at every border/corner, legitimate high-frequency detail, saturated highlights, dark noise, odd dimensions, padded rows, negative/HDR values, and tiny images.
- Property tests proving unchanged samples are bit-identical, every changed sample was classified from immutable source evidence, full-frame equals randomized legal tiling, and output/mask dimensions match input.
- Differential tests against [0007] for all supported versions and representative camera fixtures from [0009].
- CPU/WGPU, mask-on/off, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, constrained-memory, cancellation, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing hotpixels` and focused pixelpipe/mask tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation hotpixels --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` records no unexplained mismatch.
- Corrected sample positions, replacement values, visualization mask, dimensions, CFA metadata, and cache identity agree across execution paths within the assigned tolerance.
- Legitimate edge/detail fixtures show no false correction beyond the reference behavior, and no tile seam or order-dependent cascade is present.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #302, #303, #304, #305.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `hotpixels` only: migrations, plan, CPU/WGPU classification and replacement, optional mask, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement general denoising, defect-map import, demosaic, camera calibration, or UI overlay interaction.
