issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `denoiseprofile` operation, including every camera-profiled denoise mode and parameter version registered by [0005]. The operation must resolve immutable camera noise evidence, execute the selected raw/RGB wavelet or non-local-means path at the correct pipeline stage, preserve chroma/luma and detail behavior, and reproduce imported darktable histories across CPU, WGPU, tiled, cached, preview, and export execution.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::denoiseprofile`; compatibility identity remains exactly `denoiseprofile`.
- Implement all manifest-listed modes rather than approximating them through one generic denoiser: profiled non-local means, profiled wavelets, variance-stabilizing forms, auto/profile modes, RGB/Y0U0V0 processing variants, and any current raw-domain variant identified by the manifest.
- Camera noise profiles are read from the immutable camera-data snapshot established by [0076]. The resolved profile identity, interpolation inputs, ISO/exposure evidence, coefficients, and source revision are copied into the operation receipt and cache identity.
- Scalar CPU execution is canonical. WGPU is required for the registered non-local-means, wavelet, variance, and color-transform passes. A GPU adapter may fall back only to the same selected mode and resolved profile on CPU.
- The operation never estimates a replacement camera profile from pixels when a required profile is unavailable. Auto mode may interpolate only between explicitly registered profile samples according to the compatibility algorithm.
- Full-frame normalization/statistics are computed once when the selected mode requires them, then frozen in the plan. Tile-local statistics are prohibited.
- ROI overlap, multiscale support radius, internal tile dimensions, decomposition levels, patch/search radius, and memory factors are deterministic functions of the selected mode and parameters.
- Scene-linear range is retained; clipping occurs only where the exact compatibility formula requires domain limiting. Alpha/spare channels are preserved.

## Implementation

- Add complete versioned parameter DTOs and migrations for mode, strength, shadows/bias controls, patch/search radius, scattering, central-pixel weight, wavelet curves/bands, color mode, overshooting, profile selection, and all manifest-listed legacy fields.
- Register descriptor metadata: accepted raw/RGB formats and stage, colorspace requirements, ROI/overlap/full-frame rules, tiling, memory factors, CPU/WGPU capabilities, blend/mask support, generated controls, presets/defaults, and history/cache behavior.
- Build a checked `DenoiseProfilePlan` resolving camera profile, ISO interpolation, variance model, selected algorithm, color transform, scale/band coefficients, support radius, internal tiles, scratch buffers, memory estimate, quality mode, and executor.
- Implement exact profile interpolation and variance-stabilizing forward/inverse transforms with explicit finite/domain checks and tested numerical ordering.
- Implement scalar reference paths for non-local means and wavelet decomposition/reconstruction, including chroma/luma coupling, threshold curves, detail retention, edge/border rules, and compatibility-defined optimizations that affect output.
- Implement bounded internal tiling with immutable halo reads and owned output interiors. Thread count and simultaneous tile memory are derived from the shared host budget; Rayon work cannot oversubscribe the planned scratch pool.
- Add WGSL pipelines for transforms, NLM distance/weight accumulation, wavelet decomposition/threshold/reconstruction, and color conversion. Use generated shader reflection, checked dispatch arithmetic, per-device workgroup selection, pooled resources, and explicit synchronization only at operation boundaries.
- Integrate profile provenance, parameters, imported payloads, edit history, presets, operation receipts, cache keys, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated parameter metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing or ambiguous required noise profiles, unsupported mode/color/layout combinations, invalid radii/curves, non-finite coefficients, excessive decomposition levels, arithmetic overflow, and memory estimates beyond configured limits fail before publication.
- An unresolved camera profile remains a typed compatibility finding and preserves all source data; it is not replaced with a generic profile.
- Tiny images reduce decomposition levels only when the exact compatibility version defines that reduction. Otherwise they return an explicit unsupported-size result.
- Cancellation is checked between passes, bands, and bounded internal tiles; no partial image, cache, history, or pooled scratch state is published.
- GPU validation/device/allocation errors trigger one CPU retry with the same plan. Replanning into a cheaper or different denoise mode is prohibited.
- Non-finite source values follow the shared policy and cannot create non-finite weights or unbounded search behavior.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, camera-profile lookup/interpolation, variance transforms, band/curve interpolation, color transforms, support/overlap calculation, memory budgeting, cache identity, and validation path.
- Synthetic fixtures: flat fields at multiple noise levels, Gaussian/Poisson mixtures, color noise, impulse noise, gradients, edges, fine texture, low-light shadows, clipped highlights, HDR/negative values, tiny/odd/padded images, and seam-sensitive tile patterns.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every mode/version/profile combination represented by [0009], including ISO values exactly on and between profile samples.
- Property tests proving deterministic planning, full-frame versus randomized legal internal/external tiles, finite bounded weights, cancellation cleanup, and immutable profile provenance.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, mask/blend, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under the mode-specific [0008] tolerance.
- Benchmarks record megapixels/second, scratch peak, transfer bytes, GPU occupancy/dispatch time, cache reuse, and preview p95 latency by mode without changing acceptance thresholds in this PR.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing denoiseprofile` and focused color/pixelpipe/GPU integration suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation denoiseprofile --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits complete receipts for all registered fixtures and profiles.
- Every advertised mode remains within its [0008] tolerance with no tile seam, profile drift, preview/export substitution, unbounded allocation, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.
- Changing the installed camera-data revision does not alter an existing edit whose resolved profile evidence was persisted.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `denoiseprofile` only: parameter versions/migrations, camera-profile resolution, all registered algorithms, CPU/WGPU execution, tiling/memory behavior, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and algorithm contract documentation. Do not implement profile-authoring tools, generic `nlmeans`, `rawdenoise`, demosaic, sharpening, or UI interaction.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `rawdenoise` operation for normalized Bayer and X-Trans mosaics. It must remove chroma and luminance noise in the mosaic domain using the compatibility-defined multiscale decomposition, preserve CFA geometry and scene-linear range, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU results for every supported imported parameter version.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::rawdenoise`; compatibility identity remains exactly `rawdenoise`.
- Execute after `rawprepare` and before demosaic. RGB inputs are rejected.
- Implement the exact wavelet decomposition, per-scale thresholds/curves, channel/CFA handling, and reconstruction represented by [0005]; do not route through `denoiseprofile` or `nlmeans`.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for decomposition, thresholding, and reconstruction on all supported CFA layouts.
- Decompose same-color CFA planes without changing CFA phase. Full-image coordinates define color-plane membership inside tiles.
- The number of scales is limited by image dimensions and the compatibility version. Any scale reduction is explicit in the plan and receipt.
- Output dimensions and metadata are unchanged. Finite values outside `0..=1` and spare channels are preserved unless the exact formula modifies the active sample.

## Implementation

- Add complete parameter DTOs/migrations for threshold, per-band curves, strength, color controls, method/version flags, and defaults/presets.
- Register RAW format/order, ROI overlap, scale-dependent memory factors, tiling, CPU/WGPU support, generated controls, blend/history behavior, and cache inputs.
- Build a checked `RawDenoisePlan` resolving CFA layout/phase, scale count, band coefficients, support radius, internal tile dimensions, scratch buffers, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic à-trous/wavelet decomposition and reconstruction primitives for mosaic planes, compatibility-defined threshold interpolation, borders, and scale ordering.
- Use pooled, bounded scratch buffers; schedule simultaneous tiles from the shared memory budget. Tile interiors must be written once from immutable halo data.
- Add WGSL passes and generated binding metadata. Validate strides, scale dispatches, scratch sizes, workgroups, and adapter storage limits.
- Integrate operation history, imported opaque payloads, presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated parameter metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported CFA/channel layouts, invalid curves or thresholds, impossible scale counts, overflow, and memory estimates above configured limits before publication.
- Tiny images use only the exact scale-reduction rule encoded by the selected version; otherwise return a typed unsupported-size result.
- Cancellation between scales/tiles releases all scratch state and publishes no partial output or cache entry.
- GPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once; substituting another denoise operation is prohibited.
- Unknown versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, CFA plane mapping, scale selection, curve interpolation, borders, overlap, memory budgeting, and cache identity.
- Synthetic flat fields, Gaussian/Poisson/chroma noise, impulses, gradients, sharp edges, fine texture, clipped highlights, negative/HDR values, odd/padded images, and seam patterns for Bayer and X-Trans.
- Differential tests against [0007] for all supported versions and [0009] fixtures.
- Property tests for full-frame versus randomized legal tiling, deterministic reconstruction, unchanged dimensions/CFA phase, finite output, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing rawdenoise` and focused pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation rawdenoise --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` records complete fixture coverage.
- Output, CFA metadata, planned scales, and cache identity meet [0008] with no seams, hidden clipping, or preview/export substitution.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `rawdenoise` only: migrations, multiscale CPU/WGPU engine, tiling/memory behavior, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and algorithm contract documentation. Do not modify demosaic, camera noise profiles, `denoiseprofile`, `nlmeans`, sharpening, or UI.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `nlmeans` operation for scene-linear RGB/Lab-compatible pipeline data. The operation must implement darktable-compatible non-local-means patch search, channel coupling, strength and detail behavior, execute deterministically under bounded memory, and preserve every supported imported parameter version.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::nlmeans`; compatibility identity remains exactly `nlmeans`.
- This is the generic unprofiled operation. It does not read camera noise profiles and does not share persisted semantics with `denoiseprofile`.
- Implement every method/color-space/parameter variant registered by [0005], including compatibility-specific patch radius, search radius, strength, luma/chroma weighting, central-pixel weight, and scattering behavior.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU execution is required for distance accumulation, weight normalization, and output reconstruction.
- Patch distances are calculated from immutable input. Accumulation order is explicitly defined for CPU and shader paths; approximations that change search samples or truncate neighborhoods are not allowed in full/export mode.
- Preview may use only the exact quality reduction represented by the render request and recorded in cache identity. Export never substitutes reduced search/patch radii.
- ROI overlap is `search radius + patch radius` in output pixels after scale conversion. Full-image coordinates and the versioned border rule apply inside every tile.
- Output dimensions and scene-linear range are unchanged; alpha/spare channels are copied exactly.

## Implementation

- Add all parameter DTOs/migrations and exact validation for radii, strength, decay, scattering, channel mode, central weight, and legacy quality flags.
- Register format/colorspace requirements, pipeline order, overlap, tile/memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache inputs.
- Build `NlMeansPlan` with resolved radii, offsets, channel transform, weight coefficients, preview policy, internal tile grid, scratch layout, host/GPU memory estimates, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar patch-distance and weighted-accumulation kernels with bounded cancellation checks, finite weight handling, zero-weight fallback, and reusable scratch buffers.
- Schedule internal tiles from the host budget; do not allocate one full neighbor accumulator per Rayon worker without accounting for it.
- Add WGSL passes with reflected bindings, checked offset tables, adapter-specific workgroup selection, bounded accumulators, and exact output normalization.
- Integrate parameters, defaults/presets, imported payloads, edit history, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid/non-finite coefficients, radii outside registered limits, unsupported color layouts, arithmetic overflow, and memory estimates beyond configured limits before execution.
- Tiny images reduce search coverage only according to the exact border/version rule; no wraparound or unchecked indexing is permitted.
- A zero or non-finite accumulated weight returns the compatibility-defined source/central value and emits a bounded diagnostic counter, not NaN.
- Cancellation or GPU failure publishes no partial image/cache/history state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once.
- Unknown variants remain opaque and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, offset ordering, patch distance, channel transforms, weight calculation/normalization, borders, overlap, memory budgeting, and cache identity.
- Synthetic flat/noisy fields, impulses, gradients, sharp edges, periodic texture, color noise, repeated patterns, saturated/HDR/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and seam-sensitive fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/mode from [0009].
- Property tests for finite output, deterministic accumulation, full-frame versus randomized legal tile grids, bounded memory, cancellation cleanup, and exact alpha preservation.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing nlmeans` and focused pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation nlmeans --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits complete receipts.
- Output meets [0008] without tile seams, invalid weights, hidden quality reduction, unbounded per-thread memory, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308.

## One-PR boundary

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `hazeremoval` operation for scene-linear RGB images. The operation must estimate atmospheric light and transmission using the darktable-compatible model, refine the transmission map, recover scene radiance with deterministic strength/distance behavior, and reproduce supported imported histories across CPU, WGPU, tiling, caching, preview, and export.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::hazeremoval`; compatibility identity remains exactly `hazeremoval`.
- Implement the exact atmospheric-light selection, dark-channel/transmission estimate, guided-filter refinement, strength, and distance semantics represented by [0005]. No alternative dehazing model or ML inference is introduced.
- The global atmospheric estimate is computed once from the complete required analysis image at the render-request scale and frozen in `HazeRemovalPlan`. Tiles never estimate their own atmosphere.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for local minimum/dark-channel construction, guided-filter passes, transmission refinement, and radiance recovery.
- Analysis downscaling, sample selection, percentile/tie ordering, guided-filter radius/epsilon, transmission floor, and output-domain handling are explicit compatibility-version behavior and part of cache identity.
- Output dimensions, alpha, and working color metadata are unchanged. Scene-linear highlights are not clipped except where the compatibility recovery equation explicitly limits the transmission denominator.
- ROI overlap equals the complete local-minimum plus guided-filter support radius at the execution scale. Global analysis evidence is an immutable auxiliary input to every tile.

## Implementation

- Add all parameter DTOs/migrations for strength, distance, mode, analysis/refinement settings, and legacy fields; preserve unknown data opaquely.
- Register scene-linear RGB format/colorspace, pipeline order, full-analysis requirement, ROI/overlap, memory factors, tiling, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache inputs.
- Build a checked `HazeRemovalPlan` resolving analysis scale, deterministic sample set, atmospheric RGB, local/refinement radii, epsilon, transmission limits, output coefficients, tile geometry, scratch layout, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic atmospheric-light analysis with stable ordering for equal candidates and a bounded histogram/selection algorithm; record selected values and analysis hash in the receipt.
- Implement scalar local-minimum, transmission, guided-filter box statistics, coefficient interpolation, and radiance recovery using pooled buffers and bounded cancellation points.
- Add WGSL passes with reflected bindings, checked dispatch/stride arithmetic, pooled textures/buffers, and adapter-specific workgroups. Global analysis values are uploaded once per plan.
- Ensure full-frame and tiled paths trim halos identically and never show transmission seams.
- Integrate parameters, presets/defaults, imported payloads, history, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported layouts, invalid/non-finite parameters, impossible radii, empty analysis samples, degenerate atmospheric estimates, arithmetic overflow, and memory estimates beyond limits before publication.
- Flat black/white, transparent, tiny, and all-non-finite synthetic images use exact versioned fallback behavior or return a typed unsupported-input finding; no division by zero or arbitrary atmosphere is allowed.
- Cancellation during analysis or any refinement pass releases scratch state and publishes no partial output/cache/history.
- GPU failure retries the exact CPU plan once. It cannot change analysis scale, radius, or model.
- Unknown versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, deterministic atmospheric selection, dark-channel windows, box/guided-filter statistics, transmission/recovery equations, overlap, memory planning, and cache keys.
- Synthetic haze gradients, known atmosphere/transmission composites, flat fields, silhouettes, bright sky, dark foreground, saturated colors, negative/HDR values, odd/padded/tiny images, and seam-sensitive patterns.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/parameter set and representative [0009] photographs.
- Property tests proving stable analysis independent of thread/tile order, full-frame versus randomized legal tile grids, finite bounded denominators, cancellation cleanup, and exact alpha preservation.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, mask/blend, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing hazeremoval` and focused guided-filter/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation hazeremoval --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits complete receipts including atmospheric and transmission evidence.
- Output meets [0008] with identical global analysis values, no tile seams, no hidden preview substitution in export, and no non-finite output introduced from finite input.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `hazeremoval` only: migrations, deterministic analysis, CPU/WGPU local/refinement/recovery paths, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and algorithm contract documentation. Do not add generic guided-filter UI, sky segmentation, local contrast, tone mapping, or ML dehazing.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `atrous` operation for darktable-compatible multiscale contrast, sharpening, denoising, and color adjustment. The PR must implement the complete wavelet decomposition/reconstruction and every supported parameter version, preserve scale-dependent behavior across image resolutions, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU, full/tiled, preview/export results.

- Scale radii are derived from the render scale and compatibility version. A preview request may reduce unavailable high-frequency scales only through the versioned rule recorded in the plan; export uses the full legal scale set.

- Scene-linear working data is retained. Alpha/spare channels are copied exactly; no output clipping is introduced beyond the compatibility formula.

- Schedule scale/tile work from the shared memory budget. No thread may allocate an unplanned full-frame band.
- Add WGSL passes, reflected bindings, checked per-scale dispatch dimensions, pooled textures/buffers, and explicit pass ordering.

- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

- `xtask parity operation atrous --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits complete receipts.

## Dependencies

## One-PR boundary

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `equalizer` compatibility operation. It must apply darktable’s legacy multiscale equalization curves to the required luminance/chroma representation, preserve every supported historical parameter version, and provide deterministic CPU/WGPU output without substituting the newer contrast-equalizer or diffuse operations.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::equalizer`; compatibility identity remains exactly `equalizer`.
- Implement the exact legacy decomposition kernel, number and spacing of bands, curve interpolation, luminance/chroma treatment, normalization, and reconstruction defined by [0005].
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for decomposition, curve application, and reconstruction.
- Scale selection is derived from render scale and versioned parameters. Export always evaluates the complete legal scale set.
- ROI overlap equals the largest active band support. Tiled execution uses full-image scale coordinates and the exact compatibility border rule.
- Output geometry, alpha, working profile, and finite scene-linear range are preserved.
- Neutral curves must reconstruct the input within the strict reconstruction tolerance assigned by [0008].

## Implementation

- Add all versioned DTOs/migrations for band curves, interpolation nodes, strengths, channel modes, scale controls, and legacy fields/defaults.
- Register format/colorspace, operation order, overlap, tile/memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `EqualizerPlan` resolving bands/radii, curve LUTs, colorspace transforms, border policy, scratch layout, internal tiles, memory estimate, quality mode, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar decomposition/reconstruction and curve evaluation with explicit floating-point ordering, pooled buffers, and bounded cancellation checks.
- Add WGSL passes with reflected bindings, checked per-band dispatches, pooled resources, and exact pass ordering.
- Integrate imported payloads, edit history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated parameter metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/non-finite curves, unsupported layouts, impossible band/radius combinations, overflow, and memory estimates above limits before publication.
- Tiny images use only the exact versioned band reduction or return an unsupported-size finding.
- Cancellation releases all bands and publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once without reducing quality.
- Unknown versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `sharpen` operation using darktable-compatible unsharp masking. The operation must blur the selected luminance representation, derive and threshold the high-frequency residual, apply the configured amount without changing image geometry or color metadata, and reproduce every supported imported parameter version on CPU and WGPU.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::sharpen`; compatibility identity remains exactly `sharpen`.
- Implement the exact Gaussian/kernel construction, radius scaling, amount, threshold, luminance/color handling, edge/border rule, and legacy semantics registered by [0005].
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for separable blur and residual application.
- Radius is expressed in image-space pixels and transformed once for the render scale. Export uses the requested full-resolution radius; preview uses the mathematically scaled radius, not an unrelated approximation.
- ROI overlap is the complete blur support radius. Full-image coordinates and versioned borders are used in every tile.
- Output dimensions, alpha/spare channels, working profile, and finite scene-linear range are preserved. No automatic clipping or halo suppression is added beyond the compatibility algorithm.
- The operation remains distinct from `highpass`, `diffuse`, `atrous`, and demosaic sharpening.

## Implementation

- Add complete versioned DTOs/migrations for radius, amount, threshold, method/channel flags, and all legacy defaults/presets.
- Register accepted format/colorspace, order, overlap, tiling/memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `SharpenPlan` resolving scaled radius, discrete kernel coefficients/support, threshold/amount, luminance transform, border policy, scratch layout, tile grid, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar horizontal/vertical convolution, residual calculation, threshold transition, and recombination with pooled buffers and bounded cancellation checks.
- Add WGSL blur/residual passes with generated reflection, checked strides/dispatch dimensions, pooled resources, and the same kernel coefficients uploaded from the plan.
- Integrate imported payloads, edit history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated parameter metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid/non-finite parameters, radius/kernel sizes above registered limits, unsupported color layouts, overflow, and memory estimates beyond limits before publication.
- Radius zero follows exact no-op semantics and still produces the correct operation/history/cache receipt without allocating blur buffers.
- Tiny images use the versioned border rule; no unchecked convolution or arbitrary radius reduction is allowed.
- Cancellation releases scratch/output and publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, radius scaling, kernel generation/normalization, threshold behavior, luminance transforms, borders, overlap, memory planning, no-op/cache identity, and validation.
- Impulse, step edge, diagonal edge, gradient, flat field, fine texture, noise, saturated/HDR/negative, odd/padded/tiny, and seam fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/preset and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests for constant-image invariance, full-frame versus randomized legal tiles, deterministic kernels, alpha preservation, cancellation cleanup, and no-op identity.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing sharpen` and focused convolution/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation sharpen --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete receipts.
- Output meets [0008] with identical planned kernels and no tile seams, hidden clipping, radius drift, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `sharpen` only: migrations, deterministic CPU/WGPU unsharp mask, ROI/tiling/memory, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and algorithm contract documentation. Do not modify high-pass, multiscale, diffuse, demosaic, export resizing, or UI.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `bilat` operation as the darktable-compatible bilateral-grid local-contrast filter. The operation must construct, blur, and slice the bilateral grid using the persisted spatial/range settings, apply contrast/detail reconstruction at the correct pipeline stage, and remain deterministic under bounded CPU/WGPU execution.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::bilat`; compatibility identity remains exactly `bilat`.
- Implement the exact grid dimensions, splat weights, range coordinate, blur kernel/pass ordering, slice interpolation, detail/base recombination, contrast and saturation behavior represented by [0005]. It is not replaced by the direct bilateral filter in [0161].
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for grid clear, splat, blur, slice, and recombination.
- Grid sizing is computed once from full output dimensions, render scale, spatial sigma, range sigma, and registered limits. Every tile references the same global grid coordinate system.
- When the compatibility algorithm requires a full-image grid, the operation is marked full-analysis/full-frame rather than constructing independent tile grids. Pixelpipe tiling may be used only for the final slice/recombination with the frozen global grid.
- Deterministic CPU splatting uses a fixed reduction order. GPU splatting uses the registered atomic/reduction strategy and must meet the assigned tolerance; unsupported required atomics trigger CPU execution.
- Output geometry, alpha/spare channels, profile metadata, and finite scene-linear range are preserved.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for spatial/range sigma, detail/contrast, saturation, mode, grid controls, and all legacy fields/defaults/presets.
- Register format/colorspace, order, full-grid analysis, slice ROI, memory factors, CPU/WGPU requirements, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `BilateralGridPlan` resolving normalized range domain, grid origin/dimensions/strides, splat and slice coefficients, blur passes, reconstruction coefficients, memory estimate, host/GPU strategy, and executor.
- Implement checked scalar grid allocation, deterministic splat/reduction, separable grid blur, trilinear slice, and base/detail recombination using bounded pooled storage and cancellation checkpoints.
- Add WGSL grid passes with reflected bindings, checked grid arithmetic, adapter atomic capability checks, bounded resources, and deterministic clear/pass order.
- Include grid geometry, global analysis hash, algorithm path, and fallback reason in cache identity and receipts.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid/non-finite sigmas, degenerate range domains, grid dimensions/byte counts above limits, unsupported atomics/layouts, overflow, and memory estimates above configured budgets before allocation.
- Constant/zero-range images use the exact compatibility fallback and cannot divide by zero.
- Cancellation releases the full grid and publishes no partial image/cache/history.
- GPU failure or missing atomics retries the identical CPU plan once; it does not switch to direct bilateral or reduce grid resolution.
- Unknown versions remain opaque and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, grid sizing/indexing, splat/slice weights, range normalization, blur kernels, reconstruction, memory limits, cache identity, and fallback selection.
- Constant, impulse, edge, gradient, staircase, texture, color ramp, noise, HDR/negative, odd/padded/tiny, and grid-boundary fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/preset and [0009] image.
- Property tests for deterministic CPU reduction, global-grid consistency across tiled slicing, bounded indexing/memory, cancellation cleanup, and alpha preservation.
- CPU/WGPU with supported/unsupported atomics, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing bilat` and focused grid/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation bilat --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete receipts including grid dimensions and execution path.
- Output meets [0008] with no independent-tile grid seams, grid-size drift, silent resolution reduction, or unbounded allocation.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `bilat` only: migrations, bilateral-grid CPU/WGPU engine, global-grid planning, bounded memory, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement the direct `bilateral` operation, low-pass, tone equalizer, or UI.

- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for every method advertised as supported by the descriptor.
- Kernel geometry, radius, orientation, curvature/eccentricity, iterations, color mode, and blending of multiple components are transformed once into execution-space coefficients and included in cache identity.
- Method-specific ROI support is computed analytically before allocation. Tiles use full-image coordinates for oriented/anisotropic kernels and exact versioned borders.
- Export evaluates full requested support/iterations. Preview may reduce work only through an explicit render-quality rule defined by the compatibility version and recorded in the receipt.
- Output geometry, alpha/spare channels, profile metadata, and finite working range are preserved unless a listed blur mode explicitly changes channels.

- Add complete versioned DTOs/migrations for blur type, radius/size, orientation, shape/eccentricity/curvature, iterations, color/channel controls, and all legacy defaults/presets.

- Implement shared checked convolution/resampling primitives and separate scalar method modules so method semantics remain reviewable below file-size limits.
- Use pooled ping-pong/scratch storage, bounded cancellation between passes/tiles, and memory-budgeted Rayon scheduling.
- Add one reflected WGSL pipeline family per method/pass with validated bindings, dispatch arithmetic, workgroups, support limits, and pooled resources.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, presets/defaults, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Zero support/iterations use exact no-op semantics.
- Tiny images use the selected method’s border rule; no implicit method or radius substitution is allowed.
- Cancellation publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the identical CPU method/plan once; a GPU-only method cannot silently change algorithms.
- Unknown versions/method IDs remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Per-method synthetic fixtures: impulse response, oriented lines, point grids, edges, gradients, color ramps, highlights, texture, noise, HDR/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and seam patterns.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::diffuse`; compatibility identity remains exactly `diffuse`.
- Implement the exact differential stencil, directional derivatives, edge sensitivity, radius/scale mapping, iteration count, speed coefficients, anisotropy, thresholding, sign conventions, and legacy presets represented by [0005]. No preset is translated into a different RustTable operation.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for all advertised diffusion modes and iterations.
- Each iteration reads an immutable previous buffer and writes a separate next buffer. In-place checkerboard or order-dependent updates are prohibited.
- Iteration count and numerical timestep are not silently reduced in export. Preview reduction is allowed only through a named render-quality policy included in the plan/cache receipt.
- ROI overlap accounts for the stencil radius multiplied by required iterations, unless the exact algorithm uses an internal tiled halo-refresh strategy proven equivalent. Tiles use full-image coordinates and versioned borders.
- Output dimensions, alpha/spare channels, profile metadata, and scene-linear range are preserved; finite HDR/negative values are handled by the exact equations rather than clipped pre-emptively.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for iterations, radius/scale, directional and isotropic speeds, edge sensitivity, threshold, anisotropy, mode/preset fields, and legacy defaults.
- Register format/colorspace, order, method-dependent ROI/full-frame/tiling rules, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `DiffusePlan` resolving stencil coefficients, derivative/color domain, timestep/stability bounds, effective halo, iteration graph, quality policy, ping-pong buffers, tile strategy, host/GPU memory estimates, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar derivative/stencil and update passes with explicit floating-point ordering, pooled ping-pong storage, stability validation, and cancellation between bounded iteration blocks.
- Add reflected WGSL pipelines for derivative/update passes with checked strides/dispatches, workgroup halo handling, pooled buffers/textures, and exact ping-pong order.
- Implement a planner check proving the selected timestep/coefficients satisfy the compatibility-defined stability constraints; invalid persisted values remain visible but block execution.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, presets/defaults, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown modes, invalid/non-finite coefficients, unstable timestep combinations, excessive iteration/halo requirements, unsupported layouts, overflow, and memory estimates above budgets before publication.
- Zero iterations follow exact no-op semantics without ping-pong allocation.
- Tiny images use versioned borders or return unsupported-size; no arbitrary radius/iteration reduction is permitted.
- Cancellation releases both buffers and publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once and cannot choose another preset.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, stencil/derivatives, coefficient mapping, stability check, iteration/halo calculation, quality policy, borders, memory planning, and cache identity.
- Impulse, line, edge, gradient, flat, fine/coarse texture, noise, blur/deblur targets, color edges, HDR/negative, odd/padded/tiny, and seam fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/preset/mode and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests for deterministic ping-pong updates, neutral/zero-iteration identity, stable finite output, full-frame versus legal tiling, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under the mode-specific [0008] tolerance.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing diffuse` and focused stencil/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation diffuse --all-versions --all-presets --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits complete receipts including coefficients, iterations, stability, and quality path.
- Every advertised mode meets [0008] with no order-dependent update, tile seam, hidden iteration reduction in export, unstable output, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315, #316, #317, #318.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `diffuse` only: all modes/migrations, iterative CPU/WGPU engine, stability/tiling/memory behavior, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and algorithm contract documentation. Do not modify generalized blurs, sharpening, local contrast, denoise, or UI.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `exposure` operation for scene-linear RAW/RGB pipeline data. It must apply exposure compensation and black-level correction with exact darktable parameter/version semantics, support automatic exposure analysis where registered, preserve scene-linear headroom, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU results.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::exposure`; compatibility identity remains exactly `exposure`.
- Manual execution uses the compatibility formula derived from exposure EV and black offset in the registered parameter version. No display-referred contrast or highlight roll-off is added.
- Auto exposure, where present in [0005], performs one deterministic full-analysis pass over the exact requested ROI/mask and percentile/clipping settings. The resolved exposure/black values are frozen in the plan, persisted in the execution receipt, and shared by all tiles.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for point application; automatic histogram/reduction may use WGPU only when the deterministic reduction meets [0008], otherwise analysis runs on CPU and application remains GPU-capable.
- Finite values above 1.0 and below 0.0 are retained. Alpha/spare channels and geometry are unchanged.
- ROI is identity for manual application. Auto-analysis evidence, mask identity, sample scale, resolved values, and analysis path are included in cache identity.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for exposure, black, deflicker/auto modes, histogram percentile, target level, compensation, and all legacy fields/defaults/presets.
- Register accepted RAW/RGB formats and pipeline stages, identity ROI, full-analysis requirement by mode, CPU/WGPU capabilities, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ExposurePlan` resolving manual/auto mode, exact coefficients, analysis sample set/histogram, selected mask/ROI, output metadata, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic bounded histogram/percentile analysis with stable binning/tie behavior and explicit handling of non-finite/masked pixels.
- Implement scalar and WGSL point kernels with checked strides/dimensions, cancellation, identical coefficients, and exact alpha handling.
- Integrate imported payloads, resolved auto values, history, defaults/presets, cache keys, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/out-of-range parameters, invalid analysis ROIs/masks, empty valid samples, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Auto analysis with no valid sample returns a typed blocking result; it does not silently use zero EV.
- Cancellation during analysis/application publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries application on CPU with the same frozen coefficients.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, EV/black formula, coefficient precision, histogram binning/percentiles, mask/ROI sampling, cache identity, and validation.
- Gray ramps, color charts, deep shadows, clipped highlights, HDR/negative values, NaN/Inf policy, masked regions, odd/padded/tiny images, and auto-analysis ties.

- Property tests for monotonic manual mapping, tile independence after frozen analysis, deterministic percentiles, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masked/unmasked, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `xtask parity operation exposure --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete receipts.
- Applied coefficients and auto-analysis results are exact across execution paths; pixels meet [0008] without clipping, per-tile auto drift, or fallback replanning.

## Outcome

Deliver the production `lowlight` operation for darktable-compatible low-light/Purkinje-effect simulation. ## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::lowlight`; compatibility identity remains exactly `lowlight`.

- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required because the operation is a point/LUT transform.
- Curve control points are validated and compiled into a deterministic LUT/interpolator once per plan. CPU and WGPU consume the same generated table.
- The required color/luminance representation is provided through typed `rusttable-color` transforms; no display-profile conversion occurs inside the operation.
- ROI is identity. Output dimensions, alpha/spare channels, working profile, and finite scene-linear range are preserved except for exact compatibility-domain limiting.

- Add a WGSL point kernel with reflected bindings, checked table upload/strides/dispatches, and identical coefficients/LUT.

- Cancellation publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `rgblevels` operation. It must map independent red, green, blue, and linked-master black/mid/white levels using exact darktable semantics, support registered automatic level analysis, preserve scene-linear range and channel order, and execute deterministically on CPU and WGPU.

- Manual mapping is a point operation. Auto mode performs one deterministic full-analysis histogram per required channel/mask and freezes resolved points in `RgbLevelsPlan`; tiles never analyze independently.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for point mapping. Histogram analysis may use WGPU only when deterministic reduction meets [0008]; otherwise it runs on CPU.
- Channel ordering comes from typed RGB layout. Alpha/spare channels are copied exactly.
- Finite values outside the nominal input interval follow the versioned extrapolation/clipping rule exactly. No display/profile conversion occurs here.
- Analysis evidence, resolved points, channel mode, LUT/coefficient hashes, mask/ROI, and execution path are part of cache identity.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for all channel points, midpoint/gamma, linked mode, output levels, auto percentiles, preserve colors, and legacy fields/defaults/presets.

- Implement scalar and WGSL mapping with shared coefficient/LUT blocks, checked strides/dimensions, bounded cancellation, and exact alpha handling.
- Integrate imported payloads, resolved auto evidence, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite points, invalid black/mid/white ordering not defined by the version, unknown modes, empty required analysis samples, unsupported layouts, and overflow before publication.
- Degenerate intervals use the exact compatibility fallback or return a typed invalid-parameters result; division by zero is prohibited.

## Test matrix

- Independent RGB ramps, neutral gray, color charts, clipped channels, deep shadows, HDR/negative values, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, degenerate points, and histogram ties.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/mode/preset and [0009] images.
- Property tests for monotonicity where defined, deterministic auto points, tile independence after analysis, neutral identity, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, linked/independent, auto/manual, masked/unmasked, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing rgblevels` and focused histogram/color/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation rgblevels --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete receipts.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `levels` operation for the darktable-compatible Lab/luminance levels workflow. It must resolve black, gray, and white points manually or from deterministic image analysis, map luminance through the exact compatibility curve, preserve chroma and geometry, and reproduce all supported parameter versions on CPU and WGPU.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::levels`; compatibility identity remains exactly `levels`.
- Implement every [0005] version/mode for black/gray/white points, automatic levels, output mapping, and legacy color-domain behavior. It remains distinct from `rgblevels`.
- Manual application is a point transform in the registered luminance representation. Auto mode performs one deterministic full-analysis histogram over the exact ROI/mask and freezes resolved points in `LevelsPlan`.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for point mapping; histogram analysis uses CPU unless the deterministic GPU reduction contract is met.
- Chroma channels and alpha/spare channels are preserved according to the exact version. Geometry and working-profile evidence are unchanged.
- Out-of-range luminance follows the versioned clipping/extrapolation rule. Analysis evidence and resolved points participate in cache identity.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for black/gray/white points, output points, auto percentiles, mode, and all legacy defaults/presets.
- Register required format/colorspace, operation order, identity ROI, full-analysis requirement by mode, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `LevelsPlan` resolving validated points, deterministic histogram/percentiles, mapping coefficients/LUT, luminance/chroma transforms, domain policy, and executor.
- Implement bounded stable analysis with explicit masked/non-finite handling and deterministic ties.
- Implement scalar and WGSL mapping using the same coefficients/LUT, checked strides/dimensions, cancellation, and exact unchanged-channel handling.
- Integrate imported payloads, resolved auto evidence, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite or invalid point order, empty required analysis samples, unsupported layouts, unknown modes, and overflow before publication.
- Degenerate intervals use exact compatibility fallback or return a typed invalid-parameters result; division by zero is prohibited.
- Cancellation publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the same frozen CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions remain opaque and blocking.

## Test matrix

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `toneequal` operation. It must construct the darktable-compatible exposure/luminance mask, refine it with the registered guided-filter method, apply zone-based exposure corrections, and reproduce all supported parameter versions without seams or tile-local analysis drift.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::toneequal`; compatibility identity remains exactly `toneequal`.
- Implement every [0005] mask estimator, exposure-zone curve, guided-filter/refinement mode, detail preservation, contrast compensation, and legacy field exactly. It is not replaced by a generic local-contrast or tone-curve operation.
- The base exposure mask and all required statistics are computed once for the complete analysis image at the render-request scale, then frozen in `ToneEqualizerPlan`. Tiles consume the same mask coordinate system.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for mask construction, guided filtering, zone lookup, and correction application.
- Zone controls are compiled into a deterministic continuous lookup function/LUT using the exact interpolation and extrapolation rules. CPU/WGPU consume the same table.
- ROI overlap includes the complete mask estimator and guided-filter support. If full-image refinement is required for parity, the operation declares full-analysis rather than generating independent tile masks.
- Output dimensions, alpha/spare channels, working-profile evidence, and finite scene-linear range are preserved except for the exact exposure correction.

## Implementation

- Add all DTOs/migrations for zone exposure points, mask exposure/contrast, smoothing diameter, feathering/refinement, detail preservation, method flags, and legacy defaults/presets.
- Register required format/colorspace, operation order, full-analysis/mask requirements, ROI/overlap, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated curve controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ToneEqualizerPlan` resolving analysis scale, luminance transform, mask estimator, deterministic statistics, guided-filter coefficients/radii, zone LUT, exposure mapping, tile/mask geometry, scratch layout, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement scalar mask construction, box/guided-filter statistics, refinement, zone lookup, and scene-linear exposure application with pooled buffers and bounded cancellation points.
- Add WGSL mask/refinement/application passes with reflected bindings, checked dispatch/strides, uploaded zone LUT, pooled resources, and exact pass order.
- Publish the refined mask through the operation/raster-mask diagnostic contract where requested; include mask source/parameter hashes in cache identity.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/non-finite zone curves, invalid mask/refinement settings, empty required analysis evidence, unsupported layouts, overflow, and memory estimates above limits before publication.
- Flat/zero-luminance and tiny images use exact versioned fallback or return a typed unsupported-input result; division by zero and arbitrary masks are prohibited.
- Cancellation during mask/refinement/application releases all buffers and publishes no partial image/mask/cache/history.
- GPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once; it cannot reduce refinement radius or switch mask estimator.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `filmicrgb` operation with every darktable-compatible parameter version, preserve-colors mode, contrast curve, latitude, shadow/highlight reconstruction option, and automatic parameter analysis represented by [0005]. The operation must map scene-linear dynamic range to the configured display/output range without changing geometry or silently substituting another tone mapper.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::filmicrgb`; compatibility identity remains exactly `filmicrgb`.
- Implement each manifest version as an explicit migrated parameter model with the exact scene black/white relative exposure, gray normalization, sigmoid/spline construction, contrast, latitude, balance, saturation, preserve-color, gamut handling, output power, and reconstruction semantics for that version.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for reconstruction passes, curve/LUT application, color preservation, and output mapping.
- Automatic black/white/gray and reconstruction analysis is a deterministic full-analysis stage. Resolved values, sample scale, ROI/mask, histogram/statistics, and source version are frozen in `FilmicRgbPlan` and persisted in receipts/cache identity.
- The tone curve is compiled once into exact coefficients/LUT plus analytic branch data where required. CPU and WGPU consume the same plan; export never uses a preview curve.
- Preserve-colors modes remain distinct and use typed working-profile matrices/primaries from `rusttable-color`. No hidden output-profile conversion occurs.
- Highlight reconstruction runs only when enabled by the persisted version/parameters and uses the exact neighborhood/full-frame requirements. It is not delegated to `highlights` or `colorreconstruction`.
- Finite HDR/negative values follow the versioned scene/display domain rules. Alpha/spare channels and dimensions remain unchanged.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for all registered versions, including scene endpoints, gray, contrast, latitude, balance, saturation, preserve-color/gamut modes, output power, reconstruction thresholds/iterations, auto controls, and defaults/presets.
- Register scene-linear RGB input, display-referred/encoded working output semantics, operation order, full-analysis/reconstruction requirements, ROI/overlap, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `FilmicRgbPlan` resolving deterministic auto values, tone-curve branches/LUT, color-preservation matrices, saturation/gamut coefficients, reconstruction graph, neighborhood support, output metadata, scratch/memory plan, and executor.
- Implement stable histogram/statistics analysis, curve construction with continuity/monotonicity checks, scalar reconstruction, tone mapping, color preservation, gamut/saturation handling, and output mapping with pooled buffers and bounded cancellation.
- Add reflected WGSL passes for reconstruction and point mapping with checked resources/dispatches and shared curve/color data.
- Integrate imported opaque payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, generated controls, and exact compatibility findings.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid/non-finite scene endpoints, impossible curve/latitude/contrast combinations, unknown preserve-color/gamut IDs, empty required analysis, unsupported profiles/layouts, unstable reconstruction settings, overflow, and memory estimates above limits before publication.
- Auto-analysis failure is explicit and does not substitute default endpoints.
- Curve construction that cannot meet the version’s continuity/monotonicity constraints returns a typed invalid-parameters result.
- Cancellation during analysis/reconstruction/application releases all buffers and publishes no partial state.
- GPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once; it cannot disable reconstruction, alter curve resolution, or choose another preserve-color mode.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, auto statistics, scene endpoint/gray math, curve construction/continuity/monotonicity, each preserve-color/gamut mode, reconstruction support, output metadata, memory planning, and cache identity.
- Gray ramps, step wedges, color charts, saturated primaries, skin tones, deep shadows, extreme highlights, clipped channels, HDR/negative values, reconstruction patterns, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, and curve-boundary fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/mode/preset and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests for deterministic auto values/curve, tile independence after frozen analysis, monotonic luminance mapping where defined, finite output, neutral-gray behavior, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, reconstruction on/off, every preserve-color mode, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under version-specific [0008] classes.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing filmicrgb` and focused color/histogram/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation filmicrgb --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete curve, analysis, reconstruction, and image receipts.
- All supported histories meet [0008] with identical resolved parameters/curve metadata and no per-tile analysis drift, preview curve in export, silent reconstruction disablement, profile substitution, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315, #316, #317, #318, #319, #320, #321, #322, #323, #324, #325, #326, #327.

## One-PR boundary

Do not modify legacy `filmic`, sigmoid/AgX, output profiles, standalone highlight reconstruction, or UI interaction.

Deliver the production `rgbcurve` operation. It must apply darktable-compatible linked-master or independent red/green/blue curves, preserve every supported interpolation and preserve-colors mode, support registered automatic curve setup, and execute deterministically as a CPU/WGPU point transform.

- Independent RGB ramps, neutral gray, color charts, curve knots, overshoot cases, clipped/HDR/negative values, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, and histogram ties.

- CPU/WGPU, linked/independent, auto/manual, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `tonecurve` operation for darktable-compatible Lab/luminance curve editing. It must apply the registered luminance curve and chroma/saturation behavior, preserve every interpolation and parameter version, and execute deterministically as a CPU/WGPU point transform.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::tonecurve`; compatibility identity remains exactly `tonecurve`.
- Implement every [0005] curve-node representation, interpolation, autoscale/unbound mode, chroma handling, legacy preset, and extrapolation rule. It remains distinct from `rgbcurve` and `basecurve`.
- Curve nodes are validated and compiled once into deterministic coefficients/LUT. CPU and WGPU consume the same table.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- The operation applies in the exact Lab/luminance representation specified by each compatibility version using typed color transforms. Chroma channels are preserved or scaled only according to that version.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged.
- Finite values outside the nominal curve interval follow exact versioned unbound/clipping behavior; no display profile conversion occurs.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for curve nodes, interpolation, autoscale/unbound, chroma/saturation mode, and all legacy defaults/presets.
- Register required format/colorspace, operation order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated curve controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ToneCurvePlan` resolving validated nodes, interpolation coefficients/LUT, luminance/chroma transforms, extrapolation policy, output metadata, and executor.
- Implement scalar and WGSL point kernels using shared LUT/coefficients, checked strides/dimensions, bounded cancellation, and exact unchanged-channel/alpha handling.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/non-finite nodes, duplicate/unsorted x positions without versioned resolution, unknown interpolation/mode IDs, unsupported layouts, and overflow before publication.
- Neutral curve uses exact identity semantics.
- Non-finite source values follow the shared policy and cannot create invalid LUT indices.
- Cancellation publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions remain opaque and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, node validation, each interpolation, LUT generation, Lab/luminance/chroma handling, extrapolation, metadata/cache identity, and validation.
- Luminance ramps, fixed-L color patches, neutral/color charts, curve knots, overshoot, HDR/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha fixtures.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `tonemap` compatibility operation. It must implement every registered darktable local tone-mapping method and parameter version, including global adaptation and spatial/local-contrast behavior where present, without substituting `globaltonemap`, filmic, or tone equalizer.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::tonemap`; compatibility identity remains exactly `tonemap`.
- The [0005] manifest is authoritative: every listed algorithm, contrast/compression parameter, spatial extent, adaptation statistic, color/saturation mode, and legacy field is implemented exactly; unknown method IDs remain opaque and blocking.
- Any required global luminance/adaptation statistic is computed once over the exact analysis image/ROI/mask and frozen in `ToneMapPlan`. Tiles never estimate adaptation independently.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for all advertised local-filter and mapping passes.
- Local methods declare exact support radius/full-analysis requirements, border behavior, and memory factors. Independent tile pyramids/grids are prohibited when they alter output.
- Finite HDR/negative values follow each method’s exact domain rules. Output geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged.
- The selected algorithm, global statistics, local support, quality path, coefficients, and fallback reason are included in cache identity and receipts.

## Implementation

- Add complete tagged DTOs/migrations for every method and version, including adaptation, contrast/compression, spatial/detail, saturation/color, and legacy defaults/presets.
- Register method-specific format/colorspace, operation order, full-analysis/ROI/overlap, tiling/memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ToneMapPlan` as a method-tagged plan resolving deterministic global statistics, local-filter coefficients/support, mapping function/LUT, color handling, scratch/pass graph, memory estimate, quality mode, and executor.
- Implement stable bounded global analysis and separate scalar method modules with pooled storage and bounded cancellation.
- Add reflected WGSL pipelines for each advertised method/pass with checked resources/dispatches and shared frozen statistics/coefficients.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown methods, empty required analysis, invalid/non-finite coefficients, degenerate luminance domains, excessive support, unsupported layouts, overflow, and memory estimates above budgets before publication.
- Flat/black/tiny images use exact method fallback or return typed unsupported input; division by zero and arbitrary adaptation values are prohibited.
- Cancellation releases all analysis/local buffers and publishes no partial state.
- GPU failure retries the identical CPU method/plan once; it cannot switch methods or reduce local support in export.
- Unknown versions/methods remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration/method ID, global statistics, mapping equations/LUTs, local support/filter behavior, color modes, memory planning, cache identity, and validation.
- Luminance wedges, HDR step charts, gradients, halos, local contrast patterns, bright sky/dark foreground, saturated colors, clipped/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and seam fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every method/version/preset and representative [0009] HDR/RAW images.
- Property tests for deterministic global analysis, full-frame versus legal tiled application, finite output, neutral/no-op behavior where defined, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, every method, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under method-specific [0008] classes.

- Every advertised method meets [0008] with identical global statistics and no method substitution, per-tile adaptation drift, local-filter seams, export quality reduction, or fallback replanning.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `negadoctor` operation for color and monochrome scanned negatives. It must apply darktable-compatible film-base subtraction, channel normalization, exposure/density inversion, print properties, corrections, and all supported parameter versions while retaining scene/output metadata and deterministic CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::negadoctor`; compatibility identity remains exactly `negadoctor`.
- Implement every [0005] version’s film-base color/density, scanner exposure bias, Dmax/dynamic range, paper black/white, print exposure, soft-clip, shadows/highlights balance, color corrections, monochrome mode, and legacy defaults/presets exactly.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- Interactive sampling is outside this issue; the operation consumes persisted/resolved film-base and reference values. Picker results are ordinary parameter revisions.
- Calculations occur in the exact compatibility density/log and color domain. Coefficients, matrices, branch limits, and any LUT are built once in `NegadoctorPlan` and shared by CPU/WGPU.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged.
- Values outside the nominal scanner range follow versioned density/domain rules; arbitrary clipping or auto white balance is prohibited.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for film base, Dmax/dynamic range, exposure, paper properties, soft clip, color correction, monochrome mode, and all legacy fields/defaults/presets.
- Register input format/colorspace/transfer requirements, operation order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `NegadoctorPlan` resolving validated film-base/reference values, density/log coefficients, channel matrices, print mapping, soft-clip branches/LUT, output metadata, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar point processing with explicit finite/log-domain handling, row-block cancellation, and exact alpha preservation.
- Add a reflected WGSL point kernel with checked parameter/table uploads, strides/dispatches, and identical plan data.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite or physically/arithmetically invalid film-base/density/print values, unknown modes, singular matrices, unsupported layouts, and overflow before publication.
- Zero/negative log arguments use the exact versioned floor/extension or return typed invalid parameters; NaN generation is prohibited.
- Cancellation publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, density/log equations, film-base subtraction, channel normalization/matrices, print mapping, soft clip, monochrome mode, output metadata, cache identity, and validation.
- Synthetic color/monochrome negative ramps, known film-base patches, density wedges, color charts, scanner clipping, deep shadows/highlights, negative/HDR inputs, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/preset and [0009] negative fixtures.
- Property tests for deterministic coefficients, tile independence, finite output from finite valid input, neutral/reference behavior, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, color/monochrome, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing negadoctor` and focused color/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation negadoctor --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete receipts.
- Output and density/print plan metadata meet [0008] with no hidden auto-balance, log-domain NaNs, clipping substitution, or CPU/WGPU branch drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315, #316, #317, #318, #319, #320, #321, #322, #323, #324, #325, #326, #327, #328, #329, #330, #331, #332, #333, #334, #335, #336, #337.

## One-PR boundary
