Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorequal` operation. It must apply darktable-compatible hue-dependent brightness, chroma/saturation, and hue corrections, including the registered smoothing/refinement and gamut behavior, while preserving every supported parameter version and deterministic CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorequal`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorequal`.
- Implement every [0005] version’s hue-domain definition, brightness/chroma/hue curves, interpolation, node smoothing, white/adaptation handling, gamut mapping, and legacy defaults/presets exactly.
- Curves are circular. Validation, interpolation, and smoothing wrap continuously at the hue origin; CPU/WGPU consume the same generated LUTs.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for color conversion, curve lookup, correction, and any registered refinement/filter pass.
- Required working-profile/adaptation matrices are immutable typed inputs. The operation does not choose profiles.
- If a registered mode uses a spatially refined chroma/hue mask, the global/refined mask is planned with exact overlap/full-analysis semantics; independent tile masks are prohibited when they create seams.
- Output geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite HDR/negative values and out-of-gamut colors follow exact versioned rules.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for all curve nodes, interpolation/smoothing, hue origin, adaptation/white controls, gamut/refinement mode, and legacy defaults/presets.
- Register format/colorspace/profile requirements, order, mode-dependent ROI/overlap/full-analysis, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated circular-curve controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ColorEqualizerPlan` resolving validated circular curves/LUTs, color transforms, adaptation data, refinement graph/support, gamut policy, scratch/memory plan, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar color conversion, circular curve evaluation, correction/recombination, and registered refinement using pooled buffers and bounded cancellation.
- Add reflected WGSL point/refinement passes with checked table uploads, resources/dispatches, and identical plan data.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/non-finite circular curves, discontinuous wrap not permitted by the version, unknown modes, missing profile/adaptation evidence, singular transforms, excessive support, unsupported layouts, overflow, and memory estimates above limits.
- Achromatic/zero-chroma pixels use deterministic no-hue behavior.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/mask/cache state. GPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once without disabling refinement or gamut handling.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, circular node validation/interpolation/smoothing, hue wrap, color/adaptation transforms, gamut/refinement behavior, memory planning, cache identity, and validation.
- Hue wheels, color charts, neutral ramps, low/high chroma patches, skin/foliage/sky colors, saturated/out-of-gamut primaries, HDR/negative values, wrap-boundary samples, odd/padded/tiny images, and seam fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every version/mode/preset and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests for circular continuity, deterministic LUTs, achromatic stability, full-frame versus legal tiled/refined execution, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, refinement/gamut modes, profile variants, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorequal` and focused color/filter/pixelpipe/GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorequal --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete curve/mask/image receipts.
- Output meets [0008] with continuous hue wrap, identical LUT/adaptation metadata, no independent-tile refinement seams, hidden profile selection, or CPU/WGPU mode drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315, #316, #317, #318, #319, #320, #321, #322, #323, #324, #325, #326, #327, #328, #329, #330, #331, #332, #333, #334, #335, #336, #337, #338, #339, #340, #341, #342.

- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- The operation uses the exact Lab/opponent representation specified by the version through typed color transforms. Luminance is copied unchanged unless a legacy version explicitly couples it.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged.
- Out-of-range opponent values follow exact versioned limiting/extrapolation; no hidden gamut mapping/profile conversion is introduced.

## Implementation

- Add all DTOs/migrations for a/b contrast, offsets, mode/normalization, and legacy defaults/presets.
- Register required format/colorspace, operation order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ColorContrastPlan` resolving opponent-space transforms, channel coefficients/limits, recombination/domain behavior, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar and reflected WGSL point kernels with shared coefficients, checked dimensions/strides, bounded cancellation, and exact unchanged-channel/alpha handling.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/out-of-range controls, unknown modes, unsupported color layouts, singular transforms, and overflow before publication.
- Neutral coefficients use exact identity behavior.
- Non-finite inputs follow shared policy and cannot produce invalid transforms.
- Cancellation publishes no partial state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions remain opaque and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migrations, Lab/opponent transforms, a/b scaling/offset, channel limits, luminance preservation, identity/cache behavior, and validation.
- Lab a/b grids, neutral/color ramps, color charts, saturated/out-of-range colors, HDR/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha fixtures.

It must perform scene-referred global/shadow/midtone/highlight grading, contrast and chroma/brilliance/saturation adjustments with deterministic CPU/WGPU output.

- The exact compatibility color model for each version is encoded in a tagged plan; no version is approximated in generic HSL/HSV.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- Any registered auto color/neutral/fulcrum analysis runs once over the exact ROI/mask, freezes resolved values in `ColorBalanceRgbPlan`, and records analysis evidence in cache identity/receipts.
- Tonal masks are generated from immutable source luminance using the exact versioned equations and normalized so overlapping contributions match reference behavior. Independent tile analysis is prohibited.
- Required working-profile/adaptation matrices are immutable typed inputs. The operation does not select profiles.
- ROI is identity after analysis. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged; finite HDR/negative and out-of-gamut values follow exact versioned policies.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for all grading wheels/vectors, tonal masks/ranges, fulcrums, contrast, chroma/vibrance/brilliance/saturation, color model/adaptation/gamut modes, auto values, and all legacy defaults/presets.

- Implement stable bounded optional analysis and deterministic scalar point processing without algebraically reordering version-sensitive stages.
- Add reflected WGSL point kernels with shared matrices/LUTs/parameter blocks, checked dimensions/strides, bounded cancellation, and exact alpha handling.
- Expose deterministic tonal-mask diagnostic buffers when requested; processing and diagnostics must use identical mask coefficients.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

- Property tests for deterministic auto values/masks, mask normalization, tile independence, neutral settings, achromatic stability, finite output, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, every color/gamut mode, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, diagnostics on/off, profile variants, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorout` operation. It must resolve the persisted output profile, rendering intent, black-point compensation, gamut/soft-proof behavior, and transform working RGB into the requested output color space with exact darktable-compatible metadata and deterministic CPU/WGPU execution where representable.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorout`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorout`.
- Profile selection is persisted by stable profile kind plus canonical content hash/name. Supported kinds include built-in matrix profiles, file ICC profiles, export category, display/secondary display category, soft-proof category, and manifest-listed HDR/CICP profiles.
- Canonical CPU color conversion is performed through `rusttable-color`. Matrix/TRC profiles are compiled to Rust matrix/curve transforms. Complex ICC LUT/device-link profiles use the approved narrow LittleCMS adapter; no profile type or native handle escapes that crate.
- WGPU is required for matrix/TRC and pre-sampled 3D-LUT transforms that pass the [0008] LUT-resolution validation. Profiles that cannot be represented within tolerance execute on CPU and record `ComplexIccCpu` as the fixed fallback reason; they are never approximated with a matrix.
- Rendering intent and black-point compensation follow the selected profile/version exactly. Soft proof and gamut warning are separate render intents/diagnostic outputs, not destructive pixel edits persisted into export unless explicitly requested.
- Output pixel values and typed profile/transfer/primaries metadata are updated atomically. Geometry and alpha/spare-channel policy are unchanged.
- Profile bytes, content hash, intent, BPC, proof profile, LUT resolution/hash, and executor participate in cache identity. A path string alone is never the identity.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for profile kind/name/file reference, intent, BPC, proof/gamut modes, and all legacy defaults/presets.
- Register working RGB input, output-profile transition, operation order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU capability by compiled transform, diagnostic gamut-mask support, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ColorOutPlan` resolving immutable source/output/proof profile bytes and hashes, intent/BPC, matrix/TRC or ICC transform, optional validated 3D LUT, output transfer/primaries metadata, gamut diagnostic, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement bounded profile loading with size/signature/tag-count/tag-size limits and privacy-safe diagnostics. Cache compiled transforms by profile content hash plus intent/BPC.
- Implement scalar matrix/TRC and CPU ICC paths with bounded row-block cancellation. Add reflected WGSL matrix/curve/3D-LUT kernels with checked texture dimensions, interpolation, strides, and dispatches.
- Generate the optional gamut-warning mask from the same proof transform used for presentation; publish it as a diagnostic buffer, not alpha mutation.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, profile resolution, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing, unreadable, oversized, malformed, unsupported, or hash-mismatched profiles return typed blocking findings and preserve the original profile reference/payload. No silent sRGB substitution is permitted for existing edits.
- New edits may explicitly select the built-in sRGB default; that is a user-visible parameter, not fallback behavior.
- Unsupported rendering-intent/profile combinations and failed LUT validation remain CPU-only or blocking according to the compiled transform capability.
- Cancellation releases transform/LUT/output resources and publishes no partial state. WGPU failure retries the same compiled CPU transform once.
- Unknown versions/profile kinds remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, profile identity/hash resolution, matrix/TRC compilation, ICC adapter limits, intents/BPC, 3D-LUT sampling/interpolation/validation, output metadata, gamut-mask generation, cache identity, and error sanitization.
- ICC corpus: built-in sRGB/Adobe RGB/Rec.2020/Display P3, matrix-shaper profiles, LUT profiles, malformed/oversized profiles, proof profiles, HDR transfer profiles, and duplicate names with different content.
- Color-chart, neutral ramp, saturated/out-of-gamut, black-point, shadow, highlight, HDR/negative, odd/padded/tiny, and alpha fixtures.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/profile/intent represented by [0009].
- Property tests for transform-cache determinism, path-independent profile identity, tile independence, metadata consistency, gamut diagnostic stability, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU matrix/LUT, complex-ICC CPU fallback, preview/display/soft-proof/export, cached/uncached, and device-loss comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorout` plus focused `rusttable-color`, ICC-adapter, pixelpipe, and GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorout --all-versions --profiles fixtures/profiles --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits transform/profile/gamut receipts.
- Matrix/TRC and validated LUT outputs meet [0008]; complex ICC profiles use the exact CPU path with explicit receipts; no silent profile substitution, path-based cache collision, metadata drift, or destructive gamut-warning mutation occurs.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #257, #258, #259, #260, #261, #262, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315, #316, #317, #318, #319, #320, #321, #322, #323, #324, #325, #326, #327, #328, #329, #330, #331, #332, #333, #334, #335, #336, #337, #338, #339, #340, #341, #342, #343, #344, #345, #346, #347.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colorout` only: migrations, immutable profile resolution/validation, CPU matrix/ICC and WGPU matrix/LUT transforms, proof/gamut diagnostics, metadata/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement display-window profile monitoring, export encoders, input profiles, profile installation UI, or printer profiles.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorin` operation. It must resolve camera, embedded, standard, vendor, alternate, or file input profiles; perform chromatic adaptation and transform camera/image RGB into the selected working space; preserve exact darktable profile selection and clipping/gamut semantics; and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output where representable.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorin`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorin`.
- Supported profile sources and selection order are explicit and versioned: embedded ICC, embedded matrix/CICP, camera standard/enhanced/vendor/alternate matrices from immutable camera data, built-in profiles, and user file ICC. The persisted selection wins; missing selected evidence is blocking rather than silently falling through.
- Working profile selection is persisted separately from input profile selection. Existing edits never change because the default working profile changes.
- Canonical CPU conversion uses `rusttable-color`: pure Rust matrix/TRC/chromatic-adaptation paths for matrix profiles and the narrow LittleCMS adapter for complex ICC profiles.
- WGPU is required for matrix/TRC and validated sampled 3D-LUT transforms. Complex profiles that cannot meet [0008] as a LUT execute on CPU with the explicit `ComplexIccCpu` receipt; they are not approximated.
- Implement every [0005] intent, clipping, normalization, blue-mapping, gamut and legacy matrix behavior exactly. Chromatic adaptation method and white points are explicit plan data.
- The output is scene-linear working RGB with typed working-profile/primaries/white/transfer evidence. Geometry and alpha/spare-channel policy remain unchanged.
- Input/working profile bytes and hashes, camera-data revision, matrix source, intent/adaptation/clipping, LUT hash/resolution, and executor participate in cache identity.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for input profile source/reference, working profile source/reference, intent, adaptation, clipping/gamut/blue-mapping controls, and all legacy defaults/presets.
- Register camera/image RGB input and working RGB output transition, operation order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU capability by compiled transform, profile/camera-data dependencies, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ColorInPlan` resolving immutable source/working profile evidence and hashes, camera alias/data provenance, matrices/TRCs or ICC transforms, adaptation matrices, clipping/gamut behavior, optional validated 3D LUT, output metadata, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement bounded ICC/profile/matrix loading and validation with file/tag/curve/matrix limits and privacy-safe errors. Cache compiled transforms by content hash and all semantic options.
- Implement scalar matrix/TRC and CPU ICC paths with bounded cancellation. Add reflected WGSL matrix/curve/3D-LUT kernels with checked interpolation, textures, dimensions, strides, and dispatches.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, profile/camera-data resolution, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing/malformed/oversized selected profiles, unresolved camera aliases, invalid matrices/TRCs, singular adaptation transforms, unsupported ICC tags/intents, hash mismatches, and unsupported layouts produce typed blocking findings while preserving source references/payloads.
- No silent sRGB, standard-matrix, or embedded-profile substitution is permitted for an existing edit. New edits expose an explicit default choice.
- Non-finite/out-of-range camera values follow the exact clipping/gamut/version rules; invalid curves cannot index outside tables.
- Cancellation releases transform/LUT/output state and publishes no partial result. WGPU failure retries the same compiled CPU transform once.
- Unknown versions/profile kinds remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, profile-source precedence, immutable camera-profile resolution, matrix/TRC compilation, adaptation matrices, clipping/gamut/blue mapping, ICC adapter limits, 3D-LUT validation/interpolation, output metadata, cache identity, and error sanitization.
- Profile corpus: camera matrices, embedded ICC/matrix/CICP, built-in working spaces, matrix-shaper and LUT ICC profiles, malformed/oversized files, duplicate names/different hashes, and varied white points/intents.
- RAW/color-chart, neutral ramp, saturated/out-of-gamut, clipped-channel, HDR/negative, odd/padded/tiny, and alpha fixtures across representative [0009] cameras.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/profile/adaptation mode.
- Property tests for deterministic source selection, path-independent identity, matrix round trips where valid, tile independence, metadata consistency, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU matrix/LUT, complex-ICC CPU fallback, cached/uncached, preview/full/export, and device-loss comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorin` plus focused `rusttable-color`, camera-data, ICC-adapter, pixelpipe, and GPU tests pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorin --all-versions --profiles fixtures/profiles --cameras fixtures/cameras --cpu --wgpu --tiled` emits complete transform/provenance receipts.

Do not implement output/display profiles, profile installation UI, camera-data ingestion, RAW white balance, or color calibration.

## Outcome

Deliver the production `primaries` operation. It must remap working RGB primaries and white point using every darktable-compatible hue/purity/rotation/attenuation mode and parameter version, update typed color evidence, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output without selecting a new ICC profile.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement every [0005] version’s red/green/blue primary hue/purity controls, white-point hue/purity, rotations/attenuations, gamut handling, adaptation mode, and defaults/presets exactly.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- Source working primaries/white/transfer are immutable typed inputs. `PrimariesPlan` computes the exact source-to-modified and modified-to-pipeline matrices/adaptation specified by the version; the operation does not install or resolve an ICC profile.
- Matrix and gamut coefficients are built once, checked for invertibility and finite values, and shared by CPU/WGPU.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged.
- Finite HDR/negative/out-of-gamut values follow exact versioned gamut/domain behavior; no hidden clipping is introduced.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for each primary and white-point control, attenuation/rotation, adaptation/gamut mode, and all legacy defaults/presets.
- Register scene-linear working RGB/profile requirements, operation order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history, metadata transition, and cache fields.
- Build `PrimariesPlan` resolving source chromaticities, edited chromaticities, white/adaptation transforms, forward/inverse matrices, gamut behavior, output color evidence, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar and reflected WGSL point kernels with shared matrices/coefficients, checked dimensions/strides, bounded cancellation, and exact alpha handling.
- Integrate imported payloads, history, defaults/presets, output profile evidence, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

- Neutral controls follow exact identity behavior and retain source color evidence.
- Cancellation publishes no partial pixels/metadata/cache state. GPU failure retries the same CPU plan once.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

- RGB/XYZ basis vectors, color charts, neutral ramps, saturated/out-of-gamut primaries, skin tones, HDR/negative values, multiple working profiles, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha fixtures.

- CPU/WGPU, profile variants, gamut/adaptation modes, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Outcome

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `channelmixerrgb` operation with every darktable-compatible chromatic-adaptation, illuminant, channel-mixing, calibration, normalization, grayscale, saturation, and gamut mode registered by [0005]. The operation must transform scene-linear working RGB using immutable camera/profile evidence, preserve every supported historical parameter version, and execute deterministically on CPU and WGPU.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::channelmixerrgb`; compatibility identity remains exactly `channelmixerrgb`.
- Implement every [0005] version’s illuminant source, chromatic-adaptation transform, temperature/tint or xy coordinates, CAT matrix, RGB mixing matrix, channel normalization, gamut compression, saturation/lightness, gray/monochrome mode, clipping, spot-color/reference fields, and legacy defaults/presets exactly.
- The exact compatibility color model for each version is encoded in a tagged canonical parameter model. Unknown enum values remain opaque and execution-blocking; they are never mapped to the nearest supported illuminant, CAT, or gamut mode.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required because the steady-state operation is a matrix/point transform. Any registered automatic illuminant or neutral-reference analysis runs deterministically on CPU unless an equivalent GPU reduction is proven under [0008].
- Automatic analysis executes once over the exact analysis ROI/mask, freezes the resolved illuminant, white point, matrices, and normalization values in `ChannelMixerRgbPlan`, and records the evidence in the edit receipt and cache identity. Tiles never estimate illuminants independently.
- Camera white, embedded white, reference white, working-profile primaries, and adaptation white points are immutable typed inputs from [0076], [0148], [0193], and `rusttable-color`. The operation does not select input/output profiles or silently reuse current application defaults.
- Interactive picker behavior is outside this issue. Persisted picker/reference values are ordinary versioned parameters and are executed exactly.
- Matrix stage order is fixed by the imported version. The implementation must not algebraically combine or reorder adaptation, mixing, normalization, saturation, grayscale, or gamut stages when that changes floating-point or clipping behavior.
- ROI is identity after analysis. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite HDR/negative and out-of-gamut values follow the exact versioned domain/gamut rules.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs and field-by-field migrations for every manifest-listed version, including illuminant source and coordinates, CAT method, adaptation strength, RGB matrix coefficients, channel normalization, saturation/lightness, grayscale coefficients, gamut mode, clipping, spot/reference values, and all defaults/presets.
- Register scene-linear RGB/profile requirements, pipeline order, identity ROI, full-analysis requirement by mode, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated matrix/illuminant controls, history behavior, diagnostic outputs, and cache-key fields.
- Build a checked `ChannelMixerRgbPlan` that resolves the persisted illuminant source, source/reference/working white points, CAT matrices, RGB mixer matrix, normalization vector, grayscale/saturation/gamut functions, exact stage graph, output color evidence, analysis receipt, and executor.
- Implement deterministic illuminant/neutral analysis with stable sample ordering, bounded histogram/statistics, explicit mask/ROI handling, and exact exclusion of non-finite or clipped samples according to the selected version.
- Validate chromaticity coordinates, white points, matrix coefficients, matrix conditioning, normalization denominators, gamut parameters, and output metadata before any image allocation or history mutation.
- Implement scalar point processing using explicit `f32`/`f64` intermediate choices matching the compatibility manifest. Add bounded row-block cancellation and exact alpha/spare-channel handling.
- Add a reflected WGSL point pipeline using a single frozen plan parameter block plus optional small LUTs. Validate uniform alignment, matrix layout, strides, dimensions, dispatch arithmetic, and shader branch parity.
- Expose optional adaptation/gray diagnostic values through the established operation diagnostics contract. Diagnostic coefficients must be the same coefficients used for processing.
- Integrate imported opaque payload retention, history revisions, presets/default workflow, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing required camera/profile/white-point evidence, invalid or non-finite chromaticities, unknown CAT/gamut modes, singular or excessively ill-conditioned matrices, zero normalization denominators, unsupported layouts, arithmetic overflow, and empty required analysis samples before publication.
- A missing persisted illuminant or camera reference remains a typed blocking finding; it is not silently replaced with D50, D65, daylight, or as-shot values.
- Achromatic/zero-luminance inputs use the exact versioned hue/chroma fallback. Division by zero, undefined hue, and non-finite matrix output are prohibited.
- Cancellation during analysis or application publishes no partial image, diagnostics, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU validation/device/allocation failure retries the identical frozen CPU plan once. It cannot choose another CAT, disable gamut handling, or recompute automatic values.
- Unknown versions and enum values remain byte-preserved and visible in compatibility reports.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, illuminant source, xy/temperature conversion, each CAT, adaptation matrices, matrix stage order, channel normalization, grayscale/saturation behavior, gamut modes, automatic analysis, output metadata, cache identity, and validation failures.
- Fixtures covering gray cards, color charts, illuminant sweeps, mixed white points, camera/reference whites, matrix basis vectors, skin/foliage/sky colors, saturated/out-of-gamut primaries, HDR/negative values, clipped channels, multiple working profiles, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha channels.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version, CAT, illuminant, gamut mode, preset, and representative [0009] camera image.
- Property tests proving deterministic automatic analysis, matrix/stage identity under neutral parameters, tile independence after frozen analysis, finite output for finite valid input, output color-evidence consistency, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, automatic/manual, every CAT/gamut/grayscale mode, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, profile variants, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing channelmixerrgb` plus focused `rusttable-color`, camera-data, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation channelmixerrgb --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete analysis, matrix, metadata, diagnostic, and image receipts.
- Every supported history meets its [0008] class with identical resolved illuminants/matrices and no silent white-point fallback, per-tile analysis drift, stage reordering, hidden gamut disablement, or CPU/WGPU matrix-layout mismatch.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #304, and #349, #350, #351.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `channelmixerrgb` only: every migration/mode, deterministic optional analysis, CAT/matrix/gamut/grayscale CPU/WGPU execution, metadata/history/cache/diagnostic integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify legacy `channelmixer`, temperature, input/output profile selection, primaries, picker UI, or camera-data ingestion.

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `channelmixer` legacy operation. It must reproduce darktable’s historical destination-channel mixing, monochrome conversion, channel normalization, and all supported parameter versions as a deterministic CPU/WGPU point transform without rewriting the edit as `channelmixerrgb`.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::channelmixer`; compatibility identity remains exactly `channelmixer`.
- Implement every [0005] version’s red/green/blue destination coefficients, gray/monochrome output coefficients, normalization mode, destination-channel selection, clipping/domain behavior, operation order, and defaults/presets exactly.
- The canonical model is an explicit tagged destination-channel matrix plus versioned post-processing. Do not infer missing rows or normalize coefficients unless the persisted mode requires it.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- Interactive color sampling is outside this issue. Persisted mixer values and monochrome coefficients are ordinary parameters.
- Required color representation and any legacy profile assumptions are supplied through typed metadata; this operation does not perform chromatic adaptation, select illuminants, or resolve input/output profiles.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Monochrome mode outputs the exact version-defined channel layout while retaining the canonical RGB buffer contract required downstream.
- Finite HDR/negative values follow the exact versioned matrix and clipping/extrapolation rules; modern gamut compression is prohibited.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs and field-by-field migrations for every manifest-listed version, including all destination-channel coefficients, gray coefficients, normalization flags, clipping/mode fields, and defaults/presets.
- Register accepted format/colorspace, pipeline order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated matrix controls, history behavior, output channel semantics, and cache-key fields.
- Build a checked `ChannelMixerPlan` resolving the exact coefficient matrix/gray vector, normalization behavior, destination routing, stage order, domain/clipping policy, output metadata, and executor.
- Validate every coefficient, normalization denominator, destination-channel enum, and output layout before processing.
- Implement deterministic scalar point processing without algebraically combining version-sensitive normalization and clipping stages. Add bounded row-block cancellation and exact alpha/spare-channel handling.
- Add a reflected WGSL point kernel with shared coefficient blocks, explicit row-major/column-major layout tests, checked strides/dimensions/dispatches, and exact monochrome routing.
- Integrate imported opaque payload retention, history revisions, defaults/presets, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite coefficients, unknown destination/mode IDs, zero required normalization denominators, unsupported layouts, and arithmetic overflow before publication.
- Coefficients are not automatically constrained to sum to one unless the persisted normalization mode requires it.
- Achromatic and zero-luminance inputs use exact linear matrix behavior; no hue reconstruction or profile fallback is introduced.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image, metadata, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once. It cannot convert the edit to `channelmixerrgb`, enable normalization, or change clipping.
- Unknown versions and enum values remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, destination routing, matrix layout, normalization, monochrome output, clipping/extrapolation, output metadata, neutral/identity behavior, cache identity, and validation failures.
- Fixtures covering RGB basis vectors, arbitrary matrices, coefficient sums below/above/at one, negative coefficients, gray/color ramps, color charts, monochrome mixes, clipped/HDR/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha channels.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version, destination mode, normalization mode, monochrome preset, and representative [0009] image.
- Property tests proving deterministic linear mapping, exact identity for the identity matrix, tile independence, canonical monochrome layout, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, color/monochrome, normalized/unnormalized, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing channelmixer` plus focused color, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation channelmixer --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces complete matrix, metadata, and image receipts.
- Every supported history meets [0008] with exact matrix/stage metadata and no modern channel-mixer substitution, implicit coefficient normalization, hidden gamut compression, or CPU/WGPU matrix-layout drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, and #349, #350, #351, #352.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements legacy `channelmixer` only: every migration/mode, matrix/normalization/monochrome CPU/WGPU execution, metadata/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify `channelmixerrgb`, monochrome filtering, color profiles, picker UI, or unrelated grading operations.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `lut3d` operation. It must load, validate, cache, and apply every darktable-compatible 3D LUT format and interpolation mode registered by [0005], preserve immutable LUT provenance and color-domain semantics, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output without depending on G’MIC or an external LUT process.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::lut3d`; compatibility identity remains exactly `lut3d`.
- The [0005] capability manifest is authoritative for accepted formats. Implement pure-Rust bounded parsers for the registered `.cube`, `.3dl`, Hald CLUT image, and any other format explicitly listed there. Do not invoke G’MIC, ImageMagick, shell commands, or inherited native parser code.
- Persist LUT identity as canonical content hash, normalized format metadata, input/output domain, size, interpolation, and optional original relative reference. A filesystem path alone is never the cache or history identity.
- Supported interpolation modes are implemented exactly as registered: nearest only when present historically, trilinear, and tetrahedral. Unknown interpolation IDs remain opaque and blocking.
- CPU scalar tetrahedral/trilinear evaluation is canonical. WGPU is required using an uploaded 3D texture or storage buffer representation that samples the same canonical table and domain mapping.
- LUT domain min/max, channel order, axis order, table traversal, value encoding, and any shaper/1D pre-LUT are explicit parsed data. The implementation must not assume `0..1`, RGB-major order, or normalized values.
- LUT bytes are copied into managed immutable catalog/cache storage when an edit is committed or a darktable history is migrated. Existing edits therefore do not change when the external file is modified or removed.
- Color-profile conversion is outside this operation. The LUT executes in the exact input/output color domain recorded by the edit and descriptor; required profile evidence is validated but not silently converted.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite HDR/negative values follow the LUT domain’s exact clamp/extrapolation behavior for the selected version.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs and field-by-field migrations for LUT reference/content hash, format, interpolation, domain, strength/mix, colorspace mode, and all manifest-listed legacy fields/defaults/presets.
- Register accepted format/colorspace, pipeline order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated file/interpolation controls, history behavior, content dependency, and cache-key fields.
- Define a canonical `Lut3dAsset` containing format/version, dimensions, domain min/max, channel/axis order, optional shaper curve, canonical `f32` table, source content hash, and bounded metadata. Serialization is explicit and versioned; native Rust memory layout is not persisted.
- Implement streaming/bounded parsers with hard limits for source bytes, dimensions, table entries, numeric token length, comments/metadata, image dimensions, and decompressed bytes. Validate exact entry count, finite values, domain ordering, and multiplication overflow before allocating the table.
- Build `Lut3dPlan` resolving the immutable asset, domain normalization, optional shaper LUT, interpolation method, strength/mix behavior, color-domain metadata, GPU resource layout, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar nearest/trilinear/tetrahedral interpolation with explicit boundary behavior, shared table indexing helpers, row-block cancellation, and exact alpha handling.
- Add reflected WGSL kernels using validated 3D texture sampling for trilinear where hardware semantics meet [0008], and explicit storage-buffer tetrahedral interpolation for tetrahedral mode. Validate texture dimensions/formats, row alignment, table upload size, and interpolation coordinates.
- Cache parsed canonical assets by content hash and compiled GPU resources by device plus asset/interpolation/domain key. Release resources through bounded LRU/budget policy.
- Integrate imported payload retention, managed asset copying, history revisions, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing external files for an uncommitted new edit return a typed asset error. Migrated or committed edits use the managed immutable copy and remain renderable without the original path.
- Malformed, truncated, oversized, dimension-mismatched, non-finite, unsupported, or hash-mismatched LUTs are rejected before publication. Error messages expose normalized asset identity, not private absolute paths or unbounded file contents.
- Values outside the declared input domain use the exact versioned clamp/extrapolation rule. There is no modulo/wrap indexing or arbitrary `0..1` clamp.
- Cancellation during parse, upload, or application releases temporary resources and publishes no partial asset, image, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once. It cannot switch interpolation methods, reduce LUT resolution, or omit a shaper curve.
- Unknown versions, formats, and interpolation IDs remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, each parser/format, dimensions and axis/channel order, domain mapping, shaper curves, nearest/trilinear/tetrahedral interpolation, strength/mix, canonical serialization, content hashing, cache identity, and every parser limit/failure.
- Fixture LUTs include identity, channel swap, affine matrix, nonlinear shaper, hue rotation, saturated/out-of-domain mappings, non-unit domains, 2³/17³/33³/65³ tables, Hald images, comments/whitespace variants, malformed/truncated/oversized assets, duplicate names/different hashes, and external-file mutation/removal.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported darktable version/format/interpolation and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests proving identity-LUT behavior, deterministic parser output, canonical hash independence from path, interpolation continuity/bounds, tile independence, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, trilinear/tetrahedral, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, asset reload, constrained GPU memory, blend/mask, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing lut3d` plus focused asset, image-I/O, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation lut3d --all-versions --all-formats --all-interpolations --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces asset, parser, resource, and image receipts.
- Every advertised format/interpolation meets [0008] with exact content/domain identity and no G’MIC dependency, path-based cache collision, silent resolution reduction, interpolation substitution, or external-file drift after commit.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #246, #247, #248, #249, #250, #251, #252, #253, #254, #255, #256, and #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `lut3d` only: every registered format/parser/migration, immutable asset storage, CPU/WGPU interpolation, resource/cache/history integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement color-profile conversion, generic image overlays, preset marketplaces, LUT authoring UI, G’MIC integration, or export encoders.
