Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colormapping` operation for transferring the tonal distribution and chromatic palette of an acquired reference image to a target image. The implementation must reproduce the darktable Lab histogram matching, chroma-cluster mapping, dominance/equalization controls, and spatial smoothing for every supported parameter version, while making new acquisitions deterministic and preserving acquired reference statistics as immutable edit data.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colormapping`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colormapping`.
- The compatibility algorithm uses a 2,048-bin Lab-L cumulative histogram, one to five a/b Gaussian clusters, cluster means/standard deviations/weights, dominance-weighted source-to-target cluster matching, inverse-distance Shepard weighting, histogram equalization strength, and the exact bilateral/spatial smoothing behavior identified by [0005]. These constants and equations are not configurable beyond persisted darktable controls.
- Imported histories treat persisted source/target inverse histogram, histogram, cluster means, variances, weights, flags, cluster count, dominance, and equalization values as authoritative. Rendering never reacquires or refits those values.
- New reference acquisition is deterministic. Analysis samples are selected by a counter-based PRNG seeded from `BLAKE3(image-content-hash || operation-version || role || cluster-count)`. The seed, sample count, iterations, image scale, ROI/mask, and acquired statistics are stored in the edit receipt. The same image and parameters therefore produce the same preset on every platform.
- The operation has two explicit analysis roles: `SourceReference` and `TargetImage`. Source acquisition creates a portable managed reference payload/preset. Target analysis executes once per edit/render source and freezes target histogram/clusters in `ColorMappingPlan`; tiles never analyze independently.
- CPU scalar rendering is canonical. WGPU is required for histogram-remap lookup, per-pixel cluster weighting/mapping, and registered smoothing passes. K-means/acquisition analysis runs on CPU with deterministic reduction order.
- Reference payload identity is the canonical acquired-statistics hash plus optional managed thumbnail and originating image content hash. A catalog image ID or path alone is not a durable identity.
- The operation executes in the exact display-referred Lab domain declared by the descriptor/version. It does not select profiles or silently convert the reference/target through current display settings.
- Full-image target statistics and any bilateral grid/filter are constructed once using the full coordinate system. Independent per-tile histograms, clusters, or grids are prohibited.
- Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite out-of-domain Lab values follow exact versioned bounds and weighting behavior.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs and field-by-field migrations for flags/state, cluster count, dominance, equalization, source inverse histogram, source means/variances/weights, target histogram, target means/variances/weights, and all manifest-listed defaults/presets.
- Register Lab input/output requirements, pipeline order, full-analysis requirements, mode-dependent ROI/overlap, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, acquisition capability, generated controls, history behavior, managed reference dependency, and cache-key fields.
- Define an explicit versioned `ColorMappingReference` payload containing histogram data, cluster statistics, analysis scale/ROI, deterministic seed, source content hash, and bounded display metadata. Native Rust layout is never persisted.
- Implement bounded deterministic 2,048-bin cumulative histogram construction and inversion, with exact handling for empty bins, first-bin behavior, non-finite pixels, masks, and tie ordering.
- Implement deterministic a/b k-means for one to five clusters using the fixed seeded sample stream, fixed iteration count from the compatibility manifest, stable cluster-index tie breaking, empty-cluster handling, standard-deviation calculation, and normalized population weights.
- Implement exact dominance-weighted cluster assignment and inverse-distance Shepard weights. Direct cluster hits and zero total weights use the versioned explicit fallback; no NaN propagation is permitted.
- Build `ColorMappingPlan` resolving immutable source payload, frozen target statistics, L histogram mapping LUT, source-to-target cluster map, per-cluster mean/variance transforms, smoothing/grid plan, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar mapping and smoothing with pooled bounded buffers and cancellation between analysis, grid/filter, and application phases.
- Add reflected WGSL mapping and smoothing kernels with checked histogram/cluster table uploads, resource sizes, strides, grid dimensions, dispatches, and shared coefficients.
- Integrate reference acquisition/preset creation, imported payload retention, history revisions, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid flags, cluster counts outside `1..=5`, non-finite statistics/controls, malformed histogram lengths, negative variances/weights, unnormalized impossible payloads, empty required analysis samples, unsupported layouts, arithmetic overflow, and memory estimates beyond configured limits before publication.
- A missing managed source reference for a new uncommitted edit is a typed asset error. A committed or migrated edit must render entirely from its stored reference payload.
- Empty/constant target or source distributions use the exact compatibility fallback or block with a typed unsupported-analysis finding; no arbitrary palette is synthesized.
- Cluster ties use the lowest stable cluster index. Empty deterministic clusters are reinitialized using the farthest valid sample selected from the same deterministic sample stream and the action is recorded in the receipt.
- Cancellation during acquisition, target analysis, smoothing, or application publishes no partial reference, image, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical frozen CPU plan once. It cannot reduce cluster count, skip smoothing, or recompute statistics.
- Unknown versions/flags/payload fields remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, histogram construction/inversion, deterministic sample stream, k-means initialization/iterations/empty clusters, means/variances/weights, dominance mapping, Shepard weights, equalization, smoothing, canonical payload encoding/hash, cache identity, and all validation failures.
- Reference/target fixtures include neutral ramps, bimodal/multimodal palettes, one through five well-separated clusters, overlapping clusters, empty/constant distributions, direct cluster hits, color charts, skin/foliage/sky images, saturated/out-of-gamut Lab values, HDR/negative conversions, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, and smoothing seam patterns.
- Differential rendering tests against [0007] inject identical acquired source/target statistics for every supported version and compare the mapping stage exactly. Acquisition tests compare histograms/statistics against the reference algorithm where deterministic input sampling can be controlled and otherwise record the deliberate deterministic-seed behavior.
- Property tests proving repeated acquisition is byte-identical, reference identity is path-independent, histogram LUTs remain bounded/monotonic where defined, cluster weights sum correctly, full-frame equals legal tiled application after frozen analysis, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, cluster counts 1–5, dominance/equalization extremes, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, smoothing on/off, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colormapping` plus focused analysis, bilateral/filter, asset, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colormapping --all-versions --clusters 1..5 --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces source/target analysis, reference-payload, mapping, smoothing, and image receipts.
- Imported histories meet [0008] using their exact stored statistics. New acquisition is reproducible byte-for-byte across repeated runs and records its deterministic seed. No per-tile fitting/grid, missing-reference drift, cluster-order instability, hidden cluster reduction, or CPU/WGPU mapping divergence remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #315, #316, #317, and #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353, #354.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colormapping` only: every migration, managed acquired-reference payload, deterministic source/target analysis, histogram/cluster mapping, CPU/WGPU smoothing/application, history/cache/preset integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify deprecated `colortransfer`, generic bilateral filters, color-profile selection, reference-image selection UI, or preset-browser UI.
issues.title: Implement reference-image color mapping

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colortransfer` compatibility operation for legacy darktable histories and presets. It must reproduce the original Lab histogram and chroma-cluster transfer algorithm, preserve acquired reference statistics exactly, and render deterministically without exposing this deprecated operation as a default choice for new edits.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colortransfer`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colortransfer`.
- The operation remains supported for imported histories and explicitly applied legacy presets, but is marked `DeprecatedCompatibility` in the registry. New-edit search and default workflows hide it unless the user enables deprecated operations. It is never auto-converted to `colormapping`.
- Implement the exact version-1 compatibility data model registered by [0005]: 2,048-bin Lab-L histogram/inverse histogram, one to five a/b clusters, cluster means and standard deviations, acquisition/apply state flags, nearest-cluster mapping, per-cluster variance scaling, per-pixel cluster weighting, and exact output-domain behavior.
- Imported `hist`, `mean`, `var`, cluster count, and state flag values are authoritative immutable parameters. Rendering never reacquires the reference when those values exist.
- New acquisition for an explicitly created legacy preset is deterministic. The 20% sample stream and initial cluster means use a counter-based PRNG seeded from `BLAKE3(reference-image-content-hash || operation-version || cluster-count || "colortransfer")`. The fixed iteration count is 10, matching the compatibility algorithm. The seed and analysis evidence are persisted.
- Target-image histogram and clusters are computed once per frozen render/edit source using the same deterministic analysis rules. Tiles never perform acquisition or clustering.
- CPU scalar rendering is canonical. WGPU is required for histogram lookup, cluster-weight calculation, and color application. Acquisition/clustering runs on CPU with stable reduction order.
- The operation executes in display-referred Lab exactly as declared by the compatibility descriptor. It does not use current display-profile state, invoke `colormapping`, or add bilateral smoothing absent from the legacy algorithm.
- Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite out-of-range Lab values follow exact legacy clamp/index/variance behavior, except undefined/NaN behavior in the inherited implementation is replaced by an explicit typed failure or finite fallback documented below.

## Implementation

- Add the exact versioned DTO and migration/decoder for acquisition flags, 2,048-entry histogram, five mean pairs, five variance pairs, cluster count, and any padding/legacy serialization fields recorded by [0005]. Preserve the original opaque bytes alongside the canonical model until parity is accepted.
- Register Lab input/output, pipeline order, full target-analysis requirement, identity ROI after analysis, CPU/WGPU support, no default visibility, no new automatic presets, history behavior, blend behavior exactly as imported, and cache-key fields.
- Define a canonical explicit `LegacyColorTransferReference` payload containing histogram, means, standard deviations, cluster count, deterministic seed/evidence for newly acquired references, source content hash, and bounded display metadata.
- Implement bounded 2,048-bin cumulative histogram construction and inversion with exact first-bin and empty-image handling.
- Implement deterministic legacy k-means: 20% sample count, fixed 10 iterations, seeded random sample coordinates and initial means, stable cluster-index tie breaks, exact mean/variance accumulation, standard-deviation conversion, and explicit empty-cluster handling.
- Implement exact nearest source-to-target cluster mapping and legacy per-pixel cluster weights, including the versioned min/max-distance normalization and zero-range fallback.
- Build `LegacyColorTransferPlan` resolving immutable source statistics, frozen target statistics, histogram mapping LUT, cluster map, mean/variance transforms, domain bounds, cache identity, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar mapping with exact legacy operation order and bounded row-block cancellation.
- Add a reflected WGSL point kernel with checked histogram/cluster table uploads, explicit array layout, strides/dimensions/dispatches, and the same finite fallbacks as CPU.
- Integrate imported payload retention, explicit legacy preset acquisition, managed reference storage, history revisions, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and hidden generated controls required to inspect imported parameters.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject cluster counts outside `1..=5`, malformed histogram/table lengths, non-finite means/standard deviations, negative standard deviations, unknown flags, empty required target samples, unsupported layouts, and arithmetic overflow before publication.
- Direct imports containing non-finite legacy statistics remain preserved but execution-blocking. RustTable does not reproduce inherited undefined NaN behavior.
- Empty or zero-variance clusters use the exact finite legacy fallback selected in [0005]: preserve the target chroma component when a variance ratio cannot be formed, record the fallback counter in the receipt, and never divide by zero.
- A committed/migrated reference renders from managed statistics without the source image. Missing source files are irrelevant after successful acquisition.
- Cluster ties select the lowest stable index. Empty clusters during new deterministic acquisition are reinitialized to the farthest valid sample in the seeded stream and recorded.
- Cancellation during acquisition, target analysis, or application publishes no partial reference, image, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once. It cannot substitute `colormapping`, alter cluster count, or reacquire values.
- Unknown versions/flags/payload fields remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for exact DTO decoding/encoding, histogram construction/inversion, deterministic sample stream, 10-iteration k-means, empty clusters, means/standard deviations, nearest cluster mapping, legacy weights, variance-zero fallback, canonical reference hashing, cache identity, hidden registry status, and every validation failure.
- Fixtures include one through five clusters, neutral ramps, bimodal/multimodal palettes, overlapping/direct-hit clusters, constant/empty distributions, zero variance, color charts, saturated/out-of-range Lab values, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, and deliberately malformed/non-finite imported payloads.
- Differential rendering tests against [0007] inject identical stored source and target statistics and compare every supported parameter/state path. Acquisition comparisons use controlled seeded fixtures where possible and record RustTable’s deterministic replacement for inherited global randomness.
- Property tests proving repeated acquisition is byte-identical, imported reference statistics round-trip exactly, path independence, bounded histogram indices, finite variance fallbacks, tile independence after frozen analysis, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, cluster counts 1–5, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend behavior, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colortransfer` plus focused compatibility, asset, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colortransfer --all-states --clusters 1..5 --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces reference, target-analysis, mapping, fallback, and image receipts.
- Imported histories meet [0008] with their exact stored statistics; new explicit acquisition is reproducible; the operation is hidden from normal new-edit discovery; no silent conversion to `colormapping`, inherited NaN behavior, cluster-order instability, or CPU/WGPU table-layout mismatch remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353, #354, #355.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements deprecated `colortransfer` compatibility only: exact legacy DTO, deterministic optional acquisition, source/target analysis, CPU/WGPU mapping, managed reference/history/cache integration, hidden registry behavior, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify `colormapping`, generic clustering infrastructure beyond reusable private helpers, color-profile selection, reference-picker UI, or preset-browser UI.
issues.title: Implement deprecated color-transfer compatibility operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorize` operation. It must tint the image with the exact darktable hue, saturation, lightness, source-lightness mixing, and tonal weighting semantics for every supported parameter version, while preserving geometry and deterministic CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorize`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorize`.
- Implement every [0005] version’s hue, saturation, lightness, source-lightness mix, tonal weighting, color-domain conversion, clipping/extrapolation, and defaults/presets exactly. It is not replaced by split toning, a solid-color blend layer, or color balance.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- The selected hue/saturation/lightness is converted once into the exact versioned Lab/LCh/opponent target vector. Source-lightness mixing coefficients and any weighting LUT are built once in `ColorizePlan`; CPU/WGPU consume identical data.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. The operation retains or replaces source chroma/luminance only according to the selected version.
- Hue uses the exact circular convention and achromatic fallback. Finite HDR/negative/out-of-range values follow the compatibility domain rule; no hidden profile conversion or gamut compression is added.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs and field-by-field migrations for hue, saturation, lightness, source mix, tonal controls/mode, and all manifest-listed defaults/presets.
- Register required format/colorspace, pipeline order, identity ROI, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated hue/color controls, history behavior, and cache-key fields.
- Build a checked `ColorizePlan` resolving the target color vector, luminance/chroma transforms, source-lightness and tonal weights, stage order, domain/limiting policy, output metadata, and executor.
- Validate finite control ranges, color conversion, weighting denominators, and output channel semantics before processing.
- Implement deterministic scalar point processing with explicit branch behavior, bounded row-block cancellation, and exact alpha/spare-channel handling.
- Add a reflected WGSL point kernel using the same target vector and coefficients/LUT, with checked uniform alignment, strides, dimensions, dispatch arithmetic, and hue branch behavior.
- Integrate imported opaque payload retention, history revisions, defaults/presets, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/out-of-range controls, unknown modes, unsupported color layouts, singular transforms, zero required denominators, and arithmetic overflow before publication.
- Saturation zero uses the exact versioned achromatic result; amount/source-mix combinations that are defined as neutral use exact identity behavior.
- Undefined source hue/chroma does not affect the fixed target hue. Non-finite source values follow the shared non-finite policy.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image, metadata, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once. It cannot replace the operation with a blend mode, alter the target color, or change source-lightness mixing.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, circular hue conversion, target Lab/LCh vector, source-lightness weighting, tonal weighting, exact stage order, domain limits, output metadata, identity/cache behavior, and validation failures.
- Fixtures include neutral and color ramps, hue wheels, fixed-lightness and fixed-chroma patches, deep shadows/highlights, saturated colors, HDR/negative values, achromatic inputs, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha channels.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version, control extreme, preset, and representative [0009] image.
- Property tests proving deterministic hue wrap, tile independence, exact neutral behavior where defined, bounded weights, output-channel consistency, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, control extremes, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorize` plus focused color, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorize --all-versions --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces target-color, parameter, metadata, and image receipts.
- Every supported history meets [0008] with exact target/weight metadata and no split-toning/blend substitution, hue-wrap instability, hidden gamut processing, or CPU/WGPU branch drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353, #354, #355, #356.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colorize` only: every migration/mode, color/weight math, CPU/WGPU execution, metadata/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify split toning, color balance, blend modes, picker UI, color-profile selection, or unrelated grading operations.
issues.title: Implement colorize operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorchecker` compatibility operation, exposed to users as the color look-up-table operation. It must transform Lab colors using up to 49 persisted source/target patch pairs, reproduce version-1 and version-2 darktable histories and presets, solve the exact radial-basis interpolation coefficients deterministically, and apply the correction with CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorchecker`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorchecker`; user-facing display key is `color look up table`.
- Support one through 49 patch pairs. Each pair contains finite source Lab and target Lab values. Version 1 migrates its 24 target patches using the exact historical 24-patch source table recorded by [0005]; version 2 preserves explicit source and target arrays.
- The correction is the exact darktable radial-basis interpolation system: one radial basis term per source patch plus the compatibility affine polynomial tail, solved separately for output L, a, and b using deterministic pivoted Gaussian elimination. The basis function, coefficient ordering, matrix dimensions, pivot tie-breaking, and regularization behavior are copied exactly from the reviewed reference manifest.
- Patch fitting is performed once in `ColorLookupPlan`. Rendering consumes frozen coefficients; tiles never refit.
- CPU scalar rendering is canonical. WGPU is required for point evaluation using the same source patch table and solved coefficient arrays.
- Interactive chart detection, quadrilateral placement, patch sampling from the image, and patch-editor UI are outside this issue. This issue consumes and edits explicit source/target Lab values and provides the deterministic fitting service used by later UI.
- Built-in ColorChecker source tables and look presets are immutable versioned assets with content hashes. They are generated from checked source data during the build and do not depend on locale or current external files.
- The operation executes in display-referred Lab exactly as declared by the compatibility descriptor. It does not select profiles or perform chart-camera calibration outside the persisted patch transform.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite Lab values outside the nominal chart range follow the exact basis/domain behavior; no hidden gamut compression is added.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs and field-by-field migrations for versions 1 and 2: source L/a/b arrays, target L/a/b arrays, patch count, historical padding, and all manifest-listed presets/defaults. Preserve original opaque parameter bytes alongside the canonical model until parity is accepted.
- Register Lab input/output, pipeline order, identity ROI, tiling, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated patch-table controls, history behavior, preset capability, and cache-key fields.
- Define a bounded explicit `ColorLookupPatchSet` containing patch count, source/target Lab triples, built-in asset identity where applicable, and canonical content hash. Native Rust layout is never persisted.
- Build the exact interpolation matrix with checked dimensions up to 53×53, finite basis values, duplicate/near-duplicate source-patch detection, and deterministic row/column ordering.
- Implement deterministic scaled partial-pivot Gaussian elimination with the exact compatibility pivot selection and singularity threshold. Solve L/a/b right-hand sides independently while reusing the factorization when mathematically valid.
- Build `ColorLookupPlan` resolving validated patch set, source coordinates, solved L/a/b coefficients, affine tail, condition/singularity diagnostics, output-domain policy, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar point evaluation with explicit basis evaluation, stage order, row-block cancellation, and exact alpha/spare-channel handling.
- Add a reflected WGSL point kernel with checked patch/coefficient storage-buffer layout, maximum count, uniform alignment, strides/dimensions/dispatches, and identical basis/order.
- Cache solved plans by canonical patch-set hash plus operation version. Do not key by preset name or catalog ID.
- Integrate imported payload retention, history revisions, built-in and user presets, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject patch counts outside `1..=49`, non-finite patch values, malformed arrays, unknown versions, duplicate or numerically indistinguishable source patches that make the exact system singular, arithmetic overflow, unsupported layouts, and matrices exceeding the configured condition/singularity limit before publication.
- Singular systems remain typed invalid-parameter findings. RustTable does not silently drop patches, add arbitrary regularization, reduce patch count, or substitute a 3D LUT.
- Identity source/target patch sets must solve to an identity transform within the strict fitting tolerance. An invalid solve never publishes coefficients or history state.
- Cancellation during fitting or application publishes no partial plan, image, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical solved CPU plan once. It cannot refit with fewer patches or a different basis.
- Unknown future fields and unsupported versions remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for version-1 migration using the exact historical 24 source patches, version-2 round trips, patch validation, basis evaluation, matrix construction, pivot tie-breaking, Gaussian elimination, singular/ill-conditioned systems, coefficient serialization, cache identity, and output metadata.
- Patch fixtures include 1, 3, 4, 24, and 49 points; exact identity; affine transforms; known nonlinear transforms; ColorChecker Classic source/target sets; duplicate and near-duplicate patches; extreme but finite Lab values; and deliberately malformed arrays.
- Image fixtures include Lab basis grids, color charts, neutral ramps, skin/foliage/sky colors, saturated/out-of-range patches, HDR/negative-converted values, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha channels.
- Differential tests against [0007] for both parameter versions, every registered preset, 24- and 49-patch fits, and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests proving deterministic coefficient solves across thread counts, identity-patch behavior, canonical hash independence from preset name, tile independence, finite output for valid systems, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, patch counts 1–49, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, maximum storage-buffer path, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorchecker` plus focused linear-algebra, color, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorchecker --all-versions --patch-counts 1,3,4,24,49 --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces patch-set, solve, condition, coefficient, and image receipts.
- Every supported history/preset meets [0008] with exact version-1 migration and no patch dropping, arbitrary regularization, preset-name cache collision, per-tile fitting, or CPU/WGPU basis-order mismatch.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353, #354, #355, #356, #357.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colorchecker` only: exact migrations/source tables, bounded patch-set model, deterministic radial-basis fitting, CPU/WGPU application, preset/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement chart detection/placement, image patch sampling UI, camera-profile generation, generic 3D LUT authoring, or color-profile selection.
issues.title: Implement color look-up-table patch interpolation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorharmonizer` operation introduced by current darktable. It must pull image hues toward a selected harmony palette in darktable UCS/JCH space, reproduce all ten harmony-rule modes and their exact RYB geometry, apply per-node saturation and neutral protection, support deterministic auto detection and smoothing, and produce CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorharmonizer`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorharmonizer`; parameter schema version is 1 unless [0005] records a newer version before implementation begins.
- Implement these exact rule IDs and geometry: monochromatic, analogous, analogous-complementary, complementary, split-complementary, dyad, triad, tetrad, square, and custom.
- Use a 360-bin hue histogram, at most four harmony nodes, and 720-entry (0.5°) UCS↔RYB lookup tables generated from the same canonical RYB/UCS mapping used by the vectorscope harmony guide. The generated table bytes and content hash are checked fixtures and shared by processing and UI.
- Predefined rule nodes are derived by converting the UCS anchor hue to RYB rotation, applying the fixed harmony-sector angle table, and converting node angles back to UCS. Custom mode uses two through four persisted UCS hue nodes directly.
- Hue attraction uses the exact nearest-node Gaussian weighting: `sigma = pull_width * 0.5 / node_count`; choose the node with the largest `exp(-d²/(2*sigma²))`, use shortest circular hue difference, and multiply that difference by the winning weight and `pull_strength`.
- Per-node saturation multipliers, neutral protection, and smoothing follow the exact version-1 stage order. Hue and chroma are evaluated in typed darktable UCS/JCH using the immutable working-profile data from `rusttable-color`.
- Auto detection is deterministic full-image analysis. It builds the weighted 360-bin hue histogram at the exact preview/analysis scale, excludes near-neutral/invalid samples by the registered rule, and chooses the anchor/rule evidence with stable circular peak ordering. The resolved anchor and histogram hash are frozen in `ColorHarmonizerPlan`; tiles never auto-detect independently.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for hue-map construction, optional spatial smoothing, and application. Auto-analysis reduction remains CPU unless a deterministic GPU path meets [0008].
- Interactive vectorscope synchronization and buttons are outside this issue. The operation exposes a deterministic analysis service and resolved parameter revision for later UI issues.
- Smoothing uses the exact registered Gaussian radius/sigma in a full-image hue-correction map. Independent tile smoothing maps are prohibited. The final application may be tiled against the frozen smoothed map.
- ROI is identity after analysis/map construction. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. Finite HDR/negative/out-of-gamut values follow typed UCS conversion and versioned gamut behavior.

## Implementation

- Add the exact versioned DTO for rule, anchor hue, pull strength, neutral protection, pull width, four custom hues, custom node count, four node-saturation values, smoothing, and all registered defaults/presets.
- Register scene-linear RGB/profile requirements, pipeline order, mode-dependent full-analysis/map requirements, ROI/overlap, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, generated harmony controls, vectorscope diagnostic capability, history behavior, and cache-key fields.
- Define generated immutable `ColorHarmonyGeometry` assets containing the ten rule angle sets and canonical 720-entry UCS↔RYB tables. A build-time validator checks circular continuity, inverse-error bounds, node counts, and table hashes.
- Build a checked `ColorHarmonizerPlan` resolving rule/custom nodes in UCS, per-node saturation, Gaussian constants, neutral-protection function, working-profile/UCS transforms, deterministic auto histogram/result, smoothing plan, correction-map geometry, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic 360-bin weighted hue analysis with stable circular peak handling, explicit achromatic thresholds, non-finite exclusion, and mask/ROI support.
- Implement scalar hue-shift/saturation-map construction and exact circular wrap behavior. Build the correction map from immutable input; do not let corrected pixels influence neighboring corrections.
- Implement registered Gaussian smoothing with pooled bounded buffers and cancellation between map, smoothing, and apply passes.
- Add reflected WGSL map, Gaussian, and apply kernels with checked LUT/table uploads, correction-map format, strides, dimensions, dispatches, workgroup halos, and the same circular/neutral branches.
- Expose harmony nodes, analysis histogram, winning-node map where requested, and RYB/UCS geometry through the diagnostics contract. Diagnostic values must share processing data, not recompute approximations.
- Integrate history revisions, defaults/presets, auto-detect result application, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown rule IDs, non-finite/out-of-range controls, custom node counts outside `2..=4`, invalid/duplicate custom hues where the version disallows them, zero/invalid sigma, missing working-profile evidence, unsupported layouts, arithmetic overflow, and smoothing memory estimates above limits before publication.
- `pull_strength == 0` is an exact identity operation and avoids correction-map allocation unless diagnostics are requested.
- Achromatic or near-neutral pixels receive the exact neutral-protection weighting and never produce undefined hue. If auto detection has no valid chromatic samples, it returns a typed `NoChromaticEvidence` result rather than inventing an anchor.
- Circular ties choose the lowest canonical hue bin, then the lowest rule/node index. Histogram and node order are stable across thread counts.
- Cancellation publishes no partial analysis, correction map, image, diagnostic, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical frozen CPU plan once. It cannot disable smoothing, alter node geometry, or change auto-detected values.
- Unknown future schema fields remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking until migrated.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for all ten rule IDs, sector geometry, UCS↔RYB table generation/inversion, predefined/custom node resolution, circular shortest-distance math, Gaussian weights, winning-node ties, per-node saturation, neutral protection, auto histogram/peak selection, smoothing, cache identity, and validation failures.
- Fixtures include full hue wheels, hues at every node/midpoint/wrap boundary, neutral ramps, low/high chroma samples, color charts, skin/foliage/sky colors, multiple working profiles, saturated/out-of-gamut values, HDR/negative inputs, masks, odd/padded/tiny images, and smoothing seam patterns.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every rule, control extreme, smoothing mode, preset, and representative [0009] image.
- Property tests proving circular continuity, node invariance, zero-strength identity, deterministic auto detection/histograms, full-frame versus legal tiled application using the frozen map, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, all rules, custom node counts 2–4, smoothing on/off, auto/manual, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, diagnostics on/off, profile variants, blend/mask, constrained-memory, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorharmonizer` plus focused color-harmony, UCS/RYB, analysis, Gaussian, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorharmonizer --all-rules --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces geometry-table, node, histogram, correction-map, smoothing, diagnostic, and image receipts.
- Every advertised rule meets [0008] with exact rule geometry/table hashes and no wrap discontinuity, per-tile auto detection/smoothing, vectorscope/processing mismatch, hidden node reduction, or CPU/WGPU hue-branch drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #313, #314, #315, #343, and #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353, #354, #355, #356, #357, #358.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colorharmonizer` only: exact rule geometry/assets, versioned parameters, deterministic auto analysis, CPU/WGPU map/smoothing/application, diagnostics/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement vectorscope interaction, color-picker buttons, general color equalization, profile selection, or darkroom UI.
issues.title: Implement perceptual color harmonizer

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorzones` operation. It must selectively adjust Lab lightness, chroma, and hue as functions of a selected Lab channel, reproduce darktable parameter versions 1 through 5, compile every supported spline type/version into exact high-resolution lookup tables, expose the selected-zone diagnostic mask, and execute deterministically on CPU and WGPU.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorzones`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorzones`; canonical current schema version is 5.
- Support exactly three selection/output curves: lightness, chroma, and hue. Each curve supports up to 20 nodes. The selected input channel is one of lightness, chroma, or hue.
- Implement the exact interpolation IDs registered by [0005]: cubic spline, Catmull–Rom, and monotone Hermite. Preserve spline algorithm versions V1 and V2 as distinct behavior; do not upgrade old curves merely because their node values can be represented by the current schema.
- Implement both processing modes exactly: `Smooth` and `Strong`. Implement the persisted mix/strength control over its full registered range, including negative values and values above 100%.
- Version migrations are exact and field-by-field. Version 1’s six-band arrays migrate to eight nodes using the historical duplicated endpoint/±0.001 placement, Catmull–Rom interpolation, zero strength, smooth mode, and spline V1. Versions 2–4 migrate using the exact node/count/type/default rules recorded by [0005].
- Every current plan compiles three 65,536-entry `f32` LUTs from validated nodes, interpolation type, spline version, mode, strength, and circular-hue semantics. CPU and WGPU consume the same generated table bytes and hashes.
- Hue-domain curves and hue output are circular. Node validation, interpolation, lookup, and output wrapping must be continuous at the `0/1` boundary. Lightness/chroma curves are non-circular and use their exact versioned endpoint/extrapolation rules.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required.
- Area editing, curve dragging, picker capture, and UI zoom are outside this issue. The operation exposes deterministic curve mutation/lookup and selected-zone-mask services for later Iced UI issues.
- ROI is identity and tile-independent. Geometry and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged. The operation executes in display-referred Lab exactly as declared by the descriptor; no profile selection or modern color-equalizer substitution occurs.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs and migrations for versions 1 through 5, including selected channel, all node arrays, node counts, interpolation IDs, strength, processing mode, spline version, historical band arrays, and padding/serialization fields. Preserve original opaque bytes alongside canonical parameters until parity is accepted.
- Register Lab input/output, pipeline order, identity ROI, tiling, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, selected-zone diagnostic output, generated circular/non-circular curve controls, history behavior, presets, and cache-key fields.
- Define a versioned `ColorZonesCurve` containing selected channel, validated ordered nodes, interpolation type, spline algorithm version, circularity, and exact endpoint policy. Native Rust layout is never persisted.
- Implement all historical migration rules with golden byte fixtures, including version-1 duplicated endpoints and minimum x-distance behavior.
- Validate node count `1..=20`, finite coordinates, x-domain, minimum x-distance `0.0025` where required, duplicate-node handling, endpoint rules, circular continuity, and monotonic constraints for monotone Hermite before LUT construction.
- Implement exact V1/V2 cubic, Catmull–Rom, and monotone-Hermite evaluation with deterministic coefficient ordering. Compile three 65,536-entry LUTs and record content hashes in `ColorZonesPlan`.
- Build `ColorZonesPlan` resolving selected input channel, LUTs, mode, strength, Lab/domain transforms, output wrapping/limiting, selected-zone diagnostic parameters, output metadata, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar point processing with exact Smooth/Strong stage order, LUT indexing/interpolation, hue wrapping, achromatic fallback, bounded row-block cancellation, and alpha/spare-channel handling.
- Add a reflected WGSL point kernel with three checked LUT storage buffers/textures, explicit table layout, dimensions/strides/dispatches, circular hue math, and mode-specific branches matching CPU.
- Generate the optional selected-zone mask from the same selected-channel normalization and curve influence used by processing. Publish it as a diagnostic/raster buffer, never by destructively changing alpha.
- Cache compiled LUTs by canonical curve parameters plus spline version/mode/strength. Integrate imported payload retention, history revisions, defaults/presets, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown selected channels, modes, interpolation IDs, or spline versions; non-finite nodes/strength; invalid node counts/order/minimum spacing; malformed legacy arrays; unsupported Lab layouts; table-size arithmetic overflow; and impossible monotone-Hermite inputs before publication.
- Old spline versions remain old spline versions after editing unless the user explicitly creates a versioned conversion revision through a future migration command. Loading alone never changes output.
- Achromatic pixels have deterministic hue fallback and cannot generate undefined hue indices. Hue at exactly `0` and `1` maps to the same circular position.
- Neutral curves and zero strength follow the exact identity behavior and may reuse input buffers/cache entries when permitted by the pixelpipe contract.
- Cancellation during migration/LUT construction/application publishes no partial LUT, image, mask, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once. It cannot reduce LUT resolution, switch interpolation/spline version, disable Strong mode, or alter strength.
- Unknown future fields/versions remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every version migration, especially V1 six-band→eight-node placement; node validation/minimum spacing; every spline type under V1/V2; circular hue interpolation/wrap; 65,536-entry LUT construction/indexing; Smooth/Strong processing; strength extremes; selected-channel normalization; diagnostic mask; cache identity; and every failure path.
- Curve fixtures include identity, single-node, eight-band legacy, maximum 20-node, monotone/non-monotone, sharp hue-wrap, duplicate/near-duplicate nodes, overshoot, negative/200% strength, and malformed arrays.
- Image fixtures include Lab L/C/h ramps, complete hue wheels, values exactly at LUT and node boundaries, achromatic/low-chroma pixels, color charts, saturated/out-of-range Lab values, HDR/negative-converted values, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha channels.
- Differential tests against [0007] for parameter versions 1–5, every interpolation/spline/mode/selected-channel combination represented by [0009], and all registered presets.
- Property tests proving deterministic LUT bytes/hashes, circular continuity, monotonicity for monotone Hermite, exact identity for neutral curves, tile independence, diagnostic/processing consistency, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, spline V1/V2, all interpolation/mode/channel combinations, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, diagnostics on/off, blend/mask, maximum-node/table path, and forced-fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorzones` plus focused curve, color, compatibility, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorzones --all-versions --all-splines --all-modes --all-channels --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces migration, node, LUT-hash, diagnostic, metadata, and image receipts.
- Every supported history meets [0008] with exact legacy migrations/table hashes and no spline-version upgrade, LUT-resolution reduction, hue-wrap discontinuity, destructive mask output, modern color-equalizer substitution, or CPU/WGPU table-index drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #331, #332, #333, #334, #335, #336, #337, #338, #339, #340, #341, #342, #343, and #348, #349, #350, #351, #352, #353, #354, #355, #356, #357, #358, #359.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colorzones` only: exact versions 1–5 migrations, all spline types/versions and modes, deterministic LUTs, CPU/WGPU processing, selected-zone diagnostics, history/cache/preset integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement curve-editor/picker UI, area selection gestures, `colorequal`, color harmonizer, profile selection, or vectorscope integration.
issues.title: Implement Lab color zones

issues.body:
## Outcome

Reorganize the current RustTable workspace so subsystem ownership is visible in the directory tree and the iced application is no longer a flat high-level source folder. This is the first foundation child of the execution plan in #158 and closes the immediate architecture problem identified from the current tree.

The separate local darktable clone at /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/upstream remains reference material only. Its subsystem map informs ownership; no C/C++ implementation is copied, compiled, linked, loaded, or retained.

## Fixed decisions

- Follow #158's target workspace direction and preserve the existing Rust-only/native-removal boundary.
- Keep current stable domain crates instead of collapsing them: core, catalog, catalog-store, image, image-io, metadata, import, processing, render, diagnostics, and app.
- Add rusttable-ui as the owner of reusable Iced widgets, input/focus/navigation, theme, view composition, and presentation view models currently mixed into rusttable-app.
- Keep rusttable-app as the composition root and application lifecycle/command coordinator; catalog loading orchestration remains there or in an explicitly owned application module.
- Use Rust module directories with thin lib.rs entrypoints, explicit pub(crate) boundaries, no catch-all utility module, no service locator, and no circular dependencies.
- Do not add placeholder crates for future features that have no implemented contract in this PR.

## Implementation

- Inventory current modules and move them into explicit owned directories. At minimum, rusttable-app must have distinct application/lifecycle, library/catalog-loading, and composition modules; rusttable-ui must have distinct widgets, input/navigation, theme, and view/presentation modules.
- Introduce the rusttable-ui workspace crate only for code with a real reusable UI contract, then update app imports and the central dependency-DAG policy.
- Preserve public behavior, iced messages, focus order, catalog boot/retry behavior, headless UI behavior, and all existing tests.
- Add a deterministic layout contract check to the existing fast validation surfaces. It must reject a return to a flat high-level app source directory, verify the required owners and dependency direction, and remain independent of filesystem enumeration order.
- Keep every handwritten Rust file at or below 1,000 lines. Do not weaken strict warnings/Clippy, unsafe denial, native-removal checks, exact toolchain/Bun pins, or 30/60/150-second budgets.

## Failure and edge behavior

- A missing owner, duplicate owner, forbidden app-to-low-level edge, flat high-level module, C/C++ file, or oversized Rust file fails with a stable actionable diagnostic.
- Layout checking must work on macOS and Linux and must not require the darktable reference clone or network access.
- Module moves must not change runtime behavior or expose private paths, native diagnostics, or new unsafe code.

## Test matrix and evidence

- Establish a failing layout-contract test before implementation where practical.
- Test the required workspace/crate/module ownership and forbidden dependency edges.
- Run cargo fmt, locked metadata, workspace tests, strict Clippy, source policy, native-removal policy, and diff checks.
- Run pre-commit <=30s, pre-push <=60s, and PR validation <=150s.
- Verify the iced headless boot, loading, retry, library, and navigation scenarios still pass.

## Dependencies

- No child dependency beyond the current main branch and the completed foundation/UI issues already merged.
- This issue is sequence [0003] in #158; #130 and #137 remain the earlier active sequence entries as recorded by the parent plan.

## One-PR boundary

This PR changes module/crate ownership, imports, layout contracts, and required validation wiring only. It does not implement new image processing, metadata, compatibility, packaging, GPU, or product workflows. It closes only this issue and must be squash-merged to main.
issues.title: Establish subsystem-aligned Rust workspace structure

issues.body:
## Outcome

Keep ready same-repository pull requests current with main so normal work does not stall on avoidable manual rebases, while preserving draft isolation, fork safety, race safety, squash-merge policy, and the PR validation budget.

Measured evidence on RustTable:
- PR #155 hosted validation completed in 2m19s.
- PR #157 hosted validation completed in 2m18s after rebasing through concurrent main changes.
- The current PR workflow is a single validation job with a 150-second budget; branch freshness is currently maintained manually.
- The RapidRaw workflow is a useful reference but must not be copied unchanged because it mutates branches with a write credential and has repository-specific required-check dispatch behavior.

## Fixed decisions

- This is sequence [0004] from master plan #158 and maps to exactly one squash-merged PR.
- Only open, non-draft, same-repository PRs targeting main are eligible. Never mutate drafts, fork heads, closed PRs, or branches with active checks.
- Use a dedicated least-privilege READY_PR_UPDATE_TOKEN secret when branch mutation is enabled; never grant a broad write token or use a repository-wide service account. If the secret is absent, the workflow must emit a bounded skip summary and succeed without mutation.
- Snapshot PR number, base ref/SHA, head ref/SHA, repository, draft, and state before and after every operation. If any identity or base SHA changes, skip that PR as changed.
- Merge the live main SHA into the PR head in an isolated temporary worktree and push only after a remote-head race check. Use a safe lease/compare-and-swap push; never force-push an unrelated head.
- Do not dispatch duplicate validation manually when the ordinary RustTable PR workflow is triggered by the authenticated branch update. If the implementation requires an explicit dispatch, it must carry expected base/head SHAs and reject stale inputs.
- The workflow itself must be merge-only automation, read the repository policy, use immutable action SHAs, least permissions, concurrency serialization, and a finite timeout. It must not auto-merge PRs or alter review/draft state.

## Implementation

- Add a small dependency-free Bun module under the existing scripts/ci area that exposes pure update decisions/ports and a GitHub/git adapter, modeled on the tested race-safe portions of the RapidRaw reference.
- Add deterministic fixtures for draft/fork/current/active/changed/conflict/failed/updated cases, base/head races, missing secret, and idempotent reruns.
- Add one workflow triggered by main pushes, completed RustTable PR validation runs where useful, and manual dispatch. Avoid schedules and avoid a third workflow file if the existing workflow inventory policy requires a merge into an existing workflow.
- Add only the deterministic fixture test to pre-commit and the PR cheap phase; never run GitHub mutation or remote branch updates in local/PR validation.
- Keep all workflow changes compatible with the protected main + squash-only repository policy and the 30/60/150-second budgets.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing or malformed credentials, API errors, merge conflicts, active checks, changed heads/bases, fork heads, drafts, and non-main bases produce bounded named dispositions and no branch mutation.
- A failed update of one PR must not prevent safe processing/reporting of the remaining PRs.
- A second run after a successful update reports current and performs no additional commit.
- No private path, token, repository secret, or unbounded API/native diagnostic may appear in logs or summaries.

## Test matrix and acceptance evidence

- Bun unit/fixture tests cover every disposition and all race/credential branches.
- Validate workflow syntax, immutable action pins, permissions, triggers, timeout, concurrency, and repository policy.
- Run cargo metadata, source/native/layout checks, pre-commit <=30s, pre-push <=60s, and PR validation <=150s.
- Verify the workflow never touches drafts or forks and never pushes when the remote head/base changed.
- Verify a ready same-repository branch that is behind main receives one merge update and ordinary PR validation is triggered once.
- Verify no automatic merge, review approval, draft conversion, or non-squash behavior is introduced.

## Dependencies

- Sequence [0001] and [0002] packaging entries are complete.
- This child is independent of [0003] structure, but both must preserve #158's workflow policy.

## One-PR boundary

This PR delivers only guarded ready-PR freshness automation, its pure decision adapter, fixtures, and validation wiring. It does not change PR validation commands, split workflows, alter branch protection, or implement product features. It closes only this issue and must be squash-merged to main.
issues.title: Keep ready same-repository PR branches fresh safely

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `highlights` operation for RAW mosaics and supported non-RAW RGB inputs. It must avoid magenta clipping and reconstruct clipped highlight color using all six darktable methods, every parameter version through version 4, every segmentation recovery mode, exact diagnostic masks, and deterministic CPU/WGPU behavior under full-frame, tiled, cached, preview, and export execution.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::highlights`; compatibility identity remains exactly `highlights`; canonical schema version is 4.
- Implement these exact method IDs and semantics: `Clip`, `ReconstructLCh`, `ReconstructColor`, `GuidedLaplacians`, `SegmentationBased`, and `InpaintOpposed`.
- Implement exact method-specific clipping multipliers, including the registered six-entry clip-magic table, rather than using the visible UI threshold unchanged for every algorithm.
- Implement all 12 wavelet-diameter choices from 2 through 4096 pixels, iterations `1..=256`, noise level, candidating, combine, strength, solid-color inpaint, and all seven recovery modes: off, generic, flat-generic, small, large, flat-small, and flat-large.
- Versions 1–3 migrate field-by-field to version 4 using the exact historical defaults captured by [0005]. Loading a legacy edit does not modernize its method or recompute hidden values.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for all six methods and shared passes: clipping, bilinear/remosaic, masks, distance transforms, guided Laplacian/wavelet reconstruction, segmentation, opposed inpainting, color diffusion, box/fast-guided filters, and final replacement.
- The operation accepts normalized Bayer/X-Trans RAW and supported RGB inputs but preserves the input format class. Raw methods remain CFA-aware and preserve pattern/phase. RGB inputs use only the exact compatible methods; unsupported method/input combinations block rather than silently switch.
- Global segmentation, distance transforms, multi-scale recovery, and candidate/strength masks are computed in a single full-image coordinate system. Independent tile segmentation or wavelet pyramids are prohibited.
- Diagnostic raster outputs are explicit: combined mask, candidate mask, strength mask, clipped mask, and final reconstruction contribution. They share processing buffers/coefficients and never mutate alpha.
- Output dimensions, orientation, CFA metadata, profile evidence, and alpha/spare-channel policy remain unchanged. Scene-linear values are not clipped beyond the selected method’s exact reconstruction/clip behavior.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs/migrations for versions 1–4, including historical unused/padding fields required for binary decoding, all method/recovery enums, scales, clip, noise, iterations, candidating, combine, strength, and solid color.
- Register RAW/RGB format support by method, operation order, mode-dependent full-frame/ROI/overlap requirements, memory factors, CPU/WGPU capabilities, one-instance behavior, blend support, raster-mask outputs, generated controls, history behavior, and cache-key inputs.
- Build a tagged `HighlightsPlan` per method, resolving CFA layout/phase or RGB layout, effective clip threshold, clipped/candidate masks, method support radius, scales/iterations, recovery mode, segmentation/distance-transform graph, scratch buffers, memory estimate, diagnostics, and executor.
- Implement shared checked primitives for clipped-pixel classification, CFA-aware interpolation/remosaic, same-color neighbors, mask dilation, distance transforms, B-spline/à-trous wavelets, guided filters, color diffusion, and deterministic noise generation where the compatibility algorithm requires it.
- Implement each method in a separate reviewable module with exact pass ordering and immutable-input/ping-pong behavior. Segmentation labels and tie-breaking are deterministic across thread counts.
- Use bounded pooled buffers and memory-budgeted Rayon scheduling. Full-frame methods declare that requirement explicitly; tileable methods trim exact halos without seams.
- Add reflected WGSL pipelines for every advertised pass, with generated binding metadata, checked storage dimensions/strides/dispatch arithmetic, workgroup halo handling, atomic capability checks, and deterministic fallback.
- Integrate legacy payload retention, history revisions, presets/default workflow, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, diagnostics, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown methods/recovery modes, invalid clip/noise/strength values, iterations/scales outside registered bounds, unsupported CFA/RGB combinations, malformed legacy parameters, arithmetic overflow, and memory requirements above configured limits before publication.
- Tiny images use only the exact method-specific reduced-scale or border fallback; otherwise return a typed unsupported-size finding. No unchecked neighborhood access or arbitrary method substitution is allowed.
- Empty clipped masks are an exact pass-through result with a complete receipt and no unnecessary large scratch allocations.
- Degenerate segmentation regions, absent recovery candidates, zero variance, and flat-color areas use explicit finite compatibility fallbacks and emit bounded counters. NaN/undefined inherited behavior is not reproduced.
- Cancellation between analysis, scales, iterations, or replacement publishes no partial image, mask, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical frozen CPU method/plan once. It cannot switch methods, reduce scales/iterations, disable recovery, or alter thresholds.
- Unknown future versions/enum values remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, method/recovery enum, clip-magic value, CFA phase/neighbors, mask classification/dilation, distance transform, scale/support planning, segmentation labels/ties, guided/wavelet primitives, remosaic/replacement, solid-color behavior, diagnostics, memory planning, and cache identity.
- Synthetic Bayer/X-Trans/RGB fixtures include single/multi-channel clipping, magenta highlights, saturated colored lights, gradients, speculars, fine texture, flat clipped regions, small/large disconnected segments, opposed-color boundaries, noise, odd/padded/tiny images, and every border/corner.
- Differential tests against [0007] for parameter versions 1–4, all six methods, all recovery classes, representative scales/iterations, every diagnostic mask, and [0009] cameras.
- Property tests proving deterministic labels/masks/noise, unchanged pixels outside the influence mask where required, full-frame versus legal tiled modes, CFA metadata preservation, finite output, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks on/off, constrained-memory, multiple adapters, device loss, and forced fallback comparisons under method-specific [0008] tolerances.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing highlights` plus focused masks, wavelet, guided-filter, distance-transform, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation highlights --all-versions --all-methods --all-recovery --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces parameter, mask, segmentation, scale, memory, fallback, metadata, and image receipts.
- Every advertised method meets [0008] with exact migration/clip/mask metadata and no tile segmentation seams, method substitution, hidden scale/iteration reduction, destructive diagnostics, CFA-phase drift, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #263, #264, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278, #279, #280, #281, #282, #283, #284, #285, #286, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, #293, #294, #295, #296, #297, #298, #299, #300, #301, #302, #303, #304, #305, #306, #307, #308, #309, #310, #311, #312, #313, #314, #315, #316, #317, #318, #319, #302, #303, and #360.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `highlights` only: versions 1–4, all six methods/recovery modes, shared reconstruction primitives, CPU/WGPU execution, diagnostics/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify post-demosaic `colorreconstruction`, filmic reconstruction, demosaic, mask-editor UI, or overexposure display overlays.
issues.title: Implement highlight reconstruction

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `colorreconstruction` operation for repairing clipped or desaturated highlights after demosaic. It must identify affected pixels in scene-linear RGB/Lab-compatible data, propagate trustworthy chroma from surrounding regions using every darktable-compatible mode and parameter version, preserve luminance and geometry, and execute deterministically on CPU and WGPU without overlapping the RAW-domain `highlights` operation.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::colorreconstruction`; compatibility identity remains exactly `colorreconstruction`.
- Implement every method, threshold, spatial extent, hue/chroma behavior, recovery iteration, and parameter version registered by [0005]. The operation remains a distinct post-demosaic history node; it is never merged into `highlights`, `filmicrgb`, or `colorin`.
- The affected-pixel mask is computed once from immutable input using the exact versioned luminance/chroma/clipping criteria. Corrected pixels cannot influence mask classification in the same pass.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for mask construction, neighborhood/chroma propagation, registered diffusion or wavelet passes, and final recombination.
- Any method requiring global statistics, connected regions, distance transforms, or a full-resolution chroma field performs that analysis in one full-image coordinate system. Tiles consume the frozen mask/field; independent tile reconstruction is prohibited.
- Propagation preserves the exact versioned luminance component and reconstructs only the registered chroma/hue components. Alpha/spare channels, profile evidence, dimensions, and orientation remain unchanged.
- Scene-linear values above 1.0 and below 0.0 remain representable. Only exact compatibility-domain limiting is applied; no hidden tone mapping, gamut compression, or white balance is introduced.
- Diagnostic outputs include affected-pixel mask, source-confidence mask, propagated chroma field, and final contribution where the selected method can provide them. Diagnostics never mutate alpha.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs and field-by-field migrations for every manifest-listed version, including threshold, spatial size, method, hue/chroma controls, iterations, strength, and historical padding/defaults/presets.
- Register scene-linear RGB/Lab requirements by method, pipeline order, mode-dependent full-analysis/ROI/overlap, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, diagnostics, generated controls, history behavior, and cache-key inputs.
- Build a tagged `ColorReconstructionPlan` resolving color representation, affected-pixel criteria, neighborhood/support radius, global statistics or connected-region graph, propagation method, iteration count, scratch buffers, diagnostics, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic mask classification with explicit finite/clipped-channel handling and stable thresholds.
- Implement shared checked primitives for color-space conversion, connected components where required, distance transforms, nearest/confidence propagation, edge-aware diffusion, wavelet reconstruction, neighborhood interpolation, and luminance-preserving recombination.
- Implement each registered method in a separate module with immutable source evidence and ping-pong buffers. Region labels and equal-distance ties use stable row-major/lowest-label ordering.
- Use bounded pooled buffers and memory-budgeted scheduling. Full-frame methods declare the requirement; tileable final passes trim exact halos without seams.
- Add reflected WGSL pipelines for every advertised mask/propagation/recombination pass with checked buffer dimensions, strides, workgroup halos, atomic requirements, dispatch arithmetic, and exact branch behavior.
- Integrate imported payload retention, history revisions, defaults/presets, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, diagnostics, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown methods, invalid/non-finite thresholds or strengths, unsupported color/profile layouts, impossible support/iteration values, malformed legacy parameters, arithmetic overflow, and memory estimates above configured budgets before publication.
- An empty affected-pixel mask is an exact pass-through result with complete receipt and no unnecessary propagation buffers.
- Images with no trustworthy neighboring chroma use the exact method-specific finite fallback or return a typed `NoReconstructionEvidence` finding. The implementation never invents a hue from current display settings.
- Flat/achromatic neighborhoods and zero chroma use deterministic no-hue behavior. Division by zero and non-finite weights are prohibited.
- Cancellation between analysis, regions, iterations, or application publishes no partial image, diagnostic, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU method/plan once. It cannot switch method, reduce iterations/support, or disable diagnostics requested by the render.
- Unknown future versions/enum values remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration/method ID, mask criteria, color transforms, region labels/ties, distance propagation, diffusion/wavelet primitives, luminance preservation, support/overlap, diagnostics, memory planning, cache identity, and failure paths.
- Synthetic fixtures include one/two/all-channel clipping, desaturated highlights, colored speculars, gradients, isolated and large clipped regions, adjacent trustworthy/ambiguous colors, achromatic fields, fine texture, HDR/negative values, odd/padded/tiny images, and every border/corner.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/method/preset, diagnostic output, and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests proving deterministic regions/propagation, unchanged unaffected pixels where required, exact luminance preservation, full-frame versus legal tiled final application, finite output, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, diagnostics on/off, blend/mask, constrained-memory, multiple adapters, and forced fallback comparisons under method-specific [0008] tolerances.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing colorreconstruction` plus focused color, masks, distance/diffusion, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation colorreconstruction --all-versions --all-methods --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces mask, region, propagation, memory, fallback, metadata, and image receipts.
- Every advertised method meets [0008] with no RAW-highlight substitution, tile-region seams, luminance drift, invented hue, hidden method/iteration reduction, destructive diagnostics, or unexplained CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #303, #349, and #363.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `colorreconstruction` only: every migration/method, affected-mask and propagation primitives, CPU/WGPU execution, diagnostics/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not modify RAW `highlights`, filmic reconstruction, demosaic, color profiles, overexposure overlays, or darkroom UI.
