issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `dither` operation, shown as `dither or posterize`, with every darktable-compatible random-noise, Floyd–Steinberg, automatic bit-depth, grayscale/RGB, and posterization mode. It must preserve versions 1 and 2, exact quantization and scan-order behavior, deterministic random output, and CPU/WGPU support where the algorithm permits equivalent execution.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::dither`; compatibility identity remains exactly `dither`; canonical schema version is 2.
- Preserve version 1→2 field layout exactly: method, reserved palette, reserved radius/range, and damping. Version 1 migrates byte-for-field into version 2.
- Implement exact method IDs:
  - random noise;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 1-bit grayscale;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 1-bit RGB;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 2-bit grayscale/RGB;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 4-bit grayscale/RGB;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 6-bit grayscale;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 8-bit RGB;
  - Floyd–Steinberg 16-bit RGB;
  - Floyd–Steinberg automatic;
  - posterize 2 through 8 levels per channel.
- Quantization is exactly `round_half_strictly_greater`: `rf * ceil(val*f - 0.5)`, with `f = levels-1`, `rf = 1/f`. Grayscale conversion is exactly `0.30R + 0.59G + 0.11B`.
- Before posterization/Floyd–Steinberg, clamp finite values into `0..1`; NaN follows the compatibility value `0.5`. Preserve the exact handling for infinities through the shared explicit policy.
- Floyd–Steinberg execution is scan-order dependent and is therefore canonical as a deterministic full-row/full-image CPU algorithm with exact error-neighbor weights and serpentine/order behavior from [0005]. It must not be independently tiled or parallelized across row dependencies.
- Random dithering uses a counter-based deterministic stream keyed by image content ID, edit revision, operation version, full-resolution pixel coordinate, channel, method, and user-visible damping. This replaces inherited process randomness while keeping the exact distribution/amplitude formula. CPU and WGPU use the same integer hash.
- Posterization and random dithering are tile-independent and require WGPU implementations. Floyd–Steinberg may execute CPU-only unless a wavefront WGPU implementation proves identical scan-order output; the descriptor records the exact method capability rather than approximating it in parallel.
- `FSAuto` derives levels from output render type/bit depth/scale using the exact reviewed formula. Export bit depth is taken from the typed export request; preview scale and pipeline type are part of plan/cache identity.
- Output remains nonlinear display-referred RGB with unchanged geometry and alpha/spare-channel policy. The registered built-in `dither` preset is retained exactly.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs/migrations for versions 1 and 2, all method enum values, reserved fields, damping, built-in preset, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register display-referred RGB input/output, pipeline order, method-dependent tile/full-frame capability, memory factors, CPU/WGPU support, blend support, generated controls, history behavior, and cache-key fields.
- Build a tagged `DitherPlan` resolving method, grayscale/RGB mode, levels, output bit-depth/scale inputs, quantization constants, random amplitude/distribution/key, Floyd–Steinberg scan/error buffers, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar posterization, random dithering, and full-image Floyd–Steinberg with exact quantize/error-diffusion order. Use two bounded row-error buffers rather than a full-frame error image where equivalent.
- Add reflected WGSL posterization/random kernels with identical quantization/hash/distribution, checked dimensions/strides/dispatches, and exact alpha handling.
- Record CPU-only Floyd–Steinberg capability explicitly in the registry and receipts until an exact wavefront shader exists; GPU preference must route directly to CPU without first failing a shader.
- Integrate imported payload retention, history revisions, preset, cache identity, execution receipts, cancellation, method-specific fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown method IDs, invalid/non-finite damping or scale, unsupported output bit-depth metadata, malformed legacy parameters, impossible level counts, unsupported layouts, arithmetic overflow, and memory limits before publication.
- Floyd–Steinberg cancellation occurs at deterministic row boundaries and publishes no partial image/cache/history. Its full-image requirement is explicit.
- Posterization/random tile execution uses full-image coordinates, producing identical results under every legal tile grid.
- WGPU failure for random/posterize retries the identical CPU plan once. Floyd–Steinberg is deliberately CPU-planned, not an error fallback.
- Unknown future versions/methods remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for version migration, every method ID/level, auto-level formulas across scales/export bit depths, strict-half quantization, grayscale conversion, clamp/NaN behavior, random hash/distribution/damping, exact Floyd–Steinberg error weights and scan order, preset, cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include full ramps at quantization boundaries, RGB basis/gray ramps, half-step ties, flat fields, gradients/banding, color charts, NaN/Inf policy inputs, odd/padded/tiny images, and alpha.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every mode/version and deterministic reference seeds; Floyd–Steinberg comparisons are exact or strict tolerance, random mode uses the chosen deterministic-seed compatibility receipt.
- Property tests prove random/posterize tile/thread/CPU/WGPU determinism, Floyd–Steinberg repeatability independent of Rayon configuration, output-level membership, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU random/posterize, CPU Floyd–Steinberg, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing dither` plus focused deterministic-noise, export-request, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation dither --all-versions --all-methods --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces method, levels, scan-order, noise-key, cache, and image receipts.
- Every method produces exact registered levels/ordering, random/posterize are partition-independent, Floyd–Steinberg is explicitly full-image CPU without parallel approximation, and no method substitution, nondeterministic noise, or CPU/WGPU quantization drift remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, and #375.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `dither` only: versions 1–2, all methods/levels, deterministic noise, exact CPU Floyd–Steinberg, WGPU random/posterization, history/cache/preset integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement encoder-specific quantization, export UI, film grain, palette generation, or unrelated poster effects.
issues.title: Implement dithering and posterization

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `borders` operation with exact darktable outer-aspect, border-size, picture-position, frame-line, color, orientation, basis, and legacy migration behavior. It must enlarge the image deterministically, map the original image into the requested framed canvas, preserve versions 1 through 4, and produce CPU/WGPU output with exact ROI and geometry metadata.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::borders`; compatibility identity remains exactly `borders`; canonical schema version is 4.
- Implement exact outer-aspect semantics: image aspect, constant border, the registered built-in ratio list, explicit custom ratio, and orientation `Auto`, `Portrait`, or `Landscape`.
- Implement exact border-size basis modes: `Auto`, `Width`, `Height`, `Shorter`, and `Longer`, including the versioned `max_border_size` behavior.
- Preserve exact horizontal/vertical picture offsets, frame-line size, frame-line offset, border color, and frame-line color. The unused historical text fields remain decoded/preserved but do not become authoritative over canonical numeric values.
- Version 1 migration: copy color; normalize aspect to `>=1`; infer fixed portrait/landscape from old sign/orientation; use absolute old size; set `max_border_size=false`; fill remaining version-3 defaults exactly before version-4 migration.
- Version 2 migration copies its full field set and sets `max_border_size=false`. Version 3→4 adds the exact basis default and retains every numeric field. Golden fixtures define byte-to-field behavior.
- Output dimensions are computed once with checked integer/rational arithmetic from input size, aspect/orientation, border basis, border-size mode, and position. The plan records exact left/right/top/bottom border widths and frame-line rectangle; tiles never recalculate geometry.
- The image region is copied without resampling. Border and frame pixels are filled in the operation’s declared display-referred RGB color space. Color values are persisted as exact linear/display values according to the selected version; no picker/profile selection occurs here.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for canvas fill, frame-line fill, and source copy.
- `modify_roi_out` changes output dimensions; `modify_roi_in` maps only the intersection of the requested output ROI with the embedded source rectangle. Border-only tiles require no source image data.
- Geometry metadata, forward/back transforms, crop exposer behavior, cache identity, and downstream mask coordinates are updated atomically. Alpha/spare-channel policy is explicit for border/frame/source pixels.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs/migrations for versions 1–4, all aspect/orientation/basis modes, positions, colors, sizes, historical text/padding fields, defaults/presets, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register format/colorspace, geometry-changing ROI transforms, expansion bounds, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, controls, history behavior, crop-exposer interaction, and cache-key fields.
- Build `BordersPlan` resolving normalized target aspect/orientation, size basis, exact output dimensions, source rectangle, four border widths, frame-line rectangle, colors converted into processing representation, forward/back transforms, alpha policy, and executor.
- Use checked `u64`/rational intermediate calculations and one deterministic rounding rule for every dimension/offset. Record rounding results in the receipt.
- Implement scalar rendering that first fills border color, then frame-line color where applicable, then copies intersecting source pixels. Preserve source pixels bit-for-bit where the representation matches.
- Add reflected WGSL rendering with checked source/output rectangles, colors, strides, dimensions, dispatches, and exact region precedence.
- Integrate imported payload retention, history revisions, mask/geometry transformation, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite colors/ratios/sizes/offsets, invalid aspect/orientation/basis enums, degenerate or impossible target dimensions, output pixels/bytes above configured limits, arithmetic overflow, unsupported layouts, and malformed legacy parameters before publication.
- Border size zero and no frame line preserve exact input dimensions/pixels when the selected aspect permits it; otherwise aspect expansion remains explicit.
- Position offsets at 0/1 and odd remaining border pixels use the one documented rounding/allocation rule and must not drift between CPU/WGPU.
- Requested output tiles wholly outside the source rectangle do not request or dereference source data.
- Cancellation publishes no partial geometry/image/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once without changing dimensions, offsets, colors, or frame precedence.
- Unknown future versions/modes remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, registered/custom/image/constant aspects, orientation, basis/max-border modes, output-dimension rounding, source/frame rectangles, offset distributions, ROI-in/out mapping, transforms, color/alpha policy, cache identity, and all validation failures.
- Fixtures cover landscape/portrait/square/one-pixel dimensions, every built-in aspect, custom aspects, symmetric/asymmetric positions, border size 0/0.5, frame size/offset extremes, odd residual pixels, border-only tiles, masks, HDR/negative source pixels, padded strides, and alpha.
- Differential tests against [0007] for versions 1–4, every mode/preset, colors, dimensions, and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests prove rectangle bounds/non-overlap, output dimensions satisfy selected aspect within rounding, source-copy identity, full-frame versus arbitrary output tiles, transform round trips, alpha correctness, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, blend/mask, large bounded canvases, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing borders` plus focused geometry, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation borders --all-versions --all-aspects --all-bases --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces migration, dimension, rectangle, ROI, transform, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with exact dimensions/rectangles and no source resampling, CPU/WGPU rounding drift, tile boundary gap, frame precedence error, mask-coordinate drift, or legacy-migration loss.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #376.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `borders` only: versions 1–4, aspect/basis/geometry planning, CPU/WGPU canvas rendering, ROI/transforms/mask/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement enlarge-canvas, crop, watermark, print margins, UI pickers, or export layout templates.
issues.title: Implement borders and frame-line operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `watermark` operation for compositing managed SVG watermark templates with darktable-compatible placement, scale, rotation, opacity, color, metadata-variable expansion, and parameter-version behavior. It must render safely without network/filesystem script access, preserve immutable template provenance, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::watermark`; compatibility identity remains exactly `watermark`.
- Parse and render SVG using Rust `usvg`/`resvg` contracts behind `rusttable-image-io`; no librsvg, browser engine, JavaScript, external process, network fetch, remote font, or arbitrary filesystem resource is permitted.
- Implement every [0005] version’s template reference, opacity, scale, scale mode, anchor/alignment, x/y offset, rotation, color, and legacy defaults/presets exactly.
- Imported or committed edits copy the SVG template and every allowed embedded asset into managed immutable storage. Durable identity is canonical source-byte hash plus normalized parsed-tree hash, template schema version, allowed font-set hash, and variable expansion hash; a path/name alone is never identity.
- Template variables are expanded from a frozen `WatermarkContext` containing explicit image/catalog metadata, filename, sequence/version, dimensions, rating/labels, capture/export dates, and user-defined variables registered by [0005]. Expansion uses locale-independent formatting unless the variable explicitly requests localized display text.
- Unknown variables expand according to the exact compatibility rule and emit bounded findings; no environment-variable, shell, network, or unrestricted path expansion exists.
- Fonts are resolved only from the packaged/approved application font set and embedded SVG fonts supported by the parser. Missing fonts use one explicit bundled fallback and record it in the receipt; platform-dependent font substitution is prohibited.
- Render the expanded SVG once into a premultiplied linear RGBA surface at the exact required resolution using deterministic resvg rasterization. Convert into the working profile through `rusttable-color`, then inverse-map/composite over the image.
- CPU scalar compositing is canonical. WGPU is required for transformed texture sampling and alpha compositing; SVG rasterization itself may remain CPU because it is an asset-generation stage.
- Placement uses full-image coordinates and one frozen transform. Tiles never expand variables or rasterize independently. Output geometry, alpha/spare channels, and profile evidence remain unchanged.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for template reference/hash, opacity, scale/mode, anchor, offsets, rotation, color, variable options, and historical defaults/presets.
- Register working-RGB/profile requirements, pipeline order, identity output geometry with transformed asset sampling, CPU/WGPU support, blend/mask behavior, template/context dependency, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Define explicit versioned `WatermarkAsset` and `WatermarkContext` models with bounded metadata and canonical encoding. Native Rust layout is not persisted.
- Implement bounded SVG parsing: source-byte, node, path-segment, text-length, image-data, gradient, filter, recursion, rendered-dimension, and decompressed-asset limits. Reject scripts, animation, external hrefs, unsupported filters, and unbounded resources.
- Implement deterministic variable tokenization/escaping, canonical formatting, metadata precedence, sequence context, and a rendered-context hash.
- Build `WatermarkPlan` resolving managed template, expanded SVG/tree, font resolution, raster dimensions, profile conversion, inverse placement transform, interpolation/edge behavior, opacity/alpha policy, memory estimate, and executor.
- Cache parsed templates by source hash, expanded trees by asset/context hash, raster surfaces by tree/size/font/profile hash, and WGPU textures by device/resource key under bounded budgets.
- Implement scalar inverse sampling/compositing and reflected WGSL sampling/compositing with checked textures, transforms, alpha, strides, dimensions, and dispatches.
- Integrate imported payload retention, managed assets, history revisions, presets, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing uncommitted templates, malformed/oversized/unsupported SVG, rejected external resources/scripts, invalid variables, missing required metadata, singular transforms, unsupported profiles, render dimensions above limits, and arithmetic overflow fail before publication.
- Committed/migrated edits render from managed template bytes after original files change or disappear.
- Missing fonts use only the explicit bundled fallback and record exact requested/resolved font identity; no platform search drift.
- Fully out-of-frame or zero-opacity watermark is exact pass-through.
- Cancellation during parse/expansion/rasterization/upload/application publishes no partial asset/image/cache/history.
- WGPU failure retries identical CPU compositing with the same raster and transform; it cannot rerasterize at a different resolution or font.
- Errors expose template hash/name, not private source paths or unbounded metadata values.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, variable tokenizer/escaping/formatting, metadata precedence, managed-asset identity, SVG safety limits/rejections, font fallback, raster-size/placement transforms, profile conversion, alpha compositing, cache identity, and validation.
- Templates include text-only, vector paths, gradients, embedded raster, metadata variables, Unicode, missing fonts, transparent layers, all anchors/rotations/scales, malformed/hostile SVG, duplicate names/different hashes, and modified/deleted source files.
- Differential tests against [0007] for supported SVG/template features, versions, variables, and representative [0009] images.
- Property tests prove path/platform-independent output with packaged fonts, deterministic expansion/rasterization, tile independence, transparent no-op, alpha correctness, bounded parser behavior, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU compositing, preview/full/export, cached/uncached parse/expansion/raster/texture, profile variants, blend/mask, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing watermark` plus focused SVG, metadata, asset, color, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation watermark --all-versions --templates fixtures/watermarks --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces template, variable, font, raster, transform, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with no external-resource execution, platform-font drift, path-based cache collision, per-tile expansion/rasterization, alpha error, or CPU/WGPU sampling divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #222, #223, #224, #225, #226, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #235, #236, #237, #238, #239, #240, #241, #242, #243, #244, #245, #246, #247, #248, #249, #250, #251, #252, #253, #254, #255, #256, #348, #349, #367, and #377.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `watermark` only: migrations, safe managed SVG/template context, deterministic rasterization, CPU/WGPU placement/compositing, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement watermark editor UI, raster overlay, export filename templates, arbitrary web content, external font installation, or gallery generation.
issues.title: Implement SVG watermark and template operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `rasterfile` operation, shown as `external raster masks`, for loading a managed PNG or PFM asset, extracting one or more RGB channels into a full-resolution scalar raster mask, publishing that mask to downstream operations, and supporting deterministic optional vectorization into drawn mask forms. It must preserve version-1 parameters, operate safely without mutable external-file dependence, and integrate with history, masks, caching, CPU, and WGPU.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::rasterfile`; compatibility identity remains exactly `rasterfile`; schema version is 1.
- This operation publishes a raster mask and does not modify image pixels. Its descriptor carries `WriteRaster`, no implicit blend operation, and the exact RAW/RGB colorspace identity required by the pipeline.
- Support exactly PNG and PFM input for compatibility. PNG supports bounded 8-bit and 16-bit RGB decoding; PFM supports bounded floating RGB decoding. Unsupported channel layouts or encodings are blocking rather than silently converted.
- Implement channel-selection bit flags exactly: red, green, blue, red+green, red+blue, green+blue, and all RGB. For each pixel, the mask value is the maximum selected channel value, clamped to `0..1`.
- Imported or committed edits copy the selected source into managed immutable asset storage. Durable identity is original-byte hash plus canonical decoded mask hash, format, dimensions, channel mode, and decoder version; path/file strings remain compatibility metadata only.
- The source mask is interpreted at full input-image dimensions. A size mismatch with the operation input is blocking; no implicit rescale, crop, repeat, or orientation correction is allowed.
- The operation requests the complete input extent because it publishes a full-resolution reusable raster mask. Tiles may copy/expose subregions from the frozen full mask, but the asset is decoded and validated once.
- CPU scalar channel extraction is canonical. WGPU support is required for uploading/publishing mask tiles and any downstream-compatible mask texture; asset decoding remains CPU.
- Optional vectorization uses the exact threshold `0.6` and the canonical raster-to-form service. Vectorization is a user action that creates a separate mask-history revision; it is not performed during normal rendering.
- Generated vector forms are deterministic, ordered by stable geometry keys, use full-image coordinates, and do not remain linked to the source raster after creation.

## Implementation

- Add the exact version-1 DTO: channel mode, historical path buffer, historical file buffer, explicit serialization, defaults, and opaque-source retention.
- Register RAW/RGB identity input/output, full-input ROI requirement, raster-mask publication, one managed asset dependency, CPU/WGPU mask capability, no image-pixel mutation, generated controls, history behavior, and cache-key fields.
- Define an explicit `RasterMaskAsset` containing original bytes/hash, format, width/height, selected-channel mode, canonical `f32` mask, decoded mask hash, and bounded metadata. Native Rust layout is never persisted.
- Implement bounded PNG/PFM parsers through `rusttable-image-io`: file/decompressed byte limits, dimensions/pixels, row size, bit depth, finite PFM values, channel count, endian/scale handling, and exact entry counts.
- Build `RasterFilePlan` resolving managed asset identity, input-dimension match, mask buffer/texture lease, output raster-mask ID, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic scalar extraction and clamping. Preserve exact 16-bit PNG sample assembly and PFM float behavior from the reviewed manifest.
- Add reflected WGSL mask copy/publication kernels with checked mask buffers/textures, dimensions, strides, ROI offsets, and dispatch arithmetic.
- Implement deterministic vectorization command integration using the canonical mask geometry service, threshold `0.6`, stable form ordering, transactional insertion, undo/redo, and a receipt containing form count and geometry hashes.
- Cache decoded masks by byte hash+mode and GPU resources by device+decoded hash under bounded budgets. Integrate imported payloads, managed assets, history, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing uncommitted source file, malformed/truncated/oversized PNG/PFM, unsupported bit depth/channels, non-finite PFM values not allowed by the mask policy, invalid mode bits, dimension mismatch, arithmetic overflow, and memory limits fail before mask publication.
- Committed/migrated edits render from managed bytes after original path/file changes or disappears.
- No selected channel is invalid; it does not publish an all-zero mask silently.
- Decode/validation/render failure leaves the prior complete mask state untouched and publishes a typed finding; image rendering may continue only if downstream nodes do not require the missing mask.
- Cancellation during decode, upload, or vectorization publishes no partial asset, mask, forms, cache entry, or history revision.
- WGPU failure uses the same canonical CPU mask and CPU mask path once; it does not alter values or dimensions.
- Errors expose content identity and basename only, not private absolute paths or raw file contents.
- Unknown future versions/modes remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for DTO round trip, every channel flag combination, max-selected-channel extraction, clamp behavior, 8/16-bit PNG assembly, PFM endian/scale/channel parsing, managed identity, dimension validation, cache identity, vectorization threshold/order, and all parser/resource failures.
- Assets include RGB PNG 8/16-bit, PFM RGB with both endiannesses/scales, per-channel ramps, multi-channel maxima, values below/above range, non-finite PFM policy, odd/padded/tiny/large bounded dimensions, duplicate names/different hashes, and mutated/deleted source files.
- Differential tests against [0007] for all seven modes, PNG/PFM, representative masks, published raster output, and vectorized forms where the reference geometry is stable.
- Property tests prove path-independent identity, deterministic decode/hash/vector forms, full mask versus arbitrary ROI publication, output bounds, no image-pixel mutation, undo/redo integrity, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU mask publication, cached/uncached decode/texture, RAW/RGB pipelines, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under exact mask tolerance.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing rasterfile` plus focused PNG/PFM, asset, masks, vectorization, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation rasterfile --formats png,pfm --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --vectorize` produces asset, decode, mask-hash, GPU, form, cache, and transaction receipts.
- Supported histories publish exact mask values/dimensions, render after source-file removal, produce deterministic vector forms, never mutate image pixels, and have no implicit rescale, path-based cache collision, channel-order error, or CPU/WGPU mask drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #222, #223, #224, #225, #226, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #235, #236, #237, #238, #239, #240, #241, #242, #243, #244, #245, #246, #247, #248, #249, #250, #251, #252, #253, #254, #255, #256, mask/raster contracts through #360, and #378.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `rasterfile` only: version-1 parameters, bounded PNG/PFM managed assets, channel extraction, CPU/WGPU mask publication, deterministic vectorization command, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement raster overlay compositing, mask-editor UI, arbitrary image formats, implicit mask scaling, or live external-file watching.
issues.title: Implement external raster-mask file operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `clipping` compatibility operation for legacy darktable histories. It must reproduce the historical crop, free rotation, keystone/perspective, aspect, automatic crop, interpolation, and geometry behavior across every registered parameter version while remaining hidden from normal new-edit discovery in favor of the modern crop, orientation, and perspective operations.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::clipping`; compatibility identity remains exactly `clipping`; mark it `DeprecatedCompatibility` and one-instance.
- Implement every parameter version, crop rectangle convention, angle sign/origin, keystone mode/control points, aspect mode, automatic crop, interpolation, and legacy default/preset from [0005]. Unknown enum values remain opaque and blocking.
- Imported histories and explicit legacy presets remain executable. Loading never decomposes the node into `crop`, `flip`, or `ashift`; doing so would change operation order and interpolation.
- Build one immutable projective transform from output coordinates back to source coordinates. CPU/WGPU use the same matrix, lens/crop bounds, interpolation support, and border policy.
- CPU scalar inverse resampling is canonical. WGPU is required for every registered interpolation mode.
- Output dimensions and crop origin are calculated once with checked `f64` geometry and one documented rounding rule. Automatic crop solves the largest legal axis-aligned output rectangle inside the transformed source using the exact compatibility algorithm.
- `modify_roi_out`, `modify_roi_in`, forward transform, back-transform, guides, mask distortion, raster-mask distortion, and cache identity all consume the same `ClippingPlan`; duplicate transform math is prohibited.
- Tiles use global output/source coordinates and exact interpolation halos. Geometry and mask output are seam-free.
- Alpha/outside-source behavior follows the exact version: transparent/black/edge fill is explicit, not toolkit-dependent.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs/migrations for every manifest version, historical crop/angle/aspect/keystone fields, interpolation/mode enums, defaults/presets, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, method-dependent overlap, CPU/WGPU support, mask distortion, one-instance/deprecated visibility, controls, history behavior, and cache fields.
- Build `ClippingPlan` resolving canonical source/output rectangles, aspect/orientation, projective matrix and inverse, automatic-crop solution, output dimensions, interpolation kernel/support, outside policy, transform hash, and executor.
- Implement checked crop/rotation/keystone solvers, singularity/conditioning limits, deterministic dimension rounding, and batch point transformations.
- Implement scalar inverse sampling and reflected WGSL resampling for all advertised kernels with checked matrices, coordinates, strides, dimensions, halos, and dispatches.
- Integrate mask/raster transformations, history revisions, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/out-of-range crops/angles/control points, self-intersecting/degenerate keystone geometry, singular/ill-conditioned matrices, empty output, unknown modes, excessive dimensions, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Automatic crop that cannot produce at least one output pixel returns a typed invalid-geometry finding; it does not silently disable rotation/keystone.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image, transform, mask, cache, or history state.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once without changing interpolation, dimensions, crop, or perspective.
- Unknown future versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, crop conventions, angles, aspect modes, keystone modes, automatic crop, matrix/inverse, dimension rounding, ROI mapping, interpolation support, outside policy, mask/raster transforms, deprecated status, cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include identity, edge crops, arbitrary/90° rotations, portrait/landscape aspects, all keystone directions, extreme but valid quadrilaterals, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, checkerboards, impulses, masks, alpha, and seam grids.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/mode/interpolation/preset and [0009] images.
- Property tests prove transform round trips, ROI containment, source-index bounds, full-frame versus legal tiles, mask/image alignment, identity behavior, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing clipping` plus focused geometry, resampling, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation clipping --all-versions --all-modes --all-interpolations --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces migration, matrix, dimensions, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Legacy histories meet [0008]; the operation is hidden from normal discovery; no decomposition, dimension-rounding drift, tile seam, mask misalignment, automatic-crop substitution, or CPU/WGPU transform difference remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #379.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements deprecated `clipping` only: all migrations/modes, geometry/ROI/mask transforms, CPU/WGPU inverse resampling, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement modern crop/orientation/perspective UI, lens correction, or unrelated geometry operations.
issues.title: Implement deprecated crop-and-rotate compatibility operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `crop` operation as RustTable’s modern non-resampling crop node. It must preserve darktable-compatible crop rectangles, aspect constraints, auto-crop exposure, ratio/orientation behavior, every registered parameter version, exact geometry and mask transforms, and deterministic preview/export dimensions.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::crop`; compatibility identity remains exactly `crop`; one instance.
- Implement every [0005] version’s left/top/right/bottom or normalized crop convention, aspect ratio, orientation, auto/maximum crop, crop-exposer fields, and defaults/presets exactly.
- Crop performs no interpolation. The output is an integer-aligned subrectangle of the input after applying the exact versioned normalization and rounding rule.
- Persist crop edges in canonical full-image normalized coordinates plus the source geometry revision against which they were authored. `CropPlan` resolves them once to exact integer source/output rectangles.
- Aspect presets include image, free, original, square, the registered ratio set, and explicit custom ratio. Portrait/landscape/auto orientation behavior is fixed by [0005]; no locale-dependent ratio parsing is persisted.
- Automatic crop consumes the upstream geometry-valid region/crop-exposer contract and selects the largest valid rectangle satisfying the requested aspect. It runs once; tiles never solve crops independently.
- CPU scalar copy is canonical. WGPU is required for source subrectangle copy. Source pixels remain bit-identical where the buffer representation matches.
- `modify_roi_out`, `modify_roi_in`, forward/back transforms, drawn/raster masks, guides, cache identity, output dimensions, and viewport crop handles all consume the same immutable plan.
- Output origin becomes `(0,0)` with dimensions equal to the resolved crop. Alpha/profile/orientation metadata are preserved and geometric origin metadata is updated explicitly.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs/migrations for every version, crop edges, aspect/custom ratio/orientation, auto/crop-exposer fields, defaults/presets, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, no interpolation overlap, CPU/WGPU support, crop-exposer consumption, mask distortion, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `CropPlan` resolving validated normalized edges, source integer rectangle, aspect constraint, automatic-crop solution, output dimensions, forward/back translation, source geometry hash, and executor.
- Implement checked rational/aspect solving and one deterministic edge-rounding rule; record normalized and integer edges in receipts.
- Implement scalar and reflected WGSL subrectangle copy with checked dimensions/strides/ROI intersections and cancellation.
- Integrate mask/raster transformations, history revisions, cache identity, receipts, GPU fallback, and generated control schema for later UI.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/out-of-range/crossed edges, invalid aspect/orientation, stale source geometry that cannot be deterministically rebased, empty output, automatic-crop failure, excessive dimensions, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Full-image crop is exact identity and may reuse input/cache storage.
- One-pixel-wide/high crops are legal if within limits. No implicit minimum visual size is imposed by the engine.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/geometry/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU copy once.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, edge normalization/rounding, all aspect/orientation modes, automatic crop, stale-geometry rebasing, dimensions, ROI/transform/mask mapping, identity/cache behavior, and validation.
- Fixtures cover full/edge/center crops, every aspect, portrait/landscape/square, odd dimensions, one-pixel crops, upstream rotation/lens/perspective valid regions, masks/raster masks, alpha, and arbitrary output tiles.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every version/aspect/preset and [0009] image.
- Property tests prove rectangle containment, transform round trips, source-copy identity, full-frame versus tiled copy, mask alignment, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, and forced fallback comparisons under exact/strict [0008] tolerance.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing crop` plus focused geometry, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation crop --all-versions --all-aspects --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces edge, dimension, ROI, transform, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories have exact dimensions/pixels and no resampling, edge-rounding drift, tile gap, stale-geometry guess, mask misalignment, or CPU/WGPU difference.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #380.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `crop` only: all migrations/aspects, crop solving, ROI/transforms/masks, CPU/WGPU copy, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement crop-handle UI, rotation/perspective/lens correction, final scaling, or legacy clipping.
issues.title: Implement modern crop operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `flip` operation for applying camera orientation, user-selected 90° rotations, and horizontal/vertical mirroring with exact darktable parameter versions, dimensions, pixel routing, CFA/mask geometry, and CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::flip`; compatibility identity remains exactly `flip`; one instance.
- Implement every [0005] orientation bit pattern/version and migration, including automatic use of immutable source EXIF orientation and explicit user overrides.
- Canonical orientation is a dihedral transform composed from quarter-turn rotation and optional mirror. Resolve it once into exact integer forward/inverse coordinate mappings; no floating interpolation is used.
- Automatic orientation copies the source orientation value into the edit/plan receipt so metadata changes cannot alter an existing committed edit. Explicit edits persist the resolved transform.
- 90°/270° rotations swap output width/height; 0°/180° do not. Pixels are copied exactly with deterministic integer coordinates.
- CPU scalar copy is canonical. WGPU is required.
- `modify_roi_in/out`, point forward/back transforms, CFA/X-Trans phase/layout, masks/raster masks, crop-exposer geometry, and cache identity consume the same `FlipPlan`.
- After execution, output orientation metadata is canonical top-left; the physical pixel transform—not an additional EXIF tag—represents the edit.
- Alpha/profile/color values are copied unchanged.

## Implementation

- Add exact DTOs/migrations for every orientation representation/version, automatic/explicit mode, defaults, serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing integer ROI/transforms, zero interpolation overlap, CPU/WGPU support, mask distortion, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `FlipPlan` resolving immutable source orientation, canonical dihedral transform, output dimensions, integer forward/inverse mappings, CFA/X-Trans transformed pattern/phase, output metadata, and executor.
- Implement scalar integer routing and reflected WGSL copy with checked coordinate arithmetic, dimensions, strides, ROI offsets, and dispatches.
- Integrate mask/raster transforms, crop-exposer data, history revisions, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown orientation bits/modes, inconsistent dimensions/strides, invalid CFA metadata, arithmetic overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Identity orientation is exact pass-through and may reuse input/cache storage.
- One-pixel dimensions and odd sizes are fully supported; no visual minimum exists.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/geometry/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU mapping once.
- Unknown future versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration/orientation, all eight dihedral transforms, output dimensions, integer coordinate maps/inverses, EXIF automatic snapshot, CFA/X-Trans transform/phase, ROI/mask/raster mapping, metadata canonicalization, identity/cache behavior, and validation.
- Fixtures include uniquely numbered pixel grids at odd/even/one-pixel dimensions, every CFA/X-Trans phase, masks, alpha, padded strides, and arbitrary output tiles.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/orientation and [0009] image.
- Property tests prove bijective coordinate routing, exact transform round trips, full-frame versus arbitrary tiles, pixel/alpha identity, mask/CFA alignment, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, every orientation, and forced fallback comparisons under exact tolerance.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing flip` plus focused RAW, geometry, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation flip --all-versions --all-orientations --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces source-orientation, dimensions, mappings, CFA, mask, metadata, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories are pixel-exact with no interpolation, metadata double-rotation, dimension drift, CFA/mask misalignment, tile gap, or CPU/WGPU coordinate mismatch.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #302, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #381.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `flip` only: all migrations/orientations, integer geometry/CFA/mask transforms, CPU/WGPU copy, metadata/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement free rotation, crop, perspective, sensor pixel rotation, or orientation UI.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the hidden technical `rotatepixels` operation used for camera/sensor-specific arbitrary pixel rotation. It must reproduce version-1 center/angle geometry, exact output bounds and interpolation behavior, publish complete forward/back/ROI/mask transforms, and execute deterministically on CPU and WGPU without exposing normal user controls.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::rotatepixels`; compatibility identity remains exactly `rotatepixels`; schema version is 1; one instance; hidden technical operation; unsafe-copy flag semantics are preserved through explicit history restrictions.
- Parameters are exactly unsigned rotation center `rx`, `ry` and angle in degrees. When both center coordinates are zero, the node is disabled exactly as the compatibility implementation specifies.
- Build the exact 2×2 rotation matrix around `(rx, ry)` and inverse once per plan. Use `f64` planning and explicit `f32` coefficients for processing parity.
- Output dimensions follow the reviewed compatibility geometry: derive the inner valid rectangle, subtract the selected interpolation support, round down to non-negative even dimensions, and retain the exact source/output origins.
- Interpolation is the immutable render preference resolved at edit/render plan creation and included in cache identity. CPU/WGPU support every registered interpolation kernel.
- CPU scalar inverse resampling is canonical. WGPU is required.
- `modify_roi_in/out`, point transforms, drawn/raster-mask distortion, crop-exposer valid region, and cache identity use one `RotatePixelsPlan`. Unlike the inherited TODO, RustTable must implement raster-mask distortion fully and parity-test it.
- Tiles use global coordinates and exact interpolation halos. Output geometry, profile evidence, and alpha/spare-channel policy are explicit.

## Implementation

- Add exact version-1 DTO/serialization, hidden descriptor metadata, history copy restrictions, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, full-ROI tiling behavior, interpolation overlap, CPU/WGPU support, mask/raster distortion, one-instance/hidden status, no ordinary controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `RotatePixelsPlan` resolving center, angle/matrices, interpolation kernel/support, exact output dimensions, valid source/output rectangles, forward/back transforms, mask strategy, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement checked dimension/ROI bounding-box calculations and deterministic even rounding.
- Implement scalar and reflected WGSL inverse resampling plus scalar/WGPU mask resampling with identical matrices/kernels and checked dimensions/strides/dispatches.
- Integrate camera-data activation, history/copy restrictions, geometry/masks, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, and GPU fallback.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite angle, centers outside supported source bounds, singular/non-finite matrices, invalid/empty output, unknown interpolation, overflow, excessive dimensions, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Zero center disables the operation; zero angle with a valid center follows exact compatibility geometry and is tested rather than optimized without evidence.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/transform/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once.
- Unknown future versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for DTO, enable/disable rule, matrix/inverse, compatibility output-bound formula/even rounding, ROI/support, every interpolation, point/mask/raster transforms, hidden/copy status, cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include zero/small/45°/arbitrary angles, varied centers, odd/even/tiny dimensions, checkerboards/impulses, masks/raster masks, alpha, and arbitrary tiles.
- Differential tests against [0007] for version 1, representative camera-specific parameter sets, and [0009] images.
- Property tests prove transform round trips, ROI containment, full-frame versus tiles, image/mask alignment, deterministic dimensions, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU across interpolation kernels, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing rotatepixels` plus geometry/resampling/mask/pixelpipe/GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation rotatepixels --version 1 --all-interpolations --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces matrix, dimension, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Camera-specific histories meet [0008] with exact dimensions and no hidden UI exposure, mask TODO, even-rounding drift, tile seam, or CPU/WGPU transform mismatch.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #382.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements hidden `rotatepixels` only: version-1 geometry, CPU/WGPU resampling, complete mask/raster transforms, camera activation/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement normal free rotation, crop, perspective, pixel-aspect scaling, or UI.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `scalepixels` operation for correcting non-square sensor pixels and anamorphic desqueeze. It must reproduce version-1 aspect-ratio geometry, dimensions, interpolation, point/ROI/mask transforms, and deterministic CPU/WGPU output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::scalepixels`; compatibility identity remains exactly `scalepixels`; schema version is 1; one instance.
- The persisted parameter is `pixel_aspect_ratio` with registered range `0.5..=2.0`, default `1.0`. Values below 1 stretch vertically by `1/ratio`; values above 1 stretch horizontally by `ratio`; exactly 1 disables the operation.
- Resolve exact output dimensions once: transform origin/extent with the compatibility formula, floor output origin, ceil output dimensions, and enforce a minimum one pixel with checked arithmetic.
- Build x/y scale factors from the actual resolved input/output dimensions. CPU/WGPU, ROI, point transforms, and mask resampling consume those exact factors rather than independently recomputing approximations.
- CPU scalar inverse resampling is canonical. WGPU is required for every registered image and warp-mask interpolation kernel.
- Image interpolation uses the immutable user image-resampling preference; mask/raster distortion uses the immutable warp-resampling preference and clamps resulting masks to `0..1`. Both preferences and kernel hashes are in cache identity.
- Tiles use global coordinates and exact interpolation support. Output geometry/profile/alpha policy remain explicit.
- This operation is user-visible as a technical corrective module but does not infer a ratio from camera metadata after an edit is committed. Camera defaults create explicit parameter revisions.

## Implementation

- Add exact version-1 DTO, descriptor/defaults, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, interpolation overlap/full-ROI tiling, CPU/WGPU support, point/drawn/raster mask distortion, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `ScalePixelsPlan` resolving aspect, transformed output dimensions, exact x/y scales, image/mask kernels/support, transforms, memory estimate, output metadata, and executor.
- Implement checked ROI-in/out and point forward/back mappings using the resolved dimensions/scales.
- Implement scalar and reflected WGSL image resampling plus mask resampling with checked dimensions, strides, coordinates, halos, dispatches, and mask clamping.
- Integrate camera-default application, history revisions, geometry/masks, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, and GPU fallback.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/non-positive/out-of-range aspect, invalid dimensions/strides, unknown interpolation, overflow, excessive output dimensions, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Ratio 1 is exact identity and may reuse input/cache storage.
- Tiny/one-pixel dimensions remain legal; scaling and interpolation boundaries are deterministic.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/transform/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once.
- Unknown future versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for DTO/default/range, ratios below/equal/above 1, dimension rounding, x/y factors, ROI/point transforms, every image/warp interpolation, mask clamping, identity/cache behavior, and validation.
- Fixtures include checkerboards/impulses, ratios 0.5/0.75/1/1.5/2, odd/even/one-pixel dimensions, masks/raster masks, alpha, padded strides, and arbitrary tiles.
- Differential tests against [0007] for version 1, ratio extremes, interpolation preferences, camera defaults, and [0009] images.
- Property tests prove transform round trips, ROI containment, full-frame versus tiles, image/mask alignment, deterministic dimensions, identity behavior, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU across kernels, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing scalepixels` plus geometry/resampling/mask/pixelpipe/GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation scalepixels --ratios 0.5,0.75,1,1.5,2 --all-interpolations --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces dimension, scale, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Version-1 histories meet [0008] with no ratio inversion, dimension drift, tile seam, mask mismatch, preference drift, or CPU/WGPU coordinate difference.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #383.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `scalepixels` only: version-1 aspect geometry, image/mask CPU/WGPU resampling, ROI/transforms, camera defaults/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement final output scaling, crop/rotation, lens correction, or UI beyond generated controls.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the hidden technical `finalscale` operation that resolves preview, thumbnail, image-render, and export size requests into exact final dimensions and performs the final high-quality resampling once. It must preserve darktable-compatible pipeline identity, interpolation, ROI/mask transforms, output limits, CPU/WGPU parity, and prevent accidental double scaling.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::finalscale`; compatibility identity remains exactly `finalscale`; hidden technical one-instance operation enabled only for render requests marked `ImageFinal`, export, thumbnail, or an explicit final-size target.
- Define the immutable typed `RenderSizeRequest` in this PR with these modes: original, exact width/height, fit within box, long edge, short edge, megapixel limit, print physical size plus DPI, and pipeline scale. Fill/crop is explicitly not a finalscale mode. Later export services consume this contract rather than redefining it.
- Preserve aspect ratio. Compute exact output dimensions once with checked rational arithmetic and one documented nearest rounding rule, minimum one pixel, and configured maximum pixels/bytes.
- Do not upscale unless the request explicitly enables it. The resolved `allow_upscale` value is part of plan/cache identity and receipt.
- Resampling occurs in one pass from the incoming full pipeline geometry to final output using the selected immutable interpolation kernel. No separate horizontal/vertical stage may round dimensions differently.
- CPU scalar/reference separable resampling is canonical. WGPU is required for every registered final-resampling kernel.
- `modify_roi_in/out`, point transforms, raster/drawn-mask scaling where final diagnostic masks are requested, output profile/levels metadata, cache identity, and downstream encoder dimensions consume one `FinalScalePlan`.
- Preview requests may select a lower-cost registered kernel only when that kernel is explicitly part of `RenderQuality`; export and print use the requested production kernel without substitution.
- Alpha/profile/color values are resampled under the typed buffer contract. This operation performs no color-profile conversion, sharpening, dither, crop, or encoder quantization.

## Implementation

- Add versioned `RenderSizeRequest`, `RenderQuality`, and internal descriptor contracts plus compatibility decoding for any persisted finalscale parameters listed by [0005]; ordinary users do not edit this node directly.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, scale-dependent interpolation overlap, CPU/WGPU support, mask scaling, hidden/no-history or request-derived history behavior as defined by [0005], and cache fields.
- Build `FinalScalePlan` resolving source dimensions, size policy, exact target dimensions, x/y scale, upscale flag, kernel/support, quality, output levels/format metadata, mask strategy, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement checked dimension solver for every size mode and exact print-DPI conversion.
- Implement deterministic scalar separable resampling using precomputed normalized coefficient tables and bounded row/column scratch. Add reflected WGSL resampling with the identical tables, checked resources/strides/dimensions/dispatches, and method-specific halos.
- Ensure the pixelpipe detects prior equivalent scale nodes and emits a typed configuration error rather than double scaling.
- Integrate render requests, ROI/mask transforms, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and benchmark counters.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject contradictory/zero/non-finite/overflowing size requests, unsupported kernels/layouts, target dimensions above limits, impossible DPI/physical sizes, and memory estimates above budgets before publication.
- If upscaling is disabled and every requested bound exceeds source size, return exact source dimensions and record `UpscaleSuppressed`.
- Identity dimensions are exact pass-through and may reuse storage.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/mask/cache/render frame. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once without changing dimensions or kernel.
- Unknown persisted versions remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every size mode, aspect/rational rounding, upscaling policy, one-pixel/minimum/maximum limits, print DPI, duplicate-scale detection, kernel coefficient generation/normalization, ROI/point/mask transforms, identity/cache behavior, and validation.
- Fixtures include landscape/portrait/square/odd/one-pixel/very large bounded dimensions, scale up/down/identity, exact/fit/long/short/megapixel/print requests, checkerboards/impulses/color charts, masks, alpha, and padded strides.
- Differential tests against [0007] for compatibility finalscale behavior and representative [0009] previews/size targets.
- Property tests prove aspect preservation within rounding, normalized coefficients, full-frame versus arbitrary output tiles, transform round trips, no double scaling, mask alignment, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU across kernels/quality modes, preview/thumbnail/full/image-final, cached/uncached, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing finalscale` plus focused render-request, geometry, resampling, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation finalscale --all-size-modes --all-kernels --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces request, dimension, coefficient, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Resolved dimensions are exact and pixels meet [0008] with no double scaling, aspect/rounding drift, preview kernel in production quality, mask misalignment, or CPU/WGPU coefficient difference.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #381, #382, #383, #384.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements hidden `finalscale` and the shared size-request contract only: size/request solving, CPU/WGPU resampling, ROI/mask transforms, cache/receipt integration, tests, and contract documentation. Do not implement export recipes/queues, crop/fill, encoder quantization, output profiles, sharpening, export UI, or pixel-aspect correction.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `enlargecanvas` operation for adding transparent or colored space around an image with exact darktable-compatible side sizes, relative/absolute units, anchor/position, fill, geometry, mask transforms, and parameter versions. It must change dimensions without resampling the embedded source and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::enlargecanvas`; compatibility identity remains exactly `enlargecanvas`; one instance.
- Implement every [0005] version’s left/right/top/bottom extension, units/mode, anchor/position, fill color/alpha, synchronization/ratio behavior, and defaults/presets exactly.
- Canonical persisted values remain in the versioned coordinate/unit model. `EnlargeCanvasPlan` resolves them once to exact integer side widths using checked rational arithmetic and one deterministic rounding rule.
- The embedded source is copied without resampling at exact offset `(left, top)`. New canvas pixels use the persisted fill color and alpha policy; no content-aware fill or edge extension is introduced.
- CPU scalar fill/copy is canonical. WGPU is required.
- `modify_roi_out` adds side widths; `modify_roi_in` requests only the source intersection for a requested output tile. Canvas-only tiles require no source data.
- Forward/back transforms, drawn/raster masks, crop-exposer valid/source region, guides, cache identity, and downstream geometry use one plan.
- Output profile/color interpretation matches the source working representation. Fill color is converted once into that representation through typed color contracts.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for side sizes, units/modes, anchor/link controls, fill color/alpha, historical defaults/presets, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, zero source interpolation, CPU/WGPU support, mask/raster transforms, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `EnlargeCanvasPlan` resolving exact side widths, output dimensions, source rectangle/translation, fill color/alpha, transform metadata, crop-exposer region, and executor.
- Implement checked unit conversion/dimension rounding and source-intersection ROI mapping.
- Implement scalar and reflected WGSL region fill/copy with checked rectangles, colors, strides, dimensions, dispatches, and precedence.
- Integrate masks, history revisions, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite/negative side values, invalid units/anchors, impossible dimensions, fill color/profile errors, output limits/overflow, malformed legacy parameters, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Zero extension is exact identity. One-pixel/odd extensions are legal and follow the single rounding rule.
- Canvas-only tiles never dereference source buffers. Transparent fill preserves canonical premultiplied/straight-alpha invariants.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/geometry/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once.
- Unknown future versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration/unit/anchor/link mode, side rounding, dimensions/source rectangle, ROI intersections, transforms/masks/crop-exposer, fill color/alpha conversion, identity/cache behavior, and validation.
- Fixtures include each side independently/together, absolute/relative modes, odd/even/tiny inputs, transparent/opaque/HDR fills, source/canvas-only tiles, masks/raster masks, padded strides, and alpha.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every version/mode/preset and [0009] image.
- Property tests prove source-copy identity, rectangle containment, output dimensions, transform round trips, full-frame versus arbitrary tiles, mask alignment, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, large bounded canvases, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing enlargecanvas` plus geometry/color/masks/pixelpipe/GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation enlargecanvas --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces side, dimension, rectangle, ROI, transform, fill, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with no source resampling, side-rounding drift, tile gap, alpha error, mask misalignment, or CPU/WGPU region-precedence mismatch.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, #377, and #385.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `enlargecanvas` only: migrations, canvas geometry/fill, CPU/WGPU rendering, ROI/transforms/masks/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement borders, crop, generative/content-aware fill, raster overlays, or canvas-handle UI.
