issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `ashift` operation for manual and automatic perspective correction, camera shift, rotation, aspect adjustment, cropping, and lens-shift geometry. It must preserve every darktable parameter version and structure-detection mode, compute one exact projective transform, align masks and guides, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::ashift`; compatibility identity remains exactly `ashift`; one instance.
- Implement every [0005] version’s rotation, vertical/horizontal perspective, shear, lens shift, aspect, focal/crop/sensor inputs, fit/crop mode, automatic structure mode, line controls, and defaults/presets exactly.
- Manual parameters compile into one homography/inverse plus exact output bounds. CPU/WGPU, ROI, point transforms, masks, guides, and cache identity use that same plan.
- Automatic analysis is deterministic and runs once on a bounded luminance preview at the specified scale. Implement the registered edge detector, line-segment extraction, vertical/horizontal classification, robust vanishing-point/parameter fit, outlier rejection, and confidence thresholds. Stable coordinate/order tie breaks are mandatory.
- Detected lines and resolved parameters become explicit persisted edit evidence when the user applies automatic correction. Existing edits never rerun auto analysis because the source or algorithm changes.
- CPU scalar inverse resampling is canonical. WGPU is required for all registered interpolation kernels.
- Automatic crop/fit chooses the exact largest valid output rectangle for the selected aspect/fit policy using the frozen transform. Tiles never solve geometry or line analysis independently.
- Full-image coordinates and exact interpolation halos apply. Outside-source/alpha policy is explicit.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for geometry, camera, crop/fit, automatic/line-analysis fields, historical padding, defaults/presets, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register geometry-changing ROI/transforms, analysis capability, method-dependent full-analysis, interpolation overlap, CPU/WGPU support, mask/raster distortion, line diagnostics, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `PerspectivePlan` resolving camera model inputs, homography/inverse, exact output rectangle/dimensions, interpolation support, automatic-analysis evidence/lines/confidence where applicable, outside policy, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic edge/line/fit analysis with explicit sample limits and receipt of detected line hashes and fit residuals.
- Implement checked homography solving/conditioning, bounds/crop solver, ROI mappings, point/mask/raster transforms, scalar inverse sampling, and reflected WGSL resampling.
- Integrate persisted auto results, history revisions, diagnostics, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject non-finite camera/geometry values, invalid sensor/focal inputs, degenerate/self-intersecting geometry, singular/ill-conditioned homographies, empty crop, insufficient/ambiguous auto lines, unknown modes, excessive dimensions, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Automatic analysis with insufficient confidence returns a typed result and leaves parameters unchanged; it does not apply a partial guess.
- Cancellation during analysis/solve/render publishes no partial lines, parameters, image, masks, cache, or history.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU transform once without altering interpolation/crop/geometry.
- Unknown future versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, manual parameter→homography, inverse/conditioning, bounds/crop, camera inputs, line detector/classification/fit/ties, automatic confidence, ROI/point/mask transforms, interpolation, cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include synthetic architecture grids/vanishing points, rotated/shifted/sheared images, vertical-only/horizontal-only/both/ambiguous lines, noise, extreme valid geometry, odd/tiny/large dimensions, checkerboards, masks, alpha, and seam grids.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every version/manual mode/interpolation and reference-compatible automatic fixtures.
- Property tests prove transform round trips, deterministic line analysis across threads, ROI containment, full-frame versus legal tiles, image/mask alignment, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU across kernels, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing ashift` plus analysis, geometry, resampling, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation ashift --all-versions --manual --auto --all-interpolations --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces lines, fit, matrix, dimensions, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with deterministic auto results and no per-tile analysis, homography/crop drift, mask misalignment, hidden parameter guess, or CPU/WGPU transform difference.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, and #386.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `ashift` only: all migrations, deterministic automatic analysis, projective geometry/ROI/masks, CPU/WGPU resampling, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement viewport line editing, crop UI, lens distortion, legacy clipping, or camera calibration.
issues.title: Implement perspective correction and automatic line analysis

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `lens` operation for camera/lens identification, geometric distortion, transverse chromatic aberration, vignetting, scale, crop-factor, projection conversion, and automatic correction using a pinned Lensfun-compatible data snapshot. It must preserve every darktable parameter version and manual override, implement correction in Rust, align all masks/coordinates, and produce deterministic CPU/WGPU output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::lens`; compatibility identity remains exactly `lens`; one instance.
- RustTable does not link the Lensfun runtime. Implement a bounded pure-Rust parser for the pinned Lensfun XML/data schema and Rust evaluators for every calibration/projection model required by [0005]. The snapshot version/content hash is immutable evidence.
- Implement every [0005] parameter version: camera/lens maker/model, focal length, aperture, focus distance, crop factor, scale/autoscale, target geometry/projection, modification flags, reverse mode, TCA overrides, and defaults/presets.
- Camera/lens matching is deterministic: normalized maker/model aliases, exact/manual ID first, then ordered scored candidates with stable ties. Applying a match persists the selected calibration record IDs, interpolation brackets/weights, snapshot hash, and resolved coefficients so future database updates cannot change an existing edit.
- Implement geometric models and projection transforms exactly as registered: distortion interpolation, TCA per channel, vignetting, rectilinear/fisheye/equisolid/equirectangular and any additional manifest projection. Unknown models remain blocking.
- Build one immutable inverse mapping from output coordinates into source coordinates, with per-channel TCA coordinates and vignetting gain. CPU/WGPU, ROI, point transforms, masks, and cache identity use it.
- CPU scalar inverse resampling is canonical. WGPU is required for geometry/TCA/vignetting/projection combinations and all registered interpolation kernels.
- Autoscale computes the exact smallest scale avoiding invalid borders under the selected model/projection and freezes it in the plan/receipt. Tiles never solve calibration, scale, or bounds independently.
- Drawn/raster masks are transformed with the geometric mapping; color-channel TCA does not split scalar masks. Vignetting does not affect masks.
- Output dimensions, valid region, alpha/outside policy, profile evidence, and geometry metadata are explicit.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for every version and exact manual/auto fields, flags, IDs, defaults/presets, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Add `rusttable-lens-data` under the appropriate crate with bounded XML/schema parser, canonical normalized records, indexes, snapshot manifest/hash, and generated fixtures. No product behavior lives in a build script.
- Register geometry/color-dependent ROI/transforms, interpolation overlap, full-analysis for autoscale, CPU/WGPU support, mask distortion, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `LensPlan` resolving immutable calibration provenance, interpolated distortion/TCA/vignetting coefficients, crop factor, projection transform/inverse, scale/autoscale, exact output/valid rectangles, interpolation support, mask mapping, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic matching/interpolation/model evaluation, checked coordinate transforms/Jacobians/bounds, ROI mapping, and batched point transforms.
- Implement scalar inverse per-channel resampling/vignetting and reflected WGSL kernels with checked calibration tables, projection/model branches, coordinates, strides, halos, dimensions, and dispatches.
- Integrate camera metadata/default application, manual overrides, history revisions, masks, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/oversized data, unresolved required records, ambiguous auto matches below confidence, unsupported models/projections, invalid focal/aperture/distance/crop values, singular/non-finite mappings, impossible autoscale, excessive dimensions, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- An unresolved imported lens reference remains preserved and blocking; it is not replaced with a similarly named lens or no correction.
- Manual correction values remain executable without a database match when the selected version fully specifies coefficients.
- Coordinates outside the valid source use the exact outside/alpha policy; no unchecked sampling.
- Cancellation during match/interpolation/autoscale/render publishes no partial selection/image/mask/cache/history.
- WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan once without changing calibration, scale, projection, or interpolation.
- Unknown versions/models remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, XML/data limits, maker/model normalization/matching/ties, record interpolation, each distortion/TCA/vignetting/projection model, reverse mode, autoscale/bounds, ROI/point/mask transforms, snapshot/cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include calibrated synthetic grids, RGB edge targets, flat fields for vignetting, rectilinear/fisheye/projected images, multiple camera/lens aliases, focal/aperture/distance interpolation brackets, ambiguous/missing records, odd/tiny/large dimensions, masks, alpha, and seam grids.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/model/flag/projection and representative [0009] camera/lens pairs using the same data revision where available.
- Property tests prove deterministic matching, immutable resolved coefficients across snapshot changes, transform/reverse consistency where defined, ROI containment, full-frame versus tiles, mask alignment, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU across models/kernels, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, constrained memory, multiple adapters, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing lens` plus lens-data, geometry, resampling, masks, camera-data, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation lens --all-versions --all-models --all-projections --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces data-snapshot, match, interpolation, model, scale, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with no Lensfun runtime dependency, database-update drift, ambiguous silent match, autoscale/tile seam, mask misalignment, or CPU/WGPU model difference.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #232, #233, #234, geometry/mask contracts through #360, #387, and camera metadata contracts.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `lens` only: all migrations, pinned data parser/index/matching, pure-Rust calibration models, autoscale/ROI/masks, CPU/WGPU correction, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement camera-data download UI, perspective correction, lens-profile authoring, RAW decoding, or viewport geometry controls.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `liquify` operation for applying darktable-compatible point, line, curve, radius, strength, and control-node deformations. It must preserve every parameter version and path serialization, build a deterministic inverse warp field, transform pixels and masks consistently, and execute on CPU and WGPU without tile seams or order-dependent edits.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::liquify`; compatibility identity remains exactly `liquify`; one instance.
- Implement every [0005] version’s control-node/path type, source/destination coordinates, radius, strength, hardness, status/flags, interpolation, and legacy serialization exactly. Unknown node/flag types remain opaque and blocking.
- Canonical parameters are an ordered immutable list of control primitives in full-image coordinates. Preserve order exactly because deformations compose in sequence.
- Build an inverse displacement field from output coordinates back to source coordinates. Each primitive contributes through the exact radial/falloff and Bezier/path equations; compose in the compatibility-defined order.
- CPU scalar inverse resampling is canonical. WGPU is required for displacement-field construction and all registered sampling kernels.
- The full displacement field or a mathematically equivalent bounded tile field with exact global primitive evaluation is frozen before source sampling. Corrected output never influences later displacement evaluation.
- ROI planning expands requested source bounds by the proven maximum displacement plus interpolation support. If a primitive’s influence cannot be bounded tightly, request full input rather than risk clipping.
- Forward/back point transforms, drawn/raster masks, guides, cache identity, and viewport control geometry use the same primitive evaluator/field. Raster-mask warp is required.
- Output dimensions and profile/alpha metadata remain unchanged; outside-source behavior is explicit.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations and canonical encoding for every primitive/version, coordinate/flag field, defaults, explicit byte limits, and opaque-source retention.
- Register distortion ROI/transforms, primitive-dependent overlap/full-input flags, CPU/WGPU support, mask/raster distortion, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `LiquifyPlan` resolving validated primitives, Bezier/path coefficients, falloff functions, primitive bounds, maximum displacement, displacement-field strategy/format, interpolation kernel, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic primitive evaluation/composition, inverse solving where required, bounds estimation, batched point transforms, and scalar sampling.
- Add reflected WGSL field-build/resampling kernels with checked primitive buffers, counts, path limits, field dimensions, coordinates, strides, halos, and dispatches.
- Integrate history revisions, masks, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated control schema for later UI.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/truncated/oversized primitive payloads, non-finite coordinates/radii/strengths, invalid Bezier topology, unknown flags, singular/non-convergent inverse mappings, excessive displacement/field memory, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Empty/no-strength primitive sets are exact identity.
- Equal-coordinate/tie behavior and iterative inverse tolerance/maximum iterations are fixed and recorded; non-convergence yields a typed result, not arbitrary source coordinates.
- Cancellation publishes no partial field/image/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU field/plan once.
- Unknown future versions/primitives remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration/primitive/flag, falloff, Bezier/path evaluation, order composition, bounds/max displacement, inverse convergence/ties, ROI/point/mask transforms, interpolation, cache identity, and validation limits.
- Fixtures include single/multiple point/line/curve warps, overlapping/opposed controls, borders/corners, high/low hardness, extreme valid displacement, checkerboards/faces/grids, masks/raster masks, odd/tiny dimensions, alpha, and seam grids.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/primitive combination and [0009] images.
- Property tests prove deterministic composition, transform consistency, ROI containment, full-frame versus legal tiles, image/mask alignment, identity behavior, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU across kernels/field strategies, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, masks, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing liquify` plus geometry, paths, resampling, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation liquify --all-versions --all-primitives --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces primitive, field, inverse, ROI, mask, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with no primitive-order drift, inverse instability, ROI clipping, tile seam, mask misalignment, hidden primitive reduction, or CPU/WGPU field difference.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, geometry/path/mask contracts through #360, and #388.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `liquify` only: all migrations/primitives, displacement/inverse/ROI/masks, CPU/WGPU warp, history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement viewport control gestures, generic mesh editing, crop/lens/perspective, or retouch cloning.
issues.title: Implement liquify mesh-warp operation

issues.body:
## Why

Keep ready same-repository pull requests current with main without touching drafts, forks, closed PRs, active checks, review state, or merge state.

## How

- Add a dependency-free Bun decision/adapter module with immutable snapshot rechecks, bounded credential no-ops, isolated worktree merges, and lease-protected pushes.
- Add deterministic fixtures covering every disposition, race, failure, and idempotent rerun.
- Run only the pure fixture test in pre-commit and the PR cheap validation phase.
- Add a serialized, immutable-action workflow with read-only GitHub permissions and a dedicated READY_PR_UPDATE_TOKEN for branch writes.
- Document safe completed-worker reuse within the repository orchestration guidance.

## Validation

Focused Bun tests, pre-commit, pre-push, PR CI, source/native policy, repository policy, Bun toolchain policy, and actionlint pass within the declared budgets.

Closes #362
issues.title: [0004] ci: keep ready same-repository PR branches fresh safely

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `spots` compatibility operation for legacy clone/heal spot histories. It must apply every registered spot form and source offset in exact order, preserve parameter versions and mask references, compute correct source/target ROIs through upstream geometry, and render deterministically on CPU and WGPU while remaining hidden from normal new-edit discovery in favor of retouch.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::spots`; compatibility identity remains exactly `spots`; mark it `DeprecatedCompatibility` where [0005] does.
- Implement every supported parameter version’s spot count, form/mask IDs, clone/heal mode, source offsets, opacity/hardness/feather semantics inherited from the referenced forms, and exact operation order.
- Imported spots retain their original mask/form identities and order. Loading never converts them into a `retouch` node; conversion would alter history and source sampling.
- Source coordinates are defined in the operation’s full-image coordinate system before downstream geometry. Resolve source and destination forms through canonical upstream/downstream transform snapshots.
- CPU scalar clone/heal is canonical. WGPU is required for mask evaluation, source sampling, clone compositing, and the exact registered heal solver where supported; an explicitly unsupported heal variant plans direct CPU execution rather than changing mode.
- Each spot reads from the immutable operation input. A spot’s output may or may not feed the next spot exactly according to the compatibility ordering captured by [0005]; the plan encodes and tests that order.
- ROI planning unions every destination form with its transformed source region plus interpolation/heal support. It must not request the entire image when bounded source/destination regions suffice.
- Output geometry/profile/alpha remain unchanged. Scalar masks remain aligned through all prior geometric operations.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations for every version, form IDs/count/order/modes/source offsets, historical bounds/padding, explicit serialization, and opaque-source retention.
- Register format/colorspace, source+destination ROI requirements, overlap, CPU/WGPU capability by mode, mask dependency, deprecated visibility, controls/history, and cache fields.
- Build `SpotsPlan` resolving ordered forms, evaluated destination/source geometry, transformed source offsets, per-spot masks/bounds, clone/heal algorithm, interpolation/support, ROI union, scratch/memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic form-mask evaluation, source sampling, clone compositing, and exact heal blending/solver with bounded cancellation between spots/iterations.
- Add reflected WGSL mask/clone/heal kernels for advertised modes, with checked form buffers, source/destination bounds, coordinates, strides, dispatches, and ordering.
- Integrate form/history references, deprecated registry behavior, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated controls.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed spot arrays, invalid/missing form IDs, unsupported form/mode combinations, non-finite source offsets, source regions outside valid input when the version cannot edge-fill, excessive spots/support/memory, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Missing forms remain preserved and execution-blocking; they are not dropped.
- Empty/zero-opacity forms are exact no-ops but remain represented in history.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image/mask/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU mode/ordering once.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, form/order decoding, source/destination coordinate transforms, ROI union, clone compositing, heal behavior, overlap/order, deprecated status, cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include circles/ellipses/paths, one/many overlapping spots, source before/after destination, edges/corners, geometry transforms upstream, texture/gradient/color boundaries, masks, HDR/negative values, odd/tiny images, and alpha.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every version/mode/form combination and [0009] images.
- Property tests prove ROI contains all reads, deterministic ordering, source/destination transform consistency, full-frame versus legal tiles, unchanged outside pixels where required, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU clone/heal capabilities, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, constrained memory, and forced fallback comparisons under [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing spots` plus forms, geometry, healing, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation spots --all-versions --all-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces form, ROI, ordering, solver, cache, and image receipts.
- Legacy histories meet [0008], remain hidden when deprecated, and have no dropped form, source-coordinate drift, ROI clipping, ordering change, retouch substitution, or CPU/WGPU mismatch.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, mask/geometry contracts through #360, and #389.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements legacy `spots` only: migrations, form/source ROI planning, clone/heal CPU/WGPU execution, history/cache/deprecated integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement retouch UI, convert histories to retouch, add new mask editing, or modify generic healing primitives outside reusable internal helpers.
issues.title: Implement legacy spot-removal operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `retouch` operation with every darktable-compatible clone, heal, blur, fill, wavelet-scale, mask/form, source-offset, opacity, merge, display-scale, and parameter version. It must process ordered retouch forms deterministically, support exact scale decomposition/reconstruction, bound source/destination ROIs and memory, and produce CPU/WGPU parity.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::retouch`; compatibility identity remains exactly `retouch`; one instance.
- Implement every [0005] parameter version and exact algorithm modes: clone, heal, blur, and fill; original-image and individual wavelet-scale editing; residual scale; merge-from/merge-to scales; display-wavelet-scale diagnostic; form opacity and mask semantics; blur/fill algorithm variants; source offsets; and all defaults/presets.
- Canonical parameters are an ordered immutable list of retouch entries referencing stable mask/form IDs and full-image source coordinates. Preserve order exactly.
- Build the exact à-trous/wavelet decomposition required by the selected number of scales and preview/full render scale. A form assigned to a scale acts only on that scale/residual according to the compatibility rules; reconstruction order is fixed.
- Clone samples immutable source evidence from the appropriate stage/scale. Heal uses the exact darktable healing solver/blending. Blur and fill use the selected registered algorithms and colors. No mode is approximated by another.
- CPU scalar execution is canonical. WGPU is required for wavelet decomposition/reconstruction, mask evaluation, clone, advertised heal, blur, fill, and recombination. Explicitly unsupported heal variants plan direct CPU execution without changing mode.
- ROI planning unions every destination form, transformed source form, selected-scale support, blur/heal radius, and decomposition halo. Full image is requested only when scale/merge semantics require it.
- Forms are evaluated in full-image coordinates through upstream geometry. Each entry’s read-after-write behavior follows the exact operation order captured by [0005]; the plan and tests make cascading explicit.
- Diagnostic outputs include selected wavelet scale, masks, source regions, and per-form contribution where requested. Diagnostics never alter export pixels or alpha.
- Output dimensions, profile evidence, and alpha/spare channels remain unchanged.

## Implementation

- Add complete DTOs/migrations and bounded canonical encoding for every version, entry/form reference, algorithm, source offset, scale/merge/display field, opacity, blur/fill settings, and historical padding/defaults.
- Register format/colorspace, source+destination ROI, scale-dependent overlap/full-frame requirements, memory factors, CPU/WGPU capability by mode, mask dependencies, diagnostics, controls, history, and cache fields.
- Build `RetouchPlan` resolving scale graph, ordered entries, evaluated forms/source transforms, per-entry bounds/masks/algorithms, ROI union, scratch/ping-pong buffers, merge/reconstruction order, diagnostics, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic wavelet primitives, clone sampling, exact heal solver, blur/fill variants, mask/opacity compositing, scale merging, and reconstruction with bounded pooled buffers and cancellation between scales/forms/iterations.
- Add reflected WGSL decomposition/form/reconstruction kernels with checked entry/form buffers, scale resources, source/destination bounds, workgroup halos, strides, dispatches, and exact ordering.
- Integrate forms/history references, undo/redo, cache identity, receipts, cancellation, GPU fallback, and generated control schema for later UI.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/oversized entry arrays, missing/invalid forms, unknown algorithms/scales, impossible merge ranges, non-finite offsets/opacity/settings, source bounds without a defined edge policy, excessive scales/support/memory, overflow, and unsupported layouts before publication.
- Missing forms remain preserved and blocking; they are not dropped. Empty/zero-opacity entries remain in history but are no-ops.
- Tiny images reduce scales only by the exact compatibility rule; otherwise return typed unsupported-size.
- Cancellation publishes no partial scales/image/masks/cache/history. WGPU failure retries the identical CPU plan/mode/order once and cannot reduce scales or convert heal to clone.
- Unknown versions/modes remain byte-preserved and blocking.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every migration, scale selection/decomposition/reconstruction, entry/order decoding, source/destination transforms/ROI, clone/heal/blur/fill variants, merge ranges, opacity/masks, diagnostics, memory planning, cache identity, and validation.
- Fixtures include every form type, one/many overlapping/cascading entries, source before/after destination, individual scales/residual/merge, texture/color/gradient boundaries, edges/corners, upstream geometry, HDR/negative values, odd/tiny images, alpha, and seam grids.
- Differential tests against [0007] for every supported version/mode/scale/merge and representative [0009] histories.
- Property tests prove neutral wavelet reconstruction, deterministic ordering, ROI read containment, full-frame versus legal tiles, image/mask/source alignment, unchanged outside pixels where required, alpha preservation, and cancellation cleanup.
- CPU/WGPU by algorithm/scale, preview/full/export, cached/uncached, diagnostics, constrained memory, multiple adapters, and forced fallback comparisons under method-specific [0008].

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing retouch` plus wavelet, forms, healing, geometry, masks, pixelpipe, and GPU suites pass.
- `xtask parity operation retouch --all-versions --all-algorithms --all-scale-modes --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces entry, scale, ROI, solver, mask, memory, cache, and image receipts.
- Supported histories meet [0008] with no dropped form, source/order drift, scale reconstruction error, ROI clipping, tile seam, heal→clone substitution, hidden scale reduction, or CPU/WGPU divergence.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, mask/geometry/wavelet contracts through #360, #311, #312, and #391.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `retouch` only: all migrations/algorithms/scales, source/ROI/forms, CPU/WGPU processing, diagnostics/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement brush/tablet UI, mask editor gestures, legacy spots conversion, content-aware ML fill, or unrelated wavelet filters.
issues.title: Implement multiscale retouch operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production `mask_manager` integration node and the final cross-operation mask-routing layer. Every drawn, parametric, raster, detail, operation-generated, imported, and diagnostic mask must have stable identity, versioned persistence, exact geometry, deterministic evaluation, dependency tracking, cache invalidation, and CPU/WGPU availability throughout the pixelpipe.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement the compatibility operation in `rusttable-processing::operations::mask_manager`; identity remains exactly `mask_manager`; hidden, one instance, no direct pixel effect, and no ordinary blend support.
- This issue does not create new mask shapes; it integrates the mask primitives already delivered before [0204] with all operation nodes through [0233].
- Stable mask identity is `(photo_id, edit_revision, mask_id, mask_version)`. Operation-generated raster masks additionally include producer operation instance, producer mask ID, producer parameter/input/geometry hashes, and exact ROI/scale.
- Define an immutable `MaskGraph` per pipeline snapshot. Edges are explicit producer→consumer references. Cycles are invalid and block the affected render; evaluation never uses last-frame data to break a cycle.
- Resolve mask space through the ordered geometry stack using the same operation forward/back transforms used by pixels. A mask is never transformed by independently reimplemented geometry.
- Drawn/parametric masks evaluate canonically on CPU and require WGPU kernels. Raster/detail/generated masks may remain in GPU memory between compatible consumers under bounded budgets; CPU leases remain available for CPU consumers/fallback.
- Mask values are canonical finite `f32` in `0..=1`. Combination, inversion, opacity, feather, blur, and group order use exact versioned equations. Alpha is never the durable mask store.
- Cache identity includes complete producer and geometry ancestry. A parameter, form, source image, producer operation, pipeline order, scale, ROI, or transform change invalidates exactly affected nodes.
- Full-mask publication is avoided when bounded ROI evaluation is valid. Producers declaring full-frame/global masks retain that requirement explicitly.
- Unknown imported forms/raster sources remain opaque, visible, and blocking only to consumers that reference them; unrelated operations can still render.

## Implementation

- Add exact `mask_manager` DTO/version migration/descriptor behavior from [0005], including any historical selected/manager state that belongs in edit data versus UI state.
- Define versioned `MaskGraph`, `MaskReference`, `RasterMaskDescriptor`, `MaskLease`, capability/error codes, and canonical serialization boundaries.
- Implement deterministic graph construction/topological ordering, stable tie breaks, cycle/missing-producer findings, source/sink registration, mask-name preservation, and consumer-use counts.
- Implement ROI propagation through geometry ancestry, evaluation planning, CPU/GPU resource leases, bounded caches, eviction, producer lifetime, cancellation, and single-retry fallback.
- Wire every operation descriptor through [0233] to declare produced/consumed masks and exact capabilities. Remove ad hoc mask lookups or global mutable mask state.
- Add reflected WGPU combination/feather/resample/copy paths and ensure CPU/WGPU consume identical group order and coefficients.
- Integrate catalog/history revisions, undo/redo, imported IDs/names, cache receipts, diagnostic inspection, and privacy-safe errors.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject graph cycles, duplicate IDs in one revision, invalid references, non-finite values, unsupported producer formats, transform failures, dimensions/memory above limits, overflow, and unknown combination modes before publication.
- A missing referenced mask blocks that consumer with an explicit finding; it is not replaced by all-zero/all-one.
- Cancellation releases leases and publishes no partial graph/cache/history. GPU failure retries the identical CPU mask plan once without changing combination or interpolation.
- Producer failure is isolated to dependent consumers and reported once per generation.
- Unknown versions/forms remain byte-preserved and blocking only where referenced.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for migration, graph ordering/ties/cycles, identity/versioning, producer/consumer registration, ROI/geometry ancestry, all combination/inversion/opacity modes, cache invalidation, leases/eviction, error isolation, and validation.
- Integration histories cover drawn+parametric groups, rasterfile, detail masks, operation-generated masks, masks consumed after crop/lens/perspective/liquify/borders/enlarge, producer multi-instances, missing/unknown forms, and CPU/GPU mixed pipelines.
- Property tests prove deterministic graph order, no stale cache after any ancestry mutation, mask bounds, full-frame versus legal ROI evaluation, CPU/WGPU equality, resource release, and cancellation cleanup.
- Stress tests cover maximum configured masks/forms/producers/consumers and bounded host/GPU memory.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-masks -p rusttable-pixelpipe mask_graph` plus all operation mask integration suites pass.
- `xtask parity masks --all-producers --all-geometries --cpu --wgpu --tiled` produces graph, ancestry, ROI, cache, lease, and mask receipts.
- Every registered producer/consumer declares a tested contract; no ad hoc global mask state, stale geometry, silent missing-mask fallback, cycle ambiguity, alpha-backed persistence, or CPU/WGPU combination drift remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #163, #164, #165, #166, #194, mask primitives/contracts through #360, and all operations through #392.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `mask_manager` and cross-operation mask routing only: compatibility node, immutable graph, resource/cache/geometry integration, complete operation declarations, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement mask-editor UI, new form algorithms, operation image algorithms, or general pipeline scheduling unrelated to masks.
issues.title: Integrate mask-manager operation and cross-operation raster masks

issues.body:
## What changed

- Added the `rusttable-ui` workspace crate for reusable Iced widgets, input/focus, navigation, theme, UI state, view composition, and presentation view models.
- Refactored `rusttable-app` into explicit `application`, `lifecycle`, `library`, and `composition` ownership directories while keeping catalog loading and application orchestration in the composition root.
- Added a deterministic workspace layout/DAG contract and wired it into PR, pre-commit, and pre-push validation.
- Preserved strict Rust/clippy settings, unsafe denial, native-removal checks, exact Rust/Bun pins, and validation budgets.

## Why

The prior flat app source directory obscured subsystem ownership and made dependency direction easy to regress. The new boundary keeps reusable Iced concerns below the application composition root while preserving existing behavior, boot/retry behavior, headless UI scenarios, focus order, and catalog projections.

## Validation

- Focused `rusttable-ui`/`rusttable-app` tests: 30 + 25 unit/UI tests, plus 2 CLI tests.
- Full `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked` and strict workspace clippy pass.
- `cargo fmt --check`, layout/DAG contract, native-removal/source policy, Bun/toolchain fixtures, `pr-ci.sh`, pre-commit (15s/30s), pre-push (2s/60s), and actionlint pass.
- The live repository-policy checker returned GitHub 401 in this environment; its offline fixtures pass.

Closes #361
issues.title: [0003] arch: establish subsystem-aligned Rust workspace structure

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the executable operation-completeness gate for the rewrite. Every darktable image operation, parameter version, method enum, preset, order rule, input/output format, colorspace transition, ROI behavior, mask capability, CPU path, WGPU path or explicit CPU plan, diagnostic, and compatibility status must be represented by one generated RustTable manifest and verified against the pinned reference inventory before export and UI work proceeds.

## Fixed decisions

- The authoritative operation inventory is the machine-readable artifact produced by [0005] from the pinned darktable reference commit. This issue does not add a manually maintained second list.
- Generate a canonical `operation-capabilities.json` from Rust descriptors at build/test time. Stable fields include compatibility identity, Rust implementation crate/module, descriptor/schema versions, all readable legacy versions, method/enum values, default visibility/deprecation, one/multi-instance behavior, order constraints, input/output formats/colorspaces, geometry/ROI flags, tile/full-frame behavior, masks/blending, CPU/WGPU capability by method, presets, diagnostics, asset/profile/camera dependencies, and issue sequence ID.
- Classification is exact: `Implemented`, `DeprecatedImplemented`, `IntentionallyUnsupportedBlocking`, or `ReferenceOnlyNonProduct`. Release-required image operations may not remain `IntentionallyUnsupportedBlocking` after this gate. Unknown future reference entries fail generation rather than defaulting.
- WGPU absence is legal only when the descriptor explicitly plans canonical CPU execution for a named method and the performance/UX acceptance permits it. `GPU: maybe` or runtime discovery without manifest evidence is invalid.
- Every implemented operation must have at least one registered versioned fixture, scalar CPU receipt, tile/full-frame receipt where applicable, mask/geometry receipt where applicable, and WGPU or explicit CPU-only capability receipt.
- Operation order and fences are generated from descriptors and validated as a deterministic partial order. Cycles, duplicate identities, missing dependencies, or ambiguous equal-order placement are build failures.
- Preset/default inventories are compared by stable operation/version/name/content hash. Locale strings are not identity.
- This PR is a gate/integration PR. It may fix only descriptor/manifests/tests needed to expose already implemented behavior; it cannot add missing image algorithms. Missing algorithm work creates a new numbered blocking issue and this gate remains open.

## Implementation

- Add `xtask operation-manifest` to extract the pinned reference inventory and Rust descriptors, canonicalize/sort them, emit reference/current/diff JSON, and fail on unclassified differences.
- Add compile-time/CI validation for unique operation identities, schema/version coverage, enum coverage, order graph, method capabilities, mask declarations, generated controls, presets, fixture IDs, and issue IDs.
- Add a parity dashboard data artifact containing every operation×version×method×execution-path status, latest receipt hashes, tolerance class, supported platforms/adapters, and blocking finding codes.
- Add tests that instantiate every descriptor and validate parameter decode/migrate/default/encode round trips without invoking UI.
- Add an end-to-end representative pipeline corpus covering RAW→display/export with all operation families, multi-instances, masks, geometry, cache invalidation, CPU/WGPU mixing, and cancellation.
- Wire the gate into PR validation for descriptor changes and merge/nightly validation for complete parity receipts under repository time budgets.
- Update #158 with a generated link/comment to the artifact rather than duplicating hundreds of rows in prose.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing/duplicate/unclassified operations, versions, enums, presets, order rules, fixtures, methods, or issue IDs fail with stable codes and focused diff output.
- Reference inventory parse failure or reference-commit drift is blocking; CI never regenerates against an unpinned branch.
- A platform-specific WGPU limitation must appear as method/platform capability plus explicit CPU path, not be silently omitted.
- Generated artifacts are deterministic, path-independent, locale-independent, and contain no private fixture paths or metadata.
- Cancellation/interruption of corpus runs leaves atomic receipt artifacts; partial results are marked incomplete and cannot satisfy the gate.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for canonical sorting/hashing, duplicate detection, every classification, order-cycle/ambiguity detection, version/enum/preset diffing, fixture/capability validation, and deterministic artifact generation.
- Mutation tests deliberately remove/add/rename operations, versions, enum values, presets, WGPU paths, mask flags, issue IDs, and order edges and assert the exact blocking code.
- End-to-end corpus tests cover operation-free, legacy-heavy, modern scene-referred, geometry-heavy, retouch/mask-heavy, CPU-only adapter, mixed CPU/WGPU, and cancellation pipelines.
- Repeated Linux/macOS/Windows generation must produce identical semantic JSON after normalized platform capability fields.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test --workspace operation_manifest` and `cargo xtask operation-manifest --check` pass.
- The generated reference/current diff contains zero unclassified or release-blocking operation gaps.
- Every release-required operation through [0234] has registered fixtures and execution receipts; order graph is acyclic/deterministic; no descriptor relies on undocumented runtime behavior.
- Merge validation publishes the canonical manifest/parity dashboard and fails on a deliberate mutation fixture.

## Dependencies

Depends on #161, #162, #163, #164, #165, #166, operation/descriptor infrastructure through #301, and every image operation through #393.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the generated registry, diff, order/capability/fixture validators, parity dashboard artifact, corpus gate, and CI integration only. Do not implement missing algorithms, export encoders, production UI, broad documentation, or unrelated refactors; discovered gaps become separate blocking issues.
issues.title: Close the image-operation registry and parity matrix

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the single typed contract that turns a photo/edit revision into a color-managed, sized, metadata-ready export artifact request. All encoders, destinations, CLI commands, print, gallery, and UI must consume this contract rather than constructing ad hoc pixelpipe options.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-export` with domain types in `rusttable-core`; no Iced dependency.
- `ExportRequest` is immutable and versioned. It identifies photo ID, exact edit revision, optional style/preset application, pipeline quality, final size via [0227] `RenderSizeRequest`, interpolation, output profile hash/reference, rendering intent, black-point compensation, pixel encoding, alpha policy, bit depth, dither policy, metadata policy, encoder settings, destination settings, collision policy, and priority.
- An export always snapshots catalog/edit/style/profile/asset dependencies before rendering. Later edits or preset/profile changes do not alter queued work.
- Pixel encodings are explicit: scene/display linear `f32`, integer RGB/RGBA/gray at 8/10/12/16 bits, half/float where an encoder supports it, and exact transfer/primaries/profile evidence. Encoders never infer color space from buffer type.
- Output profile transform occurs before final integer quantization. Final scale occurs exactly once through [0227]. Encoder-specific subsampling/packing follows after the canonical render.
- Metadata policy is a stable include/exclude/redact rule set, not a boolean. It covers EXIF/IPTC/XMP, GPS, faces/regions, ratings/labels/tags, history, thumbnail, ICC/CICP, software/version, and user fields.
- `ExportArtifact` contains rendered buffer lease or stream, exact dimensions/stride/encoding/profile, canonical metadata packet, content/dependency hashes, deterministic filename context, and render receipt.
- Requests and receipts are canonical encoded and hashable. Private paths/credentials are referenced by opaque IDs and excluded/redacted from diagnostics.
- Cancellation is generation-safe; no partial artifact becomes destination-visible.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `ExportRequest`, `ExportArtifact`, `PixelEncoding`, `AlphaPolicy`, `DitherPolicy`, `MetadataPolicy`, `EncoderSettings`, `DestinationSettings`, `CollisionPolicy`, validation findings, and canonical encoding.
- Implement request validation/capability negotiation against encoder and destination descriptors, returning exact supported/unsupported fields without silently changing settings.
- Implement dependency snapshotting and a deterministic request hash covering every pixel-affecting and artifact-affecting input.
- Integrate pixelpipe render planning, output profile, finalscale, dither, metadata collection, buffer pools, cancellation, and receipts.
- Add encoder/destination trait boundaries using streams/sinks and typed commit/rollback; no encoder writes directly to arbitrary destinations.
- Add privacy-safe display summaries and JSON receipt schemas used by CLI/UI.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing revisions/assets/profiles, contradictory size/encoding/alpha/settings, unsupported encoder capabilities, metadata-policy conflicts, excessive dimensions/bytes, overflow, and stale dependency snapshots before rendering.
- Capability negotiation never downgrades bit depth, alpha, profile, quality, metadata, or size silently; it returns a typed alternative list for the caller to choose.
- Cancellation/error releases leases and yields no committed artifact. Receipts distinguish render, encode, destination, and cancellation failures.
- Canonical hashes are path-, locale-, and thread-independent.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every type/field, canonical round trips/hashes, validation/capability negotiation, dependency snapshots, privacy redaction, cancellation, and limits.
- Matrix tests cover size modes, profiles/intents, bit depths, gray/RGB/RGBA/float, alpha policies, dither, metadata policies, and representative encoder/destination capability combinations.
- Integration tests render representative RAW/raster edits through output profile/finalscale into typed artifacts, including CPU/WGPU fallback and cancellation.
- Property tests prove any pixel/artifact-affecting change alters the request hash, non-affecting display text does not, and unsupported settings are never silently accepted.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export export_request` plus focused pixelpipe/color/finalscale/metadata tests pass.
- `cargo xtask export-contract --check` emits stable JSON schema and capability matrix.
- Two identical snapshots produce identical request/artifact hashes and render receipts across repeated runs; every later encoder can be implemented without adding format-specific fields to the core contract.

## Dependencies

Depends on #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #348, #376, #385, and #395.

## One-PR boundary

One PR defines and integrates the canonical export request/artifact contract only. Do not implement recipe persistence, queueing, encoders, destinations, UI, or broad documentation.
issues.title: Define the canonical export render request and artifact contract

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver durable, shareable export recipes that bind encoder, size, color, metadata, naming, destination, collision, and quality settings to the canonical export contract. Recipes must validate deterministically, migrate transactionally, remain independent of UI state, and never hide capability downgrades.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-export` and persist through `rusttable-catalog-store` redb transactions.
- `ExportRecipe` is versioned and immutable by revision. Editing creates a new revision; queued jobs retain the exact recipe revision and dependency snapshot.
- Fields are explicit: stable ID/name/description, encoder ID+settings, destination ID+settings, `RenderSizeRequest`, interpolation/quality, output profile+intent+BPC, pixel encoding/bit depth/alpha/dither, metadata policy, filename template ID/version, collision policy, post-success catalog actions, and enabled state.
- Built-in recipes are generated immutable assets with content hashes. User recipes may clone but never mutate built-ins in place.
- Capability validation uses [0236] descriptors. Unsupported combinations remain invalid with stable findings; recipe load/apply never silently changes bit depth, alpha, profile, metadata, size, or destination behavior.
- Secret values are not stored in recipes. Credential references use opaque keyring IDs; display/JSON summaries redact them.
- Import/export uses canonical JSON with schema version, stable enum IDs, content hashes, and no absolute source paths by default.

## Implementation

- Define versioned DTO/domain/store models, migrations, indexes, revisions, built-in asset generation, canonical JSON, and stable hashes.
- Implement create/clone/update/disable/delete-with-reference-check, optimistic concurrency, transactional migration, and validation services.
- Add recipe resolution into a fully validated [0236] request template, leaving photo/edit-specific values unbound until queue insertion.
- Add capability/findings APIs for later UI/CLI, including exact alternatives without automatic application.
- Add import conflict policies: create new ID, replace matching revision explicitly, or reject; name collision alone never replaces.
- Integrate catalog backup/recovery, undo where appropriate, audit events, and privacy-safe diagnostics.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/unknown schemas, duplicate IDs/revisions, invalid settings, missing encoder/destination/template/profile references, credential material embedded in JSON, oversized fields, and migration failures atomically.
- Deleting a referenced recipe is blocked or converted to disabled only through an explicit command; queued jobs remain resolvable.
- Unknown future fields are preserved in an opaque extension map when schema rules allow; unknown required semantics block execution.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every field/default/migration, canonical hash/JSON, built-ins, CRUD/revisions/concurrency, validation, redaction, import conflicts, deletion references, and limits.
- Matrix tests cover all encoder/destination capability combinations as they are added, invalid bit depth/alpha/profile/metadata pairings, and recipe resolution to [0236].
- Store integration tests cover crash/rollback, backup/recovery, concurrent edits, and queued-job references.
- Property tests prove canonical round trips, immutable revision behavior, any semantic change alters hash, and secrets never appear in persisted/exported/logged recipe data.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-catalog-store export_recipe` passes.
- `cargo xtask export-recipes --check-builtins --roundtrip fixtures/export-recipes` passes.
- Built-in/user recipes resolve to [0236] without hidden changes; queued references survive later edits/deletion attempts; malformed imports cause zero partial writes.

## Dependencies

Depends on #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221 and #396.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements export recipe domain/store/migrations/import-export/validation only. Do not implement queue execution, encoders, destinations, UI, credential acquisition, or broad documentation.
issues.title: Implement versioned export recipes

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the durable export job scheduler that snapshots requests, renders, encodes, commits destinations atomically, retries transient failures, survives process crashes, exposes deterministic progress/receipts, and respects host/GPU/I/O memory limits. Every export entry must be resumable or safely restartable without duplicate committed artifacts.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-export` with durable queue state in `rusttable-catalog-store`; Tokio orchestrates I/O/state, Rayon/pixelpipe perform CPU work, and WGPU scheduling remains inside render services.
- Job states are explicit: `Queued`, `Preparing`, `Rendering`, `Encoding`, `Committing`, `Succeeded`, `FailedRetryable`, `FailedPermanent`, `Cancelled`, and `Interrupted`. State transitions are transactional and validated.
- Queue insertion snapshots the exact [0236] request, [0237] recipe revision, photo/edit/style/profile/assets, encoder/destination descriptors, and request hash. Later catalog changes do not alter jobs.
- Use a two-phase artifact lifecycle: encode into a managed staging object, validate/finalize it, then ask the destination to commit atomically/idempotently. Destination-visible partial files/objects are prohibited.
- Every job has a stable idempotency key derived from request hash plus destination logical target. Retries reuse it; destinations must return existing-success when the same key was committed.
- Concurrency is budget-driven, not a fixed unbounded task count. Planner accounts for pixelpipe host/GPU memory, encoder buffers, staging bytes, destination uploads, and file descriptors. Interactive preview receives configured priority over background export.
- Retry policy is explicit exponential backoff with bounded attempts/jitter derived deterministically from job ID. Only typed transient errors retry. User/configuration/data errors are permanent until job revision is replaced.
- Pause/resume/cancel/retry/reorder are durable commands. Cancellation propagates through render, encode, staging, and destination and leaves no committed partial result.
- Progress is stage-weighted and monotonic per attempt; receipts include stage timing, bytes, hashes, resources, retry/fallback, and final target without secrets/private-path leakage.

## Implementation

- Define versioned queue/job/attempt/state-transition records, indexes, migrations, idempotency keys, canonical encoding, and recovery rules.
- Implement transactional enqueue/batch enqueue, dependency snapshot, scheduler, fair priority queues, resource reservations, stage orchestration, progress events, pause/resume/cancel/retry/reorder, and bounded history retention.
- Implement managed staging storage with quota, atomic manifest, checksum, cleanup, startup reconciliation, and recovery of interrupted attempts.
- Add encoder and destination streaming adapters, typed commit/rollback, timeout/cancellation, retry classification, and receipt persistence.
- On startup, convert incomplete in-process states to `Interrupted`, validate staging, and either resume a supported encode/upload checkpoint or restart from the earliest safe stage; never assume partial encoder state is valid.
- Add privacy-safe queue summaries/JSON APIs for later CLI/UI and diagnostics.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid snapshots/recipes/capabilities/targets, duplicate conflicting idempotency keys, quota/resource overflow, stale schema, and impossible transitions atomically.
- Crash at every write/rename/commit boundary must recover to either no committed artifact or one valid committed artifact.
- Destination commit ambiguity invokes idempotency lookup before retrying; it never blindly creates a duplicate.
- Cancellation after external commit but before local acknowledgement reconciles by idempotency key and records success, not deletion of a valid artifact.
- Secrets remain in keyring/provider sessions and never enter queue records, logs, or receipts.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every state transition, retry class/backoff, progress monotonicity, resource reservations, fairness, idempotency, commands, migration, redaction, and limits.
- Fault-injection tests crash/fail at each catalog transaction, staging write/fsync/rename, render, encode, commit, acknowledgement, cleanup, and startup-recovery boundary.
- Integration tests cover multiple photos/recipes/encoders/destinations, constrained host/GPU/disk resources, interactive priority, pause/resume/cancel, transient/permanent failures, and process restart.
- Property tests prove at-most-once logical destination commit, no illegal transition, no reservation leak, stable snapshots, and eventual staging cleanup.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-catalog-store export_queue` plus fault-injection suites pass.
- `cargo xtask export-queue-chaos --scenarios all` produces transition, recovery, idempotency, resource, and receipt evidence.
- Every crash boundary yields zero or one valid destination artifact, no duplicate logical commit, no leaked reservation/staging object, and a durable actionable job state.

## Dependencies

Depends on #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #396, and #397.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements durable queue/state/staging/scheduling/recovery/receipts only. Do not implement specific encoders, destinations, production UI, filename templates, credential acquisition, or release packaging.
issues.title: Implement resumable and crash-safe export queue

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the `copy` export encoder for producing a byte-exact copy of the original source file, with optional separately generated RustTable sidecar and deterministic checksums. It must bypass the pixelpipe explicitly, reject incompatible render/metadata requests, and integrate atomically with the export queue and destinations.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `copy`; implement in `rusttable-export::encoders::copy`.
- The primary artifact is a streaming byte-for-byte copy of the immutable imported source object identified by content hash. It does not decode, render, resize, color-convert, dither, recompress, rewrite metadata, or modify orientation.
- Capability validation accepts only original dimensions/encoding/profile, no pixel edits, no metadata redaction/mutation, and no alpha/bit-depth conversion. Incompatible [0236] fields return explicit findings; they are never ignored.
- A recipe may request an optional secondary `.xmp` RustTable/darktable-compatible sidecar generated from the exact queued edit revision. The sidecar is a separate named artifact with its own hash and atomic commit; the original bytes remain unchanged.
- Source bytes are read through the managed import/source abstraction, not arbitrary paths. The expected source content hash and size are snapshotted when queued and verified while streaming.
- Output extension is derived from the source’s normalized filename/format evidence and retained unless the filename template explicitly supplies a compatible extension.
- Queue staging writes primary/sidecar artifacts, fsyncs/finalizes hashes, then commits them as one logical destination bundle where supported. Partial bundle visibility is prohibited.

## Implementation

- Add encoder descriptor/settings/capabilities, canonical settings encoding, and integration with [0236]–[0238].
- Implement bounded asynchronous streaming copy with cancellation, progress, BLAKE3/SHA-256 receipt hashes, source identity verification, staging, and no full-file memory allocation.
- Implement optional sidecar generation through the compatibility metadata/history serializer with stable ordering/encoding.
- Emit artifact manifest containing source/import identity, byte count, hashes, original extension, sidecar hash, and no private absolute paths.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing/changed/truncated source, hash/size mismatch, unsupported source object, incompatible recipe fields, sidecar serialization failure, staging quota, read/write/fsync failure, and cancellation produce no committed bundle.
- Source modification is never silently copied under the old identity; the job becomes permanently failed until explicitly re-snapshotted.
- A destination lacking atomic multi-artifact bundles commits neither primary nor sidecar when sidecar is requested unless it supports the queue’s staging-manifest protocol.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for capability rejection, extension handling, settings/hash encoding, manifests, progress, cancellation, and redaction.
- Integration tests copy RAW/JPEG/TIFF/unknown supported originals of varied sizes, optional sidecars, changed/deleted/truncated sources, queue restart, destination retries, and bundle commit failures.
- Property tests prove output primary bytes/hash equal source exactly, no pixelpipe call occurs, bounded memory, and at-most-once logical commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export copy_encoder` passes.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder copy --verify-byte-exact --with-sidecar` produces source/artifact/bundle/queue receipts.
- Primary artifacts are byte-exact, incompatible settings are rejected, source drift is detected, and crash/cancel paths expose no partial bundle.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #223, #224, #225, #226, #227, #228, #229, #230, #231, #232, #233, #234, #235, #236, #237, #238, #239, #240, #241, #242, #243, #244, #245, #246, #247, #248, #249, #250, #251, #252, #253, #254, #255, #256 and #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the original-copy encoder and optional sidecar bundle only. Do not implement filename templates, disk/email destinations, other encoders, source replacement, or metadata rewriting.
issues.title: Implement original-file copy export format

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production JPEG export with exact quality, chroma subsampling, progressive/baseline, grayscale/RGB, ICC, EXIF/IPTC/XMP, orientation, density, and deterministic artifact behavior integrated with the export queue.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `jpeg`; implement the Rust control/streaming layer in `rusttable-export::encoders::jpeg` and isolate the pinned `libjpeg-turbo` ABI in `rusttable-jpeg-native`. No codec pointers or error jumps escape the adapter.
- Support 8-bit grayscale and RGB JPEG. Alpha is unsupported and rejected unless the recipe explicitly composites it against a selected background before encoding.
- Settings are explicit and versioned: quality `1..=100`, chroma subsampling `4:4:4`, `4:2:2`, or `4:2:0`, progressive boolean, optimize-Huffman boolean, restart interval, density unit/value, and metadata/thumbnail policy.
- Default is quality 90, 4:4:4, progressive on, optimized Huffman on, no restart interval. Defaults are recipe data and not silently changed by platform/library.
- Input to the adapter is canonical 8-bit display-encoded gray/RGB from [0236]. Color conversion, final scale, alpha compositing, and dithering occur before the encoder.
- Embed complete ICC profile bytes using ordered APP2 chunks, EXIF in APP1, XMP in APP1 including extended-XMP chunking where required, and IPTC in APP13 from the canonical `ExportArtifact` metadata packet defined by [0236]. Orientation is physically canonical and metadata orientation is 1.
- Encoder output need not be byte-identical across a deliberate codec-version update, but is deterministic for the pinned build, settings, input, and metadata. Codec version/hash is in the receipt.
- Stream scanlines from bounded buffers into queue staging; do not allocate a second full encoded image in memory.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement the audited native adapter: setjmp/longjmp containment, allocation/error translation, scanline streaming, grayscale/RGB, sampling factors, progressive scans, Huffman optimization, cancellation checkpoints, and deterministic options.
- Implement JPEG marker serialization from the already-built canonical metadata packet, including ICC/XMP chunking, exact marker order, and size limits. The later metadata-policy issue changes packet construction, not this encoder contract.
- Validate encoded output by reparsing headers/markers/dimensions/components before staging finalization.
- Emit receipt with input/artifact hashes, dimensions, quality/subsampling/progressive, marker manifest, codec version, bytes, timing, and no private metadata values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported bit depth/alpha/color layout, invalid settings, ICC/metadata segment limits, excessive dimensions, native allocation/codec errors, cancellation, and staging failures without committing output.
- Metadata too large for supported chunking is a typed error; it is never silently dropped unless the canonical packet already omitted it under the request policy.
- Native panic/error/longjmp is contained and cannot unwind through Rust or corrupt queue state.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/defaults/migrations, sampling mappings, marker chunking/order, alpha rejection/compositing contract, native error translation, output validation, and cancellation.
- Golden/decode tests cover gray/RGB, qualities, all subsampling, progressive/baseline, optimized/unoptimized, density, large ICC/XMP, metadata stripping, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed input contracts.
- Integration tests encode representative [0009] exports, decode independently, verify dimensions/profile/metadata/orientation, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Property/fuzz tests cover marker parser/writer, arbitrary valid dimensions/settings, bounded memory, and no native error escaping.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-jpeg-native jpeg_encoder` and adapter sanitizer/fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder jpeg --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-metadata` produces codec, marker, decode-delta, memory, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised setting decodes correctly, embeds exactly requested profile/metadata, uses bounded memory, and exposes no partial artifact on failure/cancel.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements JPEG settings, isolated codec adapter, canonical packet marker serialization, streaming encode, validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement metadata-policy collection, other encoders, destinations, filename templates, or export UI.
issues.title: Implement JPEG export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production PNG export for 8/16-bit grayscale, grayscale-alpha, RGB, and RGBA with explicit compression/filter settings, ICC/CICP/color chunks, EXIF/XMP, density, deterministic chunk order, streaming encode, and queue-safe validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `png`; implement in `rusttable-export::encoders::png` using a pinned pure-Rust `png` codec crate behind RustTable-owned settings and validation.
- Support integer gray/GA/RGB/RGBA at 8 and 16 bits. 16-bit samples are encoded network-byte-order. Floating input must be converted/quantized by [0236] before encoding.
- Settings are versioned: bit depth, alpha policy, compression level/strategy, row filter (`Adaptive`, `None`, `Sub`, `Up`, `Average`, `Paeth`), interlace boolean, density, and metadata policy. Defaults are 16-bit RGB/RGBA when requested by recipe, adaptive filter, production compression, non-interlaced.
- Embed one exact color declaration: ICC profile (`iCCP`) when requested, otherwise registered CICP/sRGB/gAMA+cHRM evidence according to [0236]. Contradictory color chunks are prohibited.
- Serialize EXIF in `eXIf`, XMP as UTF-8 iTXt with canonical keyword, text fields as bounded iTXt, density in `pHYs`, and orientation physically canonical/metadata 1.
- Chunk order is deterministic: signature, critical header/color/density/metadata chunks in canonical order, image data, end. Unknown ancillary chunks are not copied from source unless explicitly supplied by the canonical artifact policy.
- Stream rows through bounded codec buffers into queue staging; no duplicate full encoded image.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement exact sample packing for every layout/depth, alpha validation, row streaming, cancellation, codec error translation, and deterministic compression/filter configuration.
- Implement bounded chunk serialization for ICC/EXIF/XMP/text/CICP/gAMA/cHRM/pHYs and CRC validation.
- Reparse the completed staged PNG to verify signature, IHDR dimensions/depth/color type, chunk order/CRC, profile/metadata presence, and end marker before commit.
- Emit receipt with settings, chunk manifest/hashes, dimensions, codec version, bytes, timing, and memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported depth/layout/alpha, contradictory profile/color evidence, invalid settings, oversized/invalid metadata chunks, dimensions/row bytes above limits, codec/write/cancel/staging errors, and malformed canonical packets without committing output.
- Metadata categories omitted by policy remain absent; the encoder never drops supplied required chunks silently.
- 16-bit byte order and alpha association are checked before encoding; premultiplied alpha must be converted under the explicit alpha policy.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/defaults/migrations, every color type/depth, sample byte order, row filters, chunk serialization/order/CRC, color-chunk exclusivity, metadata limits, validation, and cancellation.
- Golden/decode tests cover gray/GA/RGB/RGBA, 8/16-bit, alpha edges, filters/compression/interlace, ICC/CICP/sRGB, EXIF/XMP/text/density, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests encode/decode representative [0009] outputs and verify exact integer samples where lossless, dimensions, profile/metadata/orientation, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/property tests cover chunk writer/parser, arbitrary valid rows/settings, bounded memory, deterministic output for pinned codec/settings, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export png_encoder` and PNG fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder png --matrix all --verify-lossless --verify-metadata` produces chunk, sample, decode, memory, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised layout/depth round-trips exactly, requested color/metadata evidence is unambiguous, output is deterministic for the pinned build, and failures/cancellation expose no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements PNG settings, pure-Rust streaming encode, canonical chunk serialization/validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement metadata-policy collection, other encoders, destinations, filename templates, or export UI.
issues.title: Implement PNG export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production TIFF/BigTIFF export for 8/16-bit integer and 16/32-bit floating gray/RGB/RGBA, with explicit compression, tiling/strips, ICC, EXIF/IPTC/XMP, orientation, density, deterministic tag policy, streaming staging, and queue-safe validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `tiff`; Rust orchestration lives in `rusttable-export::encoders::tiff`; isolate the pinned `libtiff` ABI in `rusttable-tiff-native` because required BigTIFF, floating samples, predictor, tile/strip, and metadata coverage exceeds the accepted pure-Rust codec surface.
- Support classic TIFF when all offsets fit 32 bits and BigTIFF when explicitly selected or required by estimated size. Automatic selection is deterministic and recorded.
- Layouts: gray/GA/RGB/RGBA; bits/sample 8/16 integer, 16 half, 32 float where compatible; planar configuration contiguous only for 1.0 unless [0005] requires separate planes.
- Compression settings: none, LZW, Deflate, Zstd when compiled in the pinned codec, and JPEG only for 8-bit RGB if explicitly requested. Horizontal predictor is allowed only for compatible integer/float compression modes.
- Storage mode is deterministic strips or tiles with explicit rows-per-strip/tile size. Defaults are Deflate+predictor, bounded strip size, classic/BigTIFF auto.
- Embed ICC, EXIF IFD, IPTC, XMP, CICP/photometric/sample metadata from [0236]. Orientation is 1 because pixels are canonical. Alpha uses exact associated/unassociated ExtraSamples according to request.
- Input is canonical typed pixels from [0236]; no hidden color conversion, scale, alpha change, or quantization.
- Native error handlers and allocation callbacks are process-safe, thread-local, bounded, and cannot call arbitrary global logging.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, validation, deterministic classic/BigTIFF size planner, canonical hash, and negotiation.
- Implement audited native adapter for memory/file callbacks into queue staging, strips/tiles, all layouts/depths, compression/predictor, cancellation, and error translation.
- Implement exact tag writer/order, metadata/profile payloads, endian/sample packing, half conversion where needed, and byte/offset limits.
- Reopen/reparse staged output with an independent Rust TIFF inspector to validate header variant, IFD chain, dimensions, samples, compression, tags, offsets/byte counts, and metadata before commit.
- Emit receipt with variant, layout/depth, compression/predictor, strip/tile layout, tag manifest, codec version, hashes, bytes, timing, and memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported layout/depth/compression combinations, alpha mismatch, invalid tile/strip sizes, offset/size overflow, metadata/tag limits, unavailable optional codec support, native errors, cancellation, and staging failure without commit.
- If classic TIFF estimate exceeds safe offsets, auto mode selects BigTIFF before writing; explicit classic mode fails rather than corrupting/truncating.
- Required metadata is never silently dropped. Native callbacks cannot unwind or longjmp across Rust.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/migrations, size/variant planner, every layout/depth, endian/half packing, compression/predictor validation, tile/strip math, tag mapping, alpha semantics, native error translation, inspector, and cancellation.
- Golden/decode tests cover classic/BigTIFF, gray/GA/RGB/RGBA, 8/16/half/float, all available compression modes, strips/tiles, ICC/EXIF/IPTC/XMP, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests encode/decode representative [0009] outputs and verify lossless integer/float tolerances, dimensions, profile/metadata/orientation, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/sanitizer/property tests cover IFD/tag parser, adapter errors, arbitrary valid dimensions/settings, offset bounds, bounded memory, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-tiff-native tiff_encoder` plus native sanitizer/fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder tiff --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-metadata` produces variant, tag, sample, compression, memory, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised combination reopens correctly, classic overflow is impossible, metadata/profile/alpha are exact, and failure/cancel exposes no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements TIFF/BigTIFF settings, isolated codec adapter, tags, streaming encode, independent validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement general metadata-policy collection, other encoders, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement TIFF and BigTIFF export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome
