Deliver simple, auditable PPM/PGM and PFM export for interoperability, testing, and floating-point pipeline inspection. The encoders must have explicit layouts/endian/scale rules, bounded streaming writes, deterministic headers, exact sample behavior, and queue integration.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder IDs are `ppm` and `pfm`; implement together in `rusttable-export::encoders::portable_anymap` because they share one direct writer and one PR-sized capability surface.
- PPM/PGM use binary P6/P5 only; support gray/RGB at 8 and 16 bits. ASCII variants and alpha are unsupported. 16-bit samples are big-endian per Netpbm.
- PFM uses `Pf` grayscale or `PF` RGB with 32-bit IEEE-754 floats. Rows are written bottom-to-top. Persist a fixed little-endian scale marker `-1.0`; convert input bytes explicitly on big-endian hosts.
- PFM values remain finite scene/display float values without clipping unless [0236] requested a prior conversion. NaN/Inf are rejected by default; an explicit diagnostic-only setting may canonicalize them under the shared non-finite policy and records counts.
- PPM input is canonical 8/16-bit integer from [0236]. PFM input is canonical `f32` gray/RGB. No profile, EXIF, XMP, alpha, density, or arbitrary comments are supported; capability negotiation rejects them rather than ignoring them.
- Headers are ASCII, locale-independent, bounded, and deterministic: magic, width, height, maxval/scale, then one newline before binary data.
- Stream rows directly into queue staging with bounded buffers and incremental hashes.

## Implementation

- Add versioned settings/descriptors/capabilities, validation, canonical hashes, and [0236] negotiation for both IDs.
- Implement checked header generation, integer/float packing, PFM row reversal, endian handling, cancellation/progress, streaming write, and artifact receipts.
- Implement independent parsers used only for staged-output validation: dimensions, maxval/scale, exact payload byte count, and finite-policy checks.
- Emit format/layout/depth/endian/scale, sample/payload hashes, bytes, timing, and memory in receipts.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject alpha, unsupported channel/depth, metadata/profile requests, invalid/non-finite PFM values without the diagnostic setting, dimensions/payload overflow, malformed artifact contracts, write/cancel/staging failures, and trailing/short payload validation errors without commit.
- Zero dimensions are invalid. PFM row order is never platform-dependent.
- Comments/user strings are not emitted, preventing ambiguous parsing and private data leakage.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for descriptors/capabilities, all headers, 8/16-bit big-endian packing, PFM little-endian floats/negative scale, bottom-up rows, finite policy, byte-count arithmetic, parser, cancellation, and limits.
- Golden round trips cover gray/RGB, 8/16 integer, positive/negative/HDR float values, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, padded input strides, and cross-endian fixtures.
- Integration tests export representative pixelpipe fixtures and verify exact PPM integers/PFM float bits, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Property/fuzz tests cover header parser, dimensions/byte counts, deterministic output, bounded memory, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export portable_anymap` and parser fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder ppm,pfm --matrix all --verify-exact` produces header, endian, sample, memory, and queue receipts.
- Advertised outputs round-trip exactly, unsupported metadata/alpha is rejected, PFM is portable across host endianness, and failures expose no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements PPM/PGM and PFM descriptors/direct streaming writers/validators/tests only. Do not implement other Netpbm formats, metadata embedding, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement PPM and PFM export encoders

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production JPEG XL export for lossless and lossy photographic output, 8/10/12/16-bit integer and 16/32-bit floating inputs where supported, grayscale/RGB/RGBA, ICC/CICP color, EXIF/XMP, animation-disabled still images, deterministic settings, streaming staging, and queue-safe validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `jpegxl`; Rust orchestration lives in `rusttable-export::encoders::jpegxl`; isolate the pinned `libjxl` encoder/thread-runner ABI in `rusttable-jxl-native`. No native pointer, callback, or error state escapes the adapter.
- Still images only for 1.0. Animation/multi-frame is explicitly unsupported.
- Support gray/RGB/RGBA, lossless and VarDCT lossy, exact bit-depth/sample-type combinations exposed by the pinned codec and verified by capability tests. Unsupported combinations are rejected rather than converted silently.
- Settings are versioned: lossless boolean, distance/quality, effort, decoding speed tier, modular/VarDCT mode where applicable, chroma subsampling, alpha policy, progressive/responsive behavior exposed by the codec, container boolean fixed on when metadata/profile is requested, and metadata policy.
- Default is visually lossless photographic mode with explicit distance/effort recorded in the built-in recipe; no opaque “quality 90” translation is persisted.
- Input pixel encoding/profile is exactly [0236]. Embed ICC or CICP color evidence without contradiction, EXIF/XMP in JXL container boxes, physical orientation 1, and alpha metadata exactly.
- Use the codec’s bounded parallel runner with thread count derived from queue resource reservations; codec threads cannot oversubscribe the application budget.
- Output is deterministic for pinned libjxl, settings, input, metadata, and runner configuration; codec version/options hash is in the receipt.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capability probe, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement audited native adapter for basic info, color encoding/ICC, frame settings, sample layouts, alpha, lossless/lossy options, bounded runner, incremental output draining, cancellation, and error translation.
- Implement canonical JXL container box ordering/limits for EXIF/XMP and color evidence.
- Reopen/decode staged output with the pinned decoder or independent inspector to validate dimensions, channels, bit depth, alpha, container boxes, color profile, and decode success before commit.
- Emit receipt with mode/distance/effort/speed, sample layout, profile/box manifest, codec version, thread budget, hashes, bytes, timing, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported sample/layout/mode/alpha/settings, contradictory color evidence, unavailable codec features, metadata/profile limits, excessive dimensions, native allocation/encode/runner errors, cancellation, and staging failures without commit.
- Lossless mode must decode to exact integer samples or the declared floating-point equivalence; any mismatch fails validation.
- Native callbacks are bounded and panic-free; no unwind/longjmp crosses Rust.
- Required metadata is not silently dropped.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/migrations/capabilities, distance/quality validation, every supported layout/depth, alpha/color/box mapping, thread-budget enforcement, native error translation, validation, and cancellation.
- Golden/decode tests cover lossless/lossy, gray/RGB/RGBA, integer/float formats, distance/effort/speed extremes, color profiles/CICP, EXIF/XMP, alpha, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] images, independently decode, verify lossless exactness or lossy perceptual metrics, metadata/profile/orientation, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/sanitizer/property tests cover box inspector, native adapter failures, arbitrary valid settings, bounded threads/memory, deterministic pinned output, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-jxl-native jpegxl_encoder` plus native sanitizer/fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder jpegxl --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-metadata` produces mode, box, decode-delta, resource, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised combination reopens correctly, lossless paths are exact, lossy paths meet declared metrics, thread budgets are honored, and failures/cancellation expose no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements JPEG XL settings, isolated codec adapter, container boxes, bounded parallel encode, validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement animation, general metadata-policy collection, other encoders, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement JPEG XL export encoder

issues.body:
## Outcome

Shift high-signal Rust failures from PR GitHub Actions into local pre-commit while preserving a bounded, parallel fast path. The developer workstation has ample CPU and memory, so pre-commit may use up to 60 seconds instead of the earlier 30-second ceiling.

## Fixed decisions

- This is sequence [0005] from master plan #158 and maps to exactly one ready-for-review, squash-merged PR.
- Raise the pre-commit hard budget from 30 seconds to 60 seconds; keep pre-push at 60 seconds and hosted PR validation at 150 seconds.
- Run independent checks in parallel with process-tree cleanup and compact failure summaries.
- Add strict Rust compilation/lint coverage to pre-commit: locked workspace check/clippy for all targets/features with warnings denied, using the repository toolchain.
- Keep deterministic layout, source/native-removal, workflow, repository, Bun, documentation, and packaging-fixture checks in the same parallel phase.
- Do not run real packaging, network-dependent GitHub mutation, GUI automation, merge-only smoke jobs, or heavyweight corpus/benchmark suites in pre-commit.
- If measured clean execution on the supported developer workstation cannot stay within 60 seconds, split the check into a bounded fast component and leave only the genuinely exhaustive portion to PR/main validation; do not silently remove coverage.
- Open the implementation PR ready for review, never draft, unless the user explicitly requests otherwise.

## Implementation

- Extend scripts/precommit-fast.sh with labeled cargo check/clippy/test tasks selected by measured cost, shared locked target/cache where safe, and robust cleanup on success, failure, interrupt, or timeout.
- Keep all independent tasks concurrent; ensure a failing task does not prevent other tasks from being reaped and summarized.
- Update AGENTS.md with the 60-second pre-commit budget, parallel high-signal policy, and ready-for-review PR default.
- Add deterministic fixture coverage for task labels, timeout behavior, failure aggregation, and no orphaned child processes.
- Update any validation-surface contract and package scripts without adding a new workflow file.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Any compiler, formatting, Clippy, source, native, layout, workflow, or deterministic fixture failure blocks the commit with a bounded actionable excerpt.
- Timeout returns nonzero, terminates/reaps the process tree, and identifies the slow task without leaving children.
- The hook must not mutate GitHub, require secrets, use the darktable reference clone, or expose private paths/tokens.
- A clean run reports measured duration and budget.

## Validation

- Focused hook/fixture tests and a real clean pre-commit run on the M5 Pro workstation.
- cargo fmt, locked metadata, strict Clippy/check tasks, workspace tests selected for the fast tier, source/native/layout/workflow policies.
- pre-commit <=60s, pre-push <=60s, PR CI <=150s.
- Verify the PR workflow still retains exhaustive tests that are not safely affordable in the 60-second local tier.

## Dependencies

- Depends on #361 and #362 landing so the layout, workflow, and PR-freshness validation surfaces are stable.
- Must preserve the one-issue/one-PR and protected-main/squash-only policies from #158.

## One-PR boundary

This PR changes only local pre-commit scheduling, budgets, high-signal Rust checks, failure aggregation, related guidance, and deterministic hook tests. It does not change product behavior, release packaging, branch protection, or the master plan. It closes only this issue.
issues.title: Shift high-signal Rust validation into the 60-second pre-commit tier

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production WebP export for lossy and lossless grayscale/RGB/RGBA output with explicit quality, alpha, near-lossless, method, threading, ICC, EXIF, XMP, deterministic RIFF chunk ordering, bounded memory, staged streaming, and queue-safe validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `webp`; Rust orchestration lives in `rusttable-export::encoders::webp`; isolate the pinned `libwebp` encoder/mux/demux ABI in `rusttable-webp-native`. Native pointers and callbacks never escape the adapter.
- Support still images only for 1.0. Animation and multi-frame output are explicitly unsupported and rejected.
- Support 8-bit gray/RGB/RGBA input from the canonical [0236] artifact. Grayscale is encoded through the exact RGB-compatible path declared by the adapter capability; alpha remains 8-bit straight alpha after explicit conversion from the artifact policy.
- Settings are versioned and explicit: `Lossy` or `Lossless`, quality `0..=100`, alpha quality `0..=100`, near-lossless `0..=100`, method `0..=6`, exact/transparent-RGB preservation, sharp YUV conversion, target size/PSNR disabled for 1.0, preprocessing/filter/sharpness/segments/partitions where exposed by the pinned codec, and thread count derived from queue reservations.
- Built-in defaults are lossy quality 90, alpha quality 100, method 4, sharp YUV enabled, exact transparent RGB enabled; lossless recipes explicitly select lossless and near-lossless 100. No platform/library default is relied upon.
- Color conversion, final scaling, alpha association conversion, integer quantization, and dither happen before the encoder. WebP’s internal RGB→YUV transform is only the codec’s compression step.
- Use the WebP RIFF container. Embed ICC (`ICCP`), EXIF (`EXIF`), and XMP (`XMP `) chunks from the canonical artifact packet. Canonical order is `VP8X`, color/profile/metadata chunks, image/alpha payload, with mux validation and no contradictory color declarations.
- Output is deterministic for the pinned libwebp build, settings, input, metadata, and thread configuration. Codec build/version/options hash is recorded.
- Because libwebp produces a complete compressed buffer, the adapter writes into a bounded codec allocation whose maximum is preflighted from dimensions and configured export limits, then streams that buffer into queue staging. It must not create an additional full encoded copy.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement audited native adapter for lossless/lossy config validation, RGB/RGBA import with row stride, bounded codec allocation, thread configuration, cancellation checkpoints before/after compression, error translation, mux assembly, and resource cleanup.
- Implement deterministic RIFF metadata/profile chunk insertion, chunk size/order limits, padding, and canonical mux flags.
- Reopen the staged artifact with the pinned demuxer plus a Rust RIFF inspector to validate signature, dimensions, alpha, image mode, chunk order/sizes, metadata/profile hashes, and decode success before commit.
- Decode validation compares lossless exports exactly and records lossy pixel/perceptual metrics using the shared image-difference engine.
- Emit receipt with mode/settings, codec build, thread budget, chunk manifest, input/artifact/decode hashes, bytes, timing, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported bit depth/layout, contradictory alpha policy, invalid settings, target-size/animation requests, metadata/profile chunks above limits, excessive dimensions or codec allocation estimates, unavailable codec features, native allocation/encode/mux errors, cancellation, and staging failures without commit.
- Required metadata is never silently dropped. If a supplied canonical packet cannot fit WebP chunk limits, capability validation or encoding fails with a typed finding.
- Fully transparent RGB is preserved only when `exact` is enabled as selected by the recipe; the receipt records this because it changes decoded hidden RGB.
- Native failures cannot unwind, longjmp, or call unbounded global logging through Rust.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/defaults/migrations, every validation combination, native configuration mapping, gray/RGB/RGBA stride import, alpha/exact handling, RIFF chunk ordering/padding/limits, mux/demux error translation, output inspector, cancellation, and memory preflight.
- Golden/decode tests cover lossless/lossy, qualities/methods, near-lossless, alpha-quality/exact transparency, gray/RGB/RGBA, ICC/EXIF/XMP, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, padded input rows, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] edits, independently decode, verify exact lossless pixels or declared lossy metrics, profile/metadata/orientation/alpha, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/sanitizer/property tests cover RIFF inspector/chunk writer, native adapter failures, arbitrary valid settings, deterministic pinned output, bounded allocation, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-webp-native webp_encoder` plus adapter sanitizer and RIFF fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder webp --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-metadata` produces settings, chunk, decode-delta, resource, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised combination reopens correctly; lossless paths are exact; lossy paths meet declared metrics; profile/metadata/alpha are present exactly; and failure/cancellation exposes no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements WebP settings, isolated codec/mux adapter, bounded encode, RIFF metadata/profile handling, independent validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement animation, metadata-policy collection, other encoders, destinations, filename templates, or export UI.
issues.title: Implement WebP export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production AVIF export for SDR and HDR still images with explicit AV1 codec, quality/quantizer, speed, chroma subsampling, bit depth, alpha, ICC/CICP, EXIF/XMP, gain-map exclusion for 1.0, bounded parallelism, queue staging, and independent post-encode validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `avif`; Rust orchestration lives in `rusttable-export::encoders::avif`; isolate the pinned `libavif` ABI and its pinned AV1 encoder backend in `rusttable-avif-native`. No native image/item/property pointers escape the adapter.
- Still images only. Sequences/animation, auxiliary depth, and HDR gain maps are explicitly unsupported in 1.0 and rejected.
- Support gray/RGB/RGBA source artifacts at 8, 10, and 12 bits where the pinned encoder backend advertises and passes capability tests. Alpha uses a separate monochrome AV1 item with exact range/bit-depth policy.
- Settings are versioned: AV1 backend fixed by the packaged build, lossless boolean, color quantizer/min/max, alpha quantizer/min/max, speed, tile rows/columns, threads from queue reservations, chroma `4:4:4`, `4:2:2`, or `4:2:0`, full/limited range, matrix coefficients, lossless RGB identity mode, and codec-specific advanced options admitted only through typed stable fields.
- Built-in defaults are 10-bit YUV444, full range, visually lossless quantizer profile, speed 6, automatic bounded tiles, and exact color metadata. Lossless uses RGB identity matrix, YUV444, full range, zero quantizers, and is enabled only when the packaged backend proves exact round trip.
- Input profile/transfer/primaries are the canonical [0236] evidence. For CICP-representable profiles, write exact `nclx`/AV1 color properties. For ICC workflows, embed ICC and still write non-contradictory AV1 signaling required for decoding. Ambiguous profile/CICP combinations are rejected.
- Convert canonical RGB into AVIF YUV using one RustTable-owned, tested matrix/range/chroma conversion shared by CPU and validation; do not rely on codec-dependent implicit conversion. Alpha remains separate and unassociated according to request.
- Serialize EXIF and XMP as AVIF metadata items with correct item references and offsets. Orientation is canonical and no transform item is emitted.
- Output is deterministic for the pinned libavif/backend build, exact settings, thread/tile configuration, input, and metadata. All versions/options hashes are recorded.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capability probe, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement audited native adapter for avif image/item creation, planar YUV/alpha submission, encoder options, bounded threads/tiles, incremental memory output handling, metadata/profile assignment, cancellation boundaries, error translation, and cleanup.
- Implement RustTable RGB↔YUV matrix/range/chroma conversion for supported bit depths with explicit rounding and edge extension; reuse it for decoded-output comparison.
- Implement ISOBMFF/AVIF box inspector sufficient to validate brands, item properties/associations, dimensions, bit depths, chroma, alpha auxiliary relation, color properties, ICC, EXIF, XMP, and item extents without trusting the encoder alone.
- Decode staged output through an independent decode path and verify dimensions, color metadata, alpha, lossless exactness or lossy metrics before commit.
- Emit receipt with backend/version, mode, quantizers/speed/tiles/threads, YUV/range/color signaling, item/box manifest, hashes, bytes, timing, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported depth/layout/chroma/range/alpha/mode, unavailable exact-lossless capability, contradictory profile/CICP evidence, invalid quantizers/speed/tiles, metadata/item limits, dimensions/allocation estimates above budgets, native encode errors, cancellation, and staging failures without commit.
- Lossless selection fails capability validation when exact RGB+alpha round trip is not available; it never silently becomes near-lossless.
- Required metadata/profile/color properties are never omitted silently. Invalid item offsets/box sizes fail final validation.
- Native callbacks and backend logging are bounded, thread-safe, privacy-safe, and panic-free.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/defaults/migrations/capability probe, every supported bit depth/chroma/range/layout, RGB↔YUV conversion/rounding/subsampling, lossless constraints, alpha auxiliary metadata, ICC/CICP rules, EXIF/XMP item layout, box inspector, native errors, cancellation, and resource preflight.
- Golden/decode tests cover SDR sRGB/P3/Rec.2020, PQ/HLG HDR where supported, gray/RGB/RGBA, 8/10/12-bit, 444/422/420, full/limited, lossless/lossy quantizers/speeds/tiles, alpha, metadata, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] edits, independently decode, verify exact lossless or declared perceptual/color metrics, alpha, profile/CICP/metadata/orientation, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/sanitizer/property tests cover ISOBMFF inspector, native adapter failures, arbitrary valid settings, bounded parallelism/memory, deterministic pinned output, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-avif-native avif_encoder` plus native sanitizer and ISOBMFF fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder avif --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-color --verify-metadata` produces backend, YUV, item/box, decode-delta, resource, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised combination reopens correctly; exact-lossless is advertised only where proven; SDR/HDR signaling is unambiguous; alpha/metadata are intact; and failures/cancellation expose no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements AVIF settings, RustTable color conversion, isolated libavif/backend adapter, metadata/item boxes, independent validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement animation, gain maps, HEIF, metadata-policy collection, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement AVIF export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production HEIF/HEIC still-image export with explicit HEVC codec settings, 8/10/12-bit support where packaged, grayscale/RGB/RGBA, chroma/range, alpha auxiliary images, ICC/CICP, EXIF/XMP, bounded parallelism, staged queue integration, and independent container/decode validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder IDs are `heif` and `heic`; both use `rusttable-export::encoders::heif` and an isolated pinned `libheif` ABI in `rusttable-heif-native`. `heic` selects HEVC-in-HEIF; `heif` is an alias only when the recipe explicitly selects the packaged HEVC encoder. No ambiguous automatic codec choice exists.
- Still images only for 1.0. Image sequences, grids, thumbnails-as-primary, depth, gain maps, and derived-image editing are unsupported and rejected.
- Support gray/RGB/RGBA source artifacts at 8/10/12 bits only where the packaged HEVC encoder and libheif capability probe pass. Alpha is a separate auxiliary monochrome image with exact bit-depth/range association.
- Settings are versioned: encoder plugin ID fixed by the packaged build, quality or lossless, speed/preset, chroma `4:4:4`, `4:2:2`, or `4:2:0`, full/limited range, threads from queue reservations, tile/slice settings exposed as typed fields, and bounded encoder-specific options admitted only after stable schema review.
- Built-in default is 10-bit YUV444 full range, quality 90 equivalent expressed as the encoder’s stable quality field, medium speed, exact color metadata. Lossless is advertised only when the packaged encoder proves exact RGB+alpha round trip under the chosen identity/color path; otherwise it is unsupported, not near-lossless.
- Canonical RGB→YUV conversion is RustTable-owned and uses the same tested conversion contract as [0246], including matrix coefficients, range, chroma siting, subsampling, and rounding. The codec never implicitly chooses a conversion.
- Embed exact ICC or CICP/nclx evidence without contradiction. Serialize EXIF and XMP through HEIF metadata items. Pixels are physically canonical; no orientation transform item is emitted.
- The primary item, alpha auxiliary relation, metadata references, and brands are deterministic. Encoder/libheif/plugin versions and options hashes are recorded.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptors/capability probe for both IDs, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Reuse the shared RGB↔YUV conversion crate/API from [0246] without adding AVIF-specific coupling.
- Implement audited libheif adapter for context/encoder/image creation, planar YUV/alpha upload, quality/lossless/options, bounded threads, metadata/profile items, memory writer callbacks into staging, cancellation boundaries, errors, and cleanup.
- Implement a bounded HEIF/ISOBMFF inspector validating brands, primary item, codec configuration, dimensions/depth/chroma, alpha association, color properties, ICC, EXIF/XMP, item extents, and references.
- Decode staged output independently and compare dimensions/color/alpha, exactness for advertised lossless, or lossy metrics before commit.
- Emit receipt with encoder plugin/version, settings, YUV/range/color data, item/box manifest, hashes, bytes, timing, threads, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unavailable codec/plugin, unsupported depth/layout/chroma/range/alpha/lossless, contradictory color evidence, invalid settings, metadata/container limits, excessive dimensions/resources, native/plugin errors, cancellation, and staging failures without commit.
- If the selected encoder plugin is absent on a packaged platform, capability discovery marks the encoder unavailable before recipe/job execution. Jobs do not switch plugins or formats silently.
- Required color/profile/metadata and alpha associations are never dropped. Invalid item references or box sizes fail validation.
- Native/plugin logs and callbacks are bounded, privacy-safe, and cannot unwind into Rust.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/migrations/capabilities, encoder-ID distinction, all supported depths/chroma/range/layouts, shared YUV conversion, alpha association, ICC/CICP rules, metadata items, box inspector, native error translation, cancellation, and preflight.
- Golden/decode tests cover gray/RGB/RGBA, 8/10/12-bit, 444/422/420, full/limited, quality/speed/lossless where available, SDR/HDR signaling, metadata, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] edits on every packaged platform/plugin combination, decode independently, verify exact or declared metrics, profile/CICP/metadata/alpha/orientation, queue restart, and commit.
- Fuzz/sanitizer/property tests cover inspector, adapter/plugin failures, arbitrary valid settings, bounded resources, deterministic pinned output, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-heif-native heif_encoder` plus native sanitizer and container fuzz jobs pass on platforms where the encoder is packaged.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder heif,heic --matrix available --verify-decode --verify-color --verify-metadata` produces capability, plugin, YUV, item, decode, resource, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised platform/combination reopens correctly; unavailable plugins are reported before execution; no format/plugin substitution, color ambiguity, alpha loss, or partial artifact occurs.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398 and the shared RGB/YUV conversion contract delivered by #407.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements HEIF/HEIC descriptors, shared-color integration, isolated libheif/HEVC adapter, metadata/container validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement AVIF, sequences, gain maps, destinations, filename templates, metadata-policy collection, or UI.
issues.title: Implement HEIF and HEIC export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production OpenEXR export for half- and 32-bit floating grayscale, RGB, and RGBA with explicit compression, channel names/order, data/display windows, chromaticities, white luminance, ICC/XMP compatibility attributes where representable, tiled/scanline storage, deterministic attribute order, bounded streaming, and queue-safe validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `openexr`; implement in `rusttable-export::encoders::openexr` using the pinned pure-Rust `exr` crate behind RustTable-owned settings, limits, metadata schema, and independent validation.
- Support single-part, single-view still images only for 1.0. Deep data, multipart output, mip/ripmap levels, stereo views, arbitrary extra channels, and animation are unsupported and rejected.
- Layouts are grayscale (`Y`), grayscale-alpha (`Y`,`A`), RGB (`R`,`G`,`B`), and RGBA (`R`,`G`,`B`,`A`). Channel order and sampling are explicit and deterministic. Alpha is straight or premultiplied only as selected by the canonical artifact policy and recorded in attributes.
- Sample types are IEEE half (`f16`) and `f32`. Integer input must be converted by [0236] before encoding. Non-finite values are allowed only when the recipe explicitly selects `PreserveNonFinite`; otherwise the export request must canonicalize or reject them before the encoder.
- Settings are versioned: sample type, compression (`None`, `RLE`, `ZIP1`, `ZIP16`, `PIZ`, `PXR24`, `B44`, `B44A` only where the crate proves support), scanline or fixed-size tiled storage, tile dimensions, line order, and metadata policy. Lossy compressors are rejected for incompatible `f32`/alpha paths rather than silently changed.
- Input is scene-linear or display-linear floating data with exact profile/primaries/white-point evidence from [0236]. Write EXR chromaticities, white luminance, adopted neutral, and CICP/custom RustTable attributes where defined. ICC bytes are stored only in a namespaced opaque attribute accepted by the schema; EXR color identity remains chromaticity/transfer evidence, not an invented ICC interpretation.
- Data and display windows are both `(0,0)..(width-1,height-1)` for 1.0. Pixel aspect ratio is 1 unless the request explicitly carries a compatible value. Orientation is physically canonical.
- Attribute names/order and encoder version attributes are deterministic and locale-independent. Private paths and unbounded user strings are not embedded.
- Encode rows/tiles through bounded buffers into queue staging; no duplicate full encoded image.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement exact channel/sample packing, `f32`→half conversion through the shared numeric contract, non-finite policy, scanline/tile iteration, compression configuration, cancellation, and error translation.
- Implement bounded EXR attribute serialization for chromaticities, color/transfer evidence, density/aspect, XMP/user comments permitted by policy, software/version, alpha association, and content hashes.
- Add an independent bounded EXR header/chunk inspector that verifies magic/version, part count, channels/types/order, windows, compression, tile/scanline layout, attributes, chunk offsets/counts, and payload bounds before commit.
- Decode staged output with the pure-Rust decoder and verify exact `f32`/half equivalence according to sample type and lossy-compression tolerance.
- Emit receipt with layout/sample/compression/storage, attribute manifest, codec version, input/artifact/decode hashes, bytes, timing, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported layouts/sample/compression/storage combinations, invalid tile sizes/windows, contradictory color evidence, oversized/invalid attributes, non-finite values contrary to policy, dimensions/offsets/bytes above limits, codec errors, cancellation, and staging failure without commit.
- Half conversion overflow/underflow follows the shared explicit policy and records bounded counts; it is never platform-dependent.
- Required attributes are never silently dropped. Unknown arbitrary EXR attributes are not copied from the source by default.
- Validation failure removes staging and marks the attempt failed before destination commit.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/defaults/migrations, every layout/sample type, half conversion including subnormal/Inf/NaN policy, compression compatibility, scanline/tile math, channel/attribute order, chromaticity mapping, inspector limits, cancellation, and validation.
- Golden/decode tests cover gray/GA/RGB/RGBA, half/f32, all supported compressors, scanline/tiled, linear SDR/HDR/negative values, non-finite policies, multiple primaries/white points, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, padded input strides, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] scene-linear edits, independently decode, verify exact/toleranced samples, channels, color attributes, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/property tests cover header/chunk inspector, arbitrary valid settings, bounded memory, deterministic attribute/order output, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export openexr_encoder` and EXR inspector fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder openexr --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-color` produces channel, attribute, sample-delta, storage, memory, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised combination reopens correctly, color/alpha evidence is explicit, half/f32 behavior matches policy, and failure/cancellation exposes no partial artifact.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements OpenEXR settings, pure-Rust encode, bounded attributes, independent validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement multipart/deep EXR, metadata-policy collection, other encoders, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement OpenEXR export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production JPEG 2000 export in raw codestream (`.j2k/.j2c`) and JP2 container form with lossless/lossy wavelet compression, 8/12/16-bit grayscale/RGB/RGBA where supported, explicit progression/resolution/code-block settings, ICC/colr metadata, bounded native execution, queue staging, and independent validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder IDs are `j2k` for raw codestream and `jp2` for JP2 container; Rust orchestration lives in `rusttable-export::encoders::jpeg2000`; isolate the pinned OpenJPEG ABI in `rusttable-openjpeg-native`.
- Support single still images only. JPX composition, animation, multiple codestreams, region-of-interest coding, cinema profiles, and arbitrary component subsampling are unsupported in 1.0.
- Support gray, RGB, and RGBA at 8, 12, and 16 unsigned integer bits when OpenJPEG capability tests pass. Alpha is encoded as a fourth component and marked through JP2 channel-definition boxes; raw codestream alpha semantics are explicit but not self-describing, so `j2k`+alpha is rejected unless the recipe opts into the documented interoperability limitation.
- Settings are versioned: lossless boolean, target rate or PSNR layers, number of quality layers, irreversible 9/7 or reversible 5/3 transform, resolution levels, code-block width/height, precinct sizes, progression order (`LRCP`, `RLCP`, `RPCL`, `PCRL`, `CPRL`), tile size, SOP/EPH markers, and thread count derived from queue reservations. Unsupported advanced options are not passed through as strings.
- Lossless requires reversible transform and zero quantization/rate settings. Lossy uses explicit layer rates/PSNR; generic quality numbers are converted before persistence into stable codec settings.
- Input is canonical integer data from [0236]. Color conversion, scale, alpha association, and quantization happen before the encoder.
- JP2 embeds ICC through `colr` method 2 or enumerated sRGB/gray only when exactly representable. Contradictory color boxes are prohibited. EXIF/XMP are not part of the 1.0 JP2 contract and capability negotiation rejects required metadata rather than inserting private boxes.
- Output is deterministic for pinned OpenJPEG, settings, input, tile/thread configuration, and profile. Version/options hash is recorded.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, both descriptors/capabilities, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement audited OpenJPEG adapter for component descriptors, sample packing, codec/container selection, tile or full-image encode, layers/resolutions/code blocks/precincts/progression, bounded threads, stream callbacks into staging, cancellation, error/warning translation, and cleanup.
- Implement JP2 box/color/channel-definition writing or verified codec configuration with deterministic order and bounded sizes.
- Build independent J2K marker and JP2 box inspectors to validate signature/brands, SIZ/COD/QCD/layers/resolutions/progression/tiles/components/depth/sign, color/alpha boxes, offsets, and end markers before commit.
- Decode staged output independently and verify exact lossless samples or declared lossy metrics.
- Emit receipt with container, transform/layers/rates, resolutions/code blocks/precincts/progression/tiles, components/depth/alpha, color boxes, codec version, hashes, bytes, timing, and memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported depth/layout/alpha/container, invalid or contradictory transform/rate/layer/resolution/tile settings, required unsupported metadata, malformed/oversized ICC, dimensions/resources above limits, native codec errors, cancellation, and staging failure without commit.
- Lossless requests never become lossy. A raw codestream request requiring self-describing ICC/alpha/metadata is rejected with an explicit suggestion to use JP2.
- Native errors/warnings are bounded and cannot unwind through Rust or expose private data.
- Required color evidence is never silently omitted.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/migrations, lossless constraints, every progression order, resolution/code-block/precinct/tile validation, component/depth/alpha packing, JP2 color/channel boxes, native error translation, inspectors, cancellation, and resource limits.
- Golden/decode tests cover J2K/JP2, gray/RGB/RGBA, 8/12/16-bit, reversible/irreversible, single/multiple layers, progressions, tiled/non-tiled, ICC/enumerated color, odd/tiny/large bounded dimensions, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] outputs, decode independently, verify exact lossless or declared lossy metrics, component/color/alpha/container metadata, queue restart, and commit.
- Fuzz/sanitizer/property tests cover marker/box inspectors, adapter failure paths, arbitrary valid settings, bounded threads/memory, deterministic pinned output, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-openjpeg-native jpeg2000_encoder` plus native sanitizer and marker/box fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder j2k,jp2 --matrix all --verify-decode --verify-color` produces coding, marker/box, sample-delta, resource, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised combination reopens correctly; lossless paths are exact; unsupported metadata/alpha combinations are rejected; and no settings substitution or partial artifact occurs.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements J2K/JP2 settings, isolated OpenJPEG adapter, color/alpha container handling, independent validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement JPX/cinema/ROI coding, metadata-policy collection, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement JPEG 2000 export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver production single-image PDF export with explicit page geometry, physical size, margins, image placement, background, raster compression, ICC output intent, XMP/document metadata, deterministic object numbering, bounded streaming, queue staging, and independent PDF validation.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `pdf`; implement in `rusttable-export::encoders::pdf` using pure-Rust `pdf-writer` plus RustTable-owned image-stream preparation. No Cairo, Ghostscript, browser print engine, or external process is used.
- One input photo produces one-page PDF in 1.0. Multi-page contact sheets belong to [0257]; print spooling belongs to [0259].
- Page size is explicit: image-sized page, ISO/US named page from a fixed locale-independent table, or custom width/height in points/millimetres/inches. Orientation, margins, bleed disabled, background color, horizontal/vertical alignment, and `Fit`, `FillCrop`, `ActualSize`, or exact physical image size are versioned settings.
- All physical geometry is resolved in PDF points (`1/72 inch`) using checked rational conversion and one deterministic sub-point rounding rule. The final raster dimensions are requested through [0227] from physical size and selected DPI; the PDF encoder does not resample independently.
- The page content contains one raster image XObject plus optional clipping rectangle for `FillCrop`. It does not vectorize the photo or apply additional sharpening/color conversion.
- Raster compression choices are `Flate` for lossless gray/RGB/RGBA-with-soft-mask and `JPEG` using the already validated [0240] JPEG payload for 8-bit gray/RGB. Selecting JPEG delegates to the JPEG encoder as an internal image-stream service with fixed settings carried in the PDF recipe; it does not enqueue a second job.
- Alpha is represented by a separate grayscale soft-mask XObject. The page background remains visible through alpha. JPEG+alpha is rejected unless the request explicitly composites alpha before PDF encoding.
- Embed the exact output ICC profile as an ICCBased color space and PDF/A-style OutputIntent metadata without claiming PDF/A conformance. When the selected profile cannot be represented under PDF ICC stream constraints, fail rather than substitute DeviceRGB.
- Embed canonical XMP document metadata, title/author/subject/keywords where policy permits, creation/modification timestamps from the deterministic export context, and no private absolute paths. EXIF/IPTC remain represented through XMP rather than arbitrary attachment streams.
- PDF version is fixed at 1.7. Object numbering, dictionary key ordering, stream compression parameters, trailer IDs derived from request/artifact hash, and timestamps are deterministic for a pinned build.
- Write objects/streams through bounded staging; image compressed streams may be produced incrementally or via bounded temporary objects, but no duplicate uncompressed full image is allocated beyond the canonical artifact lease.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, named-page table, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Implement checked page/margin/image rectangle solver for every placement mode, DPI→raster request binding, clipping, background, and transform matrix.
- Implement gray/RGB image XObjects, Flate predictors, optional JPEG stream integration, alpha soft mask, ICCBased color spaces, OutputIntent, XMP metadata, catalog/pages/page/resources/content, xref/trailer, and deterministic IDs.
- Add a bounded independent PDF parser/inspector for the produced subset: header/version, xref/trailer, object references, page count/boxes, image dimensions/filters/colorspace, alpha mask, ICC/OutputIntent, XMP, content transform/clipping, stream lengths, and EOF.
- Rasterize/decode produced PDFs in CI through a pinned test-only renderer or validate image streams plus placement mathematically; runtime must not depend on that renderer.
- Emit receipt with page/image rectangles, physical units/DPI, raster dimensions, compression, profile/object manifest, document ID, hashes, bytes, timing, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid/non-finite/negative page/margin/size/DPI values, margins eliminating the printable area, unsupported alpha/compression/profile combinations, contradictory placement requests, object/stream/byte overflow, excessive raster dimensions, malformed metadata, cancellation, and staging errors without commit.
- `ActualSize` larger than the printable region follows the explicit recipe overflow policy (`Clip` or `Reject`); it is never silently scaled.
- Required profile/XMP metadata is never silently omitted. The encoder does not label output PDF/A, PDF/X, or color-certified unless a later separately scoped implementation satisfies those standards.
- Cancellation removes staging and publishes no partial document.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/defaults/migrations, named/custom page conversion, orientation/margins/alignment, all placement modes, DPI/raster binding, clipping/background, gray/RGB/alpha XObjects, Flate/JPEG streams, ICC/OutputIntent, XMP, deterministic IDs/object order, inspector, cancellation, and limits.
- Golden tests cover image-sized/A4/Letter/custom pages, portrait/landscape, varied margins/alignment, fit/fill/actual/exact physical size, gray/RGB/RGBA, lossless/JPEG, multiple profiles, metadata policies, odd/tiny/large bounded images, and malformed contracts.
- Integration tests export representative [0009] edits, inspect/rasterize PDFs, verify one page, geometry, image/profile/alpha/metadata, queue restart, and destination commit.
- Fuzz/property tests cover inspector, page geometry, object references/stream lengths, deterministic output, bounded memory, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export pdf_encoder` and PDF inspector fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder pdf --matrix all --verify-pdf --verify-placement --verify-color` produces page, image, object, profile, raster-delta, memory, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised setting produces a valid one-page PDF with exact placement/profile/alpha/metadata, deterministic objects/IDs, no false conformance claim, and no partial artifact on failure/cancel.

## Dependencies

Depends on #385, #396, #397, #398, #399, #400, #401, #402, #403.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements single-image PDF settings, geometry, pure-Rust writer, raster/ICC/XMP embedding, independent validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement contact sheets, print spooling, PDF/A/PDF/X certification, destinations, filename templates, or export UI.
issues.title: Implement PDF image export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver a bounded pure-Rust XCF encoder for round-tripping a rendered RustTable image into GIMP as one editable raster layer with optional alpha, exact 8/16/32-bit precision, embedded color profile and metadata parasites, deterministic tile/RLE layout, queue staging, and independent validation. The scope must match darktable’s flattened XCF export rather than attempting to serialize RustTable’s non-destructive operation stack as GIMP layers.

## Fixed decisions

- Encoder ID is exactly `xcf`; implement in `rusttable-export::encoders::xcf` without linking `libxcf`, GIMP, or an external process.
- Export one top-level image layer named from the deterministic artifact basename. The layer contains the fully rendered pixelpipe result. RustTable operations, masks, history nodes, styles, and source RAW are not emitted as separate GIMP layers.
- Support RGB and RGBA at 8-bit integer, 16-bit integer, and 32-bit floating precision. Grayscale is converted to canonical RGB by [0236] because the compatibility exporter is photographic RGB. Half-float and indexed color are unsupported.
- Emit the smallest pinned XCF file-format version that supports the selected precision and profile properties according to the repository’s checked XCF specification fixture. The exact version-selection table is generated and golden-tested; no runtime GIMP detection exists.
- Use XCF’s fixed 64×64 tile hierarchy and deterministic row-major tile order. Encode tile payloads with the exact XCF RLE algorithm for integer data and the specified raw/zlib-compatible path for floating precision as required by the pinned spec. Compression choice is explicit in settings when more than one legal path exists.
- Pixel samples are canonical display/working RGB(A) from [0236]. Channel order, endian representation, precision tags, alpha association, and linear/nonlinear precision enum are explicit. Premultiplied alpha is converted before encoding.
- Embed the complete ICC profile through the standard XCF color-profile property/parasite representation accepted by current GIMP. Embed canonical XMP and selected EXIF/IPTC as named parasites only where the pinned XCF/GIMP contract preserves them. Unknown source parasites are not copied.
- Image and layer dimensions/offsets are identical, with layer offset `(0,0)`, visible/normal/100% opacity, no selection/channel/path objects, and physical orientation already canonical.
- Object/property/tile offsets are written with checked 64-bit file positions and patched deterministically. Output byte order and string/property termination follow the pinned spec exactly.
- The encoder is deterministic for the same pinned writer version, request, pixels, profile, and metadata. No timestamps beyond the canonical export context and no private source paths are embedded.

## Implementation

- Add settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, precision/version/compression table, validation, canonical hash, and [0236] negotiation.
- Define Rust-owned XCF writer primitives for header, image properties, hierarchy, level, layer, channel descriptors, tile offset tables, parasites, strings, numeric/endian packing, RLE/raw tile streams, and offset patching.
- Stream/copy source rows into one bounded 64×64 tile buffer; do not allocate a second full uncompressed image. Write tiles into queue staging while recording hashes/offsets.
- Implement bounded ICC/XMP/EXIF parasite serialization with deterministic names/order, explicit maximum sizes, and no NUL ambiguity.
- Add an independent XCF inspector/parser for the produced subset: signature/version, dimensions/base type/precision, property list, layer count/name/mode/opacity/offsets, hierarchy/levels/tiles, offset bounds, RLE decode, profile/parasite hashes, and EOF consistency.
- Decode the staged output with the independent parser and compare all pixel samples exactly for integer precision and bitwise/declared float policy for `f32` before commit.
- Emit receipt with XCF version, precision/linearity/alpha, layer/property/parasite manifest, tile count/compression, input/artifact/decode hashes, bytes, timing, and peak memory.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported layout/precision/alpha association, invalid dimensions/strides, profile/metadata above limits, strings containing invalid encoding/NUL semantics, offset/file-size overflow, unsupported XCF version features, cancellation, and staging/write/patch errors without commit.
- Floating non-finite values follow the explicit [0236] export policy; they are not silently clamped by the writer.
- Required profile/metadata is never silently dropped. If the selected GIMP/XCF precision/version cannot carry the requested evidence, capability validation fails.
- Validation failure deletes staging and reports the first stable structural finding plus bounded context.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/version selection, every precision/layout, endian/sample packing, layer/properties/parasites, string termination, 64×64 tile partitioning, RLE edge cases, offset patching/bounds, inspector, cancellation, and limits.
- Golden round trips cover RGB/RGBA at 8/16/f32, linear/nonlinear profile evidence, alpha extremes, dimensions crossing tile boundaries, odd/tiny/large bounded images, padded input strides, metadata/profile combinations, and malformed contracts.
- Compatibility tests open generated fixtures with pinned headless GIMP in CI and verify image dimensions, precision, layer count/name/mode/opacity, profile, parasites, and pixel hashes; GIMP is test-only, not a runtime dependency.
- Property/fuzz tests cover RLE/parser/offset tables, arbitrary valid dimensions/settings, deterministic output, bounded memory, and no partial commit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export xcf_encoder` plus XCF parser/RLE fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask export-fixture --encoder xcf --matrix all --verify-xcf --verify-gimp` produces version, property, tile, sample, profile, GIMP, memory, and queue receipts.
- Every advertised precision reopens with exact pixels/profile/layer structure, no libxcf/runtime GIMP dependency, no false operation-layer serialization, and no partial artifact on failure/cancel.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the flattened single-layer XCF settings, pure-Rust writer, profile/metadata parasites, independent parser/validation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement multi-layer operation history, GIMP plug-ins, metadata-policy collection, destinations, filename templates, or UI.
issues.title: Implement XCF compatibility export encoder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the single metadata-policy engine that collects, normalizes, redacts, serializes, and validates all metadata supplied to export encoders. It must produce deterministic EXIF, IPTC-IIM, XMP, CICP/color, thumbnail, and RustTable history packets from the exact queued revision, prevent accidental privacy leakage, preserve requested interoperable fields, and expose format capability findings before rendering.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-metadata` with export-facing adapters in `rusttable-export`; no encoder owns policy or reads catalog metadata directly.
- The [0236] `MetadataPolicy` is authoritative. Categories are independently configurable: camera/exposure/lens, capture date/time/timezone, title/description/creator/copyright/rights, keywords/hierarchical tags, rating/color label/pick state, GPS/location/altitude/direction, people/face regions, other regions, software/version, edit history/style, source filename/document IDs, technical dimensions/color, embedded thumbnail, and unknown imported metadata.
- Each category has exactly `Include`, `Exclude`, or `Redact` where redaction is meaningful. Redaction rules are explicit: GPS removes every EXIF/XMP/IPTC coordinate/reference/altitude/direction/location derivative; people removes names and face/region coordinates; source identity removes original path/basename/unique source document IDs; history removes operation parameters and sidecar payloads.
- Default built-in policy includes camera/exposure, capture time, descriptive/rights fields, selected tags/rating, software, dimensions/color, and profile; it excludes GPS, people/regions, source path/document IDs, unknown imported metadata, and full edit history unless a recipe explicitly enables them.
- Metadata is collected from the immutable queue snapshot, not current catalog state. Imported raw metadata, catalog edits, and generated export evidence are merged by a fixed precedence table: explicit catalog edit, recipe override, immutable imported value, generated technical value. Empty explicit edits may clear imported values.
- Normalize keys/values into a canonical intermediate `MetadataPacket`: typed integers/rationals/signed rationals/floats/date-time/timezone/text/binary/arrays/regions, stable namespace+property IDs, Unicode NFC, bounded lengths/counts, locale-independent numeric/date serialization, and explicit source/provenance.
- Orientation is always 1 because exported pixels are physically canonical. Width/height/profile/transfer/bit depth/software/export timestamp come from the final artifact and override imported technical values.
- Preserve unknown imported metadata only under an explicit `IncludeUnknownSafe` policy after bounded namespace/tag allowlist validation. Maker notes, proprietary blobs, embedded thumbnails, and unknown binary payloads are excluded by default.
- Build exact format views from one canonical packet: TIFF/EXIF IFD graph, IPTC-IIM dataset block, XMP packet including extended XMP, JPEG marker payloads, PNG eXIf/iTXt, TIFF tags, AVIF/HEIF items, JXL boxes, PDF XMP, XCF parasites, and sidecar XMP. Encoders serialize supplied views but cannot add/drop fields silently.
- ICC profile bytes and CICP/color evidence are separate typed artifact fields but are cross-validated here against metadata to prohibit contradictory profile tags.
- Thumbnail generation, when enabled and supported, uses a deterministic bounded sRGB JPEG thumbnail rendered from the same edit revision through a dedicated request and contains no metadata beyond dimensions/color.
- Full edit history export uses the explicit RustTable compatibility serializer and is never inserted into ordinary EXIF user-comment fields.
- Privacy-safe diagnostics list property IDs/categories and value hashes/lengths, never redacted values, full paths, coordinates, names, or binary payloads.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `MetadataPacket`, property/value/provenance types, policy/category/redaction enums, format-view types, stable IDs, canonical encoding/hash, and schema migrations.
- Implement the precedence/clear semantics, value normalization, Unicode/date/timezone/rational conversion, duplicate/conflict resolution, limits, allowlists, and deterministic property ordering.
- Implement category redaction as a complete namespace/tag dependency map with tests proving all correlated GPS/person/source/history fields are removed.
- Implement bounded serializers for EXIF/TIFF IFDs, IPTC-IIM, canonical/extended XMP, AVIF/HEIF metadata items, JXL boxes, PDF/XCF views, and sidecar XMP. Reuse format-specific chunk/box wrapping in encoders rather than duplicating packet logic.
- Implement capability negotiation against each encoder descriptor, returning exact unsupported required fields before render. Optional fields may be omitted only when policy marks them optional and the receipt lists the omission.
- Implement deterministic thumbnail request/build/cache keyed by edit/profile/size/policy, with cancellation and bounded resources.
- Add independent parsers for generated EXIF/IPTC/XMP subsets and a packet round-trip verifier used by encoder tests.
- Integrate queue snapshots, catalog metadata revisions, export artifact construction, receipts, audit events, and privacy-safe JSON summaries.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/oversized/non-finite values, invalid rationals/timezones/UTF-8, namespace/tag collisions, contradictory profile/orientation/dimension evidence, unsupported required format fields, policy conflicts, binary/count/recursion limits, thumbnail failure when required, and serialization overflow before encoder invocation.
- Redaction is fail-closed: an unknown field associated with an excluded sensitive category is omitted, not passed through.
- A serializer failure never falls back to dropping all metadata or embedding raw source blocks. The job fails or follows the recipe’s explicit optional-omission policy.
- Canonical ordering/hashes are independent of source map iteration, locale, platform, thread count, and absolute paths.
- Cancellation publishes no partial thumbnail, packet, queue snapshot mutation, or output artifact.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every category/policy/default, precedence/clearing, typed value normalization, Unicode, dates/timezones/rationals, duplicates/conflicts, profile/orientation overrides, canonical ordering/hash, limits, and redacted diagnostics.
- Privacy matrix verifies GPS/person/source/history exclusion across EXIF, IPTC, XMP, sidecar, PDF/XCF, thumbnail, and unknown-tag paths, including aliases and correlated fields.
- Serializer/parser round trips cover camera/lens/exposure, captions/rights/tags/ratings, GPS, regions, large XMP, extended XMP, IPTC encodings, EXIF thumbnails, ICC/CICP checks, and malformed imported metadata.
- Encoder integration tests for JPEG/PNG/TIFF/JXL/WebP/AVIF/HEIF/PDF/XCF verify exact supplied metadata presence/absence and capability errors.
- Property/fuzz tests cover EXIF/IPTC/XMP parsers/serializers, arbitrary bounded packets/policies, deterministic output, no sensitive values in logs/receipts, and cancellation cleanup.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-metadata -p rusttable-export export_metadata` plus metadata parser fuzz suites pass.
- `cargo xtask metadata-policy --all-policies --all-formats --verify-redaction --roundtrip` produces policy, property, format-view, omission, privacy, and hash receipts.
- Every encoder consumes the same canonical packet; all requested supported fields round-trip; every excluded sensitive category is absent from every generated view; contradictions and unsupported required fields fail before render; and no encoder-specific policy code remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #196, #197, #198, #199, #200, #201, #202, #203, #204, #205, #206, #207, #208, #209, #210, #211, #212, #213, #214, #215, #216, #217, #218, #219, #220, #221, #235, #236, #237, #238, #239, #240, #241, #242, #243, #244, #245, and #396, #397, #398, #399, #400, #401, #402, #403, #404, #406, #407, #408, #409, #410, #411, #412.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements canonical metadata collection/policy/redaction/format views/validation/thumbnail integration and removes encoder-owned policy logic. Do not implement metadata editor UI, filename templating, destinations, new codecs, geotagging workflows, or broad user documentation.
issues.title: Implement canonical export metadata policy and packet builder

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the single deterministic template engine used to derive export basenames, relative destination paths, gallery/contact-sheet labels, sidecar names, and bundle members from the immutable export snapshot. It must support the complete darktable-compatible variable set selected for RustTable, exact formatting and sanitization, collision planning, bounded evaluation, platform-safe paths, and privacy-aware previews without shell or environment expansion.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-core::template` with export integration in `rusttable-export`; no UI, encoder, or destination may implement a separate variable expander.
- Templates produce a relative logical path split into sanitized components plus an optional extension placeholder. Absolute paths, drive prefixes, UNC roots, `.`/`..`, NUL, control characters, path separators inside values, and platform-reserved device names are never accepted as output components.
- Supported variables are explicit stable IDs with typed values from the queued snapshot: source basename/stem/extension, source relative import path where policy permits, film/import/session, photo/image ID, virtual-copy number, edit revision, sequence/index/count, rating, color label, pick/reject, title, creator, copyright, selected metadata fields, tag list, camera/lens/exposure values, capture/export date/time parts, dimensions/aspect/orientation, profile, recipe, style, encoder/extension, destination collection/album, content/request hash prefixes, and user-defined template variables stored in the recipe.
- Do not expose process environment variables, home directory, arbitrary catalog SQL, filesystem lookups, shell commands, network values, JavaScript, Lua evaluation, or unrestricted metadata-key access.
- Syntax is a parsed expression language, not ad hoc text replacement:
  - `${variable}` inserts the default format;
  - `${variable:format}` applies a variable-specific checked formatter;
  - `${variable|fallback}` supplies a literal/variable fallback;
  - `${?variable:then:else}` is a bounded conditional;
  - `${sanitize:value}` and `${slug:value}` are explicit transforms;
  - escapes are `$$` and `\` only.
- Nesting depth, expression count, literal length, output length, component count, tag/value expansion count, and formatting width are bounded. Loops, recursion, dynamic variable names, and regular-expression execution are unsupported.
- Date/time formatting uses a fixed locale-independent subset of strftime tokens and the persisted capture/export timezone. Locale-dependent month/day names are not available in path templates.
- Numeric formatting is typed and locale-independent with explicit precision/padding/sign/base. Exposure values have stable photographic formatters. Tag lists use an explicit separator and stable sorted order.
- Sanitization is performed after expansion on each path component. Canonical normalization uses Unicode NFC, trims/collapses prohibited trailing dots/spaces, replaces illegal characters with `_`, protects Windows device names on all platforms for portable recipes, bounds UTF-8 byte length without splitting code points, and uses `_` for an empty result.
- Case sensitivity is destination capability data. Collision comparison uses the destination’s canonical case/normalization rules, not the host filesystem’s implicit behavior.
- The engine does not resolve collisions by itself. It emits a `LogicalArtifactName` and stable variable/evaluation hash; [0254] and other destinations apply the [0236] collision policy transactionally.
- Extension is encoder-owned. A literal or variable extension must match the selected encoder’s allowed extensions or validation fails; destinations never append a second extension.
- Preview/evaluation summaries redact variables excluded by metadata/privacy policy. The actual logical path may include an explicitly allowed private variable, but logs/receipts store component hashes and a redacted display path where required.

## Implementation

- Define versioned AST, variable registry/descriptors, typed values, formatters, conditional/fallback/transform nodes, parse/evaluation errors with spans, canonical AST encoding/hash, and schema migrations.
- Implement a hand-written bounded parser with deterministic diagnostics and no catastrophic backtracking.
- Implement the complete typed evaluation context from [0236]/[0237]/[0252], including stable sequence context for batch jobs and explicit missing/redacted value states.
- Implement formatters for strings, integers, rationals, dates/times/timezones, exposure/aperture/focal/ISO, dimensions/aspect, hashes, tags, and enums.
- Implement portable component sanitization, reserved-name handling, normalization, byte truncation with deterministic hash suffix where configured, and relative-path validation.
- Implement template validation at recipe save/import and final evaluation at queue insertion/commit planning. Variable capability and privacy findings are exposed without evaluating sensitive values into logs.
- Add built-in immutable templates for source stem, source stem plus virtual-copy suffix, dated folders, recipe suffix, and deterministic sequence naming; built-in content hashes are generated/tested.
- Integrate logical names into queue artifact manifests, sidecar/bundle naming, destination collision planning, receipt hashes, CLI JSON, and later UI preview APIs.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed syntax, unknown variables/formatters/transforms, invalid nesting/limits, missing required variables without fallback, disallowed sensitive variables, invalid/absolute/traversal paths, illegal extension combinations, output/component overflow, and sanitizer failure before staging or destination access.
- A redacted value is distinct from missing and cannot be exposed through fallback condition side channels; conditionals see only `Available`, `Missing`, or `Redacted` according to the explicit rule.
- Batch sequence numbers are assigned durably at queue insertion and do not change when jobs are reordered/retried.
- Sanitization collisions are handled later by destination collision policy; the engine never silently mutates one item based on another evaluation order.
- Evaluation is pure, deterministic, cancellation-aware for large batches, and performs no I/O.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for every syntax node/escape/error span, variable/default/format, conditional/fallback/redaction semantics, all typed formatters, dates/timezones, photographic values, tag sorting, AST/hash round trips, limits, and deterministic evaluation.
- Sanitization matrix covers Linux/macOS/Windows illegal characters, reserved device names, trailing dots/spaces, Unicode normalization/composed/decomposed text, emojis, long UTF-8 values, separators/traversal, empty components, case/normalization capabilities, and extension validation.
- Privacy tests prove excluded/redacted metadata cannot appear in preview/log/receipt or be inferred through conditionals/fallbacks.
- Batch integration tests cover stable sequence values across reorder/retry/restart, duplicate logical names, sidecars/bundles, recipe imports, and destination capability variants.
- Fuzz/property tests cover parser/evaluator/sanitizer, arbitrary bounded ASTs/contexts, deterministic hashes/output, no absolute/traversal result, no panic, and linear bounded runtime.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-core -p rusttable-export template_engine` plus parser/sanitizer fuzz jobs pass.
- `cargo xtask template-matrix --all-builtins --all-platforms --verify-privacy --repeat 3` produces AST, variable, sanitizer, sequence, extension, privacy, and logical-name receipts.
- Identical snapshots/templates produce identical logical names across platforms; every result is a safe relative portable path; sequence/collision inputs are stable; and no encoder/destination retains a separate filename variable implementation.

## Dependencies

Depends on [0236]–[0238] and [0252].

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the parsed variable registry/template AST/evaluator/formatters/sanitizer/built-ins and export integration only. Do not implement destination collision writes, recipe/editor UI, arbitrary scripting, metadata editing, or broad user documentation.
issues.title: Implement deterministic export filename and path template engine

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the production local-disk destination for committing one or more staged export artifacts to a user-selected root with deterministic relative names, explicit collision policy, filesystem capability checks, symlink/reparse-point safety, atomic replacement, sidecar/bundle semantics, durable receipts, and crash recovery.

## Fixed decisions

- Destination ID is exactly `disk`; implement in `rusttable-export::destinations::disk` with small platform adapters only for filesystem identity and atomic primitives not exposed portably by `std`.
- A recipe stores a destination-root reference, not arbitrary absolute paths in each filename. The root is resolved at queue insertion to a canonical directory identity plus display path, and the [0253] engine supplies only safe relative logical paths.
- Root traversal uses directory handles/capability-style operations wherever the platform supports them. Every path component is opened/created without following symlinks or reparse points by default. An explicit `AllowExistingSymlinkRoot` option may allow the selected root itself after resolving its stable identity; child symlinks remain prohibited.
- Collision policies are exact:
  - `Fail` leaves existing targets untouched;
  - `SkipIfSame` compares the RustTable artifact hash stored in a side manifest/xattr when available, otherwise hashes the existing file within limits;
  - `ReplaceAtomically` writes and fsyncs a sibling temporary file then atomically replaces the target;
  - `UniqueSuffix` chooses the lowest available deterministic suffix `-01`, `-02`, … from a transactionally reserved name set;
  - `VersionRevision` uses the queued stable revision/sequence formatter and fails if still colliding.
- Collision comparison uses a probed destination capability: case sensitivity, Unicode normalization, maximum component/path bytes, reserved names, hard-link behavior, atomic rename/replace support, and fsync semantics. These capabilities are snapshotted into the job.
- Multi-artifact bundles such as original+sidecar or gallery assets commit through a hidden staging directory and manifest under the destination root. Either the complete manifest is promoted or no final bundle members are visible. For filesystems lacking directory-level atomic promotion, use per-file atomic commits plus a final manifest marker; consumers treat the bundle valid only when that marker exists.
- Destination-visible files contain exactly staged bytes. The destination performs no encoding, metadata rewrite, extension change, timestamp inheritance, or permission guessing.
- File permissions are explicit: regular files use a portable user-readable default derived from root policy; executability is never set. Directory creation uses an explicit policy. Existing ACLs on replaced files are preserved only where the selected replacement policy/platform contract guarantees it and is receipt-visible.
- Modification time defaults to export commit time from the queue context. An explicit recipe option may set capture time; it never changes file content hash.
- Idempotency key and artifact hash are written to a bounded sidecar commit manifest in RustTable’s hidden metadata directory, not into user image metadata. Retries query it before writing.

## Implementation

- Add destination settings DTO/migrations, descriptor/capabilities, root-reference model, collision policy validation, canonical hash, and [0238] destination interface.
- Implement platform capability probing and stable root identity for Linux, macOS, and Windows; normalize results into one typed snapshot.
- Implement secure relative component traversal/creation, sibling staging files, write/copy from queue staging, byte-count/hash verification, fsync of file and containing directories where supported, atomic rename/replace, cleanup, and idempotency lookup.
- Implement deterministic batch name reservation for `UniqueSuffix` under a root-scoped lock/transaction so concurrent queue workers cannot select the same name.
- Implement bundle staging/manifests/promotion/reconciliation and startup cleanup of abandoned temporary files/directories owned by RustTable.
- Store commit receipt with logical/redacted display target, root identity hash, capabilities, collision decision, bytes/hashes, timestamps, atomicity level, idempotency result, and no secret/private full path in general logs.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject invalid/missing/unwritable roots, traversal/absolute paths, symlink/reparse child components, capability/path-length/reserved-name violations, collisions contrary to policy, insufficient free-space preflight where available, hash mismatch, permission/ACL errors, non-atomic replacement when atomicity is required, cancellation, and fsync/rename failures without reporting success.
- Existing unrelated files are never deleted or truncated before the staged artifact is complete and verified.
- Crash recovery reconciles RustTable-owned staging/manifests by idempotency key. It never removes files without a matching RustTable ownership marker.
- Cancellation before final promotion removes owned staging. Cancellation after an externally successful atomic rename reconciles as success.
- Network-mounted or unusual filesystems expose reduced capabilities; recipes requiring unavailable atomic bundle/replace behavior fail validation rather than degrade silently.

## Test matrix

- Unit tests for settings/migrations, every collision policy, capability normalization, component/path validation, suffix reservation, idempotency, permissions/timestamps, manifest schemas, redaction, and failure classification.
- Platform integration tests cover case-sensitive/insensitive and normalization variants, existing files/dirs, symlinks/reparse points, read-only/permission errors, long/reserved names, concurrent jobs, replacements, bundles, sidecars, and capture/export times.
- Fault-injection tests crash/fail at each open/create/write/fsync/hash/rename/manifest/promotion/ack/cleanup boundary and verify zero or one valid committed logical artifact/bundle.
- Property tests prove root containment, no followed child symlink, no unrelated-file deletion, deterministic suffix allocation for a fixed reservation order, hash equality, idempotent retry, and cleanup of owned staging only.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export disk_destination` plus platform/fault-injection suites pass on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
- `cargo xtask destination-chaos --destination disk --all-policies --all-faults` produces capability, path, collision, atomicity, idempotency, recovery, and hash receipts.
- Every successful commit contains exact staged bytes; retries produce no duplicate logical target; traversal/symlink attacks fail closed; crash boundaries yield zero or one valid artifact/bundle; and no unrelated file is modified.

## Dependencies

Depends on #398 and #414.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the local-filesystem destination, platform capability adapters, secure atomic/bundle commit, collision/idempotency/recovery, tests, and receipts. Do not implement filename templating, encoders, removable-media sync, cloud storage, production UI, or file-browser selection.
issues.title: Implement atomic local-filesystem export destination

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome
