issues.body:
## Why

RustTable's differential oracle was comparing canonical linear buffers as if they were encoded sRGB, conflating alpha with RGB, assuming sRGB Lab matrices, using finite-neighbor heuristics, retaining unbounded reports, and emitting malformed artifacts. That could produce false parity passes or nondeterministic evidence at the exact boundary used by darktable/reference, CPU/WGPU, tiled/full-frame, and serialization comparisons.

## Implementation

- Add explicit canonical profile identifiers, typed input layout/transfer/alpha metadata, normalization, deterministic zero-alpha handling, and explicit profile conversion.
- Keep comparison fail-closed for mismatched profiles/noncanonical buffers and define finite, infinity, NaN, alpha-weighting, RMSE, and optional-peak PSNR behavior.
- Derive D50 Lab/CIEDE2000 from the selected canonical profile matrices and white point.
- Implement symmetric bounded neighborhood matching and a fixed-capacity deterministic worst-32 outlier report.
- Replace ad hoc heatmap/blink bytes with validated versioned artifacts and migrate receipts to schema v2 with fail-closed decoding.
- Add regression coverage for alpha/premultiplication, profiles, transfer functions, CIEDE2000 known-answer data, neighborhoods, non-finite values, PSNR, bounded reports, artifacts, and receipt migration.

## Validation

- `cargo clippy -p rusttable-testkit --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p rusttable-testkit --all-targets --all-features --no-fail-fast`
- `./scripts/prepush-fast.sh` and push-triggered pre-push hook: all assigned checks passed within the 150-second budget.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `git diff --check`

## Out of scope

No image operations, WGPU kernels, codec work, Rust 1.98 beta policy changes, iced 0.15 policy changes, or unrelated workflows are modified. Depends on #446, which is still open and has no merged governance PR; this branch is prepared against current `main`.

Closes #447
issues.title: Correct the differential image oracle for alpha, profiles, neighborhoods, and bounded reports

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver a resumable Piwigo destination that uploads completed export artifacts into an existing or newly created album, applies supported metadata and tags, reconciles retries without duplicate photos, and exposes bounded progress and receipts through the canonical export queue.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-export::destination::piwigo` using async `reqwest` with rustls. Do not link OpenSSL or embed a browser/WebView.
- Support HTTPS endpoints by default. Plain HTTP is rejected unless the user explicitly enables a per-endpoint insecure-local-network setting; that exception is receipt-visible. Certificate verification is never globally disabled. Custom private CAs are imported through an explicit trust-store path.
- Store only endpoint URL, server identity, selected album, and non-secret preferences in recipes. Username/password or application credentials live in #508 and are resolved through a scoped zeroizing lease only when the job starts. Recipes/catalog/logs/receipts contain only the opaque `CredentialRef`.
- Authenticate through the server’s Piwigo Web API session endpoint and retain cookies only in the in-memory destination session. Logout and zeroize secret-bearing request state when the session ends.
- Probe the server API method inventory and version before queue insertion. The accepted modern path uses chunk-capable asynchronous upload when advertised; otherwise use the bounded simple upload method only when the complete artifact fits the server-advertised/request-policy limit. Missing required API methods is a permanent capability failure, not an implicit protocol guess.
- Album identity is the immutable server category ID. Recipes may select an existing ID or request creation of one named child under an existing parent. Album creation occurs once under an idempotency key before artifact upload.
- Upload exactly the encoded artifact from #396/#398. The destination does not resize, recompress, rewrite metadata, or change the extension.
- Map title, description, author, capture date, rating, and allowed tags only where the probed server API supports them. Unsupported requested fields fail recipe validation or are explicitly disabled before queue insertion; they are never silently dropped after rendering.
- Idempotency uses the queue job/artifact hash plus destination endpoint and album ID. Persist the returned Piwigo image ID and upload transaction identity before acknowledging success. On ambiguous failure, reconcile through the server’s checksum/duplicate response and the local durable destination ledger before retrying.
- Retry only transport errors, HTTP 408/425/429/5xx, expired sessions, and explicitly transient API errors. Authentication failure, authorization failure, invalid album, rejected format, quota exhaustion reported as permanent, and unsupported API are permanent until the job is edited.
- Bound concurrent uploads per endpoint, chunk size, request/response bytes, redirects, total retry time, and server-provided error text. Redirects may not change origin unless explicitly approved in endpoint settings.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `PiwigoEndpoint`, capability snapshot, album selector/create request, metadata mapping, upload plan, durable destination ledger entry, progress event, and redacted receipt.
- Implement endpoint normalization, same-origin redirect policy, DNS/private-address policy, TLS/trust handling, API probing, #508 credential leasing, authentication, album list/search/create, tag resolution/create where permitted, upload, metadata application, and reconciliation.
- Stream artifact bytes from the staged export file; never buffer the complete image solely for HTTP upload. For chunked upload, hash each chunk and retain resumable chunk state in the queue ledger.
- Integrate with #397 recipe validation and #398 queue state transitions. Destination commit begins only after the staged artifact hash matches the queued snapshot.
- Add a deterministic fake Piwigo server implementing login, method discovery, album operations, chunk/simple upload, duplicate handling, metadata, tags, session expiry, rate limiting, redirects, partial responses, and ambiguous commit cases.
- Add source/privacy tests proving no Piwigo password/token/cookie type is serializable or persisted and no network call bypasses the approved HTTP/credential services.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject credentials in URLs, unsupported schemes, fragments, path traversal, cross-origin redirects, oversized headers/bodies, malformed JSON, duplicate/unknown response fields where ambiguity matters, invalid IDs, changed server identity, missing/locked/denied #508 credentials, and secret-bearing recipe fields.
- Cancellation stops new chunks and records resumable state. It does not delete a server photo whose commit may have succeeded; reconciliation determines the final state.
- A server session expiry during upload reauthenticates once and resumes/reconciles without restarting committed chunks.
- Logs and receipts contain endpoint alias/host hash, credential-reference alias, album ID, artifact hash, server image ID, timings, retries, and status; they never contain passwords, session cookies, tokens, private metadata values, full local paths, or full server error pages.
- Vault unavailability or user-denied access is a typed blocked-authentication result and cannot trigger plaintext/environment fallback.

## Test matrix

- Capability probe for modern chunk upload, bounded simple fallback, missing method, version mismatch, and changed server identity.
- Credential create/lease/missing/locked/denied/ephemeral cases, login success/failure, expired session, custom CA, rejected plaintext HTTP, redirect policy, rate limit, 5xx, timeout, truncated/malformed response, and oversized diagnostics.
- Existing album, deterministic child creation, creation race, missing/forbidden album, tag mapping, unsupported metadata field, and exact artifact byte transfer.
- Chunk interruption/resume, cancellation, process restart, ambiguous final response, duplicate checksum, idempotent retry, and two jobs with distinct hashes.
- Privacy tests prove credentials, cookies, metadata values, paths, environments, process arguments, and response bodies are redacted.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export piwigo_destination` passes against the deterministic fake server and #508 fake vault.
- An opt-in merge integration test uploads two fixtures to a disposable supported Piwigo instance, interrupts one upload, resumes it, and proves exactly one server image per artifact hash.
- Queue receipts preserve server IDs and retry evidence while staged bytes remain unchanged and all durable settings contain only opaque credential references.

## Dependencies

Depends on #396, #397, #398, #414, #450, and #508.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the Piwigo destination protocol, credential/session boundary, album/tag/metadata mapping, resumable upload ledger, fake server, and tests. Do not implement Piwigo UI, general WebDAV/S3 destinations, server administration, image encoding, or a credential manager.
issues.title: Implement the authenticated Piwigo export destination

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the non-interactive print service that lays out one or more rendered photos in physical page coordinates, produces a validated color-managed spool artifact, submits it through the supported operating-system print backend, and reports exact job identity and failure state without placing print logic in the UI.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement print planning/rendering in `rusttable-export::print`; platform submission lives behind `rusttable-print-native` adapters owned by the application service layer.
- The durable print request contains printer capability snapshot, paper size, printable area, orientation, margins, cell/page layout, copies, DPI, rendering intent, black-point compensation, profile policy, scaling/crop policy, captions, and source export snapshot. UI state is never part of the request.
- Page geometry uses integer micrometres and checked rational conversions. Device pixels are derived only at the final selected DPI; floating-point page accumulation is prohibited.
- Support two explicit color policies:
  - `ApplicationManaged`: RustTable converts to the selected printer/output ICC and requests driver color correction off where the backend can prove that setting.
  - `PrinterManaged`: RustTable supplies an embedded-profile RGB document and leaves conversion to the driver.
  If the backend cannot prove the requested policy, submission is rejected rather than risking double color management.
- Generate a deterministic PDF spool document using the validated PDF writer from #411, with one image placement per page/cell, embedded ICC/output intent where applicable, explicit page boxes, and no interactive content. Do not render through a browser or toolkit canvas.
- Discover printer/paper/duplex/color/resolution capabilities through IPP/CUPS on Linux/macOS and the Windows print API on Windows. Native calls are isolated in reviewed adapters; domain and layout crates remain safe Rust.
- Submission is one-way. RustTable records the OS/IPP job ID and may query status while the application runs, but it never claims physical printing completed solely because submission succeeded.
- Queue and receipts contain printer alias/capability hash and job ID, never credentials, private network addresses, document contents, or unbounded driver messages.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `PrintRequest`, `PrinterCapabilities`, `PageLayout`, `PlacedPhoto`, `PrintColorPolicy`, `SpoolArtifact`, `PrintJobReceipt`, and typed errors.
- Implement grid/single-photo/contact-sheet layout, fit/fill/actual-size, rotation, crop anchors, margins/gutters, optional caption fields, pagination, and exact preview geometry shared with the later print UI.
- Render photos through #396 using final physical dimensions/DPI and selected profile policy. Reuse immutable export artifacts where hashes match.
- Build and independently parse/validate the PDF spool artifact before submission.
- Implement platform capability discovery and submission adapters, cancellation before submission, bounded status polling after submission, and startup reconciliation for jobs submitted but not acknowledged locally.
- Add fake IPP and fake Windows adapter implementations for deterministic tests; real printer tests remain opt-in.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown printer, stale capability snapshot, unsupported paper/resolution/duplex/color mode, invalid geometry, impossible margins, profile mismatch, double-management risk, oversized raster/page count, cancellation, and spool-validation failure before submission.
- A timeout or process crash after submission is `SubmissionAmbiguous`; reconcile by backend job token and artifact hash rather than submitting a duplicate automatically.
- Printer removal or driver change invalidates cached capabilities and requires revalidation.
- Driver/IPP diagnostics are length-bounded and redacted.

## Test matrix

- Single image, multiple images, grid/contact sheet, portrait/landscape, fit/fill/actual size, crop anchors, captions, page overflow, and pagination.
- Application-managed and printer-managed color, missing profile, unsupported color-off option, black-point compensation, grayscale, and intent variants.
- Linux/macOS IPP capability/submission/status, Windows capability/submission/status, printer removal, stale capabilities, cancellation, ambiguous submit, restart reconciliation, and duplicate prevention.
- PDF geometry/object/profile validation and byte-stable repeated generation.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-app print_service` passes with fake platform backends.
- `cargo xtask print-layout --fixtures all --validate-pdf --all-platform-adapters` emits geometry, color, capability, spool, and submission receipts.
- Opt-in integration tests submit a one-page fixture to a disposable IPP printer and a Windows virtual printer and recover the backend job IDs without duplicate submission.

## Dependencies

Depends on #260, #261, #396, #398, #411, #414, and #450.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements print request/layout/rendering, deterministic PDF spool generation, platform capability/submission adapters, reconciliation, and tests. Do not implement the print UI, printer-driver installation, arbitrary document printing, or remote print-server administration.
issues.title: Implement color-managed print layouts and platform spool submission

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the UI-independent slideshow service that snapshots an ordered catalog selection, renders color-managed presentation frames ahead of time, advances through a deterministic playback timeline, handles navigation/cancellation, and degrades predictably when images or GPU presentation fail.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement playback planning in `rusttable-render::slideshow` and orchestration in the application service layer. The later Iced slideshow view consumes events and textures; it does not own ordering, rendering, timing, or prefetch policy.
- A slideshow session snapshots photo/edit revisions, query order, orientation, display/profile identity, viewport dimensions, quality, transition policy, duration, repeat/shuffle settings, and random seed. Catalog edits after session creation do not mutate the active sequence.
- Supported initial transitions are `Cut` and linear-light `Crossfade`. Crossfade duration is bounded and composited in the display working space. Random or plugin transitions are not supported.
- “Shuffle” is a deterministic Fisher–Yates order generated from the persisted session seed. Repeating the same session snapshot and seed yields the same sequence.
- Render frames through the normal preview/full pixelpipe with a slideshow-specific quality mode and cache key. Embedded thumbnails are permitted only as an explicit temporary placeholder while the requested frame is rendering and are never reported as the final frame.
- Prefetch uses a bounded forward/backward window and memory budget, adapts to measured render latency, and never evicts the currently displayed or immediately adjacent frames.
- Timing uses monotonic instants. Pausing freezes advancement; system sleep/resume does not fast-forward through missed photos. Manual next/previous restarts the display interval after the selected transition policy.
- Color/profile changes, monitor changes, viewport resize, or HDR mode changes increment the presentation generation and invalidate only incompatible frames.
- Audio playback, video files, remote-control protocols, and slideshow authoring/export are outside this issue.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `SlideshowRequest`, `SlideshowSnapshot`, `SlideshowItem`, `PlaybackState`, `TransitionPlan`, `FrameRequest`, `FrameReady`, progress/events, cache identity, and redacted receipt.
- Implement stable sequence resolution from catalog/query snapshots, shuffle/repeat rules, current-index navigation, pause/resume, start/end behavior, and generation-tagged commands.
- Integrate render scheduling with #271–#274, thumbnail/preview cache reuse, WGPU/CPU presentation fallback, and bounded cancellation of obsolete prefetch work.
- Implement linear-light crossfade frame composition on GPU where available and deterministic CPU fallback using the same normalized color/premultiplication contract.
- Add latency-aware prefetch calculation that remains within configured host/GPU memory limits and records skipped/failed items without blocking unrelated frames.
- Provide a fake monotonic clock and fake renderer for deterministic unit/integration tests.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Empty selection, stale/missing photo, unsupported decode, render failure, profile failure, oversized frame, and device loss produce typed item/session events. The default policy skips permanently failed items after displaying a bounded error placeholder; it never loops indefinitely on one failure.
- Late frame/transition completions from an older generation are discarded.
- Cancellation drains session-owned tasks and releases cache leases without invalidating shared preview entries.
- A session with one valid item remains stable under repeat; all-invalid sessions terminate with a failure summary.

## Test matrix

- Ordered, reversed, filtered, one-item, empty, deterministic shuffle, repeat, non-repeat, next/previous, pause/resume, sleep/resume, and end-of-sequence behavior.
- Cut/crossfade timing, zero/max transition duration, linear-light blend known answers, CPU/GPU parity, alpha, HDR, monitor/profile/resize generation changes.
- Slow renderer, prefetch adaptation, memory pressure, cache hit/miss, cancellation races, stale completions, missing files, decode/render failures, and all-invalid selection.
- Repeated session snapshots produce identical order, hashes, and event sequences under the fake clock.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-render -p rusttable-app slideshow_service` passes.
- `cargo xtask slideshow-sim --fixtures all --fake-clock --cpu-gpu-parity` emits sequence, timing, frame, transition, cache, and failure receipts.
- A 500-photo synthetic session remains within the configured frame-cache budget and responds to manual navigation without waiting for obsolete prefetch jobs.

## Dependencies

Depends on #221, #253, #254, #271, #272, #273, #274, #296, #420, #447, and #450.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements slideshow session/domain state, deterministic ordering/timing, render/prefetch scheduling, transitions, fallback, fixtures, and tests. Do not implement the Iced slideshow screen, video/audio playback, remote controls, or exported slideshow files.
issues.title: Implement the deterministic slideshow rendering and prefetch service

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver the UI-independent camera service that discovers supported tether-capable cameras, snapshots capabilities, opens an exclusive session, exposes typed settings, streams bounded live-view frames, performs capture and transfer, and registers captured files through the normal import/catalog path.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement the safe domain and orchestration in `rusttable-camera`. Native camera APIs are isolated behind backend crates:
  - Linux and macOS use a narrow libgphoto2 adapter for supported PTP/vendor cameras;
  - Windows uses a Windows Portable Devices/WIA adapter where the device exposes equivalent capture/live-view capabilities.
  Product/application crates never receive native pointers, callbacks, or library error buffers.
- Camera identity combines backend, stable transport/device ID, manufacturer/model/serial where available, and a capability hash. USB bus addresses alone are not durable identity.
- One process owns at most one exclusive control session per physical camera. Discovery is shared; capture/control/live view require a lease with generation and timeout.
- Capabilities are probed after opening and represented as typed descriptors: read-only/range/menu/toggle/action fields, units, choices, current value, capture targets, file formats, live-view formats, and event support. Unknown vendor properties remain opaque and are not guessed.
- Settings changes use validated typed values and return the confirmed device value. Multi-setting changes execute in a deterministic order with an explicit best-effort rollback report; atomicity is never falsely claimed when the camera API cannot provide it.
- Live view is a bounded latest-frame stream. The backend may decode JPEG previews through `rusttable-image-io`; consumers receive immutable frame values with camera timestamp/sequence, dimensions, encoding, orientation, and dropped-frame count. Backpressure drops obsolete frames rather than accumulating latency.
- Capture is a durable application job. The request snapshots camera/session identity, settings, target, transfer policy, destination/import session, metadata, timeout, and whether the camera copy is retained.
- Default transfer policy is download-and-verify, retain on camera. Delete-after-verified-import is an explicit opt-in executed only after content hash, file size, decode/probe, metadata extraction, and catalog commit succeed.
- Captured bytes enter the existing immutable source-snapshot/import pipeline. The camera service does not create a second catalog or decoding path.
- Clock synchronization is diagnostic by default. Setting camera time is a separate explicit command and never occurs automatically.

## Implementation

- Define versioned device/session/capability/setting/capture/live-view/event models, backend trait, lease state, progress events, and privacy-safe receipts.
- Implement hot-plug discovery with debouncing, stable device reconciliation, permission/busy states, backend selection, open/close, capability refresh, property get/set, event polling/callback bridge, and session recovery.
- Implement live-view start/stop, bounded decode, latest-frame broadcast, frame-rate measurement, cancellation, suspend/resume, and device-loss handling.
- Implement capture trigger, event correlation, file enumeration, streamed download to staging, content verification, import registration, optional camera deletion, and ambiguous-capture reconciliation after timeout/crash.
- Integrate all long-lived tasks with #180 structured lifecycle and cancellation. Native callbacks enqueue bounded events and never call application code directly.
- Add deterministic fake-camera backends covering every state and a hardware qualification harness for opt-in devices.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Permission denied, camera busy, unsupported backend/capability, stale session, transport loss, battery/space warnings, setting rejection, capture timeout, duplicate/ambiguous event, corrupt/truncated download, decode failure, import failure, and delete failure are typed and preserve recoverable device state.
- A timeout after shutter trigger is `CaptureAmbiguous`; reconnect and reconcile the camera file list before offering another capture.
- Device removal cancels live view and capture-owned transfers, closes leases, and emits one loss event. Late native callbacks are discarded by generation.
- Receipts redact USB paths, serial numbers by default, filenames containing personal data, and native diagnostics beyond bounded codes/messages.

## Test matrix

- Discovery/add/remove/reconnect, duplicate backend reports, stable identity, permission/busy state, capability changes, session lease contention, and stale generations.
- Every setting kind, unsupported values, confirmation mismatch, ordered multi-setting updates, partial rollback, and opaque vendor properties.
- Live-view JPEG/raw preview, frame drop/backpressure, resize/orientation, decode failure, device loss, suspend/resume, and cancellation.
- Capture/download/import success, camera-retain/delete policies, hash mismatch, truncated transfer, duplicate file, timeout ambiguity, process restart reconciliation, catalog failure, and delete failure.
- Native boundary tests for callback panic containment, allocation ownership, thread rules, and error redaction on each platform.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-camera -p rusttable-app camera_service` passes with deterministic fake backends.
- `cargo xtask camera-qualification --fake-matrix --verify-boundary --verify-recovery` emits discovery, capability, live-view, capture, transfer, import, and recovery receipts.
- Opt-in hardware tests qualify at least one supported camera on Linux/macOS and one Windows backend device or explicitly record the Windows capability gap without pretending parity.

## Dependencies

Depends on #168, #177, #180, #223, #224, #237, #247, #248, #249, #250, #252, #450, and #452.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements camera domain models, platform backend boundaries, discovery/session/settings, live view, capture/transfer/import integration, fake backends, and tests. Do not implement the Iced tethering view, vendor firmware updates, video recording, webcam support, or remote-network camera control.
issues.title: Implement camera discovery, tethered capture, and live-view data services

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Deliver a supported `rusttable-cli` binary that opens the native catalog or an explicit source/sidecar input, resolves an immutable processing snapshot, renders and exports through the same pixelpipe/export services as the desktop application, and emits stable machine-readable progress and receipts suitable for automation. This is the Rust-owned replacement for darktable’s headless `darktable-cli` composition and export responsibilities, while deliberately redesigning the command grammar, persistence, process ownership, and automation contract.

## Fixed decisions

- Add a dedicated `rusttable-cli` package as an application composition root. It may depend on application/domain services but never on `rusttable-ui` or Iced.
- The CLI is not a second processing API. Every operation, profile, size, metadata, encoder, destination, retry, and fallback decision is represented by the existing typed contracts and validated exactly as in the desktop application.
- Initial commands are:
  - `catalog check` — open/read/verify one RustTable catalog without mutation unless `--repair` is explicitly provided later;
  - `render` — render one catalog photo/edit or one explicitly supplied source plus optional supported sidecar to a staged image artifact;
  - `export` — enqueue or synchronously execute one canonical export request/recipe;
  - `queue list|wait|cancel` — inspect and control jobs owned by the current catalog.
  Import, migration, camera control, and bulk scripting have separate commands/issues.
- Identity selectors are explicit and mutually exclusive: catalog `PhotoId` plus optional `EditId`, or source fixture/path plus optional XMP. The CLI never guesses “current edit.”
- Human text is the default for interactive terminals. `--format json` and newline-delimited `--format jsonl` use versioned schemas and write only data to stdout; diagnostics go to stderr. Field ordering is stable.
- Progress events are monotonic, bounded, and generation/job identified. JSONL mode emits one event per line and a final receipt. Broken stdout is cancellation, not an application panic.
- Exit codes are fixed: 0 success, 2 usage/validation, 3 unsupported capability, 4 source/catalog incompatibility, 5 processing/export permanent failure, 6 retryable/transient failure, 7 cancellation/interrupt, 8 internal invariant failure.
- `Ctrl-C` requests cooperative cancellation once; a second interrupt performs bounded forced shutdown through the application lifecycle. Partial local destinations remain governed by their atomic commit contracts.
- Default execution uses the qualified best backend with CPU fallback. `--backend cpu|gpu|auto` is explicit; `gpu` fails when unavailable rather than silently falling back.
- Paths are accepted only as OS-native arguments. Receipts use logical aliases and content identities; absolute paths are excluded unless `--include-private-paths` is used in local human output, never JSON automation output.
- No shell evaluation, environment-variable expansion inside templates, response-file command injection, Lua execution, or arbitrary operation JSON is accepted.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

- `src/cli/main.c`: `main`, `usage`, `icc_types`, `get_icc_type`, `icc_intents`, `get_icc_intent`, `_inputs_have_xmp_sidecar`, positional/option parsing, headless `dt_init`/`dt_cleanup`, source/folder import, optional XMP attachment, history reporting, output format/storage lookup, dimensions/profile/style settings, export loop, cleanup, and process exit behavior.
- `src/common/darktable.c/.h`: `dt_init`, `dt_cleanup`, headless-versus-GUI startup, shared subsystem construction, configuration/library selection, optional-service degradation, and final teardown. Rust lifecycle ownership remains #180.
- `src/common/film.c/.h`, `src/common/image.c/.h`, and `src/control/jobs/film_jobs.c/.h`: CLI path/folder import, image registration, supported-file enumeration, source identity, and failure behavior. General import commands remain separate issues.
- `src/common/exif.cc/.h`: `dt_exif_xmp_read` and sidecar attachment behavior used by the CLI’s explicit XMP input.
- `src/common/history.c/.h`: history loading and `dt_history_get_items_as_string`-style inspection; RustTable emits typed history/receipt data instead of source-formatted prose.
- `src/imageio/imageio_common.h`, `src/imageio/imageio_module.c/.h`, and `src/imageio/imageio.c`: format/storage registries, render/export request construction, profile/intent/size/style/metadata parameters, and the central output pipeline.
- `src/imageio/storage/disk.c`: local disk destination selection, filename handling, publication, collision behavior, and storage errors. RustTable uses the canonical atomic local destination rather than mutating opaque module parameter memory.
- `src/imageio/format/*.c|cc`: output-format registration selected by CLI extension. Each actual encoder remains owned by its specific issue; this CLI only dispatches typed requests.
- `src/libs/export.c`: export settings, metadata flags, profile/intent, size/upscale/high-quality behavior, and user-visible export semantics shared with the desktop path.
- `src/control/jobs/control_jobs.c/.h`: job ownership, progress, cancellation, and process-wide shutdown behavior that becomes #180-owned task groups and the durable #398 queue.
- `src/common/colorspaces.c/.h` and `src/common/iop_profile.c/.h`: ICC profile-type/intent resolution and export color boundaries. Exact transform execution remains #257–#262.
- `src/common/file_location.c/.h`, `src/control/conf.c/.h`, and platform wrappers under `src/osx/` and `src/win/`: CLI bootstrap paths, configuration/library selection, locale/platform initialization, and environment handling.

**Preserve:** headless processing, explicit source/XMP/library selection, persisted history use, deterministic operation order, bounded output sizing, explicit color profile/intent, style application where supported, format/destination dispatch, progress/cancellation, nonzero failure status, cleanup, and artifact verification.

**Redesign:** replace darktable’s positional/options grammar, GLib/GTK argument parsing, in-memory SQLite default, global `darktable` state, dynamic imageio modules, raw stderr strings, opaque parameter casts, extension-selected implementation, and direct synchronous export loop with one typed Rust CLI parser, redb/application services, static registries, immutable requests, structured lifecycle/task ownership, durable queue integration, stable JSON/JSONL schemas, fixed exit codes, privacy-safe receipts, and atomic destinations.

**Excluded:** compatibility with every historical `darktable-cli` option or exact text, import/folder-recursion command breadth, arbitrary style/batch scripting, camera control, interactive shell, package-management commands, and GUI behavior. Their responsibilities remain with dedicated issues.

Rust ownership: binary/grammar/output schemas in `crates/rusttable-cli`; composition/lifecycle in `rusttable-app`; catalog/source/decode/pixelpipe/color/export/queue services remain in their owning crates. The CLI may coordinate only through public ports and must not contain a second decoder, processor, exporter, or persistence implementation.

The pinned executable from #494 remains the behavioral oracle for overlapping source+XMP render/export cases. RustTable-specific catalog, queue, JSON, privacy, and exit-code behavior is accepted through independent typed fixtures because darktable has no equivalent contract.

## Implementation

- Define the CLI command grammar with one maintained parser, versioned JSON/JSONL output schemas, fixed exit mapping, progress sink, cancellation handling, and privacy policy.
- Bootstrap the same configuration, catalog, color, decoder, pixelpipe, GPU, export, diagnostics, and lifecycle services as the desktop composition root, omitting UI-only services.
- Implement catalog/source resolution, sidecar compatibility scan, immutable edit/render snapshot, backend selection, render/export execution, queue commands, and final receipt writing.
- Add `--dry-run` for render/export validation that resolves capabilities, dimensions, profiles, memory estimates, destination, and expected hashes without decoding/rendering or committing output.
- Add deterministic command fixtures and an end-to-end harness that invokes the actual binary with isolated HOME/config/cache/catalog/output roots.
- Add source-accounting fixtures proving every `src/cli/main.c` responsibility and each delegated import/imageio/color/job/platform responsibility has one Rust owner.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject ambiguous selectors, unknown IDs, dirty/incompatible catalog, missing source/sidecar, unsupported operation/version/profile/encoder/destination, invalid output mode, terminal-only prompts in noninteractive mode, and secret-bearing arguments.
- Noninteractive commands never prompt. Missing credentials or overwrite decisions fail with explicit required-action data.
- Cancellation returns the cancellation exit code and final JSON receipt when stdout remains available.
- Panic is caught at the binary boundary, produces a bounded incident ID, and exits 8 without serializing private diagnostics.
- Source/body/reference drift, an unmapped darktable CLI branch, or a new upstream option with no preserve/redesign/exclusion decision fails #555 rather than being silently ignored.

## Test matrix

- Every command/help/error path, human/JSON/JSONL output, stable schema, stdout/stderr separation, broken pipe, unknown fields, and exit code.
- Catalog photo/edit, source+XMP, CPU/GPU/auto, GPU unavailable, stale generation, dry run, local atomic output, queued export, wait/cancel, and restart reconciliation.
- Missing/unsupported inputs, malformed catalog/sidecar, noninteractive credential/overwrite requirement, first/second interrupt, process crash fixture, and private-path redaction.
- Desktop-versus-CLI identical request hash and image/output parity for representative valid fixtures.
- Differential source+XMP render/export cases against the pinned #494 `darktable-cli`, plus source-accounting tests for CLI parsing/bootstrap/import/history/color/imageio/storage/job/cleanup responsibilities.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-cli --all-targets` and the relevant service tests pass.
- `cargo xtask cli-conformance --all-fixtures --verify-json --verify-exits --desktop-parity` emits grammar, schema, exit, privacy, and image/export receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 470` proves every listed darktable CLI/headless responsibility is owned or explicitly delegated.
- Representative catalog and source+XMP exports produce the same artifact hashes as the desktop service path; overlapping darktable cases satisfy the current #516 comparison policy against #494.

## Dependencies

Depends on #178, #179, #180, #193, #221, #224, #246, #266, #271, #272, #273, #274, #296, #396, #397, #398, #415, #449, #450, #494, #516, and #555.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the headless composition root, command grammar, render/export/queue commands, schemas, progress/cancellation, fixtures, source-accounting, and tests. Do not implement bulk style application, migration execution, camera control, an interactive shell, the desktop UI, or duplicate processing/export services.
issues.title: Implement the headless processing and export command-line application

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Capabilities

- `iop.clahe` — role `implementation`

## Outcome

Implement darktable operation identity `clahe`, displayed as `old local contrast`, so imported version-1 histories and explicit legacy styles reproduce the pinned reference algorithm without being converted to the modern local-contrast operation.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::clahe`; compatibility identity remains exactly `clahe`; parameter schema version is 1.
- Mark the descriptor `DeprecatedCompatibility`, include it in styles, and hide it from normal new-edit discovery unless deprecated operations are enabled.
- Parameters are exactly:
  - `radius: f64`, accepted range `0..=256`, default `64`;
  - `slope: f64`, accepted range `1..=3`, default `1.25`.
- Input and output are four-channel working RGB. Dimensions are unchanged. The operation replaces RGB lightness while preserving hue and saturation; alpha/spare-channel behavior follows the evidenced compatibility result.
- Canonical luminance is `0.5 * (clamp(max(R,G,B),0,1) + clamp(min(R,G,B),0,1))`.
- Resolve the integer window radius as the persisted radius multiplied by ROI scale and divided by pipeline input scale, using the exact truncation behavior of the reference.
- Use 257 histogram entries indexed by `round_positive(luminance * 256)`. Preserve the reference’s initial-window bounds, sliding horizontal histogram updates, per-pixel sample count, and clipping limit `round(slope * sample_count / 256)`.
- Histogram clipping and redistribution repeat until the clipped-entry count stabilizes. Preserve the exact integer division, remainder spacing, inclusive bin ranges, first-nonzero-bin search, CDF construction, and `cdfMin`/`cdfMax` normalization.
- Apply the mapped value as HSL lightness using the shared RGB↔HSL primitive. Do not substitute tile-grid CLAHE from another library.
- CPU scalar/fixed-order execution is canonical. WGPU is optional for this deprecated operation and may be added only when it matches the CPU/reference algorithm; CPU support is mandatory.
- The algorithm’s full neighborhood must be available. Tiled execution is legal only with the exact overlap and histogram state needed to produce the same result as full-frame execution; otherwise the planner requests the required full ROI.

## Implementation

- Add exact version-1 parameter codec, validation, descriptor flags, defaults, history integration, generated controls, cache fields, and compatibility fixture IDs from #448.
- Implement checked luminance allocation and bounded per-worker destination rows and histogram storage. Use the shared image-buffer pool rather than unbounded per-pixel allocation.
- Build an immutable `ClahePlan` containing resolved radius, slope, scale identity, histogram constants, ROI/full-image requirement, memory estimate, and backend.
- Implement the row-sliding histogram and iterative clipping/CDF algorithm with checked index arithmetic and row-boundary cancellation checkpoints.
- Define the compatibility behavior for `cdfMax == cdfMin` from real reference evidence. Do not allow an unreviewed division-by-zero/NaN policy.
- Integrate blending only through the shared operation wrapper when the evidence-backed descriptor permits it; the operation itself does not invent masks or blend semantics.
- Emit receipts containing parameter version, resolved radius, ROI/window bounds, histogram configuration, execution mode, memory estimate, and output identity.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed parameter bytes, non-finite or out-of-range parameters, unsupported layouts, dimension/index overflow, and memory estimates above the pixelpipe budget before publication.
- Radius zero, one-pixel/tiny images, constant images, all-black/all-white values, saturated colors, negative/HDR input, and degenerate CDFs have explicit differential fixtures and deterministic results.
- Allocation or cancellation failure publishes no partial image or cache entry.
- Unknown future versions remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.
- GPU failure, when a WGPU implementation exists, retries the identical CPU plan once without changing radius, histogram, or ROI behavior.

## Test matrix

- Exact parameter/default/range/version round trips.
- Hand-computed 1×1 and small histogram, clipping, redistribution, CDF, and HSL cases.
- Constant fields, gradients, saturated primaries, skin-like colors, negative/HDR values, alpha, odd/padded/tiny images, edges, and corners.
- Full-frame versus every legal tile layout and multiple thread counts.
- Differential CPU tests against the real pinned darktable oracle for representative radii, slopes, scales, previews, and exports.
- Property tests for deterministic output, bounded histogram indices, unchanged geometry, no tile seams, and cancellation cleanup.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing clahe` and focused HSL/pixelpipe tests pass.
- `cargo xtask parity operation clahe --all-versions --cpu --full --tiled` produces complete reference, plan, memory, and image receipts under #447.
- Imported version-1 histories render without conversion, dropped fields, generic third-party CLAHE substitution, or per-tile histogram drift.

## Dependencies

Depends on #447, #448, #450, #263, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, and #283.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements deprecated `clahe` only: version-1 compatibility, deterministic CPU algorithm, optional exact WGPU path, pixelpipe/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement the modern local-contrast operation or module-specific UI beyond generated controls.
issues.title: Implement deprecated CLAHE local-contrast compatibility operation
