issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Capabilities

- `iop.defringe` — role `implementation`

## Outcome

Implement darktable’s deprecated `defringe` operation exactly for imported histories and explicit legacy styles, including global-average, local-average, and static threshold modes, Gaussian chroma-edge detection, Fibonacci-lattice sampling, Lab behavior, tiling overlap, blending, and deterministic CPU output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::defringe`; compatibility identity remains exactly `defringe`; parameter schema version is 1; alias is `chromatic aberrations`.
- Mark the descriptor `DeprecatedCompatibility` and hide it from normal new-edit discovery in favor of the newer chromatic-aberration operation. Do not auto-convert imported nodes.
- Parameters are exactly:
  - `radius: f32`, range `0.5..=20`, default `4`;
  - `threshold: f32`, range `0.5..=128`, default `20`;
  - mode enum numeric values `GlobalAverage=0`, `LocalAverage=1`, `Static=2`, default `GlobalAverage`.
- Require four-channel Lab input. Preserve L and output alpha/spare semantics exactly; edge detection and replacement operate on `a` and `b`.
- Resolve `sigma = max(0.1, abs(radius)) * roi_scale / input_scale`; Gaussian order is 1; integer support radius is `ceil(2 * ceil(sigma))`.
- Gaussian bounds are Lab `[0,-128,-128,0]` through `[100,128,128,1]`. Edge chroma is squared difference between original and Gaussian-blurred `a`/`b`.
- Preserve the pinned reference Fibonacci sequence, lattice coordinate rounding, sample-index thresholds, minimum 13-sample rule, `small_radius = max(radius,3)`, `avg_radius = 24 + 4*radius`, and coefficient `33`.
- Global mode computes one deterministic average edge chroma over the complete required ROI and uses the exact `max(0.1, 4 * threshold * average / 33)` formula.
- Static mode uses `max(0.1, threshold)`. Local mode derives the per-pixel threshold from the large Fibonacci lattice exactly as evidenced by the reference source.
- Preserve the one-pixel neighbor growth condition, inverse-chroma weighting, boundary clamping, and replacement order from the reference algorithm.
- Tiling overlap is exactly twice the persisted radius as declared by the source, plus any Gaussian implementation support required by the shared primitive. Global-average analysis is frozen once per full pipeline plan; it is never recomputed per tile.
- CPU scalar/fixed-reduction execution is canonical. WGPU is optional for this deprecated operation and may ship only when every mode matches the CPU/reference implementation. CPU fallback is mandatory.

## Implementation

- Add exact version-1 DTO/codec, enum representation, defaults, descriptor/deprecation metadata, history/cache integration, generated controls, and blending/mask registration from #448.
- Reuse the shared bounded Gaussian primitive and validate four-channel Lab storage before allocation.
- Build immutable average and small Fibonacci lattice tables in `DefringePlan` with checked sizes, resolved sigma/support/overlap, mode, thresholds, global-analysis result, memory estimate, and executor.
- Implement deterministic global reduction independent of Rayon worker count and freeze it in cache identity and receipts.
- Implement edge-layer construction, threshold evaluation, neighborhood sampling, chroma replacement, cancellation, and output publication without reusing the alpha lane as undocumented persistent state outside the operation.
- Model full-analysis versus tile-local requirements explicitly in the pixelpipe planner.
- Emit receipts containing parameter version, mode, sigma, support radius, lattice/sample counts, global average/threshold where applicable, overlap, backend, and output identity.

## Failure and edge behavior

- If width or height is smaller than `2*support_radius+1`, reproduce the reference copy-through behavior and record `ImageTooSmallForKernel`; do not emit partially processed borders.
- Gaussian or lattice allocation failure copies input only when the reference compatibility behavior does so and records a typed degraded result; otherwise fail before publication.
- Reject malformed bytes, unknown mode values, non-finite/out-of-range parameters, unsupported color/channel layouts, dimension/index overflow, and memory estimates above budget.
- Cancellation publishes no partial image, analysis, or cache entry.
- WGPU failure, when available, retries the identical CPU plan once without changing mode, threshold, lattice, or overlap.
- Unknown future versions remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Exact parameter/default/enum/version round trips.
- Fibonacci coordinates and sample-count goldens for every sample-index threshold.
- Hand-computed global, local, and static threshold cases.
- Thin and thick purple/green fringes, hard edges, low/high chroma, flat fields, Lab extrema, tiny/odd/padded images, boundaries, alpha, masks, blends, and tile seams.
- Differential tests against the real pinned darktable oracle for all modes and representative radii/thresholds/scales.
- Property tests for thread-count-independent global analysis, finite bounded output, deterministic lattice generation, full-frame/tile equivalence where legal, and cancellation cleanup.
- Optional CPU/WGPU parity for every mode under #447.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing defringe` plus focused Gaussian/Lab/pixelpipe/blending tests pass.
- `cargo xtask parity operation defringe --all-modes --cpu --full --tiled` produces complete analysis, lattice, overlap, memory, and image receipts.
- Imported version-1 histories retain exact numeric enum and parameter values; no newer-operation substitution or per-tile global-threshold drift remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #447, #448, #450, #257, #263, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, and #283.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements deprecated `defringe` only: version-1 compatibility, deterministic analysis and CPU algorithm, optional exact WGPU path, pixelpipe/blending/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement the newer chromatic-aberration operation or module-specific UI beyond generated controls.
issues.title: Implement deprecated defringe compatibility operation

issues.body:
Closes #448

## Why

Replace heuristic and placeholder operation-manifest entries with compatibility records grounded in darktable reference source evidence, stable ownership, and deterministic validation.

## How

- Add explicit darktable reference identity and per-operation evidence records.
- Remove synthesized parameter versions, comment-derived presets/kernels, and ownership fallbacks.
- Require compile_commands.json as an AST-backed extraction input and fail closed on missing or malformed evidence.
- Validate layouts, versions, migrations, GPU programs, presets, evidence hashes, issue ownership, and deterministic output.
- Add positive fixtures plus negative tests for missing evidence, invalid ownership, and unknown OpenCL programs.
- Preserve the Rust 1.98 beta / iced 0.15-dev constraints, unsafe prohibition, and handwritten-source limit.

## Validation

- cargo test -p rusttable-parity -p rusttable-testkit --locked --no-fail-fast
- cargo clippy -p rusttable-parity -p rusttable-testkit --locked --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo xtask parity verify
- repository pre-push gate

## Dependency

#446 governance and #447 differential oracle are merged into main as #465 and #464. There is no outstanding #446 dependency. The #446 and #447 worktrees/branches were not modified.
issues.title: Replace the heuristic operation manifest with evidence-backed compatibility data

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Capabilities

- `iop.censorize` — role `implementation`

## Outcome

Implement darktable’s version-1 `censorize` operation with exact pre-blur, five-sample pixelization, deterministic statistical noise, post-blur, masks/blending, scale behavior, and CPU reference output.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-processing::operations::censorize`; compatibility identity remains exactly `censorize`; parameter schema version is 1.
- Parameters are four `f32` values:
  - input blur radius `radius_1`, range `0..=500`, default `0`;
  - pixelization radius `pixelate`, range `0..=500`, default `0`;
  - output blur radius `radius_2`, range `0..=500`, default `0`;
  - noise level `noise`, range `0..=1`, default `0`.
- Require four-channel RGB input, preserve dimensions, include the operation in styles, and support the shared blending/mask wrapper.
- Resolve `sigma_1`, `sigma_2`, and integer pixel radius from persisted values using ROI scale divided by input scale. Resolve effective noise as `noise / max(input_scale / roi_scale, 1)`.
- Stage order and buffer selection reproduce the pinned reference exactly:
  1. optional pre-blur;
  2. optional pixelization;
  3. optional post-blur;
  4. deterministic noise calls at the exact reference locations, including the pre-post-blur call and the final call when post-blur is enabled. Do not simplify the apparent double-noise behavior without differential evidence.
- Pixel cells have nominal side `2 * pixel_radius`. For each cell, sample top-left, top-right, center, bottom-left, and bottom-right with the reference clamping rules; average all four channels equally and paint the exact half-open reference bounds.
- Noise initializes xoshiro128+ state per full-image `(x,y)` using splitmix32 seeds `(x+1)`, `(x+1)*(y+3)`, `1337`, and `666`, advances four times, derives Gaussian noise from the current green-channel norm and checker parity, and multiplies RGB by the resulting epsilon with a lower clamp at zero. Alpha is not noised.
- CPU scalar/fixed-order execution is canonical. The disabled/incomplete OpenCL block in the reference source is not an implementation contract. WGPU is optional and may ship only after CPU/reference parity.
- Memory planning includes the exact temporary image plus Gaussian resources and legal overlap. Disabled stages allocate no unnecessary full-frame scratch.

## Implementation

- Add exact version-1 parameter codec, descriptor, defaults, styles, history/cache integration, generated controls, masks/blending registration, and fixture IDs from #448.
- Build a checked immutable `CensorizePlan` containing resolved sigmas, pixel radius/cell count, scale/noise, enabled stages, buffer routing, Gaussian support/overlap, memory estimate, deterministic RNG version, and backend.
- Reuse the shared bounded Gaussian primitive with RGB lower bound zero and reference upper-bound behavior.
- Implement the five-point cell sampler/filler with checked arithmetic for partial right/bottom cells and full-image coordinate identity.
- Implement the exact splitmix32/xoshiro128+/Gaussian sequence as a shared deterministic primitive with known-answer tests.
- Add row/cell cancellation checkpoints and pooled-buffer publication so cancellation cannot expose a partially censored image.
- Add WGPU only if stage order, RNG, pixel sampling/fill, Gaussian behavior, alpha, and masks meet #447 on supported adapters.
- Emit receipts containing parameter version, resolved scale/sigmas/radius/noise, enabled stages and noise-call count, memory estimate, RNG version, backend/fallback, mask/blend identity, and output hash.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed parameter bytes, non-finite/out-of-range values, unsupported layout/channel count, cell-size or dimension overflow, excessive scratch estimate, and unavailable required Gaussian resources before publication.
- Pixel radius resolving to zero disables pixelization. Zero blur/noise stages are exact no-ops without uninitialized scratch reads.
- Tiny and partial cells use the reference’s clamped sample points and half-open paint bounds; uncovered boundary behavior is fixed by differential evidence rather than assumed.
- Green-channel norm zero, negative, non-finite, and HDR inputs have explicit compatibility tests; division/non-finite behavior is never silently normalized without reference evidence.
- Allocation/cancellation failure publishes no partial image, cache entry, or history revision.
- Unknown future versions remain byte-preserved and execution-blocking.

## Test matrix

- Exact parameter/default/range/version/scale resolution.
- Hand-computed five-point cells, including partial right and bottom edges and one-pixel images.
- Splitmix32/xoshiro128+ and Gaussian known-answer values for multiple coordinates, checker parities, norms, and thread counts.
- Every combination of pre-blur, pixelization, post-blur, and noise enabled/disabled, including the reference noise-call ordering.
- Flat fields, gradients, high-frequency patterns, faces/text-like fixtures, negative/HDR values, transparent/opaque alpha, odd/padded/tiny images, edges, masks, blends, and tile boundaries.
- Differential CPU tests against the real pinned darktable oracle and optional CPU/WGPU parity under #447.
- Property tests for deterministic output across worker counts, bounded memory/indexing, unchanged geometry, mask confinement, and cancellation cleanup.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-processing censorize` plus focused Gaussian/RNG/pixelpipe/mask/blend tests pass.
- `cargo xtask parity operation censorize --cpu --all-stage-combinations --full --tiled` produces complete plan, RNG, memory, mask, and image receipts.
- Imported version-1 histories preserve exact values and stage behavior; no claim relies on the disabled OpenCL source.

## Dependencies

Depends on #447, #448, #450, #263, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #277, #279, #283, and #284.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements `censorize` only: version-1 compatibility, deterministic CPU algorithm, optional exact WGPU path, pixelpipe/mask/blend/history/cache integration, tests, receipts, and contract documentation. Do not implement censorship UI gestures, general privacy-redaction workflows, or unrelated blur/noise operations.
issues.title: Implement the censorize privacy operation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Capabilities

- `build-option.USE_AI` — role `capability`
- `lib.neural_restore` — role `foundation`

## Outcome

Implement RustTable’s reusable AI model registry and bounded ONNX inference service. It must load only hash-pinned model packages, validate tensor/color/tile contracts before execution, isolate ONNX Runtime behind a narrow native adapter, qualify provider-specific output, and provide cancellable tiled inference to later RAW denoise, RGB denoise, and super-resolution workflows.

## Fixed decisions

- Add safe product crate `rusttable-ai` and narrowly approved adapter crate `rusttable-ai-native`. Product callers see immutable Rust DTOs and typed buffers only; ONNX Runtime pointers, sessions, allocators, provider handles, and native error strings never leave the adapter.
- Use the pinned Rust `ort` binding and matching ONNX Runtime distribution selected by #456. Do not implement ONNX parsing/execution, invoke Python, spawn model scripts, or load arbitrary native custom operators.
- CPU Execution Provider is mandatory on every supported platform and is the canonical qualification path. Optional provider tiers are:
  - CoreML on Apple Silicon macOS;
  - DirectML on Windows x86-64;
  - CUDA on qualified Linux x86-64 systems.
  TensorRT, ROCm, OpenVINO, NNAPI, and arbitrary provider plug-ins are outside the initial contract.
- WGPU is not an ONNX inference provider. It may render previews or perform surrounding RustTable processing, but it is never reported as the AI execution backend.
- Model installation accepts a deterministic `.rtmodel` ZIP containing exactly `model.toml`, `model.onnx`, and optional bounded data assets declared by the manifest. No scripts, DLLs, shared libraries, external URLs, symlinks, absolute paths, or undeclared files are permitted.
- Installed model identity is the SHA-256 of canonical manifest fields plus exact model/data bytes. Installation extracts into immutable hash-addressed application storage and atomically updates a model registry.
- The versioned model manifest records:
  - stable model ID/version/task;
  - ONNX IR/opset and required standard operators;
  - input/output tensor names, ranks, dimensions/dynamic axes, dtypes, layout, channel meaning, and batch policy;
  - source/target color primaries, transfer functions, range, alpha policy, normalization, scale/offset, and finite-value policy;
  - tile width/height, overlap, alignment, valid output crop, scale factor, edge padding, blend window, and minimum image size;
  - supported provider tiers and provider-specific tolerances;
  - estimated model/session/tile memory and maximum concurrency;
  - model/data hashes and package schema.
- Initial tasks are `RawBayerDenoise`, `RawLinearDenoise`, `RgbDenoise`, and `SuperResolution2x`/`SuperResolution4x`. A model may declare exactly one task and task-specific tensor contract.
- Canonical CPU qualification uses fixed graph optimization level, fixed thread policy, sequential execution where required by the model manifest, deterministic tile traversal, and no provider fallback inside one run. Provider fallback is an application-level retry with a new explicit receipt.
- Provider use requires a one-time qualification receipt for exact model hash, provider, runtime version, adapter/driver, target, tensor contract, and reference fixtures. Unqualified optional providers are unavailable, not best-effort.
- Sessions are cached by `(model hash, provider qualification hash, runtime configuration hash)` under an explicit LRU memory budget. Concurrent runs are bounded per model/provider and owned by #180 lifecycle task groups.
- No automatic model download, marketplace, telemetry, or background network access is implemented. Models are installed from explicit local bytes by later UI/CLI workflows.

## Implementation

- Define `ModelPackage`, `ModelManifest`, task/tensor/color/tile/provider descriptors, `InstalledModel`, `ModelRegistrySnapshot`, `InferenceRequest`, `InferencePlan`, `InferenceTile`, `InferenceOutput`, progress events, cancellation, and privacy-safe receipts.
- Implement bounded ZIP validation/extraction through the fixture/archive safety services: path containment, duplicate names, compression ratio, file count/size, exact declared content, and hash verification.
- Validate ONNX model metadata against the manifest before registry commit: graph inputs/outputs, dtype/rank/static dimensions, opset, external-data references, and absence of custom domains/operators unless explicitly supported by the adapter policy.
- Implement ONNX Runtime environment/session construction, provider selection, deterministic CPU settings, tensor allocation, run termination/cancellation, native error classification, and resource cleanup in `rusttable-ai-native`.
- Build deterministic tiled inference planning with checked scale/overlap/alignment/padding/output-crop arithmetic, bounded host/native memory estimates, and a fixed overlap blend window shared by all providers.
- Add a provider qualification harness comparing optional-provider output to canonical CPU output with task/model-specific #447 policies and recording throughput/memory without making performance a correctness criterion.
- Integrate model/session cache invalidation, model enable/disable/removal, application restart reconciliation, and package registry transactions.
- Add architecture policy allowing ONNX Runtime only in `rusttable-ai-native`; no other product crate may depend on `ort`, ONNX Runtime `-sys`, or provider SDKs.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject malformed/noncanonical ZIPs, missing/extra files, hash drift, unsupported package/model schema, custom operators, external model data not bundled, unknown tasks/providers, tensor/color/tile mismatch, non-finite manifest values, arithmetic overflow, and memory/concurrency requests above policy.
- A provider initialization or execution failure returns a typed provider result. `Auto` may retry the same immutable plan on canonical CPU once; explicit provider selection fails without fallback.
- Cancellation invokes ONNX Runtime termination, drains the owned task, discards incomplete tiles, and publishes no output/session mutation beyond bounded diagnostics.
- Native panic/error/output shape mismatch cannot poison another session or registry transaction.
- Model uninstall waits for or cancels owned runs according to explicit policy and never deletes a package still referenced by an active session.
- Receipts redact local package paths, image metadata, native provider diagnostics beyond bounded codes, and device serials.

## Test matrix

- Package ZIP canonicalization, traversal/symlink/duplicate/bomb/extra-file rejection, manifest/model hash drift, install/upgrade/remove/restart, and immutable storage.
- Tensor contract validation for names, ranks, dynamic/static dimensions, dtype/layout, scale, unsupported operators/opsets, and external data.
- Tile planning for tiny/odd/large dimensions, overlap/alignment, 2x/4x scale, edge padding, valid crop, overflow, memory cap, and cancellation.
- CPU session creation/run/termination, output shape/type validation, concurrency bounds, cache eviction, and native error cleanup.
- Optional provider qualification success, tolerance failure, driver/runtime drift, unavailable provider, explicit provider failure, and auto CPU retry.
- Hostile/malformed ONNX fixtures and deterministic small identity/convolution/upsample test models generated independently of production code.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ai -p rusttable-ai-native` passes on Linux, macOS, and Windows with canonical CPU inference fixtures.
- `cargo xtask ai qualify --models fixtures --providers available --verify-limits --verify-isolation` emits package, tensor, provider, tile, cache, cancellation, parity, and resource receipts.
- Optional providers cannot execute until qualified, and no native ONNX Runtime type or handle appears in a product-facing API.
- Model packages execute with no scripts, custom native operators, ambient filesystem/network access, or unbounded allocation.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #222, #266, #447, #450, #452, #453, #456, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements model-package/registry contracts, ONNX Runtime adapter, provider qualification, tiled inference service, cache, limits, tests, and receipts. Do not implement RAW/RGB restoration workflows, DNG/TIFF output, catalog import/grouping, or AI UI.
issues.title: Implement the bounded ONNX model registry and inference service

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Capabilities

- `build-option.USE_GMIC` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_IMAGEMAGICK` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_GRAPHICSMAGICK` — role `policy`

## Outcome

Make the permanent architecture decision for darktable’s optional G’MIC, ImageMagick, and GraphicsMagick build surfaces, remove them from RustTable’s in-process dependency graph, and map retained user workflows to explicit managed external-editor issues rather than leaving an unresolved feature flag.

## Fixed decisions

- RustTable does not link, embed, load, or distribute G’MIC, ImageMagick, or GraphicsMagick libraries in product crates.
- RustTable does not use `convert`, `magick`, `gm`, `gmic`, or any other external image command as an internal decoder, encoder, thumbnailer, processing fallback, test oracle, or hidden implementation detail.
- Native RustTable codecs and processing operations remain authoritative for all supported import/export/processing capabilities. An unsupported format or effect remains unsupported until a reviewed Rust implementation issue lands; it is not delegated to an installed command.
- `USE_GMIC`, `USE_IMAGEMAGICK`, and `USE_GRAPHICSMAGICK` are classified `redesigned-external-tool` in the capability registry, not `required-native` and not generic `unsupported`.
- User-initiated interoperability is provided only through the managed external-editor service issue created from this audit. The service renders a versioned color-managed interchange artifact, launches an explicitly configured executable with an argv array, validates the returned artifact, imports it, and groups it with the source.
- G’MIC-Qt, GIMP with G’MIC, ImageMagick, and GraphicsMagick may be user-configured external applications, but RustTable does not ship app-specific command strings, assume their installation, or promise every command-line mode. The generic service exposes only reviewed placeholder arguments and return-file contracts.
- No shell interpolation, command string, environment-variable expansion, PATH-only executable lookup, plugin DLL/SO loading, temporary untracked output, or arbitrary script execution is permitted.
- External tool execution is opt-in per invocation. It never runs during ordinary import, preview, edit, export, migration, startup, or background indexing.
- External applications operate on derived interchange bytes, never the original photo path by default. The source file is immutable and no application receives catalog/database credentials or unrestricted RustTable storage paths.
- Capability ownership is split explicitly:
  - this issue owns the build/dependency/product-policy classification;
  - the managed external-editor service owns secure process/interchange/import behavior;
  - the Iced external-editor settings/action issue owns user configuration and launch UI.
- There is no product setting named `Use ImageMagick`, `Use GraphicsMagick`, or `Use G’MIC`. Users configure named external-editor presets.

## Implementation

- Update the canonical capability/issue registry and generated manifests with `redesigned-external-tool`, exact owner issue numbers/roles, and behavioral evidence IDs.
- Add dependency/source/DAG policy forbidding `gmic`, ImageMagick/GraphicsMagick bindings and `-sys` crates, native package probes, runtime dynamic loading, and invocations of known executables outside the managed service crate.
- Remove or reject any inherited flags/configuration/docs/scripts implying those libraries are accepted product dependencies.
- Add source-policy scanning for prohibited command names and shell-based image-tool use, with reviewed fixtures preventing false positives in capability metadata/tests.
- Define the retained behavior matrix: internal codec/processing owners, external-editor service, external-editor UI, and explicit unsupported cases.
- Document the decision only in the machine-readable architecture/capability records and concise developer contract; do not add a separate broad documentation project.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Adding one of the libraries/crates/native links, probing an executable from a product codec/processor, or invoking a known command outside the managed service fails dependency/source validation.
- Missing external software does not degrade any internal feature or startup state; only a configured external-editor preset reports unavailable.
- A future proposal to embed or link one of these projects requires a new architecture issue and cannot alter this classification incidentally in a codec/operation PR.
- Generic executable names appearing in fixtures/source evidence are allowed only through exact reviewed paths and cannot weaken the product-code ban.

## Test matrix

- Capability-registry classification and owner-role validation for all three build options.
- Cargo/native dependency mutations, build-script/package probes, dynamic library loading, direct process invocation, shell strings, PATH lookup, and hidden fallback attempts.
- Allowed managed-service adapter and source-evidence/test-fixture cases.
- Internal supported/unsupported format/effect matrix proving no external command is selected.
- Future capability scan drift and missing external-editor owner issues.

## Acceptance evidence

- The product dependency graph contains no G’MIC/ImageMagick/GraphicsMagick library or binding.
- Source/process policy rejects every direct hidden executable fallback outside the managed service.
- Generated capability records map all three options to this policy plus explicit external-editor implementation/UI owners.
- Ordinary product functionality and validation do not require any of the three applications installed.

## Dependencies

Depends on #451, #452, #453, #456, #492, and the managed external-editor issues created from this audit.

## One-PR boundary

One PR changes capability classification, dependency/source enforcement, owner mapping, concise architecture records, and tests. Do not implement process execution, interchange rendering/import, or UI in this PR.
issues.title: Classify G’MIC, ImageMagick, and GraphicsMagick as external-tool integrations

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Capabilities

- `build-option.BINARY_PACKAGE_BUILD` — role `policy`
- `build-option.BUILD_CMSTEST` — role `policy`
- `build-option.BUILD_CURVE_TOOLS` — role `policy`
- `build-option.BUILD_MSYS2_INSTALL` — role `policy`
- `build-option.BUILD_NOISE_TOOLS` — role `policy`
- `build-option.BUILD_PRINT` — role `policy`
- `build-option.BUILD_RS_IDENTIFY` — role `policy`
- `build-option.CUSTOM_CFLAGS` — role `policy`
- `build-option.DONT_USE_INTERNAL_LIBRAW` — role `policy`
- `build-option.DONT_USE_INTERNAL_LUA` — role `policy`
- `build-option.FORCE_COLORED_OUTPUT` — role `policy`
- `build-option.TESTBUILD_OPENCL_PROGRAMS` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_COLORD` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_ICU` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_ISOBMFF` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_KWALLET` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_LIBSECRET` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_MAC_INTEGRATION` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_OPENMP` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_PORTMIDI` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_SDL2` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_UNITY` — role `policy`
- `build-option.USE_XMLLINT` — role `policy`
- `build-option.VALIDATE_APPDATA_FILE` — role `policy`

## Outcome

Replace the remaining darktable compile-time option inventory with an exact RustTable capability policy. Every option receives a permanent classification, concrete owner issues, runtime/build behavior, dependency boundary, and executable validation. No listed option may remain a generic toggle or inherit workflow ownership because it was not analyzed.

## Fixed decisions

Create `architecture/build-capability-policy.toml` with one entry per option containing `classification`, `behavior`, `owners`, `targets`, `dependency_policy`, `evidence`, and `replacement`. Apply these decisions:

| Legacy option | RustTable classification and fixed replacement |
|---|---|
| `BINARY_PACKAGE_BUILD` | `build-profile`. Package/release builds derive from the exact compiler/dependency/artifact identity and #514 package metadata. It is not a source-code conditional and cannot alter product semantics. |
| `BUILD_CMSTEST` | `test-only-tooling`. Color-profile selection/transform conformance is implemented as `xtask color` fixtures over #260/#261/#509. No shipped `cmstest` executable. |
| `BUILD_CURVE_TOOLS` | `test-only-tooling`. Curve/LUT fixture generation is deterministic repository tooling used by #447/#450 and operation issues. No shipped curve utility and no dependency in product crates. |
| `BUILD_MSYS2_INSTALL` | `unsupported-packaging-path`. Windows uses native `x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` packaging and #514 metadata. No MSYS2 installer/runtime layout. |
| `BUILD_NOISE_TOOLS` | `test-only-tooling`. Synthetic noise/profile fixtures are generated under #450 and consumed by denoise operation qualification. No shipped calibration binary. |
| `BUILD_PRINT` | `runtime-capability`. Printing is always compiled where the supported platform adapter exists and reports runtime availability; implementation owner #467. No product build toggle. |
| `BUILD_RS_IDENTIFY` | `redesigned-cli`. Signature-first identification is #224 and the user/headless command is #470. No RawSpeed helper binary. |
| `CUSTOM_CFLAGS` | `prohibited-unreviewed-build-input`. Compiler/codegen flags come only from #456’s versioned profiles. Arbitrary environment C/C++ flags do not exist. |
| `DONT_USE_INTERNAL_LIBRAW` | `not-applicable`. RustTable uses the Rust-owned RAW path #233–#236 and does not bundle or select internal/external LibRaw. |
| `DONT_USE_INTERNAL_LUA` | `not-applicable`. Lua compatibility is the pinned mlua/Lua 5.4 host #422; there is no selectable bundled-versus-system Lua runtime. |
| `FORCE_COLORED_OUTPUT` | `redesigned-cli`. #470 exposes typed `--color auto|always|never`, honors TTY/`NO_COLOR` policy, and never uses a compile flag. |
| `TESTBUILD_OPENCL_PROGRAMS` | `reference-only-plus-wgsl-validation`. Pinned darktable OpenCL evidence is qualified by #494/#495. Product WGSL is validated by #292. RustTable does not build OpenCL kernels into product code. |
| `USE_COLORD` | `redesigned-platform-service`. Linux colord is one runtime provider in #509; macOS ColorSync and Windows WCS/ICM are peer providers. No build toggle. |
| `USE_ICU` | `redesigned-rust-service`. Fluent/ICU4X localization and collation are #510. No ICU4C/system ICU dependency or toggle. |
| `USE_ISOBMFF` | `always-available-bounded-parser`. ISOBMFF parsing is RustTable-owned in #231 and reused by #407/#408. Codec adapters may be runtime-capability-gated, but container parsing is not a build flag. |
| `USE_KWALLET` | `redesigned-platform-service`. KDE is supported through Secret Service where available in #508; no direct KWallet API. |
| `USE_LIBSECRET` | `redesigned-platform-service`. Linux Secret Service is the Linux provider in #508, alongside Keychain and Windows Credential Manager. No plaintext fallback or build toggle. |
| `USE_MAC_INTEGRATION` | `redesigned-platform-service`. Native macOS lifecycle/menu/file-open behavior is #511 and compiles only for the macOS target. |
| `USE_OPENMP` | `replaced-by-rust-executors`. CPU parallelism is owned by #180/#266 with bounded Rayon/task pools and deterministic reductions. No OpenMP runtime or pragma translation. |
| `USE_PORTMIDI` | `redesigned-rust-service`. MIDI input is #512 via `midir`; editor UI is #513. No PortMidi. |
| `USE_SDL2` | `redesigned-rust-service`. Gamepads use #512 via `gilrs`; window/pointer/tablet input uses Iced/winit. No SDL2 event loop or controller dependency. |
| `USE_UNITY` | `not-adopted-build-optimization`. Cargo/rustc incremental/codegen profiles are #456. Unity/amalgamated source builds are inapplicable. |
| `USE_XMLLINT` | `redesigned-build-validation`. Pure-Rust package metadata validation is #514; `xmllint` is not a required dependency. |
| `VALIDATE_APPDATA_FILE` | `redesigned-build-validation`. AppStream/desktop/package semantic validation is mandatory through #514 rather than a user-selectable option. |

Additional fixed rules:

- Classifications are closed enums: `build-profile`, `runtime-capability`, `redesigned-cli`, `redesigned-platform-service`, `redesigned-rust-service`, `always-available-bounded-parser`, `test-only-tooling`, `reference-only-plus-wgsl-validation`, `replaced-by-rust-executors`, `prohibited-unreviewed-build-input`, `not-adopted-build-optimization`, `not-applicable`, `unsupported-packaging-path`, and `redesigned-build-validation`.
- `test-only-tooling`, `reference-only`, `not-applicable`, and `unsupported` entries do not create product feature flags or empty UI settings.
- Runtime capabilities are detected through typed service snapshots and receipts, not Cargo feature probing at application startup.
- Target-specific adapters compile only on their target, but product-domain APIs remain stable and report unavailable states on other targets.
- Capability owners are GitHub issue numbers and roles; no legacy sequence tokens or generic catch-all ownership.
- A future change from an exclusion/replacement classification requires a dedicated architecture issue and capability diff; it cannot be changed incidentally in a product PR.

## Implementation

- Define and populate the canonical policy schema with all listed entries and exact owner roles.
- Integrate policy validation with #451/#492 issue ownership, #453 external/native dependency ownership, #456 ecosystem/source policy, generated capability manifests, and #496 foundation evidence.
- Add source/Cargo/build/workflow scanners enforcing prohibited dependencies and commands: LibRaw, system/selectable Lua, OpenMP, PortMidi, SDL2, ICU4C, direct KWallet/libsecret outside #508, direct colord outside #509, `xmllint`, MSYS2 packaging, arbitrary custom flags, product OpenCL builds, and shipped legacy helper tools.
- Add positive checks proving each redesigned capability points to its exact implementation owner and each test-only capability is absent from release artifacts/product dependency graphs.
- Add a capability-diff command that reports classification/owner/target/dependency changes and requires explicit review.
- Regenerate `architecture/darktable-capabilities.toml` so every option has the fixed classification, owner issue numbers, and evidence IDs.
- Keep documentation limited to the machine-readable policy and concise generated capability summary.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject missing/duplicate option entries, unknown classification, missing owner, owner without correct role/milestone/priority, generic fallback owner, target contradiction, prohibited dependency/command, release artifact containing a test-only helper, runtime setting for a non-runtime classification, and generated-manifest drift.
- A platform provider unavailable at runtime is not a build-policy failure; the service reports unavailable and owning UI/workflow follows its explicit behavior.
- Test fixtures/source evidence may mention prohibited library/command names only in exact reviewed roots and cannot weaken product scans.
- A newly discovered darktable build option fails capability generation until this policy receives a complete reviewed entry.

## Test matrix

- One positive and at least one mutation fixture for every table row.
- Dependency/source/build/workflow mutations for LibRaw, selectable/system Lua, OpenMP, PortMidi, SDL2, ICU4C, colord/libsecret/KWallet outside adapters, `xmllint`, MSYS2, custom flags, OpenCL product build, and legacy shipped utilities.
- Runtime provider available/unavailable cases for print, display profiles, credentials, macOS integration, and input devices.
- Test-only helper omitted/present in release artifacts and product graph.
- Capability owner missing/stale/superseded/wrong role, new option discovery, classification change, and deterministic diff receipts.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo xtask parity verify-build-capabilities` reports all listed options classified with valid concrete owners and zero fallbacks.
- Product dependency/artifact scans contain none of the prohibited legacy libraries/runtimes/tools.
- Runtime services and CLI/package/test replacements are linked to the exact owner issues above.
- Adding an unclassified build option or reintroducing a legacy dependency/command fails before merge.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #224, #260, #261, #266, #292, #422, #450, #451, #453, #456, #467, #470, #492, #494, #495, #508, #509, #510, #511, #512, #513, and #514.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the build-capability policy schema, all listed classifications/owners, generated-manifest integration, dependency/source/artifact enforcement, capability diff, tests, and concise generated summary. Do not implement the owned runtime, UI, codec, packaging, or test-tool capabilities themselves.

issues.body:
## Outcome
Keep workspace-DAG validation bounded on GitHub while preserving actionable diagnostics and deterministic receipts.

## Evidence
PR #481 failed because the `workspace-dag` check exceeded the xtask process stdout cap (`stdout-limit` at 64 KiB). PR #476 encountered the same shared validation surface before the current PR-lane fixes landed.

## Requirements
- Emit a bounded deterministic receipt/artifact instead of losing the check to stdout overflow.
- Preserve the exact first failure, affected dependency edge, and actionable artifact path.
- Add tests for large valid output, large failing output, truncation markers, and cross-platform behavior.
- Keep PR validation within 150 seconds and retain strict failure semantics.

## Dependencies
Depends on the current PR validation lane fixes landing; do not weaken the workspace-DAG check.

Only process-output/receipt bounding and workspace-DAG validation wiring.

issues.body:
## Why

Validation surfaces had contract fields that were parsed but not fully executable, and process cleanup/receipt behavior was not deterministic across platforms. This completes issue #452 without weakening budgets or strict gates.

## How

- Execute prerequisite DAGs in deterministic waves with independent concurrency, resource groups, typed skips, stable receipts/statuses, and cancellation scopes.
- Enforce platform selection before executable lookup, warning/error semantics, network-none isolation, artifact freshness/type/root/size/hash validation, and surface budgets.
- Own process trees with Unix process sessions and Windows Job Objects, including bounded drain, TERM grace, KILL fallback, timeout, and cancellation receipts.
- Redact command receipts and route GitHub API calls through gh without putting auth tokens in argv.
- Add macOS/Linux/Windows-aware process/validation coverage and keep hosted workflow/toolchain policy checks strict.
- Rebase the issue branch onto current main.

## Validation

- Rust 1.98 beta fmt, workspace all-target clippy with -D warnings, and workspace all-target tests.
- Actual macOS precommit, prepush, pull-request, and main surfaces.
- Validation-budget, PR-CI delegation, Bun policy, repository-file, and workflow-policy checks.

Fixes #452

issues.body:
## Why
Workspace-DAG validation emitted a report larger than the 64 KiB process capture limit, so a valid check could fail as `stdout-limit` and a failing check could lose its first actionable dependency-edge diagnostic. This keeps the P0 validation surface strict while making its receipt actionable and bounded.

## How
- Drain oversized child stdout/stderr without converting successful exit status into an output-limit failure.
- Record truncation in the deterministic command receipt and emit bounded head/marker/tail artifacts.
- Add a workspace-DAG report artifact with the first violation surfaced before bulk graph data, including its exact edge and context.
- Wire deterministic per-surface artifact paths into validation receipts.

## Validation
- `cargo test -p xtask`
- `cargo xtask ci pr` (under 150 seconds)
- `cargo xtask ci prepush`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #482

The workspace DAG gate only validated local edges and two feature contexts, with a hardcoded two-target list. It could miss Windows-only edges, external ownership leaks, provenance changes, and feature combinations.

## How
- Added architecture/platform-support.toml as the target/runner source of truth for Linux, macOS, and Windows; CI, layout checks, and packaging derive from it.
- Split DAG enforcement into bounded modules with target-filtered Cargo metadata contexts for default, all-features, no-default, singleton, and reviewed interaction sets.
- Added exact external dependency ownership/provenance rules and receipts for aliases, kinds, target predicates, optional activation, roles, sources, and matched rules.
- Added deterministic Windows path/target, external dependency, feature, and valid-case fixtures.
- Kept receipt/process limits bounded and raised only workspace-dag’s bounded output cap and timeout for its expanded deterministic receipt.

## Validation
- Rust 1.98.0-beta.4: fmt, locked workspace check, xtask clippy all-targets/all-features, and xtask tests.
- Bun platform/layout/toolchain tests.
- Repository files/workflow/layout checks.
- Real 24-context DAG: 3 platforms, 0 violations.
- Pre-push: all 14 assigned checks passed.

One issue, one ready PR; squash auto-merge requested.

This completes issue #450 and preserves the existing #448, #449, and #457 workstreams. The issue records a dependency on #446; this PR does not alter governance policy.

issues.body:
## Why
Agents need deterministic worktrees based on the latest `origin/main`, while selected repository-local untracked inputs must be available when they are intentionally part of the task.

## Scope
- Fetch and verify `origin/main` before creating a worktree.
- Create an isolated branch/worktree under the canonical `worktrees/` directory.
- Support an explicit, safe manifest of repository-relative untracked files to copy into the new worktree.
- Reject unsafe paths, collisions, dirty tracked source state, and duplicate worktree paths.
- Add shell tests and concise usage documentation in `AGENTS.md`.

## Acceptance criteria
- One command creates an up-to-date agent worktree from `origin/main`.
- Tracked files come from the fetched base; only explicitly listed untracked files are copied.
- The script is idempotent for an existing worktree and reports actionable errors.
- Tests cover path validation, copying, and branch/worktree safety without network access.

## Why
RustTable pull-request validation should use independent GitHub Actions jobs in parallel so the required PR path stays within the 150-second budget without reducing strict coverage.

## Scope
- Separate independent PR checks into parallel jobs with narrow responsibilities.
- Preserve the existing `rust-pr / validate` required check contract, strict Rust 1.98 beta validation, and no issue/PR-body policy gates.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Correct the capability-to-issue ownership generator delivered by #451 so it reuses existing detailed issue owners, refuses to create generic placeholder contracts, derives the correct milestone and priority, and produces a complete reviewable reconciliation plan before any GitHub mutation.

## Fixed decisions

- Existing GitHub issues are matched primarily by explicit `## Capabilities` metadata and secondarily by exact compatibility identity extracted from a reviewed issue body. Title similarity alone is not authoritative.
- Before creating an issue for a discovered capability, the generator must search every open and closed child of #158 for that capability and for an exact operation/storage/view identity.
- A new issue is created only when no current or superseded owner exists and a complete issue specification is available.
- A generated issue must contain all required sections from #158: `Outcome`, `Fixed decisions`, `Implementation`, `Failure and edge behavior`, `Test matrix`, `Acceptance evidence`, `Dependencies`, and `One-PR boundary`. A body containing only capability ID, generic outcome, and boundary is invalid and must not be created.
- Capability category determines the default delivery milestone: processing operations use the processing milestone; export and workflow integrations use their owning product milestone; repository policy uses the foundation milestone. Milestone 1 is never the generic fallback.
- Priority is explicit in the generated specification. The generator cannot infer P0 simply from issue creation time or P2 from a missing category.
- Existing detailed owners are canonical for the known duplicates:
  - `iop.clipping` → #380;
  - `iop.overlay` → #367;
  - `iop.invert` → #375.
- #471, #472, and #474 are superseded duplicates and must not remain active owners.
- Generated mutations are two-phase: produce a deterministic plan, validate it offline, then apply with optimistic issue-state checks. Partial application is reconciled idempotently.
- The issue index records canonical owner, additional role owners, superseded issues, and replacement chains. It never silently chooses the newest or lowest issue number.

## Implementation

- Extend `architecture/darktable-issue-index.toml` and its source snapshot with explicit capability roles, replacement/supersession state, issue-body contract version, milestone, priority, and body-completeness result.
- Add a typed `IssueReconciliationPlan` containing updates, creations, closures, label changes, milestone changes, and blocked ambiguities.
- Parse `## Capabilities` entries strictly, including one capability ID and role per line. Reject literal escaped `\n`, malformed bullets, duplicate roles, and unknown capability IDs.
- Add a reviewed alias table for compatibility identities whose issue title differs from the source symbol, such as `clipping`/deprecated crop-and-rotate and `invert`/deprecated film-negative inversion.
- Search the complete child-issue snapshot before proposing creation. Closed completed issues may own completed capability work; closed duplicates resolve through their replacement.
- Validate generated issue bodies against #158’s required format and require implementation-specific decisions, not boilerplate repeated across unrelated capabilities.
- Derive milestone and priority from an explicit capability policy table. Report any unmapped category rather than defaulting.
- Add `cargo xtask parity plan-issue-reconciliation` and `apply-issue-reconciliation`; apply requires the exact reviewed plan hash and current issue ETags/state identities.
- Regenerate the issue index and capability manifest after reconciliation, mapping the three duplicate capabilities to #380/#367/#375 and the detailed replacement issues created in this audit.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Multiple plausible owners without an explicit replacement decision block the plan.
- A proposed creation with an incomplete body, missing dependency, unknown milestone, missing priority, or generic boilerplate fails validation.
- An issue changed after plan generation is skipped with a conflict receipt; no unrelated mutation continues silently.
- GitHub API failure produces a resumable partial receipt and never marks the index current.
- Closing a duplicate must preserve its body, add the canonical replacement, and use a not-planned/duplicate state rather than completed.

## Test matrix

- Existing capability metadata match, compatibility-identity alias match, title-only false match, open/closed/superseded owners, one-to-many role ownership, and no-owner creation.
- Exact regressions for #471→#380, #472→#367, and #474→#375.
- Generic three-section body rejection, missing required section, copied boilerplate across unrelated operations, wrong milestone, missing/multiple priority labels, and malformed escaped capability lines.
- Plan determinism, ETag/state conflict, partial apply, retry, idempotent rerun, and index regeneration.
- Mutation tests adding a new darktable registration with and without a complete issue template.

## Acceptance evidence

- The canonical issue index maps `iop.clipping`, `iop.overlay`, and `iop.invert` only to #380, #367, and #375 for implementation ownership.
- No active child issue has a generic placeholder body.
- Re-running reconciliation proposes zero mutations.
- Adding a capability without an existing owner and complete specification fails before GitHub writes.

## Dependencies

Depends on #451 and #446. Blocks further automated capability-issue creation.

## One-PR boundary

One PR corrects issue discovery, specification validation, milestone/priority policy, reconciliation planning/application, canonical ownership, and tests. Do not implement any mapped product capability.
issues.title: Prevent capability ownership generation from creating duplicate or placeholder issues

issues.body:
## Why

Closes #491. Pull-request validation currently runs independent repository and Rust contract groups serially in one job, increasing wall-clock time and obscuring which group failed.

## How

- Run the repository, formatting/metadata, and Rust test contract groups as independent matrix jobs with fail-fast disabled.
- Preserve the required `rust-pr / validate` status as an always-run aggregator.
- Add validated `cargo xtask ci pr --group ...` selection with receipts and focused coverage.
- Keep the existing 150-second per-group budget, strict Rust 1.98-beta checks, main exhaustive validation, and no issue/body policy gates.
- Document queue-drain orchestration in `AGENTS.md`: drain current PRs, then select exactly one highest-priority PR until it merges.

## Validation

- `cargo test --package xtask --locked -- --test-threads=1` (57 unit tests, 5 CLI tests)
- `cargo xtask repo verify-workflows`
- `bash scripts/test-pr-ci.sh`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- Actual local groups: repository 18.95s, formatting/metadata 1.04s, Rust tests 19.81s

Ready for review; squash auto-merge is permitted once required checks pass.
issues.title: ci: parallelize pull request validation groups

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Provision the actual external darktable reference bundle selected by #449 and prove real deterministic `darktable-cli` execution with and without XMP. This issue turns the fail-closed identity/resolution framework into a usable behavioral oracle; placeholders and fake executables cannot satisfy it.

## Fixed decisions

- The canonical reference is darktable version `5.7.0` at commit `cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad` unless #449 is explicitly amended before completion. Every structural scanner and behavioral invocation uses this same source commit.
- The qualified baseline bundle is Linux `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and contains:
  - an exact `darktable-cli` executable;
  - the matching darktable data directory;
  - the matching OpenCL kernel/program bundle;
  - a build manifest containing source commit, compiler, build options, native-library identities, target, and reproducible build receipt.
- The bundle is test/reference infrastructure only. It is never linked into, loaded by, packaged with, or invoked by RustTable product crates.
- Every artifact has a nonzero SHA-256 and exact size. Literal zero hashes, `unprovisioned-*`, `local-source`, mutable PATH lookup, and host-dependent library discovery are invalid for a qualified identity.
- Store the bundle in the controlled external fixture cache or immutable CI artifact keyed by the full identity hash. Repository tests resolve it through #449; product code has no access.
- Use valid parser-qualified fixtures from #450:
  1. one source image without sidecar;
  2. the same or another valid source with a real darktable XMP history;
  3. at least one history containing multiple operation instances and one mask/blend record for compatibility smoke.
- Required merge evidence executes:
  - CPU render without XMP;
  - CPU render with XMP;
  - a second identical CPU render proving output/log normalization repeatability.
- OpenCL evidence runs on a separately qualified runner when a matching adapter is available. It must prove OpenCL execution from darktable logs; a request followed by silent CPU fallback is not GPU evidence. Lack of a qualified adapter is `Unavailable`, never `Passed`.
- Output is independently parsed, dimensions/profile/container are checked, canonical pixels are compared through #447, and the output bytes/hash are retained as bounded merge artifacts.
- No network download occurs from product code, unit tests, or PR checks. Bundle provisioning is an explicit CI/developer action with an immutable source and receipt.

## Implementation

- Add a reproducible reference-bundle build/provision command that checks out the exact commit, applies the reviewed build options, builds only required reference artifacts, captures dependency identities, and emits a signed/hash-addressed manifest.
- Add external-cache registration for executable, data directory, OpenCL bundle, manifest, and valid source/XMP inputs with exact hashes and sizes.
- Replace every placeholder field in the qualified `fixtures/reference/darktable.toml` with verified values generated from the bundle. Keep an explicit `UnprovisionedReference` fixture only in tests; it cannot be the repository’s accepted reference identity.
- Add `cargo xtask reference provision`, `verify-bundle`, and `run-qualification` commands using #449 and the completed shared process service from #515.
- Verify executable version/commit/build output, dynamic library closure, data files, OpenCL program registry, and source-tree commit before invocation.
- Execute real CLI argument probing followed by CPU no-XMP, CPU XMP, and repeatability cases. Validate output through production/independent decoders and #447.
- Add optional qualified OpenCL job with adapter/driver/platform fingerprint and explicit executed-backend evidence.
- Publish one versioned qualification receipt containing stable fixture IDs, reference identity, command-contract hash, output identities, normalized log hashes, decoder/profile evidence, and availability status without absolute paths.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing bundle component, zero/placeholder hash, source/executable commit mismatch, dynamic-library drift, data/OpenCL drift, unsupported CLI flag, invalid XMP, nonzero exit, timeout, descendant leak, output absence, output parse mismatch, or nondeterministic repeated output fails qualification.
- The merge gate cannot downgrade required CPU cases to skipped or unavailable.
- GPU qualification cannot pass when logs show CPU fallback or when adapter/driver identity differs from the qualified record.
- Temporary HOME/XDG/library/cache/output roots are isolated and removed only after complete #515-owned child-tree shutdown and artifact capture.
- Receipts contain fixture/path aliases and hashes only; no developer paths or private metadata values.

## Test matrix

- Reproducible bundle manifest and hash verification.
- Missing/tampered executable, data file, kernel, native library, source commit, build option, and version output.
- Real CPU no-XMP, CPU XMP, repeated CPU, malformed XMP, unsupported operation, output profile/dimension mismatch, timeout, cancellation, and process leak.
- OpenCL executed, silent fallback, adapter mismatch, unavailable adapter, driver drift, and kernel-cache isolation.
- Path/privacy normalization across two different checkout and external-cache roots.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo xtask reference verify-bundle` reports a fully provisioned non-placeholder identity.
- `cargo xtask reference run-qualification --cpu --repeat` completes the real no-XMP and XMP renders and produces independently validated output.
- The required merge job has zero skipped CPU cases.
- Structural manifests and behavioral receipts report the same commit, build identity, data hash, and target.

## Dependencies

Depends on #447, #449, #450, and #515. Blocks the foundation acceptance gate and all darktable differential operation claims.

## One-PR boundary

One PR adds reference bundle provisioning/verification, real CLI qualification, external-cache identities, merge wiring, receipts, and tests. Do not change RustTable product behavior or implement operation algorithms.
issues.title: Provision and qualify the pinned darktable CLI reference bundle

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Correct the schema-2 operation manifest delivered by #448 so it is actually usable for persisted-parameter decoding and operation acceptance across supported targets. Retain the AST/layout infrastructure, but eliminate unresolved or contradictory semantic fields that were accepted as completed evidence.

## Observed blocking defects

- The generated manifest reports source commit `0eba994d638a4ae0c4e5fccd185600cf6f2118db`, while the canonical reference selected by #449 is `cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.
- The generated reference contains only `aarch64-apple-darwin`; supported Linux and Windows ABI layouts are absent.
- Operations such as `agx` still report `input_color_space = "unknown"` and `output_color_space = "unknown"`.
- `agx` records `IOP_FLAGS_SUPPORTS_BLENDING` while `supports_blend_masks = false` without an explicit semantic distinction/evidence.
- `ashift` lists `dt_iop_ashift_data_t` as an OpenCL program even though it is a C type, not an entry from `programs.conf`.
- Current parameter records such as AgX version 7 remain `decoder = "opaque"` and `opaque_blocking = true`, so exact size evidence does not yet provide an executable persisted-parameter decoder.
- Evidence entries use broad generated reviewer/source-line assertions without proving complete field layouts, conditional callback outcomes, or per-target equality/difference.

## Fixed decisions

- The manifest consumes the single identity from #449/#494. A different source commit, executable/data hash, target, or build-option hash is a hard failure; no independent manifest pin remains.
- Generate and validate current parameter layouts for all supported target ABIs:
  - `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`;
  - `aarch64-apple-darwin`;
  - `x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`.
- Each layout records every field name/type, enum identity/value, array extent, offset, size, alignment, explicit/implicit padding interval, total size, and canonical hash. A total size alone is not sufficient evidence.
- A current persisted parameter version must have an exact generated or reviewed decoder/encoder specification. `opaque_blocking` is allowed only for an evidenced unknown historical/future payload, not for the current supported version.
- Color-space contracts are represented as explicit unconditional or conditional results from the actual `default_colorspace`, `input_colorspace`, and `output_colorspace` callbacks. `unknown` is blocking for a registered executable operation.
- Distinguish `supports_shared_blending`, `supports_drawn_masks`, `publishes_raster_mask`, and `consumes_raster_mask`; do not collapse all behavior into one ambiguous boolean. Flags and semantic capability fields must agree or carry reviewed evidence explaining why they differ.
- OpenCL program names must resolve exactly to `programs.conf`; kernel names must resolve to declarations in that program. Source comments, type names, variable names, and unresolved numeric indices are rejected.
- ROI, tiling, overlap, full-analysis, geometry, fast-pipe, and scale behavior are explicit semantic enums with source/fixture evidence; `scanline` is not a generic fallback.
- Preset evidence records exact identity, parameter version/payload, auto-apply/format behavior, and source span. A function-call source line without decoded payload is not a completed preset record.
- Historical versions and migrations remain evidence-only: no synthesized intermediate versions or adjacent edges.

## Implementation

- Replace the manifest’s embedded independent reference block with a hash/reference to the canonical #449 identity and verify it before generation.
- Extend AST/layout extraction to produce per-target field-level layouts and compare target differences explicitly.
- Generate typed current-version parameter decoding descriptors, including enum numeric values, fixed arrays, booleans/GLib widths, and canonical endianness/ABI rules used by compatibility tooling.
- Add callback/registration semantic extraction with reviewed execution-free evaluators or explicit overrides for conditional results.
- Split mask/blend/raster capability fields and migrate all operation entries.
- Resolve OpenCL numeric program handles through actual registry initialization and reject unknown source tokens.
- Add semantic validation that fails any executable operation with unknown color space, unresolved current decoder, contradictory flags, unknown program/kernel, incomplete target matrix, or independent reference identity.
- Regenerate the manifest against the exact #449 source tree and update compatibility consumers to schema 3.
- Add reviewed golden records for AgX, Ashift, clipping, overlay, rawprepare, demosaic, defringe, rasterfile, colorin/colorout, and one proven zero-parameter module.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing supported-target compile commands or target headers is a blocking generation failure, not an omitted ABI.
- Target layouts that differ require separate hashes/decoders and a documented persistence-selection rule.
- Conditional color/ROI behavior without a representable predicate remains unresolved-blocking.
- A current decoder that cannot preserve every field/padding byte fails qualification; compatibility tooling may preserve raw bytes but cannot claim executable support.
- Any program/kernel reference not present in the pinned bundle fails generation.

## Test matrix

- Three-target field/offset/size/alignment/padding goldens and deliberate target-difference fixtures.
- Exact current decoder round trips for enums, arrays, booleans, padding, and malformed/truncated payloads.
- Conditional and unconditional color-space callbacks, ROI/tiling classes, mask/blend/raster combinations, and flag contradictions.
- AgX regression: explicit color contract, blending capability consistency, current version decoder, exact presets.
- Ashift regression: no type name in OpenCL programs; exact program/kernel resolution.
- Reference commit/build drift and unsupported target failures.
- Mutation tests removing a field, changing enum value, adding padding, renaming a kernel, or returning an unknown callback branch.

## Acceptance evidence

- The manifest and #449/#494 receipts report one identical reference identity.
- Every supported target has a complete layout for every current parameterized operation.
- No executable current operation has `decoder = "opaque"`, `opaque_blocking = true`, or unknown input/output color space.
- Every OpenCL program is present in the pinned `programs.conf` and every kernel exists in the referenced source.
- Reviewed AgX/Ashift and cross-class goldens agree field-for-field and behavior-for-behavior with the pinned source.

## Dependencies

Depends on #448, #449, #494, #450, and #492. Blocks persisted-history migration and operation parity claims.

## One-PR boundary

One PR upgrades operation manifest semantics/decoders/ABI matrix and regenerates evidence. Do not implement image-operation algorithms or user catalog migration.
issues.title: Complete operation-manifest semantic, decoder, and multi-ABI evidence

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add the first executable, versioned foundation-gate framework that aggregates independently produced RustTable governance, compatibility, reference, fixture, validation, platform, compiler/ecosystem, and ownership receipts before broad product implementation is treated as foundation-backed.

This completed issue established the gate architecture and deterministic receipt model. It did **not** and could not complete the later canonical source-map and real catalog-preview requirements now owned by corrective successor #556.

## Fixed decisions

- The gate is an aggregator of independent contracts. It consumes and validates contributor receipts; it does not duplicate image comparison, manifest extraction, corpus parsing, process control, dependency analysis, or product rendering.
- Preserve two modes:
  - `pr` for fast schema, ownership, stale-receipt, deterministic fixture, and mutation checks;
  - `merge` for required real external, corpus, cross-platform, and end-to-end evidence available at the time of the accepted schema.
- Gate receipts are immutable, bounded, privacy-safe, content-addressed, and current-commit aware. They contain stable identities and findings, never private paths, secrets, full image planes, catalogs, or unbounded process output.
- Contributor issue closure alone is not evidence. The gate validates producer/schema, commit/build/toolchain/reference identities, hashes, freshness, artifact presence, and required status.
- Required rows cannot be silently converted to warning, skipped, unavailable, placeholder, or synthetic unless the reviewed contract explicitly permits that status.
- The initial accepted contributor set covered the then-current contracts for queue governance, semantic issue specifications, image-difference evidence, reference identity/bundle, corpus, validation/process ownership, platform/dependency architecture, ecosystem policy, capability ownership, and operation semantic evidence.
- Broad readiness consumes a machine-readable gate receipt rather than prose or issue status.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Migration classification: `ReferenceQualification` / `NoDirectAnalogue` governance. The foundation gate does not replace one darktable function. It validates evidence produced by the issues that replace darktable responsibilities.

Representative evidence classes include:

- lifecycle/global/job surfaces in `src/main.c`, `src/common/darktable.c/.h`, and `src/control/*`;
- catalog/compatibility schemas and readers/writers in `src/common/database.c/.h`, image/history/mask/style/preset/tag/collection/config code, and XMP paths;
- processing/order/ROI/tiling/blend/mask responsibilities in `src/develop/*`, `src/common/iop_order.*`, `src/iop/*`, and shared helpers;
- OpenCL host/program/kernel responsibilities in `src/common/opencl.*`, operation `process_cl` paths, `data/kernels/programs.conf`, and `data/kernels/*`;
- image I/O/export/storage responsibilities in `src/imageio/*`, `src/imageio/format/*`, and `src/imageio/storage/*`;
- desktop behavior in `src/views/*`, `src/libs/*`, `src/bauhaus/*`, `src/dtgtk/*`, and `src/gui/*`;
- Lua, camera, map, print, color/profile, credential, localization, packaging, and platform integration sources.

**Preserve:** foundation completion is evidence-based, contributor identities agree, required artifacts exist, failures are fail-closed, and no synthetic claim substitutes for a required real oracle.

**Redesign:** replace informal readiness and closed-issue status with typed contributor rows, canonical identities, deterministic aggregate receipts, bounded artifacts, and executable readiness integration.

**Excluded from completed #496:** the canonical full source inventory/map, exact issue source-block hashes, source drift, and the real persisted redb catalog-to-preview contributor. These were identified after PR #525 and are successor #555/#181/#556 work.

## Implementation

- Add `cargo xtask foundation verify --mode pr|merge` and a typed `FoundationReceipt` schema.
- Define `quality/foundation-gate.toml` with contributor IDs, accepted schemas, modes/platforms, identity-equality rules, artifact limits, and optionality policy.
- Validate producer/schema/status, current commit/build/toolchain/reference identities, artifact hashes, freshness, and deterministic finding order.
- Reject superseded contributor schemas and incomplete/placeholder evidence known at implementation time.
- Publish bounded aggregate JSON and referenced artifacts through the validation workflow.
- Integrate the accepted receipt with backlog readiness policy.
- Add contributor/identity mutation fixtures, deterministic hashing, cancellation cleanup, and privacy/path redaction tests.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing receipt, unsupported schema, wrong identity, stale main commit, unknown producer, invalid hash, absent artifact, expired external bundle, required skipped/unavailable, failed contributor, or contradictory evidence blocks the gate.
- A contributor warning cannot become pass unless explicitly classified nonblocking.
- Evidence from another target, source commit, build, or fixture identity cannot satisfy a required row.
- Network/external-cache failure cannot mutate committed policy or create a partial accepted receipt.
- Cancellation cleans owned child work and publishes no successful aggregate.
- Re-running unchanged inputs produces the same normalized aggregate identity and finding order.

## Test matrix

- Complete passing contributor graph plus each row missing, stale, wrong schema, wrong identity, failed, required unavailable, optional unavailable, tampered, and superseded.
- Reference/source/corpus/operation/toolchain/platform identity mismatches.
- Current versus previous commit receipts and immutable external bundle reuse.
- Deterministic receipt ordering/hashing, cancellation, privacy, and path redaction.
- Ready-queue behavior with passing, missing, and stale aggregate receipts.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo xtask foundation verify --mode pr` passes the initial reviewed contract and rejects all mutation fixtures.
- `cargo xtask foundation verify --mode merge` produces the initial complete aggregate receipt for the accepted contributor schema.
- The ready-issue command can consume the gate receipt rather than trusting issue closure alone.
- Aggregate receipts remain bounded and contain no embedded image/catalog/process payloads or private paths.

## Post-completion audit

PR #525 completed the initial gate framework, but #496 was later found materially incomplete for the actual shift-in-place program:

- #555 and `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml` did not exist when #525 merged, so the gate cannot currently prove zero unmapped darktable responsibilities or current issue source-block hashes.
- #181 was still open, so the gate cannot currently contain the required real persisted catalog-to-preview contributor.
- Rewriting this closed issue body cannot make those features exist in `main`.

Corrective successor #556 owns the new foundation schema, #555 source-map contributor, #181 real-preview contributor, rejection of the historical #496 schema, and current readiness integration. #496 remains closed and must not declare open #555 or #181 as dependencies.

## Dependencies

Completed dependencies: #446, #449, #450, #453, #492, #494, #495, #515, #516, #517, and #518. Corrective successor: #556.

## One-PR boundary

The completed PR implements the initial foundation contract, contributor identity/freshness validation, aggregate receipt, workflow/readiness integration, mutation fixtures, and tests. Canonical source-map and real catalog-preview acceptance are explicitly corrective #556 work.
issues.title: Add an executable foundation acceptance gate before broad product implementation

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement a bounded, deterministic pure-Rust DNG writer for derived RAW artifacts. It must write Bayer CFA and three-channel LinearRaw `u16` images with the calibration/geometry metadata needed for RustTable and external DNG readers to reopen them, and provide the output boundary required by AI RAW restoration workflows.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-image-io::dng_output` over RustTable-owned TIFF/DNG writing primitives. Do not use Adobe DNG SDK, libtiff, Exiv2, ImageMagick, inherited darktable code, or an external process.
- Write DNG 1.4 compatible little-endian classic TIFF. BigTIFF is not emitted in this issue; requests whose checked encoded offsets may exceed 32-bit classic-TIFF limits fail before writing.
- Support exactly two output layouts:
  - `CfaBayerU16`: one sample per pixel, 2×2 Bayer repeat pattern, explicit phase and plane colors;
  - `LinearRawRgbU16`: chunky three-channel linear RGB using DNG `LinearRaw` photometric interpretation.
- Pixel storage uses 16-bit unsigned samples. Source values are preserved exactly after an explicit checked quantization/normalization step owned by the caller; the writer does not apply black subtraction, white balance, demosaic, denoise, tone mapping, gamut conversion, or clipping beyond validating the declared sample range.
- Use strips with deterministic rows-per-strip derived from a fixed maximum uncompressed strip size. Compression is Adobe Deflate with a fixed encoder configuration and horizontal predictor where valid. No JPEG, JPEG XL, LZW, tiled DNG, lossy DNG, or floating-point DNG in this issue.
- Emit one primary image IFD and no hidden alternate RAW image. A bounded embedded sRGB JPEG preview is optional only when supplied as an already encoded, validated artifact; preview generation is not owned here.
- Required common DNG/TIFF fields include image dimensions, bits/sample, compression, photometric interpretation, samples/pixel, planar configuration, rows/strip, strip offsets/counts, orientation, DNG version/backward version, unique camera model for derived output, software, date/time policy, active area, default crop origin/size, black level, white level, baseline exposure where provided, and checksum/identity metadata in RustTable private XMP rather than private binary tags.
- `CfaBayerU16` additionally records CFA repeat dimensions/pattern/plane colors, black-level repeat dimensions, per-channel black/white levels, AsShotNeutral, color/calibration/forward matrices and illuminants when available, analog balance where available, masked areas where representable, and source camera identity/provenance.
- `LinearRawRgbU16` records three-channel black/white levels, neutral/white-point and matrix/profile evidence sufficient to interpret the linear samples. It must not be mislabeled as scene-linear sRGB when the declared color space differs.
- Metadata policy is explicit: copy only canonical nonprivate EXIF fields approved by #413 plus derived-output provenance. Maker notes, thumbnails from the source, serial numbers, GPS, and arbitrary opaque EXIF/XMP are not copied unless explicitly selected by the caller’s metadata policy.
- Output is produced through the canonical bounded/atomic export artifact writer. The writer supports cancellation between strips and never leaves a final partial file.
- Byte determinism is required for identical pixels, metadata policy, preview bytes, writer version, and compression configuration. Wall-clock timestamps are omitted by default or supplied explicitly as request data; the writer never reads the current clock internally.

## Implementation

- Define checked `DngOutputRequest`, `DngRawLayout`, `DngCfaDescriptor`, `DngLinearDescriptor`, calibration/matrix/illuminant values, metadata policy, preview descriptor, limits, and versioned receipt.
- Implement exact TIFF value/IFD/offset layout with checked arithmetic, canonical tag ordering, deterministic out-of-line value placement, strip planning, predictor, Deflate streaming, and bounded seek/write behavior.
- Validate matrix dimensions/finite values, crop/active/masked areas, CFA phase/pattern, black/white levels, orientation, sample count, and encoded-size estimate before publication.
- Reuse canonical metadata values rather than accepting raw tag/value pairs from callers.
- Add DNG signature/probe integration so outputs round-trip through #234 and the generic image registry.
- Emit receipt fields for writer/schema version, layout, dimensions, strip/compression configuration, pixel hash, metadata/profile/calibration hashes, preview hash, encoded size/hash, and atomic publication status.
- Add architecture checks preventing AI/catalog/UI crates from constructing TIFF tags directly.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject zero/oversized dimensions, buffer/stride mismatch, unsupported CFA pattern/layout, invalid crop/active/masked geometry, non-finite/singular required matrices, black level above white level, sample overflow, classic-TIFF offset overflow, metadata/preview limits, unsupported preview container, and output collision before final publication.
- Missing optional calibration fields are recorded explicitly. A required color interpretation that cannot be represented blocks writing rather than substituting sRGB.
- Compression or I/O failure removes the temporary artifact and leaves an existing destination untouched.
- Cancellation between strips drains the writer and removes temporary output; no receipt claims success.
- Reopening a written DNG must not require RustTable-private tags.

## Test matrix

- Bayer RGGB/BGGR/GRBG/GBRG phases, multiple black/white levels, active/default crop, masked areas, orientation, matrices/illuminants, and odd dimensions.
- LinearRaw neutral ramps, color primaries, wide-gamut and negative-prequantization rejection, varied crop/orientation, and maximum valid classic-TIFF sizes through sparse/fake writers.
