- Deterministic tag/offset/strip/Deflate bytes, exact-size boundaries, cancellation at every strip, collision, partial-write failure, and timestamp policy.
- Metadata privacy inclusion/exclusion, serial/GPS/maker-note omission, explicit canonical EXIF, XMP provenance, and preview/no-preview.
- Independent structural TIFF/DNG parsing plus full round trip through #234 with exact `u16` samples and interpreted metadata.
- Mutation tests for offsets, byte counts, tag types/counts, CFA descriptors, matrices, and compressed data.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-image-io dng_output` and focused metadata/export tests pass.
- `cargo xtask image-io qualify-dng-output --cfa --linear --round-trip --verify-privacy --verify-determinism` emits structural, decode, pixel, calibration, metadata, and publication receipts.
- Written CFA and LinearRaw files reopen through RustTable with exact samples and required interpretation, and an independent DNG inspector accepts the files.
- Identical requests produce byte-identical DNGs across repeated runs on all supported platforms.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #227, #233, #234, #237, #240, #396, #402, #413, #450, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements deterministic CFA/LinearRaw DNG writing, validation, atomic publication integration, round-trip tests, and receipts. Do not implement AI inference, RAW denoise, TIFF display-image export, general DNG editing, or import/grouping workflows.
issues.title: Implement deterministic CFA and LinearRaw DNG output

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the end-to-end AI denoise workflow for supported 2×2 Bayer RAW photos: resolve a versioned source/edit snapshot, normalize and pack the sensor mosaic, run a qualified `RawBayerDenoise` model through #478, blend the result at mosaic-sample level, restore the camera-domain sample representation, write a deterministic CFA DNG through #497, and optionally import/group the derived photo.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement orchestration in `rusttable-ai::workflows::raw_bayer` with application/catalog adapters outside the inference core. This is a derived-artifact workflow, not an image-operation node in the current edit history.
- Accept only a single-plane `u16` Bayer `RawFrame` with a 2×2 CFA, known phase/channel meaning, active/default crop, finite black/white levels, and usable camera calibration. X-Trans, Foveon, multi-frame, dual-pixel, four-color, and already-linear RAW inputs are owned by separate issues.
- Source bytes and edit/catalog revisions are immutable inputs. The workflow never mutates the original RAW, source XMP, or source catalog record.
- The selected model must be an installed, qualified #478 package with task `RawBayerDenoise` and a tensor contract compatible with the source CFA/channel calibration.
- Canonical model input packs each 2×2 Bayer cell into four half-resolution planes in manifest-declared `R,G1,G2,B` semantic order independent of source phase. Full-image CFA phase is used when packing tiles.
- Initial model preprocessing contract is explicit in the model manifest and must be fully specified before execution:
  - subtract per-site black level including repeat/delta evidence supplied by the normalized `RawFrame`;
  - divide by `white - black` per site;
  - apply normalized AsShotNeutral/white-balance gains with green reference `1.0` when the model declares white-balanced input;
  - apply manifest scale/offset and finite-domain bounds.
  Missing required black/white/WB evidence blocks the workflow; values are never guessed from camera names.
- Inference uses #478’s deterministic tile/overlap/blend plan over packed coordinates. Tile edges and valid crops are converted exactly between full mosaic and packed domains.
- `strength` is a checked `0..=1` value and blends source-normalized and inferred normalized samples at the original CFA sample locations after tile reconstruction. Strength zero is exact source identity; strength one is full model output.
- Inverse preprocessing reverses manifest scale/offset and optional WB, multiplies by `white-black`, adds site black, rounds with one documented nearest-even rule, and clamps to the declared `u16` black/white/sample range. Masked sensor areas are copied from the source unless the model package explicitly declares and qualifies them.
- Output is a #497 `CfaBayerU16` DNG preserving CFA phase, active/default crop, orientation, black/white levels, calibration/forward matrices, illuminants, neutral/WB evidence, baseline exposure, and source provenance. It is not demosaiced or converted to display RGB.
- Default output settings: add to catalog `true`, group with source `true`, collision policy `unique-suffix`, and output directory/filename resolved by the canonical template and local destination services. Naming includes a stable workflow/model-version token and never overwrites source bytes.
- Import and grouping commit only after the DNG is durably published and successfully probed/decoded. A restart reconciles published-but-unregistered outputs using artifact identity; retries do not create duplicates.
- Canonical CPU provider output defines correctness. Qualified optional providers may run through #478 with explicit fallback receipts.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `RawBayerDenoiseRequest`, selected source/edit/model identities, strength/output/import settings, `RawBayerPlan`, progress stages, cancellation, derived-artifact identity, and workflow receipt.
- Resolve/decode/normalize the source through #233/#234/#236 without executing the user’s display pixelpipe.
- Implement checked CFA packing/unpacking, site calibration arrays, packed/full coordinate transforms, tile assembly, overlap blending, strength blending, and inverse quantization.
- Submit inference through #478 with bounded memory/concurrency and provider policy.
- Build #497 DNG metadata from typed source calibration plus derived-workflow provenance and canonical metadata policy.
- Publish via the atomic local artifact service, probe/round-trip the output, register through the import service, and group through #206 in one revision-checked catalog transaction after durable publication.
- Add startup reconciliation for pending workflow records keyed by request/model/source/output identity.
- Emit source/model/provider/tile/calibration/strength/DNG/import/group/output hashes and durations without paths, serial numbers, or raw metadata values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported CFA/layout, invalid phase, missing/invalid black-white-WB/calibration evidence, incompatible/unqualified model, insufficient active area, tensor/tile mismatch, non-finite inference output, memory/size overflow, and destination/template errors before final publication.
- Model/provider failure leaves no output. `Auto` may retry the identical inference plan on CPU once; explicit provider selection does not fallback.
- DNG write/probe/decode failure removes the temporary output and does not change catalog/group state.
- Catalog registration failure after durable publication records a reconcilable pending artifact and does not rerun inference on retry.
- Cancellation at decode, pack, inference, blend, inverse transform, write, probe, import, or grouping produces a typed stage result and no partial catalog commit.
- Source revision/hash changes during execution invalidate the request; the result cannot be attached to a changed source silently.

## Test matrix

- All four Bayer phases, per-channel/repeated black levels, varied white/WB/calibration, active/default crop, orientation, odd dimensions, masked areas, and 12/14/16-bit sources.
- Pack/unpack and preprocessing/inverse known-answer fixtures, strength 0/intermediate/1, rounding/clamping boundaries, and full-image versus tiled inference.
- Model/provider qualified/unavailable/failure/fallback, cancellation at every stage, memory pressure, and restart reconciliation.
- Deterministic CFA DNG byte/output round trip through #234, metadata privacy, collision policies, import idempotency, and grouping transaction conflicts.
- Differential fixtures against the pinned darktable RawNIND Bayer workflow where model/package identity is equivalent; otherwise exact internal model known-answer fixtures define acceptance.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ai raw_bayer_workflow` plus RAW/DNG/import/catalog tests pass.
- `cargo xtask ai workflow raw-bayer --fixtures all --providers qualified --round-trip-dng --verify-restart` emits source, calibration, pack, inference, blend, DNG, import, grouping, and cleanup receipts.
- Strength zero round-trips source active-area samples exactly, and repeated identical requests produce one identical derived artifact/catalog record.
- No source RAW/XMP/catalog record is mutated and no unsupported CFA is approximated.

## Dependencies

Depends on #206, #222, #233, #234, #236, #240, #248, #302, #396, #398, #415, #478, #497, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the Bayer RAW AI workflow, CFA preprocessing/inversion, DNG publication, import/grouping/reconciliation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement X-Trans/linear RAW, RGB denoise, super-resolution, model installation UI, or AI preview UI.
issues.title: Implement AI Bayer RAW denoise and derived CFA DNG workflow

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the AI RAW denoise workflow for supported X-Trans and already-linear/multichannel RAW sources: build a minimal deterministic RAW pipeline into linear Rec.2020, run a qualified `RawLinearDenoise` model, blend model/source output, write a deterministic LinearRaw DNG through #497, and optionally import/group the derived photo.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement orchestration in `rusttable-ai::workflows::raw_linear`; it produces a derived artifact and does not insert an AI operation into the user’s current edit history.
- Supported inputs are:
  - validated 6×6 X-Trans `RawFrame` sources supported by #235;
  - validated already-linear three-channel RAW sources from #234/#235, including a future Foveon source only when the decoder exposes an explicit linear channel interpretation and calibration.
  Bayer is owned by #498. Unknown/nonlinear/multiframe layouts are rejected.
- Resolve one immutable source/catalog/edit revision. The original bytes, XMP, and edit history remain unchanged.
- Convert mosaic sources through a minimal RAW plan containing only the exact source-normalization, highlight-reconstruction, and demosaic stages required to create a camera-correct scene-linear RGB image. Do not run exposure, tone mapping, sharpening, creative color, output profile, or the user’s full display pipeline.
- The minimal plan uses the persisted/default RAW operation parameters that would apply when the derived DNG is re-imported, except existing `rawdenoise`/AI restoration is explicitly disabled to prevent recursive denoise. The exact operation IDs/versions/parameters and disabled-node set are frozen in the request/receipt.
- Canonical inference and output color space is linear Rec.2020 D65 with straight/no alpha. Color conversion uses typed #257 transforms; missing camera/profile evidence blocks execution.
- The selected installed model must have task `RawLinearDenoise` and explicitly accept the linear Rec.2020 range/normalization, tensor layout, tile/overlap/crop, and finite-domain policy.
- Inference uses #478 deterministic tiling and provider qualification. `strength` is checked `0..=1` and blends source/model output in canonical linear Rec.2020 after tile reconstruction. Strength zero is exact minimal-pipeline output.
- Output is #497 `LinearRawRgbU16`. Quantization uses an explicit model/workflow scale and nearest-even rule; the DNG records linear Rec.2020 interpretation, white/black levels, neutral/calibration/provenance, active/default crop, orientation, and source camera identity without claiming the output is mosaic RAW.
- Default output settings are add to catalog `true`, group with source `true`, collision `unique-suffix`, and canonical output template/local destination. Import/grouping occur only after durable write and successful round-trip probe/decode.
- Repeated/restarted requests reconcile by immutable source/edit/model/minimal-plan/strength/output identity and never duplicate a committed artifact.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `RawLinearDenoiseRequest`, source/edit/model/output/import settings, `RawLinearPlan`, minimal-RAW-plan identity, progress/cancellation, derived-artifact record, and workflow receipt.
- Resolve X-Trans/linear frames and calibration through #233–#236.
- Compile and execute the minimal `rawprepare`/`highlights`/`demosaic` plan through the ordinary pixelpipe using a dedicated `AiRawLinearPreparation` mode that excludes all other operations and records exact exclusions.
- Convert the prepared output to canonical linear Rec.2020 using #257 and validate finite/range policy.
- Submit tiled inference to #478, assemble/blend output, apply strength, and quantize under a checked LinearRaw DNG range.
- Build and atomically publish #497 DNG, independently probe/decode it, then register/import and group it through revision-checked catalog services.
- Add pending-output/restart reconciliation so catalog failure does not rerun inference or duplicate files.
- Emit source/raw-plan/color/model/provider/tile/strength/DNG/import/group/output identities and bounded timings without raw paths/private metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unsupported sensor/layout, unresolved X-Trans phase/calibration, incomplete minimal pipeline, recursive denoise node, color/profile failure, incompatible/unqualified model, non-finite inference output, memory/size/quantization overflow, and destination errors before publication.
- Highlight/demosaic/color failure leaves no output; no alternate demosaic or profile is selected silently.
- `Auto` provider policy may retry the identical inference plan on CPU once. Explicit provider failure does not fallback.
- DNG round-trip failure removes temporary output and leaves catalog unchanged.
- Durable output plus catalog failure becomes a reconcilable pending artifact.
- Cancellation at any preparation/inference/write/import stage produces no partial catalog transaction.
- Foveon/other linear sources are supported only through explicit decoder/calibration fixtures; model compatibility is not inferred from three channel count alone.

## Test matrix

- X-Trans phases, compressed/uncompressed inputs, already-linear 3-channel sources, varied crop/orientation/calibration, highlights, odd/tiny/large dimensions, and unsupported layouts.
- Minimal-pipeline inclusion/exclusion, recursive-denoise prevention, deterministic demosaic/color conversion, and reimport-equivalent processing.
- Strength 0/intermediate/1, model/provider failure/fallback, tile overlaps, thread counts, memory pressure, and cancellation at every stage.
- LinearRaw DNG metadata/quantization/round trip, privacy, collision, import idempotency, grouping conflicts, and restart reconciliation.
- Differential fixtures against the pinned darktable linear RawNIND workflow when model and source identities match; otherwise independently generated model known-answer fixtures.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ai raw_linear_workflow` plus RAW/pixelpipe/color/DNG/import tests pass.
- `cargo xtask ai workflow raw-linear --fixtures all --providers qualified --round-trip-dng --verify-restart` emits complete RAW-plan, color, inference, DNG, import, grouping, and cleanup receipts.
- Strength zero equals the canonical minimal RAW pipeline before quantization, and repeated requests produce one identical derived artifact.
- No unsupported layout, missing calibration, or full display edit is silently substituted.

## Dependencies

Depends on #206, #222, #233, #234, #235, #236, #248, #257, #266, #302, #303, #363, #396, #398, #415, #478, #497, #498, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements X-Trans/already-linear RAW preparation, AI inference orchestration, LinearRaw DNG publication, import/grouping/reconciliation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement Bayer RAW, RGB denoise, super-resolution, model management UI, or AI preview UI.
issues.title: Implement AI X-Trans and linear RAW denoise to LinearRaw DNG

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the shared RGB AI processing layer used by denoise and super-resolution workflows: deterministic working-profile↔model-space conversion, bounded tiled assembly, optional deep-shadow protection, optional wide-gamut preservation, and five-band luminance-detail recovery.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement in `rusttable-ai::rgb`; this layer surrounds #478 inference but does not choose catalog photos, export formats, destinations, or import/grouping behavior.
- Input is tightly described straight-alpha/interleaved `f32` RGBA in a resolved linear working RGB profile. The model-space contract comes from the selected model manifest; the initial accepted RGB model space is gamma-encoded sRGB, planar NCHW RGB.
- Working-profile primaries/white point are converted to linear sRGB through #257/#261 transforms, then encoded with the exact extended sRGB transfer. Missing/unsupported profile evidence is blocking; gamma-only fallback is not accepted for production workflows.
- Before model transfer encoding, negative model-space values clamp to zero and values above one clamp to one. The original working-profile input remains available for explicit preservation/detail paths.
- Optional `preserve_wide_gamut` for scale-1 denoise is fixed as:
  - classify the original working pixel after conversion to linear sRGB using a manifest/default margin of `0.01` around `[0,1]`;
  - in-gamut pixels use model output converted back to the working profile;
  - out-of-gamut pixels preserve original chroma and receive only the reference-compatible local luminance adjustment from denoised in-gamut neighbors in a clamped 5×5 window;
  - if no in-gamut neighbor exists, copy the original pixel exactly.
  It is unavailable for 2×/4× super-resolution because output pixels have no one-to-one source correspondence.
- Optional `shadow_boost` is model-capability-gated and decided once per image, not per tile. Sample every 16th row and column; enable when at least 10% of sampled pixels have Rec.709 luminance below `0.005`. Pre-model boost is per-channel square root after linear sRGB conversion/clamp; inverse is squaring after model transfer decode.
- Some models may expose additional inputs such as a noise-level map. Every auxiliary tensor is fully declared by #478. The initial NIND-style default noise map is constant `25/255` only for a manifest that explicitly selects that compatibility value.
- Tiling uses the #478 model tile size/overlap/valid crop. Edge pixels use the exact mirror function, tile input is planar RGB, valid central output regions are assembled in deterministic row order, and output scale is exactly the model’s 1×/2×/4× factor.
- Provider failure before any output rows are committed may retry the identical plan once on canonical CPU under `Auto`; failure after publication starts cannot change provider and fails the operation.
- Detail recovery applies to completed 1× RGB denoise output when strength is below full and the workflow requests it. Compute Rec.709 luminance residual `original - denoised`, calculate deterministic global mean/variance in `f64`, derive five noise thresholds from sigma multipliers `[0.25, 0.15, 0.05, 0.02, 0.01]`, run the shared five-band DWT denoise, and add `alpha * filtered_residual` equally to RGB. Alpha is unchanged.
- DWT settings are versioned workflow parameters. Configuration files cannot silently alter per-band multipliers for an existing recipe.
- CPU implementation is canonical. WGPU acceleration may be added for color conversion, masks, tiling assembly, and DWT only after #447 parity; ONNX inference remains owned by #478 providers.

## Implementation

- Define immutable `RgbAiPlan`, working/model profile transforms, gamut/shadow policies, auxiliary tensor plans, tile traversal/assembly, provider retry state, `DetailRecoveryPlan`, progress, cancellation, memory estimate, and receipts.
- Reuse #257/#261 color plans with exact hashes and no native profile handles in AI code.
- Implement full-image shadow analysis and optional scale-1 gamut mask before tiling so every tile sees one frozen decision.
- Implement checked mirror padding, planar/interleaved conversion, model transfer encoding/decoding, auxiliary tensor filling, output profile conversion, gamut pass-through/luminance smoothing, and row publication.
- Implement deterministic compensated mean/variance and five-band DWT detail extraction/application with bounded full-image memory. Preview caching may retain the filtered luminance residual by immutable source/model/output identity.
- Add memory/concurrency accounting for source references, masks, tile buffers, row buffers, model output, and optional detail residual.
- Emit color/profile, gamut/shadow, tensor, tile/provider, detail thresholds/alpha, memory, and output receipts.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject unknown/singular profiles, unsupported model color space/transfer/layout, invalid tile/overlap/scale, non-finite source/model output, auxiliary tensor mismatch, size/memory overflow, and unsupported wide-gamut preservation for scale >1.
- Empty/tiny images, no deep shadows, all out-of-gamut pixels, no in-gamut neighbor, negative/HDR values, zero variance residual, and alpha values have explicit deterministic behavior.
- Cancellation before completion publishes no final buffer/detail cache. Row-streaming callers must discard the staged artifact on failure.
- Detail allocation/DWT failure cannot silently return full-strength model output for a requested partial-strength recipe; it returns a typed failure or explicit no-detail mode selected by the request.
- Provider fallback never changes color, tile, gamut, shadow, or detail policy.

## Test matrix

- Working profiles sRGB, Display P3, Rec.2020, matrix ICC, unsupported LUT/profile, singular matrices, transfer boundaries, negative/HDR values, and alpha.
- Gamut margin/classification, exact pass-through, 5×5 luminance smoothing, no-neighbor, edges/corners, and scale>1 rejection.
- Shadow sampling threshold/fraction boundaries, full-image versus tile decisions, boost/inverse known answers, and disabled-capability behavior.
- Mirror padding, tile/overlap/valid crop/2×/4× assembly, row streaming, provider retry timing, cancellation, and memory limits.
- Five-band DWT known answers, sigma multipliers, global reduction determinism, alpha/strength boundaries, preview residual cache identity, and CPU/WGPU parity if added.
- Differential fixtures against the pinned darktable RGB restore wrapper where model/profile identity matches.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ai rgb_processing` plus color/DWT/tile tests pass.
- `cargo xtask ai qualify-rgb-wrapper --profiles all --scales 1,2,4 --gamut --shadow --detail --providers qualified` emits complete deterministic receipts.
- Tile layouts and worker counts produce the same assembled image; color-profile handling never falls back to gamma-only production behavior.
- Wide-gamut preservation changes only the documented scale-1 pixels/luminance, and detail recovery is versioned and independently testable.

## Dependencies

Depends on #257, #261, #262, #266, #267, #268, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #447, #478, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements shared RGB AI color conversion, tile assembly, shadow/gamut policies, detail recovery, tests, and receipts. Do not implement catalog workflows, TIFF export, RGB denoise job orchestration, super-resolution job orchestration, or UI.
issues.title: Implement color-managed tiled RGB AI preprocessing and detail recovery

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the end-to-end AI RGB denoise workflow: render an immutable photo/edit snapshot into a linear working-profile image, run a qualified scale-1 `RgbDenoise` model through #478/#500, apply versioned detail recovery and wide-gamut policy, encode the selected color-managed TIFF artifact, and optionally import/group the derived photo.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement orchestration in `rusttable-ai::workflows::rgb_denoise`; this is a derived-output workflow and does not insert an AI node into the source edit history.
- Resolve one immutable catalog/photo/edit/source snapshot. Render the user’s complete enabled edit pipeline at requested/full dimensions through the working-profile boundary, excluding final display/export transfer, quantization, watermark/border, and destination effects unless the workflow request explicitly includes them through an export recipe.
- Canonical AI input is straight-alpha `f32` RGBA in the resolved linear working profile. The selected model must be installed/qualified with task `RgbDenoise`, scale factor 1, and a compatible #500 model-space/tile contract.
- Use #500 for working-profile↔model-space conversion, full-image shadow decision, tile traversal, optional `preserve_wide_gamut`, provider retry, and reconstruction.
- `preserve_wide_gamut` defaults to `true`. It uses #500’s scale-1 sRGB-gamut mask and luminance-only neighbor adjustment; it is part of recipe/cache identity.
- Denoise `strength` is a checked `0..=100` integer. `100` uses full model output. Values below `100` apply the versioned five-band #500 filtered luminance residual with `detail_alpha = (100-strength)/100`. This restores denoised texture detail, not unfiltered source noise; the recipe/receipt names it `detail_recovery_strength` rather than pretending it is a simple linear source blend.
- Initial DWT band multipliers are exactly `[0.25,0.15,0.05,0.02,0.01]` and are persisted in recipe version 1. User configuration cannot alter an existing recipe implicitly.
- Output format is a single-page TIFF through #402. Supported bit depths are 8-bit integer, 16-bit integer default, and 32-bit float. Alpha, profile embedding, transfer, range, compression, metadata, and byte limits are explicit encoder options.
- Output color profile defaults to the image/export recipe setting. Conversion from working profile to the selected output profile uses #348/#261; missing or unsupported profile evidence blocks output rather than substituting sRGB.
- Canonical metadata policy copies approved EXIF/IPTC/XMP fields through #413, embeds the selected ICC profile, records source/edit/model/workflow provenance, and omits private serial/GPS/maker-note fields unless explicitly allowed by the recipe.
- Default workflow settings: add to catalog `true`, group with source `true`, collision `unique-suffix`, output directory/filename through #414/#415. Import/group only after durable output and successful TIFF probe/decode.
- Batch execution is a bounded background job over independently revision-pinned requests. One photo failure does not corrupt another; final batch receipt contains per-photo outcomes.
- Repeated/restarted requests reconcile by source/edit/render/model/provider/strength/profile/encoder/destination identity and never duplicate a committed artifact.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `RgbDenoiseRequest`, render/output/model/provider/strength/gamut/metadata/import settings, `RgbDenoisePlan`, progress stages, cancellation, batch item/result, pending artifact, and workflow receipts.
- Compile/execute the source edit through the canonical pixelpipe/export render boundary into pooled linear working-profile buffers with exact render receipt/hash.
- Invoke #500 scale-1 processing and #478 inference; retain original/rendered and denoised buffers only as required by the selected detail policy and memory budget.
- Apply detail recovery, validate finite output, convert to selected output profile/transfer, and stream/encode TIFF through #402 without an additional unbounded full-file copy.
- Publish atomically through #415, independently probe/decode/profile-check the TIFF, register through #248, and group through #206 after durable publication.
- Add startup reconciliation for published-but-unregistered outputs and batch restart checkpoints.
- Emit source/edit/render/model/provider/color/gamut/shadow/tile/detail/profile/TIFF/metadata/import/group/output identities, resource peaks, and timings without absolute paths/private values.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject stale source/edit/catalog revisions, unsupported render/profile/alpha layout, recursive AI-derived input policy violation, incompatible/unqualified model, unsupported scale, non-finite model/detail output, memory/dimension/encoder overflow, and destination/template errors before final publication.
- Provider failure may retry the identical plan on CPU only under `Auto` and before output publication. Explicit provider selection fails.
- Detail recovery failure for `strength < 100` fails the request; it cannot silently switch to full denoise.
- TIFF encode/probe/profile failure removes temporary output and leaves catalog unchanged.
- Durable output plus catalog failure is a reconcilable pending artifact and does not rerun render/inference.
- Cancellation at render, inference, detail, color, encode, publication, probe, import, or grouping yields a typed stage result and no partial catalog transaction.
- Wide-gamut preservation is not silently disabled because a profile is complex; the request either has a qualified transform/policy or fails.

## Test matrix

- Identity and complex edit pipelines; sRGB/Display P3/Rec.2020/matrix/LUT working and output profiles; straight alpha; negative/HDR values; wide-gamut in/out mask boundaries; deep-shadow cases.
- Strength 0/1/50/99/100, versioned DWT settings, zero residual, high-frequency texture/noise, and deterministic worker/tile/provider behavior.
- TIFF 8/16/32-bit, profile/metadata embedding, alpha, compression, exact limits, collision, atomic failure, probe/decode round trip, and privacy.
- Single/batch success, partial batch failure, provider fallback, cancellation at every stage, memory pressure, catalog conflict, and restart reconciliation.
- Differential fixtures against the pinned darktable neural RGB denoise workflow when source/model/profile identity matches; independently generated model known answers otherwise.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ai rgb_denoise_workflow` plus render/color/TIFF/import/catalog tests pass.
- `cargo xtask ai workflow rgb-denoise --fixtures all --strengths 0,50,100 --profiles all --providers qualified --verify-batch --verify-restart` emits complete receipts.
- Identical requests produce one byte-identical TIFF and one idempotent catalog/group result.
- No gamma-only profile fallback, silent detail omission, private metadata leakage, or duplicate output remains.

## Dependencies

Depends on #206, #248, #257, #261, #266, #348, #396, #398, #402, #413, #414, #415, #478, #500, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements RGB denoise orchestration, render/inference/detail/color/TIFF publication, batch/import/group/reconciliation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement super-resolution, RAW denoise, model management UI, or AI preview UI.
issues.title: Implement AI RGB denoise to color-managed TIFF

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the end-to-end AI super-resolution workflow for edited raster/RAW photos: render a revision-pinned linear working-profile image, run a qualified 2× or 4× model through #478/#500, convert and encode the enlarged result as a color-managed TIFF, and optionally import/group the derived photo.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement orchestration in `rusttable-ai::workflows::super_resolution`; it produces a derived artifact and never changes source edit geometry or inserts an AI operation into the source history.
- Resolve one immutable photo/edit/source snapshot and render the complete enabled edit pipeline at its natural full-resolution output through the working-profile boundary. Do not apply final output scaling, output profile/quantization, watermark/border, or destination effects before inference.
- The selected installed model must declare task `SuperResolution2x` or `SuperResolution4x`, matching the requested integer scale exactly. Arbitrary/noninteger/cascaded scaling is not supported in this issue.
- Use #500 for working-profile→model-space conversion, optional per-image shadow boost, mirror-tiled inference, output assembly, and model-space→working-profile conversion.
- Wide-gamut pass-through is unavailable for super-resolution because there is no one-to-one output pixel correspondence. The workflow records this explicitly and never presents the scale-1 preservation option.
- Model input/output color, transfer, range, tile/overlap/valid crop, auxiliary tensors, and output scale come from the model package. Missing or conflicting declarations block execution.
- Output dimensions are checked exact products `source_width * scale` and `source_height * scale`. The request fails before inference when dimensions, pixel count, output bytes, TIFF classic-offset limit, provider memory, or application image limits would overflow.
- No post-model interpolation or second resampling is performed. The model produces final output dimensions; changing them belongs to an explicit later export scaling step.
- Initial recipe has no DWT detail-recovery/strength slider. Super-resolution uses full model output. A future versioned model/task may declare a separately qualified detail path, but scale-1 residual recovery is not reused by assumption.
- Output format is a single-page TIFF through #402. Supported bit depths are 8-bit, 16-bit default, and 32-bit float, with explicit alpha/profile/transfer/compression/metadata limits.
- Output profile defaults to the selected export recipe profile; conversion uses #348/#261 and embeds the exact profile. No sRGB substitution for an unresolved profile.
- Default workflow settings: add to catalog `true`, group with source `true`, collision `unique-suffix`, and canonical template/local destination. Import/group only after durable write plus successful TIFF probe/decode/profile/dimension validation.
- Batch runs are bounded and revision-pinned per photo. Restart reconciliation uses immutable source/edit/render/model/provider/scale/profile/encoder/destination identity and never duplicates a committed artifact.

## Implementation

- Define versioned `SuperResolutionRequest`, scale/model/provider/render/output/profile/metadata/import settings, `SuperResolutionPlan`, progress/cancellation, batch/pending-artifact models, and receipts.
- Compile/execute the source edit through the canonical pixelpipe/export render boundary into pooled straight-alpha linear working-profile buffers.
- Validate #478 model/task/provider and #500 scale/tile/color plan, estimate complete input/output/session/encoder memory, and reserve resources before inference.
- Stream completed output rows from #500 into color/output conversion and #402 where legal, avoiding an extra full-resolution output copy; otherwise use bounded pooled tiles/rows with explicit memory accounting.
- Publish atomically through #415, independently probe/decode/profile/dimension-check, then import through #248 and group through #206.
- Add restart reconciliation for durable-but-unregistered outputs and batch checkpoints.
- Emit source/edit/render/model/provider/scale/tile/shadow/color/profile/TIFF/metadata/import/group/output identities, output dimensions, resource peaks, and timing without paths/private metadata.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject stale revisions, unsupported render/profile/alpha, recursive derived-input policy violation, incompatible/unqualified model, scale mismatch, non-finite output, output/memory/classic-TIFF overflow, unsupported alpha/profile conversion, and destination/template errors before final publication.
- Provider failure may retry the identical plan on CPU only under `Auto` and before output rows are committed. Explicit provider selection fails.
- Streaming/encode/probe/profile/dimension failure removes temporary output and leaves catalog unchanged.
- Durable output plus catalog failure becomes a reconcilable pending artifact without rerunning inference.
- Cancellation at render, inference, row stream, color, encode, publication, probe, import, or grouping leaves no partial final/catalog transaction.
- The workflow never silently downscales, changes 4× to 2×, tiles below the model’s valid contract, enables wide-gamut pass-through, or substitutes an interpolation scaler.

## Test matrix

- 2×/4× exact dimensions, odd/tiny/large inputs, alpha, varied edit pipelines, sRGB/P3/Rec.2020/matrix/LUT profiles, negative/HDR source values, deep-shadow boost, and model auxiliary tensors.
- Tile overlap/edge mirror/valid crop/row streaming, worker counts, provider qualification/fallback, memory limits, and cancellation at every stage.
- TIFF 8/16/32-bit, profile/metadata embedding, compression, exact limits, classic-offset rejection, atomic failure, round trip, and privacy.
- Single/batch success/partial failure, collision, import idempotency, grouping conflict, and restart reconciliation.
- Differential fixtures against the pinned darktable neural upscale workflow when model/profile/source identity matches; independent known-answer super-resolution models otherwise.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ai super_resolution_workflow` plus render/color/TIFF/import/catalog tests pass.
- `cargo xtask ai workflow upscale --scales 2,4 --fixtures all --profiles all --providers qualified --verify-streaming --verify-restart` emits complete receipts.
- Every successful artifact has exact model scale dimensions, embedded selected profile, one idempotent catalog/group result, and no hidden post-model resampling.
- Requests exceeding any dimension/memory/TIFF limit fail before inference/publication.

## Dependencies

Depends on #206, #248, #257, #261, #266, #348, #396, #398, #402, #413, #414, #415, #478, #500, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements 2×/4× super-resolution orchestration, render/inference/color/TIFF publication, batch/import/group/reconciliation, tests, and receipts. Do not implement RGB/RAW denoise, arbitrary scaling, model management UI, or AI preview UI.
issues.title: Implement AI 2× and 4× super-resolution to color-managed TIFF

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add the Iced settings surface for installing, inspecting, enabling, qualifying, and removing local `.rtmodel` packages and selecting the default AI provider policy. The UI must operate exclusively through #478’s registry/service APIs and expose exact integrity/capability state without downloading models or leaking native runtime details.

## Fixed decisions

- Add an `AI Models` page under Preferences. It lists installed immutable model packages grouped by task, with model ID/version/hash, package size, tensor/tile/color summary, supported tasks/scales, enabled state, provider qualification state, runtime/model compatibility, and last bounded validation result.
- Model installation is initiated by an explicit local file picker or drag/drop of one `.rtmodel` file. There is no URL field, built-in marketplace, background download, update service, telemetry, or arbitrary archive browsing.
- The UI stages bytes through #478 validation before showing a confirmation summary. Installation is atomic and hash-addressed; an identical package is idempotent, while a different package with the same model ID/version is a conflict requiring explicit side-by-side version or replacement behavior.
- Replacement never mutates the installed hash directory. The registry activates a new immutable model identity and retains/removes the old version according to explicit user choice and active recipe references.
- Each model row exposes only providers declared by the package and available on the current platform. States are `Unavailable`, `Unqualified`, `Qualifying`, `Qualified`, `Drifted`, and `Failed`.
- `Qualify` runs #478’s bounded fixture qualification as a service job with progress/cancellation. It never qualifies against the current user photo or promotes a provider solely because a session opens.
- Global provider policy is `Auto`, `CPU`, `CoreML`, `DirectML`, or `CUDA`; only platform/qualified options are selectable. `Auto` means qualified preferred provider followed by canonical CPU retry under workflow rules.
- Per-task default model selection is persisted by exact installed model hash, not display name. Removing the selected model requires choosing another or leaving the task unavailable.
- Model removal is blocked while an active job/session owns the package. Recipes/catalog provenance retain the model hash after removal; reopening such a workflow reports `ModelUnavailable` rather than silently substituting another version.
- Native ONNX Runtime/provider errors are mapped to bounded user-safe categories with an expandable technical receipt free of paths, device serials, and private data.
- Accessibility, keyboard operation, focus order, high-DPI, localization keys, and screen-reader descriptions are complete. A color-only provider status is prohibited.

## Implementation

- Add typed view models/messages for registry snapshots, install staging/confirmation, task/model selection, provider qualification, enable/disable/remove, conflicts, progress, cancellation, and failure receipts.
- Subscribe to #478 registry/session/job events through the application service bridge; do not poll filesystem/model directories from widgets.
- Implement local file picker/drop validation, bounded package summary, explicit confirm/cancel, and immutable registry update result.
- Implement provider qualification controls and fixture/progress result presentation with generation-safe task handling.
- Persist global provider and per-task model selections through the typed configuration service with exact identity validation at startup.
- Add model storage/size reporting and an explicit cleanup flow for unreferenced disabled versions; never delete referenced/in-use packages.
- Add architecture checks forbidding UI dependencies on `ort`, ONNX Runtime native crates, archive parsers, raw filesystem traversal, or provider SDKs.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Invalid/malicious package, unsupported task/opset/operator, hash conflict, insufficient disk, registry transaction failure, in-use removal, unavailable provider, qualification drift, cancellation, and stale UI generation have distinct states.
- Closing the window does not orphan install/qualification jobs; policy explicitly cancels transient staging and leaves durable service jobs visible through global progress.
- A failed installation leaves no registry entry or extracted partial directory.
- A failed/requalified provider cannot remain selected as usable without a visible drift/failure state.
- Missing package bytes on startup mark the registry entry corrupt/unavailable and provide a safe remove/reinstall path.

## Test matrix

- Empty/multiple models/tasks/versions, install identical/conflicting/new version, enable/disable, default selection, referenced/unreferenced removal, cleanup, restart, and missing bytes.
- Every package validation failure class from #478 plus disk/transaction/cancellation/stale-result cases.
- Provider availability/qualification/drift/failure/cancel, Auto/default selection, platform-specific options, and runtime update invalidation.
- Keyboard-only and accessibility-tree tests, high-DPI, localization, long names, large package metadata, and bounded technical details.
- Architecture tests proving no native/runtime/filesystem implementation crosses into UI modules.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ui -p rusttable-app ai_model_settings` passes.
- `cargo xtask ui ai-models --presets all --verify-a11y --verify-install --verify-provider-state` emits deterministic UI/service receipts.
- A user can install a valid local package, qualify an available provider, select exact per-task defaults, restart, and remove only safe versions without any network access or silent model substitution.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #420, #478, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the AI model settings UI, configuration integration, install/qualification/removal workflows, accessibility, tests, and receipts. Do not implement restoration previews, batch processing, model downloads, or ONNX Runtime internals.
issues.title: Implement AI model installation and provider-management UI

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add the single-photo Iced preview surface for AI Bayer/linear RAW denoise, RGB denoise, and 2×/4× super-resolution. Users must be able to choose an eligible task/model/provider, inspect an accurate bounded preview against the source/current edit with synchronized zoom/pan and split comparison, and adjust versioned settings without writing files or changing the catalog.

## Fixed decisions

- Add a `Neural restore` tool available from the lighttable/darkroom context for exactly one selected photo. Multiple selection opens the later batch surface and does not run an ambiguous preview.
- Tabs are `RAW denoise`, `RGB denoise`, and `Upscale`. Eligibility is service-derived:
  - Bayer RAW → #498;
  - X-Trans/explicit linear RAW → #499;
  - any renderable photo/edit → #501 RGB denoise and #502 upscale.
  Ineligible tabs show an exact reason and no disabled-looking control that can still dispatch work.
- Model selection lists enabled installed models of the exact task from #478/#503. Provider selection is `Auto` or currently qualified provider choices. Missing model/provider state links to AI Models settings.
- Versioned preview controls are:
  - RAW denoise strength `0..100` mapped to workflow strength `0..1`;
  - RGB denoise strength `0..100`, preserve-wide-gamut default true, output preview profile inherited from the viewport;
  - Upscale factor exactly 2× or 4× with no strength/wide-gamut control.
  Model-specific arbitrary parameters are not exposed unless declared by a future reviewed UI schema.
- Preview request uses the currently visible image ROI plus required pixelpipe/model overlap and produces at most 1536×1536 displayed pixels. It is not a downsampled whole-image approximation when the user is zoomed into detail.
- For 2×/4×, the viewport can request one output-pixel-to-screen-pixel view of the selected region. Source and restored panes synchronize in source-image coordinates while accounting for the integer output scale.
- RAW preview runs the exact #498/#499 preprocessing/inference path but returns an in-memory derived preview: CFA results are demosaiced through the same canonical preview pipeline; LinearRaw results use the same profile interpretation. No DNG is written.
- RGB/upscale preview uses #500 and the same workflow settings/provider/tile plan as final processing. Preview-specific output bounds affect only requested ROI/scale and are recorded.
- Comparison modes are `Restored`, `Source/current edit`, `Side-by-side`, and draggable split. Source/restored zoom, pan, rotation/orientation, focus point, and pixel coordinate reporting remain synchronized.
- Parameter/model/provider/ROI changes debounce for 200 ms. Each request has a monotonically increasing generation, cancellation token, immutable input identity, and cache key. A stale result can never replace a newer preview.
- Preview caches are bounded by source/edit/model/provider/task/parameters/ROI/scale/profile/implementation identity. Failed/cancelled results are not cached as images.
- Preview execution is a transient application task. Closing the tool or changing selection cancels it; durable batch jobs are not owned here.
- Display conversion, soft proof, HDR, monitor profile, gamut warning, and viewport overlays remain owned by the ordinary color-managed viewport. The AI preview returns a normal render/presentation artifact, not unmanaged widget pixels.
- The UI shows progress by stage, current provider/fallback, preview-only output dimensions, approximate full-job output dimensions/memory, and bounded failure details.
- Keyboard, focus, screen-reader names/relationships, high-DPI, touch/tablet pointer, reduced motion, and localization are complete. The split handle is keyboard adjustable.

## Implementation

- Add typed preview state/messages/view models for selection eligibility, task/model/provider/settings, generation, ROI, source/restored artifacts, comparison mode, progress, cancellation, cache status, and failures.
- Add service-level preview requests/adapters over #498/#499/#501/#502 that stop before durable encoding/import and return immutable render artifacts plus workflow-compatible receipts.
- Integrate visible-ROI coordinate transforms and required overlap with the custom WGPU viewport; keep image processing out of Iced widgets/shaders.
- Implement 200 ms debounce, abortable/generation-safe task dispatch, bounded cache, stale-result rejection, provider fallback presentation, and device-loss re-presentation without rerunning inference where cached CPU image data exists.
- Implement synchronized source/restored view state and accessible split/side-by-side controls.
- Persist only user preferences (last task/comparison/preview controls), never transient source/model results or hidden workflow changes.
- Add architecture tests forbidding UI modules from invoking ONNX Runtime, RAW decoders, pixel algorithms, output writers, or catalog mutations directly.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Selection/edit/source/model/provider/profile/ROI changes invalidate the active generation and cache identity.
- Ineligible sensor/task, missing model, unqualified provider, resource estimate above preview budget, render/inference/color failure, cancellation, stale result, and viewport presentation failure have distinct recoverable states.
- Provider fallback under `Auto` is visibly recorded; explicit provider failure does not silently switch.
- A preview never writes a DNG/TIFF, creates a pending artifact, imports, groups, or changes edit history.
- If the requested visible ROI plus overlap cannot fit the preview budget, reduce the preview output resolution with an explicit quality indicator; never reduce model-required overlap or change task parameters.
- Device loss may re-present from cached render pixels or request a new presentation, but cannot duplicate inference without cache invalidation.

## Test matrix

- Eligibility across Bayer, X-Trans, LinearRaw, raster, virtual copy, missing/changed source, and multi-selection.
- Every task/model/provider/settings state; no model, unqualified/drifted provider, Auto fallback, and task switch.
- Visible ROI at fit/zoom/edge/corner, model overlap, 2×/4× coordinate synchronization, orientation, high-DPI, and viewport resize.
- Debounce/cancel/stale generation/cache hit/eviction/device loss and rapid selection/parameter changes.
- Source/restored/split/side-by-side, keyboard split adjustment, zoom/pan synchronization, accessibility tree, localization, and reduced motion.
- Service integration proving preview/final workflow settings and central ROI pixels match within #447 while preview performs no filesystem/catalog mutation.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ui -p rusttable-app ai_restore_preview` passes.
- `cargo xtask ui ai-preview --tasks all --fixtures all --providers qualified --viewports all --verify-a11y --verify-no-side-effects` emits deterministic UI/service receipts.
- Rapid changes never display stale results, source/restored coordinates remain synchronized for 1×/2×/4×, and preview produces no durable output/catalog change.
- Preview center pixels match the corresponding final workflow result under #447 for identical immutable inputs/settings.

## Dependencies

Depends on #420, #478, #498, #499, #500, #501, #502, #503, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the single-photo AI preview/comparison UI, preview service adapters, caching/generation handling, accessibility, tests, and receipts. Do not implement model installation, durable output, batch queue UI, or new AI algorithms.
issues.title: Implement AI restoration preview and synchronized comparison UI

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add the Iced batch/output surface for running RAW denoise, RGB denoise, or 2×/4× super-resolution over one or more revision-pinned photos, configuring exact model/provider/output/import/group settings, reviewing eligibility and resource estimates, and monitoring resumable per-photo jobs through completion or recovery.

## Fixed decisions

- The batch surface is launched from one or more lighttable selections or from the single-photo preview’s `Process` action. Selection is snapshotted by photo/source/edit/catalog revisions at enqueue time; later library selection changes do not alter the batch.
- One batch uses exactly one task and one versioned settings recipe:
  - Bayer/X-Trans/linear `RAW denoise` through #498/#499;
  - `RGB denoise` through #501;
  - `Upscale 2×` or `Upscale 4×` through #502.
  Mixed tasks require separate batches.
- The review table classifies every selected item `Eligible`, `Ineligible(reason)`, `Stale`, `Missing source`, or `Already committed(same identity)`. Default enqueue policy is `Process eligible and retain explicit skipped rows`; `Require all eligible` is an optional strict choice.
- Model selection is an exact installed model hash of the chosen task. Provider is `Auto` or a qualified provider. Missing/drifted selections block enqueue and link to #503.
- Task settings are exact workflow recipe fields:
  - RAW strength 0–100, add-to-catalog default true, group-with-source default true, DNG output only;
  - RGB strength 0–100, preserve-wide-gamut default true, TIFF 8/16/32 (16 default), output profile, metadata policy;
  - Upscale exact 2×/4×, TIFF 8/16/32 (16 default), output profile, metadata policy.
- Shared output settings use #414 filename template, #415 local destination, explicit collision policy, and per-photo estimated filename/output dimensions/bytes. No hidden defaults are taken from an unrelated previous export unless the user selects a saved AI recipe.
- A preflight summary shows eligible/skipped count, selected model/provider, output directory alias/template, expected format/dimensions, worst-case host/native/GPU memory, disk estimate, grouping/import policy, and warnings. Enqueue requires explicit confirmation.
- Jobs are durable and per-photo. The UI observes the workflow queue/state records created by #498/#499/#501/#502 and their shared scheduler/lifecycle services; widgets do not own threads, files, model sessions, or catalog transactions.
- Batch controls are pause new work, resume, cancel pending/running, retry selected failed/reconcilable items, and remove completed history. Cancellation follows each workflow’s safe stage semantics; it never deletes a previously committed artifact.
- Progress is hierarchical: aggregate batch plus per-photo current stage, provider/fallback, tiles/items, output state, import/group state, elapsed/remaining work estimate, and bounded failure/reconciliation action.
- Completion rows provide `Open photo`, `Reveal output`, `View receipt`, and `Retry/reconcile` only when the underlying result supports the action. Paths are shown through user-facing aliases and never included in diagnostic JSON.
- Saved AI recipes persist exact task/model/settings/output policy and schema. If the exact model is missing/drifted, loading the recipe is blocking and never substitutes the current default model.
- Closing ordinary windows does not abandon durable work under #420 daemon policy. Global progress continues to expose the batch.
- Accessibility, keyboard operation, focus order, sortable status table, screen-reader live progress, high-DPI, localization, and reduced motion are complete.

## Implementation

- Add typed batch creation/review/confirmation state, per-item eligibility, task-specific recipe editors, output preview, resource estimate, queue submission, progress/history/recovery view models, and messages.
- Call service eligibility/preflight APIs for all selected photos with bounded parallelism and generation-safe cancellation.
- Integrate exact model/provider state from #478/#503, output template/destination/profile/metadata controls, and task-specific settings without duplicating workflow validation in UI code.
- Submit one immutable batch plus item requests to the durable workflow service; store returned batch/job IDs and subscribe to bounded event streams with coalescing/backpressure.
- Implement pause/resume/cancel/retry/reconcile/remove-history commands through service ports and reflect idempotent results.
- Present output aliases, receipts, import/group results, and actionable failure categories without exposing secrets/private metadata/native handles.
- Add architecture checks forbidding UI modules from direct filesystem, encoder, catalog, ONNX Runtime, or process access.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Empty selection, mixed/ineligible sensor/task, stale revisions, missing model/provider, invalid output recipe, insufficient disk/memory, duplicate committed identity, queue transaction failure, cancellation, and service restart have explicit states.
- Strict all-eligible mode enqueues nothing when any row is ineligible. Default mode records skipped rows in the batch receipt.
- If a source/edit changes after enqueue, that item fails stale; the batch cannot silently use new state. A user-triggered `Recreate request from current state` creates a new item identity.
- A provider fallback is shown per item and does not rewrite the saved provider choice.
- Restart reloads batch/item state and offers reconciliation for durable outputs missing catalog commit. It does not rerun completed inference automatically when artifact identity is available.
- UI cancellation/stale messages cannot mark service jobs cancelled unless the service confirms the transition.

## Test matrix

- Single/multiple selections; Bayer/X-Trans/linear/raster mixtures; eligible/ineligible/stale/missing/already-committed rows; default/strict policy.
- Every task/model/provider/settings/output/profile/metadata/import/group combination and saved recipe load with exact/missing/drifted model.
- Filename/output estimate/collision/disk/memory preflight, confirmation/cancel, queue submission failure, and duplicate idempotency.
- Aggregate/per-item progress, pause/resume/cancel/retry/reconcile, partial success, fallback, restart, window closure, and history cleanup.
- Keyboard/a11y/live-region, high-DPI, localization, large batches with virtualization/backpressure, and bounded technical receipt rendering.
- Architecture and side-effect tests proving the UI dispatches only typed service commands.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ui -p rusttable-app ai_restore_batch` passes.
- `cargo xtask ui ai-batch --tasks all --mixtures all --failures all --restart --verify-a11y --verify-backpressure` emits deterministic UI/service receipts.
- A mixed selection can be reviewed, eligible rows processed through exact recipes, failures retried/reconciled after restart, and committed outputs opened without duplicate artifacts/catalog records.
- No UI code performs inference, encoding, filesystem publication, or catalog grouping directly.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #206, #248, #398, #414, #415, #420, #478, #498, #499, #501, #502, #503, #504, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements AI batch preflight/recipe/output/progress/recovery UI, service integration, accessibility, tests, and receipts. Do not implement AI algorithms, model installation, single-photo preview processing, or new encoders/destinations.
issues.title: Implement AI restoration batch and output workflow UI

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the secure service boundary for sending an immutable RustTable photo/edit to a user-configured external image application, waiting for one explicit interchange file result, validating it, durably importing it as a derived photo, and grouping it with the source without shell execution or source-file mutation.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement service logic in `rusttable-external-editor`; executable/process plumbing delegates to #452 and rendering/encoding/import/catalog behavior delegates to existing typed services.
- An `ExternalEditorPreset` contains stable preset ID/name, exact executable location/identity, platform availability, argv tokens, wait contract, interchange mode, output profile/bit depth, metadata policy, import/group defaults, and schema version.
- Executables are selected by explicit absolute file/app-bundle picker and stored with platform identity:
  - macOS bundle ID plus resolved executable file identity;
  - Windows executable path plus file identity;
  - Linux executable path plus inode/content identity.
  PATH-only names are prohibited. Executable identity drift requires re-approval before launch.
- Arguments are an ordered array, never a shell string. The only placeholders are `{input}`, `{output}`, and optional `{xmp}` as entire argument values. No quoting language, variable expansion, globbing, pipes, redirects, command substitution, nested placeholders, or arbitrary environment expansion is supported.
- Initial wait contract is `OwnedChildUntilExit`. A preset must launch a process that remains owned until the user finishes/saves. Applications that immediately hand off to an existing detached process are unsupported unless their explicitly configured arguments provide a blocking/new-instance mode.
- Interchange modes are exactly:
  - `InPlaceTiff`: one staged TIFF path is passed as `{input}`; the app must atomically or safely replace its bytes before exit;
  - `SeparateOutputTiff`: immutable staged input and nonexistent staged output are passed separately; the app must create exactly `{output}`.
  Directory scanning, “newest file,” stdout image bytes, arbitrary extension choice, and multiple outputs are unsupported.
- Interchange is single-page TIFF only through #402. Presets choose `Tiff16Integer` default or `Tiff32Float`; the selected working/export ICC profile is embedded. JPEG/PNG/XCF/PSD and external-tool-specific formats are outside this service contract.
- The source/current edit is rendered through a revision-pinned export snapshot into the chosen profile/bit depth with orientation applied. The original source path is never passed by default and is never writable by the external process.
- A per-invocation managed directory contains only staged input, optional canonical XMP sidecar, declared output, and a private manifest inaccessible through argv. It has user-only permissions, no symlinks, bounded quota, and a random nonsecret directory name.
- The process environment is a minimal allowlist. It receives no RustTable database path, tokens, model/provider variables, source directory, or arbitrary inherited secrets. Working directory is the managed invocation directory.
- Before launch, record exact input/output manifest, hashes, sizes, profile, dimensions, metadata policy, source/edit revision, executable/preset identity, and invocation ID.
- On successful child exit:
  - `InPlaceTiff` requires the staged TIFF hash to change and remain a regular contained file;
  - `SeparateOutputTiff` requires the exact output path to exist and input hash to remain unchanged;
  - validate size/signature/TIFF structure/page count/sample type/dimensions/profile/alpha/metadata limits and decode it independently;
  - publish a durable derived artifact to the selected destination, import it, and optionally group with source.
- Geometry changes are allowed and recorded. Missing/unsupported output ICC profile is blocking; no silent source-profile assumption.
- Default import/group settings are true/true. The derived photo retains source/executable/preset/interchange/profile/output provenance by stable hashes, not private paths.
- Cancellation before launch removes staging. During execution it requests graceful child-group termination, then force-kills under #452; it never deletes a durable result already committed.
- This service has no built-in GIMP/G’MIC/ImageMagick/GraphicsMagick presets and does not claim compatibility with an app until a user preset passes the service’s local test round trip.

## Implementation

- Define versioned preset/invocation/render/interchange/process/output/import/group/reconciliation DTOs, commands, progress events, errors, and receipts.
- Validate executable identity and argv placeholder grammar before saving or launching a preset.
- Create/permission/quota-check managed staging, render and encode TIFF/XMP inputs through canonical services, and generate a content-addressed invocation manifest.
- Spawn through #452 with explicit argv/environment/cwd and complete process-tree ownership; stream bounded progress/status without interpreting arbitrary stdout as protocol.
- Validate post-exit file identity/containment/change semantics, probe/decode/profile/metadata, and construct the final durable artifact through #415.
- Register through #248 and group through #206 after durable publication with revision checks.
- Persist pending invocation state before launch and reconcile on restart:
  - child still owned/running;
  - child ended with valid staged output;
  - durable output pending import/group;
  - invalid/abandoned staging cleanup.
- Emit preset/executable/render/process/interchange/output/profile/metadata/import/group/cleanup identities and bounded diagnostics with path aliases only.
- Add policy integration proving known external image commands can be spawned only from this crate/service.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject executable/identity drift, invalid placeholders/argv, detached/nonowned process behavior, staging escape/symlink, quota/disk failure, stale source/edit, render/encode failure, nonzero exit, timeout, cancellation, unchanged in-place input, missing output, modified immutable input, extra/multiple outputs where prohibited, invalid TIFF/profile/metadata, size overflow, and catalog conflict with typed stages.
- Child success does not imply artifact success; validation/import must complete.
- A valid staged output after a crash is reconciled and never rerun automatically without checking invocation identity.
- Process output/logs are bounded and redacted. External app stdout/stderr cannot inject receipt fields.
- Final destination collision follows explicit policy and never overwrites the original or an unrelated file.
- Cleanup never follows symlinks or removes paths outside the managed invocation root.

## Test matrix

- Preset/argv grammar, placeholders, executable identity/updates, platform path/bundle behavior, environment allowlist, and prohibited shell syntax.
- In-place unchanged/changed/invalid, separate output success/missing/input-modified, geometry/profile/bit-depth/alpha/metadata variations, size/page/format violations, and symlink/path attacks.
- Fake blocking editors for success, nonzero exit, timeout, detach, child/grandchild, stdout/stderr flood, cancellation, and process leak on Linux/macOS/Windows.
- Render/TIFF/XMP/privacy, destination collision, durable publish, import/group, catalog conflicts, crash/restart reconciliation, and idempotent retry.
- Source-policy tests for direct external image command invocation outside the service.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-external-editor` plus process/render/TIFF/import/catalog suites pass on all supported platforms.
- `cargo xtask external-editor qualify --presets fixtures --modes in-place,separate --failures all --restart` emits process, containment, TIFF, profile, metadata, import, grouping, and cleanup receipts.
- A qualified blocking fake editor can modify/create a TIFF, exit, and produce one durable imported/grouped result; cancellation/crash leaves no source mutation, leaked process, duplicate output, or escaped staging.
- No shell or PATH-only execution path exists.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #206, #248, #261, #396, #398, #402, #413, #414, #415, #452, #479, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the managed external-editor service, preset/invocation contracts, staging/process/output/import/group/reconciliation behavior, policy checks, tests, and receipts. Do not implement UI, app-specific presets, additional interchange formats, or internal external-tool fallbacks.
issues.title: Implement managed external-editor round-trip service

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add the Iced configuration and invocation surfaces for #506: users can define/test secure external-editor presets, choose a preset for one or more photos, review interchange/output behavior, launch the managed round trip, and monitor/reconcile the resulting derived artifacts without editing shell commands or exposing source files.

## Fixed decisions

- Add `External editors` under Preferences and `Open with external editor…` actions in lighttable/darkroom context menus and keyboard command search.
- Preset editor fields are name, executable picker, platform identity/status, wait mode fixed to `Owned child until exit`, interchange mode `In-place TIFF` or `Separate output TIFF`, ordered argument rows, optional XMP toggle, TIFF bit depth 16/32f, profile, metadata policy, add-to-catalog, group-with-source, destination/template, and enabled state.
- Arguments are edited as individual rows. A row can be literal text or exactly one placeholder selected from `{input}`, `{output}`, `{xmp}`. The UI never accepts a free-form shell command, quotes/escapes, environment syntax, pipes, or redirects.
- The executable is chosen through the platform file/app picker. The UI displays stored versus current executable identity and requires explicit re-approval after drift.
- `Test preset` uses a generated privacy-safe color/profile TIFF fixture, runs the complete #506 round trip, validates the result, and records `Qualified`, `Failed`, or `Drifted` for the exact preset/executable identity. A preset can still be saved unqualified but cannot be invoked on user photos until it passes.
- RustTable ships no built-in GIMP, G’MIC, ImageMagick, or GraphicsMagick preset. Help text describes the blocking-process requirement generically; users provide application-specific arguments.
- Single-photo launch shows exact source/edit revision, preset/executable, interchange/profile/bit depth, destination filename, metadata/import/group policy, and expected temporary/final storage before confirmation.
- Multi-photo launch creates one independently revision-pinned invocation per photo with bounded concurrency. One external process is launched at a time by default; users may set a reviewed maximum no greater than the service policy. A failure does not corrupt other items.
- The UI observes #506 progress/state events: rendering, staged, external app running, validating, publishing, importing, grouping, reconciling, completed, cancelled, or failed.
- During the external process stage, the UI explains that completion requires saving and closing the configured child process. It exposes cancel/terminate through #506; it never manipulates OS processes directly.
- Completion actions are open derived photo, reveal output, view bounded receipt, retry validation/import reconciliation, or clean abandoned staging where the service permits.
- Saved preset identity is used by receipts and recipes. Renaming a preset does not alter prior provenance; changing executable/arguments/interchange settings creates a new preset revision/hash.
- Accessibility, keyboard operation, focus order, screen-reader status/live regions, high-DPI, localization, and long path/name truncation with safe full-value disclosure are complete.

## Implementation

- Add typed preset-list/editor/test/invocation-review/progress/history/reconciliation view models and messages.
- Integrate executable/app pickers, identity validation, argv row/placeholder editor, profile/metadata/destination/template controls, and exact service validation without duplicating #506 logic in widgets.
- Implement test-preset workflow and qualification receipt presentation.
- Implement single/multi selection eligibility, immutable request review, explicit confirmation, bounded enqueue, progress subscription, cancel/retry/reconcile, and completion actions through service ports.
- Persist preset revisions through typed configuration/catalog storage with deterministic ordering and no secret/native handle fields.
- Add architecture checks forbidding UI modules from spawning processes, reading/writing interchange files, rendering/encoding, or mutating catalog/groups directly.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing/drifted executable, invalid argument placeholder, unqualified preset, unsupported profile/bit depth, stale source/edit, insufficient staging/destination space, service/job failure, cancellation, restart, and detached child behavior have explicit actionable UI states.
- An unqualified/drifted preset cannot launch on user photos. Qualification of one revision/executable identity does not transfer to another.
- Closing the window while an owned invocation runs follows the explicit durable-job/daemon policy and leaves progress available globally; it does not orphan the process.
- Multi-photo strict mode enqueues nothing if any row is ineligible; default mode records skipped rows explicitly.
- UI history never exposes raw command arguments containing private paths; placeholders and aliases are shown instead.

## Test matrix

- Create/edit/duplicate/rename/enable/disable/delete preset, executable drift/reapproval, literal/placeholder rows, both interchange modes, XMP/profile/bit depth/metadata/import/group/destination settings.
- Test-preset success and every #506 failure class, qualification invalidation, cancellation, and restart.
- Single/multi selection, stale/missing source, strict/default eligibility, bounded serial/concurrent invocation, partial success, cancel/retry/reconcile, and completion actions.
- Keyboard/a11y/live regions, high-DPI, localization, long values, large preset lists, and bounded event backpressure.
- Architecture/side-effect tests proving all process/filesystem/render/catalog work remains in services.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-ui -p rusttable-app external_editor_ui` passes.
- `cargo xtask ui external-editors --presets all --modes all --failures all --multi --restart --verify-a11y` emits deterministic UI/service receipts.
- A user can define and qualify a blocking fake editor, send one or more photos, cancel/reconcile, and open one imported/grouped result per successful invocation without shell syntax or source mutation.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #206, #248, #414, #415, #420, #479, #506, and #496.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements external-editor preset/test/send-to/progress/reconciliation UI, configuration integration, accessibility, tests, and receipts. Do not implement process execution, TIFF interchange internals, app-specific presets, or additional formats.
issues.title: Implement external-editor presets and send-to workflow UI

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the platform credential service required by Piwigo and future authenticated integrations. Secrets must be stored only in the operating system’s protected credential facility, referenced by opaque IDs from RustTable configuration/recipes, handled in zeroizing memory, and never fall back to plaintext files, environment variables, logs, or catalog records.

## Capabilities

- `build-option.USE_LIBSECRET` — role `redesigned-platform-service`
- `build-option.USE_KWALLET` — role `redesigned-platform-service`

## Fixed decisions

- Add safe product crate `rusttable-credentials` containing opaque identifiers, account metadata, service port, policy, typed failures, redacted receipts, and an in-memory test adapter.
- Platform implementations are isolated in approved adapters:
  - macOS: Keychain Services/Security.framework;
  - Windows: Windows Credential Manager;
  - Linux: freedesktop Secret Service over D-Bus using the pinned pure-Rust zbus/secret-service stack.
- RustTable does not implement a direct KWallet API. KDE environments are supported when their wallet exposes the standard Secret Service interface. Absence of Secret Service is a typed unavailable capability.
- There is no encrypted-file or plaintext fallback. When the platform vault is unavailable/locked/denied, authenticated integrations are disabled or may use an explicitly ephemeral in-memory secret for the current operation only; ephemeral secrets are never persisted.
- A durable `CredentialRef` contains version, provider/service namespace, account ID, and opaque platform item identity. It contains no username/password/token/cookie bytes except optional nonsecret account display metadata.
- Secret material is exposed to callers only through a scoped callback/lease that yields a non-serializable, non-cloneable zeroizing buffer. Callers cannot retain a reference after the lease ends.
- Secret types implement redacted `Debug`/display behavior and do not implement `Serialize`. Errors/receipts contain operation/provider/account aliases and platform status classes only.
- Supported operations are create, replace, read lease, delete, metadata lookup, and bounded account listing by RustTable service namespace. RustTable never enumerates unrelated vault items.
- Writes are idempotent by `(service, account ID)` and use platform replace semantics. Deleting a credential referenced by an active integration requires an explicit reference check/confirmation at the application layer.
- Vault prompts/unlock/consent are initiated only by explicit user actions or an already-started authenticated job. Background indexing/startup never causes an unexpected system credential prompt.
- Access is serialized/bounded per platform where required and owned by #180 task groups. UI threads never block on vault calls.
- Native handles/pointers remain inside adapters. Any unsafe FFI is minimal, documented, tested, and prohibited from other crates by #168/#453.
- `USE_LIBSECRET` and `USE_KWALLET` are removed as compile-time product options. Supported platform adapters are selected by target and report runtime availability.

## Implementation

- Define `CredentialRef`, `CredentialAccount`, `SecretLease`, `CredentialRequest`, `CredentialStore` port, availability/capability snapshot, operation results, redaction, cancellation, and receipts.
- Implement platform adapter selection, target compilation, runtime availability/probe, create/read/replace/delete/list, prompt classification, and cleanup.
- Use stable RustTable service names and opaque account IDs. Normalize/limit nonsecret labels and never use user-entered text as a platform query expression without escaping.
- Integrate configuration/recipe serialization using `CredentialRef` only and add source/privacy policy detecting secret-like fields in config/catalog/receipts.
- Add a deterministic fake vault and platform contract test harness. Hosted tests use isolated temporary test namespaces and remove every created item.
- Add application service methods for ephemeral secret use and credential reference validation without introducing account-specific Piwigo logic.
- Emit availability, provider, operation, reference/account alias, prompt/result class, and duration receipts with no secret values or platform item paths.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Distinguish unavailable service, locked vault, user denied/cancelled prompt, item missing, duplicate/conflict, permission, corrupt platform response, timeout/cancellation, adapter/native failure, and delete-in-use policy failures.
- A read failure cannot return partial secret bytes. Failed writes do not update the durable `CredentialRef` owner configuration.
