- Intentional open research/qualification is allowed only through an explicit `Qualification decision` record naming hypotheses/candidates, benchmark/corpus, decision rule, bounded output, and follow-up owner. Generic “investigate and decide” does not pass.
- Corrective issues include an `Observed defects` section identifying current evidence and the predecessor issue/PR.
- Issue bodies use GitHub issue numbers, priority labels, and milestones. Numeric sequence tokens are invalid.
- Add a versioned committed issue snapshot and `quality/issue-spec-policy.toml`. Live GitHub refresh is an explicit authenticated operation; PR checks consume the reviewed snapshot and fail when the referenced issue body/hash differs.
- Specification status is a typed value in the snapshot/queue receipt: `ReadyV2`, `ReadyLegacyEquivalent`, `NeedsDecisions`, `NeedsFailureContract`, `NeedsTests`, `NeedsAcceptanceEvidence`, `NeedsDependencies`, `NeedsOnePrBoundary`, `DuplicateOrSuperseded`, or `Invalid`. #555 adds source-specific statuses to the successor schema.
- No separate permanent manual status label is required. Priority labels remain the scheduling labels. The ready-queue receipt and PR verifier carry specification status, avoiding another mutable source of truth.
- The completed migration updates the semantic examples/snapshot selected for #518. Broader body/source migration remains reviewed successor work rather than title-derived mass mutation.
- Closed completed issues are audited only when their receipts feed a current gate or an observed implementation defect exists. They are annotated with a `Post-completion audit` and a successor correction rather than reopened cosmetically.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` governance/tooling with cross-cutting source-accounting context. The semantic issue-spec parser itself has no darktable runtime equivalent. The following pinned sources explain why #555 must extend this contract with class-specific source responsibilities:

- `src/common/darktable.c` / `dt_init`, `dt_cleanup`: global initialization and subsystem ordering that foundation issues must account for.
- `src/develop/imageop.c`, `src/develop/imageop.h`, `src/iop/iop_api.h`: operation registration/callback responsibilities used to validate operation-issue completeness.
- `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c`, `src/develop/tiling.c`, `src/develop/blend.c`, `src/develop/masks/*`: cross-cutting execution responsibilities that must not disappear between narrowly scoped issues.
- `src/imageio/format/*`, `src/imageio/storage/*`, `src/imageio/imageio_module.c`: format/storage ownership used to validate codec/export issue splits.
- `src/views/*`, `src/libs/*`, `src/bauhaus/*`, `src/dtgtk/*`, `src/gui/*`: UI/action responsibilities used to validate Iced issue coverage.

**Preserve:** one semantically complete, measurable, dependency-correct issue contract for every selected PR.

**Redesign:** replace informal backlog prose and title/sequence heuristics with typed roles, canonical hashes, deterministic findings, reviewed snapshots, and conflict-safe apply plans.

**Excluded from completed #518:** canonical source-tree inventory, exact path/symbol validation, source-map generation/drift, source-specific readiness statuses, and mass promotion of source blocks. Those are the focused successor #555, which depends on this completed semantic foundation.

## Implementation

- Define issue snapshot/spec/policy/status/finding/remediation models with canonical hashing and bounded diagnostics.
- Implement `cargo xtask github refresh-issue-spec-snapshot`, `verify-issue-specs`, and `ready-issues` integration.
- Parse child issue bodies and semantic roles, capability metadata, dependencies, one-PR boundary, observed/post-completion audit, and decision-quality patterns.
- Validate references against the #446 issue/replacement index, priorities, milestones, capability index, and open PR claims.
- Add issue-class policies for foundation, domain/service, image operation, codec, export destination, platform adapter, UI, scripting/extension, corrective, qualification, and release/package work. Each class declares additional required decisions, tests, and evidence.
- Detect copied generic bodies across unrelated capabilities using normalized section hashes and repeated boilerplate thresholds. Exact generic placeholders fail.
- Produce a deterministic audit artifact listing issue number, class, priority, dependencies/readiness, spec status, exact findings, and a generated remediation checklist. Do not auto-generate technical decisions that require source/repository analysis.
- Add an authenticated idempotent apply command that updates only issues with an explicit reviewed replacement body/hash and refuses GitHub writes when current body/labels/milestone changed since the plan.
- Integrate the semantic gate into the PR contract: a PR cannot close an issue whose reviewed snapshot/body hash is not semantically ready.
- Integrate with #492 so generated capability issues cannot be created unless their proposed body passes the same semantic validator before mutation.
- Include representative complete v2/legacy-equivalent and deficient fixtures.

## Failure and edge behavior

- GitHub/snapshot drift, malformed Markdown, duplicate headings with conflicting content, literal escaped newlines, missing/unknown/superseded references, dependency cycles, multiple capability owners without roles, unresolved decisions, copied boilerplate, incomplete class-specific contracts, or broad incoherent scope fail readiness.
- The validator must not reject descriptive prose merely for containing words like “may” when the behavior is already fixed; decision-pattern findings are semantic and fixture-tested, not a naive grep alone.
- Legacy-equivalent mapping is fail-closed: absence of one required semantic role produces the exact missing status rather than guessing content from unrelated sections.
- A live refresh/network failure cannot overwrite the committed snapshot or change ready status.
- Apply conflicts produce no issue mutation and a deterministic re-review requirement.
- An issue becoming semantically ready does not bypass dependencies, priority, milestone, foundation gate, future source gate, or claimed-PR rules.

## Test matrix

- Complete v2 and legacy-equivalent foundation/service/operation/codec/destination/platform/UI/corrective/qualification issues.
- Missing/empty/generic fixed decisions, unresolved choices, algorithm/default/framework omissions, incomplete failure/cancellation/restart/platform behavior, vague tests/evidence, unknown/superseded dependencies, and broad multi-PR scope.
- Copied placeholder bodies, different legitimate issues sharing standard wording, escaped-newline metadata, malformed capabilities, duplicate headings, and title-sequence regression.
- Snapshot refresh/drift/network failure, apply conflict/idempotency, PR body-hash mismatch, and ready-queue integration.
- Integration with #446 ready order, #492 issue generation, #496 foundation dependencies, and open PR claims.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo xtask github verify-issue-specs` produces a deterministic complete semantic audit of the reviewed issue snapshot.
- `cargo xtask github ready-issues` excludes every semantically deficient issue with exact findings and includes valid v2/legacy-equivalent issues without requiring cosmetic heading rewrites.
- A generated generic capability issue cannot be created, and a PR cannot close an issue after its reviewed body has drifted.
- Re-running the reviewed semantic migration/apply plan proposes zero mutations.

## Post-completion audit

PR #524 completed the semantic v2 parser/policy/snapshot/ready/apply contract. It did **not** implement the canonical darktable source inventory, exact path/symbol validation, source-map body hashes, source drift reporting, or full backlog source-block migration. Those were identified after completion and are owned by #555.

#555 is therefore a successor that depends on completed #518. This issue must not declare #555 as a dependency, and no readiness tooling should construct a #518 ↔ #555 cycle. #555 may extend the schema/status set and integrate source readiness while preserving #518’s semantic body hashes and conflict-safe apply model.

## Dependencies

Depends on #446, #492, and #515. Source-map integration successor: #555.

## One-PR boundary

The completed PR implements semantic issue-spec schemas/parser/policy, snapshot/refresh/audit/ready/PR integration, reviewed migration application, fixtures, and tests. Canonical darktable source inventory/mapping and source-specific issue migration are explicitly successor #555 work.

## Outcome

Restore the pre-commit shift-left gate so its declared parallel checks complete normally within the 30-second budget instead of being spuriously cancelled after roughly two seconds.

## Observed failure

On the #455 worktree,  starts independent lanes successfully, then reports  for  and cancels the remaining Rust/policy lanes. The cancelled processes have no command failure or output, so the gate cannot provide valid pass/fail evidence.

## Scope

- Trace the interaction between the validation scheduler, shared process service, output readers, resource groups, cancellation, and post-exit drain deadline.
- Fix the owned-process/pipe-drain behavior without weakening command, cleanup, timeout, network, or receipt policy.
- Keep independent checks parallel and retain the 30-second pre-commit budget.
- Add focused regression tests covering successful quiet commands, parallel waves, cancellation, and post-exit drain behavior.
- Prove the complete pre-commit command passes on a warm developer machine; do not mask the failure by serializing all checks or suppressing receipts.

## Acceptance evidence

-  passes repeatedly with all declared error-severity checks completing normally.
- No successful/quiet child is reported as a drain timeout or spuriously cancelled.
- The pre-commit receipt preserves typed failures, bounded output, full child cleanup, and the 30-second budget.
- Regression tests exercise the previous cancellation/drain sequence.

## One-PR boundary

One focused PR fixes validation/process scheduling only. Do not change product behavior, relax validation policy, or fold unrelated benchmark work into this PR.

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Replace the shell’s placeholder photo workspace with the first real Iced lighttable/preview surface backed by persisted RustTable catalog state and the production preview service from #181. A user can launch RustTable, see actual catalog photos, select one, and view its rendered current edit with explicit loading, progress, empty, failure, retry, cancellation, and stale-result behavior. This issue migrates a bounded, usable slice of darktable’s lighttable plus selected-image preview responsibilities; it is not a generic gallery invented independently of the source application.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement composition/presentation adapters in `rusttable-app` and reusable view models/widgets in `rusttable-ui`; catalog/image/processing/render types remain below the UI boundary.
- Initial layout is fixed: existing left navigation; bounded browser of up to 200 catalog items with selection, safe filename alias, dimensions, rating when available, thumbnail/placeholder; main current-edit preview; compact catalog/render status strip.
- Use catalog query ports and exactly the #181 preview path. UI code may not read redb, open files, invoke image decoders, construct operations, or use fixture helpers.
- Selection identity is photo ID/revision + edit ID/revision. Every selection/catalog revision increments a render generation.
- Selected preview max is 1536×1536, aspect-preserving, no upscale, current persisted edit, canonical CPU processing.
- Presentation uses #420’s Iced boundary: immutable RGBA8 upload/reuse through custom WGPU presentation when available, otherwise the bounded Iced raster handle, otherwise typed presentation-unavailable while catalog remains usable. Display payload is sRGB; no ICC/HDR/soft-proof claim.
- Keep the last successful preview visible while a newer same-photo generation renders. Never publish stale generations or another photo’s prior pixels.
- Keyboard: Up/Down selection, Enter preview focus, Escape browser focus, `[`/`]` previous/next, Tab order sidebar→browser→preview→status actions.
- Stable accessible labels/selection/progress/failure relationships and focus restoration are mandatory.
- No edit controls, import/export dialog, persistent thumbnail cache, full lighttable virtualization, zoom/pan, masks, scopes, or darkroom module panels.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

- `src/views/lighttable.c` — `DT_MODULE(1)`: lighttable view-module registration; bounded browser and selection presentation context.
- `src/views/darkroom.c` — `DT_MODULE(1)`: darkroom view-module registration; selected developed-preview presentation context.
- `src/common/selection.c` — `dt_selection_select_single(dt_selection_t *selection, const dt_imgid_t imgid)`: single selected-image transition.

**Preserve:** ordered catalog browsing, visible selection, selected-image/current-history preview, previous/next keyboard navigation, asynchronous thumbnails/previews, explicit empty/loading/failure states, collection refresh, stale-result rejection, and source-unavailable behavior.

**Redesign:** replace GTK drawing areas, global selection/view proxies, mipmap callbacks, direct develop access, and imperative redraw with immutable catalog pages, generation-tagged service tasks, Iced view state, bounded content-hash thumbnails, safe preview payloads, and explicit accessibility.

**Excluded:** culling, zoomable lighttable, range/multi-selection, ratings editing, grouping, filters/collections UI, filmstrip layout, full darkroom, scopes, overlays, persistent caches, and color-managed display. Each source module/action remains mapped to a later issue.

Rust ownership: browser/query/presentation state in app/ui; catalog domain/query in catalog; preview processing in render/app service; no persistence or image processing logic in widgets.

## Implementation

- Add `PhotoBrowserService`/query adapter returning a bounded immutable page with stable photo/edit revisions and safe labels.
- Add presentation state for page, selected identity, render generation, request lifecycle, last success, progress, failure/retry, and catalog refresh.
- Invoke the real preview service through #180 task groups and generation-tagged messages.
- Initial thumbnail behavior: reuse exact identity result; otherwise max 256×256 CPU request; max four visible concurrent; cancel out-of-page work; bounded in-memory hash cache only.
- Add reusable Iced photo rows/cards, preview surface, loading/progress/failure overlays, and empty state using current design tokens.
- Reconcile catalog refresh by requesting a new page and retaining selection only when exact photo remains; do not create a competing event system.
- Record bounded workspace receipt with aliases/generation/hash/dimensions/presentation/timing/cancellation/stale counts.
- Add architecture checks preventing UI/app presentation from redb/filesystem/decoders/processing constructors/fixtures.
- Add a source-action accounting table for the bounded lighttable/darkroom/selection/mipmap/filmstrip responsibilities and explicit later owners for every excluded action.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle unavailable/corrupt/empty catalog, query failure, missing photo/edit, source changed/missing, unsupported decode/operation, render failure, invalid payload, presentation unavailable/device loss, cancellation, and shutdown as typed states.
- Thumbnail failure affects only its item and supports retry.
- Selected-preview failure keeps prior preview only for the same photo; otherwise no unrelated pixels remain.
- Rapid selection/refresh/window close/shutdown cancel owned work and reject late publication.
- Validate payload dimensions/length/hash before Iced/GPU resource creation.
- UI failure never mutates catalog/source.

## Test matrix

- Loading/empty/one/many/200 bound, deterministic order, selection retention/removal.
- Real #181 catalog/PNG/edit shown through presentation model.
- Rapid navigation/out-of-order completion/stale rejection/retry/cancel/shutdown.
- Thumbnail concurrency/cancel/cache/eviction/failure/selected priority.
- Missing source, corrupt input, unsupported edit, catalog/render/payload/presentation/device-loss failure and recovery.
- Keyboard/focus/screen-reader/reduced-motion/high-DPI/resize/theme.
- Source-map coverage for lighttable/darkroom/collection/selection/mipmap/filmstrip/actions and explicit excluded-owner checks.
- Architecture/privacy/path-independent receipt tests.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-app -p rusttable-ui workspace_preview` passes with real #181 state/service.
- `cargo xtask ui-workspace --fixture corpus.raster.png.16-alpha --verify-real-preview --verify-stale-results --verify-a11y` emits complete receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 535` proves the bounded upstream lighttable/preview responsibilities and later delegations.
- Launching against the test catalog displays actual processed pixels; stale generations never replace active selection; no duplicate catalog/decode/processing path exists in UI.

## Dependencies

Depends on #181, #420, #180, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements bounded real catalog browsing, selected preview presentation, UI tasks/state, in-memory thumbnails, accessibility, source-accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement edit controls, import/export, persistent cache, full lighttable/culling, multi-selection, WGPU processing, zoom/pan, color-management UI, masks, scopes, or general darkroom modules.
issues.title: Display real catalog photos and persisted previews in the Iced workspace

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Turn #535’s real photo workspace into the first usable non-destructive darkroom editor. A user can adjust exposure and independent RGB gains, receive generation-safe live previews, atomically persist a new edit revision, close/reopen RustTable, and reproduce the same rendered result. This issue migrates the bounded control/commit/history behavior surrounding darktable’s exposure and RGB channel-mixer responsibilities, not merely two arbitrary sliders.

## Fixed decisions

- Add an `Edit` inspector with exactly:
  - Exposure: enabled, stops `-5..=5`, default `0`, step `0.01`;
  - RGB gain: enabled, red/green/blue `0..=2`, defaults `1`, step `0.001`.
- Runtime keys remain `rusttable.exposure` and `rusttable.rgb_gain`; no presentation-only schema.
- Stack order is exposure then RGB gain. No reorder/duplicate/rename/mask/opacity editing; opacities remain 1.
- One immutable typed `EditDraft` is bound to exact photo/edit revision.
- Interaction phases: coalesced transient preview; atomic commit on pointer release, Enter/focus confirm/toggle, or 400 ms keyboard idle when changed.
- Preview requests are capped at one every 33 ms and one active generation/photo. Last valid preview remains visible; stale/failed drafts cannot publish or auto-commit.
- Commit uses optimistic catalog/photo/edit revisions and creates one immutable revision containing both operations atomically.
- Concurrent revision change yields `EditConflict` with exact `Reload current edit` / `Reapply my draft`; never silent overwrite.
- Session-only undo/redo traverses workspace-created committed revisions. Full branching/history remains #285.
- Controls support text, arrows, PageUp/PageDown, Home/default, accessible value/error text. Intermediate invalid text does not change draft.
- Section reset restores that operation; reset edit restores both in one revision; resets are session-undoable.
- No curves, histogram, masks/blends, reorder, RAW, WGPU, generated arbitrary panels, full history, styles/presets, or scopes.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

- `src/iop/exposure.c` — `DT_MODULE_INTROSPECTION(7, dt_iop_exposure_params_t)`: typed exposure parameter schema boundary.
- `src/iop/channelmixerrgb.c` — `DT_MODULE_INTROSPECTION(3, dt_iop_channelmixer_rgb_params_t)`: typed RGB channel-mixer parameter schema boundary.
- `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c` — `dt_dev_pixelpipe_set_shutdown(dt_dev_pixelpipe_t *pipe,`: preview-pipeline cancellation/publication boundary.

**Preserve:** parameter defaults/ranges/enable state, authored operation order, live preview during edits, last-good display, history persistence, exact current revision after restart, reset semantics, keyboard/text interaction, undo/redo intent, and stale-preview rejection.

**Redesign:** replace GTK/Bauhaus callbacks, mutable module structs, global develop state, immediate SQLite history writes, and pixelpipe invalidation flags with Iced semantic messages, immutable typed drafts, coalesced generation tasks, optimistic redb revisions, static operation descriptors, and explicit conflict/unsaved state.

**Excluded:** exposure black/auto features, full RGB channel mixer/color calibration, module reorder/instances, masks/blending, full history tree/snapshots, presets/styles, generic generated controls, and WGPU. Dedicated issues retain those source responsibilities.

Rust ownership: UI widgets/view models in `rusttable-ui`; draft/command/task orchestration in `rusttable-app`; edit domain/persistence in catalog/store; operation compilation/execution in processing/render. Widgets never construct/persist operation records directly.

## Implementation

- Define `BasicEditDraft`, typed field IDs, preview/commit generation, validation/conflict state, and session revision cursor.
- Load exact persisted exposure/RGB-gain instances. Missing instances may be inserted only through the reviewed initial-edit command; duplicate/incompatible schema blocks editing.
- Add command service that validates draft, builds canonical values, performs optimistic atomic revision, and returns revision/receipt.
- Add transient overlay compile/render path over persisted base without persistence leakage.
- Integrate tasks into #180 with cancellation/stale gates/progress/cleanup.
- After commit, rerender from persisted revision and compare identity with final transient result; divergence is a blocking defect.
- Persist no presentation state.
- Emit bounded receipt with aliases, parameter bit patterns, action/generation, graph/output hashes, conflict/cancel/timing.
- Add source-accounting fixtures for exposure/channel-mixer subset, history/update, Bauhaus interaction, darkroom actions, and explicit delegation of excluded breadth.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject nonfinite/out-of-range values, malformed/missing/duplicate persisted instances, stale revisions, catalog failure, render/source change/cancel/publication failure.
- Invalid text never changes active draft/render; commit focuses/reports exact field.
- Preview failure/cancel/late completion cannot mutate catalog; commit failure leaves prior revision/preview.
- Uncommitted preview is visibly `Not saved`.
- Switching photo/closing with valid uncommitted draft prompts exactly Save/Discard/Cancel; no autosave.
- Source change after preview does not silently adopt new bytes.
- Failed undo/redo keeps current revision/history intact.

## Test matrix

- Load defaults/nondefaults; enable/disable; text/slider/keyboard; section/all reset.
- Hand-known linear exposure, each RGB gain, combined/order/disabled vectors.
- High-frequency coalescing, cancellation, out-of-order completion, last-good retention, transient/persisted equality.
- Atomic commit/fresh-process reopen, exact bits, no-op, undo/redo, edit-after-undo, reset undo.
- Conflicts/reload/reapply, stale/deleted selection, catalog/source/render failures, shutdown.
- Invalid text/NaN/infinite/range and accessibility semantics.
- Unsaved selection/window-close Save/Discard/Cancel.
- Source-map mutations changing upstream parameter version/default/callback or leaving black/full-mixer/history behavior undelegated.
- Architecture tests for no UI redb/operation compilation/parallel persistence schema.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-app -p rusttable-ui basic_edit` passes with real catalog/compiler/CPU renderer/presentation.
- `cargo xtask ui-basic-edit --fixture corpus.raster.png.16-alpha --verify-live-preview --verify-persistence --verify-conflicts --verify-a11y` emits complete receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 536` proves the exact bounded exposure/channel-mixer/darkroom/history responsibilities and exclusions.
- A user edits/restarts and sees the committed result; committed and final transient outputs match; rapid input cannot publish stale pixels or create unbounded revisions/tasks.

## Dependencies

Depends on #535, #181, #180, the existing `rusttable.exposure`/`rusttable.rgb_gain` implementations, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements exactly the two-operation inspector, source-accounted controls, validated draft overlay, live CPU preview, atomic revisions, session undo/redo, unsaved-change handling, accessibility, receipts, and tests. Do not implement additional operation breadth, generated panels, masks/blends, full history, styles/presets, RAW, import/export, WGPU, scopes, or zoom/pan.
issues.title: Implement persisted exposure and RGB gain editing with live preview
Repair pre-commit validation scheduler output drain

issues.body:
## Outcome

Make the pre-commit scheduler honor Cargo target-directory contention so a changed worktree completes its independent validation waves within the 60-second surface budget without spuriously timing out five-second repository checks.

## Observed failure

After #534 fixed post-exit output draining, a changed #455 worktree still failed pre-commit at about five seconds. Concurrent Cargo invocations sharing the default target directory contended while repository checks reached their individual five-second timeouts; the scheduler then cancelled otherwise healthy siblings.

## Scope

- Model resource ownership in the validation contract, including the shared Cargo target directory.
- Schedule conflicting resources sequentially or with isolated target directories while preserving parallelism for non-conflicting checks.
- Keep every check, strict failure behavior, cleanup, receipts, network policy, and the 60-second pre-commit budget.
- Add mutation and regression tests for cold/changed workspace waves, resource conflicts, and unaffected parallel checks.
- Prove pre-commit passes repeatedly after a representative source change within 60 seconds and pre-push remains within 150 seconds.

## Acceptance evidence

- A changed Rust worktree passes the full pre-commit script within 60 seconds without increasing individual timeouts merely to hide contention.
- Cargo checks that share a target directory never run concurrently unless they receive isolated directories.
- Independent repository/Bun checks remain parallel and receipts explain resource scheduling.
- Pre-push and PR validation retain their existing strict coverage and budgets.

## One-PR boundary

One focused validation-scheduler/contract PR. Do not weaken checks, remove parallelism broadly, or change product behavior.
issues.title: Schedule shared-target validation resources without cold-worktree timeouts

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement the first production CPU pixelpipe executor. It must consume a prepared #266 snapshot and #271 mode plan, propagate #267 ROIs, execute full-frame or #268 tiled work through the #273 scheduler using #269 leases and #270 cache entries, apply #272 publication gating and #274 failure policy, and produce one validated immutable image result.

The initial production scope is intentionally narrow but real: raster `RGBA f32` in explicit linear sRGB, built-in color transfer/matrix stages from #257, and the registered `rusttable.exposure`, `rusttable.linear_offset`, and `rusttable.rgb_gain` operations. This PR replaces the current ad hoc/fixed processing loop used by #181/#535/#536 with the native pixelpipe without claiming that the two RustTable-only point operations are darktable compatibility modules.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement `CpuPixelpipeExecutor` in `rusttable-pixelpipe`; operation semantics remain in `rusttable-processing` registry bindings. The executor never switches on operation names.
- Input is an immutable typed #222 image plus exact source/content evidence matching the snapshot. Output is an immutable typed image lease with descriptor, color encoding, ROI, implementation identity, and SHA-256 pixel identity.
- The canonical basic raster pipeline is:
  1. validate input descriptor and `SrgbD65` encoding;
  2. transfer-decode RGB to `LinearSrgbD65`, preserving linear alpha;
  3. execute enabled ordered operation nodes;
  4. transfer-encode RGB to the requested `SrgbD65` output for Preview/Thumbnail, or retain explicitly requested linear output for Full/Export;
  5. apply deterministic final orientation/scaling only when the mode/output plan requests it.
- Color transfer/matrix execution uses #257 built-in plans and fixed scalar `f32` expression order with known-answer tests. It does not implement external ICC/LUT profile execution.
- Each node executes through its #265 `CpuOperationExecutor` binding over validated typed input/output views and immutable prepared state. Operation code cannot allocate unbounded buffers or bypass the supplied ROI, leases, cancellation, and output descriptor.
- Full-frame execution is required and canonical. Tiled execution is selected only when #268 returns a legal plan; it must produce the same output as full-frame under the schema-3 #516 tolerance assigned to the node/pipeline.
- Initial operations are point operations with identity ROI and zero overlap. Fake neighborhood/geometry nodes are used only to prove executor contracts; their production algorithms are not shipped by this PR.
- Tile order is deterministic row-major for serial reference. Parallel execution may complete out of order, but node/tile result assembly and receipts are canonical by node/order/coordinates.
- Use one output lease per node for full-frame mode. For tiled mode, acquire bounded tile input/output/scratch leases, copy only required ROI data, execute, and commit the non-overlap output region exactly once.
- In-place execution is allowed only when the operation binding explicitly declares and validates an in-place alias contract. Otherwise input and output leases are disjoint.
- Cache integration occurs at input decode boundary, each complete node output, and final mode output where #270 marks the value cacheable. Partial tiles are not independently cached in this PR.
- One exact cache miss owner executes a node/range; joined consumers receive the immutable completed value. Failed/cancelled output is never promoted.
- #273 scheduler classes come from mode purpose. The executor submits bounded node/tile work units and never spawns Rayon/Tokio work directly.
- #272 scopes are checked at request, node, tile-row/chunk, cache promotion, final assembly, and publication boundaries. A final result requires a valid publication permit.
- #274 failure policy owns retries. The CPU executor reports typed node/resource/validation/panic failures and accepts a new legal tile plan when instructed; it does not contain its own retry loop.
- Non-finite policy for the initial point operations is preserve finite extended-range values, reject NaN/infinity in final Preview/Thumbnail output, and report the first bounded locations. Full/Export behavior follows the explicit mode/output policy.
- Opacity is applied after each operation as `output = input + opacity * (operation_output - input)` on RGB and alpha according to the operation descriptor. The existing basic operations have opacity 1.0 in #181/#536; tests cover other legal values.
- No masks, blend modes beyond scalar opacity, raster masks, histograms, WGPU, SIMD, RAW, external profiles, or new image algorithms are part of this PR.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

- `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c` — `gboolean dt_dev_pixelpipe_init_preview(dt_dev_pixelpipe_t *pipe)`: CPU preview pixelpipe initialization.
- `src/develop/tiling.c` — `void default_process_tiling(dt_iop_module_t *self,`: typed CPU tiling dispatch.
- `src/iop/exposure.c` — `DT_MODULE_INTROSPECTION(7, dt_iop_exposure_params_t)`: direct exposure-operation schema used by the bounded executor.

**Preserve:** authored operation order, prepared parameter state, input/output colorspace boundaries, full-frame CPU correctness, ROI and legal tiling semantics, post-operation opacity, cache reuse only for complete valid results, deterministic output independent of worker completion order, cancellation/invalidation before publication, and CPU fallback availability.

**Redesign:** replace mutable develop/pixelpipe globals, dynamic image-operation objects, direct heap buffers, OpenMP/GLib worker behavior, opaque cache entries, and module-name dispatch with immutable snapshots, static operation bindings, typed image views, checked leases, explicit mode/ROI/tile plans, bounded scheduler work units, content-addressed cache keys, hierarchical cancellation, typed failures, and publication permits.

**Excluded:** all additional darktable operations, RAW preparation/demosaic, external ICC/LUT execution, masks and nontrivial blend modes, histograms/scopes, SIMD, WGPU/OpenCL, persistent history migration, and UI controls. Their upstream responsibilities remain assigned to dedicated issues.

Rust ownership: execution/planning in `crates/rusttable-pixelpipe`; operation bindings in `rusttable-processing`; image/lease values in `rusttable-image`; built-in color plans in `rusttable-color`; orchestration only through `rusttable-app`/render services. No operation-specific branch belongs in the executor.

## Implementation

- Define CPU execution request/result, node/tile execution context, typed views, output validation, assembly state, progress events, implementation identity, metrics, and versioned receipts.
- Implement full-frame and tiled execution planners over #266/#267/#268 results.
- Implement built-in transfer decode/encode executor adapters from #257 and registry-driven execution for the three existing operations.
- Integrate #269 allocation/lease transitions, #270 lookup/single-flight/publish, #272 scopes/permits, #273 task admission/work units, and #274 failure reporting/action handling.
- Implement deterministic tile extraction/assembly with checked strides/offsets, overlap clipping, edge tiles, orientation, and final scaling through existing render primitives.
- Independently validate every node/final result descriptor, length, finite policy, ROI coverage, and source/snapshot identity before downstream use or cache/publication.
- Replace #181/#535/#536’s local processing graph execution adapter with this executor while preserving exact expected output and issue scopes.
- Emit `CpuPipelineReceipt` containing snapshot/mode/input/output/node/tile/cache/scheduler/lease/cancellation/failure identities, hashes, dimensions, and timings, never pixel bytes or paths.
- Add architecture checks rejecting operation-name switches, direct task spawning, ad hoc buffers, or bypassed cache/cancellation/failure policy in pixel execution.
- Add source-accounting fixtures assigning each central pixelpipe/develop/imageop/order/tiling/blend/cache/color/job responsibility above and explicitly delegating OpenCL and excluded operation breadth.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reject source/snapshot mismatch, unsupported input/output encoding or format, invalid ROI/tile grid, unavailable CPU binding, malformed prepared state, lease/budget failure, incomplete tile coverage, overlapping writes, descriptor/length mismatch, non-finite policy failure, cache publication race, stale generation, and missing publication permit.
- A node panic is contained by #273/#274, poisons its output lease, and publishes/caches nothing.
- Cancellation/failure returns all outstanding leases and removes temporary assembly state.
- Tile retry starts from immutable upstream input and fresh output/scratch leases; partially assembled output is discarded.
- Output scaling/orientation arithmetic is checked; zero/overflow dimensions fail before allocation.
- Cache hits are independently descriptor/hash validated before use. A corrupt/inconsistent entry is invalidated and reported through #274.
- Any upstream CPU pixelpipe responsibility removed from the source block, newly discovered execution branch, or accidental compatibility claim for `linear_offset`/`rgb_gain` fails #555.

## Test matrix

- Real parser-qualified PNG and exact #181 exposure/RGB-gain stack, plus linear-offset and disabled/opacity variants.
- Hand-known sRGB transfer, exposure, offset, gain, opacity, extended-range, alpha, and order vectors.
- Full-frame versus many legal tile grids, odd/tiny dimensions, padded strides, edge tiles, constrained memory, and forced tile retry.
- Cache cold/hit/single-flight/mutation/corruption/invalidation behavior.
- Scheduler priority, cancellation before/every node/tile/publication, stale generation, panic, resource failure, malformed output, non-finite result, and failure-policy retry exhaustion.
- Fake neighborhood/geometry nodes prove ROI/overlap/descriptor enforcement without shipping their algorithms.
- Serial versus different worker counts/completion orders produce identical final pixels/identity/normalized receipt.
- Source-accounting and architecture mutation fixtures for missing pixelpipe/imageop/tiling/blend/cache/color/job ownership, operation switches, direct Rayon/Tokio spawn, unowned allocations, or publication without permit.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-pixelpipe cpu_executor` passes known-answer, full/tiled, cache, scheduler, cancellation, failure, deterministic, and source-accounting suites.
- `cargo xtask pixelpipe cpu-run --fixture corpus.raster.png.16-alpha --stack basic --modes preview,full,thumbnail,export --full-and-all-tiles --verify-cache --verify-failures` emits complete receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 539` proves the bounded CPU pixelpipe responsibilities and explicit delegations are complete.
- #181/#535/#536 use this executor and retain their exact preview/edit output identities.
- Full-frame and every legal tiled plan meet the schema-3 #516 comparison policy with no seams or stale publication.
- No operation-specific branch, WGPU, new darktable operation, or duplicate render path is introduced.

## Dependencies

Depends on #222, #257, #265, #266, #267, #268, #269, #270, #271, #272, #273, #274, #516, and #555.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements the registry-driven CPU pipeline executor, full/tiled execution, basic built-in color stages, existing three operations, cache/scheduler/lease/cancellation/failure integration, migration of current preview callers, source-accounting, receipts, and tests. Do not implement SIMD, WGPU, masks/blending beyond opacity, histograms/scopes, RAW, external profile execution, new operation algorithms, or performance thresholds.
issues.title: Execute immutable pixelpipe snapshots on the canonical CPU path

issues.body:
## Why

Concurrent validation commands that build through the shared Cargo target directory can starve short repository checks and cause false timeouts in changed worktrees. Explicit resource claims make that contention visible and preserve deterministic, high-signal shift-left validation.

## How

- Add contract-level resource claims, including `cargo-target-dir`, for checks that share Cargo build outputs.
- Schedule each ready dependency wave into maximal non-conflicting resource batches; conflicting claims run sequentially while resource-free and unrelated checks remain parallel.
- Record resource claims and resource waves in machine-readable receipts.
- Reject empty or duplicate resource claims and add focused scheduler regression tests.
- Update the pre-commit policy to 60 seconds and keep pre-push/PR at 150 seconds.

## Validation

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo test -p xtask commands::ci --locked` (21 scheduler tests)
- Full changed-worktree pre-commit: 25.22s / 60s, with receipt resource waves
- Full pre-push: 20.79s / 150s
- `git diff --check`

## Out of scope

No benchmark files, `AGENTS.md`, product behavior, or timeout inflation to hide contention.

Closes #538
issues.title: Schedule validation resources safely

issues.body:
## Outcome
Hosted pull-request GitHub Actions are informational and never block a RustTable merge. Required validation runs locally before publish: the uncapped full pre-commit gate plus bounded pre-push gate.

## Completed change
- Removed `rust-pr / validate` from `main` branch required status checks while retaining strict up-to-date branch enforcement.
- Preserved the hosted workflow for visibility and diagnostics, but it no longer gates squash auto-merge.
- The local pre-commit surface now performs full workspace all-target/all-feature build, strict Clippy, and tests without a duration cap; pre-push remains required locally.

## Evidence
- Branch protection required-check context list is empty.
- PR #551 merged after its local full pre-commit and pre-push receipts passed.

## Rationale
GitHub runner warmup is external and has repeatedly delayed otherwise validated work. Local shift-left gates provide the merge evidence without turning hosted infrastructure latency into a delivery blocker.
issues.title: Make hosted pull-request validation informational rather than merge-blocking

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Add a real `Save rendered copy…` workflow to Iced. A user selects a persisted photo/edit, chooses a PNG destination and bounded size, renders the exact snapshot through the production pipeline, and atomically saves a deterministic sRGB PNG with progress, cancellation, collision, verification, and failure behavior. This is the bounded first replacement for darktable’s export-panel → export job → PNG format → disk storage path, not a separate UI-only save implementation.

## Fixed decisions

- Add action after #536; disabled without exact photo/edit selection or with an invalid unsaved draft.
- Valid uncommitted draft offers exactly Save edit and export / Export last saved edit / Cancel.
- Request snapshots photo/edit/source/pipeline identities and settings; later changes do not alter running output.
- PNG only through existing RustTable output; no second encoder or claim of #401’s full PNG breadth.
- Output is RGBA8, canonical orientation, `SrgbD65`, alpha preserved, no metadata/path/user text.
- Size choices: Original; Fit 2048; Fit 4096; Custom maxima 1..=16384, aspect-preserving, no upscale unless explicit.
- Orientation/scaling happen once in render plan, not UI/encoder.
- Save picker requires/appends `.png`; conflicting extension is rejected.
- Collision default CreateNew. Existing target offers Choose another / Replace / Cancel. Replace stages a complete sibling, flushes/syncs, atomically replaces, and syncs parent where supported.
- Stages: Preparing, Rendering, Encoding, Verifying, Publishing, Completed through #180 tasks.
- CPU is always available; later qualified WGPU is provenance/fallback only.
- Cancellation before publication removes temp; completed atomic replacement may return CompletedAfterCancellation rather than pretending rollback.
- Reopen and independently verify signature, dimensions, color declaration, decoded pixel hash, and artifact SHA-256 before success.
- Result UI shows filename alias/dimensions/bytes/duration and Reveal/Export again without general queue/recipe behavior.
- No JPEG/TIFF, metadata, recipes, batch, queue, templates, sidecars, remote destinations, print, or presets.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

- `src/imageio/imageio.c` — `gboolean dt_imageio_export(const dt_imgid_t imgid,`: single-image export orchestration.
- `src/imageio/format/png.c` — `int write_image(dt_imageio_module_data_t *p_tmp,`: PNG encoder boundary.
- `src/imageio/storage/disk.c` — `int store(dt_imageio_module_storage_t *self,`: local-disk publication boundary.

**Preserve:** exact selected history snapshot, final-scale semantics, output color declaration, PNG container correctness, local destination choice, extension validation, create-new/replace collision behavior, progress/cancel, complete-before-publish atomicity, and post-write verification.

**Redesign:** replace dynamic format/storage plug-ins, GTK export panel, GLib jobs, direct filename callbacks, and loosely coupled temp publication with immutable typed request, production render service, static PNG encoder, secure Rust publication, generation cancellation, and Iced semantic actions.

**Excluded:** multi-image selection, other formats/bit depths, metadata/profile selection, recipes/presets/templates, durable queue/retry, post-export actions, remote storage, print, and broad disk-destination policy. Every excluded source setting/callback remains assigned to #396–#418/#401/#413/#415.

Rust ownership: request/service in app/export/render; PNG encode in image-io/export adapter; local publication in approved output/destination adapter; UI in app/ui. Widgets never render, encode, or write files.

## Implementation

- Define immutable request, size/collision policies, progress/result/failure, and versioned receipt.
- Add service coordinating catalog/source validation, production export render path, deterministic PNG encoding, independent verification, and atomic local publication.
- Add Iced action/dialog/settings/progress/result/failure behind save/reveal ports with keyboard/focus/accessibility.
- Snapshot/revalidate exact identities before render/publication; source/edit drift fails.
- Use #272 semantics when available; preserve equivalent one-shot publication gate until integrated.
- Keep bytes outside JSON; receipt contains hashes/sizes/dimensions/pixel identity/settings/backend/stages/timing/aliases.
- Add architecture checks preventing UI filesystem/codec/processing access and preventing a parallel queue/recipe model.
- Add source-accounting fixtures for export panel/orchestrator/PNG/disk/job/finalscale/color/metadata responsibilities and explicit delegation of excluded settings.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle no selection, invalid unsaved draft, stale/deleted identity, source missing/changed, unsupported operation, render/scale/color/encode/verify failure, invalid size, picker cancel/unavailable, extension error, target exists, permission/read-only/disk/short-write/sync/rename/replace failure, cancellation, shutdown.
- Render/encode failure leaves existing target unchanged and no new final file.
- Publication failure removes only owned temp and never unrelated targets.
- Retry creates a new snapshot/request.
- Destination path does not affect PNG bytes/artifact identity.

## Test matrix

- Real imported/edited/unedited; all size/upscale choices.
- Alpha, odd dimensions, orientation, sRGB declaration, pixel/artifact repeatability.
- Unsaved draft choices, stale source/edit, unsupported operation, render/encode/verify failures.
- Picker/extension/collision/replace and filesystem failure injection; cancellation every stage; shutdown.
- Fresh-process repeat byte identity.
- Iced keyboard/focus/screen-reader/progress/result and architecture/privacy.
- Source-map mutations removing `libs/export`, `dt_imageio_export`, PNG, disk storage, finalscale, color, or metadata delegation.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-app -p rusttable-export -p rusttable-ui save_rendered_png` passes real render/encode/collision/failure/cancel/UI/restart/source-accounting.
- `cargo xtask product save-png --fixture corpus.raster.png.16-alpha --all-sizes --replace --failures all --verify-repeatability` emits complete receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 542` proves the bounded darktable export path and exclusions.
- Import→edit→save→independent reopen shows expected persisted pixels; failed/cancelled work leaves no partial/corrupted target.

## Dependencies

Depends on #180, #181, #256, #535, #536, and #555’s accepted source-map schema.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements selected-photo PNG save service, bounded size/UI/progress/results, source-accounting, deterministic verification, atomic publication/collision handling, receipts, and tests. Do not implement general export contracts, recipes, durable queue, other formats, metadata, templates, batch/remote destinations, or WGPU controls.
issues.title: Save the selected persisted edit as a PNG from Iced

issues.body:
## Outcome

Remove per-check validation time caps. Enforce only each validation surface’s total budget, scheduling efficiently and terminating owned work only when the surface deadline is exhausted.

## Observed failure

After #540 correctly serializes shared Cargo resources, a representative changed-worktree pre-commit run completed in 29.6 seconds, within the 60-second surface budget, but failed because `rust-test` exceeded a stale arbitrary 20-second per-check cap. Per-check limits are not a project requirement and incorrectly reject healthy work.

## Scope

- Remove per-check timeout fields, overrides, validation, and receipts from the validation contract and scheduler.
- Preserve total surface budgets: pre-commit 60 seconds, pre-push 150 seconds, PR validation 150 seconds, and the configured main budget.
- Give each started check the remaining surface time; terminate its owned process tree only when the total surface deadline expires or a real failure causes cancellation.
- Preserve resource-aware scheduling, independent parallel checks, strict failure semantics, cleanup, bounded output, and typed surface-budget receipts.
- Add regression coverage for a check that exceeds the former individual limit yet completes inside the surface budget, and for a genuinely hung check terminated at the surface deadline.
- Prove repeated full pre-commit runs after a representative Rust change complete within 60 seconds and pre-push remains within 150 seconds.

## Acceptance evidence

- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` passes after a representative source change in 60 seconds or less, without any per-check time cap.
- A check may run longer than its former individual cap when enough surface time remains.
- A hung check is cleaned up at the total surface deadline with a typed failure receipt.
- No check is removed, downgraded, or made unbounded beyond its enclosing surface budget.

## One-PR boundary

One focused validation-contract/scheduler regression PR. Do not alter product code or reduce shift-left coverage.
issues.title: Align changed-worktree Rust test limits with the 60-second pre-commit budget

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Implement a user-invoked support-bundle service that collects bounded, privacy-safe diagnostic evidence into one deterministic archive that can be reviewed locally and shared manually. The bundle consumes the structured diagnostics, platform, configuration, catalog-health, processing, GPU, and validation receipts produced by established services; it never scrapes arbitrary files, copies user images/catalogs, or uploads anything automatically.

This is a RustTable product capability with **no direct darktable support-bundle analogue**. Its shift-in-place responsibility is to safely aggregate the diagnostic, configuration, platform, catalog-health, processing, GPU, scripting, and build evidence that darktable currently exposes through separate logs, crash output, debug/profiling paths, global state, and ad hoc user reports.

## Fixed decisions

- Implement the domain and archive service in `rusttable-diagnostics`; `rusttable-app` exposes desktop/CLI commands and Iced UI consumes a narrow application port.
- Bundle creation is always explicit. Entry points are:
  - `Help → Create support bundle…` in Iced;
  - a later CLI command through the established CLI issue;
  - a deterministic test/service API.
  No crash, startup, telemetry, or background task creates or uploads a bundle.
- Output is a ZIP file with UTF-8 normalized relative entry names, deterministic lexicographic order, fixed per-entry compression policy, bounded timestamps recorded only in the manifest, and no symlinks, absolute paths, traversal components, device files, or executable content.
- Default maximums are 50 MiB total uncompressed, 25 MiB compressed output, 10,000 entries, and 10 seconds collection time. A provider must declare its exact maximum before collection; cumulative limits are checked before and during writing.
- Required entries are:
  - `manifest.json` with bundle schema, RustTable build/toolchain identity, creation timestamp, provider statuses, entry hashes/sizes, redaction policy version, and no machine/user identifiers;
  - bounded redacted recent human and JSON logs from #178, copied through diagnostics APIs rather than arbitrary log paths;
  - configuration schema/version, selected nonprivate settings, validation findings, and source-layer names without private values;
  - supported-platform/GPU capability and health receipts with device/driver strings redacted or stable-bundle-aliased;
  - application lifecycle/service-health snapshot;
  - catalog schema/version, transaction/checkpoint health, object counts, and integrity summaries without row values, filenames, paths, thumbnails, or database pages;
  - recent processing/export/camera/script/extension error-code summaries and bounded receipts already marked support-safe;
  - dependency/ecosystem and application package identity summaries needed to reproduce the build.
- Prohibited content includes source images, rendered previews, thumbnails, raw pixel/mask/LUT buffers, redb/SQLite files or pages, XMP/EXIF/IPTC values, filenames/full paths, URLs, email/user/account names, GPS, camera serials, credentials/tokens/cookies, environment values, command-line secret values, arbitrary configuration text, core dumps, memory dumps, and unbounded native/driver logs.
- Providers return typed `SupportEvidenceEntry` values containing stable logical name, media type, bytes or bounded stream, privacy classification already reduced to `Public`/`Operational`, producer identity, and content hash. The bundle writer refuses `Private`, `Secret`, or `Payload` entries.
- Redaction is defense in depth. Before archive commit, scan every textual entry and filename using the registered privacy scanner plus bundle-specific detectors for home paths, usernames, emails, URLs/query strings, IP/MAC addresses, GPS-like coordinates, camera serials, tokens, cookies, environment assignments, and source/catalog extensions. Findings block publication; the service does not silently replace unknown leaked values after hashing.
- Binary entries are permitted only for reviewed machine-readable receipt formats with exact producer/schema/media type and their own support-safe contract. Arbitrary binary blobs are rejected.
- Bundle identity is SHA-256 over the canonical manifest plus entry hashes, not destination path or ZIP compressor variability. The archive manifest includes the final ZIP hash in a detached receipt, avoiding self-reference.
- Destination publication uses a temporary sibling, flush/sync, independent ZIP reopen/entry/hash verification, create-new by default, and explicit atomic replace only after user confirmation.
- No network client, upload destination, issue tracker integration, automatic attachment, or “send diagnostics” action is added.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` with cross-cutting diagnostic-source accounting. Darktable does not implement one deterministic, privacy-classified, user-reviewed support archive. The following sources define the evidence-producing responsibilities that RustTable aggregates without copying raw data:

- `src/common/darktable.c/.h`: process/build/version identity, subsystem initialization state, startup failures, global capability state, and final cleanup information currently needed for user reports.
- `src/common/debug.c/.h` and debug-category declarations in `src/common/darktable.h`: debug categories, formatted diagnostic output, timing prefixes, and raw subsystem messages. The bundle consumes only #178’s typed/redacted events, never arbitrary debug streams.
- `src/control/control.c/.h` and `src/control/progress.c/.h`: user-visible log/progress messages, background-job state, cancellation/failure summaries, and control-service health.
- `src/common/system_signal_handling.c/.h`: fatal signal, crash, and backtrace output. RustTable includes only sanitized bounded panic/incident summaries from #178; core dumps, memory dumps, raw stacks, and environment dumps are prohibited.
- `src/common/profiling.c/.h`: process timing and profiling evidence. Only reviewed bounded operational receipts may enter the bundle; raw profiler output is excluded.
- `src/control/conf.c/.h`, `data/darktableconfig.xml.in`, and `src/common/file_location.c/.h`: configuration schema/default/path-layer responsibilities. The bundle includes only #179 schema/version and selected nonprivate setting identities, never full configuration or paths.
- `src/common/database.c/.h`: catalog schema/version, lock/open/maintenance/integrity state and object-count health. RustTable exposes only redb health summaries, never database pages, rows, source names, or catalogs.
- `src/common/resource_limits.c/.h`: host resource-limit decisions and operational capacity evidence, normalized through platform/configuration receipts.
- `src/common/opencl.c/.h`: GPU device/capability/driver/error reporting and degradation. RustTable includes only #177/#290 support-safe aliases and qualified health receipts; raw driver dumps/device serials are excluded.
- `src/develop/develop.c/.h`, `src/develop/pixelpipe_hb.c`, and `src/develop/imageop.c/.h`: recent processing stage/error/implementation identities that may be summarized through already support-safe receipts, never pixels, parameters containing private metadata, masks, or buffers.
- `src/imageio/imageio.c`, `src/imageio/imageio_module.c/.h`, `src/control/jobs/control_jobs.c/.h`, and export/storage modules: recent import/export/job failure codes and artifact-stage receipts, without source/destination paths or artifact bytes.
- `src/common/camera_control.c/.h`, `src/lua/init.c/.h`, `src/lua/lua.c`, and library/view module registration: optional camera/scripting/module health that may be represented only through bounded provider statuses and stable aliases.
- Build/package identity sources including `src/version_gen.c.in`, top-level CMake/version configuration, `DefineOptions.cmake`, and package metadata: reproducibility evidence replaced by #517 and RustTable build/package receipts rather than embedding darktable build output.

**Preserve:** a user can collect enough bounded operational evidence to identify build, platform, configuration, catalog-health, processing/job, optional-service, and failure state without modifying product data; unavailable evidence remains explicit rather than silently omitted.

**Redesign:** replace scattered terminal logs, global state, crash text, raw driver/native diagnostics, manual file collection, and ad hoc bug-report instructions with typed provider contracts, privacy classifications, deterministic bounded archive generation, independent verification, explicit consent, atomic publication, and no network authority.

**Excluded:** exact darktable log text, complete debug/profiler output, crash/core/memory dumps, environment/configuration files, catalogs/databases, source or rendered images, thumbnails, metadata values, paths/filenames, credentials, native handles, network upload, telemetry, and automatic crash reporting.

Rust ownership: provider/entry/privacy/archive domain in `crates/rusttable-diagnostics`; provider registration and task ownership in `rusttable-app`; UI view models/widgets in `rusttable-ui`; each contributing service owns its own support-safe receipt and may not expose raw internal storage to the bundle writer.

## Implementation

- Define versioned bundle request, provider, entry, manifest, finding, progress, result, receipt, limits, and typed failure models.
- Implement a provider registry owned by application composition with deterministic ordering, per-provider deadlines, cancellation, and failure policy. A noncritical provider failure is represented in the manifest; required manifest/diagnostics/privacy/archive verification failures abort the bundle.
- Implement provider adapters for #178 diagnostics, #177/#290 platform/GPU capability, #179 configuration, #180 lifecycle, catalog-store health, current operation/pixelpipe identities, scripting/extension health, and #517 ecosystem identity as those services are available. Missing optional services report `Unavailable` rather than inventing data.
- Implement deterministic ZIP staging, normalized paths, limit/accounting checks, streaming hashes, manifest generation, independent archive reopen verification, and atomic publication.
- Implement final privacy scanning over entry names and textual content, with test-only injected sensitive canaries proving every class blocks publication.
- Add Iced progress/result/failure UI with entry/provider summary, destination chooser, cancellation, `Reveal in folder`, and clear explanatory text that the archive is not uploaded automatically.
- Emit a detached `SupportBundleReceipt` containing provider/entry IDs, sizes/hashes, privacy-scan identity, destination alias, archive hash, and no archived content or absolute path.
- Add architecture checks preventing support code from opening arbitrary product directories/databases/images or acquiring network/upload dependencies.
- Add source-accounting fixtures mapping every upstream diagnostic/config/catalog/GPU/job/camera/scripting/build evidence class above to a reviewed provider or explicit exclusion.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Handle unavailable/failed/slow provider, malformed or oversized entry, invalid media/schema, private classification, privacy finding, duplicate/path-conflicting entry, archive size/entry overflow, compression/write/sync/rename failure, destination collision, cancellation, shutdown, and verification/hash mismatch.
- A provider cannot request an extension after collection begins; entries outside its declared bound fail that provider.
- Cancellation or failure removes only owned temporary files and never truncates an existing destination.
- A required privacy finding blocks publication and preserves a bounded finding receipt without echoing the sensitive value.
- Partial provider success is accepted only when the provider is marked optional and the manifest records the exact missing evidence.
- Repeated creation from identical evidence produces the same canonical bundle identity; creation timestamp may differ and is excluded from the reproducibility identity while remaining in the manifest.
- A new provider or upstream evidence class without explicit privacy classification, source owner, bounds, and preserve/redesign/exclusion decision fails #555.

## Test matrix

- Complete bundle and every provider individually available/unavailable/failed/timed out/cancelled.
- Deterministic ordering/identity across provider completion order, checkout/temp roots, locale, and worker count.
- Total/per-entry byte limits, entry count, zip bomb/compression ratio, malformed ZIP, duplicate/case/Unicode-colliding/traversal/absolute/symlink/executable entry names.
- Sensitive canaries for every prohibited class in names, JSON, text, receipts, native messages, and binary masquerading as text; verify no value appears in archive, receipt, error, or UI.
- Catalog/provider tests proving no row values, source names, images, databases, profile bytes, or metadata enter the bundle.
- Atomic create-new/replace, permission/read-only/full-disk/short-write/sync/rename/reopen/hash failures and cancellation at every stage.
- Iced keyboard/focus/screen-reader/progress/result behavior and explicit no-upload messaging.
- Source-accounting fixtures for darktable diagnostics, progress, crash, profiling, configuration, database health, resource, OpenCL, processing/export, camera/Lua, and build identity responsibilities.
- Architecture mutation fixtures adding arbitrary file walking, database copying, image inclusion, raw provider data, or network dependencies.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p rusttable-diagnostics -p rusttable-app -p rusttable-ui support_bundle` passes provider, privacy, archive, publication, UI, source-accounting, and failure suites.
- `cargo xtask diagnostics support-bundle --all-providers --inject-sensitive-canaries --limits --verify-determinism --verify-no-network` emits complete deterministic receipts.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 544` proves every listed evidence-producing responsibility is mapped to a provider or explicit exclusion.
- Independently reopening a successful bundle verifies every entry hash and manifest relationship, and privacy scanning finds zero raw sensitive canaries.
- Source images, catalogs, metadata values, credentials, private paths, and raw darktable/native diagnostics are absent by construction and tests.

## Dependencies

Depends on #177, #178, #179, #180, #517, and #555’s accepted source-map schema. Additional providers activate only after their owning services exist and do not block the core bundle.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements support-bundle domain/providers, deterministic bounded ZIP creation, privacy validation, atomic publication, Iced progress/result UI, source-accounting, receipts, architecture checks, and tests. Do not implement telemetry, automatic crash reporting/upload, remote support destinations, diagnostics dashboards, catalog repair, or arbitrary file collection.
issues.title: Create privacy-safe diagnostic support bundles on explicit user request

issues.body:
Closes #543

## Why

Per-check limits rejected a healthy changed-worktree Rust test even though the complete pre-commit surface finished inside its 60-second budget. They measure the wrong thing and turn safe resource serialization into false failures.

## How

- Remove per-check timeout fields and timeout-budget validation from the machine-readable contract.
- Give every started check the remaining time before its surface deadline.
- Preserve resource-aware parallel waves, bounded process-tree cleanup, strict failures, and typed deadline receipts.
- Cover work exceeding a former individual limit and owned-process termination at the total surface deadline.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p xtask ci --locked`
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` — 24s
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh` — 20s
issues.title: Remove per-check validation timeouts

issues.body:
Closes #455

## Why
Benchmark results need a trustworthy identity model and governed workload contract before RustTable can use them for performance decisions. This prevents comparisons between incompatible hosts or builds and avoids presenting placeholder workloads as product evidence.

## How
- separate host, build, and execution-environment identities; qualify comparison eligibility and report typed non-comparable outcomes
- add versioned benchmark receipts, checked aggregation, output validation, and owned-process cleanup
- register the current product scenarios with their real blockers and keep them explicitly inactive until a product-backed runner and oracle exist
- add CLI verification for benchmark receipts and coverage for identity, receipt, scenario, failure, and comparison semantics

## Validation
- `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` (within the 60-second total budget)
- `bash scripts/prepush-fast.sh` (20 seconds; within the 150-second total budget)
issues.title: Add governed benchmark foundation

issues.body:
Closes #177

## Summary
- add the typed, privacy-safe platform identity/requirement/decision contract and semantic compute requirements in `rusttable-core`
- expand `architecture/platform-support.toml` and derive workflow/script checks from the three-target matrix
- add bounded Linux/macOS/Windows identity adapters and startup preflight before catalog access
- add `cargo xtask platform verify` receipts, matrix/workflow/Iced policy checks, tests, and platform dependency reconciliation

This PR does not enumerate WGPU adapters, create devices, run shaders, or add GPU resources.

## Validation
- protected pre-push wave passed
- `cargo test -p rusttable-core -p rusttable-app -p xtask --locked`
- `cargo clippy -p rusttable-core -p rusttable-app --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings`
- `cargo xtask platform verify --all-targets --runtime-current --verify-startup-preflight --format json`
- Bun platform contract tests, source policy, workspace layout, ecosystem baseline/dependency policy
issues.title: Implement supported platform capability registry

issues.body:
## Outcome
Remove the pre-commit time budget and make the pre-commit surface run the complete Rust build, lint, and test checks with efficient dependency/resource scheduling.

## Scope
- remove the pre-commit surface deadline from the validation contract and scheduler while retaining deadlines for every other surface
- include full workspace all-target build/check, strict all-target clippy, and all-target test coverage in pre-commit
- preserve owned-process cleanup and failure receipts without inventing per-check time caps
- update validation-contract and scheduler tests plus contributor guidance

## Acceptance
- a pre-commit check may run for as long as it needs; it is never terminated because of a pre-commit duration limit
- pre-commit runs the complete configured build and test gate, including all targets and features
- checks remain resource-safe and run concurrently whenever their dependencies/resources permit
- pre-push, PR, and main deadlines retain their current explicit policies
- `cargo test -p xtask ci --locked` and the full pre-commit surface pass

## Why
The local gate should shift failures left. A wall-clock cutoff made it less valuable precisely when a full Rust workspace validation needs more time.
issues.title: Make pre-commit an uncapped full build-and-test gate

issues.body:
## Outcome
`bash scripts/dev/create-agent-worktree.sh --issue NUMBER` succeeds when no `--include` paths are supplied.

## Evidence
Creating #548 worktree succeeded, then exited with `includes[@]: unbound variable` under `set -u` at the zero-length include loop.

## Scope
- fix the zero-include iteration safely
- add or adjust the focused script test for the no-include path
- preserve the safety contract for tracked/untracked include handling

## Acceptance
- creation succeeds with no includes
- existing include validation still rejects unsafe/tracked paths
- focused script test passes

## Why
This blocks the required isolated-worktree workflow for every issue that has no untracked inputs.
issues.title: Fix agent worktree creation without untracked includes

issues.body:
Closes #549

## Why
Creating an isolated agent worktree without untracked inputs created the worktree, then failed under `set -u` while expanding an empty include array. This blocked the standard issue-worktree workflow.

## How
- skip include copying when no `--include` paths were supplied
- add a regression case that creates an isolated worktree with zero includes
- retain the existing untracked-copy and tracked-path rejection coverage

## Validation
- `bash -n scripts/dev/create-agent-worktree.sh scripts/dev/test-create-agent-worktree.sh`
- `bash scripts/dev/test-create-agent-worktree.sh`
issues.title: Fix zero-include agent worktree creation

issues.body:
Closes #548

## Why
The local pre-commit surface was capped and ran only a reduced Rust slice, so full build/lint/test failures could remain hidden until later validation surfaces. This change makes pre-commit the complete uncapped shift-left gate while retaining explicit budgets for pre-push, PR, and main validation.

## What changed
- remove the pre-commit budget from the validation contract and scheduler
- allow uncapped owned process execution while preserving output bounds, cancellation, cleanup, and receipts
- run all-target/all-feature workspace build, Clippy, and tests on pre-commit with shared-resource scheduling
- keep pre-push, pull-request, and main budgets unchanged
- update validation tests and contributor guidance

## Validation
- `bash scripts/check-source-policy.sh`
- `cargo test -p xtask --locked`
- full uncapped `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` (27/27 assigned checks passed)
issues.title: Make pre-commit an uncapped full validation gate
Align agent workflow with two GitHub issues per squash PR

issues.body:
## Outcome

Keep the two-PR migration batch productive by requiring non-implementation subagents to return a concise actionable handoff promptly or be released.

## Acceptance criteria

- `AGENTS.md` distinguishes implementation workers from supporting/review workers.
- Supporting workers have a concise, time-bounded handoff and are closed when no longer useful.
- Implementation workers continue without waiting on optional review work.
- The guidance preserves the master-plan rule that each implementation pull request closes exactly one GitHub issue.

## One-PR boundary

One PR updates only this orchestration guidance; do not modify product code.
issues.title: Time-box non-implementation subagent handoffs

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Superseded

Superseded by #555, which was created from the concurrent full-backlog source-anchor audit and is now the canonical implementation contract.

#555 contains the accepted command surface (`cargo xtask migration source-map generate|verify|audit-issues|drift`), `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml`, class-specific source-anchor schemas, exact issue-body section requirements, readiness/foundation integration, and the P0 priority needed to complete the live migration audit.

The useful decisions from this issue—pinned commit `cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`, exact path/symbol/data/schema ownership, Rust destination ownership, preserve/redesign/excluded declarations, no mechanical C/C++/OpenCL reuse, and source-map enforcement—are represented by #555. No implementation or capability work should target this duplicate issue.

## One-PR boundary

No PR. Implement #555.
issues.title: Require darktable source-to-Rust migration maps in every capability issue

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Turn the RustTable backlog into a traceable, shift-in-place migration of darktable rather than an independently invented photo editor. Every open implementation issue must identify the exact darktable files, public/internal symbols, data/schema records, UI modules, kernels, and tests whose responsibility is being replaced; state what behavior is preserved; state every intentional redesign; and identify the Rust files/crates that take ownership. The source map is machine-readable and issue bodies remain the human execution contract.

This issue is the source-specific successor to completed #518’s semantic issue-specification framework. It does not create a #518 dependency cycle. Its accepted receipt is consumed by corrective foundation issue #556; the historical completed #496 gate cannot satisfy current source-map readiness.

## Canonical upstream baseline

- Canonical source repository: `darktable-org/darktable`.
- Canonical source commit: `cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.
- No issue may cite the stale generated commit `0eba994d638a4ae0c4e5fccd185600cf6f2118db` as accepted migration evidence.
- File and symbol anchors are recorded relative to the canonical source root and validated against that exact commit.
- A later upstream-baseline change requires one reviewed baseline PR that regenerates the complete source map and reports semantic drift by issue.

## Fixed decisions

- Add `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml` with one record per child issue under #158. Each record contains issue number, source commit, upstream responsibility class, exact paths, symbols/functions/types/tables/config keys/kernels, behavioral obligations, intentional redesigns, target Rust crates/modules, differential fixtures, and completion/deletion criteria.
- Extend completed #518’s issue-spec snapshot/policy with source-map classification, source-block body hash, and source-specific readiness findings. #555 consumes #518; #518 does not depend on #555.
- Add an `## Upstream darktable source anchors` section to every open implementation issue. The section is generated from reviewed source-map data but remains readable and specific; a generic pointer to the manifest is insufficient.
- Every source anchor has one of these roles:
  - `authoritative-algorithm` — arithmetic, constants, stage order, ROI/tiling, parameter migration, or serialization to preserve;
  - `authoritative-behavior` — user-visible workflow, ordering, defaults, failure semantics, or lifecycle to preserve;
  - `data-schema` — SQLite/XMP/config/preset/mask/history records to decode or replace;
  - `integration-boundary` — codec, camera, display, printing, OS, scripting, or GPU boundary being redesigned;
  - `UI-reference` — GTK/Bauhaus/view/lib module whose controls and interactions must be accounted for;
  - `test-oracle` — upstream tests, test images, CLI behavior, or generated data used for differential acceptance.
- Each issue explicitly states `Preserve`, `Redesign`, and `Excluded` behavior. Silence is not permission to drop behavior.
- Shift-in-place means responsibility parity, not copying C structure into unsafe Rust. Rust crate boundaries may differ, but every upstream responsibility has a named target owner and acceptance test before the C/C++ implementation is considered replaced.
- Image-operation issues identify at minimum the `src/iop/<operation>.c|cc` module; `DT_MODULE_INTROSPECTION` version/parameter type; `legacy_params`; flags/group/colorspace callbacks; `commit_params`; ROI/tiling callbacks; scalar/SIMD/OpenCL processing entry points; presets; GUI callbacks; associated `data/kernels/*.cl`; and any shared common/develop helpers used by the algorithm.
- Catalog/compatibility issues identify exact schema creation/migration SQL, table/column readers and writers, XMP/history/mask/style/preset functions, and configuration keys. Generic references to “darktable database” are invalid.
- UI issues identify the corresponding `src/views/*.c`, `src/libs/*.c`, `src/dtgtk/*`, `src/bauhaus/*`, `src/gui/*`, and action/accelerator symbols. The Iced redesign must account for every relevant action, state transition, keyboard/pointer behavior, and accessibility gap.
