- Codec/export/storage issues identify central imageio dispatch plus the exact applicable `src/imageio/format/*` and `src/imageio/storage/*` modules and the metadata/color hooks they rely on.
- GPU issues map every OpenCL program/kernel and its host-side creation/argument/dispatch/fallback path to a WGPU shader/dispatch owner; a broad “port OpenCL” issue cannot conceal unmapped kernels.
- New Rust-only infrastructure still identifies the darktable global/service/call-site responsibility it replaces, or is explicitly classified `NoDirectAnalogue` with nearest architectural context and a reason no direct source contract exists.
- Closed completed issues are source-audited when their output feeds current work. Missing responsibility creates a focused corrective issue and a post-completion audit annotation; closure alone is not evidence. #177, #518, and #496 demonstrate the audit/successor model.
- Open P0/P1 issues receive reviewed source blocks directly during this audit so usable-product and native-engine work can become ready. P2–P4 bodies are migrated in reviewed batches by the implemented plan/apply tooling; they remain blocked until their exact source block validates.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`.

Migration classification: **`NoDirectAnalogue` source-accounting/governance infrastructure with `ReferenceQualification` inputs**. Darktable has no issue-to-source ownership manifest or Rust migration readiness tool. #555 inventories the complete pinned tree and assigns its product responsibilities to other issues; it does not itself implement those responsibilities.

The authoritative inventory surface is the whole tracked pinned source tree, with class-specific primary anchors:

- Top-level `CMakeLists.txt`, `DefineOptions.cmake`, `cmake/`, `src/CMakeLists.txt`, `data/CMakeLists.txt`, platform-conditioned source lists, generated headers/introspection steps, package/install rules, and `src/external/*`: capability registration, build-option guards, platform/native dependency boundaries, generated data, and source inclusion used to discover complete responsibility classes.
- `src/main.c`, `src/cli/main.c`, `src/common/darktable.c/.h`, and `src/control/*`: GUI/headless entry, subsystem initialization/cleanup, global/service/job/signal/progress ownership, and command behavior used to map foundation, lifecycle, diagnostics, CLI, and scheduling issues.
- `src/common/database.c/.h`, database schema/migration SQL, `src/common/image.*`, `film.*`, `history.*`, `styles.*`, `tags.*`, `collection.*`, `metadata.*`, `grouping.*`, `undo.*`, `control/conf.*`, XMP/sidecar readers/writers, and related `src/libs/*`: durable catalog/configuration/compatibility tables, columns, records, indexes, migrations, and workflows.
- `src/imageio/imageio*.c|cc|h`, `src/imageio/format/*`, `src/imageio/storage/*`, RAW/thumbnail/metadata/profile loaders, and central registration/dispatch: probing, decode/encode, import/export, destination, color, metadata, and container responsibilities.
- `src/develop/develop.*`, `pixelpipe_hb.c`, `pixelpipe.h`, `imageop.*`, `tiling.*`, `blend.*`, `blends/*`, `masks/*`, `src/common/iop_order.*`, `iop_profile.*`, `colorspaces*`, interpolation/cache/resource helpers, and every `src/iop/*.c|cc`: operation registration/parameters/migrations, stage order, color, ROI/tiling, CPU/SIMD/OpenCL processing, masks/blending, history, caches, and publication.
- `src/common/opencl.*`, `src/common/dlopencl.*`, `data/kernels/programs.conf`, every referenced `data/kernels/*.cl`, shared kernel includes, and every host kernel creation/argument/enqueue/fallback call site: device/program/kernel/resource/transfer/dispatch/submission/tiling/failure responsibilities mapped to WGPU and per-operation owners.
- `src/views/*`, `src/libs/*`, `src/gui/*`, `src/dtgtk/*`, `src/bauhaus/*`, accelerators/actions/input modules, workspace/window code, and UI data assets: lighttable/darkroom/import/export/map/tethering/print/slideshow/preferences/control/action/focus/pointer/keyboard/accessibility responsibilities mapped to Iced/service issues.
- `src/lua/*`, `src/lua/types/*`, Lua module registration, events/widgets/storage/configuration, `data/lua/*`, and Lua documentation generation: scripting API behavior, identities, permissions, lifecycle, and compatibility ownership.
- Camera/tethering, map/geolocation, print/CUPS, display/profile/credential/localization, macOS/Windows/Linux platform integration, package metadata, and optional build-feature source trees: integration boundaries and explicit redesigned/unsupported classifications.
- `tests/`, tools, generated data, default presets, color profiles, style/watermark/config assets, reference CLI behavior, and parser-qualified artifacts: test-oracle/data evidence linked to owners without treating test files as implementations.

Source records are finer than file ownership. A shared file maps to multiple issues only through named symbols, tables, registration entries, data records, kernels, call sites, or disjoint responsibility ranges. Directory wildcards are inventory queries and cannot be committed as a final owner record without reviewed expansion.

**Preserve:** complete discovery of every tracked product responsibility; exact canonical source identity; paths/symbols/tables/columns/config keys/actions/programs/kernels/data/test evidence; one canonical owner or explicit exclusion; source-to-Rust target ownership; preserved behavior and persisted versions/defaults/order; reviewed redesign rationale; body-hash linkage; drift reporting; and proof before declaring the C/C++/OpenCL responsibility replaced.

**Redesign:** replace no migration map, title/capability heuristics, broad directory pointers, independent stale manifests, mutable comments, closure-as-evidence, and manual source drift detection with a versioned typed source inventory/map, class schemas, AST/text/data/CMake evidence joins, canonical issue blocks/hashes, deterministic plan/apply, exact readiness findings, upstream drift reports, completed-work audits, and current foundation receipts.

**Excluded:** porting algorithms or UI behavior, copying source text, mechanical C/C++/OpenCL translation, compiling/linking/loading/invoking upstream product code, making product decisions from file names alone, altering the pinned baseline, or treating source-map completion as implementation completion. All product behavior remains in its owner issues.

Rust ownership: source inventory/map/schema/verification/drift and GitHub migration tooling in `crates/xtask` and appropriate parity/testkit modules; committed map under `architecture/`; semantic body/hash integration with completed #518; contributor receipt consumed by #556. Product crates may consume stable generated ownership IDs but never upstream source contents or native handles.

## Implementation

- Build a canonical source-tree inventory from the pinned commit, including tracked C/C++/headers, CMake registration, generated introspection metadata, SQLite schema/migrations, XMP/config keys, OpenCL programs/kernels, image I/O modules, Lua modules, views/libs, tests, and data files.
- Join the inventory to `architecture/darktable-capabilities.toml`, `architecture/darktable-operations.toml`, `architecture/darktable-issue-index.toml`, completed #518’s issue-spec snapshot, and live child issues.
- Correct generated inventories that still use the stale commit before source-map generation or acceptance.
- Add `cargo xtask migration source-map generate|verify|audit-issues|drift`.
- `generate` emits a proposed map and issue-body blocks without GitHub mutation.
- `verify` proves every mapped path/symbol exists at the pinned commit, every open implementation issue has a concrete class-valid anchor, every discovered upstream responsibility has an issue owner or explicit exclusion, and every issue-owned capability appears in its source block.
- `audit-issues` compares live issue body hashes with reviewed source-map blocks and reports missing/stale/generic anchors, stale numeric references, wrong commit, missing preserve/redesign/excluded decisions, dependency contradictions, and one-PR scope conflicts.
- `drift` compares a candidate upstream commit with the accepted commit and reports added/removed/changed files, symbols, parameter versions, schema records, kernels, actions, and owning issues.
- Add class-specific source-anchor schemas for foundation, compatibility/catalog, image I/O/metadata, pixelpipe/color, operation, masks/blending, GPU, export/storage, desktop UI, camera/map/print, scripting/extensions, platform/package, corrective work, reference qualification, and no-direct-analogue infrastructure.
- Populate the initial complete map by reviewing every current child issue, not by guessing from title text alone.
- Integrate source readiness into #158 and completed #518’s parser/snapshot/ready/apply contracts without rewriting #518 as dependent on this issue.
- Update #492 so future generated capability issues cannot be created without a complete reviewed source-anchor block.
- Produce the source-map contributor receipt required by #556. #556, not historical #496, upgrades current foundation acceptance and ready-queue consumption.
- Add post-completion source-audit records for completed contributors whose evidence feeds current readiness/foundation gates. Create focused corrective successors when merged implementation cannot satisfy the newly reviewed source contract.
- Add a self-record for #555 classified `NoDirectAnalogue`, with this source block/body hash and the complete inventory surface above, so the source-map tool is subject to its own parser/hash/drift rules.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing path, missing symbol, wrong commit, ambiguous overload/registration, generated-file-only evidence, stale issue body, capability without source ownership, source responsibility without issue ownership, generic directory wildcard without reviewed expansion, unexplained redesign, or a dependency cycle fails verification.
- A source file may map to multiple issues only when symbol/responsibility ranges are disjoint and explicitly named.
- An issue may map to many files only when the one-PR boundary remains coherent; otherwise split the issue before it becomes ready.
- `src/external/*` dependencies are mapped as integration/reference inputs, not silently copied into RustTable.
- UI text/localization files are evidence only when tied to the owning view/lib/action; listing `po/POTFILES.in` does not satisfy source ownership.
- Source-map generation never mutates GitHub. Apply requires a reviewed plan containing expected issue body hash, labels, milestone, and replacement body; drift causes a conflict and no write.
- Live GitHub/source resolution failure cannot overwrite the committed map or reviewed issue snapshot.
- Absolute local paths, source-tree credentials, private fixture locations, source contents, or copyrighted source dumps never enter committed receipts or issue bodies.
- A completed issue cannot be made current merely by rewriting its body after merge. Missing implementation evidence creates a successor such as #556.
- #555 cannot exempt itself from source classification, body hashing, dependency-cycle detection, or drift merely because it implements the verifier.

## Test matrix

- Representative source mapping for a zero-parameter IOP, versioned IOP, ROI-changing geometry IOP, GPU IOP, mask/blend subsystem, database table/migration, XMP history, raster decoder/encoder, storage destination, GTK/Bauhaus UI, camera/tethering, map, printing, Lua API, platform integration, reference qualification, corrective work, and no-direct-analogue infrastructure.
- Complete root/CMake/source/data/test inventory, ignored/build/generated boundaries, shared-file symbol splits, transitive kernel includes, registration tables, schema/config/action records, and self-record validation.
- Missing/renamed path or symbol, wrong source commit, stale generated capability commit, duplicate owner, unowned responsibility, generic wildcard, incomplete operation callbacks, missing OpenCL kernel, missing schema column, stale issue-body hash, and dependency cycle.
- Upstream drift adding/removing an operation, parameter version, database column, kernel, action, UI module, build option, registered imageio/Lua module, source file, generated data record, or CMake guard produces exact owning-issue findings.
- Reviewed multi-file and split-responsibility cases.
- Comment-to-body promotion, body-hash drift, concurrent edit/apply conflict, idempotent rerun, and offline snapshot verification.
- Completed issue with missing current responsibility creates an audit/successor and cannot pass by rewritten prose alone.
- Full P0/P1 source-block audit plus complete P2–P4 blocked-remediation output.
- Mutation removing or weakening #555’s own source block/self-record fails the same readiness verifier.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --commit cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad` reports every open child issue source-anchored or explicitly blocked, and every discovered required darktable responsibility accounted for.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map verify --issue 555` validates this issue’s `NoDirectAnalogue` self-record and exact body hash.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map audit-issues --parent 158` reports zero missing, generic, wrong-commit, stale, contradictory, or cyclic source-ready issue sections.
- Every operation issue names its exact IOP translation unit and relevant kernels/callbacks; every UI issue names its view/lib/Bauhaus/action sources; every compatibility issue names its schema/XMP/config sources.
- `cargo xtask github ready-issues` composes completed #518 semantic status with #555 source status and excludes any body whose source block is absent, stale, or hash-mismatched.
- Generated capability issues cannot be created and PRs cannot close source-owned issues without a reviewed map matching the exact body hash.
- A repository scan proves no product package compiles, links, loads, invokes, or mechanically translates the pinned C/C++/OpenCL implementation.
- #556 consumes the current source-map receipt and rejects the historical #496 foundation schema, stale capability/operation manifests, generic issue pointers, and source maps pinned to another commit.
- Re-running the reviewed source migration/apply plan proposes zero mutations.

## Dependencies

Depends on completed #492, #495, #515, and #518. Blocks broad P2/P3 implementation acceptance until the corresponding issue source block is valid; it does not block a P0/P1 item whose exact live body has already been directly source-anchored and validated during this audit. Current foundation consumer: #556.

## One-PR boundary

One PR implements source inventory/map schemas, generators/verifiers/drift reporting, reviewed initial map, issue-spec/readiness integration, contributor receipt for #556, reviewed issue-body migration, completed-work audits, fixtures, self-record validation, and tests. It does not port product algorithms or UI behavior, repair contributor implementations, or change the pinned darktable baseline.
issues.title: Make every migration issue source-anchored to the pinned darktable tree

issues.body:
Parent: #158

## Outcome

Correct the foundation gate accepted under #496/PR #525 so the current `main` commit cannot be treated as foundation-complete until it has a valid canonical darktable source map, current reviewed issue-body hashes, and the real persisted catalog-to-preview evidence from #181. Keep the existing gate architecture, but replace its pre-source-map/synthetic acceptance with one current, executable, fail-closed foundation receipt and migrate open issue dependencies away from treating historical #496 closure as current foundation evidence.

## Observed defects

- #496 was closed before #555 existed and before #181 delivered its real persisted catalog-to-preview slice. PR #525 therefore could not have consumed either required receipt.
- The live #496 body was later expanded to describe source-map and real-preview acceptance that is not present in the merged implementation. A rewritten closed issue body is not implementation evidence.
- The current gate has no accepted `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml` identity, no exact source-block/body-hash audit for every open implementation issue, and no proof that source-map, operation, capability, fixture, and executable references use the same pinned commit.
- A closed gate cannot depend on open #555/#181 and still represent completed current-main evidence. The correction must be an explicit successor, not a cosmetic reopen.
- Broad product readiness remains ambiguous until the ready-issue command consumes the corrected gate receipt for issue classes that require foundation acceptance.
- Many later open issues still name closed #496 in their dependency prose. Without a reviewed migration, legacy dependency parsing can treat those issues as foundation-unblocked even though the required #555/#181 evidence is absent.

## Fixed decisions

- #496 remains closed as the completed first gate implementation. This issue is its sole corrective successor for source-map and real-preview acceptance.
- Preserve `cargo xtask foundation verify --mode pr|merge`, `quality/foundation-gate.toml`, typed contributor rows, deterministic aggregate receipts, and content-addressed external artifacts where their current implementation remains sound.
- Bump the accepted foundation receipt/contract schema. The #496 schema is historical and cannot satisfy current readiness after this issue lands.
- Required current contributors are:
  - #446 priority/dependency governance;
  - completed #518 semantic issue-spec snapshot/body hashes;
  - #555 canonical source inventory/map, path-symbol validation, issue source-block hashes, source drift, and zero unowned required responsibility;
  - #516 schema-3 differential image evidence and external artifact hashes;
  - #449/#494 canonical source/executable/data identity and real CPU/XMP renders;
  - #450 parser-qualified fixture corpus;
  - #495 current operation semantics/decoders/ABI evidence;
  - #515 process/validation ownership and platform receipts;
  - #453 platform/dependency architecture;
  - #517 compiler/ecosystem/lock/advisory receipts;
  - #492 capability ownership/replacement index;
  - #181 real persisted redb catalog → source snapshot → decoder → persisted edit → CPU preview → deterministic PNG receipt.
- PR mode validates schemas, hashes, source-map/issue-body consistency, current commit identity, deterministic fixtures, and mutation coverage without requiring expensive external executions.
- Merge mode requires the real qualified external/source/corpus/platform/preview rows. Required rows cannot be converted to warning, skipped, stale, unavailable, or synthetic.
- Source-map completeness is checked against the exact current issue snapshot. An issue body updated after the reviewed map invalidates the gate until the map/body hash is reviewed again.
- The aggregate receipt contains identities and bounded findings only. It never embeds source files, image planes, catalog/database bytes, process output, private paths, or secrets.
- The ready-issue command requires a current passing merge receipt for the issue classes declared by policy; closing this issue alone is insufficient if the receipt is stale or missing.
- Every open issue reference to #496 is classified during a deterministic migration:
  - replace #496 with #556 when the issue means “current foundation acceptance must pass before this implementation”;
  - retain #496 only when the issue is discussing the historical gate implementation or a completed predecessor relationship, and make that historical role explicit;
  - add #555 separately when exact source-map readiness is an issue-level prerequisite rather than relying only on the aggregate gate.
- Dependency migration is two-phase and conflict-safe: generate a reviewed plan containing current issue body hash, proposed dependency block, labels/milestone, and reason; apply only exact reviewed replacements; changed bodies are conflicts and receive no partial mutation.
- The committed issue-spec/source-map snapshots and live GitHub bodies must converge after migration. A stale `Depends on #496` token cannot remain authoritative merely because #496 is closed.
- No product algorithm, UI feature, codec, catalog migration, or source-map technical decision is repaired inside this PR. Contributor defects remain in their owner issues.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` RustTable infrastructure. The nearest typed darktable architectural context is retained only as an evidence boundary; this issue does not claim a product-selector analogue.

- `src/main.c` — `int main(int argc, char *argv[])`: nearest application lifecycle entrypoint.
- `src/common/darktable.c` — `void dt_cleanup()`: nearest coordinated teardown boundary.
- `src/control/control.c` — `void dt_control_shutdown()`: nearest typed work shutdown boundary.

**Preserve:** no upstream product responsibility is declared replaced until it has one current owner, explicit preserve/redesign/exclusion decisions, and acceptance evidence against the pinned source or a reviewed exact data/schema oracle.

**Redesign:** replace informal foundation completion, closed-issue status, and legacy dependency tokens with typed current-commit contributor identities, source-map coverage, issue-body hashes, real external/vertical-slice evidence, conflict-safe dependency migration, and fail-closed readiness integration.

**Excluded:** implementation of the mapped capabilities, source-map generation itself, fixture creation, external reference provisioning, operation decoding, platform services, or preview rendering. This gate only validates contributor evidence and migrates the issue references needed to consume it.

Rust ownership: `crates/xtask` foundation verification/readiness/dependency-migration commands, `quality/foundation-gate.toml`, bounded receipt/artifact verification, reviewed issue mutation plans, and workflow publication.

## Implementation

- Define the new foundation contract/receipt schema and explicit migration rejection for the #496 schema.
- Add #555 source-map contributor fields: canonical source commit, source-map schema/hash, source inventory hash, issue snapshot hash, zero-gap counts, issue-body source-block hashes, drift status, and bounded findings.
- Add #181 contributor fields: exact catalog/source/photo/edit/graph/decoder/pixel/output identities, process/restart evidence, artifact hash, and current producer schema.
- Reconcile every existing contributor to the current commit/build/toolchain/reference/source identities and accepted schema; reject superseded #447/#452/#456 and stale #496-era rows.
- Implement PR and merge execution policies, external artifact resolution/hash checks, current-main freshness, deterministic ordering/hashing, cancellation cleanup, and privacy/path redaction.
- Update `cargo xtask github ready-issues` and the PR closing contract to consume the new foundation receipt where policy requires it.
- Add `cargo xtask foundation plan-dependency-migration --from 496 --to 556` and a reviewed optimistic apply mode, or integrate equivalent typed plan/apply behavior into the existing #518/#555 issue migration command. The plan audits every open issue and classifies current-foundation, historical, source-only, and unrelated references.
- Update the committed issue-spec/source-map snapshots and all reviewed live open issue dependency blocks affected by the plan. Re-running the plan must propose zero mutations.
- Update #158 and #496 post-completion audit links to name this successor and remove any apparent closed-issue dependency on open work.
- Add mutation fixtures for every contributor and identity edge, including missing/stale/tampered source map, body drift, wrong source commit, synthetic preview, stale main receipt, required unavailable rows, legacy #496 bypass, apply conflict, and partial-mutation prevention.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Missing/unsupported contributor schema, wrong producer, wrong commit/build/toolchain/reference, stale issue body, source-map gap, missing path/symbol, unowned responsibility, stale preview, missing artifact, hash mismatch, required skipped/unavailable/warning, or expired external bundle blocks the gate.
- A closed contributor issue with stale or incomplete current evidence does not pass by status alone.
- An open issue whose semantic meaning requires current foundation evidence but still resolves only to historical #496 is `StaleFoundationDependency` and is excluded from the ready queue.
- Historical references to #496 are allowed only when explicitly classified and must not produce a readiness edge.
- Missing/ambiguous/multiple foundation references, issue-body/hash drift, labels/milestone drift, dependency-cycle introduction, or apply failure produces no mutation for that issue and no silent continuation.
- Network/external-cache failure cannot mutate committed contracts or create a partial accepted receipt.
- Gate cancellation cleans owned work through #515 and publishes no success artifact.
- Re-running unchanged inputs produces the same normalized aggregate identity and finding order.

## Test matrix

- Complete synthetic contract graph plus each contributor missing, stale, wrong schema, wrong identity, failed, required unavailable, warning, tampered, and superseded.
- Source-map wrong commit/path/symbol/body hash, duplicate/unowned responsibility, unexplained redesign, generic directory anchor, and upstream drift.
- Real-preview missing/synthetic/mock/direct-table/direct-decoder/wrong-edit/wrong-hash/stale-process/collision cases.
- Reference/source/corpus/operation/toolchain/platform identity mismatches.
- Current versus previous main commit receipts, immutable external-bundle reuse, cancellation, privacy/path redaction, and deterministic aggregate hashing.
- Current-foundation #496 dependency replacement, explicit historical retention, source-only #555 addition, unrelated numeric reference, malformed dependency block, duplicate references, cycle prevention, body/label/milestone conflict, partial apply failure, retry, and idempotent rerun.
- Ready-queue behavior before/after a current passing merge receipt, after deliberate receipt staleness, and with a stale legacy #496 dependency.

## Acceptance evidence

- `cargo test -p xtask foundation` passes contract, contributor, source-map, preview, readiness, dependency-migration, privacy, and mutation suites.
- `cargo xtask foundation verify --mode pr` passes within the PR surface and rejects every required mutation fixture.
- `cargo xtask foundation verify --mode merge` emits a current aggregate receipt with zero source gaps/stale issue blocks, real #494 CPU/XMP evidence, zero required corpus skips, complete #495 evidence, all supported platform receipts, complete #517 evidence, and real #181 persisted-preview evidence.
- `cargo xtask github ready-issues` blocks applicable product issues without the current merge receipt, with a stale #496 dependency, or after source/body/main drift; it unblocks according to their own dependencies after the current receipt passes.
- The reviewed dependency migration leaves zero open issue bodies that use #496 as current foundation acceptance, while preserving explicitly historical references.
- Re-running the dependency migration proposes zero issue mutations.
- The previous #496 foundation schema can no longer satisfy current readiness.

## Dependencies

Depends on completed #496 and #518, plus open #181 and #555.

## One-PR boundary

One corrective PR upgrades the foundation contract/receipt, integrates #555 and #181, rejects the historical #496 schema, migrates reviewed open issue dependency references, updates readiness/workflow publication, and adds mutation tests. Do not implement or repair contributor capabilities, mass-rewrite unrelated issue prose, port product code, or change the pinned darktable baseline.
issues.title: Complete foundation acceptance with source-map and real catalog-preview evidence

## Outcome

After the canonical repository layout was renamed to sibling `RustTable`, `Darktable`, and `worktrees` directories, ensure the agent worktree helper creates new worktrees in the sibling `worktrees` directory when invoked from the primary RustTable clone.

## Acceptance criteria

- `scripts/dev/create-agent-worktree.sh` resolves `/Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/worktrees` from the primary clone without requiring `--worktrees`.
- Existing invocations from a linked worktree continue to resolve the same canonical sibling directory.

issues.title: Make agent worktree helper use the sibling worktrees directory

issues.body:
## Parent

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Make the required full local validation path deterministic by eliminating the `reference_runner` exit-status race.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` RustTable infrastructure. The nearest typed darktable architectural context is retained only as an evidence boundary; this issue does not claim a product-selector analogue.

- `src/main.c` — `int main(int argc, char *argv[])`: nearest reference-process lifecycle entrypoint.
- `src/control/control.c` — `void dt_control_shutdown()`: nearest coordinated shutdown boundary.
- `src/control/jobs.c` — `void dt_control_job_cancel(_dt_job_t *job)`: nearest typed cancellation terminal state.

**Preserve:** repeated source-anchored reference execution produces deterministic evidence or reports the first true failure.

**Redesign:** RustTable uses bounded Rust child/process-group orchestration and structured receipts rather than darktable globals or C job structures.

**Excluded:** changing darktable behavior; weakening comparisons; retries/quarantine; fixture removal; product behavior or workflow changes.

## Rust ownership

- `crates/rusttable-testkit/src/reference/runner.rs` — child lifecycle, reader completion, leader exit-status collection, cancellation and timeout behavior.
- `crates/rusttable-testkit/tests/reference_runner.rs` — deterministic completed-run regression and fixture behavior.

## Fixed decisions

- After stdout and stderr readers disconnect, continue polling the bounded leader until its real exit status is observable.
- Preserve the first real timeout, cancellation, output-limit, spawn, or child-exit failure; never synthesize success or retry it away.
- Keep Unix process-group descendant signalling for cancellation/timeout.

## Implementation

- `crates/rusttable-testkit/src/reference/runner.rs` keeps the reference leader alive after reader completion and records the actual terminal status.
- `crates/rusttable-testkit/tests/reference_runner.rs` exercises the completed-run race through the testkit integration point.

## Failure and edge behavior

- Reader-pipe shutdown before leader reaping must not produce `Completed { code: None, success: false }` for a successful run.
- Timeout, cancellation, output limit, spawn failure, and non-zero leader exit remain terminal diagnostic receipts.
- A completed leader with valid output produces one completed receipt with its actual exit status.

## Test matrix and acceptance evidence

- Oracle fixture: `fake_executable(..., "success")` in `success_receipt_is_repeatable_and_keeps_raw_logs` emits an expected exit-code-0 completed receipt for `reference.synthetic`.
- Focused regression: `cargo test -p rusttable-testkit --test reference_runner --locked --offline`.
- Stress evidence: 100 repeated focused suite invocations pass without a synthetic `code: None` receipt.
- Scope evidence: `cargo test -p rusttable-testkit --all-targets --all-features --locked --offline`.
- Full evidence: `bash scripts/precommit-fast.sh` and pre-push validation during the pushed implementation.
- The implementation must preserve reference evidence; it must not skip, weaken, quarantine, or retry the check.

## Dependencies

None.

## One-PR boundary

One ready-for-review PR isolates the deterministic reference-runner repair only.
issues.title: Make full-workspace reference-runner validation deterministic

issues.body:
## Outcome

Align pre-push validation with the uncapped local-gate policy by removing the stale 150-second surface budget while retaining efficient parallel execution and complete failure reporting.

## Acceptance criteria

- No pre-push deadline rejects a correct full validation run solely for elapsed time.
- Process cancellation, output bounds, and cleanup remain safe.
- Tests cover uncapped pre-push scheduling and receipt behavior.

## One-PR boundary

One PR changes only validation deadline policy and its tests.
issues.title: Remove the obsolete pre-push validation deadline

issues.body:
## Parent

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Eliminate all pull-request-triggered GitHub Actions validation. A local pre-commit run must perform the complete merge-readiness validation; GitHub Actions must run the complete and long-running validation only after a squash merge reaches `main`.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` RustTable infrastructure. The nearest typed darktable architectural context is retained only as an evidence boundary; this issue does not claim a product-selector analogue.

- `CMakeLists.txt` — `project(darktable VERSION`: nearest top-level product build declaration.
- `src/CMakeLists.txt` — `add_subdirectory(external)`: nearest typed source-tree composition declaration.

**Preserve:** every merge is validated against the complete RustTable build, tests, strict linting, formatting, source policy, workspace policy, and repository contracts before it reaches `main`.

**Redesign:** RustTable runs merge-readiness checks locally through pre-commit and reserves hosted Actions for post-merge verification, cross-platform coverage, and long-running checks.

**Excluded:** changing darktable behavior, weakening any validation command, branch-protection bypasses, or adding a PR-only replacement workflow.

**NoDirectAnalogue:** GitHub Actions event routing and Git hook orchestration are RustTable development infrastructure, not a darktable product subsystem.

## Rust and validation ownership

- `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.githooks/pre-commit`, and `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` — complete local merge-readiness gate.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/ci.rs` — authoritative validation scheduler.
- `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` and `scripts/main-ci.sh` — post-merge full and long-running validation.
- `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml` — removed.

## Fixed decisions

- No workflow may trigger on `pull_request` or `pull_request_target`.
- Pre-commit has no elapsed-time cap and must run every merge-readiness check locally.
- Main-only Actions rerun the complete validation and additionally run platform, package, provenance, rolling compiler, nightly, and other long-running lanes.
- GitHub Actions failures do not block local merge-readiness; they are repaired from the post-merge signal.

## Acceptance criteria

- `rg -n 'pull_request|pull_request_target' .github/workflows` returns no workflow trigger.
- The PR workflow file is removed and workflow inventory validation expects only main-triggered workflows.
- `pre-commit run --all-files` executes the same full merge-readiness contract as `cargo xtask ci precommit` without per-check or total time caps.
- A push to `main` runs `rust-main.yml`, which retains full validation and its long-running cross-platform/compiler/package lanes.
- Workflow, hook, and scheduler tests cover the absence of PR triggers and the full pre-commit delegation.

## Dependencies

None.

## One-PR boundary

One ready-for-review PR changes only PR-triggered workflow removal, local pre-commit coverage, and main-only workflow validation.


## Rust ownership

- `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.githooks/pre-commit`, and `scripts/precommit-fast.sh` own the complete local merge-readiness entrypoint.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/ci.rs`, `crates/xtask/src/commands/ci_support.rs`, and `crates/xtask/src/commands/ci_tests.rs` own scheduler surfaces, full-check delegation, and policy tests.
- `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` and `scripts/main-ci.sh` own post-merge hosted validation; `.github/workflows/rust-pr.yml` is intentionally absent from the resulting tree.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/repo.rs` and `scripts/github/repository-policy.ts` own workflow inventory and event-policy enforcement.

## Acceptance evidence

- Oracle fixtures: `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `scripts/test-pr-ci.sh`, and the repository-policy fixtures under `scripts/github/`.
- `pre-commit run --all-files`, `cargo xtask ci precommit`, and `cargo test -p xtask --all-targets --locked --offline` must agree on the full local contract.
- `rg -n 'pull_request|pull_request_target' .github/workflows` must return no trigger declarations, while main-only validation remains present.
issues.title: Move pull-request validation entirely into local pre-commit

issues.body:
## Parent

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Centralize every internal crate dependency plus Tokio, Wasmtime, and process-wrap in the root workspace dependencies, and add a deterministic manifest-policy gate that enforces inherited package metadata, workspace lint opt-in, canonical naming, and centralized dependency declarations.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` RustTable infrastructure. The nearest typed darktable architectural context is retained only as an evidence boundary; this issue does not claim a product-selector analogue.

- `CMakeLists.txt` — `project(darktable VERSION`: nearest top-level dependency/build declaration.
- `src/CMakeLists.txt` — `add_subdirectory(external)`: nearest typed component-composition declaration.

**Preserve:** a single authoritative dependency graph and strict unsafe-code/lint policy across every Rust crate.

**Redesign:** Cargo workspace dependencies and workspace lints replace scattered crate-local declarations.

**Excluded:** changing product behavior, adding unpinned dependency sources, or weakening lint inheritance.

**NoDirectAnalogue:** Cargo manifest policy is RustTable development infrastructure.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every internal dependency is declared once in root workspace.dependencies and consumed with workspace = true.
- Tokio, Wasmtime, and process-wrap are centralized at the root; duplicate crate-local version declarations are removed.
- Every workspace crate opts into workspace lints, including rusttable-import and rusttable-metadata.
- A manifest verifier checks inherited package fields, lint opt-in, canonical names, and centralized internal dependencies.
- The verifier is included in the complete local pre-commit contract and covered by focused fixtures.

## Dependencies

None.

## One-PR boundary

One PR changes manifests and the manifest-policy verifier only.

## Rust ownership

- Root `Cargo.toml` owns `[workspace.dependencies]` and `[workspace.lints]` declarations.
- `crates/rusttable-import/Cargo.toml` and `crates/rusttable-metadata/Cargo.toml` own their workspace lint opt-in and centralized dependency consumption.
- Every other workspace `Cargo.toml` owns only `workspace = true` references for internal crates and the approved Tokio, Wasmtime, and process-wrap entries.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/dependencies.rs`, `crates/xtask/src/commands/repo.rs`, and `crates/xtask/tests/cli.rs` own manifest-policy verification and its offline CLI contract.

## Acceptance evidence

- Oracle fixtures: root `Cargo.toml`, all workspace manifests, `Cargo.lock`, and `crates/xtask/tests/fixtures/` manifest-policy fixtures.
- `cargo xtask dependencies verify --offline`, `cargo check --workspace --all-targets --locked --offline`, and the manifest verifier must reject a missing lint inheritance or duplicated internal path/version declaration.
- The source-map receipt records this issue as the owner of the Cargo manifest policy surface.
issues.title: Centralize workspace dependencies and enforce Cargo manifest policy

issues.body:
## Parent

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Rewrite CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md, and RELEASE_NOTES.md for RustTable; add ownership for crates, architecture policy, dependency configuration, workflows, packaging, and security-sensitive code.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` RustTable infrastructure. The nearest typed darktable architectural context is retained only as an evidence boundary; this issue does not claim a product-selector analogue.

- `CMakeLists.txt` — `project(darktable VERSION`: nearest project identity/build entrypoint.
- `src/CMakeLists.txt` — `add_subdirectory(external)`: nearest typed source ownership/composition context.

**Preserve:** clear contributor responsibilities and release accountability.

**Redesign:** RustTable documentation and ownership replace inherited darktable/RawSpeed references.

**Excluded:** preserving GTK, CMake, C API, RawSpeed, or darktable release guidance as active project documentation.

**NoDirectAnalogue:** GitHub ownership and contributor guidance are RustTable infrastructure.

## Acceptance criteria

- CODEOWNERS has explicit current RustTable ownership for crates, architecture, dependency configuration, workflows, packaging, and security-sensitive paths.
- CONTRIBUTING documents RustTable worktrees, issue-to-PR workflow, just/pre-commit commands, beta toolchain, strict lints, and no-unsafe policy.
- RELEASE_NOTES describes RustTable release evidence and no longer describes darktable.
- No project-facing document presents darktable, GTK, CMake, C APIs, or RawSpeed as RustTable implementation guidance.

## Dependencies

None.

## One-PR boundary

One PR changes only current project-facing documentation and ownership policy.

## Rust ownership

- `.github/CODEOWNERS` owns path-level review routing for `crates/`, `architecture/`, dependency configuration, `.github/workflows/`, packaging, and security-sensitive files.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` owns contributor workflow, worktrees, issue-to-PR boundaries, validation commands, and strict Rust policy.
- `RELEASE_NOTES.md` owns RustTable release evidence and user-visible migration notes.
- `scripts/dev/test-readme-contract.sh`, `crates/xtask/src/commands/repo.rs`, and `crates/xtask/tests/cli.rs` own documentation and ownership policy checks.

## Acceptance evidence

- Oracle fixtures: `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `RELEASE_NOTES.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and the repository policy tests.
- Project-facing documents must contain RustTable guidance and must not present darktable GTK, CMake, C APIs, or RawSpeed as active implementation instructions.
- `cargo xtask repo verify` and the documentation contract tests must identify stale ownership or project-facing guidance.
issues.title: Replace stale project-facing documentation and ownership policy

- `src/CMakeLists.txt` — `add_subdirectory(external)`: nearest typed component-composition declaration.

**Preserve:** complete post-merge validation and pinned-action supply-chain policy.

**Redesign:** reusable GitHub workflows with one authoritative owner per check.

**Excluded:** PR-triggered Actions, duplicate validation lanes, and weakened post-merge coverage.

## Acceptance criteria

- rust-main.yml is split into reusable rust, repository-checks, dependencies, and post-merge distribution/coverage workflows.
- Every complete or long-running check has exactly one workflow owner.
- Dependency security executes once per main revision.
- Workflows remain main-only and all action SHAs are pinned.
- Workflow inventory and scheduler tests prove ownership and no duplicate check execution.

#560.

## One-PR boundary

One PR changes workflow decomposition and workflow tests only.

## Rust ownership

- `.github/workflows/rust-main.yml` owns main-only workflow composition and calls the reusable responsibility workflows.
- `.github/workflows/rust.yml`, `.github/workflows/repo-checks.yml`, `.github/workflows/dependencies.yml`, and post-merge distribution/coverage workflows own exactly one validation responsibility each.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/repo.rs`, `crates/xtask/src/commands/ci.rs`, `scripts/main-ci.sh`, and `scripts/github/repository-policy.ts` own workflow inventory, scheduler ownership, and duplicate-check detection.

## Acceptance evidence

- Oracle fixtures: the complete `.github/workflows/` tree and `scripts/github/repository-policy.test.ts` fixtures.
- `cargo xtask repo verify`, workflow inventory tests, and the main-only workflow smoke must prove one owner per check and exactly one dependency-security execution.
- `rg -n 'pull_request|pull_request_target' .github/workflows` remains empty after the split.
issues.title: Split main validation into reusable responsibility workflows

## Parent

Parent: #158

## Outcome

Backfill the required canonical source-anchor, ownership, evidence, and Preserve/Redesign/Excluded metadata for every open RustTable migration issue reported by the #555 source-map audit.

## Upstream darktable source anchors

Canonical source: `darktable-org/darktable@cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad`. Each typed identifier below was verified with `git -C /Users/cgas/Documents/RustTable/Darktable show cfe57f3bbf5269bfacf31e832267279caa6938ad:<path>`.

Migration classification: `NoDirectAnalogue` RustTable infrastructure. The nearest typed darktable architectural context is retained only as an evidence boundary; this issue does not claim a product-selector analogue.

- `CMakeLists.txt` — `project(darktable VERSION`: nearest canonical project inventory root.
- `src/CMakeLists.txt` — `add_subdirectory(external)`: nearest typed component-registration context for source-map traversal.

**Preserve:** every migration issue names exact upstream evidence, intended behavior, and Rust ownership before it is accepted.

**Redesign:** structured RustTable source maps and issue hashes replace broad prose-only migration planning.

**Excluded:** bare directory globs, stale commits, inferred ownership without evidence, or treating an audit finding as accepted work.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every open migration issue under #158 has canonical commit, exact typed upstream anchors, Preserve/Redesign/Excluded, Rust owners, oracle fixtures, source class, parent, milestone, priority, and dependency evidence.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map audit-issues --parent 158 --format json` returns zero findings.
- `cargo xtask github verify-issue-specs --format json` verifies the recorded body and source-anchor hashes.
- Shared source files use disjoint selectors or explicit shared ownership; no generic directory claims are accepted.
- The backfill is delivered in reviewable issue-body batches with a receipt identifying every updated issue.

## Dependencies

#555.

## One-PR boundary

One PR adds the audit receipt/fixtures needed for the completed issue-body backfill; GitHub metadata updates are reviewed in bounded batches.


## Rust ownership

- `crates/xtask/src/commands/source_map/operations.rs` owns source-map generation, strict audit classification, deterministic ordering, and apply-plan validation.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/source_map/policy.rs` owns the P0/P1 and anchored-issue strictness policy and explicit `debt-*` classification.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/source_map/io.rs` and `crates/xtask/src/commands/source_map/model.rs` own receipt serialization, schema identity, and bounded finding records.
- `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml`, `architecture/darktable-source-inventory.json`, and `architecture/source-map-migration-debt.json` own committed source identity, inventory, and migration-debt evidence.
- `crates/xtask/src/commands/issue_spec/commands.rs` and `architecture/issue-spec-snapshot.json` own live issue body/source-block hash refresh and offline verification.

## Acceptance evidence

- Oracle fixtures: `architecture/issue-spec-snapshot.json`, `architecture/darktable-source-map.toml`, and the pinned `architecture/darktable-source-inventory.json`.
- `cargo xtask migration source-map audit-issues --parent 158 --api-fixture architecture/issue-spec-snapshot.json --format json` must return zero fatal findings; any legacy debt remains explicit in `architecture/source-map-migration-debt.json` and links to #570.
- `cargo test -p xtask --all-targets --locked --offline`, `cargo xtask github verify-issue-specs --api-fixture architecture/issue-spec-snapshot.json --format json`, and source-map verification must validate the receipt without GitHub mutation.
issues.title: Backfill canonical source anchors for all migration issues
